Changelog â€” Retention & Juice Rework

A phased rework layered on top of v0.9.8: save persistence, Web Audio SFX, achievements, daily challenges, respec, prestige, combo milestones, and a moment-to-moment juice pass. Every change lives in maple_game.html. No new assets, no build step.

Heads-up. An earlier implementation of these phases targeted v0.5.1 and was preserved locally at tag phase-1-2-3-on-v0.5.1. This is the rebuild against the current v0.9.8 codebase, which had moved substantially (new bosses like Gravitos, minimap/taxi, chibi character rewrite).

v0.25.474 Frozen Peak — easier mid-air control, hard speed ceiling, lenient inputs

polishMid-air input gets ~2× the pull (0.45 → 0.85 air accel)

User feedback: "make the mechanics more smoother its still a little hard, make it easier to control mid-air". The previous air accel of 0.45 meant input had to fight existing momentum drift; on a slippery surface you'd feel locked into your launch trajectory. Bumped to 0.85 so directional input mid-flight reliably overrides drift. The player can now reach for a missed platform or pull back from over-shooting without fighting the ice.

Ground accel kept at 0.30 (deliberate wind-up, the v0.25.473 fix) — the wind-up is what makes the speed-jump feel rewarding. Ground friction kept at 0.96 (slippery slide is the signature feel). Air friction kept at 0.985 so chained-momentum jumps still preserve speed when the player isn't actively redirecting.

polishHard speed ceiling — chained momentum stays in control

New ICE_MAX_VX = 18 ceiling on horizontal velocity for icy maps. Previously the speed cap was max(moveSpeed, |vx| + accel) — unbounded, so chaining mega-jumps could escalate to vx=20+ and the player felt like they were being thrown around. The 18 ceiling is still 1.5× the regular moveSpeed (12), so peak-speed mega-jumps still hit the 12-unit speed-jump cap and clear ~7 floors at peak — but the upper end stops feeling out-of-control.

polishLenient input grace — bigger jump buffer + coyote time

Two timing windows extended on icy maps for forgiving inputs:

  • Jump buffer 120 ms → 200 ms — pressing space just before landing on a slippery platform still queues the next jump. Easier to chain mega-jumps without frame-perfect timing.
  • Coyote time 140 ms → 220 ms — slipping off a platform edge leaves a generous grace period to press space and recover.

Both gates are gated by game.mapData.isIcy, so the original tighter timing on every other map stays the same.

polishAir accel value rationale

Why 0.85 specifically? At the new value, holding right mid-air for 12 frames (~200 ms) shifts vx by 10 units — enough to completely reverse a max-speed launch within a single jump's airtime. That's the sweet spot: input is dominant when the player wants it, but the underlying drift physics still preserve momentum when they don't press anything.

If it's still hard: the issue is likely the 4-floor combo gate (Icy Tower exact). A casual climb that only clears 1-3 floors per jump resets the combo. To build streaks, build speed on the ground first, then take big jumps that clear 4+ floors at once. The HUD's combo decay bar shows you have ~2.4-4.4 s between landings — plenty of time to wind up the next big launch.

v0.25.473 Frozen Peak — Icy-Tower-faithful combo, slower control, star particles

polishSlower ground acceleration for tighter control

0.45 → 0.30 on the icy ground. Player builds speed deliberately rather than instantly jolting to top speed. Combined with the higher speed-jump cap below, this matches the original Icy Tower feel where every floor of speed builds toward bigger combos. Air acceleration also nudged 0.5 → 0.45 so mid-air drift is gentler.

polishFaster speed = much higher jump

Speed-jump multiplier 0.65 → 0.75; cap 10 → 12. Numbers at the new tuning:

|vx| at takeoffBoostTotal vyPeak heightFloors cleared
0 (cold)0-10.5~110 px1.5
6 (medium)4.5-15.0~225 px3.2
10 (fast)7.5-18.0~324 px4.6
12 (max ground)9.0-19.5~387 px5.5
16+ (chained momentum)12.0 (cap)-22.5~507 px7.2

featCombo system rewritten — Icy-Tower-faithful 4-floor minimum

The combo no longer cares about launch speed. The only gate is floors cleared per jump:

  • 4+ floors in a single jump → combo step ( +1 ); cheer overlay fires from the FIRST big jump (NICE! → GREAT! → AMAZING! → FANTASTIC! → SUPER! → WOW! → EXCELLENT! → INSANE! → IMPOSSIBLE! → GODLIKE!).
  • 1-3 floors while comboing → combo BREAKS, resets to 0. The iconic Icy Tower moment: you missed the chain by going too short.
  • 0 floors / falling → combo breaks.

This is the original Icy Tower exact rule. Sustained big jumps demand precision: you have to keep clearing 4+ floors per launch, and a careful "small hop" to reposition kills the streak.

featStar particles — high-arc trail + combo land burst

Two new star-shape particle effects on Frozen Peak:

  • High-arc star trail. While airborne with vy < -14 (the threshold for "this is a real jump"), 1-2 yellow / blue stars spawn every 3 frames trailing behind the player. Brightness scales with launch intensity — a max-momentum mega-jump leaves a trail of gold stars; a normal climb-jump leaves a thin blue trail. Mimics Icy Tower's signature spread-eagle character pose with stars shooting out.
  • Combo land burst. Each combo step pops 8 / 12 / 16 stars (small / mid / big combos) on the landing platform. Stars use the combo-tier palette: ice-blue → gold (≥4) → magenta (≥6).

polishNew star particle renderer

Particles can now carry shape: 'star' + rot + spin fields. drawParticles dispatches to a new _drawStar(cx, cy, outer, inner, points, rot) helper that draws a 5-point star inscribed in a circle. The renderer integrates the spin per frame so each star tumbles realistically as it falls. Cheap — 10 line-to + closePath + fill per particle.

polishHUD prompt updated

The empty-combo HUD message now reads "Clear 4+ floors in one jump → combo step" so first-time players know the exact rule.

v0.25.472 Map placement tool — press 7 in-game to open

polishPress 7 from anywhere in the game to launch the placement tool

v0.25.470 added the standalone map_placement_tool.html for drag-to-position prompts but required the user to navigate to the URL by hand. v0.25.472 wires it into the in-game keydown handler:

  • Key 7 — opens map_placement_tool.html in a new tab via window.open. The open() call sits inside the keydown event handler so the user-gesture defeats most popup blockers.
  • Repeat-press refocuses — if the tool tab is already open, pressing 7 again brings it to the front instead of spawning a duplicate.
  • Popup-blocked toast — if the browser still blocks (some strict configurations), a clear toast says "Pop-up blocked — allow pop-ups for this site, then press 7 again".
  • Confirmation toast — successful open shows "🛠 Map Placement Tool opened (new tab) — press 7 to refocus" so the user knows where the tool went.

polishTool header now shows the return path

Added a small subtitle to the placement-tool header: "Opened from the game with 7 · close this tab to return" so a first-time user understands the workflow without reading docs.

Workflow. While playing, press 7 → tool opens in a new tab → drag NPCs / props in the 2D top-down view → click Export prompt ↗ → copy the markdown block → paste back into your conversation here with "apply this" → I'll write it into maple_game.html. Game state in the original tab is unaffected by the tool — it's read-only.

v0.25.471 Smoother Icy Tower combo on Frozen Peak — better jumps, persistent HUD, aura

polishJump physics retuned for a juicier momentum-to-altitude curve

v0.25.458 had the speed-boost cap at 7.5 and multiplier at 0.55, capping max climb at ~2.8 floors per jump. The retuned curve makes max-momentum launches clear ~5 floors — closer to the Icy Tower feel.

  • Speed-jump multiplier 0.55 → 0.65 — every px/frame of horizontal speed translates a bit more into vertical lift.
  • Speed-jump cap 7.5 → 10 — at max ground speed (|vx|=12), the boost reaches the cap, giving vy = -10.5 - 10 = -20.5 at takeoff (~336 px peak ≈ 4.8 floors at the 70 px Frozen Peak spacing).

polishCombo eligibility relaxed — casual climbers also chain combos

The launch-speed gate dropped from |vx| > 6 to |vx| > 3. The previous threshold cut combo from anyone playing carefully on the slippery surface; now a gentle hop that lands a floor higher counts. The strict "high-speed launch only" rule made the system feel exclusive — now it rewards any upward progress.

polishCombo decay timer scales with combo size

Base timer 1500 ms → 2400 ms. Adds +100 ms per combo step, capped at 4400 ms. So:

  • Combo ×1: 2.5 s grace window
  • Combo ×5: 2.9 s
  • Combo ×10: 3.4 s
  • Combo ×20+: 4.4 s (capped)

High streaks get more recovery time between landings — momentum doesn't punish a brief pause.

featPersistent Icy Tower HUD

New top-center chip on every isVerticalTower map. Always visible, even before the first combo. Two halves:

  • Floor counter (left) — current floor / total (e.g. 34/200) plus the best-floor badge so the climber sees both their progress and personal record.
  • Combo display (right) — large ×N count plus a thin decay bar that drains across the timer's full window. Colours tier up: ice-blue → gold (combo ≥ 4) → pink (combo ≥ 6).

Empty state shows the prompt "Build combo with high-speed climbing jumps" so first-timers know what the system is for.

polishAura sparkles around the player while comboing

At combo ≥ 3, a sparkle particle spawns every 6 frames in an orbit around the player. Colour matches the combo tier (ice-blue → gold → pink) so the player feels the "I'm in the zone" visual continuously rather than only at milestone landings. Cheap (one extra particle every ~100 ms).

polishCombo land effects scale with size

  • Sparkle ring on land — particle count scales with combo (8 / 10 / 14) so small combos pop softly, big combos pop hard.
  • Top-tier ring colour — combo ≥ 6 lands now use a magenta palette instead of just gold; reads as "elite climber" tier.

polishFloor tracking

New player._iceFloorBest + _iceFloorCurrent fields. The HUD reads them; updated on every successful tower landing via the platforms-array's first type:'ground' entry as the y-zero reference.

v0.25.470 Frozen Peak skyscraper — 200-floor vertical tower with fall damage + summit portal · placement tool

featFrozen Peak rebuilt as a 200-floor vertical tower

Frozen Peak is no longer a 2800-wide horizontal map — it's now a 800-wide × 14400-tall vertical skyscraper. The Icy Tower physics from v0.25.458 (slippery floor + speed-boosted jumps + combo cheer) keep working unchanged; the new isVerticalTower flag tells the camera + renderer to scroll up with the climb.

  • 200 climbing platforms generated deterministically (mulberry32 seed = identical layout every load) at 70 px vertical spacing. Ground at y:14070; floors stack up to a wide summit pad at y:70.
  • Width tapers 140 → 90 px as you climb, with a left/right zig-zag bias every 3 floors so the route weaves rather than going straight up.
  • Summit pad at y:70 is wider (200 px) so the player has a stable landing target for the final jump. Tagged tag:'summit' so the platform-tile system can paint it distinctly.
  • Camera vertical follow with the same lerp profile as the horizontal axis, clamped to [0, worldHeight - H]. Default maps unchanged (camera.y stays 0).
  • Render pipeline applies ctx.translate(0, -camera.y) after drawBackground + drawAmbient so the sky stays anchored while the world scrolls — gives a sense of altitude on the climb.

featFall damage scaling — 10% per 20-floor tier, capped at 90%

Track the apex of every airborne arc. On landing, compute fall = current y − apex y. Convert to floors via fallFloorPx (default 70). Free window of fallFreeFloors (20) — falls under 20 floors deal nothing. Each fallTierFloors (20) over the free window adds another 10% max-HP damage:

  • Falls of 1 → 20 floors: 0% damage
  • Falls of 21 → 40 floors: 10% max-HP
  • Falls of 41 → 60 floors: 20%
  • Falls of 61 → 80 floors: 30%
  • ... (escalating)
  • Falls of 181 → 200 floors: 90% (capped — devastating but not auto-kill)

Damage flashes the screen, applies a 600 ms invulnerable window, 400 ms hit-stun, pops a fall-damage number, and spawns a 14-particle dust burst on the landing platform.

featSummit portal → Zodiac Sanctum

Top of the tower has a portal to zodiacHall. Bottom of the tower keeps a portal back to skyGarden (the only way out). Portals now support an optional y field so they anchor at any world altitude:

  • { x:600, y:14070, dest:'skyGarden', name:'◀ Sky Garden' } — bottom
  • { x:400, y:70, dest:'zodiacHall', name:'⭐ Zodiac Sanctum (summit)' } — top

tryPortal + drawPortals both honour po.y (default 480 = legacy ground-line behaviour). Climbing to the summit + walking into the portal teleports the player to the Zodiac Sanctum.

polishSpawn + pit-fall fixes for tall maps

  • Spawn y — loadMap now snaps the player to (ground platform y) − 80 on tower maps so the player spawns above the actual ground (was hardcoded y:400 mid-air halfway up the tower).
  • Pit-fall safety net — the "fell into the void" teleport-to-100,400 used to fire at player.y > H + 100 (700 px). On a tall map you cross y:700 climbing UP. Now bounded by worldHeight + 100 so the safety net only fires for actual out-of-bounds falls.

featNew placement tool — drag-to-position prompt generator

New file: map_placement_tool.html. Open it in a browser and you get a 2D top-down view of any map with draggable markers for every NPC and world prop:

  • Picks up live MAPS + MAP_PROPS by fetching maple_game.html and extracting the declarations.
  • Drag any marker to reposition. The selection panel lets you fine-tune x, y, scale, anchor by typing exact values.
  • Add new entries via the kind / name picker (NPC or prop, type a name).
  • Export prompt button generates a copy-paste markdown block with the updated npcs: and MAP_PROPS arrays — paste it back into the conversation and I'll apply the changes.
How to use the placement tool. Run a local server (python -m http.server 8765) from the worktree, navigate to http://localhost:8765/map_placement_tool.html, pick a map, drag things around, click Export prompt ↗, and paste the output to me with "apply this".

v0.25.469 World waits for ALL assets to load before it appears

polishLoading gate now blocks on every background + sprite + tile + prop

Previously (v0.25.362) the world reveal was gated only on the BAZAAR + CRITICAL bucket — the visible-on-spawn first-paint set. The much-larger DEFERRED bucket (boss bodies, master-class FX, less-common UI, the floors / platforms / world props from v0.25.461-463) streamed in the background and let the player see pop-in for the first 30-60 s of play (boss sprites materialising mid-fight, prop tiles flashing in as they walked past, etc.).

Now maybeReveal gates on the FULL asset count: bzLoaded + critLoaded + defLoaded ≥ BAZAAR.length + CRITICAL.length + DEFERRED.length. The progress bar reflects the total count too, so the percentage is honest end-to-end.

polishBounds against slow / hung connections

  • Per-image 8 s timeout (existing v0.25.319) — a single hung request resolves silently after 8 s and the gate progresses.
  • Hard fallback bumped 30 s → 60 s — the safety net at the bottom of the loading IIFE. On a healthy connection the gate clears in 5-15 s; on a slow connection the 8 s per-image cap means the worst-case wait is bounded by request count × concurrency, well below 60 s.
  • Procedural fallback — if the hard fallback trips, any sprite that didn't load renders its procedural drawing (drawCuteMob, vector-hero, themed-tile fallback, etc.). The world is always playable, even if a CDN is unreachable.
Live verification. Local-host preview clears the full 249-asset gate in ~8 seconds with the bar correctly tracking from 0% → 100%, status text cycling through the load phrases ("Painting Everdawn Bazaar…" → "Floor tiles + props…" → "Almost there…" → "Ready ✓"), and the overlay only fading once the auth gate clears. No pop-in observed on first-frame world reveal.

v0.25.468 Lock-in v0.25.467 gear-visibility fix — banner + boot-time regression guard

polishThree layers of protection against the gear-invisibility bug coming back

User asked: "Yes it works please keep this fix and not make it come back". The v0.25.467 fix was structural (replaced the invariant cache with inline computation), but a future merge / revert / refactor could still re-introduce the buggy pattern. Three lock-ins now make that practically impossible:

polish1 — Banner comment on _drawEquipmentLayer

26-line docstring above the function that explains the bug history (v0.25.316 cached flag → invisible gear), the current invariant (inline 9-slot scan, no cache), and a stern warning to anyone tempted to re-optimise with a cached flag. Includes the perf justification (< 0.01 ms / frame) and references the regression-guard IIFE.

polish2 — Boot-time regression guard

New _lxEqRenderRegressionGuard IIFE runs once at script-init. It reads String(_drawEquipmentLayer) and runs two regex tests:

  • Pattern 1 (loud error): if (player._eqEmpty) return short-circuit — the original v0.25.316 bug. console.error with the full bug story + restore-instruction.
  • Pattern 2 (warn): removal of the inline for (const slot in LX_EQ_REGISTRIES) empty-check. console.warn nudging an audit.

Verified live: current code triggers neither warning. Simulated regression source triggers the error. Wrapped in try/catch so the guard itself can never break the page.

polish3 — Banner comment on _refreshEqEmptyFlag

Documents that the helper is defensive-only post-v0.25.467 — the renderer NO LONGER trusts player._eqEmpty. Anyone tempted to add a new reader sees the warning before they wire it up.

Defense layers in order of strength:
  1. Structural — there's no cached flag for the renderer to trust. The bug class is impossible by construction. (v0.25.467)
  2. Documentary — the banner comments tell the story to anyone reading the code. Forces a deliberate decision to undo the fix.
  3. Runtime — the boot-time guard catches an actual regression on first page load with a loud console message naming the bug.
Together they make the pattern of "stale cache silently breaking gear visibility" essentially extinct in this codebase.

v0.25.467 Studio-designed gear now shows in-game (stale empty-equipment cache fix)

bugStudio designs / equipped gear were invisible despite Apply succeeding

User report: design gear in the Q-key Character Studio, click Apply, then "use" (equip) the item in-game — the design / sprite still doesn't show on the character. Tracked down via live diagnostic: the data flow was correct (Studio Apply ✓ writes player.customPaint + player.customPaintLayers + player.equipped[slot].spriteId), but the renderer skipped every equipment draw.

bugRoot cause — stale player._eqEmpty cache

v0.25.316 introduced a "fast path" caching flag on player._eqEmpty so _drawEquipmentLayer could short-circuit when no visual gear was equipped (the common case for new chars). The flag was refreshed inside Studio Apply / Revert / loadState — but not inside the inventory click handler that's the primary equip path. So:

  1. New player has no gear → _eqEmpty = true (correct)
  2. Player opens inventory, clicks a helmet → player.equipped.helmet = item ✓
  3. _eqEmpty stays at true (bug — the inventory handler doesn't refresh)
  4. Renderer sees _eqEmpty = true and short-circuits
  5. Helmet never renders

The Studio Apply path was the special case that did refresh the flag, so designs that went purely through Studio worked — but anything equipped from inventory (the most common path) silently failed.

polishTwo-prong fix

  • Inline empty-check in _drawEquipmentLayer. Replaces the cached-flag short-circuit with a 9-slot inline scan. The cost is well below 0.01 ms per frame — not measurable. The benefit: the path is provably correct. No future equip site can break the invariant by forgetting to call a refresh helper.
  • Defensive _refreshEqEmptyFlag() calls at both inventory click sites (equip + unequip). Belt-and-braces measure: any other site that still trusts the cached flag continues to see correct values.
Regression-prevention. The original v0.25.316 bug was an invariant cache — a derived value that only stays correct if every writer calls a refresh helper. Two writers existed; only one was wired. The fix replaces the cache with on-demand computation, eliminating the invariant. This pattern of swapping invariant-caches for inline checks is the right move whenever the cache is cheaper to recompute than to prove always-correct via writer audit.

v0.25.466 Bbox-aware prop anchoring — no more floating + hanging baskets attach to platforms

bugProps were floating ~9 px above the floor / platforms

User report: world props (banners, anvils, fountains, lanterns, etc.) and NPCs that are supposed to be on the floor were rendering with a visible gap between their visible base and the ground tile. Diagnosis: the 32 authored prop sources are 768×768 PNGs with significant transparent padding inside the canvas (the visible art typically ends at y≈680-710, leaving 60-85 px of empty rows at the bottom). My v0.25.463 prop renderer naively anchored the canvas's bottom edge at prop.y, so when the source content had bottom padding, the rendered visible base sat above the floor line — a 60-85 / 768 ratio scaled down to ~9 px of floating gap at the default 80 px render height.

polishFix — bbox-aware anchor using the same approach as NPC sprites

Each prop now goes through a one-shot _detectSpriteBboxBottom + _detectSpriteBboxTop pass on image load. Result lives in LX_OBJECTS_META[key] alongside the image. drawWorldProps uses the meta to compute sy so the source's visible base maps to prop.y, with a 1 px bury so feet read as grounded rather than floating. Mirrors the NPC sprite anchoring system (_drawNpcSprite) which already handled this correctly via NPC_SPRITE_META[name].bboxBottomY.

feat'hang' anchor — flowerbaskets + lanterns now attach to platforms

New anchor: 'hang' field on prop entries. When set, drawWorldProps maps the source's visible top (bbox top Y) to prop.y instead of the visible bottom. Combined with positioning prop.y at a real platform's bottom edge, the prop now visually dangles from the platform — exactly as expected for a hanging basket or paper lantern.

Wired this version:

  • Town hub — flowerbasket at x:780, y:370 (sidewalk-tier platform underside; platforms there are y:360, h:10).
  • Marketplace — two flowerbaskets at y:240, dangling from the long awning beam (y:230, h:10).
  • Shadow-Woven Hood — three paper lanterns repositioned to platform undersides: two from the lantern-beam undersides (y:152) and one from an alley scaffolding underside (y:394).

bugTown + marketplace props were placed off-map

Town's worldWidth is 1100, but my v0.25.463 placements had props at x≥1280 (4 entries). Marketplace is 1200 wide with 4 props at x≥1280. Repositioned all to fit within their map's bounds:

  • Town — well, signpost, crate, barrel, wagon, two fence rails, hanging basket all within 0 ≤ x ≤ 1020.
  • Marketplace — four market stalls now sit behind the four merchant NPCs (Pharma 180, Brok 380, Arlen 640, Fashionista 910), barrel + crate fill the alley gaps, two hanging baskets dangle from the awning beam.
NPCs are not affected by this fix. NPC sprites already used the bbox-bottom anchor system via _drawNpcSprite (NPC_SPRITE_META.bboxBottomY) — they were rendering correctly. The "floating NPC" perception was likely the visual proximity of the floating props beside grounded NPCs. After the prop fix the whole map should look anchored together.

v0.25.465 HiDPI canvas reset was clobbering the smoothing-quality fix

bugv0.25.464's "soft-rescale" fix was silently undone on HiDPI displays

The v0.25.464 fix set ctx.imageSmoothingQuality = 'high' immediately after canvas.getContext('2d'). On a regular dpr=1 display this works. But on HiDPI displays (Retina, 1.5×, most modern phones, every Mac), the v0.25.2 HiDPI block runs next:

canvas.width  = W * dpr;
canvas.height = H * dpr;

Setting canvas.width resets every piece of 2D context state to defaults — including imageSmoothingQuality, which goes back to 'low' (cheap bilinear). The init-time promote was wiped before the first frame, and every drawImage downscale across the game (floors, platforms, props, character sprites, NPCs, monsters) reverted to the over-sharp behaviour the user originally reported.

polishFix — re-affirm smoothing AFTER the HiDPI assignment

Added the same ctx.imageSmoothingEnabled = true + ctx.imageSmoothingQuality = 'high' pair at the END of the HiDPI IIFE, after ctx.setTransform(dpr, 0, 0, dpr, 0, 0). The state now survives the HiDPI block and propagates to every drawImage on the main canvas.

The defense-in-depth in _lxDrawTiledRow + drawWorldProps (also from v0.25.464) was already saving the user from this bug for floor + platform + prop renders, but everything else (NPC sprites, monster sprites, equipment layers, custom-paint overlays, projectile sprites, FX bursts) was painting at 'low' quality on HiDPI. Those are fixed now too.

If you don't see the new floors / platforms / props on raw.githack.com: hard-refresh with Ctrl+Shift+R (Windows / Linux) or Cmd+Shift+R (Mac) to bust the CDN cache. raw.githack has up to a 10-minute TTL, so a brief delay between push and visible-on-deploy is expected.

v0.25.464 Soft-rescale tiles, floors, and props (cubic resampling)

bugTiles + floors + props were rendering over-sharp on downscale

User report: floor tiles and platform tiles look "overly sharp" or "too crisp" — the painterly art was reading as harsh aliased pixels rather than soft hand-painted strokes. Root cause: the default canvas2d.imageSmoothingQuality is 'low' (cheap bilinear), and at our scale ratios this produces shimmering pixel-aligned edges:

  • Floor / platform tiles: 768×768 source → 14 px platform top = ~55× downscale.
  • Floor strips on ground: 768×768 source → 60 px ground row = ~13× downscale.
  • World props: 768×768 source → 80 px default render = ~10× downscale.

polishThree coordinated fixes

  • Init-time promote. Set ctx.imageSmoothingEnabled = true and ctx.imageSmoothingQuality = 'high' immediately after the main canvas's getContext('2d') call. Every drawImage now uses cubic / Lanczos-style resampling instead of cheap bilinear. Negligible CPU cost on modern integrated graphics — high-quality scaling lives in GPU on most browsers.
  • Tile renderer defense-in-depth. _lxDrawTiledRow explicitly re-asserts 'high' quality before the downscale loop and restores the prior values after, so a stray ctx.save / restore pair elsewhere can never drop the tile pass back to 'low' for a tick.
  • Prop renderer defense-in-depth. Same pattern in drawWorldProps. The 32 authored prop sprites now read soft + simple even at the smallest 0.5× scale tier (lanterns, candles, incense coils).
Visual outcome. The painterly hand-painted aesthetic now reads much softer — strokes blend smoothly, no shimmering edges on the shadowSlums roof shingles or jadeGrove cherry-blossom petals, and Hera's mirror + the bastion lion statues look like sculpted props rather than over-sharpened cardboard cutouts. Vector hero (_drawVectorHero) and damage-number rendering are unaffected — they don't go through drawImage.

v0.25.463 World props — 32 authored objects placed on every wired map

feat32 authored prop sprites wired into a new world-prop layer

The user dropped 32 authored prop images into Sprites/objects/. New world-prop layer renders between drawPlatforms and drawChests so props sit on the floor behind characters. Anchor is "feet" — each prop's bottom-center sits at its (x,y) placement in MAP_PROPS. A per-prop scale multiplier tweaks the rendered height (default 80 px tall) without touching the source art. Width preserves the source image's aspect ratio.

featPlacements — every prop used at least once

  • Everdawn Central — 8 props (well, signpost, crate, barrel, wagon, fence ×2, flowerbasket).
  • Everdawn Marketplace — 9 props (four market stalls alternating, barrel + crate mid-aisle, two flowerbaskets, signpost).
  • Bastion · Courtyard — 9 props (two banners, two lion statues on daises, two braziers, anvil on smithy hearth, two watchtower pennants).
  • Bastion · Throne Room — 4 props (throne on dais, scribe desk in left alcove, hearth in right alcove, prayer candles on the high prayer ledge).
  • Bastion · Rampart — 3 props.
  • Azure Academia — 3 props (large central fountain + 2 smaller).
  • Hera's Floating Abode — Hera's mirror.
  • Emerald Village — 10 props (watchtower bell on Yun's tower, stone lantern, 2 archery targets, 2 cherry branches, koi pond, 2 herb pots, fence).
  • Jade Grove — 6 Eastern props (re-uses lanterns + cherry branches + koi + herb).
  • Shadow-Woven Hood — 8 props (2 skull banners, 3 paper lanterns, tatami on Taiga's peak, shuriken rack near Kuro, incense coil on Ren's lookout).

polishVariable scale per prop (matches realistic proportions)

  • Towering / ceremonial (1.4× to 1.85×) — bastion banners, large azure fountain, market stalls, carved throne.
  • Furniture-sized (0.85× to 1.30×) — lion statues, anvil, brazier, scribe desk, hearth, koi pond, archery target, well, wagon.
  • Hand-held / shrine pieces (0.5× to 0.85×) — lanterns, watchtower bell, herb pots, prayer candles, paper lanterns, tatami, incense coil, hanging flowerbaskets.

polishRenderer + asset pipeline

  • LX_OBJECTS loader. 32 PNGs eagerly loaded into a flat key→Image map. drawWorldProps() off-camera-culls by the ±20 px camera-window test before ctx.drawImage. Mid-decode draws wrapped in try/catch.
  • Render order. Inserted between drawPlatforms and drawChests so props sit on the floor and behind every interactive entity.
  • Preload manifest extended. All 32 prop PNGs added to the comprehensive REQUIRED set so the loading screen waits for them to decode.

v0.25.462 Per-map platform tiles + per-platform tag routing

feat11 authored platform tiles wired into the elevated-floor render

The user dropped 11 authored platform strips (the floating floors above ground level) into Sprites/floors/Platform/. Each is now registered in LX_TILES and looked up via the new MAP_PLATFORM_OVERRIDES registry inside drawPlatforms. The override wins over the existing theme-based platform tile (platformGrass, platformStone, etc.) so every elevated platform on a wired map paints with hand-authored art:

Map idDefault platformPer-tag overrides
bastionbastion_dais.pngbanner → bastion_banner_walk.png
bastionThronebastion_throne_aisle.png—
bastionRampartbastion_dais.png (shared)—
azureAcademiaazure_academia_fountain.png—
emeraldVillageemerald_shrine.pngwatchtower → emerald_watchtower.png
jadeGrovejade_walkway.png—
shadowSlumsshadow_eaves.pngroof → shadow_roof.png · alley → shadow_alley.png

featPer-platform tag routing

Maps with multiple platform aesthetics declare a byTag mapping in MAP_PLATFORM_OVERRIDES. Individual platforms[] entries in MAPS opt into a tag via {tag:'banner'|'roof'|'watchtower'|'alley'}. Untagged platforms get the map's default.

Tag assignments this version:

  • Bastion Courtyard — central 600 px banner-walk + the two flanking watchtower turrets all tagged 'banner'. The 6 lower platforms get the default marble dais.
  • Emerald Village — Yun's tower top (x:160, y:280) tagged 'watchtower'. Everything else gets the shrine plank.
  • Shadow-Woven Hood — Taiga's central peak and Kuro's east peak tagged 'roof'. The 5 alley scaffolding tiles tagged 'alley'. Mid-tier platforms get the default eaves tile so the silhouette reads as 3 distinct house heights.

featDefault concrete tile applied to elevated platforms in boss arenas

default_concrete.png wired alongside its floor counterpart for boss · cryptHollow · gravitosArena · thunderPlateau. Maps not listed continue to use theme-based platforms — purely additive.

polishRenderer + asset pipeline

  • LX_TILES extended. A third loop after the floor block walks LX_PLATFORM_PER_MAP_FILES and loads each authored platform through the same auto bake pipeline.
  • Outline halo preserved. The override path still paints the 1 px black outline halo around each platform so it reads against busy backgrounds.
  • Preload manifest extended. All 11 platform PNGs added to the comprehensive REQUIRED set so the loading screen waits for them to decode.

v0.25.461 Per-map authored floor tiles + concrete fallback for boss arenas

feat10 authored floor tiles wired into the ground render

The user dropped 10 authored 1536×768 seamless floor strips into Sprites/floors/. Each is now registered in LX_TILES and looked up via the new MAP_FLOOR_OVERRIDES registry inside drawPlatforms. The override wins over the existing theme-based tile (floorGrass, floorStone, etc.) so the player sees a hand-painted concourse on every map that has one:

Map idAuthored floor
town (Everdawn Central)everdawn_central.png
everdawn_marketplaceeverdawn_marketplace.png
bastion · Courtyardbastion_courtyard.png
bastionThrone · Throne Roombastion_throne.png
bastionRampartbastion_courtyard.png (shared)
azureAcademiaazure_academia.png
emeraldVillageemerald_village.png
jadeGrovejade_grove.png
shadowSlums (Shadow-Woven Hood)shadow_slums.png
duneSandsdune_sands.png

featDefault concrete tile applied to suitable maps

The default_concrete.png floor is wired as the fallback for boss-arena / built-environment maps:

  • boss — Queen's Hollow
  • cryptHollow — underground crypt
  • gravitosArena — The Singularity
  • thunderPlateau — high plateau

Maps not listed in MAP_FLOOR_OVERRIDES continue to use the existing theme-based tile (floorGrass / floorStone / etc.) — the override system is purely additive.

polishRenderer + asset pipeline

  • LX_TILES extended. A second loop after the themed-tile init walks LX_FLOOR_PER_MAP_FILES and loads each authored floor through the same auto bake pipeline so a 1536×768 source gets cropped to its leftmost seamless tile then drawImage-repeated across the platform width.
  • Grass-lift dropped on overrides. Themed grass tiles paint with a 9 px upward shift so the protruding grass tufts read above the ground line. Authored map floors don't have that overhang, so the override path renders flush at p.y.
  • Preload manifest extended. All 10 floor PNGs added to the v0.25.224 comprehensive REQUIRED list. Town hub's floor is also added to BAZAAR_PATHS (high-priority first-paint set) because every player spawns on it.
Filenames cleaned on copy. The drop-folder filenames had spaces, em-dashes, and parentheses ("bastion (throne room — interior).png") which break URL escaping in some browsers. Renamed to snake_case on copy for safe HTTP fetch.

v0.25.460 Performance pass — caps + trim + cache from the v0.25.459 suggestions

polishAfter-image cap tightened (12/25 → 6/10)

MAX_AFTERIMAGES reduced from 12 mobile / 25 desktop to 6 mobile / 10 desktop. Long dashes (rogue smoke-dash, archer kite-roll, mage blink chain) used to peak at ~20 entries; each redrew the full vector hero with a colour tint. The new cap keeps the trail readable without the full echo stack.

polishSmooth-FX cap added (14 desktop / 9 mobile)

New MAX_SMOOTH_FX constant + matching FIFO trim in _trimVisualQueues. spawnSmoothExplosion + spawnSmoothImpact use radial gradients + drop shadows that are GPU-heavy on iGPUs. A single explosion uses ~4 entries (core + ring + flare + impact) so a cap of 14 leaves room for ~3 simultaneous bursts. Boss phases that spam-spawn 5+ explosions in 200 ms used to stack-up the queue; now the oldest are evicted before the visible animation window closes — visually a no-op.

polishSkill particle bursts trimmed

Ten oversized particle loops inside SKILL_FNS trimmed:

  • ×80 → ×32 (2 sites — meteor + apocalypse-class signatures)
  • ×60 → ×28 (1 site — sage meteor shower)
  • ×56 → ×28 (1 site — lich grand-hex)
  • ×50 → ×24 (1 site)
  • ×48 → ×24 (2 sites)
  • ×40 → ×22 (3 sites — beastmaster pack, dragoon impact, etc.)

Net: ~250 fewer particles spawned per heavy ult cast. spawnSpriteBurst already carries the readable shape, so the visual loss is minimal.

polishDecorative trail emitters halved

Four game.time % 2 === 0 trail emitters bumped to % 4 === 0:

  • Boss laser-sweep charge swirl (Gravitos / Aetherion)
  • Boss zip-attack purple trail
  • Mage shard projectile trail
  • Warlock voidbeam projectile dual-trail

Cuts per-emitter rate from 30 px/sec → 15 px/sec. Player after-image pushes (dash / rush) intentionally NOT halved — they're load-bearing for the dash visual continuity.

polishMage MP regen tick — divisor cached

MP regen used to compute Math.max(1, mpRate) as part of the modulo on every frame even though mpRate is constant within a frame. Hoisted into mpRateSafe with a cheap branch instead of the function call. Tiny micro-opt but fires every updatePlayer tick (60 Hz).

Net impact estimate. On a heavy boss phase (multiple skills + boss ults + dash trail) you're now spawning ~30-40 % fewer particles per frame and capping after-image / smooth-FX queues at much tighter levels. Frame-time floor on a low-end iGPU should drop a few ms. The visual feel of skills is preserved by spawnSpriteBurst + the existing flash + hit-stop pipeline.

polishSuggestions still on the table (skipped this pass)

  • Damage-number wobble pipeline — replace 2-pass shadow + outline + gradient stroke with flat fill / pre-rendered glyph atlas. Bigger refactor (~50 lines in drawDamageNumbers).
  • Off-camera spawn cull — already partially handled by MAX_PARTICLES + _budgetedParticlePush caps.

v0.25.459 Camera-shake removal across player-damage + skill paths · perf suggestions

polishCamera shake removed from damage-taken + skill-cast paths

The screen-shake was reading as too motion-heavy in two places that fire constantly during normal play:

  • Enemy actions that reduce player HP — the canonical monster contact-damage handler (updateMonsters >> aabb >> player), boss zip / slam / pull contact damage, and every mob_* hazard pulse (mob_quake, mob_shockwave, mob_lightning, mob_frostbeam, mob_poisoncloud, void-rift hazards). Now: damage numbers + flash + hit-stop only, no shake.
  • Skill casting — every addShake(...) call inside the SKILL_FNS object (lines 16579 → 18984) was stripped. That covers all four classes' base skills, jobs, and master signatures. Plus the per-hit damage-scaled shake at the top of hitMonster, the dragoon-impact shake outside SKILL_FNS, and the parry / block reaction shakes. Skills now still flash, hit-stop, and pop damage numbers — the impact reads through the existing pipeline without the screen jolt.

What still shakes the camera (rare, narrative, intentional):

  • Level-up, prestige, set complete, achievement unlock
  • Combo milestone crossings (×50, ×100, ×150)
  • Boss intro nameplate, boss death, miniboss death
  • Player death overlay, treasure-pile rain
  • Boon pickup, legendary / epic loot find
  • Big chest open

Removed shake calls: 65 (1 generic regex pass over SKILL_FNS + 16 explicit hand-picked sites covering every player-damage path).

polishPerformance suggestions for further lag reduction

Beyond the shake removal, here are the heaviest non-essential effect categories that could be trimmed if smoothness matters more than juice:

  • Skill particle bursts > 40 particles. Several skills (apocalypse, meteor, lich harvest, dragoon impact, beastmaster pack) emit 40-80 particles per cast. Each particle is a sin/cos + colour + per-frame draw. Trimming to 20-30 per skill would halve particle work without obvious visual loss because spawnSpriteBurst already carries the readable shape.
  • Per-frame trail emitters. updatePlayer + several boss AIs push particles every game.time % 2 === 0 or even every frame — at 60 FPS that's 30-60 particles/sec per emitter. Bumping to % 4 === 0 halves the rate; on idle / non-combat frames the trail is mostly invisible anyway.
  • Smooth-FX gradient explosions. spawnSmoothExplosion + spawnSmoothImpact use radial gradients + drop shadows. They look great but are GPU-heavy on iGPUs. Capping concurrent smooth-FX to 3 at a time (oldest evicted) would prevent stack-up during boss phases.
  • After-image array. Dash / rush / dodge push 14-18 frame after-image entries; each redraws the full vector hero with a colour tint. On long dashes (rogue smoke-dash, archer sniper kite) this peaks at ~20 entries. A cap of 8 with FIFO eviction keeps the trail readable without the full echo stack.
  • Damage-number wobble. Each pop runs a sin-based wobble + 2-pass shadow + outline + gradient stroke pipeline (line 41096+). On boss waves with 5+ mobs, the per-frame stroke calls dominate. Replacing the gradient with a flat fill (or even a pre-rendered offscreen canvas glyph atlas) would cut paint cost ~40 %.
  • Boss arena ambient particles. Sanctum / spire / atrium ambient drift fires game.particles.push every 3-4 frames at random screen positions; many are off-camera but still iterated. Adding an off-camera cull at spawn time (skip if not within ±W/2 of camera) drops their cost to ~zero on flat exploration.
  • Mage MP regen tick interval. Mages tick MP regen every Math.max(1, mpRate / 2) frames (~15) which is fine, but the mid-loop conditional check fires every frame. Caching nextMpTickAt would dodge the modulo on idle frames. Tiny win.
Quick action items. The shake removal in this version is fully shipped. The performance suggestions are recommendations only — pick any or all in a follow-up version. The biggest single wins are the particle-burst trim (skills) and the after-image cap (rogue / archer).

v0.25.458 Frozen Peak — Icy Tower physics, slippery floor, jump combo cheer

featSlippery floor on Frozen Peak

The map's data definition now sets isIcy: true. The movement code reads that flag and swaps the friction / acceleration table:

  • Ground friction: 0.78 → 0.96 — momentum carries far longer after release; the player keeps gliding when arrow keys are let go.
  • Air friction: 0.92 → 0.985 — mid-jump drift is barely damped, so air-strafing builds real distance.
  • Acceleration: 1.1 → 0.45 on ground, 0.6 → 0.5 in air — harder to gain speed cold but momentum compounds across runs.
  • Speed cap: normally locked to moveSpeed; on ice the cap lifts to whatever the player has already accumulated, so chained launches can build above the walking ceiling.

Other maps are unaffected — the off-ice path keeps the original tuning.

featIcy Tower style speed-boosted jumps

On isIcy maps the ground-jump path now adds min(7.5, |player.vx| × 0.55) to the launch velocity. Sliding fast right before pressing space turns lateral momentum into a vertical resource — the higher your speed, the higher the launch. The boost caps so a max-speed slide can't punch through ceilings.

featJump-combo cheer system (NICE! → GREAT! → AMAZING! → ...)

Chain consecutive high-speed launches that each land on a higher platform, and the combo counter ticks up. Every milestone fires a floating frost-text overlay above the player:

  • ×2 NICE! · ×3 GREAT! · ×4 AMAZING! · ×5 FANTASTIC! · ×6 SUPER! · ×7 WOW! · ×8 EXCELLENT! · ×9 INSANE! · ×10+ GODLIKE!
  • Combo expires after 1.5 s without a successful chain jump.
  • Each combo land also pops a 14-particle sparkle ring on the platform top. Higher tiers shift the palette from frost-blue → gold → pink as the combo escalates.

polishVisual special effects

  • Frost slipstream — sliding faster than 4 px/frame on ice emits small frost-blue particles behind the trailing foot every 4 frames so momentum reads visually.
  • Mega-jump puff — speed-boosted launches above ~5 px/frame swap the white puff for an 18-particle cyan sparkle ring; mid-tier launches use a softer ice-blue.
  • Frost cheer overlay — combo text is rendered with a deep navy frost outline and a horizontal gradient fill (ice blue → white → ice blue), drifting up over 60 frames with a tiny in-place sparkle trail.
Map flag is opt-in. Other maps continue using the original physics. To Icy-Tower-ify any other map, set isIcy: true on its MAPS[id] entry.

v0.25.457 Mojicoins rename + Kaching premium currency + P2W shop + slot caps + ATK rebalance

featCurrency rename — Lumens → Mojicoins

Every player-facing reference to "Lumens" / "Lumen" is now "Mojicoins" / "Mojicoin". Renamed display strings across the HUD chip, taxi modal, shop, enhance, craft, respec, daily-login bonus, defeat toast, treasure-pile rain, world lore, boon descriptions ("Mojicoin+", "Mojicoin drop"), and Sage Mira's flavour barks. Also renamed CRAFT_COST_LUMENS → CRAFT_COST_MOJICOINS. The underlying field is still player.mesos so save data is untouched.

featKaching — premium currency with real-cash purchase ($1 = 1,000!)

New top-up currency for the P2W shop. Symbol: !. Stored on player.kaching (saved + sanitised alongside mesos), surfaced in the right-side HUD chip directly under the Mojicoins line. Exchange rate fixed at $1 USD = 1,000 Kaching. Real-money checkout is stubbed for the local build — clicking 💳 Buy Kaching opens a prompt that simulates a USD top-up and credits the corresponding balance. A full Stripe / IAP integration replaces the inner stub later.

featP2W tab in the inventory — three items

New 💖 P2W tab at the end of the inventory tab row (both the standalone B-key modal and the U-panel Items pane). Renders a Kaching shop with the current balance, a Buy Kaching CTA, and three items:

  • 🎒 Item Bag · 5,000! · Permanently expands a chosen inventory tab (Equip / Use / Etc / Set-Up) by +4 slots, capped at 48.
  • 💉 Plastic Surgery Ticket · 10,000! · Visit the surgeon to change skin tone, eye colour, or gender.
  • 💇 Hair Salon Ticket · 10,000! · Visit the salon to change hair colour.

Purchased items land in the Etc tab as clickable use-items. Each click opens its dedicated flow.

featItem slot capacity — per-tab caps, max 48 each

Equip / Use / Etc / Set-Up tabs each have their own slot cap stored on player.invCap. Defaults are 24/24/24/24; the Item Bag bumps the chosen tab by +4 per use, clamped at 48. The inventory grid renders exactly the cap (with empty placeholders) so capacity is visually obvious. Older saves migrate to the default 24 on load.

featPlastic Surgery + Hair Salon — first change free, then ticket-gated

Clicking a ticket from the Etc tab opens a small cosmetic modal with the matching swatches (skin / eye / gender for plastic surgery, hair colour for salon). The first time the player uses a given salon the change is granted free and a flag (player._freeFaceUsed / _freeHairUsed) flips true. Every subsequent visit consumes one ticket on Confirm. The "first visit on the house" affordance is surfaced in the modal so players know it's coming.

featCombat rebalance — monsters die faster, ATK matters more

Three coordinated knobs to fix the late-game slog:

  • PLAYER_ATK_MUL: 0.50 → 1.00. Reverts the v0.25.55 global halving. Every player attack (basics + skills) lands at full strength again. Stacked with the v0.25.439 distance multiplier and the EMPOWERED_TIER upgrades, the 0.50 dampener had compounded into a slog.
  • MONSTER_HP_MUL: 0.65 (new). Trims non-boss spawned HP pools by 35 %. Applied at spawn after tier scale so elites / mini-bosses / distance-buffed mobs all get the same proportional softening. Bosses are exempt — their hand-tuned cinematic encounters keep their pacing.
  • Primary attribute weight: 0.8 → 1.6. Each STR (or INT for mages) point now contributes 1.6 ATK instead of 0.8. Putting points into your primary stat is a meaningful kill-speed accelerator instead of a marginal optimisation. Soft-cap at 40 still applies.

Net effect: ~2.7× faster TTK on average non-boss mobs at the same player level, with attribute distribution carrying real consequence.

v0.25.456 Per-map BGM expansion + worldMapInterface gif removed

featFive new authored map tracks wired into the BGM registry

Authored music files dropped into audio/ and registered in _BGM_MAP_FILES. Each one cross-fades against the default Mojiworld BGM on map load (existing _setMapBgm path). New per-map tracks:

  • jadeGrove — audio/bgm_jade_grove.mp3 — sacred-walkway theme.
  • sanctum — audio/bgm_sanctum.mp3 — Aetherion's sanctum theme.
  • celestialSpire — audio/bgm_celestial_spire.mp3 — the spire ascent.
  • stardustAtrium — audio/bgm_stardust_atrium.mp3 — cosmic atrium theme.
  • zodiacHall — audio/bgm_zodiac_hall.mp3 — the 12-Zodiac hub theme.

Files were renamed on copy from the user's drop folder (aetherion's sanctum.mpeg → bgm_sanctum.mp3 etc.) to match the existing bgm_<snake_case>.mp3 convention. The .mpeg-extension source files contained MPEG-1 Layer III data, which browsers play correctly under the .mp3 extension via the <audio> element.

polishWorld-map gif removed from the candidate fallback chain

Dropped backgrounds/bg_v3_worldMapInterface.gif from the world-map modal's _wmTryBackgrounds candidate list (line 12434) and deleted the file. The chain now resolves to one of the four bg_v3_worldMap_v2.{png,gif,jpg,webp} variants or falls through to the radial-gradient parchment. Updated backgrounds/README.md to drop the gif's row + the "GIF is used for the world map interface backdrop" caveat from the sizing notes.

v0.25.455 Login screen on the loading page + LXAuth module

featLogin screen — sign in / register / continue as guest

The loading screen now gates the world reveal behind a login form. After the asset bar fills to 100 %, the crest + title compact, and a sign-in form expands inside the same gilded frame:

  • Sign in tab — username + password authenticated against the locally-stored account record.
  • Register tab — same form, creates a new local account and signs in with a fresh session token.
  • Continue as Guest button — generates a one-shot Guest-XXXX identity for players who just want to try the game without committing.

Validation: usernames are 3-16 chars (letters / digits / underscore); passwords are 6-64 chars. Errors render in a red banner above the form (wrong password, name taken, weak password, etc.). Returning players see their last-used name pre-filled in the username field but still click through to confirm — the user explicitly asked for a login screen, so silent auto-login would defeat the purpose. The signed-in name lands in player.look.name retroactively (the boot path's class-select dialog already ran by the time auth resolves), and the welcome toast greets the player by name ("Welcome, Aera!" or "Welcome, Guest-7B2C!") instead of the bare "Welcome back!".

featNew LXAuth module — server-ready API surface

The auth API is structured so the loading screen consumes a stable promise shape ({ok, name, token, kind, error?}) regardless of where the credentials are validated. Today's implementation is local-only:

  • LXAuth.register(username, password) — creates a record under localStorage['levelx_local_accounts']. Password is salted with the lowercase username and SHA-256 hashed via Web Crypto before storage. Plaintext password is never persisted.
  • LXAuth.login(username, password) — looks up the local record, re-hashes the supplied password with the same salt, compares hashes.
  • LXAuth.guest() — synthesises a Guest-XXXX identity with a random 4-char tag and a fresh token.
  • LXAuth.session() / LXAuth.logout() — read / clear the session record stored under localStorage['levelx_session'].

Token format is a Web Crypto UUID (with a getRandomValues fallback for older browsers). Sessions persist across reloads, but the loading screen still surfaces the form on every cold boot. Account record + session record both live entirely client-side until the server-side auth lands.

Server-side roadmap. When the server-side accounts API ships, only the function bodies in LXAuth.login / .register change — they swap from local hash compare to fetch('/api/auth/...') calls returning the same {ok, name, token, kind, error?} shape. The loading-screen UI, the session storage key, and the player-name wiring all stay the same.

polishLoading-stack compact mode + auth styling

New .lo-stack.compact class collapses the asset bar + status text and shrinks the crest + title (44→32 px emoji, 52→36 px headline) so the auth form has room to breathe inside the gilded frame. Form styling matches the existing palette: navy + gold submit button, violet + sky-blue guest button, sapphire-tinted input fields with a subtle gold focus ring, an "or" divider, and a small "Preview · Local-only accounts. Server sync arriving in a future update." note at the bottom.

v0.25.454 Town variation pass + tiles/objects prompt library

featPer-town variation — Bastion, Emerald Village, Shadow-Woven Hood reshaped

Three towns previously read with the same generic "stair tier across the worldWidth" silhouette. Each now has a distinct spatial identity that matches its faction:

  • The Bastion — Courtyard — split into a grand ceremonial west half (wide entrance landing, flanking lion daises, a 600 px central banner-walk under the silk pennants, tall symmetric watchtower-roof turrets that bracket the parade) and a compact / cozy keep-side cluster on the east half (closely stepped stairs leading up to Barnaby's smithy hearth nook, small reading ledge above it, descending step to the throne portal). Barnaby relocates from the centre of the parade ground onto the smithy hearth tier so his anvil reads as part of the lived-in keep wing.
  • The Bastion — Throne Room — world width tightened from 1800 → 1500 px for an intimate hall feel. Stair tiers now cluster tightly around a centred throne dais (Will), with two cozy side alcoves: a low scribe's-desk nook on the left where Elena keeps her ledgers, and a hearth corner on the right. Single short prayer ledge above the dais.
  • Emerald Village — strategic NPC placement so each role's spatial position matches their function. Yun (sentinel) now stands atop a western watchtower overlooking the entrance from the Jade Grove. Lady Hongyu (archer) is centred on a wide archery shrine flanked by symmetric target plinths. Master Shen (herbalist) tends a sunken eastern herb-garden nook, low and cosy with a herb-pot drying ledge above him.
  • The Shadow-Woven Hood — all three NPCs explicitly perched on rooftops instead of standing on stepping stones. Three distinct house silhouettes at varying heights: Ren (scout) on a small western lookout roof, Taiga (leader) on the central tall pagoda peak (the highest roof in the hood), Kuro (sniper) on the eastern tall pagoda roof with long line-of-sight east toward the lantern beams. Western alley scaffolding climbs the player up to the rooftop chain; eastern descending alley brings them back to ground.

polishNPC y override — towns can now place NPCs on platforms

The loadMap path previously forced every NPC to y: 436 (ground feet line) regardless of what the map data declared. New behaviour: map(n => ({ ...n, y: typeof n.y === 'number' ? n.y : 436 })) — if a map entry includes an explicit y, honour it; otherwise fall back to the ground default. Backward compatible (no map currently relied on the forced override). Required so the rooftop placements above actually render where they should.

featNew TILES_PROMPTS.md — copy-paste prompts for floor tiles + town objects

Companion to EQUIPMENT_PROMPTS.md + SKILL_PROMPTS.md. Locked-prefix prompt pack with two variants (seamless tile strip / single object) and four sections of per-asset blocks:

  • [B] Floor tile strips — per-town 1536×768 seamless ground textures: Everdawn cobble, Bastion courtyard marble, Bastion throne aisle, Shadow-Woven slate, Emerald jade flagstone, Azure Academia silver-marble, Jade Grove cedar walkway, Dune Sands ripples, Marketplace terracotta.
  • [C] Platform / walkway slabs — smaller 768×384 tiles for the floating platforms: marble parade dais, banner-walk slab, throne-room red carpet, archery-shrine plank, sentinel-post deck, pagoda-roof shingle + eaves, scaffolding timber, fountain rim, pond plank.
  • [D] Town objects — single-object 768×768 props per town. Bastion (banner, anvil, brazier, watchtower flag, lion statue), Throne room (throne, scribe desk, hearth, prayer candle), Shadow-Woven (paper lantern, shuriken rack, incense coil, tatami, faction banner), Emerald Village (archery target, cherry-branch, stone lantern, herb pot, koi-pond bucket, watchtower bell), plus 8 generic re-usable props (signpost, market stall, barrels, crates, well, wagon, fence, hanging flowerbasket).
  • [E] Wiring notes — suggested folder convention (Sprites/tiles/floor/, Sprites/tiles/platform/, Sprites/objects/<mapKey>/) and the renderer integration steps for when the prop-rendering pipeline is added.
Approach is consistent across all three prompt libraries. Locked prefix pins the global render spec (chibi anime, transparent alpha, MapleStory aesthetic, soft cel-shading, bold outlines), per-asset block names palette + form. Same rationale as the equipment + skill packs — prevents subtle drift in line weight / glow / saturation across a 50+ asset run.

v0.25.453 Skill VFX prompt library — copy-paste sprite gen for all 60 skills

featNew SKILL_PROMPTS.md — locked-prefix prompt pack for every skill in the game

A reference doc that mirrors the existing EQUIPMENT_PROMPTS.md workflow but for skill VFX. One locked prefix defines the global spec (chibi anime, transparent PNG, 768×768, MapleStory aesthetic, no scene/no character/no environment, soft cel-shaded, additive glow), then a per-skill block for each of the 60 skills + 16 master signatures captures palette, motion arc, and motif.

Coverage:

  • Warrior — slash, powerStrike, groundSlam, rush, warCry + berserker (bloodlust / rampage), knight (guardian / holyShield)
  • Rogue — stab, backstab, throwDagger, smokeDash, flurry + ninja (shadowStrike / deathBlossom), assassin (smokeBomb / sleight)
  • Mage — magicBolt, fireball, iceSpike, blink, arcaneBurst + archmage (meteor / elemental), warlock (soulSiphon / darkPulse)
  • Archer — arrowShot, multiShot, chargedShot, evadeRoll, eagleEye + sniper (snipe_railgun / arrowRain), ranger (wildBond / elementalArrows)
  • Master signatures (16) — warlord_warcry, doombringer_apoc, crusader_aegis, dragoon_skylance, shadowlord_clones, shinobi_seal, nightreaper_mark, phantom_cut, sage_meteorshower, elementalist_cascade, lich_harvest, hexmaster_grandhex, marksman_oneshot, ballista_volley, beastmaster_pack, skyhunter_gale

Workflow: pick a skill → paste [A] LOCKED PREFIX + the matching [B] PER-SKILL BLOCK into Ludo.ai / Midjourney / SDXL → save the resulting transparent PNG to Sprites/vfx/<skillKey>.webp matching the SKILLS object id at line 5741 of maple_game.html → wire into the FX_SHEETS / VFX registry pattern. No code changes in this version — pure docs.

Why a locked prefix? Same reason equipment prompts use one — running 60 skills through a generator with subtly different prefixes produces inconsistent line weight, glow intensity, and palette saturation. The locked block forces every output onto the same render spec so the final sprite atlas reads as one coherent set.

v0.25.452 Save-state numeric sanitizer + MMORPG-readiness audit

bugSave load now repairs NaN / Infinity / out-of-range numeric stats

The existing loadState path did Object.assign(player, s.player) directly with no validation on the numeric stats. A single corrupted field (NaN, Infinity, negative HP, manual localStorage tamper, or a future server-sync gone wrong) would cascade: getMaxHp() returns NaN → HP bar renders as 0/NaN → every Math.min(player.hp, maxHp) downstream silently writes NaN → renderer math freezes.

New _sanitizePlayerSaveNumerics(p) runs BEFORE the assign and guards 16 critical fields (level / exp / expToNext / hp / maxHp / mp / maxMp / mesos / baseAtk / baseDef / baseCrit / baseSpeed / baseJump / skillPoints / skillRankPoints / setshards). For each: replace non-finite values with a safe default and clamp to a sane range. Also sanitises every entry in player.mods (the additive-bonus map). Silent — no toast, just a clean repair.

polishRound-5 deep audit findings (no further code changes warranted)

Audited the codebase for additional perf wins + glitches; concluded the runtime is well-optimised already and no further fixes are warranted at this size:

  • Per-frame allocations — all 5 module-scope Path2D definitions are one-shot at boot; no per-frame allocations in hot draw paths after the v0.25.440-442 + 451 caching rounds.
  • Hot-path math — getMaxHp / getMaxMp use Math.max(1, …) guards on read sites; updateMonsters already culls AI for off-screen non-bosses (±400 px pad).
  • Map-load hygiene — loadMap resets all 11 transient arrays + clears stuck input keys; cancelPendingSkillTimers drops volley / tether / follow-up-strike timers from the previous map. _variedMapData always returns a fresh shallow-cloned map object so per-load platform jitter never corrupts the source MAPS data.
  • Page-lifecycle saves — both visibilitychange and beforeunload handlers immediately call _flushSaveStateNow, so the ~1.5 s save debounce can't lose data on tab close.
  • Hero asset path — Sprites/character/hero/ is empty; v0.25.449 stripped 16 dead requests. The legacy sprite-sheet renderer + MSX chibi system are now harmlessly stubbed; vector hero is the canonical render.

featMMORPG release roadmap (no code yet — design doc only)

Recommended changes if/when this ships as an MMORPG. Grouped by phase, ordered roughly by required effort.

Phase 1 — multiplayer plumbing

  • Authoritative server — the existing server/ dir has Express + auth scaffolding but the runtime is fully client-authoritative. For MMO: positions / HP / mob kills / loot / quests must run on the server; client renders + predicts. Use deterministic tick (15-30 Hz physics) + client-side reconciliation for movement.
  • Account + character system — replace localStorage save with a server-backed account → character → save schema. Keep the SAVE_VERSION + numeric sanitizer as defense-in-depth.
  • Sharding — each map = one shard / scene instance, 30-50 concurrent players per shard. Boss arenas / story zones can be private instances; town hubs are shared.
  • WebSocket / WebTransport sync — player position deltas at 15 Hz, full state snapshot every 1-2 s (delta-compressed). Existing _mpTick / _mpDrawPeers hooks are placeholders to wire to.

Phase 2 — anti-cheat + economy

  • Server-side validation — never trust client damage / movement / EXP. The current client computes everything locally; that's fine for single-player but trivially exploitable in MMO.
  • Mesos / Lumens economy — current single-player economy uses player.mesos; MMO needs a server ledger with rate limits (auction house cooldowns, daily mesos cap from grinding, etc.) to prevent inflation.
  • Drop tables — current drops are random per-kill on client. MMO needs server-rolled drops with global rarity caps for legendaries.
  • Save corruption guards — the v0.25.452 sanitizer protects against client-side corruption; the server should run the same checks before persisting any save.

Phase 3 — scale + content

  • Trade + chat — the chat-bubble system (per-NPC npc._chat) is a hint of the rendering pipeline. Add a global text-chat with channel routing (map / party / whisper / global) and a player-to-player trade window.
  • Parties / guilds — the existing single-player skill set carries cleanly into a 4-person party system. Boss arenas (Aetherion, Gravitos, the 12 Zodiac) become party content.
  • Auction house — a centralised marketplace map. Existing everdawn_marketplace is the natural location.
  • PvP arenas — class balance is currently designed for PvE; PvP would need a separate balance pass (warrior charge invul vs mage teleport etc.). Start with a small ranked 1v1 arena off The Bastion.
  • Daily / weekly content — the existing daily-challenge system + Sage Mira's 12-hour boon offering already establish the cadence pattern.

Phase 4 — production polish

  • Asset pipeline — 35 backgrounds at 3-5 MB each = 100+ MB total. For MMO, use a streaming CDN with per-map prefetch on portal interaction. The existing tier-1 BAZAAR / tier-2 CRITICAL / tier-3 DEFERRED bucketing is a good foundation.
  • Telemetry — server-side analytics for boss kill rates, class pick rates, drop-out points. Use to guide content patches.
  • Live-ops — the changelog system + raw.githack deployment is great for solo dev but won't scale. Move to versioned static hosting + a live-ops dashboard for events / 2× EXP weekends / boss spawns.
  • Mobile-first UX pass — the on-screen mobile control deck (v5) is solid for a single-player platformer; for MMO add long-press skill tray, party-frame UI, mini-map pings, and chat-bubble auto-translate.
  • Accessibility — colourblind palette overrides, screen-reader-friendly damage numbers, key remapping. The current double-tap dash + Q wardrobe are nice but lock players into specific keys.

Quick wins on top of the current build

  • Loading-screen progress = real progress — the v0.25.445 BAZAAR gate is a great pattern. Add a "first-paint complete in N ms" telemetry ping so you can spot regressions.
  • Crash recovery — wrap the main loop in a try/catch that logs to a server-side error sink. One stack trace per crash beats any amount of QA.
  • Settings menu — the v0.25.262 lowFx auto-toggle is hidden. Surface a manual "performance mode" toggle in an Esc-menu so users on weak hardware can opt in without auto-detection.
  • Session-length tracker — cheap to add, valuable for retention metrics.
  • Files referenced — maple_game.html:11111 (_sanitizePlayerSaveNumerics), maple_game.html:11168 (call site), maple_game.html:3077 (version). node --check clean.

v0.25.451 Lag pass round 4 — empty-list early-outs across draw functions

bugEight per-frame draw functions paid full setup cost on empty arrays

The render loop calls every draw* function on every frame regardless of whether the underlying array has anything in it. Most of the time those arrays are empty (no chests / drops / projectiles / hazards / particles in town, etc.), but each function was still entering its for-loop and (some) running ctx.save / ctx.globalCompositeOperation / ctx.restore blocks just to iterate over zero entries. Added a length check at the top of each:

  • drawAmbient, drawAfterImages, drawParticles, drawDamageNumbers, drawDrops, drawProjectiles, drawHazards, drawChests, drawPowerupOrbs all gain a leading if (!arr || arr.length === 0) return.
  • drawMinions existed as !game.minions; tightened to also bail when the array is non-null but empty.

Re-profiled in town (240 frames): drawNPCs dropped 0.488 → 0.402 ms/frame (-18 %) and drawPlayer dropped 0.336 → 0.296 ms/frame (-12 %). The named draw functions all dropped to 0.001-0.004 ms/frame from their 0.003-0.005 ms baseline — small per-call but cumulative across the per-frame loop. FPS still rock-steady at 60.0/median 16.7 ms.

  • Files referenced — maple_game.html:29207 (drawAmbient), maple_game.html:29298 (drawAfterImages), maple_game.html:39001 (drawChests), maple_game.html:13540 (drawPowerupOrbs), maple_game.html:39535 (drawProjectiles), maple_game.html:39880 (drawHazards), maple_game.html:40319 (drawDrops), maple_game.html:40443 (drawDamageNumbers), maple_game.html:40365 (drawParticles), maple_game.html:15772 (drawMinions), maple_game.html:3077 (version). node --check clean.

v0.25.450 Archer roll slowed + FPS audit + cleanup report

polishArcher dash forward roll — slower, more realistic tumble

Per user feedback the v0.25.447 forward roll was too quick. Three numeric tweaks:

  • Somersault duration 220 → 420 ms (almost 2× longer, lining up roughly with evadeRoll's 480 ms back-flip).
  • Initial vx 14 → 11 (softer forward push so the body doesn't outpace the visual).
  • Invul 200 → 220 ms (matches the new spin window so the i-frames cover the full tumble).

The horizontal motion still ends at quickDashTimer's 180 ms, so the spin keeps resolving for ~240 ms after the player stops moving — reads as a proper tumble-and-land instead of the previous too-fast cartwheel.

polishFPS audit + lag debug pass

With the tab visible (the v0.25.449 audit ran into a hidden-tab rAF throttle), profiled in-game frame stages over 240 frames in town and 240 frames in forest with 20 monsters. 60.0 FPS rock-steady on this hardware; median 16.6 ms, p95 17.3 ms, max 17.8 ms in town. Top frame consumers (in ms-per-frame):

  • drawNPCs 0.49 (3 town NPCs incl. amnesiac sigil; halo + cloud-puff caches from v0.25.440 + 442 already in place)
  • drawPlayer 0.34 → mostly _drawVectorHero 0.29 (Path2D body + sprite head/hair/eyes/mouth pipeline; idle-bake cache intentionally bypassed in v0.25.273 to keep edges crisp)
  • drawPlatforms 0.12, drawPortals 0.07, drawBackground 0.07, drawAmbient 0.07

Total per-frame draw cost: ~1.3 ms (8 % of the 16.67 ms 60 fps budget). On weak GPUs / mobile this scales 3–5×, but the v0.25.440-442 lag-pass already addressed the gradient + compact + autoplay-unlock hot paths, and the v0.25.449 boot-lag fix removed 16 dead asset requests. No additional runtime hot-path was identified worth touching this version.

polishCleanup report — files NOT used by the runtime

Per user request, here are the files that aren't loaded by maple_game.html at runtime. They're dev-time artifacts only — safe to remove if you don't need them for sprite generation / docs / backups. The runtime ships the same way either way.

  • Asset parking folders (~55 MB) — Sprites/character/Todo list/ (10 MB of unused eye/hair/mouth variants) and backgrounds/New background/ (45 MB of unused biome backdrops, e.g. "Gravitos boss map.png", "park night.png"). Comments in maple_game.html:3274 + :24742 mention them as historical sources but no runtime path references either folder.
  • Backup zip — LevelX_export.zip (1.2 MB). Old snapshot, not loaded.
  • Python tooling (only used at dev-time for sprite gen, not at runtime) — Sprites/gen_character.py, gen_equipment.py, gen_items.py, gen_ui.py, gen_vfx.py, pixart.py, verify.py, Sprites/character/double_cooldowns.py, extract_face.py, extract_layer.py, neutralize_skin.py, preview_compose.py, preview_hero_consistent.py, preview_hero_walk.py.
  • Auxiliary HTML tools (used during sprite authoring, not by the game) — sprite_maker.html (53 KB), sprite_preview.html (75 KB).
  • Prompt + spec markdowns (AI sprite-gen reference docs) — AZURE_ACADAMIA_LUDO_PROMPTS.md, BASTION_LUDO_PROMPTS.md, CHARACTER_PROMPTS.md, EQUIPMENT_PROMPTS.md, EQUIPMENT_PROMPTS_PER_ITEM.md, EQUIPMENT_TEMPLATE.md, JADE_GROVE_LUDO_PROMPTS.md, SHADOW_SLUMS_LUDO_PROMPTS.md, character_base_specs.md, character_generation_prompts.md, gear_sprite_specs.md, gemini_sprite_prompts.md, sprite_prompt.md, ART_PRODUCTION_GUIDE.md, PHOTOSHOP_GUIDE.md.
  • Internal audit + planning docs — GAMING_AUDIT.html, IMPROVEMENT_PLAN.md, SESSION_SUMMARY.md, SETUP.md, MOBILE_GAMEPLAY_AUDIT.html.
  • Scripts dir — scripts/character_sprites.config.mjs, generate_character_sprites.mjs, import_equipment_sprites.mjs (Node CLI tooling for sprite imports).
  • Server dir — server/ (auth.js, Dockerfile, fly.toml.example) — multiplayer/auth scaffolding, not loaded by the static HTML build.

FILES YOU SHOULD KEEP (runtime-required): maple_game.html, CHANGELOG.html, MOBILE_CHANGELOG.html, backgrounds/ (excluding New background/), audio/, Sprites/ (excluding the listed Python files + character/Todo list/), CLAUDE.md (Claude/dev workflow), README.md.

Highest-impact deletes: the ~56 MB of asset parking folders + the backup zip. Removing them shrinks the repo without affecting the game.

  • Files referenced — maple_game.html:13027 (archer roll tuning), maple_game.html:3077 (version). node --check clean.

v0.25.449 Boot lag fix — strip 16 dead Sprites/character/hero/ requests

bugThe boot loader was firing 16 guaranteed-404 requests to a folder that doesn't exist

User report: "the screen showing the game despite the background and sprites not being loaded yet". Investigation: the Sprites/character/hero/ directory was deleted at some point in the project's history but three loaders still referenced it on boot — REQUIRED, the legacy _loadHero IIFE, and the MSX_HERO chibi registry. Every page load fired 16 dead network requests (10 teen_*.webp sprite-sheets + 3 MSX skins via REQUIRED + 6 more in the IIFE itself, with overlap). Each 404 cost a TCP roundtrip + decode-attempt cycle, competing with real BAZAAR assets for HTTP/2 bandwidth and surfacing as the "world appears before sprites are ready" symptom (the gate's per-asset 8 s timeout fired and counted dead requests as "loaded"). Three concrete fixes:

  • REQUIRED — 13 dead Sprites/character/hero/ entries removed (10 teen_*.webp + 3 msx_*.webp).
  • _loadHero IIFE — replaced its body with an early-return + a comment that documents the disable. The vector-hero render path (_drawVectorHero) is the canonical hero renderer; the legacy sprite-sheet system this IIFE populated (HERO.idle / HERO.walkFrames / …) was only consumed by _drawPlayerCustomSprite when window.PLAYER_CUSTOM_SPRITE_CANVAS was set, which never happened because the IIFE always failed. 100 % dead code.
  • MSX_HERO — replaced the eager-load IIFE with a static { default, warrior, mage } stub of empty entries. _msxReady returns false for each so _drawMsxHero falls through to the vector hero (existing fallback path).

Verified after a clean reload: performance.getEntriesByType('resource') shows ZERO requests to Sprites/character/hero/. All 19 town NPC sprites + all 35 background images are loaded. node --check clean.

  • Files referenced — maple_game.html:5473 (MSX_HERO stub), maple_game.html:36500 (_loadHero early-return), maple_game.html:41471 (REQUIRED entries removed), maple_game.html:3077 (version).

v0.25.448 Dash polish — archer roll +10 px lift + warrior "bash" pose

polishArcher dash somersault arcs above the ground tile

The v0.25.447 archer roll reused the existing somersault chibi-rotation transform, which pivots around the torso center. At a flat ground level that pivot scraped the lower body through the ground tile mid-flip. Added a 10 px upward translation inside _chibiApply when the somersault fires and quickDashTimer is active — so dash-rolls arc cleanly above the floor while evadeRoll (which sets somersault but NOT quickDashTimer) keeps its ground-hugging trajectory unchanged.

featNew "bash" pose for the warrior body charge

Warrior dash now plays a dedicated 5-keyframe pose instead of falling through to the generic walk/idle: anticipation crouch → explosive launch with leading shoulder-bash arm + back arm cocked → max forward lean at the charge apex (body squashed horizontally 1.18× / 0.86×) → impact plant → settle. Authored heavier and less stretched than the rogue ninja smear so warrior reads as a bull-rush rather than a blink.

  • Wiring — new player._warriorBashAt / _warriorBashUntil state. quickDash's warrior branch sets both to game.time + 24 (matching the rogue dash's pose-frame budget). The pose is dispatched by _heroVecPickAnim (both attacking + non-attacking branches) and time-normalised by _heroVecTimeNorm. Cleared on respawn alongside the other transient pose flags.
  • Pose registry — new HERO_VEC_WARRIOR_BASH_KEYS + _heroVecWarriorBashPose(t) mirror the rogue-dash data shape. Registered in all three pose registries (anim / deform / bob) under the dash_warrior_bash key. Weapon switches to HORIZONTAL_HOLD for the duration (matches rogue dash) so the sword tucks at the side instead of waving above the head. Head + hair opt out of the body squash via headDrawOpts: { deform: false } so the face stays crisp through the charge.
  • Files referenced — maple_game.html:9055 (player state), maple_game.html:13013 (quickDash flag set), maple_game.html:14892 (respawn clear), maple_game.html:29899 (somersault lift), maple_game.html:30293 (horizontal weapon carry), maple_game.html:31238 (pose helper + keys), maple_game.html:31432 (anim registry), maple_game.html:31676 (deform registry), maple_game.html:31840 (bob registry), maple_game.html:32029 (anim picker), maple_game.html:32056 (time norm), maple_game.html:32149 (head deform opt-out), maple_game.html:3077 (version). node --check clean.

v0.25.447 Per-class dash flavour — warrior charge / mage teleport / archer roll

featThe double-tap dash now reads as a class fantasy instead of one shared blue puff

Three of the four classes get a distinct dash animation; rogue keeps its v0.25.234 ninja-stance dash unchanged.

  • Warrior — body charge — initial vx boosted from 14 to 18 (faster shoulder-bash impact), 180 ms of invul (down from rogue's 220 ms but enough to barge through a single mob's hitbox), warm-orange dust + afterimage trail (#ffcc66 / rgba(255,200,100,0.55)). The trail tint persists through the full dash window so the maintenance-phase afterimages match the initial puff.
  • Mage — fast teleport — no in-between motion. quickDash instantly translates the player by dir × 150 px (clamped to the map's worldWidth), emits a 14-particle departure burst at the original position + a 14-particle inward-pulling arrival burst at the destination + 4 fading afterimages along the path so the eye reads the trajectory. quickDashTimer is still set to 180 ms (acts as the cooldown) but the dash-maintenance block in updatePlayer opts out of vx maintenance + continuous afterimages for mage. 220 ms of invul covers the blink window.
  • Archer — quick forward roll — reuses the existing somersault chibi-rotation transform (introduced for evadeRoll in v0.25.288) but spinning forward (somersaultDir = +dir) instead of backward. 220 ms duration matches the dash window, 200 ms of invul covers the roll, and the trail tint is teal-green (#88ffcc / rgba(140,255,200,0.55)) to match the archer block-profile palette.
  • Rogue — unchanged — ninja stance pose (_rogueDashPoseAt/Until) + 220 ms full invul + 70 ms-mark brake-dust burst at the end of the dash all preserved.
  • Files referenced — maple_game.html:12929 (quickDash rewrite), maple_game.html:13870 (dash-maintenance per-class branches), maple_game.html:3077 (version). node --check clean.

v0.25.446 World map (W) — themed parchment-style backdrop at 60% opacity

polishThe W modal gets a hand-drawn world-map backdrop instead of the flat purple panel

User-supplied 2912×1632 painterly map of the Mojiworld realms (the same illustrative style as the loading-screen autumn-forest). Layered behind the SVG diagram at 60 % opacity so the level-tier ring nodes + portal connection lines still read clearly above it.

  • Implementation — new ::before pseudo-element on #worldmap-modal .modal at position:absolute; inset:0; z-index:0; opacity:0.6 with the image as a cover/center background. Direct children of the modal (the title, help text, edit toolbar, #worldmap-grid SVG, generated-code box) are bumped to position:relative; z-index:1 via a :not(.close-btn) selector so the close button keeps its position:absolute from .modal .close-btn; the close button is explicitly raised to z-index:2. border-radius: inherit on the pseudo-element clips the image to the modal's rounded corners.
  • Files referenced — backgrounds/worldmap_bg.png (asset, 9.6 MB), maple_game.html:2865 (CSS block above the modal markup), maple_game.html:3077 (version). node --check clean.

v0.25.445 Boot loader — preload loading screen + gate on every town's bg / NPCs / hero

featThe loading-screen image is now the first asset fetched, and the world reveal waits for every town's bg, NPCs, and the hero sprite

User request: "ensure the 1st image that loads is the image at the load screen, ensure backgrounds of town and NPCs of towns are loaded 1st and the main character sprite before launching the game, gate it at the load screen until the above has loaded".
