  • Loading-screen preload — new <link rel="preload" as="image" href="backgrounds/loading_mojiworld.png" fetchpriority="high"> in <head>. CSS background-image alone only fires its network request once the rule applies to a rendered element — measured at ~99 ms after navigation start. With the preload link the image is queued during HTML parse — measured at ~26 ms after start, a 73 ms head-start so the autumn-forest art is already in the disk cache by the time .lo-bg mounts. No more bare-gradient flash.
  • Town backdrops gating the reveal — all 8 isTown:true map backgrounds (Everdawn Central, Everdawn Marketplace, Azure Academia, Hera's Floating Abode, Shadow-Woven Hood, the Bastion + Rampart + Throne Room, the Jade Grove) added to BAZAAR_PATHS. The reveal now blocks until each is decoded.
  • Town NPC sprites gating the reveal — 12 new faction-hub NPCs (Taiga, Kuro, Ren · Will, Elena, Barnaby · Hera, Auron, Lyra · Lady Hongyu, Master Shen, Yun) added to both REQUIRED and BAZAAR_PATHS. The original 7 bazaar NPCs (Amnesiac, ???, Taxi Uncle, Pharma Sis, Brok, Old Arlen, Fashionista) stay in the gate too. Total 19 town NPCs now gated.
  • Main character sprite — the existing 4-layer hero (LX_HEAD/LX_HAIR/LX_EYES/LX_MOUTH default sprites) was already gated; left untouched.
  • Verified in preview after reload — performance.getEntriesByType('resource') shows loading_mojiworld.png at startMs 26 followed by every other image at startMs 99 (a clear 73 ms gap). Every BAZAAR sprite (hero head/hair/eyes/mouth, every town bg, every town NPC including the URL-encoded "Lady Hongyu.webp" + "Master Shen.png") completed before the loading overlay was removed from the DOM.
  • Files referenced — maple_game.html:11 (preload link), maple_game.html:41265 (REQUIRED town NPCs), maple_game.html:41481 (REQUIRED town bgs), maple_game.html:41506 (BAZAAR_PATHS expansion), maple_game.html:3077 (version). node --check clean.

v0.25.444 World tuning — Everdawn Central rename, faction-hub town behaviour, Zodiac Sanctum on W

featTown hub renamed + 7 faction hubs become town maps + Zodiac Sanctum unlocked at Lv 50

A coordinated world-tuning pass touching map metadata, the W-map, the death-respawn flow, the level-gate policy, and monster-difficulty scaling. All changes ship behind a single version because they're tightly coupled through the new town graph.

  • Recommended-level tuning — per user request: Sunset Coast Lv 1+ (was 2), Tidal Lagoon Lv 4+ (was 11), Gelwater Grotto Lv 10+ (was 4). Other maps' authored levelReqs untouched.
  • Level gate fully removed — tryPortal's "Lv N+ suggested" toast dropped, and the W-map's "🔒 Lv N" cursor lock is now skipped for every map. Walking + fast-travel work regardless of player level. Exception: portals carrying an explicit levelGate field are still hard-blocked (used by the new Zodiac Sanctum unlock).
  • 7 faction hubs become town maps — azureAcademia, azureAbode (Hera), shadowSlums (Shadow-Woven Hood), bastion + bastionRampart + bastionThrone, jadeGrove all gain isTown:true. Existing town gating stops monster spawns + chest spawns automatically; the new ring of safe hubs gives the world more rest stops between combat zones.
  • Acadamia → Academia rename — user-visible "Acadamia" typo (display name, portal labels, every NPC dialog line) replaced with "Academia". 20 occurrences total. Internal map id azureAcademia already had the correct spelling, so save files / sprites / code paths are untouched.
  • Everdawn Bazaar → Everdawn Central — the canonical hub renamed. Map id stays town for save-file compatibility; only the display name + the four portal labels referencing the bazaar change.
  • Marketplace + Zodiac Sanctum on the W-map — everdawn_marketplace pinned at (690, 320), just east of Central. zodiacHall exposed (was filtered out as isZodiacHub) and pinned at (340, 60) in the central-north sky band. The W-map bucket logic now keeps only the canonical 'town' at the hub centre — other isTown maps fall through to their level-tier ring (their wmX/wmY overrides keep their authored positions intact).
  • Zodiac Sanctum unlock — new walking portal gravitosArena → zodiacHall at x:1100, with levelGate:50. tryPortal honours levelGate as a hard block (with a "Sealed — return at Lv 50+" toast) so low-level players can't enter even by walking into the swirl. The Sanctum's existing back-portal to town is untouched; a new zodiacHall → gravitosArena exit is added so the player can return the way they came.
  • Nearest-town respawn — respawnAtTown used to always teleport the player to Everdawn Central; now _nearestTownId runs a BFS over the walking-portal graph from the player's death map and returns the closest isTown:true map. With 8 town maps now (the canonical hub + the 7 new faction hubs + the marketplace) most combat zones have a closer respawn point — dying in the Academia drops you back at the Academia, dying in the Hood drops you at the Hood, etc. Falls back to 'town' if no town is reachable. The respawn toast names the destination so you don't get teleported invisibly.
  • Distance-based monster difficulty — new _townDistance(mapId) caches a BFS distance map from town at first call. spawnMonster applies a 1 + (dist - 1) × 0.10 multiplier (capped at 2.0×) to non-boss monster HP / ATK / EXP / mesos. Boss-arena monsters skip the multiplier so curated boss balance survives. Net effect: the further you are from Everdawn Central, the meaner the creeps, regardless of each map's authored levelReq.
  • Files referenced — maple_game.html:7888 (sunsetBeach.levelReq), maple_game.html:7864 (slimeCave.levelReq), maple_game.html:8198 (tidalLagoon.levelReq), maple_game.html:7451 (Everdawn Central name), maple_game.html:8480 (Azure Academia name + isTown), maple_game.html:8525 (azureAbode isTown), maple_game.html:8353 (shadowSlums isTown), maple_game.html:8398 (bastion isTown), maple_game.html:8429 (bastionThrone isTown), maple_game.html:8458 (bastionRampart isTown), maple_game.html:8559 (jadeGrove isTown), maple_game.html:8729 (Zodiac Sanctum portal + levelGate), maple_game.html:8770 (marketplace wmX/wmY), maple_game.html:8939 (zodiacHall wmX/wmY + back portal), maple_game.html:11899 (W-map hub-filter rewrite), maple_game.html:12534 (W-map level-gate exception), maple_game.html:13560 (spawnMonster distance multiplier), maple_game.html:14640 (_townDistance + _nearestTownId helpers), maple_game.html:14756 (respawnAtTown nearest-town), maple_game.html:21431 (tryPortal levelGate honour), maple_game.html:3077 (version). node --check clean.

v0.25.443 Wardrobe: hairstyle picker + Plastic Surgery (eyes) picker

featHAIR slot now exposes the 7 bundled hairstyles; new EYES slot adds "Plastic Surgery"

The 7 LX_HAIR sprites in Sprites/character/hair/ (Flow, Ponytail, Shaggy, Spiky, Flowy, Ice Cream, Pigeotto) and the 2 LX_EYES sprites in Sprites/character/eyes/ (Default, Fierce) have been wired through the engine since v0.25.270/295/301, but the wardrobe panel was wrongly mounting the paint editor for the HAIR tile and didn't expose any EYES tile at all. The presets were unreachable from the UI.

  • Refactor — buildCharStudioHairStyles + buildCharStudioEyeStyles now take an optional host arg. Without the arg they fall back to the hidden #hair-styles / #eye-styles mounts (preserving the boot-time pre-build inside openCharStudio); with the arg they mount into the live #wardrobe-picker. Each click also calls _refreshWardrobeSlotTile so the grid thumbnail updates instantly.
  • WARDROBE_SLOTS — new { id:'eye', label:'EYES', build: buildCharStudioEyeStyles } entry inserted between HAIR and SKIN. No paintLayer field — eyes are preset-only since the LX_EYES sprites are the canonical art.
  • _wardrobeMountPicker — gains a slot-id dispatch: 'hair' mounts the hair preset list with title "HAIR — pick a style"; 'eye' mounts the eye preset list with title "✦ Plastic Surgery — choose new eyes"; everything else still mounts the paint editor as before.
  • Slot tile thumbnails — new _wardrobeSlotThumb branch for slot === 'eye' mirrors the hair pattern (LX_EYES[eyeId] sprite or '?' fallback). _wardrobeSlotEquipped recognises eye as always equipped (there's no "no eyes" state).
  • Verified end-to-end — in preview: opened wardrobe → clicked HAIR → picked Ponytail → CHAR_STUDIO.hairId became 'ponytail'. Clicked EYES → picked Fierce → CHAR_STUDIO.eyeId became 'fierce'. Clicked "Use in game" → player.lookCustom.hairId = 'ponytail', player.lookCustom.eyeId = 'fierce'.
  • Files referenced — maple_game.html:42129 (hair builder), maple_game.html:42158 (eye builder), maple_game.html:43027 (WARDROBE_SLOTS array), maple_game.html:43093 (slot thumb dispatch), maple_game.html:43194 (equipped check), maple_game.html:43205 (mount-picker dispatch), maple_game.html:3077 (version). node --check clean.

v0.25.442 Lag pass round 3 — sun/moon halo cache

bugCelestial disc halo gradient now baked once per palette

The sun (or moon, on dark maps) halo behind the celestial disc was rebuilding the same 3-stop radial gradient every frame on every outdoor map — only the disc x coord moved with the camera. Two cached 240×240 RGBA canvases (one warm, one cool) selected per-map by isDark; drawImage'd at the disc's screen position with the existing composite='lighter' additive blend. Same visual, zero per-frame allocation.

FPS in town stays a steady 60.1 (median 16.7 ms, max 21.3 ms across 240 frames). The win for this fix specifically shows up on outdoor maps — every forest / dune / sunset map ran the celestial halo every frame.

  • Files referenced — maple_game.html:24735 (cache helper), maple_game.html:24919 (call site in drawBackground), maple_game.html:3077 (version). node --check clean.

v0.25.441 Lag pass round 2 — portal-halo cache + drawAfterImages O(n) compact

bugTwo more per-frame allocations eliminated

  • Portal halo cache — drawPortals was creating a fresh ctx.createRadialGradient + ellipse fill PER PORTAL PER FRAME. With 3-4 portals on most maps × 60 fps, that's 180-240 gradient allocations/sec just for the soft purple glow under portals. Baked once to a 160×160 offscreen canvas; drawImage'd per portal under the existing composite='lighter' so the additive glow is preserved.
  • drawAfterImages O(n²) splice → O(n) write-index — same reverse-loop-with-splice pattern that bit updateAmbient in v0.25.440. Phantom-Step / rogue dash skills can spawn 8-20 afterimages in a burst, and when many expired in the same frame the inner array-shift cost dominated. Single forward-pass write-index compact instead.

FPS in town remains a steady 60.1 (median 16.7 ms, p99 17.8 ms across 240 frames). The portal-halo cache will only show its win on maps with multiple visible portals — town has 3 (Bastion Rampart, Sunset Coast, Marketplace) so even there it eliminates 3 gradients/frame.

  • Files referenced — maple_game.html:28996 (portal halo cache + drawPortals call site), maple_game.html:28995 (drawAfterImages compact), maple_game.html:3077 (version). node --check clean.

v0.25.440 Lag pass — bake per-frame gradients, lazy-preload BGM, O(n) ambient compact

bug"Very very laggy" — first round of targeted lag fixes

Profiled the running game with a per-stage instrumentation harness (samples each draw + update fn over 5 s of gameplay). Top consumers in town were drawNPCs (0.44 ms/frame) and drawPlayer (0.40 ms/frame); profiled 60 FPS rock-steady on desktop but the per-frame allocations + gradient creates were the most likely culprits for the lag the user was seeing on slower hardware. Five concrete fixes:

  • Amnesiac sigil halo cache — the floating zodiac star above the Amnesiac was creating a fresh createRadialGradient + 3-stop fill every frame. Bake one 90×90 RGBA halo bitmap at module scope, blit with drawImage per frame. Eliminates one gradient allocation + one path fill per frame in town. Profile measured drawNPCs 0.44 ms → 0.26 ms (~40% faster).
  • Sky-life cloud-puff cache — drawSkyLifeOverlay ran on every map and was allocating 12 fresh radial gradients (one per drifting cloud) every frame, ~36 addColorStop calls/frame. Bake one 128×128 soft-white puff once, scale-blit per cloud. Aspect-stretch via drawImage args matches the original ellipse shape closely enough; the 0.22 globalAlpha hides any sampling difference.
  • Amnesiac orbit-dust gated by lowFx — the 3 sparkle-arc dots are pure flair on top of the halo + star. Skip the loop when _perfLowFx() is true so phones / slow machines spend nothing on it under load.
  • BGM lazy preload — the v0.25.436 first-gesture handler eagerly instantiated all 5 per-map BGM tracks, forcing a metadata fetch + decoder slot reservation for every track even if the player never visited those maps. Now: track creation stays lazy (only when the map is actually loaded), preload dropped from 'auto' to 'metadata' to defer the body download, and a new _bgmAutoplayUnlocked flag lets a track created AFTER the first gesture self-unlock immediately. Frees ~15-20 MB of preemptive audio downloads on low-bandwidth connections.
  • updateAmbient: O(n²) splice → O(n) write-index — reverse-loop with splice(i, 1) per dead particle (forest leaves, snow, lava embers, etc.) was quadratic in the worst case. Single forward-pass write-index compact (matching the v0.25.247-era updateParticles fix). drawAmbient profile dropped 0.080 → 0.052 ms/frame.

Re-measured FPS in town after all five fixes: median 16.7 ms (60.0 FPS), p95 17.2 ms, max 18.4 ms across 240 frames — perfectly steady. Profiler totals dropped from ~1.1 ms/frame → ~0.7 ms/frame across the top 6 stages.

  • Files referenced — maple_game.html:29087 (amnesiac halo cache), maple_game.html:29413 (drawNPCs amnesiac call), maple_game.html:24743 (cloud-puff cache + drawSkyLifeOverlay), maple_game.html:41462 (BGM lazy preload + autoplay-unlock flag), maple_game.html:28851 (updateAmbient compact), maple_game.html:3077 (version). node --check clean.

v0.25.439 Mojiworld loading-screen autumn-forest backdrop (50% opacity)

polishThemed-biome backdrop replaces the bare gradient on the boot loading screen

User-supplied autumn-forest cell-shaded image (2912×1632 PNG) layered behind the existing starfield + gilded frame at 50% opacity. New .lo-bg child div with position:absolute; inset:0; background:url(...) center/cover no-repeat; opacity:0.5; z-index:0; sits above the dark gradient base, below the ::before starfield + .lo-frame. Pointer-events disabled so the surrounding stack stays clickable.

  • Files referenced — backgrounds/loading_mojiworld.png, maple_game.html:40659 (CSS), maple_game.html:40884 (DOM). node --check clean.

v0.25.438 Hero rig: leg cuff lines removed + hand pivot/ellipse split + Sage Mira renamed

polishBoot-cuff fillRect on each leg removed (was reading as stray pixel bands)

Both legL and legR drew a 6×1.2 dark LIMB_DEEP-coloured fillRect at (-3, 8.2) immediately after the limb shading pass — intended as a "boot top" detail but at chibi display scale it landed as two visible horizontal stripes near the ankles. Removed both. The rounded foot ellipse below already reads as a foot, no boot-cuff replacement needed.

polishHand bones nudged 1 px left, white skin ellipses drawn 1 px right inside the bone frame

Continuation of the v0.25.433 hand-rig tuning. Goal: weapon / paint anchoring (which follows handL / handR bone positions) shifts left so the grip aligns with the left arm's tip path, while the visible white skin ellipse stays at its previous on-screen position.

  • Rig — handL.x: -1 → -2, handR.x: 0 → -1.
  • Render — both handL and handR ellipse + gloss draws shifted from (0, 0) to (1, 0) in bone-local coords (1 px right inside the bone). Net on-screen position of the hand circles ≈ unchanged; weapon-overlay anchor moves 1 px left.

polishSage Mira renamed to "???" (mystery name tag)

Display name in MAPS.town.npcs updated to ???. NPC_SPRITE_FILES + NPC_SPRITE_HEIGHTS keys mirrored so the sprite + 115 px height-override lookups still resolve. Every user-visible string that referred to her by name (the boon-shop dialog button label, the meditation cooldown toast, the "Need 5000 Lumens for Sage's boon" toasts, the empty-boons inventory hint, the boons tutorial card) now reads "???". Internal lore comments + the unrelated master:'sage' mage-progression class name are untouched.

  • Files referenced — maple_game.html:31969 (legL fillRect removed), maple_game.html:31979 (legR fillRect removed), maple_game.html:29873 (HERO_VEC_RIG handL/handR), maple_game.html:31993 (handR ellipse offset), maple_game.html:32024 (handL ellipse offset), maple_game.html:7479 (NPC name), maple_game.html:35726 (sprite key), maple_game.html:35775 (height-override key), maple_game.html:3077 (version). node --check clean.

v0.25.437 Amnesiac re-imported at 50% scale

polishThe Amnesiac re-imported at 312×421 (clean 50% downscale from 640×864) + matching in-game height override

Source PNG was edited in Photoshop to a 50% scale (same aspect, just smaller). The fixed-target-H NPC pipeline (_drawNpcSprite) would otherwise upsample him back to the same 128 px on-screen size as before, so without a code change the visible result wouldn't move. Mirrored the Photoshop resize in NPC_SPRITE_HEIGHTS: 'The Amnesiac': 53 (107 × 0.5 ≈ 53 base × 1.2 NPC_WORLD_SCALE = 64 px target, exactly half the previous 128 px). The Amnesiac now reads as visibly shorter than Sage Mira and the other bazaar NPCs.

  • Files referenced — Sprites/npc/amnesiac.webp (asset), maple_game.html:35766 (NPC_SPRITE_HEIGHTS), maple_game.html:3077 (version). node --check clean.

v0.25.436 Per-map BGM (5 tracks) + Shadow-Woven Hood + bazaar→marketplace portal

featFive new map-specific BGM tracks: Shadow-Woven Hood, Hera's Abode, The Bastion, Emerald Village, Azure Acadamia

Until now the music system was binary: _bgmEl (Mojiworld theme) vs _bgmBossEl (Gravitos Ascends), toggled by isBossArena. Extended to a per-map registry so the right track plays when you walk into Hera's bubble-stair, the Bastion courtyard, the Acadamia, the Emerald Village or the Hood.

  • How it works — new _BGM_MAP_FILES object maps map ids → audio files (audio/bgm_shadow_slums.mp3 etc.). _bgmMapEl(id) lazy-builds the HTMLAudioElement on first demand. _setBossBgm(isBoss, mapId) now picks: boss arena wins outright; else if the map id has a registered track, fade-in that one and fade-out everything else; else fall back to the Mojiworld default.
  • Autoplay-unlock — startBgm's first-input gesture handler walks every entry in _BGM_MAP_FILES on the same gesture so the first map-load swap doesn't stall waiting on a fresh user input.
  • Cross-fade — reuses the existing _bgmFade 600 ms ramp and the 0.55 target volume so map-to-map transitions feel the same as the boss-room swap.
  • Verified in preview — _setBossBgm(false, 'bastion') brings bastion to 0.55 and mojiworld to 0.00, then _setBossBgm(false, 'forest') fades bastion back to 0 and mojiworld back up. _bgmActiveMapEl tracks the currently-playing per-map element so a swap from one custom map to another fades the previous one cleanly.

polish"The Shadow-Woven Slums" renamed to "The Shadow-Woven Hood"

Display name + the candy-canyon → slums portal label + every NPC dialog line that referenced "the Slums" / "the slums" updated to "the Hood". The internal map id stays shadowSlums so save files, sprite folders, and historical version-tagged comments aren't broken.

featBazaar ↔ Marketplace walking portal (Marketplace was taxi-only before)

Everdawn Marketplace was reachable only via the taxi NPC since v0.25.267. Added a walking portal pair: town at x:550 (mid-bazaar, between the left-end Bastion Rampart portal and the right-end Sunset Coast portal — fits the v0.25.434 spacing rule for 3-portal maps), and the matching back portal in everdawn_marketplace at x:100. Both maps stay flagged isTown:true so monster spawns + boss BGM behaviour is unchanged.

  • Files referenced — maple_game.html:41356 (BGM registry + lazy loader), maple_game.html:41388 (_setBossBgm with mapId), maple_game.html:13148 (loadMap call site), maple_game.html:8347 (Shadow-Woven Hood name), maple_game.html:8716 (bazaar↔marketplace portal pair), maple_game.html:3077 (version). node --check clean.

v0.25.435 Wardrobe Apply button stuck below the panel fold

bug"Image drawn or uploaded does not get used in game" — the Apply button was off-screen

The data path from buildCharStudioPaint → _csCommitPaintCanvas → CHAR_STUDIO.customPaintLayers → applyCharStudioToPlayer → player.customPaintLayers → _drawCustomPaintLayerPinned all worked correctly when reached programmatically. The actual blocker was UI: the "▶ Use in game" action bar lived at the very end of the scrollable wardrobe panel, and the paint editor's controls (toolbar, swatches, transform sliders, status line) made the inner panel content ~1110 px tall against a panel that's clamped at 92vh. On a 800-px-tall viewport that put the Apply button at top: 985 px — 217 px below the visible viewport — and the user had to know to scroll inside the panel to reach it. They didn't, so they read "no Apply button" as "drawing/uploading doesn't work."

  • Fix — position: sticky; bottom: 0 on .wardrobe-actions with a vertical-fade backdrop so the Apply / Revert buttons stay pinned to the bottom of the panel regardless of scroll. The status line gets the same treatment one row up so the "Applied · player is now using…" confirmation isn't hidden either.
  • Verified — reloaded the preview, opened Wardrobe, picked the ALL slot (the tallest picker), pointer-stroked a stamp on the paint canvas, clicked the now-visible "Use in game" button, closed the studio. player.customPaint populated (3,734 bytes of PNG), player.lookCustom set, and the painted layer rendered on the in-game player.
  • Files referenced — maple_game.html:41635 (.wardrobe-actions sticky CSS), maple_game.html:3077 (version). node --check clean.

v0.25.434 Multi-portal spacing + grounded NPC/monster shadows

bugMulti-portal maps had every forward portal piled on the same x — only the first was reachable

The walking-portal block at the bottom of the maps section pushed every ▶ portal at x:2700/2500/2400/2300 and every ◀ portal at x:100 regardless of how many destinations a single source map served. tryPortal iterates portals in order and triggers the first one within ±50 px of the player — so on maps with 2+ stacked portals (ancient, bastion, bubblegumSwamp, candyCanyon, celestialSpire, cryptHollow, forest, graniteBluffs, jadeGrove, mushroom, skyGarden, stardustAtrium, sunsetBeach, thunderPlateau, tidalLagoon, town, wildflowerPlains) only ONE destination was actually walkable; the others were dead links. Re-spaced per the new house rule:

  • 2 portals — one at each end of the map (x:100 and x: worldWidth - 100 ish).
  • 3 portals — ends + a single middle pin (roughly worldWidth / 2).
  • 4 portals — ends + two evenly-spaced middles (e.g. x:100 / 900 / 1700 / 2500 on the 2600-wide Fungal Hollow).
  • Side-effect on entry-X — tryPortal computes entryX = backPortal.x ± 80 based on which half of the destination map the back-portal sits in, so when a portal moves from x:100 to x:2500, the player now lands near the right side of the source map when returning. Symbols updated (◀/▶/⬥) so the on-portal arrows still match the player's facing direction at each end.
  • Files referenced — maple_game.html:8631 (the 31-pair walking-portal block), maple_game.html:21286 (tryPortal entry-X computation).

polishNPCs and monsters appeared to float — shadow ellipse sat below the feet, sprites had transparent bottom-padding

Two compounding bugs made every NPC and ground monster look like it was hovering 2–6 px above the tile it was standing on:

  • NPC sprite bbox-bottom anchor — _drawNpcSprite drew every sprite at a fixed -targetH * 0.96 offset, ignoring transparent padding at the bottom of the source PNG. Many of the new ludo renders (Taiga, Will, Hera, etc.) ship with 5–10% empty pixels under the feet, which mapped to 5–10% of targetH floating below the visible body. Now mirrors the monster path: detect the lowest opaque pixel via _detectSpriteBboxBottom at load time, cache it in NPC_SPRITE_META, and compute dyOffset = -((bboxBottomY+1)/srcH) * targetH + targetH * 0.02 so the actual visible feet land on the foot line with a 2% bury into the ground tile (same GROUND_BURY_FRAC the monster path uses). Falls back to the historical 0.96 ratio if the bbox detector hasn't run yet.
  • Shadow ellipse position + thickness — the NPC shadow was centred at feetY + 2 with vertical radius 3.5, so the ellipse poked 5 px below the feet and read as a separate dark blob under the character. Pulled up to feetY exactly, flattened to vertical radius 2.2, and bumped alpha 0.35 → 0.4 so the thinner puddle still reads at chibi scale. Same treatment for monsters: was at m.y + m.h with radius 4, now at m.y + m.h - 1 with radius 2.5 and alpha 0.32. Floating mobs (wraith, cherub, seraph, …) keep their FLOATING_GROUND_BLEED lift so their shadow stays grounded while the sprite hovers above — that's still correct.
  • Files referenced — maple_game.html:29089 (NPC shadow), maple_game.html:35767 (_drawNpcSprite), maple_game.html:35759 (NPC sprite loader + bbox cache), maple_game.html:36126 (monster shadow). node --check clean.

v0.25.433 Hero rig: right arm + both hand ellipses nudged left

polishThree rig pivots tuned to better centre the right arm + hand ellipses on the body

Adjustments to the HERO_VEC_RIG bone offsets to fix slight drift in the right-arm shoulder pivot and both hand-ellipse pivots:

  • armR — x: 13 → 12 (1px left), y: -22 → -21 (1px down). The front/free-arm shoulder pivot sat slightly outboard of the torso silhouette and slightly too high relative to the spine drop. Pulling it inward + downward sits the shoulder under the collar curve more naturally.
  • handL — x: 0 → -1 (1px left). The left-hand ellipse + weapon pivot drifted slightly right of the arm-tip path; nudging the local-x by 1 keeps it centred on the rounded teardrop limb path.
  • handR — x: 1 → 0 (1px left). Same correction for the right hand — the v0.25.136 introduction of the handR child bone defaulted to x: 1 as a guess; centring at x: 0 aligns the ellipse with the arm-tip path's symmetry axis.
  • Knock-on effects — legL/legR/handL all stay where they were; the spine + head bones are unaffected. Animations that read handR (per-class weapon overlay anchor, the v0.25.394 weapon paint layer) follow the new pivot automatically since they read _heroVecRestPos('handR') rather than hardcoding the x.
  • Files referenced — maple_game.html:29856, maple_game.html:29858, maple_game.html:29859, and maple_game.html:3077 (version). node --check clean.

v0.25.432 NPC sprites enlarged to 128 px (clean 6× downsample)

polishDefault base height bumped 78 → 107 so target render lands on a clean integer downsample of the source

The v0.25.430/431 NPC sprites (Taiga, Kuro, Ren, Will, Elena, Barnaby, Hera, Auron, Lyra, Lady Hongyu, Master Shen, Yun, Fashionista) all dropped in as 768×768 PNGs. The v0.25.86 default render-height of 78 px (× 1.2 NPC_WORLD_SCALE = 94 px target) produced a 768 / 94 ≈ 8.17× downsample — off-integer ratio, so the bilinear smoothing produced visible fuzz. Bumping the default base to 107 makes the target 128 px, which is exactly 6× downsample on a 768 source — the sharpest practical integer ratio for a character that still reads at chibi scale.

  • What changed — one line in maple_game.html:35740: _npcSpriteBaseHeight's fallback from 78 to 107. The whole rendering pipeline (label head-Y, chat-bubble anchor, click hit-box, etc.) reads through _npcSpriteTargetHeight so layout adapts automatically.
  • Effect on the new 768×768 sprites — render at 128 px tall, exactly 6× downsample. ~36% bigger on screen than before (was 94 px). High-quality bilinear smoothing on a 6× clean ratio is visibly sharper than the previous 8.17× off-integer ratio.
  • Effect on the 640×864 sprites (Pharma Sis, Brok, The Amnesiac, Old Arlen, Taxi Uncle) — same 128 px target, 864 / 128 = 6.75× downsample. Not perfectly integer, but better than the previous 9.19×. They look modestly sharper too, and now match the new NPCs in size for a consistent NPC scale across the world.
  • Sage Mira keeps 115 — her per-NPC override in NPC_SPRITE_HEIGHTS still wins. Renders 138 px, 864 / 138 ≈ 6.26× downsample, matches her "tall sage" lore.
  • Why not per-source-resolution tuning — an alternative (90 base for 864 sources, 107 base for 768 sources) would give cleaner downsamples but visibly different sizes between the original 6 NPCs and the new 13 (108 vs 128 px tall = 18% gap). Consistency across the cast outweighs the tiny sharpness gain on 864 sources.
  • Files referenced — maple_game.html:35740 (default base), maple_game.html:3077 (version). node --check clean.

v0.25.431 Fashionista sprite refreshed

polishNew ludo render swapped in; manifest + Phaser preload realigned

The user dropped a new full-body Fashionista sprite into Sprites/npc/ as a generic Ludo-named PNG (gen-96e729ea-...) and removed the previous fashionista.webp + fashionista_raw.webp. Renamed the new file to fashionista.png (clean name; matches the rest of the v0.25.430 batch); updated all three references in the source.

  • Filesystem — gen-96e729ea-aa50-410c-a75c-77f912dac0a2.png renamed to fashionista.png via mv.
  • Manifest — NPC_SPRITE_FILES.Fashionista updated 'fashionista.webp' → 'fashionista.png' at maple_game.html:35707.
  • Phaser preload — both eager-preload entries (one near line 40930, one near line 41176) switched to fashionista.png so the new sprite decodes before world reveal alongside the other 6 always-loaded NPCs.
  • Files referenced — maple_game.html:35707, maple_game.html:40930, maple_game.html:41176, and maple_game.html:3077 (version). node --check clean.

v0.25.430 12 NPC sprites wired up to match dropped-in assets

fixNPC_SPRITE_FILES filenames re-aligned with the actual files in Sprites/npc/

The user dropped in 12 NPC sprite files (Taiga.png, kuro.png, ren.png, will.png, elena.png, barnaby.png, hera.png, auron.png, lyra.png, Lady Hongyu.webp, Master Shen.png, yun.png). The NPC_SPRITE_FILES manifest still pointed at the v0.25.411–414-era expected names (taiga.webp, lady_hongyu.webp, etc.) which never existed on disk, so each of those NPCs was rendering via the procedural fallback path. Manifest entries updated to the actual filenames so the sprites load.

  • Updated mappings (12) — Taiga / Kuro / Ren / Will / Elena / Barnaby / Hera / Auron / Lyra all switched from *.webp to *.png (with capital T on Taiga). Master Shen + Yun likewise switched to .png with their actual capitalisation. Lady Hongyu uses Lady Hongyu.webp (literal space; browsers URL-encode it in img.src). Auron's old auron.webp stays in the directory but is no longer referenced.
  • Pre-existing seven sprites unchanged — Pharma Sis / Brok / The Amnesiac / Sage Mira / Old Arlen / Taxi Uncle / Fashionista all still resolve via their existing *.webp entries.
  • Phaser preload list intentionally not updated — the eager-preload at maple_game.html:40921 only covers the original 7 NPCs; the 12 new ones load lazily via _loadNpcSprites() at module init. They appear procedurally for one frame on first paint, then swap to the loaded sprite once decoded — same behaviour as v0.25.411–414's intended fallback flow.
  • Files referenced — maple_game.html:35711-35728 (NPC_SPRITE_FILES entries) and maple_game.html:3077 (version bump). node --check clean.

v0.25.429 Second editor pass — 31 portal pairs + 31 free-position pins

featWorld graph filled out: every map now reachable on foot, every node free-positioned

Second pass driven by the W-key edit mode. v0.25.428 had 15 walking links + 23 angles + 2 corner-pin pixel positions; v0.25.429 doubles the portal density to 31 pairs, drops 3 v0.25.428 links the user filtered out, and replaces every map's tier-ring placement with explicit wmX / wmY canvas pixels (except forest, which wasn't dragged this pass and falls back to wmAngle).

feat+19 new walking portals (consolidated source, no filter+push churn)

  • Forest depths re-opened — ancient ↔ duneSands, ancient ↔ jadeGrove, forest ↔ mushroom, forest ↔ skyGarden, mushroom ↔ bubblegumSwamp (and the previous mushroom ↔ candyCanyon stays). Elderwood Grove is no longer a foot-traffic dead end.
  • Cosmic + endgame chain — celestialSpire ↔ sanctum, celestialSpire ↔ stardustAtrium, gravitosArena ↔ sanctum, cryptHollow ↔ pearlBathhouse / stardustAtrium / thunderPlateau. Sanctum is now a 3-way endgame hub (celestial / gravitos / lavaCavern via duneSands chain).
  • Highlands + plateau — frozenPeak ↔ skyGarden, graniteBluffs ↔ thunderPlateau, duneSands ↔ lavaCavern, boss ↔ thunderPlateau. Queen's Hollow now bridges the eastern forest chain (via ancient) to the desert plateau (via thunder).
  • Eastern bridge — bubblegumSwamp ↔ honeycombHollow / jadeGrove / mushroom; emeraldVillage ↔ jadeGrove. Bubblegum Swamp is the connective tissue between Forest, Jade Grove, and the southern Honeycomb deeps.
  • Azure ↔ Tidal moved upstream — the v0.25.428 azureAbode ↔ tidalLagoon link snipped; replaced by azureAcademia ↔ tidalLagoon (Azure's main hub, not Hera's bubble, faces the lagoon).
  • Bastion shrunk — bastionRampart ↔ wildflowerPlains snipped (rampart is now only town-facing); bastion cluster keeps its 3 internal portals + town link.
  • Forest detour — forest ↔ town snipped (forest now reaches town only via wildflowerPlains → bastionRampart → town, or via mushroom → ancient → boss → thunder → cryptHollow → … the long way). May want to re-add later if the routing feels too indirect.

feat31 maps free-positioned via wmX / wmY

  • Tier ring fully unlocked — every dragged map carries explicit canvas-pixel coordinates that win over both wmAngle and the level-tier ring radius. The renderer's wmX / wmY override path (added in v0.25.428) is now the primary placement mechanism for the W-map.
  • Notable position moves — sanctum (411, 178) and gravitosArena (412, 88) sit stacked at top-centre instead of the corner-pin (80, 60) and (200, 60); celestialSpire (337, 198) sits between them; the _WM_BOSS_OVERRIDES corner-pin entries for these are now overridden by the user's free positions. boss (Queen's Hollow) at (178, 442) similarly sits mid-left instead of bottom-right.
  • town nudged — from v0.25.428's (604, 371) to (619, 344). Slight shift up-and-right of canvas centre.
  • Forest edge case — forest wasn't dragged in this pass, so it has no wmX/wmY. It falls back to its v0.25.428 wmAngle = -0.7428 on the early tier ring (radius 140), placing it at roughly (704, 302). Will become free-positioned once the user drags it.
  • Source consolidation — instead of stacking v0.25.429 deltas (filter + push) on top of v0.25.428's deltas, the post-MAPS authoring block was rewritten to a single canonical list of 31 portal pairs and 31 wmX/wmY assignments. No filter+push churn in source; cleaner diff for the next iteration.

v0.25.428 First editor-authored portal graph + level-tier ring unlock

featMap nodes can now sit anywhere on the W-map; tier ring no longer locks coordinates

Removes the level-tier ring constraint on W-map placement. Previously every non-corner map was forced onto one of four concentric rings (140 / 235 / 320 / 405 px) determined by its levelReq; the editor's drag-to-reposition only let you change the angle on that ring. v0.25.428 adds a free wmX/wmY override that wins over both wmAngle and the tier ring, so any map can sit at any pixel on the canvas.

  • New override — setting MAPS.X.wmX = N; MAPS.X.wmY = N; places the map at canvas pixel (N, N) regardless of level. Renderer in _wmComputePositions applies overrides last (after tier-ring + boss/tower corner pins), so a map with explicit wmX/wmY ignores both. Maps without overrides still use their wmAngle on the tier ring — nothing breaks for the existing layout.
  • Editor drag is free — the v0.25.426 drag handler used to constrain ring-tier maps to their tier circle (emitting wmAngle only). v0.25.428 removes that constraint; drag now updates {x, y} for every map. The cursor moves the node to wherever you drop it, ring or no ring.
  • Codegen emits wmX / wmY — ⚙ Generate Code now outputs MAPS.X.wmX = N; MAPS.X.wmY = N; for every dragged node (replacing the v0.25.426 split between wmAngle for ring maps and a comment-block snippet for corner maps). Both ring and corner moves now share one paste target: anywhere in the post-MAPS authoring block.

featFirst editor-authored portal graph re-pasted on top of the v0.25.427 wipe

Re-builds the world graph from scratch with 15 walking links the user authored via the v0.25.422 + v0.25.426 W-key edit mode and pasted back as code. Plus 23 dragged angles + 2 free-position pins for slimeCave and town.

  • 15 walking portals — restores the core forest path (forest↔town, forest↔wildflowerPlains, mushroom↔candyCanyon, candyCanyon↔shadowSlums, mushroom↔wildflowerPlains), bastion cluster (bastion↔throne, bastion↔rampart, rampart↔town, rampart↔wildflowerPlains), south coast (sunsetBeach↔town, sunsetBeach↔tidalLagoon, slimeCave↔sunsetBeach), Azure hub (azureAcademia↔azureAbode, azureAbode↔tidalLagoon), and a north→south bridge (graniteBluffs↔tidalLagoon).
  • Notable absences — no portal back to ancient/Elderwood Grove from any map yet (so Queen's Hollow, the eastern forest depths, the Lava Cavern chain, and Aetherion's Sanctum are unreachable on foot until you re-add their gates from the editor). Same for stardustAtrium/celestialSpire/pearlBathhouse/cryptHollow/honeycombHollow/duneSands/thunderPlateau/jadeGrove/emeraldVillage/skyGarden/frozenPeak/lavaCavern/bubblegumSwamp — all reachable only via the W-key teleport for now.
  • 23 wmAngle deltas — the user-pasted angles replace the v0.25.418/423–425 authoring values for every map they dragged. Maps not in the drag list (cryptHollow, frozenPeak, pearlBathhouse, stardustAtrium) keep their previous angles.
  • Free-position pins — slimeCave at (499, 311) and town at (604, 371) use the new wmX/wmY override. town's was a hub move (originally hard-pinned to canvas centre cx/cy = 600/375); slimeCave's replaces the dynamic "beside-forest" anchor in _WM_BOSS_OVERRIDES.
  • Cleanup — two stragglers from the v0.25.427 wipe removed in this pass: a MAPS.boss.portals.push with extra whitespace that escaped my regex, and the zodiac hub portal MAPS.town.portals.push({dest:'zodiacHall'}) that lived past the script's stop marker. Zodiac Sanctum is now also unreachable on foot until re-added.

v0.25.427 Portal graph wiped — re-author from the W-map editor

featClean slate: every map's portal list emptied for editor-driven re-authoring

One-shot destructive operation. Every portals: [ ... ] array in the MAPS = { … } literal is now portals: [], and every MAPS.X.portals.push(...) / MAPS.X.portals = MAPS.X.portals.filter(...) line in the post-MAPS authoring blocks is stripped. The graph is a blank slate — every map exists with the same layout, NPCs, decor, and W-map position, but no walking links between them. Re-author the portal graph from scratch using the v0.25.422 + v0.25.426 W-key edit mode (click-to-pair to add/remove links, drag-to-reposition for positions, then Generate Code).

  • Scope of the wipe — 34 multi-line portal arrays cleared (one per map), 70 post-MAPS portal-mutation lines removed (every push and filter, including v0.25.50/51/411-415/422/423/424). Done by a one-shot Python script (.tmp_clear_portals.py, deleted post-run) that brace-matched the MAPS literal range so it didn't touch any non-portal code.
  • What survived — the 35 MAPS.X.wmAngle = ... assignments from the v0.25.418 + v0.25.423–425 authoring blocks all stayed, so every map keeps its W-map angular position. Map definitions, NPC rosters, decor functions, level requirements, world widths, layouts, sprites — none touched. Only the portal arrays lost their contents.
  • Effect on play — walking off either edge of any map does nothing (no portals to trigger). Bosses, the Tower of Ascension, the Zodiac Sanctum, and every secret descent are unreachable on foot until you re-add their portals. The W-key map still teleports to any unlocked node (the teleport path runs through loadMap() directly, independent of portals[]), so you can still travel between maps by pressing W and clicking. Use that to test layouts while you re-wire the graph.
  • How to rebuild — press W, click ✏ Edit Connections, click pairs of nodes to add walking links (the v0.25.422 click-to-pair). Drag any node to nudge its position (v0.25.426). When done, ⚙ Generate Code dumps the deltas. Paste the portal-push lines into the post-MAPS push block (right after the const MAPS = {…}; close, before the ZODIAC BOSSES divider) and any MAPS.X.wmAngle = … lines into the v0.25.418 angle authoring block.
  • Syntax-checked — node --check clean on the main inline script after the wipe; the empty portals: [] arrays parse fine and the renderer treats them as "no portal lines drawn from this node," so the W-map opens with all 35 nodes visible and zero edges between them.

v0.25.426 W-map edit mode: drag-to-reposition + position codegen

featEdit mode now adjusts node positions in addition to portal links

The v0.25.422 W-key edit mode previously supported click-to-pair for portal authoring. v0.25.426 adds drag-to-reposition: while edit mode is on, click-and-drag any node to move it, then hit Generate Code to dump MAPS.X.wmAngle = ...; lines for the moved nodes (alongside any pending portal deltas). Click-vs-drag is auto-detected by movement threshold — a clean click still toggles a portal link the way it used to.

  • Drag interaction — mousedown on any node captures it as the drag source. mousemove updates the node's pending position live (re-renders the whole diagram each frame so portal lines and the node sub-label follow); mouseup commits. If the cursor moved <4 px between mousedown and mouseup, the gesture is treated as a click instead and the v0.25.422 pair-toggle logic runs — one set of input handlers, two interactions.
  • Ring constraint for tier maps — early / mid / high / end ring maps stay locked to their tier's radius (the level-tier ring structure is gameplay-relevant). Drag updates only the angle; the new wmAngle is computed from the cursor's position via atan2, with the placeRing 0.78 y-squash undone so the node lands directly under the cursor on the squashed ellipse. Boss / Tower / Hub corner-pinned maps drag freely (clamped to a 40 px canvas margin).
  • Visual indicator — nodes with pending position deltas get a dashed gold ring (#ffd166) painted under the disc, so you can see at a glance what's been moved. The cursor switches to move in edit mode (was crosshair) to telegraph that nodes are draggable.
  • Cross-render persistence — the drag listeners live on document, not the SVG element. Each renderWorldMap() rebuilds the SVG from scratch, which would otherwise drop svg-scoped listeners mid-drag and freeze the gesture. Document-scoped listeners with a once-only wiring guard (window._wmEditDragWired) survive re-renders. Mid-drag SVG coords are recovered each frame by re-querying #worldmap-grid svg and using createSVGPoint + getScreenCTM().inverse().
  • Code generation split — Generate Code now emits up to three labelled blocks: portal deltas (paste into post-MAPS push block), wmAngle deltas for ring maps (paste into the v0.25.418 wmAngle authoring block), and a comment-style snippet for any corner-pinned moves (manually replace the matching entries inside the _WM_BOSS_OVERRIDES const literal in _wmComputePositions). Each block is omitted if there are no edits of that kind.
  • Reset clears positions too — the ↺ Reset button now wipes both portal deltas and position deltas (and the in-flight drag state) in one go, returning the diagram to its source-of-truth layout.

v0.25.425 W-map repositioned for connectivity over regional theme

polishLinked maps now sit side-to-side; cross-region long-line offenders fixed

Connectivity-driven re-author of the W-key map. After v0.25.423/424 settled the portal graph, several remaining edges still drew long diagonals because the regional quadrant authoring (v0.25.418) put map nodes in their thematic quadrant rather than next to their walking neighbours. This pass identifies the cross-region edges that are actually fixable (without breaking the level-tier ring structure) and pulls each map to a slot beside its real-graph neighbour.

  • bastionRampart out of the W bastion cluster — moved from π−0.45 (SW-early, beside bastion + bastionThrone) to π/2+0.45 (SSW-early, beside sunsetBeach). The earlier slot drew a 1.12-rad line across the entire south to sunsetBeach; SSW makes the rampart→sunset link the shortest possible early-ring line. The bastion cluster reads as bastion (W) + bastionThrone (NW) + the rampart now part of the southern coast cluster — matches the v0.25.423 narrative ("Bastion Rampart fans east to coast + meadow").
  • EAST mid ring shifted CW one slot — bubblegumSwamp pulled to the NE-most slot (−0.55) so its new jadeGrove link drops from 1.27 rad to 0.82 rad of arc. The rest of the chain shifted CW: shadowSlums at −0.30 (still adjacent to candyCanyon, its only link), candyCanyon at −0.05 (just NE of cardinal-E mid; central 4-way hub for mushroom + ancient + shadowSlums + bubblegumSwamp), mushroom at +0.20, ancient at +0.45 (radial w/ wildflowerPlains, unchanged).
  • stardustAtrium moved from NE end to SE end — −π/2+0.25 (top-right corner) was the worst offender for cross-region distance: stardust's only links are thunderPlateau (high SE) and cryptHollow (high SW), both in the southern hemisphere. NE-end placed stardust ~525 px from each. The new π/2−0.20 slot puts stardust directly under thunderPlateau (~120 px) and across from cryptHollow (~150 px). Celestial Spire stays NW-end beside Sanctum's corner pin.
  • What still draws long diagonals — ancient↔lavaCavern (E mid ↔ NW mid), frozenPeak↔tidalLagoon (NW mid ↔ SE mid), cryptHollow↔graniteBluffs (SW high ↔ NE mid), skyGarden↔thunder/honeycomb (N mid ↔ SE high), and tidalLagoon↔azureAcademia (SE mid ↔ W mid) all stay long. Each one is constrained by the level-tier ring structure (you can't pull both endpoints into the same quadrant without putting a low-level map on the high-level ring or vice versa).

v0.25.424 Boss-graph cleanup + EAST mid ring re-author

featEdit-mode pass two: revert the boss detour, snip Singularity, re-flow the east wedge

Second pass driven by the v0.25.422 W-key edit-mode codegen plus a hand re-author of the eastern mid ring. After playtesting the v0.25.423 graph, two of the boss-area edits read wrong on the map and one was a missed snip:

  • ancient ↔ boss restored — v0.25.423 dropped this as "stale," but it's the canonical Lv-15 boss gate from the eastern forest chain (Elderwood Grove → Queen's Hollow). Re-added at x:2700 (forward) / x:100 (back).
  • boss ↔ lavaCavern removed — the v0.25.423 detour from the boss arena into the Lava Cavern doesn't make progression sense (Lava Cavern is deeper than Queen's Hollow). Snipped.
  • gravitosArena ↔ sanctum removed — the Singularity arena is meant to be reachable only through Sanctum's portal-mechanic gate, not by a back-walking exit. Cleaned up the implicit walking link so the W-map no longer suggests a foot route.

polishEAST mid ring re-spread + Crypt of Whispers pulled in from deep-SW

The eastern mid ring (5 nodes from forest chain to Elderwood crossroads) had uneven spacing and put bubblegumSwamp on the SE side, drawing a long diagonal up to its new jadeGrove link. Re-authored every node with explicit per-position rationale:

  • Even 0.25-rad spacing across the EAST wedge — shadowSlums at -0.55 (NE far, narrative tail), candyCanyon at -0.30 (was -0.25; central hub of the 4-way crossroads), bubblegumSwamp at -0.05 (was +0.25; NE-side now shortens the jadeGrove link), mushroom at +0.20 (was 0.0; slight SE en route to ancient), ancient at +0.45 (was +0.55; radial spoke with wildflowerPlains at +0.45 early ring).
  • Forest chain reads SE-ward — forest (early E) → mushroom (mid SE-ish) → ancient (mid SE deeper). candyCanyon and shadowSlums sit on the NE branch off mushroom; bubblegumSwamp sits on the NE branch with its visible link climbing to jadeGrove.
  • cryptHollow pulled in from π/2+0.45 to π/2+0.20 — v0.25.423's swap of cryptHollow's only N-side link (frozenPeak → graniteBluffs NE) made the deep-SW position draw a diagonal cutting across the entire south region. Just-SW-of-cardinal-south keeps the SW lean (still the deepest crypt) while shortening both the graniteBluffs link and the radial pearlBathhouse drop into the end ring.

v0.25.423 World graph reshape — 8 stale links pruned, 16 new walking routes

featTown hub-spokes thinned out, neighbouring biomes now connect on foot

First batch of edits driven by the v0.25.422 W-key edit-mode codegen. The world graph was structurally lopsided — Sunset Coast, Thunder Plateau, and Celestial Spire dangled off Town as direct hub-spokes, and several mid-ring biomes (Bubblegum Swamp, Pearl Bathhouse, Honeycomb Hollow, Stardust Atrium) had no walking neighbour despite sitting visibly adjacent on the map. This pass prunes the stale links and stitches in 16 new bidirectional walking portals so each region's nodes connect to their neighbours instead of teleporting through Town.

  • Pruned 8 stale links — ancient↔boss, celestialSpire↔town, cryptHollow↔frozenPeak, cryptHollow↔stardustAtrium, duneSands↔sunsetBeach, lavaCavern↔sanctum, sunsetBeach↔town, thunderPlateau↔town. Most were left over from earlier exploratory authoring; cleaning them up makes the W-map's portal-line graph readable instead of a starburst out of Town.
  • SE crossroads — Elderwood Grove (ancient) now opens east to Tidal Lagoon and Wildflower Plains. Reads as the spoke where the Eastern Forest chain meets the Southern Coast.
  • Bastion Rampart fans east — the wall now connects to Sunset Coast and Wildflower Plains, replacing the earlier town hub-spoke as the way out of the Western Stronghold.
  • Bubblegum Swamp bridges — new walking links to Emerald Thicket (forest) and The Jade Grove, so the swamp finally has incoming foot traffic instead of being a Candy Canyon dead-end.
  • Crypt↔Bluffs — Crypt of Whispers no longer climbs to Frozen Peak / Stardust Atrium; instead it climbs east to Granite Bluffs, matching the SW-to-NE reading of the new sectioned world map.
  • Frozen Peak descent — new Tidal Lagoon exit gives the highlands a southern walking path down to the coast.
  • Honeycomb Hollow as middle-southern crossroads — new walking links to The Jade Grove and Sky Garden turn the Hollow into a vertical bridge between the SE coast and the N highlands.
  • Pearl Bathhouse opens to coast — Sunset Coast walking link gives the deep-south bathhouse a non-taxi neighbour.
  • Sky Garden + Stardust both reach Thunder Plateau — the plateau used to be a town hub-spoke; now reachable by walking from either the highlands (Sky Garden) or the cosmic ring (Stardust Atrium).
  • Boss arena bridge — Queen's Hollow (boss) now connects to Lava Cavern. Removes the stale ancient link and gives the Lv 40 arena a thematic flame-cavern neighbour.
  • Door positions — codegen defaulted forward-portals to worldWidth-100 and back-portals to x:100; hand-spread to 100 / 200 (and 2200/2400/2500/2600/2700) where two portals share an edge so they don't visually stack on the same door tile.
  • W-map repositioning — new edges revealed three long cross-quadrant lines that were the worst readability offenders. Three angle nudges shorten them: Honeycomb Hollow moved from SW to SE side of the south region (π/2+0.15 → π/2−0.30) so its new links to Jade Grove + Sky Garden drop ~vertically instead of cutting diagonally; Stardust Atrium and Celestial Spire swapped on the end ring (stardust → NE over Thunder Plateau, celestial → NW next to Sanctum's corner pin) so each cosmic spire's remaining walking link is short. Dune Sands nudged east-of-cardinal-south (π/2−0.20 → π/2−0.05) so it doesn't crowd Honeycomb's new SE position. Every other map keeps its v0.25.418 quadrant placement.
  • What stays — v0.25.415's connectivity adds (forest↔wildflowerPlains, sunsetBeach↔tidalLagoon, lavaCavern↔graniteBluffs, duneSands↔thunderPlateau, sanctum↔celestialSpire) all stay intact. Town still owns the Bastion / Tower / Forest / Marketplace / Sunset Coast routes through its in-source portals[] definitions; only the post-MAPS push entries to thunderPlateau / celestialSpire / sunsetBeach were removed.

v0.25.422 World map edit mode + portal codegen

featClick-to-pair portal authoring with a one-button code dump

The W-key world map gets an Edit Connections mode: toggle it on, click two nodes to add or remove a walking portal between them, and hit Generate Code to spit out the matching MAPS.X.portals.push(...) / filter(...) snippet you can paste into the post-MAPS push block in source. No live mutation of the running game state — just pure codegen, so your edits are reviewable before they ship.

  • Toolbar — new row above the SVG with three buttons: ✏ Edit Connections (toggle), ↺ Reset (clears pending deltas), ⚙ Generate Code (renders JS into a textarea). The toggle button turns red when active so the mode is unmistakable; a pending-edit counter sits beside it as live status.
  • Click-to-pair — in edit mode, click any node to mark it as the source (pulsing dashed-green halo). Click a second node to toggle a link between the two: if a portal already exists in either direction it flips to "remove"; if not, "add". Clicking the same node twice deselects. Clicking the same pair twice reverts the delta.
  • Visual diff — existing portal lines stay lavender as before. Pending removals repaint the existing line in red dashed; pending additions draw a bright-green dashed overlay between the two nodes. The diff stays visible until you Reset, exit edit mode, or close the modal.
  • Generate Code — emits one block per delta:
    • Add A ↔ B: MAPS.A.portals.push({x:..., dest:'B', name:'▶ <B name>'}); + the reverse push for B. The forward x defaults to worldWidth(A) - 100; the back-link x defaults to 100. Adjust by hand if you want a specific door position.
    • Remove A ↔ B: MAPS.A.portals = MAPS.A.portals.filter(p => p.dest !== 'B'); + the symmetric filter on B.
    Output appears in a read-only textarea below the diagram with a 📋 Copy button. Paste into the post-MAPS push block in maple_game.html to make the edits permanent.
  • Safety — nothing mutates MAPS at runtime. Edit mode is purely a UI overlay + delta dictionary; closing the modal preserves pending edits so you can re-open and keep going. The teleport-on-click handler is suppressed while editing, so a stray click can't accidentally fast-travel out of the page.

v0.25.421 Authored backdrops for the new biomes

featHand-painted backgrounds wired up for 8 recently-added maps

The eight maps added since v0.25.411 (Shadow Slums + Bastion complex + Azure Acadamia + Jade Grove + Emerald Village) were running on procedural parallax with no authored backdrop. User-supplied paintings dropped into New images/ are now copied into backgrounds/ under the project's bg_v3_*.png convention and wired into both the vanilla BG_IMAGES registry and the Phaser preload, with each map's bg: field pointing at the matching key.

  • Files copied into backgrounds/:
    • bg_v3_shadowSlums.png ← "The shadow woven slums.png"
    • bg_v3_bastion.png ← "The bastion courtyard.png"
    • bg_v3_bastionThrone.png ← "The bastion throne room.png"
    • bg_v3_bastionRampart.png ← "The bastion rampart.png"
    • bg_v3_azureAcademia.png ← "The azure academia.png"
    • bg_v3_azureAbode.png ← "Hera's floating abode.png"
    • bg_v3_jadeGrove.png ← "The jade grove.png"
    • bg_v3_emeraldVillage.png ← "Emerald village.png"
  • BG_IMAGES registry — eight new keys (shadowSlums, bastion, bastionThrone, bastionRampart, azureAcademia, azureAbode, jadeGrove, emeraldVillage) each pointing at their bg_v3_*.png. The standard _loadBG() path applies, so missing files fall back gracefully to procedural parallax.
  • Phaser preload — mirrored as this.load.image('bg_<key>', 'backgrounds/bg_v3_<key>.png') for each of the 8 keys, so the Phaser-handled background path picks them up too.
  • Map definitions — bg: field added to each of the 8 map entries (shadowSlums, bastion, bastionThrone, bastionRampart, azureAcademia, azureAbode, jadeGrove, emeraldVillage). The drawBackground() path then routes each map to its painted backdrop while the existing per-biome decor (cherry blossoms, neon paper lanterns, glowing fountain, etc.) still paints on top.

v0.25.420 World map canvas blown up + node spacing

polishBigger SVG canvas, bigger nodes, bigger gaps between rings

The previous W-key map was structurally cramped — ~30 nodes squeezed onto a 760×460 SVG with ring radii of 90/155/220/285. Even with the v0.25.418 quadrant labelling, click targets were tight and labels overlapped. Whole canvas redrawn at proper scale.

  • Canvas — SVG viewBox 760×460 → 1200×750 (~2.5× the area). Modal grew to match: min-width:1080px; max-width:1320px; width:92vw; max-height:92vh.
  • Ring radii — scaled ~1.4× so each tier ring has more arc length per node:
    • Early ring 90 → 140
    • Mid ring 155 → 235 (most crowded; six maps in the north band now sit cleanly)
    • High ring 220 → 320
    • End ring 285 → 405
  • Node sizes — disc radius 19 → 28 (idle) and 22 → 32 (current / hover). Halo 28 → 40. Biome icon font 20 → 28. Map name label 10 → 14 at y:38 → 52; sub-label 9 → 12 at y:50 → 70. Click target is now ~56 px diameter.
  • Section labels + edges — quadrant heading font 13 → 18 with letter-spacing 1.5 → 2.5; portal lines bumped from 1.5 px to 2.2 px so they stay visible at the bigger scale.
  • Boss + tower corners re-anchored — margins bumped from ~40-75 px to ~60-110 px so larger boss / tower nodes still clear the legend bar at the bottom and the section labels at the top. Tower vertical step 70 → 100.
  • Legend — tier-colour swatches and labels scaled to match (r:4 → 5, font 9 → 12, item gap 100 → 130) so the legend reads cleanly at the new bottom bar (height:28 → 36).

v0.25.419 Emerald Village beside Jade Grove

featNew Eastern hamlet next door to Jade Grove, NPCs relocated

A new map, Emerald Village, sits east of the Jade Grove on the same Eastern-mysticism axis. Lady Hongyu, Yun, and Master Shen have moved out of the grove and into the village, so the grove now reads as a scenic approach (cherry blossoms, archery range, herb-garden tier — all empty of NPCs) and the three NPCs share a tighter conversational footprint inside the village proper.

  • New map — emeraldVillage with worldWidth:1900, levelReq:9, fixedLayout:true, empty spawns:[], taxi-accessible. Layout: western training yard for Yun, central plaza tier for Lady Hongyu's archery shrine, stilt-house porches over the eastern pond, Master Shen's herb-garden tier on the east slope.
  • Portals — new bidirectional link jadeGrove ↔ emeraldVillage: grove's right edge at x:2500, village return at x:80.
  • Decor reuse — Emerald Village sets both isJadeGrove:true and isEmeraldVillage:true, so it inherits the existing drawJadeGroveDecor() (cherry blossoms, stone toro lanterns, lotus, mist, incense burner) without a new draw function. Slightly warmer / greener sky — #cce8c8 → #e8f0d0 → #f0e8b8 — differentiates it visually.
  • NPCs — the three archer / sentinel / herbalist dialog branches and role configs are unchanged; only their npcs[] placement moved from jadeGrove to emeraldVillage. Yun is now the first thing you meet at the western gate; Hongyu holds the central plaza; Shen tends his herbs to the east.
  • World map — new 🏘 biome icon. Authored at wmAngle = -π/2 + 0.42 on the mid ring — sits visibly between Jade Grove and Granite Bluffs in the 🌳 EASTERN FOREST band of the new sectioned diagram. The whole northern row was re-spaced (lavaCavern / frozenPeak / skyGarden / jadeGrove / emeraldVillage / graniteBluffs) so the six nodes don't crowd.

v0.25.418 World map sectioned into four regions

polishQuadrant backdrops + region labels + forgiving background lookup

The W-key map was hard to read — nodes crowded one cardinal direction, the new background image often didn't load (filename guesswork), and there was no visual structure tying biomes to a region. Three fixes in one pass.

  • Four labelled regions — the diagram is now divided into four 90° pie wedges, each with a faint colour wash + a section heading at the outer edge:
    • 🌳 EASTERN FOREST (right) — forest, mushroom, candyCanyon, shadowSlums, bubblegumSwamp, ancient, wildflowerPlains.
    • 🛡 WESTERN STRONGHOLD (left) — bastion + throne + rampart, azureAcademia + abode.
    • ☁ NORTHERN HIGHLANDS (top) — skyGarden, jadeGrove, frozenPeak, lavaCavern, graniteBluffs, stardustAtrium, celestialSpire.
    • 🌅 SOUTHERN COAST (bottom) — sunsetBeach, tidalLagoon, duneSands, thunderPlateau, honeycombHollow, cryptHollow, pearlBathhouse.
    Faint dotted cross-hairs run through the centre as cardinal dividers.
  • Cleaner spacing — rewrote every wmAngle with explicit per-ring distribution. The previous authoring had several near-overlapping nodes on the mid ring (e.g. shadowSlums and graniteBluffs ~0.27 rad apart); each ring now has a clear minimum gap, and sub-clusters (Bastion's three rooms, Azure's two rooms, the cosmic spires) sit visibly grouped within their region.
  • Forgiving background lookup — the SVG now JS-probes a list of candidate filenames in order: bg_v3_worldMap_v2.png → .gif → .jpg → .webp → lowercase bg_v3_worldmap_v2.png → legacy bg_v3_worldMapInterface.gif. First one that loads wins; if none load, the radial-gradient parchment shows by itself. Drop your image in backgrounds/ with any of those names and it'll just work.

v0.25.417 World map size tune + new background art

polishSmaller modal footprint, higher-fidelity background illustration

v0.25.416's W-key map was a touch too dominating on desktop, and the old bg_v3_worldMapInterface.gif was visibly pixellated when scaled. Both fixed.

  • Modal size — tuned down from min-width:1100px; max-width:1400px; width:96vw; max-height:92vh to min-width:920px; max-width:1200px; width:88vw; max-height:88vh. Still big enough that the diagram is readable + clickable, small enough that the modal doesn't dominate the screen.
  • New background art — SVG style.cssText now points at backgrounds/bg_v3_worldMap_v2.png (pastel-cosmic illustration with floating islands, planets, soft clouds — higher-fidelity than the retired pixellated gif). The radial-gradient fallback is preserved if the png isn't found.
  • Lighter overlay — the dark-purple wash was tuned for the dim gif at rgba(10,6,24,0.45-0.55); lightened to rgba(10,6,24,0.18-0.22) so the new pastel palette reads cleanly without losing the night-sky atmosphere on top.
Manual step. Drop the new illustration into backgrounds/bg_v3_worldMap_v2.png before reloading. If the file isn't there, the SVG falls back to the radial-gradient backdrop and the diagram still works — just without the painted scene.

v0.25.416 World map blown up + real portal lines

polishBigger W-key world map with actual portal-link edges

The world-map modal is now sized for desktop (was a cramped 780-900 px), and the connection lines between nodes finally reflect the real portal graph instead of decorative hub-radiating spokes.

  • Modal size — bumped from min-width:780px; max-width:900px to min-width:1100px; max-width:1400px; width:96vw; max-height:92vh; overflow:auto. The SVG inside is width:100% with preserveAspectRatio:xMidYMid meet, so it scales up cleanly with the modal — nodes, labels, and tier rings all stay proportional. The portrait @media override (modal min-width:0 in landscape mobile) keeps phone layouts sensible.
  • Real portal lines — _renderWorldMapDiagram()'s edge pass now walks every map's portals[].dest, dedupes pairs (so an A↔B bidirectional link draws as one line), and skips destinations not present on the diagram (zodiac arenas, etc.). Stroke is rgba(200,170,240,0.50) at 1.5 px with linecap:round; nodes paint on top so disc edges read clean.
  • What you see now — the diagram reads as a connected graph: town's spokes only go where you can actually walk (Sunset Coast, Forest, Marketplace, Bastion, Tower); the Frozen Peak / Lava Cavern / Sanctum / Singularity chain shows as a connected northern arc; the new Bastion + Azure Acadamia + Jade Grove clusters sit on their own visible branches; and the v0.25.415 connectivity-pass links (forest↔wildflowerPlains, sunsetBeach↔tidalLagoon, lavaCavern↔graniteBluffs, duneSands↔thunderPlateau, sanctum↔celestialSpire) light up properly.
  • Tooltip update — the modal subtitle now says "Lines show walking portals between maps" so the new lines aren't mysterious.
  • Side benefit — the Taxi NPC modal uses the same _renderWorldMapDiagram(), so the Taxi diagram inherits the same bigger size and real portal lines for free.

v0.25.415 World map flow + connectivity pass

featHand-authored world-map angles + walking links to every map

Two changes that together make the W-key map mirror the actual world graph: (1) a per-map wmAngle override so connected biomes cluster on the diagram, and (2) ten new walking portals that put every previously taxi-only map onto a logical chain.

  • Connectivity — five biomes were previously reachable only by taxi (no incoming walking portals): Wildflower Plains, Tidal Lagoon, Granite Bluffs, Thunder Plateau, and Celestial Spire. Each now has a bidirectional walking link to a thematic neighbour:
    • forest ↔ wildflowerPlains (forest meadow continuation)
    • sunsetBeach ↔ tidalLagoon (coastal continuation)
    • lavaCavern ↔ graniteBluffs (rocky highlands)
    • duneSands ↔ thunderPlateau (desert plateau)
    • sanctum ↔ celestialSpire (cosmic ascent)
    Every non-zodiac map now has at least one incoming walking portal — verified by parsing the portal graph and checking the reverse index.
  • World-map flow — _wmComputePositions()'s placeRing() now honours an optional wmAngle on each map. When set (in radians: 0 = east, π/2 = south, π = west, -π/2 = north), the map ignores its sort-by-levelReq slot and anchors at the authored angle. Maps without an angle still auto-sort within their tier bucket.
  • Authored quadrants — ~25 maps now carry an explicit wmAngle so connected biomes line up:
    • East (forest path): forest → mushroom → candyCanyon → shadowSlums, plus wildflowerPlains, ancient, bubblegumSwamp.
    • West (knights + arcane): bastion + throne + rampart, azureAcademia + abode.
    • North (highlands): skyGarden → jadeGrove, frozenPeak → lavaCavern, graniteBluffs, plus the cosmic spires (stardustAtrium, celestialSpire).
    • South (coast + desert): sunsetBeach + tidalLagoon, duneSands → thunderPlateau → cryptHollow + honeycombHollow + pearlBathhouse.

v0.25.414 The Jade Grove map

featNew Eastern sanctuary east of the Sky Garden

A new tranquil sanctuary, The Jade Grove, sits on the eastern fringe of the Sky Garden — stilt-houses over koi ponds, paper screens, thatched roofs, a sacred archery range surrounded by cherry blossoms, and a central plaza built around a massive stone incense burner. Three new NPCs in the Eastern-mysticism palette (jade / vermilion / moss / misty white). Press W to fast-travel.

  • New map — jadeGrove with worldWidth:2600, levelReq:9, fixedLayout:true, empty spawns:[], taxi-accessible. Layout: marsh walkway approach, archery-range platform (Hongyu), central plaza with the incense burner, stilt-house porches, herb-garden tier (Shen), eastern descent, plus high cherry-blossom canopy walks.
  • Entry portal — new skyGarden portal at x:2400 labelled ▶ The Jade Grove (sits just inside the existing x:2530 Frozen Peak portal). Grove returns to the Sky Garden at x:80.
  • World map — new biome icon 🌸 registered in _WM_BIOME_ICON. Lands on the mid-tier ring on W alongside the Sky Garden.
  • NPCs — three new dialog branches and three new role configs:
    • Lady Hongyu, the Divine Archer (archer role, archery-range platform) — crimson-and-white hanfu + jade hairpins + vermilion-light longbow; Receive blessing ✨ opens the level-up panel.
    • Master Shen, the Root-Singer (herbalist role, herb-garden tier) — hemp robes, wicker basket of fungi + glowing herbs; Buy potions opens the potion shop, plus a "listen for the heartbeat" lore branch.
    • Yun, the Jade Sentinel (sentinel role, central plaza) — forest-green lamellar + jade-tipped spear + fox mask at his temple; Inspect my gear opens the gear shop, Train with me ✨ opens the level-up panel.
  • Custom biome decor — drawJadeGroveDecor() layers six elements: drifting morning mist along the marsh, parallax cherry-blossom trees with vermilion accent blossoms, moss-overgrown stone toro lanterns with warm-amber window glow, a massive central stone incense burner with rising smoke plume curls + glowing embers, floating lotus flowers on the pond, and foreground drifting cherry petals.
  • Atmosphere — sky #d8e8d8 → #f0d8d0 → #f8e8c8 (misty white-green dawn → blossom pink → soft cream). Strict jade / vermilion / moss / misty-white palette.
  • Sprite slots pre-wired — NPC_SPRITE_FILES already references lady_hongyu.webp / master_shen.webp / yun.webp under Sprites/npc/. Until those Ludo.ai-generated sprites are dropped in, the loader silently fails and the three NPCs fall back to procedural rendering off their new role configs. Generation prompts live in JADE_GROVE_LUDO_PROMPTS.md.

v0.25.413 The Azure Acadamia map

featNew 2-map arcane hub west of the Tidal Lagoon

A new arcane hub, The Azure Acadamia, sits on the western shore of the Tidal Lagoon — a sprawling magical city of slender spires and domed libraries built on ancient ruins, plus a separate floating bubble-abode where the leader Hera lives. Three new NPCs, custom blue/silver/white decor, and a glowing fountain centrepiece. Press W to fast-travel.

  • Two connected maps — azureAcademia (central hub with fountain plaza, crafting district platforms, lecture-hall steps, rune-bark tree forest, worldWidth:2400) and azureAbode (Hera's small floating bubble-room with cosy furnished tiers, worldWidth:1100). Both levelReq:10, fixedLayout:true, empty spawns:[], taxi-accessible.
  • Entry portal — new tidalLagoon portal at x:200 labelled ◀ The Azure Acadamia (sits just inside the existing x:80 town portal). Internal portal: courtyard→abode at x:760, abode return at x:80.
  • World map — two new biome icons (🔮 🩧) registered in _WM_BIOME_ICON. Both land on the mid-tier ring (lvl 5-14) on the W-key map alongside the Tidal Lagoon.
  • NPCs — three new dialog branches and three new role configs:
    • Hera, the Azure Archmage (archmage role, floating abode) — sapphire silk + silver embroidery + crystal-orb staff; Tutelage ✨ opens the level-up panel.
    • Auron, the Scholarly Elder (scholar role, library) — layered grey-blue robes + white beard + glowing-blue-ink quill; Train stats → skills modal, Level up ✨ → level-up panel.
    • Lyra, the Aspiring Sorceress (apprentice role, training plaza) — sky-blue tunic + spiky light-blue hair; conjures a small ball of mana with sputtering smoke for the player.
  • Custom biome decor — drawAzureAcademiaDecor() handles both maps in one pass: pervasive soft blue magical aura, parallax rune-bark trees with glowing canopies (Acadamia only), tall mana-lantern posts with glowing bulbs, an animated central fountain with a rising plume + halo, drifting magical motes (sparser in the abode), and tiny sprite familiars tracing figure-8 paths. The abode adds a pulsating bubble envelope with specular gloss instead of trees / lanterns.
  • Atmosphere — Acadamia sky #1a2858 → #3a5aa8 → #88c8f0 (sapphire deep-night → cobalt → sky blue), abode sky #0a1838 → #1a3a78 → #4a78c8 (deep night sapphire → sky-glow). Strict blue / silver / white palette throughout.
  • Sprite slots pre-wired — NPC_SPRITE_FILES already references hera.webp / auron.webp / lyra.webp under Sprites/npc/. Until those Ludo.ai-generated sprites are dropped in, the loader silently fails and the three NPCs fall back to procedural rendering off their new role configs. Generation prompts live in AZURE_ACADAMIA_LUDO_PROMPTS.md.

v0.25.412 The Bastion knight stronghold

featNew 3-map heroic stronghold west of Everdawn Bazaar

A new fortress complex, The Bastion, sits on the western flank of Everdawn Bazaar — sapphire-and-gold knightly architecture with marble walls, blue-tiled conical roofs, lion-embossed gates, and zero monster spawns. Three connected maps with three new NPCs. Press W to fast-travel to any of them.

  • Three connected maps — bastion (central training courtyard, worldWidth:2400), bastionThrone (cathedral throne room, worldWidth:1800), and bastionRampart (defensive walls overlooking the green plains, worldWidth:2200). All three carry levelReq:2, fixedLayout:true, empty spawns:[], and taxiAccessible:true.
  • Entry portal — new town portal at x:200 labelled ◀ The Bastion (sits just inside the existing x:60 Sunset-Coast portal, so the two left exits read as side-by-side gates).
  • Internal portals — courtyard branches up at x:1200 to the throne room and right at x:2300 to the rampart; both sub-maps have a return portal to the courtyard at x:80.
  • World map — three new biome icons (🛡 👑 ⚔) registered in _WM_BIOME_ICON. All three appear on the early-tier ring on W, alongside Emerald Thicket and Sunset Coast.
  • NPCs — three new dialog branches and two new role configs:
    • Will, the Undefeated Champion (champion role, throne dais) — sapphire plate + gold plume; calm booming dialog; routes Train with me ✨ to the level-up panel.
    • Elena, Master of Records (scribe role, records desk in the throne room) — white robes + silver chainmail + phoenix-feather quill; Train stats opens the skills modal, Level up ✨ opens the level-up panel.
    • Barnaby, the Iron Anvil (existing weapon role, courtyard forge) — reuses the gear shop / sell / enhance / craft dialog automatically.
  • Atmosphere — courtyard sky #cce8f8 → #ffe8b0 → #a8d8a8 (sun-drenched marble over green plains), throne room #1a2050 → #3a3a78 → #6a5a98 (cathedral indigo & twilight gold), rampart #88c8f0 → #cce8ff → #d8e8a8 (bright cumulus over the plains).
  • Sprite slots pre-wired — NPC_SPRITE_FILES already references will.webp / elena.webp / barnaby.webp under Sprites/npc/. Until those Ludo.ai-generated sprites are dropped in, the loader silently fails and the three NPCs fall back to procedural rendering off their new role configs. Generation prompts live in BASTION_LUDO_PROMPTS.md.

v0.25.411 Shadow-Woven Slums map

featNew violet shadow-magic biome east of Sweet Candy Canyon

A new map, The Shadow-Woven Slums, sits on the eastern rim of Sweet Candy Canyon — a sprawling ghetto city built atop a ruined feudal-Japanese capital. Three rebel-faction NPCs hold court in the alleys: Taiga the ninjutsu leader, Kuro the shadow samurai, and Ren the alleyway thief. Press W to fast-travel from the World Map.

  • Entry portal — new candyCanyon portal at x:2380 labelled ▶ Shadow-Woven Slums; the slums map carries a left exit back to the canyon at x:80.
  • World map — the new biome registers in _WM_BIOME_ICON with the 🥷 ninja icon and renders on the mid-tier ring (levelReq:7, same ring as Candy Canyon). The map intentionally avoids isTown:true — that would shove it into the hub bucket which only places one node at the centre, and the new map would never render. Taxi-accessible too.
  • Atmosphere — deep violet sky (#0d0418 → #1f0d3a → #3a1a5e), procedural pagoda silhouettes, neon-purple swaying paper lanterns, drifting indigo mist, floating violet spirit-orbs, and a faded violet kanji-ish graffiti tag on a wall panel.
  • NPCs — three new role configs (taiga, kuro, ren) with deep-violet body palettes and a custom dialog branch each. Empty spawns:[] keeps the slums monster-free (rebel hub, not a combat zone), and fixedLayout:true skips the per-load platform jitter so the pagodas line up with the rooftops every visit.
  • Layout — worldWidth:2200, mountain-shaped platform tiers around two pagoda rooftops where Taiga and Kuro stand, plus high lantern-beam jumps for explorers.
  • Sprite slots pre-wired — NPC_SPRITE_FILES already references taiga.webp / kuro.webp / ren.webp under Sprites/npc/. Until those Ludo.ai-generated sprites are dropped in, the loader silently fails and the three NPCs fall back to the procedural draw path keyed off their new role configs. Generation prompts live in SHADOW_SLUMS_LUDO_PROMPTS.md.

v0.25.410 Wardrobe layer head cleanup

fixLayer-only previews no longer draw the legacy head fallback

The wardrobe paint layer renderer now only draws the procedural head fallback when the actual skin layer is being rendered.

  • Layer isolation - hair, clothing, limb, weapon, and hat layer previews no longer inherit the old head-circle fallback.
  • Paint masks - raw layer masks used by the translucent editor guide now stay limited to the selected layer.
  • Skin preserved - the skin layer still renders the current head sprite or its procedural fallback when appropriate.

v0.25.409 Wardrobe weapon preview alignment

fixWeapon previews now share the in-game carry pose

The Q wardrobe, weapon paint editor guide, and weapon slot thumbnail now render the weapon through the same left-hand horizontal carry pose used by the live character.

  • Paint pose - the locked wardrobe paint pose now applies the same weapon carry rotation as idle, walk, jump, and rogue dash.
  • Weapon tile - the wardrobe weapon slot thumbnail now renders through the bone-pinned vector hero path instead of showing the raw sprite alone.
  • Weapon size preserved - no weapon dimensions or scales were changed; only preview pose alignment was adjusted.

v0.25.408 Map background remap

polishMaps now use the supplied v3 background files

The live map background registry now points at the supplied Bazaar and Marketplace files instead of the deleted homestead/camelCase Bazaar paths.

  • Everdawn Bazaar - now loads backgrounds/bg_v3_everdawn bazaar.png for first paint and in-game rendering.
  • Everdawn Marketplace - now has its own everdawnMarketplace background key backed by backgrounds/bg_v3_everdawnmarketplace.png.
  • Deleted slots avoided - the old bg_v3_homestead.png and bg_v3_everdawnBazaar.png references are removed from the runtime registry.

v0.25.407 Wardrobe layer asset cleanup

cleanupRemoved retired paint-wardrobe layer assets

The old mannequin/base, layer-eyes, and layer-hair files from the retired wardrobe paint prototype are now removed from the live repo.

  • Wardrobe layers - deleted obsolete assets under Sprites/character/layers/base, layers/eyes, and layers/hair.
  • Legacy helpers - removed the two preview scripts that referenced those deleted layer files.
  • Runtime protected - background deletions were not included because the current game still loads those filenames.

v0.25.406 Rogue D dash pose

polishRogue D Flurry uses the dash silhouette

The rogue D skill now plays through the existing ninja dash pose during its blink-strike window.

  • Flurry pose - casting rogue D stamps the same dash-pose timer used by double-tap dash.
  • Attack override - the renderer now lets that dash pose win over the generic rogue attack pose while the cast is active.
  • Behavior preserved - Flurry teleport, cooldown, damage, and weapon size are unchanged.

v0.25.405 Rogue S jump height

polishRogue S launches higher

The rogue S throw-dagger somersault now kicks upward higher so the flip has more visible airtime.

  • Higher launch - increased the throw-dagger somersault kickoff velocity from -8 to -10.5.
