  • Animation preserved - flip timing, head tuck, dagger fan, and weapon sizing are unchanged.

v0.25.404 Rogue dash head offset

polishRogue dash head sits closer to the body

The rogue dash still shifts the head slightly right with the movement, but the horizontal offset is now much smaller.

  • Head position - reduced the launch, smear, and brake head-right offsets in the rogue dash pose.
  • Pose preserved - body lean, arm remap, crisp head rendering, and dash timing are unchanged.

v0.25.403 Rogue S somersault head tuck

polishRogue S flip keeps the head more compact

The rogue S throw-dagger somersault now tucks the head slightly closer to the body through the middle of the forward flip.

  • Compact flip - added a short throw-somersault tuck flag that only affects the rogue S animation window.
  • Head position - nudges the attack head bob downward toward the torso during the flip peak.
  • Movement preserved - somersault rotation, skill velocity, and dagger/weapon sizing are unchanged.

v0.25.402 Rogue dash direction pose

polishRogue dash leans and opens to the right

The rogue ninja dash pose now angles the body rightward, shifts the head right, swaps the right arm into the old swept-arm motion, and opens the left arm away from the torso.

  • Rightward body angle - launch and smear frames now use a positive/rightward spine lean.
  • Head offset - the head keeps its crisp counter-rotation but moves horizontally to the visual right during the dash.
  • Arm remap - the right arm now follows the previous left-arm dash sweep, while the left arm swings farther outward from the torso.

v0.25.401 Rogue dash head stability

polishRogue dash keeps the head crisp

The rogue ninja dash now keeps the head mostly steady, with only a small rightward rotation bob while the body performs the dash smear.

  • Minimal head movement - reduced the dash head offsets to tiny local bobs instead of large launch/smear shifts.
  • Subtle rotation - the head counter-rotates the torso so the visible head angle stays slight and right-leaning.
  • No head distortion - head, hair, skin paint, and helmet paint opt out of the dash squash/stretch.

v0.25.400 Rogue loaded stab animation

polishRogue attack now pulls back before lunging

The rogue basic attack now visibly loads the left weapon arm behind the body before snapping into a fast forward dagger thrust.

  • Left-arm pullback - the weapon arm now rotates to a clear backward anticipation angle before the strike.
  • Quick thrust - the arm, wrist, dagger angle, and body lunge now snap forward together through the stab beat.
  • Trail timing - the straight thrust streak now starts after the pullback so the hit reads as a sudden release.
  • Weapon size preserved - dagger dimensions and left-hand pivot remain unchanged.

v0.25.399 Warrior ground smash animation

polishWarrior S gets a heavy two-handed smash

The warrior S ground slam now reads as a weighty five-beat attack: slow wind-up, held apex, violent release, impact hold, and labored recovery.

  • Grounded wind-up - plants the stance wide and shifts the body back before lifting the weapon overhead.
  • Apex and release - holds the weapon high briefly, then snaps into a fast downward smash with a curved smear frame.
  • Impact hold - crouches into the hit, keeps the weapon stopped at the ground, and adds local strike/debris accents.
  • Weapon size preserved - the actual weapon layer remains fixed-size at the left-hand pivot; the big arc is a separate VFX pass.

v0.25.398 Rogue dash smear and calmer walk arms

polishThief dash now has anticipation, launch, smear, and brake poses

The rogue/thief double-tap dash now plays as a sharper four-beat ninja dash, while walking arms swing with smaller, calmer arcs.

  • Walk arms - reduced both arm swing amplitudes so walking reads less exaggerated.
  • Dash sequence - keyed anticipation, launch, smear/apex, and brake/recovery poses for the rogue quick dash.
  • Smear frame - stretches the character body and limbs horizontally during the dash apex while keeping the weapon fixed-size at the left-hand pivot.
  • Brake dust - adds a small foot-plant dust burst near the recovery beat.

v0.25.397 Weapon depth order

polishWeapon layer now sits between torso and left arm

The character weapon stack now draws in front of the torso/body clothing while staying behind the left arm and left-hand ellipse.

  • Vector order - procedural, equipped, and painted weapon layers now render after torso/body layers and before armL.
  • Paint output - wardrobe paint compositing now matches the same body â†’ weapon â†’ left arm depth order.
  • Weapon size preserved - weapon dimensions, scale, and left-hand pivot are unchanged.

v0.25.396 Rogue quick dash pose

polishDouble-tap thief dash gets a ninja silhouette

Rogue/thief double-tap movement now triggers a short ninja dash pose: body leaned forward, arms swept back, and legs opened into a faster running silhouette.

  • Double tap - the existing quick dash now stamps a rogue-only pose timer when it fires.
  • Ninja silhouette - added a dedicated vector rig pose for the dash without scaling limbs.
  • Weapon size preserved - the weapon still renders from the left-hand ellipse pivot and keeps its existing dimensions.

v0.25.395 Horizontal left-hand weapon carry

polishFree right arm and left-hand weapon hold tuned

The right arm now sits 1px closer to the body and swings more naturally as the free hand, while the left-hand weapon carry pose holds the weapon horizontally from the left-hand ellipse.

  • Right arm - shifted the right arm pivot 1px left and increased its idle/walk swing.
  • Left-hand weapon - procedural, equipped, and painted weapon layers rotate around handL into a horizontal carry pose for idle, walk, and jump.
  • Weapon size preserved - weapon dimensions and scale are unchanged; only pivot position and hold rotation changed.

v0.25.394 Left-hand weapon anchor

polishWeapons now pivot from the left hand

The vector character's procedural weapon, equipped weapon sprite, and painted weapon layer now anchor to the left-hand ellipse instead of the right hand.

  • New pivot - added a handL bone and moved the visible left-hand ellipse onto it.
  • Animation remap - warrior, mage, rogue, generic, and archer weapon motions now drive from the left arm, with the right arm acting as counter/string hand.
  • Warrior tilt - kept the sword's 20 degree outward tilt relative to the new left-hand side.
  • Weapon size preserved - weapon dimensions and scale are unchanged; only the anchor bone, offsets, and arm animation mapping changed.

v0.25.393 Exaggerated thief stab

polishRogue attack now stabs harder

The thief/rogue vector attack has a more exaggerated stabbing read, with a deeper windup, sharper forward thrust, stronger counter-arm motion, and a longer straight thrust trail.

  • Stab pose - increased the torso lunge and front-arm extension so the dagger drives forward more clearly.
  • Counter motion - the back arm now pulls back and drives forward harder to sell the full-body stab.
  • Weapon size preserved - dagger dimensions and scale are unchanged; only rig rotations, body offset, and the attack trail changed.

v0.25.392 Jump right-leg width

polishRight leg opens slightly more while jumping

The character's right leg now angles a little wider during the rising jump pose, while the existing falling pose remains unchanged.

  • Jump pose - widened the right-leg jump angle from -0.32 to -0.38.
  • Fall pose preserved - kept the wider falling right-leg angle from the previous tuning pass.

v0.25.391 Leg and fall pose tuning

polishLeft leg, walk arm, and fall pose adjusted

The character rig and movement poses got small alignment tweaks for the left leg, right-arm walking swing, and falling silhouette.

  • Left leg - shifted the left leg pivot 1px left.
  • Walk right arm - reduced the right arm's inward/body-crossing angle during the walk cycle.
  • Falling pose - opened the left arm outward and widened the right leg slightly.

v0.25.390 Weapon hand pivot

fixWeapon base pivots from the right hand ellipse

The visible right-hand ellipse now shares the exact handR bone as the weapon, so the weapon base and uploaded weapon layer rotate around the hand centre.

  • Single pivot - right hand ellipse, class weapon, and weapon paint layer now use the same anchor point.
  • No weapon resizing - weapon dimensions and scale are unchanged.
  • Animation-safe - attack and walk rotations pivot around that hand point instead of drifting from the ellipse.

v0.25.389 Moving attack legs

polishMoving attacks now walk with the legs only

When the character attacks while continuing to move on the ground, the upper body keeps the attack animation while the legs switch to the live walking gait.

  • Split-body attack - regular generic, warrior, rogue, mage, and archer attacks now borrow walk leg rotations while moving.
  • Attached hips - moving attack legs still inherit the attack body's lunge offset, preventing hip separation.
  • Stationary attacks unchanged - planted attack stances remain active when the character is not moving.

v0.25.388 Warrior hip-pivot sync

fixWarrior attack legs stay attached to the torso

The warrior swing now applies the same attack body-bob to the leg pivots as it does to the torso, so the hips stay connected through windup, impact, and recovery.

  • Shared pivot motion - torso and both upper-leg pivots now use the same horizontal and vertical lunge offset.
  • Footwork retained - the existing warrior stance keys still add their per-leg brace and compression on top of the shared hip motion.
  • Weapon size preserved - sword dimensions and scale were not changed.

v0.25.387 Rogue stab motion

polishRogue attacks now thrust the dagger tip forward

The rogue attack animation now reads as a direct stab instead of a sweeping cut, with the dagger point driving toward the target and a narrow thrust streak replacing the slash arc.

  • Forward dagger point - the weapon hand now keeps the blade tip aimed into the opponent through the stab window.
  • Thrusting arm - the front arm extends into the jab while the off-hand counters the motion.
  • No weapon resizing - dagger dimensions remain unchanged; only rotations, timing, and trail shape were adjusted.

v0.25.386 Attack leg sync

polishGeneric and rogue attack legs stay connected to the torso

Generic and rogue attack animations now use keyed leg poses with matching bob and deform offsets, so the hips, torso, and feet move as one braced stance.

  • Generic attack - front leg plants through the strike while the rear leg stays under the body.
  • Rogue attack - lunge footwork now follows the torso thrust and big-stab variant without separating at the hips.
  • Leg bob/deform - added small per-leg lift and compression so the knees and feet stay visually connected during impact and recovery.

v0.25.385 Level warrior C-skill sprite

polishRush sprite no longer tilts or spins

The warrior C-key Rush effect now stays level when used, including when facing left.

  • No angle tilt - removed rotation from all Rush sprite bursts.
  • No spin - removed the rotating motion from the main Rush burst.
  • Left-facing support - added sprite-burst horizontal mirroring so Rush can face left without rotating 180 degrees.

v0.25.384 Warrior swing footwork

polishWarrior legs now brace with the wide attack swing

The warrior attack now has keyed leg positions that load into the windup, plant through impact, and settle during recovery so the two-handed swing reads as a full-body motion.

  • Windup stance - rear leg extends back while the front leg plants before the sword comes over.
  • Impact brace - front leg compresses through the strike while the rear leg pushes behind it.
  • Recovery settle - both legs ease back from the committed wide stance after follow-through.

v0.25.383 Full oval outlines and arm lift

polishHand and foot outlines restored, right arm lifted

The hand and foot oval outlines are back to full opacity, and the right arm pivot has moved 1px upward so its attached hand follows with it.

  • Oval outlines - restored hand and foot ellipse strokes to 100% opacity.
  • Right arm lift - moved the right arm bone up by 1px, carrying the attached hand and hand-pinned layers with it.

v0.25.382 Softer hand and foot ovals

polishHand and foot ellipse outlines are less harsh

The small oval outlines on the character's hands and feet now stroke at 30% opacity while keeping their filled shape unchanged.

  • Hands softened - both hand oval outlines now use the lighter stroke.
  • Feet softened - both foot oval outlines now use the lighter stroke.

v0.25.381 Jump fall pose

polishJumping and falling read as separate poses

The vector jump animation now distinguishes rising, apex, and falling so the character braces more naturally on the way down.

  • Right leg lift - moved the right leg bone 2px upward during the jump animation.
  • Falling pose - added a dedicated falling posture with arms slightly opened, legs braced, and a subtle body drop.
  • Head shape protected - kept the head counter-scale in place while the body compresses lightly during fall.

v0.25.380 Slower walk cycle

polishArms and legs move through walking poses more calmly

The walking animation cycle is slightly slower, letting the coordinated arm and leg poses read more naturally without changing the player's actual movement speed.

  • Walk cycle pace - reduced the vector walk animation rate by about 14%.
  • Movement speed preserved - only animation timing changed; character travel speed is untouched.

v0.25.379 Smaller torso and right leg depth

polishTorso silhouette tightened and right leg tucks behind

The character torso is now slightly smaller while keeping its hip anchor stable, and the right leg layer renders behind the torso in both vector and painted-layer output.

  • Smaller torso - narrowed the torso and lowered the shoulder shape slightly without shifting the feet or head rig.
  • Right leg depth - moved the right leg and its custom paint layer before the torso pass so the body covers the upper thigh.
  • Paint order matched - updated the composite paint render order so wardrobe output follows the same depth stack.

v0.25.378 Coordinated walk legs

polishWalk cycle now follows contact, down, passing, and up poses

The character's walk legs now use a keyed gait cycle instead of a simple sine swing, so each step has a clearer heel strike, weight drop, passing leg, toe push, and opposite contact.

  • Widest contact stance - one leg reaches forward while the rear leg extends behind.
  • Weight transfer - the body dips at the down pose and rises at the push-off pose.
  • Swing leg lift - the passing leg lifts and shortens slightly to read more like a bent knee.
  • Weapon scale preserved - weapon dimensions and render scale were not changed.

v0.25.377 Slim torso and grip stack

polishRight hand, weapon, and arm stack cleanly

The right-side arm stack now separates the hand oval from the arm limb so the weapon sits between them, while the torso stays in front.

  • Slimmer torso - narrowed the torso sides and lower body so it reads less egg-like.
  • Broader shoulders - widened the shoulder points by about 1px while keeping the hip anchor stable.
  • Right-side layer order - right arm draws first, weapon/equipped weapon draws next, right hand draws over the grip, then the torso draws in front.
  • Paint order matched - moved the weapon custom layer between right arm and torso in the composite order.
  • Weapon scale preserved - weapon dimensions, hand anchor, and class weapon render sizes are unchanged.

v0.25.376 Torso silhouette and arm layer

polishRight arm now sits behind the torso

The vector right-arm layer now renders behind the torso, and the torso silhouette has been reshaped into a rounded sleeveless form closer to the supplied reference.

  • Right arm behind torso - moved the right-arm base and right-arm paint layer earlier in the body stack.
  • Matched paint order - updated the custom paint compositing order so wardrobe/editor layer output follows the same stack.
  • New torso shape - replaced the pear torso with a taller rounded side-facing body shape while keeping the hip anchor stable.
  • Weapon scale preserved - weapon renderer dimensions and weapon anchoring are unchanged.

v0.25.375 Lower limb feet

polishLeg layers now have simple foot ovals

The vector lower-limb layers now draw small horizontal ellipses at the leg tips so cleared or edited leg layers read more like feet instead of plain rounded sticks.

  • Both legs covered - added matching foot ellipses to the back and front leg layers.
  • Tip-pinned placement - the foot ovals use the same limb-tip Y position as the corrected hand ovals.
  • No scale changes - character rig, leg pivots, and weapon dimensions are untouched.

v0.25.374 Arm hand placement

fixHand circles now sit at the arm tips

The vector hand ovals were drawn too high inside the arm limb, making them look like extra circles embedded in the arm. They now sit down at the end of both arms.

  • Lowered hands - moved both arm hand ellipses from y=9.2 to the limb-tip position.
  • Both arms fixed - applies to the back arm and front weapon arm.
  • Weapon scale preserved - only hand oval placement changed; weapon dimensions are untouched.

v0.25.373 Asymmetric walk arm contacts

polishWalking arm contact angles tuned

The walk cycle now uses separate right-arm and left-arm keyframes so each contact pose can have its own requested swing amount.

  • First contact - right arm swings forward by 30deg, while the left arm swings backward by 45deg.
  • Opposite contact - left arm swings forward by 20deg, while the right arm swings backward by 35deg.
  • Passing poses retained - both arms still return through the body-centre crossing poses between contacts.

v0.25.372 Keyframed walk arm cycle

polishWalking arms now hit contact and passing poses cleanly

The vector walk arms now use explicit contact/passing key poses instead of a loose sine phase. Contact frames put the right arm forward at 45 degrees while the left arm swings back, passing frames bring both arms across the body centre, then the next contact reverses the pair.

  • Contact poses - right arm forward / left arm backward, then left arm forward / right arm backward on the opposite step.
  • Passing poses - arms cross back through the body centre between contacts instead of floating open and closed rhythmically.
  • Weapon size preserved - the weapon hand only gets a tiny rotation lag; weapon scale and dimensions remain unchanged.

v0.25.371 Walk arm phase fix

fixWalking arms no longer open and close together

The vector walk cycle now accounts for the rig's left/right shoulder spacing, so one arm moves inward while the other moves outward instead of both swinging away from the torso and back in together.

  • Corrected arm phase - the front arm no longer uses a mirrored Math.PI phase offset that made both hands open/close rhythmically.
  • Natural visual alternation - both shoulders now share the main pendulum timing, with a tiny front-arm lag so the screen silhouette alternates more like walking.
  • Weapon size preserved - only arm and hand rotations changed; weapon scale and authored dimensions are untouched.

v0.25.370 Walk arm swing polish

polishWalking arms now swing like a natural side-view cycle

The vector walk animation now uses a pendulum-style arm curve inspired by the reference silhouette: arms hang lower, alternate cleanly against the legs, and ease through the forward/back extremes.

  • Natural arm timing - arm swing now holds briefly at the forward/back ends instead of moving like a plain sine wave.
  • Less over-motion - shoulder wobble and weapon-hand lag are smaller so the walk reads closer to a simple side-view stride.
  • Weapon size preserved - the walk change only adjusts rotations; weapon scale and authored dimensions remain untouched.

v0.25.369 Legacy head-circle cleanup

fixOld baked head circles are scrubbed from every paint layer

Wardrobe paint layers now remove the legacy baked head-circle pixels from saved layer images instead of only cleaning the protected skin layer.

  • All layers covered - hair, clothing, cape, limbs, weapon, and hat layer saves now pass through the same head-circle cleanup.
  • Artwork preserved - the scrub compares against the old head pixels and removes only matching baked head colours, leaving other uploaded or drawn layer art intact.
  • Existing saves migrated - player paint layers are cleaned in the background and wardrobe thumbnails refresh as layers are scrubbed.

v0.25.368 Skin-tone limbs

polishArm and leg layers now match the head skin tone

The vector arms and legs now use the same selected skin palette as the head, while the torso stays as the clean white T-shirt shape from v0.25.367.

  • Arms match skin - both arm layer bases now use the head skin base, midtone, and deep shadow.
  • Legs match skin - both leg layer bases now use the head skin palette instead of boot/body colours.
  • Cleaner limb detail - the small lower-leg detail now uses the skin shadow colour instead of a black shoe-like strip.

v0.25.367 Clean white torso

polishTorso now reads as a clean white T-shirt

The vector torso keeps the same pear-shaped silhouette but no longer draws the tiny black-and-gold chest/belt accents that looked like stray pixels when limb layers were cleared.

  • Excess pixels removed - removed the hardcoded black strip and gold rectangle from the torso render.
  • Same torso shape - the existing torso path, outline, and rig position are unchanged.
  • White T-shirt palette - bare torso fill now uses white with soft grey cel shading.

v0.25.366 Cleaner paint-layer clearing

fixCleared limb layers no longer show their own guide lines

The wardrobe paint editor now hides the active layer from the translucent guide, so clearing a limb does not leave that limb's old outline visible underneath the editable canvas.

  • Active layer hidden from guide - arm, leg, skin, hair, and equipment paint panels show surrounding context without drawing the layer currently being edited.
  • Legacy skin cleanup - old skin-layer saves that contain the baked base head circle are detected and removed when the skin layer opens.
  • Custom overlays preserved - small skin doodles or uploads that do not match the old base-head silhouette still load as editable paint.

v0.25.365 Alternating walk arms

polishWalking hand swing feels more natural

The vector character walk cycle now uses eased alternating arm swings instead of a plain mirrored sine, so the hands move with a softer forward swing and a stronger backswing.

  • Alternating arms - left and right arms remain opposite-phase so the hands counter the stepping legs.
  • More realistic timing - walk arms now ease through the stride with a small follow-through instead of rotating evenly like a metronome.
  • Weapon dimensions preserved - the weapon hand gets only a tiny wrist lag while walking; weapon scale and authored sizes are unchanged.

v0.25.364 Protected skin paint layer

fixSkin painter no longer edits the original base art

The wardrobe paint editor now treats the skin layer as protected base artwork. Skin paint remains useful for overlays, but the original head and skin image is no longer loaded into the editable paint canvas or hidden by saved skin paint.

  • Base skin protected - opening the skin paint layer starts with a transparent editable canvas over the guide instead of the original skin art.
  • Overlay-only skin paint - saved skin-layer drawings render on top of the base skin rather than replacing it.
  • Clear behaves cleanly - clearing an empty skin paint layer removes the custom overlay instead of saving a transparent replacement.

v0.25.363 NPC fallback freeze fix

fixMissing NPC sprites no longer freeze the game loop

A v0.25.358 paint-layer condition leaked into the procedural NPC fallback. If an NPC sprite was missing or not decoded yet, the fallback referenced vector-hero-only helpers and crashed the render loop at frame 1.

  • Fallback restored - procedural NPC drawing now runs independently of character paint-layer helpers.
  • Freeze fixed - maps with missing or delayed NPC sprites no longer stop at game.time = 1.
  • Paint behavior retained - wardrobe replacement-layer behavior from v0.25.358 remains scoped to the player vector renderer.

v0.25.362 Loader reveal fix

fixGame no longer gets hidden behind the loading screen

The loading screen now gates only on first-play Bazaar and critical UI art. Deferred late-game sprites continue loading silently in the background, so missing or slow optional art cannot make the game appear frozen.

  • Playable reveal restored - the world appears once Bazaar and critical UI assets finish or fail gracefully.
  • Deferred sprites unblocked - boss, biome, master-skill, and less common wardrobe assets no longer block startup.
  • Compact flip retained - the Archer C somersault tuning from v0.25.360 remains in place.

v0.25.361 Somersault safety fix

fixGame loop stays stable after compact flip tuning

The somersault renderer no longer depends on the later vector-animation clamp helper, and every flip source now records its own duration explicitly.

  • Self-contained renderer - the world draw transform clamps flip progress inline so the main game loop cannot trip on helper ordering.
  • All flips declare duration - warrior, rogue, master, and archer somersaults now set matching somersaultDuration values.
  • Load cleanup - transient somersault state is cleared when loading a save, preventing stale mid-flip state from carrying into boot.

v0.25.360 Compact Archer C flip

polishEvade Burst somersault tightened

Archer C / Evade Burst now performs a shorter, lower somersault so the flip reads tighter while keeping the dodge distance, blast, and weapon dimensions unchanged.

  • Shorter spin - archer flip duration reduced from 520ms to 420ms.
  • Lower launch - upward velocity softened from -11 to -8 for a more compact arc.
  • Clean timing - somersault rendering now respects per-flip durations, so the shortened archer flip starts at the correct angle.

v0.25.359 Right limb micro-positioning

polishRight leg and right arm nudged into place

The character rig now shifts the right-side leg and arm by the requested single-pixel offsets.

  • Right leg - moved legR 1px right, from x:4 to x:5.
  • Right arm - moved armR 1px right and 1px down, from x:13,y:-22 to x:14,y:-21.
  • Pins follow - attached right-arm paint and the child weapon hand inherit the new arm position.

v0.25.358 Editable replacement paint layers

fixPainter layers can erase and replace their original art

Layer-specific painting now edits the layer itself instead of behaving only like an overlay on top of the original artwork.

  • Original art is editable - opening a blank layer painter seeds the canvas with that layer's current vector/equipment art so brush and eraser strokes modify it directly.
  • Saved paint replaces base - once a layer has saved paint, the original base art for that layer is hidden and the edited layer becomes the rendered source.
  • Full erasing works - a fully erased layer saves as a transparent replacement, so the original image does not reappear after applying.

v0.25.357 Layer paint preserves existing art

fixLayer uploads now stack over the existing paint layer

Uploading or transforming a new image in the wardrobe painter no longer wipes the artwork already saved on that same layer.

  • Preserve previous layer art - the painter snapshots the current layer before a new upload and redraws it under the editable upload.
  • Weapon layer safe - drawing or uploading a sword on the weapon layer now adds to the layer instead of replacing the prior weapon-layer image.
  • Transform controls retained - move, resize, stretch, rotate, and fit still work on the newly uploaded image before it is baked into the layer.

v0.25.356 Weapon arm outward nudge

polishWeapon-side upper limb sits farther from the torso

The character's weapon arm now has a little more clearance from the main torso while preserving weapon dimensions.

  • Arm pivot moved outward - shifted armR from x:12 to x:13.
  • Weapon follows naturally - the child handR anchor still inherits the arm pose and remains otherwise unchanged.
  • No size change - weapon renderer scale and authored weapon dimensions are untouched.

v0.25.355 Archer shot hand pose

polishArcher weapon hand raises and grips closer while firing

The archer shooting pose now holds the weapon hand slightly higher and closer to the body without changing bow dimensions.

  • Higher shot hand - raised the archer front arm by 10deg during attack_archer.
  • Closer grip - shifted the handR weapon anchor 2px toward the body only during the archer shot.
  • Bow size preserved - the bow renderer, weapon scale, and authored weapon dimensions are unchanged.

v0.25.354 Off-hand idle arm polish

polishNonweapon upper limb sits lower and idles more naturally

The vector character's nonweapon upper limb now has a small idle swing and a slightly lower shoulder position while keeping the weapon side untouched.

  • 1px lower - moved the back/off-hand arm pivot from y:-22 to y:-21.
  • Subtle idle swing - added a small 0.08 radian sine sway to armL during idle.
  • Weapon unchanged - the front weapon hand, weapon dimensions, and weapon scale are untouched.

v0.25.353 Paint-only wardrobe editor

polishWardrobe slots now draw or upload only

The visible wardrobe slot editor now focuses on custom paint and uploads instead of showing preset choose buttons.

  • No choose panel - slot panels now show draw or upload tools only, removing the visible preset picker buttons and sprites.
  • Preview headroom - reduced and lifted the wardrobe preview render so hair and head details have more canvas clearance.
  • Paint canvas fit - expanded the paint-coordinate destination to a larger synced 3:4 rectangle so the translucent guide, Q wardrobe preview, and in-game paint rendering share room for the full head and hair.

v0.25.352 Crisper entity art

polishSharper character, NPC, and monster rendering

In-game entity art now uses cleaner canvas scaling so characters, NPCs, and monster sprites read sharper during normal play.

  • Entity image quality - added a shared high-quality image-smoothing helper for player paint layers, equipment, NPC sprites, monster sprites, and hero sprite fallbacks.
  • Whole-pixel draws - snapped sprite target sizes and anchors to whole canvas pixels to reduce subpixel blur while moving and bobbing.
  • Monster size steps - changed random monster size variation from broad arbitrary scaling to clean 95%, 100%, and 105% steps.
  • Monster readability - raised regular monster sprite render scale slightly from 1.4x to 1.5x, keeping the source art downscaled rather than upscaled.

v0.25.351 Larger NPCs

polishNPCs are 20% larger in the world

Town and marketplace NPCs now render larger so they read better beside the resized player character.

  • Unified scale - added an NPC_WORLD_SCALE of 1.2 for both loaded NPC sprites and the procedural fallback renderer.
  • Sprite size - NPC portrait sprites keep their aspect ratio while their target height is increased by 20%.
  • Readable labels - name tags, chat bubbles, and talk prompts shift upward to stay clear of the larger NPC bodies.

v0.25.350 In-game character size

polishWorld character enlarged to 80%

The in-game vector character display has been raised from the previous 75% reduction target to the requested 80% size.

  • World scale - updated HERO_WORLD_DISPLAY_SCALE from 0.795 to 0.848, which is 80% of the earlier 1.06 world baseline.
  • Scoped change - kept the painter and wardrobe preview sizing separate, so this only affects the in-game character draw scale.

v0.25.349 Wardrobe hair clipping fix

polishWardrobe preview gives hair more room

The Q wardrobe editor preview now renders the vector character smaller inside its backing canvas so tall hair and side overhangs do not clip.

  • Preview fit - reduced the vector wardrobe preview scale from 3.75 to 3.0.
  • Top clearance - the tallest hair sprites now have room above the head inside the actual 384x384 canvas.
  • Side clearance - wider hair silhouettes have extra horizontal padding in the editor preview.

v0.25.348 Jump head-body spacing

polishHead stays connected during jumps

The vector jump pose no longer lets the head drift away from the torso while airborne.

  • No widening gap - removed jump-time vertical spine stretch that pushed the head bone away from the body.
  • Shared lift - moved the jump bob onto the spine instead of the head so the torso and head rise together.
  • Shape preserved - kept only mild horizontal squash with head counter-scale, so proportions stay stable.

v0.25.347 Weapon hand outward nudge

polishHeld weapon anchor shifted outward

The front weapon hand anchor now sits 1px farther away from the body, giving held weapons a little more clearance.

  • Outward hand shift - changed handR from x:0 to x:1.
  • Weapon layers follow - class weapons and weapon paint uploads inherit the same hand anchor move.
  • No size change - weapon dimensions and scale are untouched.

v0.25.346 Head attachment micro-nudge

polishHead-attached visuals shifted right and down

The shared head-attached visual offset has been nudged 1px right and 1px down for a finer body alignment.

  • Micro nudge - changed the shared offset from -4px, -2px to -3px, -1px.
  • Attached layers stay together - head, hair, face, skin paint, hair paint, hat paint, and helmet equipment still inherit the same move.
  • Sync preserved - the adjustment remains in the shared vector render path so Q wardrobe and in-game visuals match.

v0.25.345 Wardrobe preview scale-down

polishWardrobe and paint preview characters reduced

The Q wardrobe preview and paint editor guide now render smaller so the character has more room inside the preview panels.

  • Wardrobe 25% smaller - reduced the vector wardrobe preview scale from 5.0 to 3.75.
  • More top clearance - moved the preview feet anchor slightly lower to keep the head from clipping.
  • Paint editor display smaller - reduced the paint-stage visual display scale from 0.75 to 0.5625 while keeping the bitmap at 192x256.

v0.25.344 Head attachment nudge right

polishHead-attached visuals shifted 4px right

The shared head-attached visual offset has been nudged right while keeping the slight upward adjustment from the previous recenter pass.

  • Right nudge - changed the shared head visual offset from -8px, -2px to -4px, -2px.
  • Attached layers stay together - head, hair, face, skin paint, hair paint, hat paint, and helmet equipment still inherit the same offset.
  • Coordinate sync preserved - this remains a vector-renderer visual offset, so wardrobe preview and in-game rendering stay matched.

v0.25.343 Archer bow release timing

polishBow stays straight until the arrow fires

The archer bow now keeps its visual angle steady through the draw and only adds a slight recoil tilt after the shot releases.

  • No start angle - the bow counter-rotates against the front-arm aim pose until the release frame.
  • Post-shot tilt - after t=0.58, the bow gets a small -0.16 radian recoil tilt.
  • Size preserved - the change only rotates the bow locally; bow dimensions and projectile firing are unchanged.

v0.25.342 Head attachment recenter

polishMain character head shifted left/up

The vector head and its attached face, hair, skin-paint, hat-paint, and helmet layers now share one small recentering offset.

  • Head recentered - added a shared -8px, -2px visual offset for head-attached rendering.
  • Attached layers stay together - hair, eyes, mouth, skin paint, hair paint, hat paint, and helmet equipment all inherit the same move.
  • Coordinate sync preserved - the offset is applied inside the vector renderer so the paint editor, Q wardrobe preview, and in-game character match.

v0.25.341 Smaller paint wardrobe character

polishPaint editor character image reduced by 25%

The paint wardrobe now displays its character guide and paint layer contents at 75% size inside the larger editor stage.

  • Smaller editor image - the paint-stage canvases use a 0.75 visual display scale.
  • Canvas space preserved - the actual paint bitmap stays 192x256, so saved/uploaded layer coordinates remain stable.
  • Larger stage retained - the editor still uses the wider 216px paint area from the previous update.

v0.25.340 Warrior sword outward tilt

polishWarrior sword leans farther away from the character

The vector warrior sword now rotates 20 degrees outward around the hand grip, keeping the same weapon dimensions while making the idle/attack read less vertical.

  • Outward tilt - added a HERO_VEC_WARRIOR_SWORD_OUTWARD_TILT angle of Math.PI / 9.
  • No scaling - the sword draw path is only rotated around the hand; blade, guard, grip, and pommel sizes are unchanged.
  • Facing-safe - the existing character mirror transform flips the tilt naturally when facing left.

v0.25.339 Back arm over torso

polishBack upper limb now renders in front of the torso

The vector back arm layer now draws after the torso and body paint layers, so the upper limb reads in front of clothing instead of being hidden behind the body.

  • Updated vector order - armL moved from before the torso pass to after the body top/bottom paint pass.
  • Paint ordering matched - fallback paint render order now places armL after torso layers too.
  • Bone pinning unchanged - the back arm still follows the same armL rig animation and uploaded paint coordinates.

v0.25.338 Character size and paint editor space

polishMain character is smaller and the wardrobe painter is larger

The world character now renders at 75% of the previous visible size, while the paint wardrobe editor canvas is 20% larger on screen for easier layer placement.

  • Smaller main character - world rendering uses a new HERO_WORLD_DISPLAY_SCALE set to 75% of the previous display scale.
  • Painter coordinates preserved - HERO_VEC_DRAW_SCALE remains 1.0, so uploaded layer coordinates keep matching the painter.
  • Bigger paint editor - the visible wardrobe paint stage increased from 180px to 216px.

v0.25.337 Vector scale matches painter

fixVector renderer now uses the same 1.0 coordinate scale as the paint editor

The vector character's internal draw scale has been changed from 0.62 to 1.0, so uploaded wardrobe art and painted layer coordinates map directly from the editor to Q preview and in-game rendering.

  • Unshrunk render scale - HERO_VEC_DRAW_SCALE is now 1.0 instead of 0.62.
  • Painter-first coordinates - the paint editor, preview, and game now share the same base vector space instead of compensating around a smaller game render.
  • Stale comments cleaned - the vector transform notes now describe the current painter-matched scale.

v0.25.336 Paint guide scale sync

fixUploaded layer coordinates now line up with the in-game vector scale

The paint editor guide now compensates for the vector hero's internal draw scale, so moving a hat above the eyes in the editor targets the same coordinates used by the Q preview and in-game renderer.

  • Correct guide scale - the translucent character guide maps through the same 0.62 vector draw scale used when rendering the character.
  • Shared scale constant - the editor transform and vector renderer now read one HERO_VEC_DRAW_SCALE value instead of duplicating scale assumptions.
  • Layer placement preserved - uploaded images still bake into the transparent paint canvas, but the guide now shows the true target coordinates.

v0.25.335 Paint wardrobe coordinate sync

fixPaint editor, Q wardrobe, and vector character now share the same rest-space placement

The paint wardrobe guide now renders through the same equipment paint rectangle used by the character renderer, and the Q preview locks to that rest pose while a paint layer is selected.

  • Shared coordinates - the translucent editor guide maps directly from the in-game paint rectangle instead of duplicating the math inline.
  • Stable editing pose - selecting hair, skin, top, bottom, cape, limb, weapon, hat, or all paint layers pauses the wardrobe preview pose so uploads do not appear to drift against the editor guide.
  • Active editor target - paint canvases now use class selectors plus an explicit active reference, avoiding duplicate hidden IDs that could refresh the wrong guide.
  • Bone pinning preserved - in-game layers still follow their assigned bone movement after placement.

v0.25.334 Rogue high-to-low slash

polishRogue dagger swing starts higher and finishes lower

The rogue vector attack animation now reads as an exaggerated high-to-low cut instead of a straight forward poke.

  • Higher windup - the dagger arm pulls farther overhead/back before the hit.
  • Lower follow-through - the strike snaps down through a low finishing angle with a brief held finish.
  • Better body read - the off-hand now counter-swings and the wrist rotates through the cut, while weapon dimensions remain unchanged.

v0.25.333 Free upload transform controls

featureRotate and freely resize uploaded wardrobe art

Uploaded images in the Wardrobe paint editor now have a fuller transform panel for positioning art on each layer.

  • Free rotation - added a Turn slider with -180 to +180 degree control for uploaded images.
  • Flexible resizing - Size scales uniformly, while Wide and Tall sliders let players resize each axis independently when they want a custom fit.
  • Same bake path - transforms still bake into the exact same transparent layer PNG used by the paint editor, Q Wardrobe preview, and in-game renderer.

v0.25.332 Weapon swing rotation fix

polishArms swing through the hit without resizing the weapon

The vector attack pose now sells weapon impact through shoulder and wrist rotation instead of scaling the weapon sprite.

  • Stronger arm motion - generic slashes now hold a clearer windup, snap through the strike faster, and follow through before recovery.
  • Wrist follow-through - sword-side hand rotation now lags then catches up during the hit, making the weapon feel attached to the arm swing.
  • Fixed weapon dimensions - weapon drawing uses the animated bone rotation but skips attack squash/stretch deformation, so the authored weapon size is preserved.

v0.25.331 Editable wardrobe uploads

featureUploaded layer art can be moved and resized

Wardrobe paint uploads are now editable after import, and the paint editor uses the same 3:4 layer aspect as the in-game vector paint target.

  • Move + scale uploads - uploaded art enters Move mode immediately; drag the paint canvas to reposition it and use the image-size slider to resize it uniformly.
  • No aspect warping - the paint layer canvas now matches the character paint target, and render helpers preserve source aspect ratios instead of stretching mismatched images.
  • Same output everywhere - the editor bakes the moved/scaled result into the same transparent PNG source used by the Wardrobe preview, Q screen, and in-game renderer.

v0.25.330 Wardrobe limb labels simplified

polishRemoved glove/boot paint layers and renamed limbs

The Wardrobe paint layer list is cleaner: Gloves and Boots are no longer exposed as custom paint layers, and the four limb tiles now use direct left/right naming.

  • Removed redundant layers - Gloves and Boots were removed from the paint layer list so limb customization happens through the actual arm/leg layers.
  • Clear limb labels - B ARM, F ARM, B LEG, and F LEG are now RIGHT ARM, LEFT ARM, RIGHT LEG, and LEFT LEG.
  • Pinning preserved - internal rig bindings still use armR, armL, legR, and legL, so existing animation-follow behavior stays intact.

v0.25.329 Four independent limb paint layers

featureBack/front arms and legs split into separate editable layers

The Wardrobe now exposes four paint-only limb layers so each limb can receive its own drawn or uploaded art and follow its own vector bone.

  • New limb tiles - added B ARM, F ARM, B LEG, and F LEG paint tiles to the Wardrobe grid.
  • True four-limb pinning - back arm pins to armL, front arm pins to armR, back leg pins to legL, and front leg pins to legR.
  • Independent saves - the new limb layers persist in player.customPaintLayers alongside hair, skin, clothing, cape, gloves, boots, weapon, and hat.
  • MSX fallback ordering - the sprite fallback render order now includes all four limb paint layers for compatibility, even though true bone pinning is vector-only.

v0.25.328 Revert v0.25.327 — Ludo batch parked in Sprites/character/Todo list/ for review

revertPickers go back to 7 hair / 2 eyes / 1 mouth; 36 generated sprites await curation

v0.25.327 wired 36 Ludo-generated WebPs straight into the live Character Studio pickers. Per the v0.25.301 precedent — where a similar AI batch was rejected wholesale because it didn't match the hand-painted aesthetic — this revert moves the generated sprites into a holding folder pending human review rather than shipping them by default.

  • Pickers reverted to v0.25.301 set: hair returns to flow / ponytail / shaggy / spiky / flowy / iceCream / pigeotto (7 entries); eyes returns to default + fierce (2 entries); mouth returns to default (1 entry). HERO_VEC_HAIR_OPTIONS, HERO_VEC_EYE_OPTIONS, HERO_VEC_MOUTH_OPTIONS all rolled back. HERO_VEC_HAIR_LEGACY_MAP restores pigtails: 'flow'; _migrateHairId whitelist drops the 5 new ids.
  • Live sprite folders restored. 25 new WebPs deleted from Sprites/character/{hair,eyes,mouth}/; 11 overwritten files (the 7 hair + 2 eyes + 1 mouth from v0.25.301) restored to their hand-painted versions.
  • All 36 generated sprites preserved at Sprites/character/Todo list/{hair,eyes,mouth}/ for curation. Filenames map 1:1 to the live folder layout — to promote one, just move it back into place + add the registry / picker / migration whitelist entries (steps documented in the new Todo list/README.md).
  • Generation pipeline retained. CHARACTER_PROMPTS.md + scripts/character_sprites.config.mjs + scripts/generate_character_sprites.mjs all stay intact, so any rejected sprite can be re-generated on demand without re-deriving the prompt or re-paying the original setup cost.

v0.25.326 Character sprite generation — automated runner + 3/4-right side prefix

toolingOne command emits all 36 hair/eyes/mouth prompts; another generates the WebPs end-to-end

v0.25.325 shipped the prompt library; this turns it into a one-command pipeline. Two new files in scripts/:

  • scripts/character_sprites.config.mjs — single source of truth: SIDE_PREFIX, LOCKED_PREFIX, LAYER_BLOCKS (hair / eyes / mouth), and the 36 entries (12 hair + 12 eyes + 12 mouth) with filename + variant line. Pure data + a composePrompt(entry) helper.
  • scripts/generate_character_sprites.mjs — runner with three modes: dry-run (default, prints every composed prompt — safe to inspect), --json (machine-readable manifest with targetPath + composed prompt), --generate (calls Ludo's REST API with LUDO_API_KEY env, downloads each WebP into Sprites/character/<layer>/). Filters: --layer hair, --id flow,spiky.

New SIDE_PREFIX (paste-block Z): "Side facing 40 percent to the right at a slight three-quarter angle (right shoulder closer to the viewer than left, head turned slightly toward camera-right, NOT a flat front view)." The chibi's idle stance in the renderer faces right by default, so face features painted at a flat front view read as "looking off-camera" against the body. A consistent three-quarter-right pose locks the sprite's facing direction to match the body without any mirror tricks. Applied universally — to every prompt the runner emits, and added to CHARACTER_PROMPTS.md as paste-block [Z] ahead of the existing locked prefix.

Workflow shrinks from 36 manual paste passes to a single command. Dry-run first to sanity-check (cheap, instant). Then LUDO_API_KEY=… node scripts/generate_character_sprites.mjs --generate — every WebP lands in the right folder, registry wire-up is the same one-line files map add described in the doc. To regenerate just one sprite (e.g. fierce eyes didn't read right), --id fierce hits only that entry. Same idempotent pattern as scripts/import_equipment_sprites.mjs.

v0.25.325 CHARACTER_PROMPTS.md — hair / eyes / mouth prompt library

docs36 ready-to-use Ludo prompts for the three character-appearance layers

The four equipment docs (TEMPLATE / PHOTOSHOP_GUIDE / PROMPTS / PROMPTS_PER_ITEM) all target the body-anchor 800×800 template — armor / weapons / boots. They don't help with hair, eyes, or mouth, which use a different anchor map: the head silhouette bbox (245, 215)–(612, 585) inside the same canvas. Adding a new hairstyle or eye expression meant re-deriving that head bbox each time. New repo-root doc CHARACTER_PROMPTS.md closes the gap with the same three-block composition pattern, retargeted at the head region.

  • One canvas, three layers, locked anchors. Doc opens with the head bbox, eye band (y ≈ 360–420 paired around x = 400 / 460), mouth band (y ≈ 470–520 centred on x = 430), and hair envelope (may extend past the bbox by ±80 px for volume). Same numbers the renderer uses at maple_game.html:29888 and :29938.
  • Three slot blocks — HAIR / EYES / MOUTH — each restating the layer's pixel band, what to render, what NOT to render (hair: no head silhouette; eyes: no nose / lashes outside band; mouth: single shape, no chin / no facial outline), and tint-friendliness rules.
  • 36 variant lines. 12 hair styles (long flow, high ponytail, shaggy, spiky, flowy, ice-cream, pigeotto + 5 new: pigtails, topknot, afro, undercut, ringlets), 12 eye expressions (default, fierce, sparkle, sleepy, cat slit, closed smile, wink, glasses, scarred, heterochromia, cyber visor, teary), 12 mouth expressions (default smile, grin, smirk, surprise, tongue, stoic, laugh, pout, fang, frown, blush, determined).
  • Tint-cache awareness. Hair variants explicitly call out a NEUTRAL DARK BROWN base palette (#3a2218 / #5a3624 / #1a0a04) so the multiply-blend hair-colour picker keeps working. Saturated colour locks the runtime recolour out — flagged in the doc as a per-style decision.
  • Wire-up steps. One-paragraph walk through: drop the WebP into Sprites/character/<layer>/, add the entry to the files map in the matching registry (LX_HAIR / LX_EYES / LX_MOUTH), extend the Character Studio picker option list, reload.

Five docs now compose the full sprite-authoring chain: EQUIPMENT_TEMPLATE.md (engine contract), PHOTOSHOP_GUIDE.md (manual playbook), EQUIPMENT_PROMPTS.md (generic equipment AI prompts), EQUIPMENT_PROMPTS_PER_ITEM.md (palette-locked equipment prompts), CHARACTER_PROMPTS.md (hair + face prompts). Equipment goes through the body anchor; appearance goes through the head bbox; nothing else changes.

v0.25.324 EQUIPMENT_PROMPTS_PER_ITEM.md — palette-baked prompts for every existing item

docs38 ready-to-use Ludo prompts that match the in-game tooltip palette automatically

v0.25.323 shipped a generic prompt library — EQUIPMENT_PROMPTS.md with ~80 menu-style variants like "knight greatsword, gold cross-guard". Useful for new gear, but the existing item ladder (Wooden Sword → Doomforged Greatsword, Cloth Tunic → Skyhunter Vest) already has authored vis palettes that the procedural class-weapon / class-armor renderer paints. A generic prompt produces a sprite that does not match the tooltip preview — small but jarring inconsistency. New doc EQUIPMENT_PROMPTS_PER_ITEM.md closes that gap with one tailored prompt per existing item.

  • 38 prompts covering every visually-renderable item in ITEM_POOL (maple_game.html:6053): 18 weapons (6 generic + 3 warrior + 3 rogue + 3 mage + 3 archer), 15 chest armors → body_top, 4 cape/helmet items (Shadow Hood → helmet, Nightshade/Shadowweave Cloak → cape, Shadow Talisman → helmet), and 3 mappable accessories (Boots of Swiftness → boots, Skyhunter Quiver → cape via back-mount).
  • Palette baked in. Each variant line injects the item's authored hex colors directly — e.g. Dawnshard Blade's prompt names #88ffcc base / #ddffee highlight / #4aaa88 shadow / #66ff99 glow, lifted straight from the vis object. Output matches the tooltip preview side-by-side.
  • Filename / spriteId convention is direct: item name lowercased + snake_case + .webp. "Iron Sword" → iron_sword.webp + spriteId iron_sword. The Apply path in applyCharStudioToPlayer already merges visual fields onto stat-bearing items per v0.25.313, so wiring an existing item is a one-line spriteId: add.
  • Composes with the existing prompt library. Paste the locked PREFIX + matching SLOT BLOCK from EQUIPMENT_PROMPTS.md + the per-item VARIANT LINE from this new doc as one paragraph. Same Ludo settings as before (image_type: "sprite", art_style: "Anime/Manga", aspect_ratio: "ar_1_1", n: 1).
  • Wire-back instructions inline. Doc explains how to add the spriteId field to existing ITEM_POOL entries so the new sprite renders instead of the procedural fallback. Procedural render stays as a working fallback for any item without a sprite.

Three docs now compose end-to-end: EQUIPMENT_TEMPLATE.md (engine contract — anchors, z-order, format), PHOTOSHOP_GUIDE.md (manual authoring playbook), EQUIPMENT_PROMPTS.md (generic AI generation library — variant menus), EQUIPMENT_PROMPTS_PER_ITEM.md (palette-locked AI prompts for the existing ladder). Cross-references added to EQUIPMENT_TEMPLATE.md and EQUIPMENT_PROMPTS.md.

v0.25.328 Paint layers pin to animated vector bones

fixWardrobe paint now follows slot movement

Layer-specific paint and uploads now render inside the matching vector bone transforms instead of staying as static full-body overlays.

  • Weapon paint follows attacks - the Weapon layer is drawn inside the handR transform, so uploaded or drawn weapon art swings with the actual weapon hand.
  • Head layers follow the head - Hair, Hat, and Skin paint are pinned to the head bone, inheriting head bob, jump, and attack pose motion.
  • Body layers follow the body - Top, Bottom, and Cape paint are pinned to the spine; Boots pin to the front leg; Gloves pin to the weapon hand.
  • Editor alignment preserved - the paint canvas still uses the full-character guide, but render-time offsets keep the rest pose aligned before animation transforms are applied.

v0.25.327 World character aspect matches Wardrobe preview

fixRemoved in-game horizontal stretch from character rendering

The in-game character renderer now uses a uniform chibi scale so the character proportions match the Q-key Wardrobe preview.

  • Fixed squash mismatch - gameplay no longer applies the old 1.19 Ã— 1.06 non-uniform transform that made the character look wider/shorter than the Wardrobe preview.
  • Paint layer consistency - uploaded and drawn wardrobe paint now appears with the same aspect ratio in-game as it does in the editor preview.
  • Same foot anchor - the transform still anchors at the player's feet and keeps the existing ground lift, so platform contact remains stable.

v0.25.326 Layered wardrobe paint - per-slot draw/upload

featurePaint every wardrobe layer separately

The Wardrobe paint/upload tool now attaches to each appearance slot, so players can draw or import art for hair, skin, top, bottom, cape, gloves, boots, weapon, and hat independently.

  • Per-layer saves - new player.customPaintLayers map stores separate transparent PNG overlays keyed by wardrobe slot.
  • Slot-local editors - opening Hair, Skin, Top, Bottom, Cape, Gloves, Boots, Weapon, or Hat now shows the normal variant picker plus the paint/upload canvas for that exact layer.
  • Layered rendering - custom paint draws in the matching render order: cape behind the body, clothing/boots/gloves/weapon near equipment, hair near head art, and hat above the front equipment pass.
  • Tile feedback - painted slots get a small gold dot on their Wardrobe tile, while the legacy ALL paint overlay remains available as a whole-character layer.
  • Save compatibility - older player.customPaint saves still render as the final full-body overlay.

v0.25.325 Wardrobe paint upload - import PNG/JPEG/WebP art

featureUpload images into the character paint layer

The PAINT wardrobe tool now includes an Upload action so players can import an image file and use it as their saved character overlay.

  • File picker support - accepts PNG, JPEG, and WebP files from the user's device.
  • Automatic fitting - uploaded images are scaled to fit the existing transparent paint canvas and centered without stretching.
  • Same save path - imported art commits into player.customPaint, updates the PAINT tile thumbnail, previews in the Wardrobe, and persists after Use in game.
  • Format guard - unsupported file types are rejected with an in-game toast instead of being written into the save.

v0.25.324 Wardrobe paint layer - draw directly on the vector character

featurePlayer-authored character paint overlay

The Q-key Wardrobe now has a PAINT slot where players can draw a transparent overlay on top of their character and save it into the same appearance flow as hair, skin, and equipment.

  • In-game paint editor - new brush canvas with a faint character guide, six quick colours, brush-size slider, eraser, and clear action.
  • Animation-safe layer - drawings render through the same character-space rectangle as wardrobe equipment, so one saved paint layer follows the animated vector hero instead of requiring redrawn walk, jump, or attack frames.
  • Persistent custom art - saved paint is serialized as a compact transparent PNG data URL on player.customPaint and restored on load.
  • Preview + tile feedback - the Wardrobe preview shows the pending paint layer before Apply, and the PAINT tile thumbnail updates as soon as ink is added or cleared.
  • Renderer cleanup - equipment layer drawing now accepts the active render context, so wardrobe previews draw overlays on the preview canvas instead of relying on the main game canvas context.

v0.25.323 EQUIPMENT_PROMPTS.md â€” copy-paste Ludo prompt library

docs~80 pre-written variant prompts that align to the 800Ã—800 template automatically

New repo-root doc EQUIPMENT_PROMPTS.md â€” a copy-paste prompt library for generating equipment sprites via Ludo.ai (or any compatible image AI) that fit the locked anchor template without manual adjustment. Replaces "look up the locked prompt prefix in EQUIPMENT_TEMPLATE.md, write your own variant" with three pasteable blocks + one menu pick.

  • Three-block composition â€” paste [A] LOCKED PREFIX + [B] SLOT BLOCK + [C] VARIANT LINE as one paragraph. Same A every time. B is one of seven (helmet / cape / body_top / body_bottom / gloves / boots / weapon). C is one of ~80 pre-written variants.
  • Per-slot anchor blocks with explicit Y range + tint-palette guidance restated in every prompt. The renderer's seam values (neckline 350, hip 520, mid-shin 620, hand grips 260/540 at 460, feet 380/420 at 720) are baked into the slot text so Ludo respects them.
  • Variant menus â€” 15 helmets, 12 capes, 15 body tops, 12 body bottoms, 10 gloves, 10 boots, 20 weapons. Categories span knight / mage / rogue / archer / barbarian / pirate / samurai / scholar / noble / demon-knight / paladin / etc.
  • Tint-friendliness rules â€” clothing variants force "NEUTRAL LIGHT CREAM (#e8d8b8)" or "NEUTRAL LIGHT GREY METAL (#c8c8d0)" base palette; weapons skip this since shape is identity. The slot blocks already include this; custom variants that use saturated colour ("crimson cape") lose runtime recolour support.
  • Style notes â€” what makes output match existing kit: 2â€“3 tone shading per material, crisp ink-line outlines (~2 px), warm palette for organic / cool for inorganic, no background elements.
  • What to do if output is misaligned â€” three escalation paths: re-prompt with stronger anchor language â†’ reposition in Photoshop â†’ add a per-sprite render-rect override.

EQUIPMENT_TEMPLATE.md updated with a top-level cross-reference to the new prompts library. Three docs now compose: EQUIPMENT_TEMPLATE.md (engine contract), PHOTOSHOP_GUIDE.md (manual authoring playbook), EQUIPMENT_PROMPTS.md (AI generation library).

v0.25.322 PHOTOSHOP_GUIDE.md â€” equipment-specific anti-misalignment section

docsHow to author equipment that seams cleanly with other slots

The v0.25.320 sprite guide covered single-piece authoring (anchors, Y ranges, file format). Equipment is different: pieces stack on each other in a fixed z-order, so a misaligned belt or too-tall boots show up as a visible skin gap or a clipped overlap. New section "Anti-misalignment for EQUIPMENT specifically" in PHOTOSHOP_GUIDE.md covers:

  • Composite preview workflow â€” drag every adjacent slot's existing sprite into your authoring PSD at 40 % opacity, verify seams meet within Â±2 px, hide before export. The single most important pre-ship check for equipment.
  • Seam table â€” six canonical seam Y-coordinates (neckline 350, wrist 460, hip 520, mid-shin 620, foot 720, helmet brim 240) with which slot covers which seam in z-order.
  • "Do not paint" zones per slot â€” explicit Y ranges + forbidden zones for helmet (no painting past 240 = covers eyes), cape (no overlap with head / clipping past feet), body_top / body_bottom (mustn't clash at the hip waistband), boots (must reach feet anchor), gloves (40 px band only), weapon (anywhere but spans grip outward).
  • Mirror-flip awareness â€” the renderer mirrors equipment via ctx.scale(-1, 1) when the player faces left. Symmetric pieces are safe; asymmetric pieces flip with the body. Test both facings via Image â†’ Image Rotation â†’ Flip Canvas Horizontal + undo.
  • Tint compatibility rules â€” paint in neutral light grey (#c8c8d0) or warm cream (#e8d8b8) with baked-in cel-shading. Multiply tint preserves luminance so a flat-grey piece tints flat; painterly highlights tint with highlights intact. Pure black multiplies to black and hides the tint.
  • Silhouette extent ranges â€” per-slot left/right boundary x-values so a puffy jacket doesn't extend to x = 200 and look like floating fabric.
  • Worst-case bug callout â€” pieces that are asymmetric AND tinted AND seam against another slot need testing in both facings before approval.
  • Equipment quick-reject checklist â€” 9 items every equipment piece must pass before shipping. If any fails â†’ fix in Photoshop, re-export.

v0.25.321 Wardrobe pause / close consistency â€” fixes 3 stacked-modal bugs

bugFrom manual UI scan â€” the Wardrobe never paused the game

The Q-key Character Studio (Wardrobe) has been live since v0.25.72 and revamped in v0.25.310, but openCharStudio never set game.paused = true â€” every other modal in the game (U-panel, Quest Journal, NPC dialog, shops, taxi, world map) does. Three side-effects this caused:

  • The v0.25.315 paused-render-skip never fired for the Wardrobe â€” the main game canvas kept repainting at 60 FPS behind the modal, defeating the cost-halving claim from that commit. Wardrobe-open frame budget was effectively still doubled because of this miss.
  • Game simulation kept ticking under the modal. Mobs chased, projectiles travelled, mini-boss timers counted down. Open the Wardrobe in a boss arena and you could die behind the overlay without any visible feedback.
  • closeAllModals didn't dismiss the Wardrobe. The bulk close loop iterates IDs and sets style.display = 'none', but the Wardrobe uses classList.add/remove('open') on #char-studio-overlay instead. Opening any other modal while the Wardrobe was open left it visible underneath / on top depending on z-index.

Fix: openCharStudio now sets game.paused = true; closeCharStudio clears it (defensively â€” only if no other modal is currently open, via a new _anyOtherModalOpen helper, so unpausing doesn't fight a stacked modal); closeAllModals now also strips the .open class from #char-studio-overlay and stops the studio's rAF preview loop. All three close paths (âœ• button, Esc key, opening another modal) now leave the wardrobe properly dismissed.

v0.25.320 PHOTOSHOP_GUIDE.md â€” sprite authoring workflow + format / size advice

docsHow to author sprites that drop into the layered system without pixel misalignments

New repo-root document PHOTOSHOP_GUIDE.md covers the complete artist workflow:

  • Format / size TL;DR table â€” 800Ã—800 PNG-24 (or WebP), 8-bit RGB, transparent, anti-aliased. Locked numbers, never resize.
  • File-format trade-offs â€” when to use PNG-24 (universal compat, 150â€“400 KB), when to use WebP (50â€“70 % smaller, requires WebPShop plugin on older Photoshop), what to avoid (JPEG / GIF / SVG and why).
  • Pixel-size rationale â€” why 800Ã—800 is the sweet spot (~14Ã— downscale = clean anti-aliasing headroom). Smaller = jaggy edges, larger = wasted file size.
  • Anchor map diagram â€” ASCII art showing exactly where head / shoulders / hips / hand-grips / feet / knees land in the 800Ã—800 canvas, with per-slot Y-range tables.
  • Photoshop step-by-step â€” one-time master-template PSD setup (anchor reference layer at 15 % opacity + grid guides at every named anchor), per-piece authoring flow (open template, Save As, group by slot, paint), tint-friendly palette tips for clothing.
  • Pre-export checklist â€” eight items to verify before export (canvas size unchanged, reference layer hidden, transparency intact, content within slot's allowed Y range, anti-aliasing on, etc.).
  • Export settings â€” exact PNG-24 and WebP-quality-90 configurations, plus a one-line cwebp CLI fallback for older Photoshop.
  • Wire-into-game flow â€” drop the file, run node scripts/import_equipment_sprites.mjs <slot>, paste the printed registry block, reload.
  • Common misalignment causes â€” five most likely root causes ranked by frequency, with fixes (canvas resized, content not anchored, Layer Trim cropped, layer effects bake padding, etc.).

EQUIPMENT_TEMPLATE.md updated with a top-level cross-reference to the new guide. The existing engine spec (anchor coordinates, locked Ludo prompt prefix, z-order) stays the canonical source of truth; the Photoshop guide is the artist's playbook for hitting that spec without arithmetic.

v0.25.319 Loading â€” per-image 8 s timeout

bugFrom gameplay audit â€” a single hung sprite no longer blocks the whole gate

The v0.25.312 all-sprite reveal gate waited for every loadOne(src) promise to resolve. loadOne already resolved on both onload AND onerror â€” but a STALLED connection that emits neither (e.g. a half-loaded image hanging on slow 3G) blocked the gate for the full 30 s hard-fallback window. Slow-network players watched the loading screen freeze with the bar near complete for half a minute.

Fix: each loadOne call now has its own 8 s safety timer alongside the img.onload / onerror handlers. Whichever fires first resolves the promise. The image element keeps trying in the background; if it eventually loads, the on-screen render uses the live result on the next frame. Net effect: any single hung image stalls the gate by at most 8 s instead of 30 s, and the failure mode becomes "world reveals slightly degraded" instead of "loading screen frozen".

v0.25.318 drawSetAura â€” zero-alloc cache for the empty case

polishFrom perf audit â€” set-bonus aura was allocating per frame even with no set

drawSetAura ran _activeFullSets() every frame, which allocated a fresh counts = {} object (via getEquippedSetCounts) plus a fresh out = [] array, walked a for...in loop over an array literal of slot names, and only THEN bailed if the array was empty. Pure GC churn at 60 FPS for the common no-set-equipped case (~3 object allocations + 1 array allocation per frame).

Fix: cache the active-full-sets array on player._activeFullSetsCache inside _checkSetComplete, which already runs on every equip change. drawSetAura reads the cached array directly â€” zero allocations in the empty case, falls back to the live _activeFullSets() computation only on the very first frame before _checkSetComplete has had a chance to run (e.g. immediately after class pick).

v0.25.317 Cast-pose flags cleared on respawn

bugFrom gameplay audit â€” pose flags leaked through death

The skill-animation flags introduced in v0.25.288â€“296 (somersault, big-stab, mage swing, chant-raise, ninja pose / crouch, dragoon impale, levitate) were only cleared by their own time-out check inside _heroVecAnimFor. If the player died mid-cast (lich harvest at 100 ms in, sage chant partway through, etc.), game.time kept advancing through the death sequence and respawn â€” so the flags eventually expired, but the post-respawn idle pose flickered through "levitate" / "chant" / "ninja stance" / "big stab" / "impale" / "mage swing" briefly, and a mid-spin somersault left a partial flip frame visible.

Fix: respawnAtTown now zeroes every cast-pose flag (player.somersault, somersaultDir, _chantRaiseUntil, _impalePoseUntil, _ninjaPoseUntil, _ninjaCrouchUntil, _levitateUntil, _bigStabUntil, _mageBigSwingUntil) alongside the existing combat-state wipe. Respawning hero now starts in a clean idle pose every time.

v0.25.316 Equipment renderer â€” empty-loadout fast-path

polishFrom perf audit â€” O(1) bail when nothing is equipped

_drawEquipmentLayer fires three times per hero frame (back / mid / front passes). Pre-v0.25.316, even when the player had no visual equipment equipped, each call walked LX_EQ_SLOT_ORDER[layer], did a property lookup on equipped[slot], hit LX_EQ_REGISTRIES[slot], and tested spriteId presence â€” 7 hash misses + 7 truthy checks per hero per frame, ~1,260 wasted ops/sec at 60 FPS for the common no-equipment case.

Fix: precompute a player._eqEmpty flag whenever the loadout changes and bail at the top of _drawEquipmentLayer when it's set. Three call sites refresh the flag: applyCharStudioToPlayer, revertCharStudioFromPlayer, and the loadState backfill. The studio-preview path (_LX_EQ_PREVIEW_OVERRIDE set) still goes through the full loop since CHAR_STUDIO.equip mutates as the user clicks â€” caching there would just churn.

Net: zero ops in the empty case; identical behaviour in the equipped case.

v0.25.315 Skip main render while paused â€” Wardrobe-open cost halves

polishFrom perf audit â€” biggest single hotspot fix

The main game loop only gated simulation updates (updatePlayer / updateMonsters / etc.) on game.paused; the entire render block (drawBackground + per-mob drawMonster + drawPlayer + drawAmbient + drawProjectiles + drawArtPostFX) ran every frame regardless. When the Q-key Wardrobe opened, game.paused = true stopped the simulation but the canvas behind the modal kept repainting at 60 FPS â€” pure waste, since the modal overlay covers the canvas with a 94 % opacity backdrop. Plus the studio's own renderCharStudio rAF was simultaneously rendering _drawVectorHero at 5Ã— scale to its own canvas, so wardrobe-open frame cost effectively doubled.

Same waste applies to every other paused modal â€” U-panel, Quest Journal, NPC dialog, shops, taxi, world map, etc. All set game.paused = true with a fullscreen 70â€“94 % opacity backdrop. None of them needed live render under the modal.

Fix: early-return the entire render block when game.paused. updateUI + _trimVisualQueues + the next requestAnimationFrame still fire so the loop stays alive and rendering resumes the instant the modal closes. The static last-rendered canvas frame stays painted underneath the modal, which is what the player effectively saw before â€” but now without burning 4â€“6 ms/frame redrawing it.

v0.25.314 Backstab pose duration â€” 6 s â†’ 360 ms

bugFrom gameplay audit â€” Backstab's "big-stab" pose was sticking around for six full seconds

The v0.25.296 backstab revert set player._bigStabUntil = (game.time | 0) + 360, treating the value as milliseconds. But game.time is a frame counter (incremented +1 per frame at 60 Hz), not ms â€” so + 360 meant 360 frames â‰ˆ 6 seconds. Every rogue basic attack for those six seconds after a Backstab cast rendered with the cranked big-stab geometry (4.15 rad swing, 1.5Ã— spine + head lurch) instead of the normal v0.25.220 thrust. Looked like the rogue had a permanent over-committed pose.

Fix: change + 360 to + 22 (â‰ˆ 360 ms at 60 Hz). Now matches the convention used by every other cast-pose flag (_chantRaiseUntil, _levitateUntil, _impalePoseUntil, _ninjaPoseUntil, _ninjaCrouchUntil, _mageBigSwingUntil â€” all + 22 or + 32 frames). The big-stab pose now reads as a single committed strike, then the body returns to the normal thrust on the next basic.

v0.25.313 Wardrobe weapon-slot fix â€” Apply / Revert no longer wipes loot stats

bugShowstopper from gameplay audit â€” Wardrobe was silently nuking the equipped weapon

The v0.25.308 wardrobe iterates all 7 visual-equipment slots (body_top, body_bottom, cape, gloves, boots, weapon, helmet) when committing or reverting picks. Six of those are visual-only â€” but weapon ALSO carries gameplay state (atk, vis palette, rarity, stars, setId) from loot drops. The pre-v0.25.313 paths blindly replaced player.equipped.weapon with a fresh {spriteId, tint} object, silently destroying every gameplay property the weapon carried. The player's loadout was nerfed the moment they hit Apply (or wiped entirely on Revert) without any feedback.

Fix: detect a stat-bearing item via the presence of an id field (loot items always carry one; pure visual overrides don't). For stat-bearing items, patch the visual fields (spriteId, tint) onto the existing item instead of replacing â€” gameplay properties stay intact. For purely-visual slots, the original replace-or-null path is unchanged.

Same fix applied to both applyCharStudioToPlayer and revertCharStudioFromPlayer. Other wardrobe slots (cape / gloves / etc.) currently never carry stats so the new branch is dormant for them, but the guard is correct if a future patch makes any of them stat-bearing too.

v0.25.312 Loading gate â€” wait for ALL sprites before world reveals

polishNo more sprites popping in mid-gameplay

The v0.25.237 three-bucket loader (BAZAAR / CRITICAL / DEFERRED) gated the world reveal on BAZAAR alone (~14 sprites â€” bazaar bg + first-paint NPCs + tiles). DEFERRED sprites (the other ~95 â€” boss bodies, master-class skill FX, equipment, less-common UI) streamed via requestIdleCallback AFTER the player spawned, which meant boss arenas opened with placeholder rectangles, master skills cast invisible until 5â€“30 s of streaming finished, and equipment never showed on first display until that bucket completed.

Fix: the gate now waits for ALL sprites. Three architectural changes:

  • All three buckets contribute to the reveal gate via maybeReveal, which now checks totalLoaded() >= totalCount() instead of just BAZAAR's count.
  • Progress bar tracks total progress across all 110+ paths instead of just BAZAAR's 14, with a longer phrase rotation (Painting Bazaar â†’ Lighting lanterns â†’ Waking merchants â†’ Stocking anvil â†’ Tuning monster brushes â†’ Sharpening boss blades â†’ Loading boss arena cinematics â†’ Polishing skill flourishes â†’ Almost there).
  • DEFERRED starts immediately instead of via requestIdleCallback â€” there's no urgency to defer when the player isn't playing yet. Fetch priority still favours BAZAAR + CRITICAL (fetchPriority: 'high') so the bar ramps fast at the start, slows for the tail.

Hard fallback timer raised from 4 s to 30 s so slow mobile connections have time to complete instead of force-revealing into a half-loaded world. Players on very slow networks still eventually see the game if one image is wedged. The corner-pill #defer-loader is no longer shown (DOM + CSS left for clean removal in a follow-up). Net effect: reveal takes a few seconds longer on first load (cold cache), but the game looks finished the moment the world appears.

v0.25.311 Loading page polish â€” gilded frame, prismatic sweep, serif title

polishThe boot loading screen now reads as an illuminated invocation panel

The pre-game loading overlay was already on a starfield with a gradient title + shimmer bar (v0.25.104), but the title floated alone over the stars with no anchoring frame. v0.25.311 wraps the content in a posh gilded panel:

  • Gilded outer ring â€” a conic-gradient border via a pseudo-element drawn in gold / amber / violet / sky blue, sitting just outside the panel.
  • Four corner ornaments â€” small filigree marks (gold L-brackets) at each corner of the frame, evoking an illuminated manuscript / spell tome.
  • Prismatic light sweep â€” a slow 6.5 s diagonal gradient sweep travels across the frame in screen blend mode. Subtle, never overpowering.
  • Serif title â€” switched from Inter sans to a display-serif stack (Cinzel â†’ Trajan Pro â†’ Cormorant Garamond â†’ Garamond â†’ Georgia) with letter-spacing bumped to 6px. Title now reads as engraved/typeset rather than UI-flat.
  • Gilded under-rule â€” a thin gold gradient line beneath the crest, above the title, separating crest from typography.
  • Crest refresh â€” the âœ¨ emoji swapped for a Unicode âœ¦ (four-pointed black star) rendered in cream gold, smaller (44 px vs 56 px) so it reads as a sigil rather than a face.

Backdrop, starfield, breath halo, shimmer bar, status / progress / tip lines all preserved. No behavioural changes; the reveal-gate timing and BAZAAR critical-path are untouched.

v0.25.310 Wardrobe revamp â€” MapleStory-style 3Ã—3 loadout grid

featQ-key Character Studio rebuilt as a slot-grid loadout panel

The linear-list-of-rows layout from v0.25.308 had grown to twelve <h3>+row sections â€” every slot stacked vertically, the panel scrolled forever, and the clutter buried the live preview. v0.25.310 replaces it with a MapleStory-inspired 3Ã—3 grid of clickable slot tiles (matching the user's reference screenshot) plus a single inline picker that switches between slots on click.

  • Nine tiles arranged 3Ã—3: HAIR, SKIN, TOP, BOTTOM, CAPE, GLOVES, BOOTS, WEAPON, HAT (helmet) â€” the user's exact requested slot list.
  • Each tile shows a live thumbnail of the currently-picked sprite (or a colour swatch for SKIN). Border colour reflects state: grey (unequipped), green (equipped), gold (active picker).
  • Click a tile â†’ the inline picker below rebuilds with that slot's options (None + every sprite in LX_EQ_REGISTRIES[slot], or the existing skin/hair/eye/mouth swatches for the cosmetic slots). Click an option â†’ state updates, that tile's thumbnail refreshes in-place. Other tiles + the picker DOM are untouched, so no flicker.
  • Live preview canvas moves to the left of the grid in a tighter 200Ã—200 frame; full _drawVectorHero render with the in-progress equipment override, same as v0.25.308.
  • Apply / Revert buttons unchanged behaviourally (commit / strip equipment + lookCustom).

Implementation: the existing builders (buildCharStudioHairStyles, buildCharStudioEquipPicker, etc.) still write into hidden mount divs in the modal. A new _wardrobeMountPicker moves the rendered DOM children into the visible #wardrobe-picker when a tile is activated, preserving every event handler. Picker option clicks fire a small _refreshWardrobeSlotTile that replaces just the affected tile's thumb. Net result: drop a new sprite into Sprites/character/<slot>/ and re-run the import script â€” it appears in the new grid automatically.

v0.25.309 Equipment invisibility fix â€” backfill slots after loadState / prestige

bugVisual equipment slots were getting wiped by save load + prestige reset

v0.25.308 wardrobe Apply silently dropped equipment picks for many users â€” root cause: player.equipped was being re-initialised in two places to the pre-v0.25.303 shape { weapon, armor, accessory }, dropping the six visual slots (body_top, body_bottom, cape, gloves, boots, helmet) before Apply ran.
