The Apply loop iterated CHAR_STUDIO.equip's slot list and wrote into player.equipped[slot], which technically added the new property... but the renderer's path also iterates the same way, so when both paths re-fired during the same session, ordering meant the visual data could land on a stale object.

Fix: the loadState defensive default now backfills any missing visual slot to null, and the prestige reset hard-assigns the full nine-slot shape. Both paths leave the existing stat-bearing slots (weapon, armor, accessory) untouched. Equipment now shows on character after Apply across all save states.

v0.25.308 Q wardrobe â€” equipment picker rows + live preview

featThe Character Studio now drives all 7 equipment slots end-to-end

Previously the only way to actually see the v0.25.307 equipment art was a console player.equipped.cape = { spriteId: 'short_cape' } incantation. v0.25.308 wires the full loop into the existing Q-key Character Studio:

  • Seven new picker rows in the studio modal â€” Body Top, Body Bottom, Cape, Gloves, Boots, Weapon, Helmet â€” each populated from the matching LX_EQ_REGISTRIES[slot] with a None button at the front for unequipping. One generic builder (buildCharStudioEquipPicker(slot)) drives all of them, so adding a new sprite means dropping the file in the slot folder and re-running the import script â€” no UI changes.
  • Live preview via a new _LX_EQ_PREVIEW_OVERRIDE module-level pointer. renderCharStudio sets it to CHAR_STUDIO.equip for the duration of the studio's _drawVectorHero call, then clears it. _drawEquipmentLayer now resolves equipment via that override first, falling back to player.equipped. Net effect: studio canvas reflects pending picks immediately, in-game render keeps using the committed loadout.
  • Apply commits CHAR_STUDIO.equip back into player.equipped alongside the existing lookCustom commit. Revert nulls every visual equipment slot AND mirrors the wipe into the studio's pending picks. Stat-bearing slots (armor, accessory) are deliberately untouched â€” they belong to gameplay loot, not the wardrobe.
  • Open seeds picks from player.equipped, shallow-cloning each slot so picker mutations don't leak into the live state until Apply is clicked.

Press Q in-game to try on any of the 17 sprites from v0.25.307 across all 7 slots. Tint UI is a follow-up; for now picks equip with tint: null (raw cream / grey palette). Combine with the v0.25.273 razor-sharp player render to see the wardrobe at full fidelity.

v0.25.307 Equipment art â€” first 17 Ludo-generated pieces wired in

featStarter wardrobe across all 7 slots, painted on the locked anchor template

First content drop into the v0.25.303-306 equipment scaffold. Seventeen Ludo-generated sprites authored using the locked prompt prefix from EQUIPMENT_TEMPLATE.md â€” every prompt explicitly cited the canvas anchor coordinates (head 400,130; shoulders 320/480, 400; hips 400,520; feet 380/420, 720; hand grips 260/540, 460) so the content lands at the right Y inside the 800Ã—800 canvas without per-piece offset tuning. Slot-by-slot:

  • body_top â€” light_tunic (cream V-neck), plate_chest (grey knight chest plate)
  • body_bottom â€” cloth_pants (cream loose trousers), plate_legs (grey articulated greaves)
  • cape â€” short_cape (cream shoulder cape), hooded_cloak (grey full-length hooded cloak)
  • gloves â€” cloth_gloves (cream fingerless), iron_gauntlets (grey knight gauntlets)
  • boots â€” leather_boots (cream knee-high), plate_sabatons (grey sabatons + greaves)
  • weapon â€” sword (cruciform longsword), staff (wizard staff w/ crystal orb), dagger (curved blade), bow (recurve)
  • helmet â€” wizard_hat (pointed brim), knight_helm (visored), hood (rogue cloth)

Every clothing piece (body_top, body_bottom, cape, gloves, boots, helmet) was prompted in NEUTRAL CREAM (#e8d8b8) or LIGHT GREY (#c8c8d0) base palettes per the EQUIPMENT_TEMPLATE.md tint-friendly authoring spec â€” multiply-blend tint via item.tint can recolour any of them at runtime, so 17 art pieces support 50+ items via tint variants. Weapons stay at full saturation since shape (not colour) is their identity.

Wired through: factory _lxEqRegistry(slotFolder, files) refactored to accept the file map inline (each call carries its sprite list), all 7 LX_* calls updated, all 17 paths added to the REQUIRED preload manifest. Import script (scripts/import_equipment_sprites.mjs) updated to print the full _lxEqRegistry call rather than a separate _LX_*_FILES object â€” output is now a one-line paste.

v0.25.306 Equipment system 4/4 â€” tint variants (one sprite, N items)

featItem-level tint field activates a cached multiply-blend recolor

Final commit of the equipment scaffold. Item definitions can now carry a tint: '#hex' field â€” when set, the renderer dispatches through a new _lxGetTintedEqSprite(img, tint) helper that returns a cached multiply-blended variant of the sprite. One authored sprite Ã— N tints = N distinct equipment items.

  • Recipe reuses _lxBakeTintedHead (the v0.25.191 head-tint baker): draw sprite â†’ globalCompositeOperation = 'multiply' + fillRect with the tint â†’ destination-in drawImage(sprite) to clip the multiply layer back to the sprite's alpha shape. Cached in _LX_TINTED_EQ_CACHE per (sprite-src, tint, dimensions) tuple â€” each baked canvas is ~50â€“200 KB; 20 active tinted variants â‰ˆ < 4 MB.
  • Authoring tip: paint master sprites in a neutral light grey / warm cream so the multiply produces a clean recolor. Pure white sprites multiply cleanest. Already documented in EQUIPMENT_TEMPLATE.md.
  • Cache key includes naturalWidth + naturalHeight so a hot-reload of the underlying image invalidates the cached variant naturally.

Item example: { id:'iron_plate_red', name:'Crimson Plate', slot:'body_top', spriteId:'iron_plate', tint:'#cc4444' } â€” same iron_plate.webp sprite, recolored red. Three more colours = three more items, no extra art.

summaryFour-commit equipment scaffold complete

The whole MapleStory-style layered equipment system is now wired end-to-end:

  • v0.25.303 â€” slot registries (LX_BODY_TOP + 6 more) + player.equipped state extension
  • v0.25.304 â€” EQUIPMENT_TEMPLATE.md spec + scripts/import_equipment_sprites.mjs batch importer
  • v0.25.305 â€” three-pass z-order render dispatch hooked into _drawVectorHero
  • v0.25.306 â€” tint variants via item.tint + multiply-blend cache

Engine ready, no art needed yet. Drop authored sprites into Sprites/character/<slot>/, run the import script, paste the printed registry block, equip items via gameplay (loot drops, shop purchases, set bonuses) â€” they show up on the character automatically.

v0.25.305 Equipment system 3/4 â€” z-order render dispatch

featThree-pass equipment rendering wired into _drawVectorHero

The locked z-order from EQUIPMENT_TEMPLATE.md is now actually wired. Three passes wrap around the existing bone rendering â€” same chibi-scale + facing-flip transform context, so equipment sprites mirror with the character automatically.

  • BACK pass â€” drawn before any bone. Sits behind the entire body. Slots: cape.
  • MID pass â€” drawn after the spine bone (torso) but before the head. Overlays the bone-rendered torso + limbs, sits UNDER the head silhouette so high-collar shirts cut cleanly at the neckline. Slots (in z-order): body_bottom, body_top, boots, gloves, weapon (back-hand).
  • FRONT pass â€” drawn after the procedural class-weapon overlay. Helmet sits on top of head + front-hair + face. Slots: helmet.

Each pass walks LX_EQ_SLOT_ORDER[layer], looks up player.equipped[slot], resolves item.spriteId through LX_EQ_REGISTRIES[slot], and drawImages the sprite at _LX_EQ_DEST ({x:-30, y:-80, w:60, h:80}). The dest rect covers the full chibi character â€” sprites authored on the 800Ã—800 anchor template land at the right Y automatically (head crown of canvas â†’ top of dest, feet of canvas â†’ near bottom). Per-slot offsets can be added later if a piece needs micro-tuning.

Defensive: bails if player.equipped is missing (Character Studio preview), or if a slot has no equipped item, no spriteId, or the sprite isn't decoded yet. Inert until art lands. Tint hook is a single placeholder line ready for commit 4 (v0.25.306).

v0.25.304 Equipment system 2/4 â€” canvas template + batch import script

featLocked anchor template + Node helper that turns a folder of sprites into ready-to-paste registry blocks

  • EQUIPMENT_TEMPLATE.md at repo root â€” the locked 800Ã—800 canvas spec. Documents anchor positions (head/shoulders/hips/hand-grips/feet), per-slot guidance for what content lands in each canvas, the locked Ludo prompt prefix to use for every generation, the filename â†’ spriteId convention, the final z-order, and tint-variant authoring tips. Author / Ludo-generate every piece against this spec â€” alignment is then automatic.
  • scripts/import_equipment_sprites.mjs â€” Node helper. Walks Sprites/character/<slot>/, lists *.webp / *.png that aren't placeholders (READMEs, .gitkeep), and prints two ready-to-paste blocks per slot: the inner files object for _lxEqRegistry, and a sample item-definition array. Filename â†’ spriteId conversion is automatic (lowercase, CamelCase â†’ snake_case, sanitised).

Workflow once you author / Ludo-generate a piece: drop it into Sprites/character/<slot>/<file>.webp, run node scripts/import_equipment_sprites.mjs, paste the printed block into maple_game.html at the indicated location, reload. You only edit content; the script handles the wiring.

Smoke-tested with no sprites (empty folders) and with a synthetic two-cape drop â€” converts RoyalCape.webp â†’ spriteId royal_cape + title Royal Cape automatically. Z-order rendering ships in commit 3 (v0.25.305); tint variants in commit 4 (v0.25.306).

v0.25.303 Equipment system 1/4 â€” slot registries + player state

featSeven new wearable-equipment registries scaffolded (no art yet)

First commit of a 4-part scaffold for a MapleStory-style layered equipment system. This commit adds the engine plumbing â€” registries + player state â€” without drawing anything yet. Z-order rendering (commit 3) and tint variants (commit 4) follow.

  • Seven Image registries matching the LX_HAIR / LX_EYES / LX_MOUTH pattern: LX_BODY_TOP, LX_BODY_BOTTOM, LX_CAPE, LX_GLOVES, LX_BOOTS, LX_WEAPON, LX_HELMET. Built via a tiny _lxEqRegistry(slotFolder) factory so adding an 8th slot in the future is one line.
  • LX_EQ_REGISTRIES â€” slot-id â†’ registry lookup so the dispatch in _drawEquipmentLayer (commit 3) can resolve slot â†’ sprite without a switch.
  • player.equipped extended with six new visual slots (body_top, body_bottom, cape, gloves, boots, helmet) alongside the existing stat-bearing weapon / armor / accessory. armor + accessory remain back-compat (still stat-only, not on-character); weapon now ALSO carries a spriteId for the on-character render.
  • Seven new sprite folders created with placeholder README.md pointing to the canvas template (commit 2): Sprites/character/{body_top,body_bottom,cape,gloves,boots,weapon,helmet}/.

Item schema for the new system: { id, name, slot:'body_top', spriteId:'iron_plate', tint:'#aaaadd' }. The tint field activates in commit 4. No gameplay changes ship in this commit â€” the registries are inert until the renderer hooks them in commit 3.

v0.25.302 Mobile controls fix â€” first tap was auto-hiding the deck

bugSynthetic key events from mobile buttons no longer trigger the keyboard auto-hide

The v0.25.246 "keyboard usage detected" feature listens for any gameplay key press and auto-hides the mobile control deck once, on the assumption that anyone typing on a real keyboard doesn't need the on-screen buttons. The check at window.addEventListener('keydown', ...) didn't distinguish synthetic KeyboardEvents (which the mobile deck itself dispatches via _mkeyDispatch when the player taps a button) from physical key presses.

Result: mobile players opening the game with no localStorage preference set hit the bug on their very first tap â€” the synthetic event from tapping any d-pad / skill / pot button was treated as "keyboard detected", the deck auto-hid, and the player couldn't tap anything anymore (since the buttons were now display: none). Looked exactly like "mobile controls don't work".

Fix: gate the auto-hide on e.isTrusted. Synthetic new KeyboardEvent(...) events have isTrusted=false by spec; physical keystrokes have isTrusted=true. Only physical key presses now arm the auto-hide. The deck's manual âœ•/â—€ toggle still works for desktop users with touchscreens, and the ?nomobile=1 URL escape still works as before.

v0.25.301 Q wardrobe â€” user-curated face-feature set

polishDropped the v0.25.295 AI-generated face features that didn't match the hand-painted look

v0.25.295 added 18 Ludo-generated face-feature sprites (6 hair / 6 eyes / 6 mouth) but several didn't quite hit the hand-painted aesthetic of the existing flow / ponytail / default sprites. v0.25.301 ships the user's curated final set:

  • Hair (7): Flow, Ponytail, Shaggy, Spiky, Flowy (re-rendered), Ice Cream ðŸ¦ (NEW â€” soft tower-bun silhouette), Pigeotto ðŸ¦ (NEW â€” front-tuft cropped). Dropped: bob, emo, mohawk, pigtails, topknot.
  • Eyes (2): Default, Fierce (re-rendered as a clean cropped pair, 14 KB vs the previous 145 KB). Dropped: sparkle, sleepy, star, puppy, cat.
  • Mouth (1): Default. Dropped: grin, smirk, surprise, tongue, stoic, laugh. New mouth variants will be authored manually as they're produced.

Wired through LX_HAIR.files / LX_EYES.files / LX_MOUTH.files registries, HERO_VEC_*_OPTIONS picker lists, the _migrateHairId save-compat whitelist, and the REQUIRED preload manifest. Saves still using a dropped hair id fall through to flow via the migration whitelist's trailing return â€” no save corruption. ice cream.webp was renamed to ice_cream.webp for URL safety; the picker id is iceCream.

v0.25.300 BGM â€” fully delete the legacy procedural synth

polish~265 lines of dead chiptune code removed; mp3 BGM is now the only music system

Follow-up to v0.25.299, which only stubbed music.play() to a no-op. v0.25.300 deletes the entire legacy procedural Web-Audio synth: the music object (init / play / stop / _schedule / _tone), the MUSIC_TRACKS per-map note tables (town / forest / mushroom / boss / zodiac / tower), the MAP_MUSIC map-id â†’ track-id table, the _mapTrackId resolver, the _mnote note-name â†’ frequency helper, and the REST constant. All call sites in loadMap and audio.toggleMute are gone too.

Mute wiring rebuilt directly on the v0.25.233 mp3 BGM elements: audio.toggleMute now sets _bgmEl.muted + _bgmBossEl.muted, and the boot path that applies the saved game.muted state from localStorage syncs the same fields. Single source of truth, no parallel music systems possible.

Net diff: ~265 lines deleted from maple_game.html, +14 lines of mp3-BGM mute wiring. Players hear exactly one track at a time â€” bgm_mojiworld.mp3 on regular maps, fading to bgm_boss.mp3 on boss arenas via the v0.25.265 _setBossBgm hook.

v0.25.299 BGM â€” disable old procedural synth (was double-playing)

bugOnly the main mp3 BGM plays now

The v0.25.233 looping mp3 BGM (_bgmEl â†’ audio/bgm_mojiworld.mp3, with the v0.25.265 boss-arena variant) was running in parallel with the legacy procedural Web-Audio synth at music (lines ~10672+). Both started on every map load â€” players heard the mp3 + the chiptune lead/bass scheduler at the same time, two tracks layered.

Fix: stub music.play() to a no-op. It still records _lastMap (so the audio.toggleMute unmute path doesn't choke) and calls stop() to clear any in-flight scheduling that pre-dated this update. The synth's _schedule / _tone / MUSIC_TRACKS / _mapTrackId code is left intact in case the procedural system is ever re-enabled, but no call site triggers it now. Result: only the mp3 BGM (bgm_mojiworld.mp3 on regular maps, bgm_boss.mp3 on boss arenas) is audible.

v0.25.298 Authored sprites â€” Lich G vortex pool + sage F/G ground marker

featTwo new Ludo-generated FX sprites land in the hazard renderer

  • Lich Â· Soul Vortex (G) â€” new soul_vortex.webp drawn UNDER the existing rotating-ring + wisp particle stack. The pool now reads as a proper authored portal of swirling green soul-energy with violet inner glow + cracked ground bleed, while the procedural rings keep playing on top so the channel still has motion. Falls back to the v0.25.201 procedural ellipses if the sprite hasn't decoded yet.
  • Sage Â· Meteor + Meteor Shower (F + G) â€” new meteor_marker.webp replaces the procedural red rect + yellow ellipse outline that marked the meteor impact zone on the ground. The marker is now a glowing magic rune circle with concentric rings + fiery cracks. Alpha pulses brighter as the impact approaches (tracks prog). The meteor sprite itself is unchanged â€” only the ground marker changed, per request.

Wired through LX_FX.files registry; both paths added to the REQUIRED preload manifest so they decode before first paint. Hazard dispatch in drawHazards renders the sprite under the existing animation layers when ready, falls back to the procedural draw otherwise â€” no behaviour break for old saves or slow connections.

v0.25.297 Archer attack â€” realistic draw, settle, release

polishBow shooting now reads as a real archery stance: brace, draw, hold, release

The v0.25.194 archer cycle was already structured as windup â†’ draw â†’ release, but the curves were too even and the body was a static torso. Tuned to feel like a real archer:

  • Front (bow) arm raises faster (0.08 vs 0.10 windup) so the bow settles before the back arm starts pulling, holds steady through the aim with a subtle 22 Hz string-tension wobble (~0.02 rad), then kicks UP on release before recovering â€” the bow visibly recoils up + back instead of just lowering.
  • Back (string) arm draws deeper (+2.10 rad, was +1.90), holds at full draw with a small tremor selling the held tension, snaps forward sharply (0.05 release window vs 0.07), and overshoots forward briefly before settling â€” the string actually firing.
  • Body leans subtly BACK during the 0.50 s draw (-0.10 spine, -0.09 head), then snaps FORWARD on release (+0.18 / +0.14) as recoil travels through the torso. Legs widen into an archery brace (front -0.20, back +0.18).

Net effect: instead of a static body holding a bow at the same angle for the whole shot, the archer now settles into their stance, holds tension visibly, fires with a snap, and recovers â€” same shot timing, much more committed read.

v0.25.296 Backstab â€” revert somersault, restore as exaggerated big stab

polishBackstab now reads as a bigger committed stab instead of a back-flip

v0.25.288 layered a backward somersault on top of Backstab's 3-stab combo, but the spin clashed with the skill's own blink-strike movement and made the read confusing. Reverted to a flag (player._bigStabUntil) that amplifies the existing rogue thrust curves into an even bigger committed stab â€” deeper windup pull behind the hip, fuller arm extension past the body, longer held thrust, and a 50 % stronger spine + head lurch through the strike. Default rogue thrust geometry is preserved when the flag isn't live.

  • Front arm: -1.95 â†’ +2.20 rad (4.15 rad swing) vs the default 3.40 rad
  • Back arm: mirrors with deeper windup raise (+1.15) and harder forward drive (-0.70)
  • Body: spine + head bob amplitudes Ã—1.5 during the strike, selling the full hip-rotation commit

v0.25.295 Q-key wardrobe â€” 6 new hair / eyes / mouth sprites each

feat18 Ludo-generated face-feature sprites land in the Character Studio

The Q-key wardrobe (Character Studio) was capped at 4 hairstyles + 1 eye style + 1 mouth style after the v0.25.270 expansion. v0.25.295 adds 6 of each â€” 18 new sprites total â€” generated via Ludo.ai with consistent prompts targeting the same chibi-anime style and 800Ã—800 head-aligned anchor as the existing flow / ponytail / default sprites. Output is 768Ã—768 native (~4 % up-scale by the head-rect dispatch); positioning is best-effort across AI generations and may need per-sprite micro-tuning if a feature lands a few pixels off.

  • Hair (6 new): Flowy ðŸŒŠ, Pigtails ðŸŽ€, Bob âœ‚ï¸, Topknot ðŸŽŽ, Mohawk ðŸ¦…, Emo ðŸ–¤ â€” joins Flow / Ponytail / Shaggy / Spiky for 10 hairstyles total.
  • Eyes (6 new): Sparkle âœ¨, Sleepy ðŸ˜´, Fierce ðŸ”¥, Star â­, Puppy ðŸ¶, Cat ðŸ± â€” joins Default for 7 eye styles.
  • Mouth (6 new): Grin ðŸ˜„, Smirk ðŸ˜, Surprise ðŸ˜®, Tongue ðŸ˜, Stoic ðŸ˜, Laugh ðŸ˜† â€” joins Default for 7 mouth styles.

Wired through LX_HAIR.files / LX_EYES.files / LX_MOUTH.files registries, HERO_VEC_HAIR_OPTIONS / EYE_OPTIONS / MOUTH_OPTIONS picker lists, the _migrateHairId save-compat whitelist, and the REQUIRED preload manifest so all 18 decode before first paint of the Studio. Press Q in-game to try them on.

v0.25.294 Lich + Hexmaster G â€” levitate, arms spread wide

featDark-magic hover pose for the warlock master signatures

Lich's Soul Vortex (key G on the Lich master) and Hexmaster's Grand Hex (key G on the Hexmaster master) now play a unique attack_mage_levitate animation across a 32-frame (~540 ms) window:

  • Rise (first ~40 %): body lifts ~10 px via the spine + head bob (no physics change so the player stays anchored for the AoE), both arms sweep from neutral out to fully horizontal palms-down (â‰ˆ 1.55 rad each), spine straightens with a slight back arch, head tilts up.
  • Hover (last ~60 %): hold the spread + lift with a slow 2 Hz breath wobble for the rest of the cast â€” sells the "still levitating, vortex/hex actively channelling" beat.

Implementation: the warlock/archmage chant-raise branch (v0.25.290) now explicitly excludes lich_harvest and hexmaster_grandhex so the new levitate flag wins. castSkill stamps player._levitateAt = game.time and bumps attackTimer to 540 ms. New entries in HERO_VEC_ANIMS / DEFORM / BOB; _heroVecAnimFor + _heroVecTimeNorm route to the new pose. Other warlock skills (Soul Siphon, Dark Pulse) still use the chant-raise pose.

v0.25.293 Shadowlord + Shinobi G â€” exaggerated ninja poses

featStance + crouch hand-seal poses for the rogue ninja masters

Two new pose-driven anims for the ninja-line G skills, both held for ~75 % of a 32-frame (~540 ms) window with a small 3 Hz breath wobble before relaxing back to neutral in the last 25 %:

  • Shadowlord Â· Mirror Shadow (key G) â€” attack_rogue_ninja: front arm extended forward at shoulder height (open-palm seal, -1.4 rad), back arm cocked low at the hip (+0.5 rad), legs in a wide stance (-0.55 / +0.62), spine slightly leaned into the front foot, head turned with the seal.
  • Shinobi Â· Kage Rush (key G) â€” attack_rogue_crouch: deep squat (legs at -0.85 / +0.85, body dropped 3-4 px), BOTH hands meeting at the chest in a hand-seal mudra (-0.4 / +0.4), head tucked, spine stooped (+0.30). Reads as the classic mudra-charge before the kage-rush teleport chain kicks in.

Implementation: castSkill stamps player._ninjaPoseAt / _ninjaCrouchAt for the matching ids and bumps attackTimer to 540 ms. New entries in HERO_VEC_ANIMS / DEFORM / BOB; _heroVecAnimFor + _heroVecTimeNorm route to the new poses only while the flags are live. Other rogue masters (Phantom, Nightreaper) keep the v0.25.220 thrust pose.

v0.25.292 Dragoon Sky Lance â€” exaggerated impale to ground

featTwo-handed lance hoist + downward thrust on Sky Lance casts

Dragoon's Sky Lance (key G on the Dragoon master) now plays a unique attack_warrior_impale animation across a 32-frame (~540 ms) window:

  • Windup (first ~40 %): both arms hoist the lance straight overhead (-2.0 rad), spine arches BACK, head tilts up to track the lance tip â€” body coiled like a spring, lifted ~4 px.
  • Thrust (last ~60 %): both arms slam DOWN past horizontal (~3.6 rad sweep), spine collapses forward, head dives with the strike, legs fold into a deep landing crouch, body drops ~5 px to sell the impale into the ground.

Implementation: castSkill stamps player._impalePoseAt = game.time for id === 'dragoon_skylance' and bumps attackTimer to 540 ms. New entries in HERO_VEC_ANIMS / DEFORM / BOB; _heroVecAnimFor + _heroVecTimeNorm route to the new pose only while the flag is live. Other warrior masters (Crusader, Warlord, Doombringer) keep their existing animations.

v0.25.291 Berserker masters â€” jump somersault into slashing frenzy

featWarlord + Doombringer signatures get a flip launch

Warlord's Banner (key G on the Warlord master) and Blade of Calamity (key G on the Doombringer master) now lead with a forward jump somersault â€” same transform as Warrior Â· Ground Slam (v0.25.288) but lifted higher (vy = -12 vs -10) and held longer (700 ms vs 600 ms) so the roar / 10-cleave visuals get room to land alongside the spin. attackTimer is extended to 700 ms so the attack pose stays active through the landing.

v0.25.290 Warlock + Archmage F/V/G â€” chant pose, then raise the staff

featTwo-phase signature pose for every warlock + archmage signature skill

The six big mage skills â€” Warlock Soul Siphon (F), Dark Pulse (V), Soul Vortex / Grand Hex (G), and Archmage Meteor (F), Elemental Convergence (V), Meteor Shower / Prismatic Cascade (G) â€” now play a unique attack_mage_chant animation that splits the cast into two readable beats:

  • Chant (first ~55 % of the cast): hands close near the chest, spine stoops slightly, head bows. A small ~6 Hz tremor on the head + 8 Hz tremor on the arms sells the muttering incantation. ~300 ms.
  • Raise (last ~45 %): dominant arm sweeps overhead with the staff (~2 rad lift), off arm drifts to the side opening the silhouette, spine extends, head tilts up to look at the staff tip. ~240 ms.

Implementation notes: castSkill stamps player._chantRaiseAt = game.time and bumps attackTimer to 540 ms (the default 18-frame attack budget would zoom past both beats). New entries in HERO_VEC_ANIMS / DEFORM / BOB drive the spine, head, and arm bones across the 32-frame window. _heroVecAnimFor returns 'attack_mage_chant' while the flag is live; _heroVecTimeNorm uses the longer custom budget. Other mage skills keep their existing pose (basic Z = calm cast, S/D = v0.25.289 big swing).

v0.25.289 Mage S + D â€” exaggerated staff swing

featIce Spike + Arcane Burst now read as wide arcing casts

The default mage cast pose (v0.25.194) raises the staff overhead and holds it for the channel â€” visually calm, suited for charged spells. Ice Spike (key S) and Arcane Burst (key D) are short-cooldown burst skills that should feel committed, so they now drive a wider, more athletic swing instead.

Implementation: castSkill stamps player._mageBigSwingUntil = game.time + 360 for those two ids. While the flag is live, _heroVecMageFrontArm sweeps the staff from behind the head all the way past horizontal (~3.8 rad arc, vs the calm cast's 1.5 rad raise), _heroVecMageBackArm swings opposite for counterweight, and the attack_mage spine + head bob lurches forward through the strike (with a windup back-lean) instead of leaning back. Other mage skills (basic Z, F, V, G, Q) keep the calm cast.

v0.25.288 Skill animation flourishes â€” somersault flips

featThree signature skills now somersault during the cast

Reuses the existing player.somersault + somersaultDir transform built for the archer's evadeRoll (which rotates the chibi figure around its torso centre over a configurable duration). New triggers in castSkill:

  • Warrior Â· Ground Slam (key S) â€” vy = -10 launch + forward 600 ms somersault. Reads as "hop, flip, slam" feeding into the existing shockwave AoE.
  • Thief Â· Throw Dagger (key S) â€” vy = -8 hop + forward 480 ms flip. The 5-lane dagger fan releases mid-spin, so the wide swing visual is now sold by the body twist.
  • Thief Â· Backstab (key X) â€” backward 420 ms somersault during the 3-stab combo. No vy change since backstab already has its own blink-and-strike movement; the spin is pure flair on top.

v0.25.287 World Map â€” Gelwater Grotto anchored beside Emerald Thicket

featJelly King's grotto sits next to its lore-aligned onramp

Gelwater Grotto (slimeCave) now positions ~56 px to the right of Emerald Thicket (forest) on the early ring instead of the bottom-left corner slot. The Jelly King is the first boss a new player faces, and the in-game route from forest â†’ grotto is the first boss-fight onramp â€” so the W map now reads that connection at a glance. Computed dynamically from positions.forest after the early ring is placed; falls back to the legacy bottom-left corner if forest isn't on the map for some reason (defensive â€” shouldn't happen in practice).

v0.25.286 Drop boot-time bake-cache wipes â€” 1â€“5 MB of throwaway alloc gone

polishLoading-to-play transition no longer thrashes the bake cache

From perf audit (#8). The LX_HAIR and LX_HEAD sprite loaders had onload callbacks that wiped _LX_HERO_IDLE_BAKE_CACHE (and the tinted-head cache). 4 hair sprites + 1 head sprite = up to 5 cache wipes during the first ~200 ms after boot, each forcing re-bakes at 500Ã—500 = ~1 MB throwaway canvas each. Visible jank during the loading-to-play transition.

Fix: removed the wipes entirely. Since v0.25.273 the player render path doesn't read from the bake cache anyway (vectors render directly each frame for razor-sharp edges), so the wipes were pure overhead. The tinted-head cache wipe is also dropped â€” it's keyed by skinIdx, and sprite paths are static at boot so hot-reload isn't a real flow. Net win: 1â€“5 MB of throwaway canvas allocation eliminated during the transition.

v0.25.285 Zodiac god-ray gradient â€” bake once, drawImage forever

polish~5 KB / frame GC churn eliminated on light-element zodiac arenas

From perf audit (#5). Light-element zodiac arenas (Virgo) draw 5 animated god-rays each frame, and each ray previously allocated a fresh createLinearGradient call. Gradient creation is one of the hottest GC sources in the canvas API, so this was ~5 KB of throwaway allocation per frame whenever the player was inside one of those arenas.

Fix: bake the per-ray diagonal gradient once into a module-level 80Ã—H offscreen canvas (_getZodiacGodRayCanvas), then drawImage it at each animated ray position. The intra-ray geometry is constant â€” only the X offset changes per frame â€” so the cached bake is visually identical. drawImage is ~10Ã— cheaper than allocating a fresh gradient + fillRect.

v0.25.284 Mini-boss ember trail â€” drop dead callback

polishOne closure + one property write removed per ember emit

From perf audit (#4). The mini-boss ember-trail emit allocated a color: i => Math.random() < 0.5 ? '#ffdd66' : '#c88aff' arrow function on every push, then immediately overwrote it with a literal string on the very next line â€” a historical workaround comment even said "Fix: life cb not used". Net effect: one wasted closure + one wasted property write per emit. Cleaned up so the color is picked inline at literal-creation time.

v0.25.283 Film grain â€” half the frames, tighter rect

polishdrawArtPostFX grain pass costs ~50% less per second

From perf audit (#2). The film-grain pass was an overlay composite over the full 1.5Ã—-DPR backing buffer (~1.2 M pixels) every frame, plus an over-sized (W+128, H+128) rect. The grain animation rate is ~7 Hz (from the * 0.11 / 0.09 step), so the eye can't distinguish 60 Hz grain from 30 Hz anyway.

Fix: gate to every other frame ((game.time & 1) === 0) and shrink the rect to (W, H). The +128 padding was vestigial â€” translate(-ox, -oy) shifts UVs by max 128, but the canvas pattern repeats automatically so a (W, H) rect tiles correctly. Net: ~50% less overlay-blend GPU work per second from this pass.

v0.25.282 drawAmbient â€” batch glow halos under one composite swap

polish~80 composite swaps + save/restores â†’ 1 of each, per frame

From perf audit (#6). Each ember / firefly / wisp particle did its own ctx.save() + globalCompositeOperation = 'lighter' + fillRect + ctx.restore(). With up to 80 active particles on lava / firefly / sanctum / zodiac maps, that's 80 save/restores + 80 composite swaps every frame â€” composite swaps are heavy on Chrome's compositor.

Fix: two-pass split. Pass 1 batches every glow halo (ember + firefly + wisp) under a single shared 'lighter' composite block. Pass 2 draws crisp cores + non-glow types (leaf, petal, spore, drop, ash, snow, sand) under the default composite. Same matching pattern already used by drawParticles. Net: ~80 composite swaps + 80 save/restores collapsed to 1 of each per frame on glow-heavy maps. Visuals are pixel-identical.

v0.25.281 drawMonster â€” skip redundant ctx.filter reset on burning mobs

polishEliminate compositor stalls on burning-but-not-flashing mobs

From perf audit (#3). The burn-effect block in drawMonster unconditionally assigned ctx.filter = 'none' on every burning frame. Each filter assignment forces Chrome's compositor to flush, and the assignment was a no-op when the mob wasn't also hit-flashing (filter was already 'none'). With 8â€“15 burning mobs in combat, that's 8â€“15 redundant compositor stalls per frame.

Fix: gate the reset behind if (flashOn). Burning + flashing mobs still get the reset so burn particles render at baseline brightness instead of brightness(2.5); burning-only mobs skip the swap entirely. Behaviour is identical, the no-op stalls are gone.

v0.25.280 drawAfterImages off-screen X cull

polishPhantom-Step / dash afterimages no longer pay full pixel cost off-screen

From perf audit (#7). drawAfterImages wrote a translucent fillRect for every active clone unconditionally â€” Phantom-Step and similar dash skills spawn 8â€“20 clones whose origins are often on the previous platform, well outside the camera window. Cheap rects, but with a globalAlpha rebind per frame they still hit the rasteriser. Mirrors the cull pattern already used in drawMonster and drawAmbient: skip when the screen-space rect is fully left-of or right-of the viewport.

v0.25.279 Drop-through ghost-drop fix â€” intent captured at press time

bugBuffered jumps no longer phase through platforms unintentionally

Repro from gameplay audit (#7): jumping over stacked mobs while crouching to peek would occasionally phase through one-tile platforms. Cause: the jump buffer consulted k['arrowdown'] at fire time, not at press time. A fresh mid-air space press (no arrowdown) would buffer for 120 ms; if the player started holding arrowdown before they landed within that window, the buffered jump fired the drop-through branch.

Fix: capture player._jumpBufferDropIntent = !!k['arrowdown'] at the moment Space is pressed, and gate the drop-through branch on that flag. Drop-through now requires â†“ + Space to have been pressed together, matching the input the player actually intended. Normal jump buffering for late-press-into-landing still works exactly as before.

v0.25.278 Boon inventory cap enforced on every acquisition path

bugPowerup choice modal + legacy migration now respect BOON_INVENTORY_CAP

Two related gameplay-audit findings on the boon-inventory cap (25). acquirePowerup already enforced the cap, but two side paths bypassed it:

  • Powerup choice modal (#6) â€” the card.onclick in showPowerupChoice did its own player.boons.push(inst) without checking the cap. Boss-kill drops could grow the inventory past 25, and the U-panel's bag grid (which assumes the cap holds) would render the 26th boon outside its bounds. Now shows the same "Boon bag full â€” discard one first" toast and closes the modal cleanly.
  • Legacy migration (#4) â€” _migrateLegacyPowerups looped over the saved _powerups array unbounded. Low-likelihood since the POWERUPS array is short, but a hand-edited or pre-cap save could break the invariant. Added a break when the cap is reached.

v0.25.277 Charge-release defensive guards (Tower / death / silent-consume)

bugThree audit findings collapsed into one fix on tryStart/ReleaseClassCharge

  • Tower basicOnly windup (#3) â€” warriors / archers in a Tower run with the basicOnly modifier could still start a charge windup on a non-d skill, only to have castSkill silently drop it on release. Added an early-return in tryStartClassCharge so the windup never begins; the player no longer wastes animation frames + sound on a misfire.
  • Death-state leak (#5) â€” _tickClassIdentity clears _warCharge when the player drops to 0 HP, but a synchronous keyup can race past it on the same frame. Added a hard guard at the top of tryReleaseClassCharge so the cast no longer deducts MP / sets cooldown on a corpse.
  • Silent-consume feedback (#8) â€” when a Tower modifier or skill-tree mid-charge change made the skill un-ready, the charge was eaten with no UI feedback. Added a brief "Skill not ready" toast.

v0.25.276 Projectile-sprite lookup hoisted out of the render loop

polish~3,600 closure allocations/sec eliminated from drawProjectiles

Top finding from the perf audit. The skill-id â†’ sprite resolver inside drawProjectiles was an IIFE (immediately-invoked function expression) running per-projectile-per-frame. With 30â€“60 player projectiles in flight (rapid-fire ranger, mage AoE), that allocated 1,800â€“3,600 closure objects per second of pure GC churn â€” for a constant-string branch that didn't depend on per-frame state.

Fix: hoisted the resolver to a module-scope _LX_PROJ_FOR_SKILL lookup table sitting next to LX_PLAYER_PROJ. The render-loop call collapsed from a 14-branch chain inside an IIFE to a single dict lookup: _LX_PROJ_FOR_SKILL[p.skill] || null. Behaviour is identical (same skillâ†’sprite mapping, including the v0.25.209 bolt â†’ mage_orb remap), and the procedural-draw fallback for unknown skill ids still fires when the lookup misses.

v0.25.275 Quickening exploit fix â€” Sleight excluded from cooldown bypass

bugQuickening no longer refunds Sleight charges (infinite-teleport exploit)

Showstopper from gameplay audit. Quickening's cooldown bypass at the top of castSkill ran before the Sleight charge block, so when a rogue/Phantom's Sleight burned its third charge, the bypass would zero player.skillCooldowns['sleight'] â€” and the Sleight branch's charges <= 0 â†’ refill 3 path then triggered on the very next press. Net effect: ~12 % chance per cast to refund the entire Sleight cadence and gain three more zips, by far the strongest exploit any rogue/Phantom could uncover.

Fix: sleight joins phantom_cut and nightreaper_mark in the Quickening exclusion list. The 3-charge system stays authoritative on Sleight's cooldown regardless of cdrChance rolls; the two 12 s ultimates remain CDR-immune as before. Other skills still benefit from Quickening normally.

v0.25.274 Sage Mira refund fix â€” bag-full check before deduction

bugSage Mira no longer eats 5000 Lumens + 12 h cooldown when bag is full

Showstopper from gameplay audit. buyRandomPowerup deducted SAGE_PRICE (5000 Lumens) and set _sageNextAt = Date.now() + SAGE_COOLDOWN_MS before calling acquirePowerup. When the player's boon bag was at BOON_INVENTORY_CAP (25), acquirePowerup bailed with a "Boon bag full" toast â€” but the side effects had already landed. Player paid in full, got nothing, and was locked out of the Sage for 12 real-time hours.

Fix: pre-check player.boons.length < BOON_INVENTORY_CAP before any deduction. If full, the player gets the same "discard one first (press U)" toast that acquirePowerup would have shown, with no Lumens lost and no cooldown imposed.

v0.25.273 Razor-sharp player edges â€” direct vector render

polishVectors now rasterise at the final pixel grid (zero resamples)

Three previous attempts (v0.25.266 nearest-neighbour, v0.25.269 3Ã— super-sample, v0.25.271 5Ã— super-sample) all kept the v0.25.176 idle bake-cache and tried to make the bilinear downsample look sharper. Bilinear is slightly soft by definition â€” every cached blit went through â‰¥1 resample (chibi 1.19Ã— horizontal, optional 1.5Ã— HiDPI vertical) so the player edges always looked a touch fuzzy compared to the Q-key Character Studio preview, which renders the same vectors directly to its panel.

Fix in v0.25.273: _drawVectorHeroCached now always falls through to _drawVectorHero(sx, sy). The vector pipeline rasterises the body / legs / arms / face directly at the post-chibi, post-HiDPI pixel grid â€” no offscreen canvas, no resample, no bilinear softening. Now that the v0.25.157 SVG goo filter is gone (replaced by sprite hair / head / eyes / mouth in v0.25.178+), the per-frame cost of _drawVectorHero is dominated by four small drawImage calls plus a handful of Path2D fills â€” well within budget at 60 FPS on mobile.

The bake function (_lxBakeHeroIdle) and cache map (_LX_HERO_IDLE_BAKE_CACHE) are kept in place so any path that still references them keeps linking, but the player render no longer reads from the cache. Net result: the in-game character now matches the Q-window's razor-sharp edges.

v0.25.272 Cinematic zodiac arena bgs Â· World Map boss layout fix

featAuthored cinematic backdrops on every zodiac arena

The 12 zodiac boss arenas now ride the four authored bg_v3_cinematic_zodiac_1..4.png hero backdrops instead of the procedural starfield from drawZodiacArena. A new _ZODIAC_BG_CYCLE = ['cosmos', 'zodiacDawn', 'zodiacDusk', 'zodiacSunset'] cycles by sign order, so each of the four backdrops covers three signs across the progression (Aries / Leo / Sagittarius on cosmos, Taurus / Virgo / Capricorn on dawn, etc.). The v0.25.268 drawBackground gate already passes through to the bitmap pick when md.bg is set + loaded â€” no procedural code needed to change.

bugWorld Map boss layout â€” Singularity beside Aetherion, Queen's Hollow lifted

The level-sorted slot rotation in _wmComputePositions only had three explicit corner slots, so the 4th boss arena (The Singularity, gravitosArena) was collapsing onto slot 0 and stacking on top of Gelwater Grotto at the bottom-left. Replaced the rotation with an explicit per-id override map:

  • Gelwater Grotto (slimeCave) â€” bottom-left, unchanged
  • Queen's Hollow (boss) â€” bottom-right, lifted from y = H âˆ’ 40 to y = H âˆ’ 75 (a few dozen pixels up, as requested) so the node + label clears the legend bar
  • Aetherion's Sanctum (sanctum) â€” top-left, unchanged
  • The Singularity (gravitosArena) â€” pinned at (140, 40) directly to the right of Aetherion, so the two endgame fights read as a coupled pair on the W map

Any future boss arena that lacks an override entry falls through to the original 3-slot rotation, so the legacy behaviour is preserved.

v0.25.271 Crisper player render Â· Sage Mira taller

polishCrisper in-game character render

Pushed the cached idle bake from 3Ã— â†’ 5Ã— super-sample (_LX_HERO_BAKE_SUPER). The bake canvas is now 500Ã—500 physical pixels for a 100Ã—100 logical footprint, so the source-to-destination ratio comfortably exceeds the combined HiDPI canvas backing buffer (1.5Ã— DPR on desktop) plus the 1.19Ã— chibi horizontal scale. The browser's imageSmoothingQuality = 'high' downsample then has plenty of source pixels to anti-alias from, eliminating the residual softness from v0.25.269's 3Ã— setting. Memory â‰ˆ 1 MB per cached look (Ã—6â€“12 active looks â‰ˆ 12 MB max), well within budget. Diminishing returns above 5Ã— â€” going higher trades memory for no visible benefit.

featSage Mira renders taller without bilinear blur

New NPC_SPRITE_HEIGHTS map provides per-NPC render-height overrides for _drawNpcSprite. Sage Mira's entry sets her to 115 px (vs the 78 px default), reading as the lore-implied sage who towers over the bazaar regulars. Her source sprite is 640Ã—864, so even at the new 115 px height the draw is still a ~7.5Ã— downsample â€” no upscale, no bilinear blur. Smoothing quality bumped to 'high' at the same site so the larger render benefits from the better downsampler.

v0.25.270 Two new hairstyles â€” Shaggy + Spiky

featShaggy + Spiky hairstyles in the wardrobe

Two new sprite hairstyles drop into the Q-key Character Studio picker alongside Flow and Ponytail: Shaggy ðŸ‚ (loose unkempt layered cut) and Spiky âš¡ (shounen pointed clusters). Both ship as 800Ã—800 head-aligned WebPs in Sprites/character/hair/, matching the existing format so they slot straight into the LX_HAIR dispatch in drawBone('head') with no render-rect changes.

  • Wired sites: LX_HAIR.files (sprite registry), HERO_VEC_HAIR_OPTIONS (picker list), _migrateHairId whitelist (saves with the new ids pass through untouched), and the REQUIRED preload manifest so the sprites are decoded before first paint
  • No vector goo entries: sprites are the canonical source. flow and ponytail already shipped sprite-only and the renderer falls back gracefully to flow's vector goo for the brief decode window if needed
  • Cache invalidation: the existing LX_HAIR onload hook clears _LX_HERO_IDLE_BAKE_CACHE when each new sprite finishes loading, so the v0.25.269 super-sampled idle bake re-bakes with the sprite once it's ready

v0.25.269 Sharp idle hero Â· Amnesiac under Sanctum portal Â· Quickening boon

polishIdle hero stays sharp under the chibi transform

The cached idle frame (_lxBakeHeroIdle, introduced in v0.25.176 for the 60 % CPU win) used to bake the hero at logical 100Ã—100 pixels and then blit through the _chibiApply 1.19Ã—1.06 scale. v0.25.266 disabled imageSmoothingEnabled around that blit to escape the bilinear soft-blur, but the trade-off was nearest-neighbour pixel jaggies â€” the player still didn't read as razor-sharp the way the Q-key Character Studio preview does at 5Ã— scale.

Fix: the bake is now super-sampled at 3Ã— (300Ã—300 physical pixels for the same 100Ã—100 logical footprint), and the blit re-enables imageSmoothingEnabled = true with imageSmoothingQuality = 'high' to anti-alias the downscale. The cache key is unchanged so the per-look memoisation still hits, and the 60 % perf win is preserved (vector eval still happens once per look swap, not every frame). Net result: idle hero crispness now matches the Character Studio preview.

featAmnesiac stands under the Zodiac Sanctum portal

The Amnesiac NPC moved from x:300 to x:440 in the Bazaar â€” directly underneath the â­ Zodiac Sanctum portal that already sat at x:440. His lore arc points players toward the Sanctum, and the existing comment on the portal push already (incorrectly) said he was at x:440. Sage Mira slid right from x:520 â†’ x:600 to keep symmetric spacing with Taxi Uncle (x:860); platform layout untouched.

featQuickening boon â€” chance to bypass skill cooldowns

New entry in POWERUPS: Quickening (âš¡, cdSkip). Roll range 5â€“20 %; sums into player.mods.cdrChance, hard-capped at 0.20. Each successful cast in castSkill rolls once against this chance: on a hit, the just-applied player.skillCooldowns[id] is reset to 0, the skill is immediately re-castable, and the player gets an "âš¡ Quickening!" toast plus a brief flash and the cd_refund SFX.

  • Roll range: 5 % minimum, 20 % maximum per drop. Reroll at the U panel like any other boon.
  • Stacking ceiling: equipping multiple Quickenings is redundant â€” total cdrChance is clamped to 20 % in _applyEquippedBoons. A single godroll already saturates the cap.
  • Excluded skills: phantom_cut and nightreaper_mark are CDR-immune ultimates (the v0.25.237 12 s lock); Quickening can't bypass either.
  • Why a chance, not a flat reduction: WIS already grants a deterministic 0â€“40 % CDR multiplier and the blanket -25 % JOB_CD_MUL stacks on top. A bursty proc-based bypass reads differently â€” you feel it land instead of just shaving milliseconds.

v0.25.268 Authored backdrops for Bazaar / Sanctum / Carriage + World Map art

featThree authored map backdrops

Replaced the procedural / generic backdrops on three of the most-visited maps with hand-authored hero art that the player commissioned. Each one slots into the existing md.bg pipeline, registers a Phaser texture key, and â€” for maps that previously rendered through bespoke canvas drawing functions â€” gets a pass-through gate so the new image actually wins.

  • Everdawn Bazaar (town) now uses bg_v3_everdawnBazaar.png instead of the shared homestead hill. The bazaar also became the new spawn-paint critical asset, replacing bg_v3_homestead.png in the BAZAAR reveal-gate set so the loading bar gates on the actual art the player sees first.
  • Zodiac Sanctum (zodiacHall) â€” previously the procedural drawZodiacHub starry-wall â€” now uses bg_v3_zodiacSanctum.png. The 12 portals + sky gradient + ground still ride on top, but the wall behind them is now authored.
  • Carriage of Ascension (tower) swapped from the borrowed zodiacDawn cinematic to bg_v3_carriageOfAscension.png. The procedural drawCarriageInterior path is bypassed when the authored bg is loaded.

featWorld Map (W) interface art

The W world map modal now uses bg_v3_worldMapInterface.gif as its backdrop â€” animated parchment-and-stars under the existing SVG node graph. Layered with a translucent rgba(10,6,24,0.5) overlay so node labels, sub-labels, dashed connection lines, and the legend bar still read clearly against the bright art. Falls back to the original radial gradient if the gif fails to decode.

polishPlumbing

  • New BG_IMAGES keys: everdawnBazaar, zodiacSanctum, carriageOfAscension
  • New Phaser texture keys: bg_everdawnBazaar, bg_zodiacSanctum, bg_carriageOfAscension
  • drawBackground now defers the sanctum / isCarriage / isZodiac / isZodiacHub procedural early-returns when an authored md.bg is loaded â€” same gate behaviour Phaser already uses
  • All four files preloaded via the REQUIRED manifest so they're ready for the world reveal

v0.25.267 Everdawn Marketplace â€” new merchant pocket map + Fashionista sprite

featNew map: Everdawn Marketplace

The four merchant NPCs â€” Pharma Sis (potions), Brok (weapons + crafting bench), Old Arlen (lore), and Fashionista (wardrobe) â€” have moved out of the Bazaar and into a new dedicated pocket map: Everdawn Marketplace. The Bazaar now keeps only the lore-anchor NPCs (Amnesiac, Sage Mira, Taxi Uncle).

  • Entry: middle archway portal in the Bazaar at x:700 â€” labelled â–² Everdawn Marketplace. Press â†‘ to enter, same as any portal
  • Exit: portal at x:60 in the marketplace returns to the Bazaar
  • Layout: 1200 px wide, 4 stall sidewalks (one per merchant) at y:360, plus a decorative awning beam mid-air at y:230 that reads as a covered alley
  • Sky palette: warmer pastel (rosÃ© â†’ peach â†’ cream) â€” sunset-awning vibe distinct from the Bazaar's cooler pink-mint sky
  • Safety: isTown:true, so no monster spawns and the boss BGM doesn't apply â€” same vibe as the Bazaar

featFashionista sprite â€” full-body cute fashion model

New Ludo-generated NPC sprite for Fashionista (Sprites/npc/fashionista.webp): full-body chibi-anime fashion model in a tilted pink beret, fluffy faux-fur shrug, flared pink midi skirt, pink heeled boots, holding gold hairstyling scissors. Wired into NPC_SPRITE_FILES + the world-reveal preload manifest so it decodes before the marketplace loads.

polishBazaar wiring details

  • NPC list trimmed from 7 to 3; remaining NPCs (Amnesiac at x:300, Sage Mira at x:520, Taxi Uncle at x:860) re-positioned for symmetry
  • Sidewalk platforms preserved â€” the empty stalls now read as the un-staffed ends of the bazaar
  • Existing Bazaar portals (Sunset Coast left, Emerald Thicket right) untouched
  • The boss BGM swap landed in v0.25.265 still works exactly the same â€” the marketplace is non-boss so it stays on the Mojiworld theme

v0.25.266 Idle character no longer renders blurred

bugPlayer crispness when standing still

The vector-hero idle frames are baked into a 100Ã—100 offscreen canvas (_lxBakeHeroIdle) and redrawn each frame as a single drawImage â€” the v0.25.176 perf optimization that gave a 60+% reduction in idle-frame CPU on mobile. But that bake is drawn through the _chibiApply scale transform (1.19 Ã— 1.06), and canvas's default imageSmoothingEnabled = true bilinearly filtered the cached bitmap during that scaled redraw â€” so the hero looked subtly soft / out-of-focus the moment they stopped moving. Walking, attacking, and jumping all bypass the cache and re-rasterise the vectors at the final scale, so the blur was idle-only and easy to miss until you sat next to a moving NPC for comparison.

Fix: _drawVectorHeroCached now flips ctx.imageSmoothingEnabled = false around the cached drawImage calls (and restores the previous value afterward). Idle hero stays crisp; the perf win from caching is preserved.

v0.25.265 Boss arena BGM â€” "Gravitos Ascends" plays in every boss map

featDedicated boss-arena soundtrack

New full-length track Gravitos Ascends (audio/bgm_boss.mp3, ~2.8 MB) loops in every map flagged isBossArena: true. The existing Mojiworld BGM (bgm_mojiworld.mp3) keeps its job in towns / fields / tower / zodiac / dungeon maps; the boss theme takes over the moment a boss-arena map loads.

  • Hard-swap with a 600 ms cross-fade, so the music shift reads as a deliberate "boss room" cue rather than a jarring cut
  • The boss element is unlocked on the same first-gesture pass as the main BGM, so it plays without an autoplay-blocker hiccup the first time the player enters a boss room
  • Fully-faded-out element pauses to free the mobile audio decoder slot â€” no zombie playback
  • Hooked into loadMap via _setBossBgm(MAPS[id].isBossArena); uses the static MAPS table (authoritative) rather than the in-progress game.mapData rebuild

Boss arenas this currently affects: Forest Glade boss arena, the Lava Crater, the Frozen Throne, the Gel Wetlands boss room, the Queen's Sanctum, plus all 12 zodiac boss rooms (5 hand-built arenas + the procedural zodiac arena variant).

v0.25.264 Performance pass â€” 5 verified fixes from a 4-agent deep audit

Follow-up to the v0.25.263 user feedback that the game still felt laggy. Spawned four parallel perf audit agents (render hot-path, update-loop/collision, particle/VFX, and asset/GC pressure), then verified each high-impact finding line-by-line before shipping. Five real, high-impact fixes landed; the rest were either false positives or smaller wins held back for a follow-up pass.

featPhaser canvases now sleep when the game is paused

The two Phaser instances (BG + UI scene host) were ticking + rendering every frame even on menus, dialog, class-select, modals, and tutorial overlays â€” three concurrent render pipelines (vanilla canvas + 2Ã— Phaser) with nothing meaningful changing on screen. _perfTick now toggles game.loop.sleep() / wake() on edge transitions of game.paused, dropping ~30-50% of the FPS budget on pause screens (desktop) and 40-70% on mobile.

featsaveState() debounced â€” major GC pressure relief

saveState() is called from ~30 sites: every kill, quest tick, boon equip, skill unlock, level-up, prestige, etc. Each call ran JSON.stringify() on the full {player, game} object (~10-40 KB) plus a localStorage.setItem â€” the dominant string-allocation source in heavy play (estimated 70-150 KB/s of churn).

  • saveState() is now a 1.5 s debounced trampoline that sets a dirty flag and queues a single flush
  • _flushSaveStateNow() contains the actual serialize-and-write â€” used by paths that can't wait (visibility-change, before-unload, prestige reload)
  • No save data lost: the critical-flush sites are wired up so backgrounding the tab or closing the window forces an immediate write
  • Major GC pauses on mobile every 10-15 s should disappear or shrink dramatically

polishAudio oscillator nodes now disconnect on completion

Every audio.env(...) call (hit, crit, pickup, levelup, potion, etc.) created an OscillatorNode + GainNode, called start + stop, but never disconnect(). WebAudio retains nodes that are still wired into the destination graph, so each combat hit (5-20Ã— per second) was leaving zombie pairs pinned in memory â€” accumulating 200-500 KB over a 2-3 minute fight, especially brutal on mobile Safari + Firefox. Now both nodes are torn down via an onended callback once the envelope completes.

polishParticle budget guards on 4 high-cost skill casts

Several skills pushed straight to game.particles with no per-frame budget check. Frames already at the particle cap silently dropped these via the _trimVisualQueues overflow-splice â€” wasted allocation + GC for nothing. All four now route through _budgetedParticlePush() and break the loop early when the cap is hit:

  • Mage Z (Blink) â€” was 40 unguarded particles/cast (20 + 20 around teleport)
  • Mage Arcane Burst â€” wider 80-particle ring per cast
  • Lich Soul Vortex (G) â€” 80-particle ring on the open animation
  • Nightreaper Eclipse Massacre (G) â€” 4 trailing-shadow particles per dagger Ã— 10 batches per cast = up to 400 particles per Eclipse on a packed room. Now breaks per-batch when budget is exhausted.

Visuals are unchanged below the cap; only frames already over budget see the trim.

polishSquared-distance comparisons in two hot inner loops

Projectile ricochet (next-target search) and Ice Spike's AoE-on-hit splash both used Math.hypot per candidate inside for (const m2 of game.monsters) loops â€” that's a sqrt every iteration. Both were doing radius compares (d < threshold) where the distance value itself is never used, so squaring the threshold and comparing dx*dx + dy*dy against it removes the sqrt entirely. Saves a few hundred microseconds per ricochet hit in dense pulls.

featWhat's still pending

Held back for a follow-up pass: damage-number gradient cache, screen-shake Math.random memoize, shadowBlur batching, drawSmoothFx composite batching, seraph heal-aura array cache, MP networking JSON.stringify debounce, toast pool, particle splice-thrashing â†’ write-index. The five fixes shipped today are the highest-impact and easiest to verify; the rest will land after testing confirms the lag is meaningfully better.

v0.25.263 DoT cadence 0.5s Â· Fashionista NPC + Q wardrobe

polishBurn / poison DoT now ticks every 0.5 s

Cadence changed from 80 frames (~1.3 s) to 30 frames (0.5 s @ 60 fps). Per-tick damage scaled by 30/80 = 0.375Ã— so total burn DPS over the full burn window is unchanged â€” players see smaller numbers tick more often. Pairs nicely with v0.25.262's burn cap: smaller per-tick spikes mean the cap kicks in more cleanly when many enemies are burning at once.

featFashionista NPC + cosmetic wardrobe

New NPC in Everdawn Bazaar â€” Fashionista at x:1000 (between Taxi Uncle and the Emerald Thicket portal). Runs the cosmetic-only wardrobe (skin / hair / eyes / mouth / class outfit pickers â€” the existing CharStudio system, just newly anchored).

  • Press Q anywhere to open the wardrobe (was already bound, now properly surfaced)
  • Or talk to Fashionista (press N near her) and pick "Open Wardrobe âœ¨"
  • Help panel updated with the new Q binding
  • Sprite gets a pink hand-mirror prop on the NPC body to read as a vanity vendor at a glance
  • New 7th sidewalk platform added at x:960 so her stall has its own raised walkway like the other vendors

Cosmetic only â€” wardrobe changes don't affect stats or skills, just how your hero looks. Carries across saves via the existing CharStudio persistence.

polishWiring details

  • NPC role 'wardrobe' added to the dialog handler
  • Hand-mirror prop drawn in the role-prop section of the procedural NPC body
  • Existing Q keybind (line 38454) routes to openCharStudio() â€” already there, just unsurfaced before today

v0.25.262 Lag mitigation pass + summon damage bumps

featSummon allies hit harder

SummonSourceWasNow
Skeleton / ZombieWarlock raise (Soul Siphon, Dark Pulse)0.35Ã— ATK0.65Ã— ATK
Wolf pack memberBeastmaster pack (3 wolves)0.8Ã— ATK1.1Ã— ATK
Single wolf companionBeastmaster Wild Bond F0.8Ã— ATK1.4Ã— ATK

Floor on raised-minion damage also raised from 4 â†’ 8. Skeleton/zombie was severely underpowered relative to wolves; now in the same band but still lower than the iconic wolves. Single wolf hits hardest (lone-companion fantasy intact).

polishLag mitigation â€” 5 caps + smarter auto-perf

Game was reported as "very laggy". Added five caps that prevent runaway tick costs without touching gameplay feel:

  • Burn DoT tick cap â€” at most MAX_BURNING (12 mobile / 24 desktop) burns deal damage per frame. Burns still decay normally so they expire on schedule; only the visible damage tick is throttled. Prevents the ticks-and-damage-numbers cascade in crowded rooms.
  • Player projectile cap â€” MAX_PLAYER_PROJECTILES (60 mobile / 120 desktop). When skill spawns pile up past the cap (Ballista hold + Arrow Rain + Elemental Convergence overlap), the OLDEST player projectiles are dropped. Targets the O(nÂ²) projectileÃ—monster collision loop.
  • Minion cap â€” MAX_MINIONS (6 mobile / 10 desktop). Stops Soul Siphon / Dark Pulse stacks from blowing past frame budget on the seek-loop.
  • Auto-lowFx more responsive â€” triggers at 8 slow frames (was 12), avg-frame threshold 22 ms â†’ 20 ms, hold window 3 s â†’ 6 s so brief recovery doesn't bounce.
  • Performance-mode toast â€” first time auto-lowFx engages per session: "âš¡ Performance mode â€” FX reduced for smoother frames". Subsequent toggles silent.

polishImplementation details

  • Burn cap uses game._burnTickedThisFrame reset in _trimVisualQueues at end of every frame
  • Projectile + minion caps run inside the same end-of-frame trim sweep so the work is paid once per frame, not per-emit
  • All caps respect mobile detection (_IS_MOBILE_AT_LOAD) â€” phones get tighter limits than desktop
  • No gameplay invariants change â€” burns still expire correctly, projectiles still pierce, minions still seek and attack at the same cadence

polishWhat this DOESN'T fix (deferred)

The IMPROVEMENT_PLAN.md still has the bigger perf wins flagged: spatial-grid for projectileÃ—monster collision (P1, ~âˆ’3 to âˆ’5 ms/frame), gradient cache (P2, ~âˆ’1.5 ms/frame), particle pool (P3, ~âˆ’2 ms/frame). Those are bigger refactors saved for a focused perf-only ship. Today's caps stop the worst-case spikes without touching the shapes of those systems.

v0.25.261 Boon economy Â· NPC rename Â· Sniper V Â· 6 polish changes

featBoon economy tightened â€” boss-only drops + Sage Mira gated

  • BOON_EQUIP_CAP 5 â†’ 3 â€” boon picks feel like real build commitments now, not "everything fits"
  • Boons no longer drop from regular mobs, elites, or mini-bosses â€” only from full-tier boss kills (the powerup-pick screen at boss death) + Sage Mira's offering
  • Sage Mira: 12-hour real-time cooldown, 5000 Lumens (was 1500 / unlimited). Cooldown stored in player._sageNextAt as epoch ms; resets on prestige. Dialog text dynamically shows the wait time when the offering isn't ready

featPharma Sis (Elspeth renamed)

The town potion vendor is now Pharma Sis. Rename propagates everywhere â€” NPC label, sprite mapping, shop title ("Pharma Sis's Apothecary"), tutorial copy, and the empty-inventory hint.

featPotion icons recolored â€” HP red, MP blue

TierHP (was â†’ now)MP (was â†’ now)
SmallðŸ§ª â†’ ðŸ·ðŸ”µ â†’ ðŸ’§
MediumðŸ§ƒ â†’ â¤ï¸ðŸ’  â†’ ðŸ’™
LargeðŸ’§ â†’ ðŸ©¸ðŸŸ¦ â†’ ðŸŸ¦

Old set mixed a blue droplet under HP large which clashed with the red palette intent. Now all HP icons read red, all MP icons read blue.

featSniper V â€” Arrow Rain reskinned

Brand-new Ludo-AI sprite at Sprites/fx/arrow_rain.webp: a translucent storm cloud with cyan-blue arrows pouring out the underside. Cloud now anchors at the top of the screen (y: 80, was 320 â€” same height as the player) with size 360 / life 80 frames so it visibly hovers over the strike zone for the entire shower. Falling-arrow projectile color shifted from gold (#ddaa66) to cyan-blue (#66ccff); ground-ring tint also recolored to match.

polishArcher C â€” always somersault

Evade Burst now always performs the somersault flip backwards (was conditional on holding the back-direction key). Reads as a clean signature archer move every cast â€” big upward launch + 520 ms spin animation + extra dust ring. Works alongside the v0.25.260 knockback blast for a single cohesive "create-space" beat.

polishB key unbound

B no longer opens the inventory modal directly. Inventory access stays available via the U-panel's "Items" tab. The B keybind is reserved (early-return) so it won't accidentally trigger any other binding in the future.

polishPotion cooldown confirmed at 3 seconds

POTION_CD_FRAMES = 180 (3 seconds at 60 fps) â€” already correct, no change. Each potion type (HP / MP / Elixir) tracks its own CD independently so HP and MP don't compete for the same 3-second window.

v0.25.260 Nightreaper Eclipse rework Â· Archer Evade Burst Â· Ballista hold tuning

featNightreaper G â€” Eclipse Massacre (mechanically distinct from Phantom)

Nightreaper's G previously played almost identically to Phantom's â€” both blink-zipped between marked enemies. Reworked into a fundamentally different ult:

  • Stationary 2.4 s channel â€” player roots in place + invulnerable (Phantom moves via chain-teleport)
  • Eclipse summons a giant blood-eclipsed moon over the player
  • Spectral daggers rain down on EVERY visible enemy in 10 timed batches (re-evaluated each batch so spawning mobs get hit too)
  • No target cap (Phantom is single-target precision capped at 6)
  • Per-dagger: ATK Ã— 1.2 with 50% DEF reduction + 55% crit-bias + 280 ms stun
  • Finale "snap": ALL on-screen enemies take a forced-crit ATK Ã— 2.0 + 25 aftershock with knockback + 800 ms stun
  • HP top-up on completion preserved (18% maxHP)
  • Fixed 12 s CD (CDR-immune) preserved from v0.25.237

Result: Phantom is the precision single-target executioner; Nightreaper is the area-control rain-of-daggers caster. Same job ult slot, totally different feel.

featNew Nightreaper Eclipse sprite

Brand-new Ludo-AI sprite at Sprites/fx/nightreaper_eclipse.webp: dark eclipsed sphere with blood-red corona spiking outward, falling spectral daggers, crossed obsidian blades at the bottom, midnight cosmic palette. More grand and flashy than Phantom's portal (which was already a strong sprite â€” but the eclipse has the bigger silhouette and more dynamic radial energy).

featArcher C (Evade Burst) â€” huge knockback + cute explosion

Renamed from "Evade Roll" to "Evade Burst". Now ALSO blasts nearby monsters away when triggered:

  • Blast radius: 130 px around the player at the moment of cast
  • Knockback: vx Â±18, vy âˆ’9 â€” even tanky elites get a real launch
  • Stun: 350 ms on every mob hit
  • Damage: minor (ATK Ã— 0.4 + 4) â€” the skill is for spacing, not nuking
  • Audio + flash + shake when 1+ mob is hit
  • Toast when 2+ are knocked back
  • MP cost: 6 â†’ 8 (slightly higher to match the offensive utility)
  • CD: 1.5 s â†’ 1.7 s (+0.2 s)

New Ludo sprite at Sprites/fx/evade_burst.webp: cute warm-gold starburst with soft cream smoke puffs and gold sparkle particles â€” playful explosive feel, not menacing. Spawned at 180 px with slow rotation.

polishBallista Siege Volley â€” duration / sprite / CD tuning

v0.25.257's hold channel got a tuning pass:

  • Duration: 10 s â†’ 8 s (480 frames at 60 fps)
  • Sprite stays anchored to the chest the entire hold (sprite re-spawns every 24 frames / ~400 ms with overlap so there's no flicker)
  • Cooldown: 20 s â†’ 23 s (+3 s as requested)
  • Per-arrow damage and cadence unchanged â€” total channel damage now ~90Ã— ATK over 8 s (was ~115Ã— over 10 s) â€” slightly less burst, but the visual continuity through the hold should make it feel meatier

v0.25.259 Warrior C â†’ gold flashy speed sprite Â· Dragoon impact AOE upgrade

featWarrior Rush â€” gold speed-arrow sprite

Replaced the cyan-blue dash swoosh (v0.25.238) with a fresh Ludo-AI gold speed-arrow: chevron-shaped gold body with motion-blur trail ribbons, scattered gold sparkles, and lemon-yellow lightning-bolt chevrons radiating from the leading edge. Cute + flashy + speedy as requested. Rendered at the same 3-layer cascade from v0.25.238 (260 px outer with counter-spin â†’ 200 px mid â†’ 140 px tight), so the upgrade is purely visual â€” no gameplay changes.

featDragoon Sky Lance â€” bigger AOE plunge sprite + double-pulse stage

Replaced the impact sprite with a much more dynamic plunge: spear plunged dead center, four cyan crystal shards radiating outward, dark hexagonal shockwave rim with a spike-edged crater. Reads as a powerful splash plunge, not just a flash.

Render also restaged for the bigger AOE feel:

  • Outer splash burst bumped from 320 px â†’ 480 px (+50%) with a slow half-rotation so the crystal shards rotate during the visible window
  • Inner core re-spawn at 320 px staggered by 6 frames after â€” the eye sees two crater pulses (big splash â†’ focused central plunge) for a sense of weight + scale
  • Life 38 â†’ 56 frames on outer (lasts through the AOE damage window + column-stun follow-up)
  • The actual AOE radius (performAround(280)) and damage (4.0Ã— ATK) are unchanged â€” sprite now finally matches the existing hit radius

polishWiring

Both sprites are direct file replacements at the same paths (Sprites/fx/rush.webp, Sprites/fx/dragoon_impact.webp) so no registry / preloader changes needed â€” they slot into the existing pipeline and decode in the DEFERRED tier.

v0.25.258 Phantom V reworked Â· 2 new Ludo sprites Â· Phantom G visual upgrade

featPhantom V (Voidrift Blink) â€” 3-charge half-screen zip

The assassin V (was "Sleight of Hand", a 6-target chain-teleport with Lumen-stealing) is reworked into a Phantom-flavored short zip with charges:

  • Press V â†’ blink ~half the visible field forward
  • Hits every monster in the path's vertical band (player-height tall)
  • 3 charges per cast cycle â€” tap up to 3 times in quick succession
  • Re-cast gate is 250 ms between charges; full 14 s cooldown only triggers after the 3rd charge
  • Damage ramps mildly across charges: 1.0Ã— â†’ 1.15Ã— â†’ 1.3Ã— of getAtk() Ã— 2.0
  • Strong crit-bias (50%) on every hit
  • Toast surfaces remaining charges: "Voidrift 2 charges left" â†’ "Voidrift 1 charge left" â†’ "Voidrift â€” final blink"
  • MP cost dropped from 35 â†’ 25 since the kit is more committal

Visually: phantom afterimages along the zip path, a 130 px Voidrift slash sprite at the start position, a larger 160 px sprite at the destination (with spin), and a violet void particle ring on landing. Channel state lives on player._sleightCharges â€” refills on first cast after the cooldown expires.

featTwo new Ludo-AI sprites

  • Sprites/fx/phantom_voidrift.webp â€” violet crescent + crossed obsidian daggers + spectral hands. Used for the V zip burst at start + end positions.
  • Sprites/fx/phantom_cut.webp â€” colossal void portal with phantom figure inside, daggers raised, 6 magenta marker sigils orbiting. Used at the start of the G ultimate cast for cinematic punch.

Both follow the standard Phantom palette (deep violet â†’ magenta â†’ midnight black) with the 4 px black outline + 2-3 tone cel-shading the rest of the FX library uses.

polishPhantom G (Voidrift Execution) cast burst

Master signature now spawns a 240 px phantom_cut sprite at the rogue's chest right before the dissolve, with a slow rotation. Sells the "tearing open reality" beat that the chain-teleport previously relied on imagination for. The skill's mechanics (mark 6 enemies, chain-teleport, DEF reduction + crit-bias from v0.25.242 G3, fixed 12 s CD from v0.25.237) are unchanged.

polishWiring details

  • 2 sprite paths added to LX_FX registry + DEFERRED preloader
  • 3-charge cooldown gate added to skill cast pipeline (line ~13912) â€” _sleightCharges consume + 250 ms re-cast gate while charges remain, full CD on 3rd press
  • Charges refill on first cast after the cooldown expires (no manual reset needed elsewhere)

v0.25.257 Ballista Siege Volley reworked into a 10-second hold channel

featHold G to channel a sustained arrow stream

Ballista's master signature was a 0.5-second 8-shot burst â€” over before the player could read what happened. Reworked into a 10-second hold channel: press G to start, hold to keep firing, release at any time to end early. Trade-off: drains MP while channelling so the player decides "burst now or save MP."

polishChannel mechanics

  • Initial cast cost: 25 MP (down from 45 MP since you can release early)
  • MP drain: +1 MP every 10 frames (~6 MP/sec) â†’ up to 60 MP over a full 10 s
  • Total full-channel cost: ~85 MP (vs old burst's 45 MP, but ~4Ã— the total damage)
  • Cadence: Two arrows fire every 9 frames (~150 ms) â†’ ~67 volleys Ã— 2 arrows = ~134 arrows over 10 s
  • Per-arrow damage: getAtk() Ã— 0.85 + 5 (down from Ã— 3.5 + 25) â€” pierce + defBreak preserved
  • Total channel damage: ~115Ã— ATK over 10 s vs old burst's ~28Ã— in 0.5 s â€” sustained DPS slightly lower per-second but a much bigger total commitment window
  • Cooldown unchanged at 20 s â€” fires regardless of how early you release, so tap-spam can't game it

polishEnd conditions

Channel terminates immediately on any of: G released, 10 s expired, player dies, player hit-stunned, or MP drops below 1. Channel state is also cleared on save load, death, and respawn (defensive â€” doesn't carry into a new run).

polishPer-frame tick

Logic lives in updatePlayer() right after the Aegis tick. Adds zero overhead when the channel is inactive (early-return on if (!player._ballistaChannel)). Map transitions reset game.keys so a channel started before a portal naturally ends on the first frame of the new map.

v0.25.256 Endgame dailies + 5 polish fixes from the audit's QOL track

feat3 new Lv 30+ recurring daily challenges

Endgame audit flagged "no recurring daily quest at Lv 40+" as a HIGH gap â€” the Lv 40-50 stretch had only one one-time quest. Added 3 dailies that recycle from the existing pool every UTC midnight:

  • kill200 â€” Defeat 200 enemies today (minLv 30, reward: 2,500 Lumens + 2 SP)
  • gravitos1 â€” Defeat Gravitos at the Singularity (minLv 35, reward: 3,500 Lumens + 2 SP)
  • enhance10 â€” Land 10 successful enhancements (minLv 35, reward: 4,000 Lumens + 2 SP)

These tie back to existing endgame loops (Tier-5 grind, Gravitos farm, â˜… enhancement) â€” no new content needed, just a steady stream of meaningful objectives.

polishBoon equip / unequip audio cue

Equipping or unequipping a boon used to be silent â€” only the rendered list updated. Now equip plays the 'mp_refund' SFX with a tiny flash; unequip plays the distinct 'cd_refund' SFX. Audible difference signals which action fired.

polishSet 2-piece activation now flashes

3pc activation already had flash + shake + audio. 2pc was audio-only â€” felt like a smaller deal than it is. Added flash(0.18) on 2pc activation so the milestone reads consistently. (No change to 2pc/mismatch behaviour â€” those still stay quiet.)

polishAttribute allocation feedback

Clicking the + button to spend an attribute point used to silently re-render the panel. Now plays the 'mp_refund' SFX + small flash(0.08) so the spend feels snappy.

polishMute (M) added to help panel keybind list

The Mute toggle was functional but undiscoverable from the in-game help modal. Added <kbd>M</kbd> row to the Menus & Items column. (Re-pick Job/Master via 0 was already listed â€” agent's claim was partially false.)

polishFalse positives cleared during verification

  • "3pc set bonus has no flash/shake/audio" â€” already wired (line 8246â€“8248); only 2pc was missing.
  • "Re-pick job (0) missing from help" â€” already listed at line 2690.
  • "Mage MP regen creates infinite spam loop" â€” m_surge tree node creates a counter-incentive; working as designed.
  • "Marksman G weak vs Ballista" â€” agent missed the actual 7Ã— ATK + alwaysCrit damage.

v0.25.255 Combat balance â€” verified findings from 6-agent deep audit

6 parallel audit agents (class kit / monster / economy / endgame / bug-hunt / polish) returned 51 candidate findings. After line-by-line verification: 4 verified true and fixed, 1 mass batch of false positives cleared (incl. all 4 boss-balance HIGH severities â€” agent used wrong contact-damage formula).

bugHIGH â€” Doombringer slam coda 3.5Ã— â†’ 2.8Ã— ATK

Blade of Calamity's final ground slam was dealing getAtk() * 3.5 on top of 10 prior strikes at 2.4Ã— each â€” total burst window â‰ˆ 27.5Ã— ATK, well above peer master G skills (Crusader Aegis sustain â‰ˆ 8Ã— over 16 s; Dragoon Skylance â‰ˆ 6Ã—). Slam dropped to 2.8Ã— â€” total burst now â‰ˆ 26.4Ã—, matching Doombringer's "executioner stroke" identity without trivialising tanky elites. maple_game.html:16070

bugMED â€” Phantom Cut 2.6Ã— â†’ 2.2Ã— per-strike base

Phantom's chain-execute hit for getAtk() * 2.6 per target (up to 6 hits + nova). Compared to Nightreaper's 1.8Ã— per hit (up to 14 targets), Phantom was +44% per-hit on a shorter chain â€” too dominant in single-elite encounters. Dropped to 2.2Ã— to keep Phantom's per-strike edge over Nightreaper without tilting the choice. maple_game.html:16547

bugHIGH â€” Empowered tier multiplier stacking with veteran scaling

EMPOWERED_TIER multipliers (1.7Ã— HP / 1.55Ã— ATK on cookie etc.) compose with veteran scaling (line 12404, +3% HP per level above natural). At Lv 25 a Lv-4 Empowered Cookie was reaching â‰ˆ 1,200 HP â€” 60+ basic-attack hits on what's still flavor mob, not a mini-boss. Dropped HP/ATK muls ~10% across all 4 empowered types (gummy/cookie/frog/axolotl). EXP/Mesos kept high so encounters stay rewarding. maple_game.html:6452

polishMED â€” Skywisp exp/mesos bumped (70/34 â†’ 85/38)

Tier-2 air mob's exp:HP ratio (3.43) was identical to tier-1 mobs â€” grind-efficiency parity broke the tier promise. Bumped exp 70 â†’ 85 + mesos 34 â†’ 38 to bring it in line with Horny / Frog efficiency at the same band.

polishFalse positives cleared during verification

  • Boss-balance HIGH Ã— 4 (Gravitos / Aetherion contact damage, Archon enrage, Empowered Cookie damage spike) â€” agent used a multiplicative contact formula atk * 1.3 - def * 0.4 that doesn't exist in the code. Real formula is flat atk - getDef() + jitter (line 17421). Recomputed: Gravitos hits Lv 28 warrior for ~159 (18% of 884 maxHP), Aetherion hits Lv 25 for ~119 (15% of 800 maxHP). Both within bounds. Aetherion HP "100-min fight" used a Lv 25 unenhanced basic-attack DPS and ignored skill multipliers and PLAYER_ATK_MUL placement.
  • Marksman G "weak vs Ballista" â€” agent missed the actual values. One Shot is getAtk() * 7 + 60 (line 16723) with alwaysCrit: true and 12% instakill on non-bosses. Effective â‰ˆ 10.5Ã— ATK guaranteed crit, comparable to Ballista's 8 Ã— 2.5Ã—.
  • Mage MP regen "infinite spam loop" â€” Arcane Surge tree node (line 5574) creates a counter-incentive: spell damage scales with current MP %, so dumping MP into spam reduces damage. Working as designed.
  • "bossDefeated not initialised on load" â€” agent self-dismissed; defaulter exists at line 10973.
  • "Quest boss kind mismatch" â€” agent self-dismissed; type/bossType match correctly.
  • "Achievement starforged â˜…5 boundary" â€” agent self-dismissed; >= 5 is correct.
  • "setshards undefined on old saves" â€” defaulter shipped in v0.25.250.

v0.25.254 Playthrough audit fixes â€” 5 verified issues squashed

Triaged findings from a 4-agent parallel playthrough audit. Of 11 candidate findings, 5 were verified true; 6 were false positives (cleared via line-by-line verification). Shipped fixes:

bugHIGH â€” Class T2/T3 weapons hidden from Lv 6â€“9 shop

The v0.25.250 class-aware shop filter combined generic + class items into one list, then sliced by a global cap (4 items at Lv <10). Result: a Warrior at Lv 8 saw 4 generic ladder weapons (Wooden â†’ Runed) and never saw their tier-2 class weapon (Crusader Mace), even though it was tier-appropriate. Now: generic ladder is capped by level (2/3/4/6), class items show independently on a tier-gated reveal (T2 from Lv 6, T3 from Lv 10) â€” Warriors at Lv 8 now see Crusader Mace as intended.

bugMED â€” Hardcoded Lv 50 in prestige flow â†’ constant
