3 sites still hardcoded 50 for prestige (gate, modal copy, dev cheat). Introduced PRESTIGE_LEVEL = 50 constant alongside the existing JOB_ADVANCE_LEVEL / MASTER_ADVANCE_LEVEL so the entire advancement curve is now declared in one block. Future tweaks won't desync.

bugMED â€” Boon-loadout cap silently truncates on save load

v0.25.247's defensive sanitiser correctly clamped boonsEquipped to BOON_EQUIP_CAP, but did so silently â€” players reloading a save with too many equipped boons would see them quietly disappear. Now fires a toast: "â—ˆ Boon loadout capped at 6 â€” extras unequipped".

polishLOW â€” Daily quest reach25 ID renamed to reach20

v0.25.253 changed the goal from 25 â†’ 20 to align with the new job-advance level, but the internal id was still 'reach25'. Renamed for clarity in the codebase + future quest-tracking telemetry.

polishLOW â€” Dev cheat HP/exp curve realigned with G2 rebalance

The dev "jump to max level" cheat (press 0) was using a stale 1.20 / 1.24 / 1.28 multiplier curve. Realigned with the live _maybeLevelUp tier curve (1.20 â†’ 1.40 â†’ 1.40 â†’ 1.42 â†’ 1.55) from v0.25.242 G2 so the post-cheat expToNext matches reality. Hardcoded 49 "missed levels" replaced with PRESTIGE_LEVEL - player.level so the cheat works from any starting level.

polishFalse positives cleared during verification

  • "W-key binding missing" â€” verified at line 8479, routes to toggleWorldMap(). Works.
  • "Modal stack closes all at once" â€” agent flagged as bug; this is intentional behavior since v0.25.224.
  • "Lv 50 achievement broken by advancement rebalance" â€” agent self-corrected; the lv50 achievement tests player.level >= 50 atomically and is independent of advancement gating.
  • "Tutorial X-slot Lv 5 unlock comment unclear" â€” comment was actually accurate; agent misread.
  • "Apostrophe sprite-key collision" â€” verified _itemKey() normalises both straight and curly apostrophes correctly.
  • "Forced-modal Esc bypass" â€” verified at line 22337; tutorial / class-select / advancement all properly forced.

v0.25.253 First-time tutorial Â· Job advance Lv 20 Â· Master advance Lv 40

featFirst-time tutorial walkthrough

An 8-step modal tutorial fires once per save slot on first class pick (gated by localStorage.mojiworld_tutorial_seen). Covers everything a new player needs:

  1. ðŸ‘‹ Welcome â€” sets the tone
  2. ðŸŽ® Movement & combat â€” arrow keys, jump, attack, full keybind reference at ?
  3. âš”ï¸ Defeat monsters â€” XP, Lumens, combo multiplier, mini-bosses
  4. â­ Level up & allocate points â€” U opens the character panel; STR/AGI/INT/VIT/WIS/LCK breakdown; new skill slot every 5 levels
  5. ðŸŽ’ Inventory & enhancement â€” B for inventory, Brok the Blacksmith for â˜…0â€“10 enhancement, Setshard crafting for missing set pieces
  6. âœ¨ Boons â€” passive perks from mini-boss drops or Sage Mira; equip up to 6 in the U-panel Boons tab
  7. ðŸ—ºï¸ World map & fast travel â€” W opens the map; Taxi Uncle offers the same routes
  8. âœ¦ Job & Master advancement â€” Lv 20 picks Job, Lv 40 picks Master class

Skippable at any step. Esc is blocked while open (would otherwise leave the seen-flag unstamped); the Skip button explicitly marks-seen so you don't get re-prompted on the next class pick.

featJob advancement at Lv 20 (was 25), Master at Lv 40 (was 50)

Earlier advancement makes the class identity reach players faster. Cascading skill-slot unlocks rebalanced to align:

  • D (skill 5): Lv 20 â†’ Lv 18 (lands BEFORE job advance so you have a full kit at the unlock moment)
  • F (job signature): Lv 25 â†’ Lv 20 (unlocks AT job advance)
  • V (job ultimate): Lv 30 â†’ Lv 25 (5 levels after job pick)
  • G (master signature): Lv 49 â†’ Lv 39 (1 below master advance, live the moment you choose)

Class-select panel + lore intro updated to match. Daily quest "Reach character level 25" bumped to "Reach character level 20" with a lower minLv prerequisite. Prestige cap stays at Lv 50, giving 10 levels of post-master endgame to grind.

v0.25.252 15 high-quality 2D item sprites â€” standardised cel-shaded outline

featCustom 2D sprites for all 15 set legendaries

Generated via Ludo AI with a unified prompt template so every sprite shares the same look â€” bold thick black outline (4 px), 2-3 tone cel-shading, 3-quarter angle, transparent background, top-left key light, vibrant saturated colors. Anime/JRPG mobile-gacha aesthetic similar to Final Fantasy / Genshin Impact item slots.

SetWeaponArmorAccessory
ðŸŒŸ DawnshardEmerald-cyan greatsword w/ gold filigreeWhite-gold paladin plate w/ holy glowGold sunburst medallion w/ green gem
âš”ï¸ DoomforgedCrimson demon greatsword w/ skull crossguardRed-black plate w/ embers seeping throughBlood ruby skull-band ring
ðŸ¥· ShadowweaveCurved obsidian dagger w/ shadow wispsTattered violet hood + dark feather trimBlack silk veil w/ amethyst brow gem
ðŸª„ VoidcallerPurple staff w/ orbiting arcane runesRune-embroidered purple wizard robeFloating violet crystal in gold claw-stand
ðŸ¹ SkyhunterEmerald recurve bow w/ leaf-etched limbsForest-green hunter's vest w/ bucklesGreen leather quiver w/ feathered arrows

featLX_ITEMS sprite registry + sprite-with-emoji-fallback render

New itemIconHtml(item, sizePx) helper renders an <img> tag if the item has a sprite registered AND the image has finished decoding; otherwise falls back to the existing emoji icon. Wired into every equipment-display surface:

  • Inventory grid (B / U-panel Items tab) â€” 44 px sprites in equipment slots
  • Equipped slot row (character panel) â€” 42 px in each slot
  • Item tooltip (hover) â€” 24 px next to the name
  • Enhancement Forge modal â€” 32 px in the item list
  • Crafting bench modal â€” 32 px in both the disassemble and craft panes
  • Brok's shop rows â€” 36 px when buying or selling
  • Auto-equip toast â€” 22 px in the gear-change popup

The fallback path means non-set items (the generic ladder + class T2/T3 weapons + class T2/T3 armors) keep their emoji icons until their sprites are added in a future ship â€” no broken rendering.

polishWiring details

  • _itemKey(it) normalises item names to lookup keys (lowercase, underscore-separated, ASCII-only)
  • _itemReady(img) checks complete && naturalWidth > 0 so partially-decoded images don't render as broken icons
  • 15 sprite paths added to the preloader DEFERRED list â€” they decode in the background after the bazaar reveal
  • image-rendering: auto on item sprites so they scale smoothly at any size (HUD vs tooltip vs panel)
  • Sprites stored at Sprites/items/<name>.webp â€” same naming convention as Ludo-generated FX sprites

polishRoadmap (deferred)

Sprites for the 31 remaining items (6 generic weapons + 8 mid-tier class weapons + 6 generic armors + 8 mid-tier class armors + 6 generic accessories) â€” same prompt template, just need more Ludo calls. Will land in a future ship; the registry already supports them via the same key-based lookup, so adding them is just a 1-line registry edit + 1-line preloader entry per sprite.

v0.25.251 8 mid-tier class armors Â· Gravitos drops Setshards

feat8 new T2/T3 class-flavored armors

Mirrors the v0.25.250 weapon expansion â€” each class now has a full identity-building gear ladder for both weapon and armor slots before the T5 set legendaries:

ClassT2 (rare)T3 (epic)
âš”ï¸ WarriorCrusader Hauberk (def 14, hp 50, atk 4)Berserker Pauldron (def 21, hp 90, +5% ATK)
ðŸ¥· RogueShadow Hood (def 8, hp 30, crit 6, spd 0.2)Nightshade Cloak (def 14, hp 60, crit 10, +4% Dodge)
ðŸª„ MageApprentice Robe (def 6, hp 25, mp 30, crit 3)Arcane Vestments (def 12, hp 50, mp 50, +5% ATK)
ðŸ¹ ArcherHunter's Tunic (def 9, hp 35, crit 5, spd 0.15)Falcon Mail (def 16, hp 70, crit 8, spd 0.3)

Brok's class-aware shop already filters by player class, so each class sees their own armor ladder alongside the existing generic Cloth â†’ Plate options.

featGravitos now drops Setshards

Defeating Gravitos at the Singularity grants a guaranteed +6 Setshards per kill â€” making the Singularity worth re-running as an endgame Setshard farm route. Per-kill burst: violet shards explode from the boss corpse, an audio sting fires, and a delayed legendary toast announces the haul ("Gravitos shed 6 Setshards from his Singularity!").

6 shards = enough for ~0.6 of a craft on its own â€” combine with disassembled cross-class drops (4 shards each) for steady set-completion.

polishCrafting modal copy

Modal header now spells out the two Setshard sources: "Disassemble unwanted set legendaries for +4 Setshards each â€” or defeat Gravitos at the Singularity for +6 shards per kill â€” Forge any set piece for 10 Setshards + 3,000 Lumens."

polishItem count

  • v0.25.248: 30 base items
  • v0.25.250: 38 base items (+8 mid-tier class weapons)
  • v0.25.251: 46 base items (+8 mid-tier class armors)

v0.25.250 Crafting bench Â· 8 mid-tier class weapons Â· Setshard currency

feat8 mid-tier class-flavored weapons (T2 / T3)

Each class now gets its own progression ladder before reaching the Tier-5 set legendaries:

ClassT2 (rare)T3 (epic)
âš”ï¸ WarriorCrusader Mace (atk 18, def 4)Berserker Cleaver (atk 28, hp 40, +6% ATK)
ðŸ¥· RogueQuickfang Daggers (atk 13, crit 8, spd 0.3)Nightshade Kunai (atk 22, crit 12, +4% lifesteal)
ðŸª„ MageApprentice Wand (atk 11, mp 25, crit 4)Crystal Scepter (atk 26, mp 40, +6% ATK)
ðŸ¹ ArcherHunter's Shortbow (atk 14, crit 6, spd 0.2)Falcon Recurve (atk 24, crit 10, +1 multishot)

Brok's shop is now class-aware: shows the generic ladder (cls: any) plus only the class-matching items for the player's class. Set legendaries are excluded â€” those remain loot-only / craftable. Shop tier-cap bumped to 8 at Lv 10+ so the new mid-tier weapons all fit in.

featSetshard crafting bench at Brok

Solves the "I never got my third Voidcaller piece" RNG frustration. Brok now offers "Craft Set Piece ðŸ”§" in his dialog:

  • Disassemble any set legendary you don't want (off-class drops, duplicates, low-roll affixes) for +4 Setshards each
  • Craft any set piece in the game for 10 Setshards + 3,000 Lumens
  • Single fungible Setshard pool â€” disassemble a Skyhunter Vest as a mage, use the shards to craft a Voidcaller Robe
  • Crafted pieces have base stats only (no affixes); set bonus eligibility still gated by player class
  • Effective rate: ~2.5 disassembles per craft, plus a meaningful Lumen sink â€” cross-class drops are now always useful

polishCrafting modal UX

  • Top pane lists every set legendary in your inventory + equipped slots with one-click +4â—ˆ disassemble buttons
  • Bottom pane lists every craftable set piece across all 5 sets, with stats preview + class-affinity warning when a piece is for a different class
  • Disabled craft buttons carry a title tooltip telling you exactly what you're missing ("Need 4 more Setshards" / "Need 1,500 more Lumens")
  • Currency header always visible: Setshards: Nâ—ˆ + Lumens: M
  • Sparkle burst + audio + flash on every successful craft
  • Setshards persist across sessions via player.setshards; reset on prestige (fresh crafting economy each ascension)

v0.25.249 Set pieces wearable by everyone Â· class-gated bonuses Â· 3pc set auras

featAll set pieces are now wearable by every class

Per request: a mage CAN equip a Doomforged Greatsword and benefit from its raw stats (ATK, HP, etc.) â€” but the set bonuses only fire when the set's class matches the player's class. 'any' sets (Dawnshard) work universally.

  • getSetBonus() now skips contributions from sets where set.cls !== player.cls (and not 'any')
  • Tooltip shows the gate explicitly: "âš  Bonuses only active for Warrior" in orange when you're looking at an off-class set piece
  • Set-complete toast differentiates â€” class-match crossing 3pc shows the celebratory "âœ¦ SET COMPLETE" with audio + flash; mismatched 3pc shows a quiet "3 [Set] pieces equipped â€” bonus is warrior-only"
  • 2pc mismatch is silent (no point pinging the player about a bonus that isn't activating)

feat3-piece set auras â€” visible thematic glow

When you've got 3 pieces of your class's set equipped, the player gets a subtle thematic aura. Each aura has 3 layers â€” a soft body halo + a ground glow + per-set decorative particles:

  • ðŸŒŸ Dawnshard (any) â€” warm gold radiance with rising sparkle motes
  • âš”ï¸ Doomforged (warrior) â€” hot red embers smoldering up from the feet
  • ðŸ¥· Shadowweave (rogue) â€” violet shadow wisps trailing behind your direction
  • ðŸª„ Voidcaller (mage) â€” three slowly-orbiting arcane runes at chest height
  • ðŸ¹ Skyhunter (archer) â€” green leaves drifting on a gentle breeze

Auras are render-only (don't affect gameplay), pulse gently so they read as a status effect rather than decor, and emit ~1 particle every 8 frames so the per-frame budget barely notices. Drawn behind the chibi base via drawSetAura() which fires from drawPlayer(); only renders for sets whose class matches yours, so a mage in 3 Doomforged pieces correctly sees no aura.

polishWiring details

  • _activeFullSets() helper returns the list of class-matched 3pc sets â€” used by both the aura render and any future set-effect hooks
  • Particle bursts use the existing game.particles queue so they auto-cull via _trimVisualQueues from v0.25.240
  • Auras + halo use globalCompositeOperation: 'lighter' so they additively brighten the character rather than tint it

v0.25.248 Equipment categorization Â· class-affinity loot Â· 5 set bonuses

featEvery item now categorised by class + tier + set

The 18 base items each carry three new fields:

  • cls â€” 'warrior' / 'rogue' / 'mage' / 'archer' / 'any' (class affinity)
  • tier â€” 1 (starter) â†’ 5 (endgame legendary), surfaced in the tooltip as Common Â· T1 / Legendary Â· T5
  • setId â€” optional set membership; matching pieces grant 2pc / 3pc bonuses

feat5 sets, 12 new legendary items

The existing Dawnshard legendaries (sword + aegis + medallion) form the universal Dawnshard set. Each combat class gets its own thematic 3-piece set:

SetClass2-piece3-piece
ðŸŒŸ Dawnshard any +10% ATK +15 Crit, +50 HP
âš”ï¸ Doomforged warrior +60 HP +12% ATK, +5 DEF
ðŸ¥· Shadowweave rogue +12 Crit +0.5 Spd, +10% Dodge
ðŸª„ Voidcaller mage +30 MP +20% Crit DMG, +1 Multishot
ðŸ¹ Skyhunter archer +0.4 Spd +1 Multishot, +15 Crit

New items added: Doomforged Greatsword + Plate + Sigil Â· Shadowweave Dagger + Cloak + Veil Â· Voidcaller Staff + Robe + Orb Â· Skyhunter Longbow + Vest + Quiver. All Tier-5 legendaries with class-thematic stats.

featSet-bonus engine + tooltips

  • getSetBonus(key) walks equipped items and adds 2pc / 3pc contributions; folded into getEquipBonus so every existing stat lookup picks them up
  • Item tooltip shows class affinity badge (âš”ï¸ Warrior) and set membership status (ðŸŒŸ Dawnshard Set 2/3) with bonus lines lit green when active, dim grey when not
  • _checkSetComplete() fires a toast when player crosses the 2pc or 3pc threshold (with audio + flash on completion); wired to all three equip sites (auto-equip, manual equip, manual unequip)

polishClass-affinity drop weighting

rollItemDrop now biases toward the player's class:

  • Items with cls: 'any' â€” full rarity weight (unchanged)
  • Items matching player's class â€” 1.5Ã— weight
  • Items mismatched to player's class â€” 0.3Ã— weight (still possible for sale value, just rare)

Cross-class drops still happen for trading / selling, but the loot you find is mostly relevant to your build. A warrior won't be drowning in Voidcaller Staffs.

v0.25.247 Bug fix sweep â€” 7 verified glitches across combat, UI, save, animation

bugC1 (HIGH) â€” Elemental Chain zombie callback

Mage skill-tree node "Elemental Chain" used a raw setTimeout for the chain follow-up. The callback fired even after death, map transitions, or skill cancellation â€” applying damage to a stale monster reference (or worse, in the wrong map context). Switched to scheduleSkillTimer so it's properly cancelled in cancelPendingSkillTimers(). maple_game.html:17791

bugC2 (MEDIUM) â€” Sniper Railshot was always crit-damage

Railshot unconditionally multiplied damage by getCritDmg() at the call site (baseAtk * 3.2 * stackMul * getCritDmg()) and passed true for the crit flag â€” making every hit a guaranteed crit regardless of crit chance. Now rolls crit per-hit with strong crit-bias (40% bonus floor, plus normal crit chance). Skill scales with build investment again. maple_game.html:15450

bugC3 (LOW) â€” Nightreaper ricochet didn't check pierce hits

Ricochet maintained its own hitSet separate from the projectile's pierce hitIds. Theoretical bug: a projectile with both pierce: true and ricochet: N could ricochet back to a monster it had already pierced. Now the bounce candidate-search excludes both sets. maple_game.html:18615

bugA3 (MEDIUM) â€” Camera not snapped to player on map load

loadMap() reset most map state but left game.camera.x at the previous map's last position. The camera lerp converged in ~30 frames, but during that ~0.5 s the parallax background, clouds, and ambient birds rendered at the wrong horizontal offset on every map transition. Now the camera snaps to the player's center immediately. maple_game.html:11594

bugU5 (MEDIUM) â€” uiConfirm Promise hung if Esc pressed

The in-game confirm modal (added in v0.25.244) registered onclick handlers on Yes / No but had no Esc handler. When Esc routed through closeAllModals, the modal hid but the awaiting Promise never resolved â€” calling code (reset save, respec, prestige, boon discard) hung in limbo. Now: capture-phase Esc handler resolves false; closeAllModals also calls game._uiConfirmCleanup() if a confirm is in flight.

bugS4 (LOW) â€” Boon equip indices defensively cleaned on save load

Out-of-bounds or duplicate indices in player.boonsEquipped (from a corrupted save, manual edit, or migration mismatch) used to silently no-op inside _applyEquippedBoons, leaving an "equipped slot" that contributed nothing. Load now sanitises: integer-only, in-bounds, deduped, capped at BOON_EQUIP_CAP.

polishR1 â€” Mobile-ctrl auto-hide gameplay-keys-only filter

The keyboard auto-hide added in v0.25.246 fired on any keydown including Tab, Esc, F11, and modifier-only presses. Now restricted to actual gameplay keys (movement, skills, common HUD shortcuts) and ignores Alt/Ctrl/Meta combinations.

v0.25.246 Mobile-controls toggle persistence + 7 QOL fixes from a debug scan

bugTouch controls now properly toggleable on desktop fullscreen

Reported: the touch-control deck was appearing on desktop fullscreen mode (likely from pointer: coarse matching on hybrid laptops / touchscreen monitors). Three fixes layered:

  • Toggle state persists across sessions via localStorage (key mojiworld_hide_mobile_ctrl). Hit the corner âœ• once and it stays hidden forever.
  • URL opt-out: append ?nomobile=1 to permanently force-hide regardless of pointer detection. Useful for streamers / desktop reviewers.
  • Auto-hide on keyboard usage: any gameplay key press flips the deck off (one-shot per session, gated to first-time users only â€” never overrides a saved preference).
  • Toast feedback on every toggle so the change registers visually + via screen reader ("Touch controls hidden" / "Touch controls shown").
  • Keyboard accessible: the toggle button now responds to Enter and Space (was mouse/tap-only).

polishQ1 â€” Audio + toast feedback on every shop purchase

Buying potions or gear used to feel silent â€” Lumens vanished and a tiny toast fired but no SFX. Both buy paths now play the 'meso' coin-jingle SFX (already defined in audio.play()) and tint the toast with rarity color. Insufficient-funds attempts now show a "Need X more Lumens" toast instead of a silent no-op.

polishQ4 â€” Disabled buy buttons explain why

Greyed-out "Buy" buttons in shops now carry a title="Need X more Lumens" tooltip on hover. Removes the "is this broken?" moment when a button looks dead.

polishQ5 â€” Empty sell-shop copy hints at the loop

The empty sell shop used to say "Nothing to sell." Now: "ðŸ›ï¸ Sell unwanted gear here for ~40% of its buy price. Pick up drops or buy from Brok's Forge to fill your bags." â€” surfaces the buy/equip/sell loop for new players.

bugR2 â€” Character Studio rAF leak fixed

renderCharStudio() early-returned when the canvas was missing (modal closed) but never cancelled the queued requestAnimationFrame. The callback chain ran forever (~60 calls/sec) on a closed modal. Now the early-return cancels the queued frame first.

polishR3 â€” Diagnostic console.log calls gated behind LX_DEBUG

Three boot-time console.log calls ([phaser], [Hero], [CharStudio]) were always firing on every page load. They now check localStorage.LX_DEBUG === '1' first â€” set the flag if you want detailed boot logging, otherwise the console stays clean.

v0.25.245 Multiplayer wedge â€” co-op XP bonus when party members are nearby

featS2 â€” +50% co-op XP when a peer is on your map

Previously multiplayer was presence + chat only â€” two solo runs in adjacent windows. Now: if any peer is on the same map within ~400 px of the killed monster, you get +50% XP for that kill. Pure client-side; uses the existing 14 Hz presence broadcast (no protocol changes, server unchanged). A toast surfaces the bonus the first time it fires per 6 s window so the mechanic is discoverable.

  • Helper: _coopXpMul(monster) walks net.peers, returns 1.5 if any peer is on the same map within range, else 1
  • Multiplied into the standard player.exp calculation in killMonster alongside combo / prestige / curve mults
  • Multiplayer modal copy updated with the new co-op bonus tagline

Why the wedge: server-authoritative combat (the README's stated next milestone) is a heavy lift â€” server has to own monster state, damage events, and loot. This change unlocks 80% of the "we're farming together" experience for ~30 lines of client code, and the multiplayer loop becomes worth running today instead of waiting on the bigger refactor.

v0.25.244 In-game confirm modal + skill-slot unlock toast

featX4 â€” Browser confirm() replaced with styled in-game modal

Reset save / respec / prestige / boon-discard all used native confirm() popups that broke the visual world (and on mobile look identical to phishing prompts). New uiConfirm({ title, body, yesLabel, noLabel, danger }) Promise helper renders a polished modal that matches the rest of the UI:

  • Reset save: was a double-confirm; now a single in-game modal with red "Erase everything" button
  • Respec: in-game confirm shows the cost
  • Prestige: ascend modal with full bonus breakdown rendered inline
  • Boon discard: red "Discard" button when destructive

The modal is registered in closeAllModals() so Esc dismisses it (defaults to "No"). Falls back to native confirm() only if the markup is somehow missing.

featX5 â€” New skill slot triggers an unlock toast

Hitting a level threshold (5 / 10 / 15 / 20 / 25 / 30 / 49) now fires a dedicated toast: "ðŸ”“ X-slot unlocked: [Skill Name] â€” [first sentence of desc]". Pulls from SLOT_LEVEL_REQ + the current class kit so the toast names whatever skill became available. No more silently expanded skill bars.

v0.25.243 UX & accessibility pass â€” 6 first-session wins

polishX1 â€” Death screen names the killer

Death overlay used to say "Returning to Everdawn Village..." with no context. Now: "ðŸ’€ Defeated by [enemy name] Â· Returning to Everdawn Village...". Tracks player._lastDamageSource in the contact-damage and projectile-damage paths so the most recent hit is named.

polishX2 â€” Skill unlock toast carries effect description

Tree-node unlock toast went from "âœ¦ Unlocked: Berserker Mastery" to "âœ¦ Unlocked: Berserker Mastery â€” +50% ATK while raging". Pulls the first sentence of node.desc; falls back to bare name if desc is missing.

polishX3 â€” NPC dialog auto-dismisses on walk-away

Walking > 110 px from the active NPC now auto-closes the dialog. Tracks game._activeNpc in openNPC; per-frame distance check in updatePlayer; cleared in closeDialog. Removes the friction of "click to open shop, glance at prices, hit Esc to leave" â€” you can just walk.

polishX6 â€” First-session tip toast for the hotkey panel

After class pick, surface a one-time localStorage-gated toast: "ðŸ’¡ Tip: the hotkey panel (top-left) shows your skills. Press ? for full controls." Backed by a generic _firstTimeTip(key, txt) helper.

polishX8 â€” Toast region is now screen-reader accessible

Every toast now carries role="status" + aria-live="polite", so VoiceOver / NVDA / TalkBack announce drops, level-ups, deaths, and tutorial tips without interrupting other UI announcements.

featS3 â€” World Map discoverability at Lv 5

Hitting Lv 5 fires a one-time toast: "ðŸ—ºï¸ Press W to open the world map â€” fast-travel between unlocked areas." The world map (and Taxi Uncle) is currently hidden behind two layers of discoverability; many new players never find it. Same one-shot pattern as X6.

v0.25.242 Balance pass â€” 6 tuning ratchets across enhancement / XP / assassin / mage

polishG1 â€” Star enhancement curve flattened, success floor raised

The â˜…4â€“7 cost shoulder was a wall most players bounced off. Old curve cumulative ~99,000 Lumens with a 5% success floor; new curve cumulative ~70,000 with a 15% floor. â˜…8â€“10 stay punishing but reachable. Costs shifted: â˜…4 800 â†’ 1500, â˜…5 1500 â†’ 1500, â˜…6 3000 â†’ 1500, â˜…7 6000 â†’ 2000, â˜…8 12000 â†’ 8000, â˜…9 25000 â†’ 20000, â˜…10 50000 â†’ 35000.

polishG2 â€” Lv 25â€“39 XP grind softened

The mid-grind was the longest dead-zone in the curve (~8â€“12 hours). Cut multipliers: 20â€“24 1.45 â†’ 1.40, 25â€“29 1.45 â†’ 1.40, 30â€“39 1.50 â†’ 1.42. Endgame (40+) curve unchanged so the ascend-to-50 ceremony still feels earned.

polishG3 â€” Phantom + Nightreaper G now respect DEF

Both ultimates wrote damage directly to target.currentHp with guaranteed crit, bypassing DEF entirely â€” tanky elites melted regardless of build investment. Reworked:

  • Nightreaper Mark: ATK Ã—2.2 (always crit) â†’ ATK Ã—1.8 with 50% DEF reduction and 60% crit-bias
  • Phantom Cut: ATK Ã—3.2 (always crit) â†’ ATK Ã—2.6 with 40% DEF reduction and 70% crit-bias

Net DPS on squishy mobs is similar; tanky bosses now actually trade with the skill instead of dissolving.

polishG4 â€” Assassin CDR refund cap

Assassin job's cdRefundOnKill was 30% with no internal cooldown â€” chain kills in AoE clears reset every cooldown, locking a power ceiling above other rogue subs. Now 15% with a 2 s gate so a single multi-hit doesn't refund off every mob.

polishG5 â€” Tier-5 zone level requirements raised

v0.25.225 rebalanced map level reqs but didn't adjust monster ATK to match. Two zones now match the new XP curve:

  • levelReq 24 â†’ 26 (gives players two extra gear levels before the wraith / skeleton wall)
  • levelReq 28 â†’ 30 (tier-5 boss prep)

polishG6 â€” Mage Chain Cast 1.5 s internal cooldown

Chain Cast (1.5Ã— damage on alternating skills within 1 s) had no internal cooldown. With multishot affixes, every chain triggered 6 empowered hits â€” broke single-class power curve. Trigger now fires at most once per 1.5 s.

v0.25.241 Performance pass â€” Y-cull + tighter particle bounds + frame budget

polishP4 â€” full off-screen cull on monster draw

drawMonster() only checked the X-axis off-screen, so aura draws + particle emissions still fired for mobs above/below the viewport on tall maps. Added a Y-axis cull (sy + m.h < -30 || sy > H + 30) right after the existing X check. Estimated saving: âˆ’0.6 to âˆ’1 ms/frame on tall maps.

polishP5 â€” tighter particle cull margin + first-pass life skip

drawParticles() uses two passes (halo + crisp core). The halo pass kept emitting for old particles whose alpha curve had already faded the glow below visibility. Tightened cull margin from Â±30 to Â±10, and added a p.life > 250 skip for the halo pass. Estimated saving: âˆ’0.8 to âˆ’1.2 ms/frame in dense particle scenes.

polishP7 â€” per-frame "extras" particle budget

Burst-spike frames (Nightreaper ricochet + ice AoE + multishot stacked) could dump 200+ particles in one frame, causing stutter even after the queue cap. New _budgetedParticlePush(p) helper that decorative emitters can route through; first 120 (desktop) / 60 (mobile) particles per frame go through, the rest are skipped. Counter resets in _trimVisualQueues. Load-bearing skill FX still fire unconditionally.

v0.25.240 Bug-fix sweep: 5 plan items (B1â€“B5)

bugB1 â€” dead string-vs-object comparison removed (HIGH)

Line 17935's if (game.currentMap === game.mapData ...) compared a string id to an object â€” always false, the early-return never fired. The bug was masked by the next guard's !mapD.isTown check, but the dead expression was a footgun for future edits. Removed.

bugB2 â€” closeAllModals null-guards forced-modal lookups

closeAllModals() read cs.style.display and av.style.display on un-guarded getElementById results. If either modal hadn't been injected yet (or got removed), the throw broke Esc + X across every overlay. Now both forced-modal lookups + the dialog close are null-guarded.

bugB3 â€” defensive defaulters on save load

Object.assign(player, save.player) doesn't add missing fields. Older saves predating skillCooldowns, buffs, consumables, equipped, or inventory would crash on next access. Added explicit defaults at the load site so any save schema gap fills cleanly.

bugB4 â€” divide-by-zero clamps in damage scaling

player.hp / getMaxHp() and player.mp / getMaxMp() appear in 6 buff-scaling formulas (Berserker passive, Blood Rage, Berserker tree, Arcane Surge, Second Wind, etc.). If a (theoretical) boon ever drops max to 0 the result becomes NaN/Infinity. All call sites now divide by Math.max(1, getMaxHp()) / Math.max(1, getMaxMp()).

bugB5 â€” _trimVisualQueues now runs every frame

The trim helper existed (_trimVisualQueues() at line 18923) but had no caller. Particle / afterImage / damage-number arrays would grow unbounded on burst-spike frames (Nightreaper ricochet + AoE in one frame can dump 200 particles). Wired into the main RAF loop right after updateUI(); uses the existing mobile-aware _particleCap() / _afterImageCap() helpers.

v0.25.239 Enhancement Forge UI overhaul Â· star badges on equipment

featAnvil sprite + polished forge mood

The Enhancement Forge modal got a major visual upgrade. A custom Ludo-AI anvil sprite (Sprites/ui/anvil.webp â€” transparent background, glowing forge fire, golden sparks) now floats on the left of the panel and gently bobs idle. The whole modal background gets a warm radial forge-glow, the title is a gold-to-orange gradient, the preview card has a subtle amber border, and the Forge button has a continuous sheen sweep so it pulls the eye. Anvil hides itself on viewports under 540 px so mobile is unaffected.

featStar count badges on every equipment surface

Once an item has been enhanced, a tiny golden â˜…N chip floats on the upper-left of its slot â€” visible in the inventory grid (B and the Items tab in U), in the equipped slot row, and on the enhance modal's own list. Tier color shifts with star count:

  • â˜…1â€“4 â€” gold gradient
  • â˜…5â€“7 â€” orange gradient (mid-tier)
  • â˜…8â€“10 â€” magenta-violet gradient with white text (legendary)

The badge has a slow brightness shimmer so high-star gear catches the eye even in a packed inventory. aria-label="N stars enhanced" for screen readers.

polishForge feedback animations

  • Success: the anvil rocks + glows on each successful star (one quick hammer-stroke); a star-shaped spark burst erupts radially from the anvil with an expanding gold ring
  • Failure: the anvil still rocks once but the modal pulses red (fail-flash); no sparks
  • Selected card now slides 3 px right + gets a soft amber inner-glow so the active item is unambiguous

polishWiring details

  • Anvil added to BAZAAR + DEFERRED preloader sets (also fetchPriority=high on cold load)
  • _starBadgeHtml(it) helper used everywhere â€” single source of truth for the badge markup
  • _playForgeAnim('success' / 'fail') helper handles the reflow-and-replay pattern so the animations always retrigger even on rapid forging

v0.25.238 Skill-sprite upgrade pass â€” 11 new / replaced FX sprites

featEleven Ludo-AI skill sprites shipped or upgraded

Big visual upgrade pass for under-served kit slots. Each sprite is a Cartoonish 2D platformer cel-shaded VFX, transparent background, sized for chibi side-scrolling combat:

  • Shadow Strike (Ninja F / Shadowlord F) â€” replaced the lavender bolt with a dramatic X-cross ink-splash twin-blade impact (deep violet + black ink).
  • War Cry (Warrior D / Dragoon D) â€” replaced the generic blue starburst with a golden brass war-horn radiating crimson-gold sound shockwaves.
  • Holy Shield (Knight V / Dragoon V) â€” new sprite. Massive divine cross + angel-wing halo radiating golden light beams. Cast at 280 px with slow rotation.
  • Blade of Calamity (Doombringer G) â€” new sprite. Colossal demon greatsword with crimson hellfire runes + skull crossguard. Cast at 280 px ahead of player.
  • Rampage (Berserker V / Doombringer V) â€” new sprite. Crossed blood-red battle axes inside a flame whirlwind, slow spin during the 3.5 s spinning AOE.
  • Prismatic Cascade (Elementalist G) â€” new sprite. Grand four-element starburst (fire / water / lightning / nature) with prismatic crystal core. Cast at 280 px, slow rotation across all 4 projectile launches.
  • Elemental Convergence cast (Archmage V) â€” re-themed from violet bolt + flame to a brilliant gold-electric bolt with magic-circle runes.
  • Chain Lightning link node â€” new per-hop sprite. Each chain jump now spawns a glowing 8-point golden star with rune halo at the target, much more readable than the old bare beam.
  • Grand Hex (Hexmaster G) â€” new sprite. Intricate emerald compass-rose hexagram with violet swirl and rune ring. Cast at 320 px to read as map-wide curse.
  • Soul Siphon summoning ring (Warlock F / Hexmaster F) â€” new sprite. Open emerald rune-circle that wraps around the caster.
  • Dark Pulse (Warlock V / Hexmaster V) â€” new sprite. Wizard's witch-hat over violet-and-cyan hex web pulse â€” clear hex wizard theme.

polishRush effect significantly upgraded

Warrior C (Rush) used to spawn a single 130 px swoosh sprite. Now stacks three layered bursts â€” large outer 260 px (with subtle counter-rotation), mid forward 200 px, tight forward 140 px â€” creating a much grander dash impact across the dash arc instead of a single launch puff.

polishWiring details

  • All new sprites added to LX_FX registry and the REQUIRED preloader list (DEFERRED tier â€” they don't gate the bazaar reveal)
  • Skill funcs use spawnSpriteBurst with appropriate size/life/spin parameters per skill aesthetic
  • elemental_link sprite spawns once per chain hop (up to 6 hops in Elemental Convergence); 80 px size, 22-frame life, fast spin

v0.25.237 Bazaar-priority loader Â· clone tuning Â· CDR-immune assassin ults

featThree-tier loader prioritises Everdawn Bazaar

The preloader now has a BAZAAR tier ahead of CRITICAL. Only the ~14 sprites the player sees the moment they spawn into Everdawn Bazaar (hero face/hair/eyes/mouth, the bazaar bg, the 6 bazaar NPCs, walkway tiles, portal) gate the world reveal. UI tabs + dialog box dropped to a second tier that loads with high priority right after reveal but no longer blocks the loading screen. Net effect: reveal gate shrunk from ~21 â†’ ~14 sprites, and the bazaar paint isn't held up by UI assets the player isn't looking at yet. Hard fallback dropped 5 s â†’ 4 s.

  • Loading status copy reworked to bazaar-flavored phrases: "Painting Everdawn Bazaarâ€¦", "Lighting the lanternsâ€¦", "Stocking Brok's anvilâ€¦"
  • BAZAAR sprites still get fetchPriority='high' + img.decode() like before
  • UI tier (dialog box, char_header, tab_*) now starts immediately after BAZAAR fires, parallel-loading while the player is already in town
  • DEFERRED tier behavior unchanged â€” still streams via requestIdleCallback with the corner-pill progress bar from v0.25.235

polishShadowlord G â€” long-range turret screen

Mirror Shadow tuned for sustained-control gameplay:

  • Lifetime: 4 s â†’ 15 s
  • Cooldown: 20 s â†’ 22 s â€” uptime sits at ~68%
  • Spawn fan distance: 130 px â†’ 320 px per side. Clones now plant themselves far from the player, projecting their AOE rings across most of the visible width without overlapping
  • Toast updated to "âœ¦ MIRROR SHADOW â€” 3 floating clones, 15s"

polishShadowlord V (Death Blossom) â€” fast cycle, 5-target cap

  • Cooldown: 20 s â†’ 8 s for tight rotational play
  • Each of the 3 waves now strikes up to 5 closest enemies in radius (was unlimited). Pairs the faster cycle with a tighter target cap so it doesn't clear entire rooms by itself
  • Damage per hit unchanged (ATK Ã— 1.15)

bugPhantom + Nightreaper G â€” CDR-immune at 12 s

Both assassin ultimates were drifting below 12 s with stacked WIS CDR + skill-tree CDR + the JOB_CD_MUL blanket modifier. The earlier v0.25.226 floor (Math.max(12000, computedCd)) only kicked in when CDR pushed below the floor, so the listed cooldown didn't match what players saw. Both skills now lock at exactly 12 s regardless of CDR. Skill table cooldowns updated from 30 s/28 s base to a flat 12 s, and the description tags "Fixed 12 s CD (CDR-immune)" so it's clear in tooltips.

v0.25.236 Shadowlord clones reworked into levitating AOE turrets

featFloating summons, fixed positions

The Shadowlord G ultimate (Mirror Shadow) used to orbit the player like escorts â€” they drifted along whenever you moved, which meant repositioning yanked your DPS with you and walls/ledges occasionally cut their swings short. The three clones now anchor to the world at the moment of cast and hover in place with a gentle bob animation. They behave like spirit turrets you drop, not pets that follow.

featCircular AOE strike (no more directional hitbox)

Each clone pulses an 110 px radius circular AOE every ~400 ms, hitting up to 5 monsters per pulse â€” anything inside the ring gets struck regardless of which direction the clone is facing. Damage rebalanced to ATK Ã— 1.1 + 6 per hit (down from Ã—1.4) since you can now catch ringed-up packs of mobs much more reliably. Net DPS on a single target lands close to the old behavior; AOE clears are dramatically faster.

polishVisible AOE telegraph

  • Persistent faint violet ring drawn on the floor under each clone â€” you can see the strike radius at a glance
  • Ring brightens + thickens during each pulse so the strike timing is readable
  • 360Â° spinning sweep ring around the clone body replaces the old directional crescent slash
  • Drop-shadow puddle on the ground beneath each clone reinforces the "levitating" read
  • Clones spawn further apart (130 px fan, was 80 px) so their AOEs cover more territory without overlapping fully

v0.25.235 Persistent corner loader for deferred sprites

featIn-game asset-stream pill

Added #defer-loader â€” a tiny top-right pill that fades in after the world reveals, showing how many of the v0.25.234 DEFERRED sprites are still streaming. So if the player alt-tabs in or starts running around town immediately, they can see at a glance whether the boss bgs / master FX / biome tiles have finished decoding.

polishPill design

  • Tiny spinning ring icon + text "Loading 32/95" + 60 px progress bar
  • Backdrop blur, soft purple border, gold-and-violet bar gradient â€” matches the loading screen palette
  • pointer-events: none + user-select: none so it doesn't capture clicks or interrupt gameplay
  • Hides itself with a 400 ms fade once defLoaded === DEFERRED.length â€” gone the moment everything is decoded

v0.25.234 Tiered preloader + mobile warm-up â€” faster reveal, smoother first seconds

featTiered loading: critical first, defer the rest

The all-or-nothing v0.25.224 preloader blocked the world reveal until all ~120 sprites finished decoding â€” slow on mobile. Split into two phases:

  • CRITICAL (~21 sprites): town bg, town NPCs, hero base + face layers, first-biome tiles, dialog box + U-panel UI, portal. Loaded in parallel with fetchPriority='high' + img.decode() â€” guaranteed ready-to-paint before world reveal.
  • DEFERRED (~95 sprites): boss bgs, master-class FX, summons, all biome tiles + bgs, mob projectiles. Started via requestIdleCallback after critical finishes â€” streams in background while the player explores town.

Loading bar fills from CRITICAL only, so the percentage progresses much faster on screen. Hard fallback dropped 12 s â†’ 5 s for the smaller critical bucket.

featDecode + fetchpriority hints

Each loader image now uses img.decoding = 'async' + img.fetchPriority = 'high' (critical only) + img.decode() on completion. The decode promise gives the browser a chance to pre-rasterise so the first frame on world reveal doesn't stall.

featMobile starts in low-FX warm-up

LX_PERF.lowFx now defaults to true on mobile (pointer: coarse OR max-width: 900px) for the first 8 seconds of play. The adaptive heuristic continues to run, so:

  • Mid-tier phones with consistently fast frames release the gate after the warm-up window expires.
  • Low-end phones never escape lowFx â€” the additive glow halo + extra particle counts stay disabled, keeping the framerate playable.
  • Desktop is unaffected â€” still starts at full effects and only drops to lowFx after 12 sustained slow frames.

v0.25.233 Archer F wolf sprite + Mojiworld BGM loop

bugWolf sprite now shows for archer F (Wild Bond)

Traced why LX_SUMMON.wolf never appeared on the F-skill summon: drawPet() only routed the BEASTMASTER pack array through drawWolf; the single player.pet spawned by wildBond was hand-rendered with procedural rect-art directly in drawPet, bypassing the sprite. Replaced the inlined draw with a single drawWolf(player.pet, null) call so it picks up LX_SUMMON.wolf (or wolf_alpha for beastmaster, wolf_sky for skyhunter).

featMojiworld main BGM

User-supplied BGM mojiworld.mp3.mpeg copied to audio/bgm_mojiworld.mp3 (3.3 MB). Wired as a looping background track that starts the moment the loading screen fades out. Browsers block autoplay before user interaction, so a one-shot pointerdown / keydown / touchstart listener retries the play on first input â€” guarantees the music kicks in by the time the player presses the arrow key to start moving. Volume sits at 0.55 to stay below SFX. The audio element loops indefinitely via loop = true.

v0.25.232 Aetherion + Gravitos bg fix + better arrow rain sprite

bugAuthored bgs now render in sanctum + singularity arenas

The boss-arena maps for Aetherion (sanctum) and Gravitos (gravitosArena) were configured with explicit bg: 'aetherion' / bg: 'gravitosArena' back in v0.25.213, but the bg renderer kept falling through to a procedural sky gradient. Traced the root cause:

  • Vanilla path _pickBGImage (line 21218): had an early-return at the top â€” if (md.sanctum || md.singularity || ...) return null; â€” which fired BEFORE the md.bg override check.
  • Phaser path pickBgKey (line 2934): same ordering bug.

Both render paths now check the explicit md.bg override FIRST. The early-return for "special backdrops without authored art" is preserved as a fallback for any map that genuinely doesn't want a bitmap. Boss arenas with their own bg keys (sanctum / gravitosArena) finally show the authored Ludo art.

featBetter arrow rain sprite

Regenerated fx/arrow_rain.webp with a denser, more iconic bundle of 15+ sharp golden arrows in a tight downward fan, steel arrowheads with flame trim, golden mist at the impact base. Replaces the earlier sprite which read more as a sparse diagonal scatter.

v0.25.231 Potion rework â€” small/medium/large %-of-max heals

balancePOTION_ITEMS rebuilt as percentage heals

PotionTypeHealPrice
Small Red Potion ðŸ§ªHP25% of max HP85 ðŸ’°
Medium Red Potion ðŸ§ƒHP50% of max HP280 ðŸ’°
Large Red Potion ðŸ’§HP75% of max HP680 ðŸ’°
Small Blue Potion ðŸ”µMP25% of max MP115 ðŸ’°
Medium Blue Potion ðŸ’ MP50% of max MP340 ðŸ’°
Large Blue Potion ðŸŸ¦ (new)MP75% of max MP780 ðŸ’°
Elixir â­full100% HP + MP1250 ðŸ’°

Per user spec: red = HP, blue = MP, no fixed-value heals. Renamed the orange/pure-water/mana-elixir variants to "Medium/Large Red/Blue" so the size tier reads in the name. Added the missing Large Blue Potion (75% MP) for symmetry.

balanceHeal logic + drops use percentages

  • useConsumable now computes heal as Math.ceil(getMaxHp() * pct / 100) â€” scales with the player's actual stat pool instead of a fixed amount
  • Mob-dropped potions are smaller (HP 20% / MP 15%) than shop potions, encouraging buying for big-fight stockup
  • Drop pickup damageNumber + toast show the actual heal amount + the percentage (e.g. "+45 HP (20%)")
  • Shop description rewritten: "Restore 50% of max HP" instead of "Restore 150 HP"

polishElspeth still sells everything

Elspeth was already the potion vendor (NPC role: 'potion') â€” opens "Elspeth's Elixir Stand" which iterates the full POTION_ITEMS list. With the rework, her stand now stocks all 7 potions including the new Large Blue and the renamed sizes. Starting potions on a fresh save remain hp_s Ã— 5 and mp_s Ã— 3 (same ids, new % heals).

v0.25.230 5 new biome-specific floor + platform tile sets

feat10 new biome tiles

Generated 5 floor + 5 matching platform tile pairs via Ludo AI for biomes that previously fell back to grass / stone:

ThemeFloorPlatformMaps using it
candyfloor_candy_v1.webpplatform_candy_v1.webpSweet Candy Canyon
swampfloor_swamp_v1.webpplatform_swamp_v1.webpBubblegum Swamp Â· Tidal Lagoon

honeyfloor_honey_v1.webpplatform_honey_v1.webpHoneycomb Hollow
coralfloor_coral_v1.webpplatform_coral_v1.webpPearl Bathhouse

featTheme dispatch extended

Added 5 new entries to _THEME_FLOOR_KEY + _THEME_PLATFORM_KEY + LX_TILES.sources. Updated _pickFloorTheme(md) to auto-pick the new themes by game.currentMap id (no per-map md.floorTheme override needed). All 10 webp paths added to the v0.25.224 preloader so the world reveal still gates on full asset decode.

v0.25.229 Cute parchment dialogue box for NPC chat

featLudo-generated dialogue box

New Sprites/ui/dialog_box.webp: ornate scroll-banner shape with golden filigree corners, deep purple parchment border, cream interior. Wired into #dialog via background-image at 100% 100% size â€” the box stretches with the text. color flipped to dark purple #2a1a3a + bold weight so the dialogue copy reads cleanly on the cream interior; NPC name in deeper purple #6a3d99 with a cream text-shadow.

polishDialog option buttons retuned

Buttons go from dark-glass to soft purple gradient (#6a3d99 â†’ #4a2870) with a thicker gold border (#ffce5a) and cream text â€” pops against the parchment without overwhelming the dialogue copy. Hover state glows brighter gold.

polishPreloader entry added

Sprites/ui/dialog_box.webp appended to the v0.25.224 REQUIRED list so the box decodes before the world reveal â€” no flash of un-skinned dialog on first NPC interaction.

v0.25.228 Sniper / Ballista / Skyhunter / Dragoon sprite refresh (7 new + 1 regen)

featMechanised bowgun for Ballista G

Regenerated fx/ballista_volley.webp as a steampunk industrial siege bowgun on a wheeled tripod â€” bronze + iron riveted frame, copper steam pipes, glowing red core energy chamber, twin loaded crossbow rails with 8 fanned bolts, golden gear cogs. Replaces the simpler bow design from v0.25.218.

featSniper F (Railshot) â€” laser bow + arrow

New fx/railshot.webp: futuristic crystal-tipped energy bow firing a long horizontal cyan-and-white piercing laser beam with electric arc sparks. Spawned at the firing point, mirrored to facing direction.

featSniper V (Arrow Rain) â€” torrent of arrows

New fx/arrow_rain.webp: massive cascading volley of dozens of golden arrows raining down diagonally with motion-blur trails + sparkling streaks. Spawned at the AOE centre at 320 px / 50 frame life.

featSkyhunter V (Elemental Arrows) â€” epic rainbow burst

New fx/elemental_arrows.webp: massive radial explosion of multi-coloured magic arrows (fire/ice/lightning/nature/void) bursting outward from a rainbow-prismatic core. Spawned at the player with slow spin for the ult-tier presence.

featSkyhunter G â€” sharper Gale projectile

Regenerated p_gale.webp with a SHARP pointed arrowhead tip (instead of the puffy round orb), tighter curling whirl-lines, retained the cute friendly eyes. Same path so LX_PLAYER_PROJ.gale picks it up automatically.

featSkyhunter F â€” sky-spirit wolf summon

New summons/wolf_sky.webp: cream-and-cyan wolf with white wing-feather markings on its sides, soft blue eyes, fluffy feather-tipped tail. Added LX_SUMMON.wolf_sky. drawWolf dispatch updated: skyhunter master class â†’ wolf_sky; beastmaster â†’ wolf_alpha (existing); plain ranger â†’ wolf (existing).

featDragoon G â€” epic AOE impact crater

New fx/dragoon_impact.webp: dragon-themed lance speared into a cracked-stone star-shaped crater with cyan-and-gold shockwave rings, blue plasma lightning arcs, and stone shards flying upward. Spawned at the slam point inside the existing player.dragoonSlam impact branch (held visible for ~38 frames during the AOE window).

polishPreloader manifest updated

Added the 5 new LX_FX + LX_SUMMON entries (railshot, arrow_rain, elemental_arrows, dragoon_impact, wolf_sky) to the v0.25.224 preload list so they all decode before the world reveal â€” no pop-in on first cast.

v0.25.227 Warrior Z (Slash) sprite removed

polishZ basic attack stripped of slash sprite

Per user request, removed the spawnSmoothSlash(... '#ffcc66' ...) call from the warrior slash cast. The basic Z attack now reads as the procedural pixelBurst('arc') flourish only â€” no big gold crescent sprite firing on every swing. The slash_warrior.webp sprite stays in LX_FX since powerStrike (X) and other warrior-gold-palette spawnSmoothSlash callsites still use it.

v0.25.226 Phantom + Nightreaper G â€” hard 12-second cooldown floor

balanceCooldown floor on assassin master G skills

Per user request, ensured phantom_cut and nightreaper_mark always recharge for at least 12 seconds. Both have generous base cooldowns (28 s and 30 s respectively), but stacked CDR (mage class 0.70Ã— Ã— WIS up-to-40% Ã— blanket -25%) could collapse the effective cd well below 12 s. Added computedCd = Math.max(12000, computedCd) branch in the cooldown-apply path scoped to these two skill ids.

SkillBase cdBest-case CDR floor
phantom_cut (Voidrift Execution)28 sâ‰¥ 12 s (was ~10.5 s w/ max stacks)
nightreaper_mark (Blink Barrage)30 sâ‰¥ 12 s (was ~11.3 s w/ max stacks)

v0.25.225 Monster + map balance pass â€” spread Lv 1-10, beef Lv 15-30

balance11 mid-tier mobs buffed ~20%

Spread the 15-map jam at Lv 1-10 across 6 distinct tiers (0-3) so progression doesn't crowd. Lv 15-30 range now has 6 anchor maps (was 4) for a more layered mid/late game.

v0.25.224 All sprites preload before loading screen ends + W modal close fix

featComprehensive preloader manifest (~120 sprites)

The loading-screen REQUIRED list grew from ~50 to ~120 entries. Every sprite the game references via a runtime registry now blocks the world reveal until decoded â€” no more pop-in when the player first sees a new biome / class skill / NPC / FX. Added:

  • 9 player projectile sprites (p_arrow, p_dagger, p_fireball, p_gale, p_icespike, p_lightning, p_mage_orb, p_meteor, p_shockwave)
  • 8 class summon sprites (skeleton, zombie, wolf, wolf_alpha, sword, 3 unique clones)
  • 25 class skill FX sprites (every LX_FX entry from the v0.25.210â€“220 series)
  • 5 U-panel UI sprites (header banner + 4 tab icons)
  • 12 floor + platform tiles + 2 pillars (the LX_TILES set used in drawPlatforms)
  • 22 map biome backgrounds (every BG_IMAGES + cinematic key from v0.25.200/205/213)

Hard-fallback timeout bumped 6 s â†’ 12 s to accommodate the larger asset budget on slow first loads. If anything 404s the loader still reveals; per-sprite _lx*Ready guards continue to gate per-frame draws.

bugW modal X button + Esc now close it

The world-map modal (worldmap-modal) was missing from the closeAllModals id list â€” so neither the X button (which calls closeAllModals) nor the Esc key (same call) actually hid it. Added the id; both close paths now work as expected.

v0.25.223 World map redesigned as a clickable SVG diagram (W key + Taxi share it)

featDiagram-style world map

Replaced the prior bucket-grouped grid of cards with a single SVG diagram. Hub town sits at the centre with concentric tier rings around it: Beginner (Lv 1â€“4) â†’ Intermediate (Lv 5â€“14) â†’ Advanced (Lv 15â€“29) â†’ Endgame (Lv 30+). Boss arenas pin to the bottom corners; the Tower of Ascension pins to the top-right. Subtle dashed connection edges link the hub to every ring node. Background gradient + sparkle stars give the map a celestial feel. Each node is a clickable disc with the biome's emoji icon, name, and sub-label (level requirement / fare / lock reason).

featShared by W key + Taxi NPC

New _renderWorldMapDiagram(host, opts) takes an isAccessible(id, m) predicate and an onPick(id, m) handler â€” letting the same SVG render power two different fast-travel modes:

  • W key (renderWorldMap): any map at or below player level is reachable; clicking warps via loadMap. Higher-level maps render dimmed with a ðŸ”’ sub-label.
  • Taxi NPC (renderTaxi): only visited / taxiAccessible maps are reachable, boss arenas always blocked, fare gating (50ðŸ’°) baked into the predicate. Clicking deducts the fare and warps.

polishModal containers widened

Both worldmap-modal and taxi-modal bumped to min-width:780px / max-width:880-900px to fit the 760-px-wide diagram cleanly. Removed the old grid-with-overflow scroll containers â€” the diagram is fixed-size + responsive via SVG preserveAspectRatio.

polishVisual affordances

  • Current map: gold halo + cream label + "â–¸ YOU ARE HERE" sub-label
  • Inaccessible map: dimmed disc + tier-coloured ring + reason text in muted red
  • Hover any disc to grow it from r=19 to r=22 (cursor: pointer)
  • Tier-colour legend strip along the bottom of the diagram
  • <title> tooltips on every node show full name + level + status

v0.25.222 Compact Skills tab + cleaner tab icons (user edits)

polishSkills tab fits without scrolling

Was a 2-col grid of tall cards each carrying full description + Lv 1â†’50 growth chart, requiring max-height:430px; overflow-y:auto. Now compressed to a 3-col compact tile grid:

  • Description moved from inline block to title tooltip on hover
  • Lv 1â†’50 growth chart removed (marginal value, biggest space hog)
  • Keybind chip 34Ã—34 â†’ inline 18-px pill
  • Cost row consolidated to a single 9-px line: 30MP Â· 5.0s Â· Lv 25+
  • Rank pips 9-px â†’ 6-px circles, rank label condensed (+8% dmg â†’ +8%, rank 0 â†’ r0), [+] button 26Ã—22 â†’ 18Ã—16

All 9 skill tiles now fit on a 1280Ã—800 viewport without scrolling. Hover any tile to see the full description + flavour. Header pool badge + helper text also tightened.

polishUser-edited tab icons

User updated 3 of the 4 tab icons (cleaner illustrative style):

  • tab_levelup.webp â€” replaced with a much sharper 53 KB version (gold up-chevron with sparkles)
  • tab_skills.webp â€” replaced with cleaner spellbook + cyan rune wisps
  • tab_items.webp â€” replaced with cleaner leather backpack + brass buckle
  • tab_boons.webp â€” promoted from tab_boons copy.webp (faceted pink gem in gold setting). Old version (461 KB) replaced with cleaner 152 KB; copy file removed

No code change for the icon swaps â€” the existing background-image:url(...) CSS picks up the new files at the same path.

v0.25.221 U-key Character panel â€” ludo-generated UI art

feat5 new UI sprites for the U panel

SpriteUsed by
Sprites/ui/char_header.webpDecorative scroll-banner behind the "âœ¦ Character âœ¦" title
Sprites/ui/tab_levelup.webpLevel Up tab â€” gold up-chevron with sparkles on purple disc
Sprites/ui/tab_boons.webpBoon Loadout tab â€” magenta gem amulet
Sprites/ui/tab_skills.webpSkills tab â€” arcane spellbook with cyan runes
Sprites/ui/tab_items.webpItems tab â€” adventurer's leather backpack

polishInline-wired into openLevelUpPanel

The header banner is rendered as a background:url(...) center / contain div above the title, with the <h2> overlaid in cream-on-purple text-shadow. Each tab's emoji glyph (ðŸ“ˆ / â—ˆ / ðŸ“– / ðŸŽ’) replaced with a 22Ã—22 span.u-tab-ico that pulls its background-image from the matching tab_*.webp. CSS lives in a small inline <style> block alongside the modal HTML so it scopes cleanly to #u-tabs.

polishLevel-up icon orientation

Ludo's first generation read as a downward chevron (intended interpretation of "up-pointing chevron arrow" went sideways). Auto-flipped vertically with PIL post-download so the gold gem now points up. Saved back to the same path so no code change needed.

v0.25.220 Rogue dagger thrust dramatic + Archer D (Eagle Eye) sprite

featArcher D (Eagle Eye) sprite

Generated fx/eagle_eye.webp via Ludo AI â€” golden eagle eye with sharp piercing pupil, rotating cyan crosshair-and-radar reticle, glowing wing-feather motifs fanning outward. Spawned at the player's chest with slow rotation as the precision-vision buff ignites (200 px, 50 frame life).

v0.25.220 Rogue dagger thrust â€” much more dramatic strong stab

featBigger arc + back-cock + opposing back-arm drive

BoneBefore (v0.25.219)Now (v0.25.220)
Front-arm windup pullâˆ’0.30 â†’ âˆ’0.70 (0.40 rad)âˆ’0.30 â†’ âˆ’1.60 (1.30 rad, +225%)
Front-arm thrust extentâˆ’0.70 â†’ +1.55 (2.25 rad)âˆ’1.60 â†’ +1.80 (3.40 rad, +51%)
Held-thrust window0.30 â†’ 0.45 t (0.15)0.36 â†’ 0.55 t (0.19)
Back-arm motionDrifts back to +0.45Raises high to +0.95 on windup, then DRIVES forward to âˆ’0.50 as the front arm thrusts (opposite-direction body rotation)

featHeavy body commit

  • Spine windup back-twist âˆ’0.06 â†’ âˆ’0.20, strike commit 0.18 â†’ 0.50, recovery whip 0.10 â†’ 0.28
  • Head matches at 0.5Ã— scale
  • Legs: lunge stance âˆ’0.32 / +0.40 (was âˆ’0.18 / +0.22) with extra 0.18 rad shift on strike â€” front foot stamps forward into the lunge

featBob channel â€” windup back-lean â†’ forward lunge

Body now leans BACK âˆ’1.5 px during the windup (using the existing _heroVecAttackWindup ramp) before lunging FORWARD +5.0 px on the strike (was 2.0 px, +150%), then recoils 2.5 px back. Head: âˆ’0.8 px back-lean â†’ +2.2 px forward (was 0.8 px) with a 1.6 px dip mid-strike.

featSquash-stretch overhauled

  • Front arm vertical stretch: 0.30 â†’ 0.55 (more lunge reach)
  • Hand grow on impact: 0.22 â†’ 0.42 + 0.10 sin overlay (dagger-tip emphasis)
  • Spine compress: 0.99 â†’ 0.94, horizontal stretch 1.02 â†’ 1.06 (heavy hip-rotation read)
  • Off-hand vertical stretch: 0 â†’ 0.10 (slight tension as it drives forward)

v0.25.219 Rogue / thief now uses a forward dagger thrust attack animation

featNew attack_rogue animation â€” committed forward dagger thrust

Per user request: the thief class now does a knife-stab thrust instead of inheriting the warrior's wide swing. Built two new bone-curve helpers:

  • _heroVecRogueFrontArm(t) â€” tiny windup pull to âˆ’0.70, then SNAP forward to +1.55 (just past horizontal so the dagger points forward), brief held tremor at full extension, smooth retract back to neutral.
  • _heroVecRogueBackArm(t) â€” off-hand drifts back slightly during the thrust for body balance, stays settled, then returns.

New entries added to:

  • HERO_VEC_ANIMS.attack_rogue â€” modest spine + head lean forward, light leg squat
  • HERO_VEC_DEFORM.attack_rogue â€” front arm stretches lengthwise on the thrust to sell the lunge reach; hand grows slightly at full extension
  • HERO_VEC_BOB.attack_rogue â€” sharp 2 px forward poke + 0.8 px recoil pull (less than the warrior's 5.5 px lurch since a dagger is small + quick)

polishDispatcher + time-norm wiring

_heroVecAnimFor now returns 'attack_rogue' when player.cls === 'rogue'. _heroVecTimeNorm recognises the new anim name so the shared attack timer drives it identically to warrior / archer / mage variants. Slash-arc trail visual stays gated to the warrior-style swings (a thrust doesn't have an arc).

v0.25.218 Flurry rebalance + Mage D cd + warrior C/D/F sprites + 3 unique clones + ballista G

balanceFlurry (D) â€” 1 s cooldown, lower damage

  • SKILLS.flurry.cd: 0 â†’ 1000 ms (engine handles the lockout for both outbound + return)
  • Damage per blink-hit: atk Ã— 1.6 (guaranteed crit-tier) â†’ atk Ã— 0.85 (normal crit roll). Net DPS roughly halved but uptime up dramatically â€” flurry now reads as a mobility tool with damage as side benefit.
  • Removed the manual player.skillCooldowns.flurry = 2000 override on the return-warp branch since the base 1 s cd applies automatically. Description updated.

balanceMage D (Arcane Burst) cooldown 2.8 s â†’ 3 s

Per user request. Skill stays at the v0.25.216 buffed AOE radius (320 px); just slightly longer recovery between casts.

feat3 unique shadowlord clones

Generated 3 distinct ninja silhouette sprites for the Shadowlord master G summons:

  • summons/clone_left.webp â€” crouching kunai stance (phase 0)
  • summons/clone_center.webp â€” upright dual-tanto leader (phase 1)
  • summons/clone_right.webp â€” leaping shuriken throw (phase 2)

drawShadowClones now picks the matching sprite per c.phase and renders via drawImage instead of the procedural silhouette stack. Mirror flip on facing. Procedural silhouette retained as a cold-start fallback.

featWarrior C/D/F skill sprites (no warrior gold so no dust ring leak)

SpriteSkillPalette
fx/rush.webpC â€” Rush dashCyan-blue motion swoosh
fx/warcry.webpD â€” War Cry shoutBlue-white sound waves
fx/bloodlust.webpF â€” Bloodlust rageCrimson + violet aura

All three sprites use deliberately non-gold palettes to avoid triggering the warrior dust-ring auto-dispatch. Wired into the matching cast functions via spawnSpriteBurst.

featBallista G (Siege Volley) sprite

fx/ballista_volley.webp â€” ornate steel ballista weapon with 8 fanned arrows + golden charging glow + ready-to-launch tension. Spawned at the player's chest with facing-mirrored rotation as the volley charges.

v0.25.217 Shinobi G epic chain anim + master flourish gold fix + rogue Z sprite removed

featShinobi G â€” 7-seal animated chain

The Kage Rush cast now spawns SEVEN paper-talisman seals along the dash path (was 2: start + dest). Each seal scheduled i Ã— 90 ms apart so the chain reads as a flowing animated trail. Endpoint seals are bigger (170 px / 32 frames); intermediate seals scale up subtly toward the destination (110â†’146 px) for a "chain accelerating into the rush" feel. Alternating spin direction (Â±1.4Ï€ rad/lifetime) makes the trail visually dynamic.

bugDust ring leaking onto every master skill

masterSkillFlourish spawned a triple-ring burst with the second ring coloured #ffdd44 (gold) â€” which sits in the warrior-bucket palette set, so the heuristic dispatch in drawSmoothFx auto-triggered the dust_ring sprite on EVERY master-tier cast (including thief/archer/mage ones). Switched the ring colour to #fff080 â€” visually similar pale yellow, but outside both the warrior and rogue palette sets so no sprite is auto-triggered. Shinobi G + every other non-warrior master skill now flourishes cleanly.

polishRogue Z (stab) sprite removed

Per user request, the basic Z thrust no longer fires the stab sprite. Reads as the procedural pixelBurst flourish only. The stab.webp stays in LX_FX â€” still used by backstab's triple-thrust finisher (v0.25.214).

v0.25.216 Mage D (Arcane Burst) â€” much larger AOE

featArcane Burst AOE radius 120 â†’ 320 (+167%)

Mage's arcaneBurst (D skill) is now a proper "clear the room" panic button instead of a tight 120-px self-AOE. Damage tier bumped 2.0Ã— â†’ 2.4Ã— and knockback 5 â†’ 9 so enemies caught in the much wider blast actually feel the impact.

polishVisual presence scaled to match

  • Magic-circle sprite size 180 â†’ 360 px (2Ã— larger), held visible 28 â†’ 34 frames
  • Pixel-burst ring: 20 â†’ 36 particles, speed 7 â†’ 11, size 3 â†’ 4, life 28 â†’ 34
  • Particle ring: 40 â†’ 80 particles, speed 8 â†’ 12-18 (varied), 3-tone palette (white / lilac / violet)
  • Screen shake 5 â†’ 9, flash 0 â†’ 0.32 intensity

polishSkill description updated

SKILLS.arcaneBurst.desc: "AoE explosion around you" â†’ "Massive 320-px AoE explosion centred on you".

v0.25.215 8 new class skill sprites + meteor scale-up + cuter icicles

feat8 new sprites wired across all classes

SpriteSkillFile
Warrior gold crescentpowerStrike (X)reuses fx/slash_warrior.webp per-lane
Knight shield bubbleguardian (F)fx/knight_guardian.webp
Wolf paw-print aurawildBond / beastmaster Ffx/wild_bond.webp
Crosshair + sniper lasermarksman_oneshot (G)fx/marksman_oneshot.webp
Thunder chainelemental / archmage Vfx/archmage_elemental.webp

Bigger epic meteormeteor / sage_meteorshowerprojectiles/p_meteor.webp (regen)

featSage G meteor scaled up + longer trail

Meteor render in drawHazards's meteor_warn branch now draws at 100â†’170 px (was 50â†’80, ~2Ã— the visual presence). Trail extended from 4 wisps to 6 with wider spacing for a more dramatic comet plummet. The sprite itself was regenerated with explicit "epic, dramatic, screen-shaking impact feel" prompt.

polishMage S icicles cuter

Regenerated p_icespike.webp as a cluster of 3-4 chubby smiling icicles with big eyes and rosy cheeks instead of the prior sharp shard. Same path so LX_PLAYER_PROJ.icespike picks it up automatically.

polishFlurry cooldown reset behaviour confirmed

The v0.25.214 implementation already matches the user's spec: outbound press has SKILLS.flurry.cd = 0 (instant repeat), the return-warp branch sets player.skillCooldowns.flurry = 2000, the cooldown ticks down naturally, and once it hits 0 the next outbound is available with zero cooldown. No code change needed â€” verified the engine writes the cd before calling the cast function (line 12791 â†’ 12817), so the manual override after the warp persists correctly.

v0.25.214 Master G skill sprites + epic sword + flurry cooldown rework + dust ring fix

feat5 new master-class G skill sprites

Generated and wired epic 2D burst sprites for the warrior + thief master signatures via Ludo AI. Each fires through spawnSpriteBurst at the cast moment with appropriate size + lifetime + spin.

SpriteSkill
fx/warlord_banner.webpWarlord â€” banner planted at cast
fx/dragoon_skylance.webpDragoon â€” sky-lance dive
fx/crusader_aegis.webpCrusader â€” divine cross + 5 holy orbs (slow spin)
fx/shadowlord_clones.webpShadowlord â€” 3 ninja silhouettes fanned out
fx/shinobi_seal.webpShinobi â€” paper talisman seal at start + dest

featEpic doombringer Blade of Calamity sword

Replaced Sprites/summons/sword.webp with a much more epic regen â€” dark obsidian blade with glowing molten-orange runes, gold cross-guard with red gem, leather grip, flame aura. LX_SUMMON.sword picks it up automatically (same path).

featWarrior S (groundSlam) burst sprite

Generated fx/ground_slam.webp â€” golden-orange star-shaped shockwave with cracked-stone center + jagged lightning rays. Spawned at the slam impact (slow spin) on top of the existing dust ring auto-trigger.

bugDust ring no longer has white opaque centre

Regenerated fx/dust_ring.webp with explicit "centre completely empty and transparent" prompt. Verified centre pixel alpha is 0. The rim dust still reads as a shockwave but no longer punches a white disc through the play area when it triggers on warrior gold explosions.

featFlurry cooldown â€” 0 s outbound, 2 s on return-warp

Per user request: the outbound blink-strike now has zero cooldown (mash-able for instant reposition), but pressing the skill again to warp back imposes a 2 s cooldown immediately after the return fires. Implemented via SKILLS.flurry.cd = 0 + manual player.skillCooldowns.flurry = 2000 at the end of the return-warp branch.

polishBackstab finisher now uses the stab sprite

Per user request: backstab's triple-stab finisher now spawns three stab sprites (the Z-skill piercing-thrust line) staggered 60 ms apart instead of a single dark-burst. Reads as the rapid combo of stabs at the target rather than one big flash.

v0.25.213 5 new dungeon/boss-themed map backgrounds

feat5 new user-supplied map bgs

Slotted from backgrounds/New background/ as theme-matching keys. Each map moved off its prior generic-cinematic fallback to the new dedicated art:

File (renamed)BG keyMapWas
bg_v3_gravitosArena.pnggravitosArenaThe Singularity (gravitosArena)zodiacSunset
bg_v3_aetherion.pngaetherionAetherion's SanctumzodiacDusk
bg_v3_cryptHollow.pngcryptHollowCrypt of Whispersdungeon

Added to BG_IMAGES + Phaser preloader. The prior generic keys (zodiacSunset, zodiacDusk, dungeon, underwater, misty) stay registered and still pin other maps + drive the random cinematic rotation.

v0.25.212 Thief skill sprites + Flurry reworked as teleport-toggle

feat5 new flashy thief skill sprites

SpriteUsed by
fx/flurry.webpD â€” Flurry asterisk burst at both teleport endpoints
fx/stab.webpZ â€” Stab piercing-thrust effect
fx/backstab.webpX â€” Backstab finisher critical burst
fx/death_blossom.webpV (ninja) â€” sakura petal burst per wave
fx/shadow_strike.webpF (ninja) â€” blink-strike flash at each target

featNew spawnSpriteBurst helper

Generic one-shot sprite-spawn helper that pushes a 'spriteBurst' entry to game.smoothFx. Supports size / life / angle / spin options. Renderer in drawSmoothFx grows the sprite 0.5Ã—â†’1.1Ã— over the burst lifetime with a soft fade-out.

featFlurry (D) reworked â€” teleport blink-strike

Previously: airborne-only 6-strike V-slash combo. Now: classic Naruto-style flash-step.

  • First press: teleports the player ~240 px forward, slashing every enemy in the corridor (guaranteed crit-tier hits, +60% atk per hit). Saves a return anchor.
  • Second press (within 6 s): teleports back to the original spot, slashing the corridor again on the return path.
  • Flurry asterisk sprite spawns at BOTH endpoints with opposite-spin so the cast reads as a flashy double-tap. Ghosted blink trail of afterimages between the points. Hit-stop + flash + shake on each warp.
  • Toast feedback: "FLURRY Â· press again to return" on outbound, "FLURRY RETURN" on warp-back.
  • Skill description updated to match the new behaviour.

v0.25.211 Whole-body Taxi Uncle + cleaner slash/shockwave sprites

featTaxi Uncle as whole-body 2D NPC sprite

Regenerated Sprites/npc/taxi_uncle.webp via Ludo AI as a full-body flat-2D character â€” yellow taxi cap with checkered band, bushy black mustache, beige short-sleeved shirt with black tie, navy trousers, brown shoes, holding car keys. Previous version was head-and-shoulders bust which read awkwardly when rendered at the same scale as full-body NPCs. Pure flat 2D vector style with bold black outlines, no 3D shading.

polishUser-edited slash + shockwave sprites cleaned up

The user manually re-cropped three FX sprites for cleaner, tighter framing:

  • Sprites/fx/slash_warrior.webp â€” gold crescent, transparent padding trimmed
  • Sprites/fx/slash_rogue.webp â€” pure magenta crescent (motion blur removed)
  • Sprites/projectiles/p_shockwave.webp â€” red crescent with white teeth + leading motion-blur blob

No code changes needed â€” same paths, registries pick them up automatically.

v0.25.210 Cute 2D skill-FX sprites for warrior + thief (4 new ludo-generated)

feat4 new class-FX sprites

  • Sprites/fx/slash_warrior.webp â€” gold/yellow crescent slash (warrior)
  • Sprites/fx/slash_rogue.webp â€” magenta/pink narrow slash (rogue/thief)
  • Sprites/fx/smoke_puff.webp â€” purple-gray smoke cloud (rogue smoke)
  • Sprites/fx/dust_ring.webp â€” tan dust shockwave (warrior ground slam)

featHeuristic dispatch in drawSmoothFx

New LX_FX registry + _lxFxBucket(color) helper. The slash + explosion branches in drawSmoothFx now check the spawn colour against two palette buckets (warrior gold, rogue purple/pink). When a match hits AND the sprite has decoded, drawImage the cute crescent / smoke / dust sprite instead of the procedural arc-stack or radial gradient. Falls through to the existing procedural path for any non-class palette.

featTagged warrior + thief skill spawns

Added explicit class-coloured spawnSmoothSlash / spawnSmoothExplosion calls so the new sprites trigger on cast:

  • Warrior slash: spawnSmoothSlash(... '#ffcc66' ...) â€” gold crescent ahead of the player
  • Warrior groundSlam impact: spawnSmoothExplosion(... '#ffcc66' ...) â€” dust ring at the slam point
  • Rogue smokeDash: spawnSmoothExplosion(... '#9944cc' ...) â€” smoke puff at the dash end
  • Rogue smokeBomb: spawnSmoothExplosion(... '#9944cc' ...) â€” smoke puff at the bomb point

Existing warrior / rogue skills that already spawn smoothFx with class palettes (Blade of Calamity slam, Death Blossom, Shadow Strike impacts, etc.) automatically pick up the new sprites without any callsite changes.

v0.25.209 Mage orb + Sage meteor + Beastmaster alpha wolf + Taxi Uncle NPC

featMage Z basic attack â€” blue/yellow energy orb

New p_mage_orb.webp: a cute round magical sphere with sky-blue outer glow and golden-yellow core. Wired into LX_PLAYER_PROJ.mage_orb. magicBolt (the mage's Z basic attack, internal skill: 'bolt') now renders this orb instead of the prior lightning sprite. 'lightning' (any future skill using that exact label) still maps to the lightning sprite.

featSage F + G â€” proper 2D fireball meteor

New p_meteor.webp: round flaming fireball â€” molten orange-red rock core wrapped in bright yellow flame, no face/eyes (more dramatic than the cute eye-faced fireball). Wired into drawHazards's meteor_warn branch. The mage's job-F meteor and Sage's master-G sage_meteorshower both push meteor_warn hazards, so both render with the new sprite + 4 trailing fiery wisps. Falls back to the procedural circle stack if the sprite hasn't decoded yet.

featBeastmaster F â€” alpha wolf variant

New Sprites/summons/wolf_alpha.webp: a cute alpha wolf â€” dark gray-blue fur, white chest mane, golden eyes, small red flower marking behind one ear. Added LX_SUMMON.wolf_alpha. drawWolf now picks the alpha variant when player.master === 'beastmaster'; ranger jobs without the master advancement keep the regular wolf. Both share the same hitbox and animation.

featNPC: Caravan Yara â†’ Taxi Uncle

Renamed the caravan NPC from Caravan Yara to Taxi Uncle. New Sprites/npc/taxi_uncle.webp sprite generated via Ludo AI: a cute middle-aged plump big-headed taxi driver with rosy cheeks, bushy mustache, yellow taxi cap, beige shirt + tie, holding car keys. Updated 3 call sites â€” town NPC list (maple_game.html:6335), portrait map (:30013), and preloader manifest (:34998). Old caravan_yara.webp stays on disk as fallback in case any save reference still points to it.

v0.25.208 Cute sprites for every player projectile skill (7 new ludo-generated)

feat7 new player projectile sprites

Generated cute simple chibi sprites for every player projectile skill family. New LX_PLAYER_PROJ registry + _lxPlayerProjReady helper sit next to LX_MOB_PROJ:

SpriteSkills covered
p_arrow.webparrow, charged, deadeye, rainArrow, elemArrow, siege, oneshot
p_fireball.webpfire
p_icespike.webpice
p_dagger.webpdagger
p_lightning.webpbolt, lightning
p_gale.webpgale
p_shockwave.webpbloodwave, shockwave

featSprite-first dispatch in drawProjectiles

Inserted at the top of the p.owner === 'player' branch: a single lookup picks the right sprite per p.skill, then drawImage rotated to atan2(p.vy, p.vx) draws the cute sprite at 1.6Ã— the hitbox size so the image reads cleanly. continue skips the procedural branches below. If the sprite hasn't decoded yet (cold-start), the procedural shape stack still draws as a fallback.

v0.25.207 Floor + platform tiles regen seamless + portal at 65% opacity

bugFloor tiles had visible gaps between repetitions

The v0.25.199 ludo-generated tiles were rendered as TWO distinct blocks side-by-side per source image, separated by a small transparent gap. The 'auto' seamless-bake didn't break on the gap (12-col threshold) so it captured both blocks AS ONE TILE â€” including the doubled black outlines and the internal gap between them. Tiled across a long ground span, this produced a visible vertical seam every tileW pixels.

featRe-generated all 10 tiles as continuous strips

Dispatched fresh prompts to Ludo AI explicitly requiring "seamless horizontally tileable, ONE continuous strip, no outlines or borders on the left or right edges, edges blend smoothly so it tiles infinitely". New 16:9 source aspect bakes to one full-width seamless tile.

ThemeFloor (was)Floor (now)Platform (was)Platform (now)
Grassfloor_grass_v4floor_grass_v5platform_grass_v3platform_grass_v4
Stonefloor_stone_v2floor_stone_v3platform_stone_v3platform_stone_v4
Icefloor_ice_v2floor_ice_v3platform_ice_v3platform_ice_v4
Lavafloor_lava_v2floor_lava_v3platform_lava_v3platform_lava_v4
Sandfloor_sand_v2floor_sand_v3platform_sand_v3platform_sand_v4

Verified the new floor_grass_v5 bake captures full 1344Ã—768 source as a single tile â€” no internal break â€” and tiles 3Ã— without seams.

polishPortal opacity 65%

Added ctx.save() / globalAlpha = 0.65 / restore() around the drawImage(img, -55, -78, 110, 156) call in drawPortals(). The pastel portal now blends softly with the scene rather than reading as a solid opaque sprite. Halo + sparkle additive passes still draw at full alpha.

v0.25.206 Warrior 2H swing â€” much more dramatic

featBigger arc + anticipation hold

Arm rotation arc went from âˆ’2.40 â†’ +1.90 (4.30 rad) to âˆ’2.95 â†’ +2.50 (5.45 rad, +27%). New 9-frame anticipation HOLD at the apex of the windup so the sword visibly cocks behind the head before snapping forward. Strike phase tightened from 0.36 â†’ 0.28 for a sharper SNAP. Recovery whip extended from 1.45 â†’ 1.95 for heavier follow-through.

featBody commits like an executioner

  • Spine pre-twist on windup âˆ’0.22 â†’ âˆ’0.42, strike commit 0.65 â†’ 1.10, recovery whip 0.30 â†’ 0.55
  • Head matches at half scale (more aggressive lookahead during the strike)
  • Leg bend on strike 0.22 â†’ 0.40 â€” knees squat into the swing

featForward lurch overhaul

New _heroVecAttackWindup(t) ramps 0â†’1 through the windup phase then decays. Warrior bob channel now leans the body BACK during windup (âˆ’2.4 px spine, âˆ’1.4 px head) before snapping forward (+5.5 px, was +3.6). Vertical dip 1.6 â†’ 3.0, head dip 4.2 â†’ 6.5.

featSlash-arc visual fires for warrior + scales up

The arc trail under the weapon was gated to animName === 'attack' only â€” warriors weren't getting it at all. Now fires for attack_warrior too, with warrior-specific scale: arc radius 25 â†’ 42, arc span 2.95 â†’ 5.45, outer stroke width 7 â†’ 11, inner stroke 2 â†’ 4, brighter alpha. Plus a new gold "shockwave" arc behind the trail at peak swing for the dramatic flash.

polishSquash-stretch overhauled

Arm vertical stretch 0.40 â†’ 0.65. Spine stretch 0.10 â†’ 0.18, compress 0.96 â†’ 0.92. Hand grow on impact 0.22 â†’ 0.40 + 0.16 â†’ 0.26 sin overlay. The whole body reads as a coiled spring releasing.

v0.25.205 5 new theme-specific backdrops slotted to literal-match maps

feat5 new user-supplied backdrops

User dropped 5 new bg PNGs in backgrounds/. Renamed (no spaces), added as new BG_IMAGES keys + Phaser preloader entries, and reassigned the literal-match maps to use them:

FileBG keyMapWas
bg_v3_bubblegum.pngbubblegumbubblegumSwampcoralReef
bg_v3_candyland.pngcandylandcandyCanyonzodiacSunset
bg_v3_celestialAtrium.pngcelestialAtriumstardustAtriumcosmos
bg_v3_galaxy.pnggalaxycelestialSpiremeadow
bg_v3_sandDune.pngsandDuneduneSandsmeadow

The previous bg keys still exist and are wired (cosmos/zodiacSunset/coralReef still pin maps like boss / gravitosArena / nothing yet). No deletions â€” just additions plus 5 reassignments.
