The front arm sweeps through a much wider arc, the back arm counter-swings, the torso leans into the hit, quick attacks keep a short one-shot visual timer, and the slash trail is widened so the swing reads as actually reaching toward monsters.

v0.25.144 Vector hero cleanup

polishCleaner face, hair, and walk cycle

The vector hero now uses simpler black-and-white eyes, softer head shading, lighter face accents, and cleaner hair silhouettes across the Character Studio hair options. Walking was toned down with smaller arm and leg swings, reduced head bob, less body squash, and a slower cycle so the character feels calmer in motion.

v0.25.143 Early map variety

polishLess standard low-level zones

Early progression maps now roll named layout variants on load instead of relying only on generic platform jitter. Emerald Thicket, Fungal Hollow, Sunset Coast, and Elderwood Grove gain distinct set-piece routes such as canopy bridges, fallen-log runs, spore rings, tide pools, shell cliffs, elder ruins, and hanging groves.

v0.25.142 Vector hero animation polish

polishSharper hair, darker eyes, livelier motion

The vector hero rig now has a snappier weapon swing with anticipation, fast follow-through, and a wider motion trail; walking gets stronger arm/leg overlap plus head bob; jumping now poses differently on rise versus fall. Hair silhouettes were redrawn with more layered bangs, side locks, curls, and sharper profiles, while eyes are now mostly black with smaller coloured iris glints and tiny highlights.

v0.25.141 Vector hero art polish

polishChatGPT Image-style character pass

The canvas vector hero gets a more polished illustration read while staying fully code-native: coloured glossy irises, visible ears, a tiny nose, stronger hair ribbons, outfit collar/belt accents, little hands, and boot soles. Because this lands inside _drawVectorHero, the in-game player and the Q Character Studio preview update together without adding bitmap assets.

v0.25.140 Performance

polishOutdoor overlay cache

Outdoor fog now reuses one cached canvas gradient per sky palette instead of rebuilding it every frame, and optional god rays are skipped while the adaptive low-FX mode is active. This cuts repeated full-screen canvas work in busy maps without changing combat, hitboxes, or enemy AI.

polishCooler cooldown UI

The skill dock now caches cooldown text and conic-fill percentages, so active cooldowns only write to the DOM when the visible rounded value changes. Combat timing and skill readiness are unchanged; this just removes unnecessary per-frame style and text churn.

Phase 1 Foundation

Save / load â€” versioned localStorage

  • saveState() / loadState() / clearSave() / hasSave() with explicit field whitelists.
  • SAVE_VERSION = 1 gate â€” future schema changes either migrate or fall back cleanly.
  • Autosaves every 15 s of wall time, plus on class-select, level-up, boss-kill, respawn, tab-hide, and beforeunload.
  • Continue-from-save always lands in town with clean transient state â€” never loads into hp â‰¤ 0 or a mid-boss killbox.
  • Phase-2 placeholder fields (achievements, dailyState, prestige, comboRecord, muted) pre-added to the schema so follow-up features don't need a version bump.

Web Audio â€” synthesized, no assets

  • 4 SFX via AudioContext oscillator envelopes:
    • Hit â€” 240 Hz square blip
    • Crit â€” 520 / 780 Hz two-tone
    • Level up â€” C-E-G triangle arpeggio
    • Death â€” 180 Hz sawtooth slide
  • Initialized on first user gesture (class-select click, keydown, or pointerdown) to satisfy Chrome/Safari autoplay policy.
  • M toggles mute. Mute state persists in the save.

Phase 2 Retention hooks

ðŸ†Achievements + codex (press N)

  • 12 milestones tracked in game.achievements, persisted via the Phase 1 schema.
  • Modal shows unlocked + locked rows, live kill counter, and best-ever combo (game.comboRecord).
  • Hooks fire from killMonster, the level-up loop, bumpCombo, and attemptEnhance.

ðŸŒ…Daily login + rotating challenge

  • Deterministic UTC-day rotation â€” Math.floor(Date.now() / 86400000) % 4. Four challenges: 50 kills, reach level 15, enhance once, 30-hit combo.
  • Login bonus: +200 Ã— min(7, streak) Lumens.
  • HUD banner (top-right) shows the current challenge, progress bar, and streak.
  • Rolls on new-game class-select and on every load.

âŸ³Respec (button inside the attributes modal, U)

  • Cost formula: 2000 Ã— max(1, level âˆ’ 5) Lumens.
  • Refunds all allocated AP to skillPoints and resets milestonesUnlocked.
  • Live cost + "you have X" preview rendered every frame while the modal is open.

âœ¨Prestige / Ascension

  • Auto-offered when the player reaches level 50.
  • Each ascension: +30 % XP, +30 % damage, +1 permanent starting AP â€” all stackable.
  • Resets level / exp / attributes / job / master / inventory / defeated-boss set.
  • getAtk() applies game.prestige.dmgMult. All XP gains (normal + super-boss) apply game.prestige.xpMult.

âš¡Combo milestone buffs (inside bumpCombo)

  • Cross 50 hits â†’ +50 % ATK for 8 s.
  • Cross 100 hits â†’ next 3 crits guaranteed.
  • Cross 150 hits â†’ +100 % XP for 10 s.
  • Timer buffs decay via the existing player.buffs tick loop. The guaranteed-crit counter is tracked on player._guaranteedCrit so it doesn't decay as a timer.

Phase 3 Juice pass

Per-hit impact flash

Every swing now fires an extra screen flash â€” flash(0.1) on basic hits, flash(0.35) on crits â€” on top of the existing shake / hit-stop / audio / particles.

Damage-scaled feedback

  • Shake now scales with finalDmg: addShake((isCrit ? 5 : 1.8) + min(6, finalDmg/200)).
  • Damage-number font size scales with damage for both normal and crit, so a 500-damage crit visibly towers over a 40-damage jab.

Drop-rarity theatrics

  • emitRarityBurst(x, y, rarity) fires rarity-keyed bursts on every loot site (normal kill, mini-boss, boss extras, super-boss pile):
    • Rare 14 blue particles + small flash.
    • Epic 24 purple particles + flash(0.18) + addShake(2.5).
    • Legendary 40 gold particles + flash(0.35) + addShake(5) + level-up chime.
  • emitNearMiss(x, y) teases 6 gray sparkles when a drop roll failed by < 4 % margin.

Enemy squash-stretch on hit

  • hitMonster sets m.scaleX / m.scaleY â€” 1.22 / 0.74 on crit, 1.14 / 0.82 on normal.
  • updateMonsters eases them back to 1.0 at 22 %/frame.
  • The drawMonster call site wraps in ctx.save / scale / restore, anchored at the sprite's feet so the entire monster pops without floating.

Boss phase transitions

  • Aetherion phase 2 + 3 now call addHitStop + audio.play('crit') for cinematic punch.
  • Gravitos (v0.9.x addition) gets the same treatment â€” phase 2 and phase 3 both carry a hit-stop + audio cue.

Patches Follow-ups

Reset button

Destructive option inside the attributes modal (U), below Respec. Double-confirms, calls clearSave(), and reloads to class-select. Sets game._resetting = true first so the autosave / tab-hide / beforeunload handlers don't rewrite the save during the reload window.

Also surfaced on the HUD as a small red â˜  Reset pill next to the help button (bottom-right), so a friend loading the build can wipe the save without digging into the attributes menu. Reuses the same resetSave() flow. The pill sits at z-index: 60 so it stays reachable even while the class-select modal (.modal-overlay, z-index 50) is open.

Codex keybind fix â€” C â†’ N

Skills bind to fixed slot letters ['d','s','a','e','w','q','c','x'], so C triggers a skill cast for any class with a c-slot skill. The codex shortcut was firing in parallel with the skill cast, opening the modal mid-combat. Moved codex to N; no collision.

âœ¦Juicy damage numbers

Dopamine-driven pass on enemy damage popups so every hit lands as a tiny reward. All the effects live in drawDamageNumbers() and the main hitMonster push, so secondary pushes (EMPOWERED, ECHO, VOID!, EXEC, etc.) inherit the juice automatically via field back-fill.

  • Pop-in overshoot. easeOutBack scale from ~0.4x to 1x over 8 frames â€” each number punches onto screen instead of appearing flat.
  • Shrink-and-fade tail. Final ~14 frames shrink while fading so numbers "poof" out cleanly, closing the micro-reward loop.
  • Scatter. Random horizontal vx (with drag) plus a short rotation wobble on spawn so stacked hits don't read as a single column.
  • Tiered colour ramp. Non-crit tiers by damage â€” <80 white â†’ <200 warm â†’ <500 orange â†’ 500+ hot red. Crits stay gold; 500+ crits shift to amber. Magnitude is readable pre-attentively before the eye parses the digits.
  • Embossed crits. Crits render with a coloured shadowBlur glow and an inner #fff7b0 stroke over the black outline â€” the "gold foil" look that telegraphs a big hit.
  • Comma formatting. finalDmg.toLocaleString() â€” 1,234 reads bigger than 1234, and four-figure hits now feel like a milestone.
  • Linger on big hits. Life bumped from 46 â†’ 52 (tier â‰¥ 2) / 58 (crit) so the player has time to admire the number.

Hotkey 0 â€” change job / unlock mastery (v0.9.9)

Hotkey 0 now acts as a shortcut to the advancement flow so players don't have to wait for the automatic modal to re-fire.

Master skill bar fix â€” level-20 X slot

At level 20 every master advancement unlocks an ultimate skill bound to the x slot (Grand Warcry, Apocalypse Swing, Shadow Clones, Meteor Shower, One Shot One Kill, etc.). renderSkillBar was calling skillsForClass(player.cls, player.job) â€” missing the third master argument â€” so the filter at skillsForClass silently dropped any skill scoped to a master, leaving the X slot stuck on its locked placeholder. Casting also effectively failed because the player never saw the icon to know it was ready. Same bug fixed in the NPC skill-trainer modal (openSkillsModal).

v0.12.1 Endgame pass Â· identity Â· feel

Eight-item release: a unique sniper skill, cooldown tuning across every class, cel-shaded Dragon Quest-style mob art, stronger knockback, name + cosmetic picker, silent-reward feedback, cosmetic unlocks, and an infinite-floor tower mode.

Sniper
Railshot (S slot)
Pierce line Â· empowered chain
Balance
Cooldown pass
~15% faster across classes
Art
DQ cel-shaded mobs
2-tone Â· 2 px stroke Â· glossy eyes
Feel
Knockback +35%
Global KB multiplier
Identity
Name + cosmetic picker
Hair / skin / eye swatches
Flow
Silent-reward feedback
Pickup / refund / chest sfx + toast
Mastery
Cosmetic unlocks
Bestiary + tower + boss gated
Mastery
Tower of Ascension
Infinite floors Â· rotating modifiers

âœ¦Sniper S â€” Railshot

Sniper-specific override for the archer S slot. Instant piercing rail in a flat line from the player; every hit empowers the next by +30% damage (escalating chain). Thick beam, electric sparks on impact, muzzle burst, shake. Cooldown 1300 ms, MP 16. Triggers snipe_railgun entry in SKILL_FNS; selection happens through the new skillBySlot() specificity scorer (master > job > class).

Cooldown rebalance

Full tuning pass across every skill definition in SKILLS. Rogue combat feels faster, mage skills recast cleaner, warrior heavy swings breathe. Master signatures trimmed 5â€“15% except lich_harvest (60s, spec-designed).

ClassRepresentative CDs (ms)
WarriorSlash 360 Â· Power 720 Â· Slam 1000 Â· Rush 900 Â· WarCry 7000
RogueStab 150 Â· Backstab 650 Â· Dagger 650 Â· Dash 750 Â· Flurry 900
MageBolt 210 Â· Fireball 800 Â· Ice 650 Â· Blink 650 Â· Burst 1400
ArcherArrow 260 Â· Multi 700 Â· Charged 950 Â· Evade 750 Â· EagleEye 7000

âœ¦Dragon Quest-style mob art

Full rewrite of the grunt renderer (drawCuteMob) to cel-shaded 2-tone style. Every mob now gets an iconic silhouette with hard-edge lighting, a thick 2 px outer contour, and glossy anime-style eyes with a large upper-left highlight.

  • 2-tone cel-shading. _celFillPath paints the base color, clips to the silhouette, and fills a diagonal polygon wedge in the shade tone â€” crisp, no gradients. Light source implied upper-left.
  • 2 px outer stroke. _celStroke with rounded joins / caps for the signature DQ "confident contour" feel.
  • Glossy anime eyes. _celEye â€” white sclera, vertical-oval pupil shifted by facing, large primary highlight + small secondary gloss. Evil variants get a red iris core and a soft red glow halo.
  • Per-type iconic silhouettes. Slime (teardrop), snail (round shell + antenna stalks), mushroom (dome + polka dots), horned (bone-triangle horns + fangs), stump (barrel + wood rings + leaf sprout), zombie (drippy cap + X eyes + drool), mummy (bandage wraps + one slit covered), skeleton (skull shape + ribcage + spine), wraith (robed ghost + wavy tail + glow halo), scorpion (segmented carapace + pincer claws + curled stinger).
  • Unified dispatch. drawMonster routes all grunts through drawCuteMob; bosses (king slime, mushmom, aetherion, gravitos) keep their signature per-type renderers.

Knockback +35%

Global Ã— 1.35 multiplier applied inside performMelee / performAround, plus direct-site bumps on warrior smash (10 â†’ 14), rogue V-slash (4 â†’ 6), ground slam (7 â†’ 10), misc front-facing skill hits (3 â†’ 5). Enemies fly further on impact without feeling unfair.

âœ¦Character name + cosmetic picker

Class-select modal now includes a name input (#hero-name-input) and three swatch rows (#swatch-hair / #swatch-skin / #swatch-eye). Selecting a swatch writes to player.look and auto-computes complementary tones via _mixHex (e.g. picking a hair color also sets hairHi; picking a skin color sets skinHi + skinShd). The chosen name renders as a gold-bordered tag above the player's head in-game via _drawPlayerNameTag, alongside their level.

Silent-reward feedback pass

Closed dopamine leaks on previously-silent rewards. New Web Audio envelopes in audio.play: potion_hp, potion_mp, meso, mp_refund, cd_refund, chest_open, pickup.

  • Potion pickup. Color-tinted particle burst + HP/MP toast + envelope sfx.
  • Meso pickup. Large meso drops (â‰¥ 40) now play a chime; huge drops (â‰¥ 120) also toast with rarity color.
  • MP refund (Mana Font milestone) plays a rising cue; CD refund (Spellweaver) plays a short recast cue + -1s CD number.
  • Chest open. 3-note chime, rising sparkle column, tier-scaled shake + flash. Gold/silver chests also layer the levelup cue.

âœ¦Cosmetic unlock system

COSMETICS catalog for hair / cape / weapon skins earned through trackPickup hooks on kills, chests, boss kills, and tower floors. Bestiary counts persist via game.bestiary; unlocks persist on player.cosmetics.

  • Hair â€” Flame Tips (50 mushrooms), Mint Frost (30 slimes), Twilight (20 wraiths), Royal Gold (5 gold chests).
  • Cape â€” Shadow (25 horny), Ember (10 scorpions), Frost (tower floor 10), Astral (tower floor 25).
  • Weapon â€” Crystal Blade (kill Aetherion), Void Edge (kill Gravitos), Sunfire (tower floor 15), Heirloom (10 silver chests).

Unlocks announce with an epic toast + levelup cue; checkCosmeticUnlocks() fires on every trackPickup call, saving automatically.

âœ¦Tower of Ascension

New infinite-floor map (MAPS.tower) with a permanent portal from town (MAPS.town.portals). startTower sets up wave state; killing all enemies on a floor advances to the next. HP scales +18%/floor, ATK +12%/floor. Best floor persists on game.towerBestFloor.

ModifierEffect
Basic Attacks OnlySkills disabled â€” only D basic attack fires
Double TimeEnemies move 2Ã— speed
Vampiric FoesEnemies heal 20% of damage they deal to you
Swarm+50% enemies per wave
Glass HeroYou deal +50% and take +50% damage
Burning FloorHeat pulses damage you if stationary
Dim LightEnemies gain +25% HP

HUD overlay (#tower-hud) shows floor / modifier / cleared / best. Floor clear heals 15% HP + 20% MP. Modifiers rotate every 3 floors.

Entry point. Town â†’ rightmost bazaar portal (â†‘) â†’ Tower of Ascension.

v0.12.2 Polish Â· art Â· difficulty

Follow-up to v0.12.1. Player sprite now matches the enemies' DQ-style cel-shading, Tower of Ascension becomes a cramped subway carriage with tougher mobs, monster names go full plush-toy, and damage numbers get a chunky gold-foil upgrade.

Art
Player cel-shaded
Thicker outline Â· hard shadow Â· slightly taller
Map
Carriage of Ascension
Subway train interior Â· 760 wide
Mobs
Cuter names
Snailkin Â· Goober Â· Mushie Â· Hornsy Â· â€¦
Combat
Mob DEF + HP bump
~1.5Ã— HP Â· flat DEF Â· elite/boss DEF scaling
Feel
Damage font
Impact Â· gradient fill Â· sparkles Â· highlight

âœ¦Player cel-shading + stroke upgrade

The procedural chibi renderer now matches the enemies' Dragon Quest look. The chibi transform gets a subtle vertical stretch (scale 1.19, 1.06 up from 0.97) so the player reads as slightly taller without breaking proportions.

  • Torso silhouette. Factored out into a reusable torsoPath() closure. First pass strokes the path at 3 px for the outer contour, second pass fills the body color, third pass clips to the silhouette and drops a hard diagonal shadow wedge in cc.bodyShd, fourth pass lays a 1 px inner stroke for the outline-over-fill look.
  • Head. Outer ring goes from hr + 1 to hr + 2; the old crescent shadow is replaced with a hard polygon clipped to the head circle so the light/dark boundary reads as one crisp line.
  • Legs + boots. Each leg and boot now has its own dark outline rect before the fill so the bottom half has the same confident contour as the top.

âœ¦Carriage of Ascension (tower rework)

The tower becomes a cramped subway carriage â€” feels like an endless train ride where each stop is a fresh wave of fares refusing to let you off. World width drops 1500 â†’ 760; two drop-through benches and one overhead handrail replace the old platform layout.

  • Custom backdrop. New drawCarriageInterior() paints a full train car interior: parallax tunnel streaks outside the windows, overhead fluorescent strip with glow, windowed wall with individual panes + glowing tunnel streaks behind the glass, rivet rows, floor grooves + yellow safety line, two sliding side doors with EXIT / NEXT signs, and hanging handholds that bob with the train.
  • Tier pools tougher. Early carriage floors skip snails/slimes entirely â€” you fight slime/mushroom on floor 1 and horny variants by floor 4. Later tiers lean into wraiths, scorpions, and mummies.
  • HP/ATK scaling harder. HP +28%/floor (was +18%), ATK +18%/floor (was +12%). Wave cap at 8 mobs so the small carriage doesn't turn into a soup.
  • Rename. Portal, toasts, and HUD updated: "ðŸš‡ Carriage of Ascension", floors renamed Stop 1, Stop 2 â€¦.

Cuter monster names

TypeOldNew
snailShellsnipSnailkin
slimeGumbudGoober
mushroomCaplingMushie
hornyHexcapHornsy
orangeSunbackSunny
stumpGrumpwoodStumpling
zombieRotshadeGrimmy
scorpionDune ScuttlerPincho
mummyDuneborn MummyBandie
skeletonBone ArcherBonesy
wraithPale WraithGhostie
kingGelmonarchKing Blobby
mushmomQueen FungaraMama Capling

Aetherion and Gravitos keep their menacing titles â€” they're super and hyper bosses, not plush toys.

Harder to kill

  • HP bumped ~1.5Ã— on top of v0.10.4's 1.7Ã— pass. Snailkin 38 â†’ 60 Â· Goober 62 â†’ 95 Â· Mushie 105 â†’ 165 Â· Hornsy 190 â†’ 290 Â· Sunny 260 â†’ 395 Â· Stumpling 350 â†’ 530 Â· Grimmy 450 â†’ 680 Â· Pincho 310 â†’ 470 Â· Bandie 560 â†’ 840 Â· Bonesy 380 â†’ 570 Â· Ghostie 480 â†’ 720. Bosses bumped ~50%: King Blobby 2000 â†’ 3000, Mama Capling 4200 â†’ 6300, Aetherion 28000 â†’ 42000, Gravitos 45000 â†’ 68000.
  • Flat DEF per mob. Each monster type now has a def field (1 on Snailkin up to 11 on Bandie). hitMonster subtracts def from final damage after combo/tower multipliers; result is floored to 1 min so mobs never go immune.
  • Elite / mini-boss / boss DEF scaling. defVal *= 1.4 on elites, Ã— 1.8 on mini-bosses, Ã— 2.2 on bosses. Big HP pools are now complemented by meaningful damage floor-loss â€” early-game skill spam won't chew through bosses any more.
  • Ballista exemption. siegeVolley and any skill tagged breakDef bypass DEF entirely, preserving the fantasy that ballista pierces armor.

âœ¦Eye-catching damage font

Big rewrite of drawDamageNumbers() for a chunkier, more arcade-y feel.

  • Font. Swapped from Trebuchet MS to Impact, "Arial Black", "Trebuchet MS" with weight 900 â€” the letterforms themselves are thicker and more aggressive.
  • Drop shadow. Every number now paints a darker shadow copy at +2, +3 px (alpha 55%) before the main pass, making text pop off busy backgrounds.
  • Heavy outline. 4.5 px (crit 6 px) round-joined black stroke. Crits add a 2 px gold-foil inner stroke over the black.
  • Gradient fill. Crits render with a top-to-bottom gradient (#fff4b8 â†’ #ffd84a â†’ #ff8a1f) for the gold-foil look; non-crit big hits use a color mixed against white for a similar top-bright â†’ base feel.
  • Specular band. Crits/big hits add a clipped top highlight â€” horizontal band reserved in the upper 38% of the glyph area, filled white at 70% alpha. Reads as a sheen across the top of the digits.
  • Crit sparkles. 4 tiny + sparkles rotate around the number during the first 8 frames of its life, fading out with age.
  • Bigger overshoot. Pop-in eases to 1.4Ã— (crits 2.6Ã—) before settling; idle bob keeps numbers gently breathing on screen.

v0.13.0 Zodiac Â· endgame Â· variation

Huge endgame expansion. 12 horoscope bosses, each with a unique arena, artistic renderer, and three-phase mechanics. Tower carriage gets a streaming spawn model â€” kill faster, spawn faster. Maps now have per-load variation. Mob names leaned into short & simple.

Endgame
12 Zodiac Bosses
Aries â†’ Pisces Â· level 15-37
Endgame
Zodiac Sanctum hub
12 portals Â· marble hall
Carriage
Streaming spawns
Kill â†’ spawn in 220ms floor
Variation
Per-load map shuffle
Platform jitter Â· surprise plats Â· Â±25% mob count
Mobs
Simpler names
Slippy Â· Jelly Â· Shroom Â· Horncap Â· â€¦

âœ¦12 Horoscope Bosses

A full endgame arc: one boss per zodiac sign, each with its own map, element, attack pattern, and visual design. Unlock from level 15; difficulty ramps from Aries (tier 1) to Pisces (tier 12). Config lives in ZODIAC_SIGNS; drawing in ZODIAC_DRAW; AI in ZODIAC_AI.

SignNameElementSignature mechanic
â™ˆ AriesAriel the Ember RamFireCharge across arena leaving a fire trail Â· expanding flame rings
â™‰ TaurusTaur the Granite BullEarthStomp â†’ 6 ground shockwaves Â· headbutt charge
â™Š GeminiGem & Mini the TwinstarAirSplits into two at phase 2 Â· dual star-beams
â™‹ CancerCancer the TidecallerWaterRising homing bubbles Â· pincer snap Â· stalked eyes
â™Œ LeoRegulus the SunmaneFireRoar stuns player (600ms) Â· fire comets from sky
â™ VirgoVirga the SeraphLightBanishes your projectiles Â· heals 5% Â· light pillars
â™Ž LibraLibra the ScalelordBalanceTwo scale orbs â€” half damage while either alive Â· balance beams
â™ ScorpioScorpio the VenomlordPoisonVenom shots apply 3s poison DOT Â· burrows to flank
â™ SagittariusSagitta the StarchaserArrowArrow rain (8-16) Â· piercing star-beam
â™‘ CapricornCaprikor the PeaklordIceIce breath cone Â· freezes Â· slippery floor
â™’ AquariusAqua the TideswornWaterGround-sweeping waves Â· homing droplets
â™“ PiscesPisces the Twin CurrentWaterOrbits player Â· 14-spoke whirlpool Â· pull vortex

Art direction

  • Unified boss canvas. Shared _drawZodiacHalo paints a rotating constellation ring + a pulsing sigil glyph above every zodiac boss. _zBodyPath factors stroke / fill / cel-shadow into one call so every zodiac renders with the same DQ-style contour.
  • Per-sign silhouettes. Each boss has a distinct shape â€” Aries' woolly body with spiral ram horns rendered procedurally; Cancer's stalked eyes and hinged pincers; Virgo's outstretched wings with individual feather lines; Libra's floating scale with tilting pans and chain links; Pisces orbiting twin fish with a central whirlpool.
  • Phase crackle. Phase transitions spawn a 40-frame electric particle burst in the boss's aura color.

Arena design

Each zodiac arena is a 1800-wide map at zod_<id> with the sign's element dictating ambient decor via drawZodiacArena:

  • Giant faded glyph painted behind the whole scene in the sign's color.
  • Constellation dots â€” 14 stars twinkle at positions seeded from the sign's order, with connecting lines linking the first 6 into the sign's shape.
  • Element decor. Fire = lava floor line + rising embers; Water = rippling bottom waves + floating bubbles; Earth = rock silhouettes with parallax; Air = wind streaks; Ice = frost crystals + snowfall; Poison = toxic sludge line + bubble spores; Light = divine god-ray shafts; Arrow = silhouettes of arrows stuck in the ground.

Zodiac Sanctum hub

New zodiacHall map accessible from the town's leftmost portal (â­ Zodiac Sanctum). A 2200-wide purple marble hall with 12 portals â€” one per sign â€” arranged left-to-right by zodiac order. A central rotating wheel shows all 12 glyphs. Starfield background, 14 decorative pillars between portals.

Mechanics / stats

  • HP scales from 16,500 (Aries) to 66,000 (Pisces). ATK from 78 to 166. DEF from 16 to 38.
  • 3 phases at 66% / 33% HP with shortened attack cycles and stronger mechanics each tier.
  • Libra's _libraShield halves all incoming damage while either scale orb survives â€” must be burned down first.
  • New projectile flags plumbed through the engine: homing: true (enemy-owned projectiles gently steer toward the player), freeze: <ms>, poison: <ms>. Player update loop now ticks a poison DOT every 45 frames with its own damage number.
  • Win rewards use the normal boss drop table (large Lumens + epic/legendary roll).

âœ¦Carriage streaming spawns

Old model: _towerBeginFloor dumped all wave mobs on the floor at once and waited for a full clear. New model splits the wave into spawn queue + live-count cap:

  • Each floor has a total mob budget (waveTotal) but only maxAlive live at once (2-5 depending on floor).
  • When a mob dies, the next spawn is queued with a timer that shrinks by 60 ms per kill, from a 1600 ms base down to a 220 ms floor â€” kill streaks make the carriage feel relentless.
  • Spawned mobs burst in with a gold/white particle puff (_towerSpawnOne).
  • Wave cap raised to 14 mobs per floor so the streaming model has runway.

Map variation pass

New _variedMapData(id) wraps MAPS[id] on every loadMap:

  • Y-jitter (Â±10 px) + width-jitter (Â±10 px) on every non-ground platform so jumps and sight-lines feel slightly different each run.
  • 50% chance surprise platform at a random mid-map position (width/y also randomized) on big maps.
  • Mob count jitter (Â±25%) on non-boss spawns.
  • 8% rare wanderer â€” one mismatched mob type injects itself into a non-boss map (slime, mushroom, horny, stump, scorpion, or wraith) for flavour.
  • Town, boss arenas, and tower skip the shuffle (fixedLayout flag). Zodiac arenas opt in via fixedLayout: true.

Simpler monster names

TypeOldNew
snailSnailkinSlippy
slimeGooberJelly
mushroomMushieShroom
hornyHornsyHorncap
orangeSunnySunbun
stumpStumplingStumpy
zombieGrimmyRotter
scorpionPinchoPincer
mummyBandieWrappy
skeletonBonesyBones
wraithGhostieSpook
kingKing BlobbyJelly King
mushmomMama CaplingMomma Shroom

v0.13.1 Unified art pass

NPCs, town buildings, platforms, chests, and portals all now share the same 2-shade DQ cel-shading as the character and mobs. One visual language across the whole screen.

NPCs
DQ cel-head + torso
Thicker 2 px outline Â· hard shadow wedge
Town
Flat 2-tone buildings
No gradients Â· hard vertical cel seam
Platforms
Ground + wood cel
Upper lit / lower shadow + black seam
Chests
Rounded lid cel
Clipped polygon shadow Â· keyhole + gem
Portals
Stone frame + swirl
Frame cel Â· flat-tone magic swirl Â· rivets

NPCs

  • Chibi scale bumped to match the player (1.19, 1.06) â€” NPCs were still on the old 0.97 height so they looked shorter than the hero.
  • Head â€” outline ring thickened (hr + 1 â†’ hr + 2), crescent shadow replaced with a hard polygon clipped to the head circle (same technique as the player / mobs).
  • Torso â€” 2 px stepped outline on every seam (was 1 px). Hard diagonal cel shadow wedge clipped to the body rect so the lit/shadow boundary is a single crisp line rather than a soft band.
  • Legs + boots â€” dedicated outline halo rects before each fill, matching the player's new leg treatment.

Town buildings

drawTownDecor rewritten around the DQ cel rules:

  • Removed the 3-tone soft blend (deepShd â†’ shd â†’ mid â†’ wall â†’ lit). Buildings now use just two flats â€” lit wall + shadow wall â€” separated by a thin #1a0818 vertical seam at 68% of the width.
  • Thick 2 px outline around the whole silhouette.
  • Window frames get an outline-then-fill pass; windows that sit on the shadow side paint their lit fill, then overlay a dark flat over the right half so they match the building's cel partition.
  • Door re-designed with outline, lit base, right-half shadow flat, thin seam, and a brass handle.
  • Rooftop parapet + shop plaque follow the same 2-tone rule.

Platforms

  • Ground (grass + dirt). Old: 3-4 layered dirt tones + mid-tone bands + grass gradient. New: grass is two flat colors (#6cb847 lit, darker step below), dirt is two flat colors (#8b6f47 top, #5a4027 lower), all separated by crisp 1 px black seams. Tufts + flowers simplified to flat 2-tone.
  • Wood platforms. Single lit tone on top half, single shadow tone on bottom half, horizontal black seam in the middle. Plank separators and nail outlines pure #1a0818. No gradient, no grain squiggles.
  • Result: jump pads read cleanly at a glance, consistent with the character/mob silhouettes jumping over them.

Chests

  • Body outline (2 px halo rect) + 2 flat fills (lit/shadow) with a vertical cel seam at 55% width.
  • Lid is a rounded cap with its OWN outline pass + clipped shadow polygon (so the upper curve of the lid shows the wedge, not a gradient).
  • Lock plate is now a true plate â€” outline + lit face + darker right half + keyhole. Small gem sparkle above the lock breathes with a sine pulse.
  • Opened state: dark interior rect + flipped lid bar.
  • All three tiers (wood / silver / gold) share the structure; only the lit/shadow/trim/gem colors change.

Portals

Old portal was three animated rings + a soft alpha blob. New portal is a real stone archway:

  • Frame. Thick black outer rim, lit stone fill, hard diagonal shadow wedge clipped to the ellipse.
  • Swirl. Two solid purple flats (outer + inner) with a crisp black seam between them. Three rotating inner ellipses in escalating darkness act as the "magic swirl" â€” all flat, no gradient.
  • Rivets. 4 cardinal studs (outlined flat circle + lighter centre).
  • Floating white sparkles orbit inside as before.

v0.13.2 Potions Â· art polish

Quick-use potion hotkeys moved to PgUp / PgDn with auto-buy fallback when inventory is empty. Building and portal outlines dropped for a softer look; portals are translucent; town plaza floor cel-shaded; layered aesthetic background replaces the flat mountain silhouette.

Combat
PgUp / PgDn potions
Auto-buy with gold if empty
Art
No building outlines
Pure 2-tone silhouettes
Art
Translucent portals
Additive halo Â· alpha swirl
Art
Cel-shaded floor
3-band dirt + plaza walkway
Art
Aesthetic background
Sun / moon Â· 2 mountain layers Â· pine treeline Â· birds

Potion hotkeys + gold fallback

  • PgUp consumes a red HP potion. PgDn consumes a blue MP potion. Old R / T aliases kept so muscle memory still works.
  • If the player has no potion of the requested type, useQuickPotion now falls back to the smallest same-type potion the player can afford and deducts Lumens directly. Shows an auto-buy toast with the price so the spend is visible.
  • If the player can't afford even the cheapest potion, a muted hit sfx + "Need X Lumens" toast fires â€” no accidental zero-gold death.
  • README + in-game help panel updated to show the new bindings.

Building outlines removed

The thick #1a0818 outline rects around every town high-rise are gone. Buildings now read as pure 2-tone silhouettes â€” lit wall + shadow side with a clean tonal break at 68% width. Windows drop their outline; their only structure is the 2-tone fill + a 1 px internal divider (in a shadow tone, not pure black). Doors and shop plaques same treatment.

Translucent portals

drawPortals rewritten to drop all hard outlines and lean into additive glow + alpha layers:

  • Outer additive halo (radial gradient, 70Ã—80 ellipse) for ambient glow.
  • Translucent stone frame (globalAlpha 0.55) with a destination-out cutout for the portal interior â€” no hard edge between frame and void.
  • Two translucent purple swirl layers (60% and 55% alpha) with no black seam between them.
  • Three rotating additive-blend interior arcs at ~38% alpha â€” reads as magical motion rather than solid shapes.
  • Rivets at cardinal positions drop to 55% alpha flat ovals.
  • Sparkles use globalCompositeOperation: lighter so they bloom against the translucent interior.

Cel-shaded floor

  • Town plaza walkway â€” old two-rect flat design replaced with a 4-band cel: lit top stripe (#d6cfbe) â†’ crisp seam â†’ shadow band â†’ darker curb strip. Cobble tone-marks dash across every 44 px for visual rhythm.
  • Ground platform dirt â€” was 2-band, now 3-band: sunlit ochre top (#a08050, 6 px) â†’ seam â†’ midtone (#7a5a38, 14 px) â†’ seam â†’ deep soil (#4a3018, rest). Adds pebble motes in the lit band and root tufts in the shadow band so the floor reads as scenery rather than a coloured rectangle.

Aesthetic layered background

The old single-mountain + round cloud fallback is gone. New drawBackground fallback renders:

  • Celestial disc. Sun on bright maps, moon on dark maps (auto-detected via _hexLuma of the sky color). Soft radial halo via additive blend. Moon gets a crescent shadow circle; sun gets a warm amber inner core.
  • Starfield. On dark maps, 50 twinkling stars drift with subtle camera parallax and phase-offset shimmer.
  • Two mountain ranges. Far range (4% parallax, low-contrast tint) + mid range (8% parallax, blue-gray tint). Mid range now gets procedural snow caps â€” small triangular fills on every local peak.
  • Pine treeline. 20 two-tier pine silhouettes as foreground parallax (18% shift), tone-matched to the sky's lowest color.
  • Clouds reshaped. Each cloud is now a 4-lobe puff with a dedicated underside shadow layer (a 2-tone cloud instead of flat white circles).
  • Birds. On bright maps, 4 M-shape silhouetted birds drift with flapping wings at 12% parallax + slow scroll.

v0.14.0 QoL Â· Amnesiac Â· mobile Â· radar

Big quality-of-life + mobile release. Town regenerates HP/MP instantly, potions get a 3 s shared cooldown with gold-fallback buying, "Master Ren" becomes The Amnesiac with a dramatic reset flow, weapons change visually with what's equipped and swing more smoothly, the level-up screen gains a live radar chart, and the whole game is now fully playable on touch screens.

QoL
Town instant regen
HP + MP refill every frame
QoL
Potion 3 s cooldown
Separate HP / MP tracks
NPC
The Amnesiac
Dramatic 4-stage reset dialog
Combat
Equipped weapon art
Swing pulls item vis palette
UI
Radar level-up
Live 6-axis chart of your build
Platform
Mobile playable
D-pad + skills + potions on-screen

Town instant regen

While on the town map (game.mapData.isTown), both HP and MP regenerate every single frame for ~4% of max per tick. Makes the town feel like a proper safe haven â€” step out of the dungeon, breathe, and you're instantly topped off for the next run. Combat maps keep the old gradual regen rates.

Potion cooldown (3 s)

  • New POTION_CD_MS = 3000 constant with separate HP / MP tracks (player._potionCdHp, player._potionCdMp) so tanking and mana-burn tick independently.
  • useQuickPotion gates on _potionReady at the top â€” tap on cooldown shows a toast with seconds remaining + muted hit sfx.
  • Cooldown stamps after both successful inventory use AND gold-fallback purchase, so exploits aren't a thing.

âœ¦The Amnesiac (formerly Master Ren)

The skills-trainer NPC in the town bazaar has been re-characterised. The ominous robed figure who lost his name â€” and now offers to unmake yours.

  • 4-stage dramatic dialog (game._amnesiacStage). Each stage has its own text block and branching options:
    1. Ominous greeting + normal stat/LP shortcuts.
    2. Backstory â€” "the World drank me empty."
    3. The offer â€” reset everything with stark warning.
    4. Final verbal confirmation â€” "Say: I wish to forget."
  • Reset runs through _amnesiacReset() â€” no confirm() popups since the dialog chain IS the confirmation. Adds a fade-to-black overlay ("the door closes behind youâ€¦") + flash(0.8) + addShake(24) + audio.play('death') before the reload.
  • The old corner "â˜  Reset" HUD button is hidden â€” reset is now a narrative beat, not a UI pill.
  • NPC palette & role config updated: amnesiac role gets a dark purple robe + silver hair + wizard hat to match the tone.
  • Dialog CSS gains white-space: pre-line so paragraph breaks read properly.

âœ¦Smoother weapon swing + equipped-weapon art

Both drawCuteSwingingBlade and drawCuteWeapon rewritten around a palette-driven piece renderer.

  • New _weaponPalette() reads the equipped weapon's vis block (every gear entry in ITEM_POOL already has one) and returns a blade/hi/shd/guard/gold/glow bundle. Falls back to CUTE_PAL for unequipped characters.
  • Swing arc now uses easeOutBack across a wider ~1.05Ï€ arc â€” crisper windup, visible overshoot at the peak.
  • Three trail ghost frames render at offset angles with globalCompositeOperation: 'lighter', producing a proper motion-blur feel.
  • Slash crescent is thicker (7 px) with a bright 3 px inner line, tinted by the weapon's glow color (legendary blades draw their own hue).
  • Weapon itself is a larger, outlined silhouette. Warriors get a new pointed tip via path, outlined crossguard, grip, and pommel with a rarity-colored trim dot (blue/purple/gold). Rogues get the same treatment at shorter proportions. Mages get a full staff with orb + additive glow halo. Archers get a bow with optional drawn arrow (fires during attack frames).
  • Every piece respects vis.glow â€” equipping the Dawnshard Blade gives you a mint-green aura, the Enchanted Katana a violet pulse, etc.

âœ¦Radar chart level-up

The U / LP spending screen now shows a live 6-axis radar graph of your build alongside the LP lanes.

  • Canvas radar at 280Ã—280 â€” one vertex per lane (HP, MP, ATK, DEF, Crit, Speed).
  • 4 concentric rings for visual tick marks + 6 axis spokes.
  • Outer amber polygon = lane cap. Inner filled polygon = current investment (ratio of spent/cap). Each vertex gets an outlined amber dot.
  • Labels rendered outside each vertex (e.g. "HP 12/40") with live colour â€” green when maxed, amber while investing.
  • Cards on the right have inline progress bars + "spent / cap" counters â€” everything redraws the moment a point is spent, so the build shape visibly morphs as you invest.

ðŸ•¹Mobile on-screen controls

The game is now fully playable on touch screens. New #mobile-ctrl overlay auto-shows on coarse-pointer devices (or screens under 900 px wide).

  • D-pad (bottom-left): â—€ â–¶ â–² â–¼ buttons for left/right movement, portal-enter (up), drop-through-platform (down).
  • Jump button (large, bottom-right, amber) â€” 76 px circular press target.
  • Basic attack (bottom-right, crimson) â€” the D slot.
  • Skill grid (4Ã—2): S, A, E, W, Q, C, X skills + interact key, all labeled by slot letter so the UI matches the on-screen skill bar.
  • Top-right stack: HP potion (â™¥), MP potion (â—†), Level-up, Inventory.
  • All buttons dispatch real KeyboardEvents via _mkeyDispatch so the existing keyboard logic handles everything â€” no divergent code paths. pointerdown / pointerup (+ cancel / leave) cover touch and mouse.
  • Audio auto-initializes on first button tap (satisfies mobile autoplay policy).
  • Smart key mapping â€” slot letters on the labels (S, A, Eâ€¦) dispatch the actual keyboard keys (x, s, câ€¦) per KEY_TO_SLOT.
  • Tap âœ• in the top-right to hide the overlay on hybrid devices (reappears on reload or class-toggle).
  • touch-action: manipulation + pointerevents-only children disable page scrolling / zoom over button regions.
  • Narrow-screen media query (< 500 px) shrinks button sizes further for small phones.

v0.14.1 Dock polish Â· skills reference

Block/Parry is now a permanent slot in the skill dock (desktop + mobile), and pressing I opens a full Aâ€“V keybind reference modal that reads your current class + job + master and shows each skill's MP cost, cooldown, and description.

Dock
A slot â†’ Block/Parry
Shows live CD + parry flash
UI
Skills Reference (I)
Live Aâ€“V binding grid
Mobile
Shield + Dash + ðŸ“– buttons
Defense row + reference shortcut

Block / Parry in the skill dock

The A keyboard action (Block / Parry) is now a permanent leading slot in renderSkillBar. Shows live state:

  • Icon â€” ðŸ›¡ normally, âš¡ the instant a parry fires (while player.parryWindow is open).
  • Label â€” "Block" when ready; countdown seconds while on cooldown.
  • Ready-glow when blockCD === 0.
  • Vertical fill drains as the 2 s cooldown ticks down (matches the normal skill CD overlay).
  • Blue-tinted border + gradient background so it reads as defense rather than a skill cast.
  • Tooltip: "hold to mitigate 70% damage; perfect timing triggers a parry (400 ms window)."

Mobile dock additions

  • New defense row above the skill grid with two extra buttons:
    • ðŸ›¡ Block/Parry â€” dispatches A, blue-accented.
    • ðŸ’¨ Dodge â€” dispatches Shift, green-accented.
  • New ðŸ“– Skills Reference icon in the top-right menu stack alongside HP/MP potions + Level-Up + Inventory.

âœ¦Skills Reference modal (I)

New openSkillsReference() function â€” press I to open. Lists every Aâ€“V keyboard binding in a two-column grid:

  • Skill rows (C / D / F / G / S / V) read the most-specific skill for that slot via skillsForClass(cls, job, master) + specificity scoring. Shows the icon, name, MP cost, cooldown, and full description. Greyed-out placeholder when a slot hasn't been unlocked yet (e.g. "Unlocks at Master (Lv 20)" for X).
  • Defense row (A) shows Block/Parry mechanics.
  • Menu rows (B inventory, I this panel, K/U level up, M mute, N codex) each list what they open.
  • Item rows (R = HP alias, T = MP alias) note the PgUp/PgDn mains.
  • Unbound rows (H, J, L, O, P, Q) are rendered at low opacity for completeness.
  • Each row is colour-coded by category â€” amber for skills, blue for defense, pink for items, cool-blue for menus, grey for unbound.
  • Scrollable (max-height 440 px) so everything fits on shorter screens.

Help panel (?) also updated to show the new I binding.

v0.14.2 Mobile fullscreen

The game now locks to the mobile viewport â€” no more scrolling or pinch-zoom. The 960Ã—560 canvas scales to fit any screen while preserving aspect ratio, and a rotate-to-landscape nag helps portrait users get the best fit.

Viewport + scroll lock

  • Added the missing <meta name="viewport"> with width=device-width, initial-scale=1, maximum-scale=1, user-scalable=no, viewport-fit=cover.
  • Added apple-mobile-web-app-capable, mobile-web-app-capable, theme-color, and apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style=black-translucent for a near-native feel when installed to Home Screen.
  • html, body pinned to 100dvh (dynamic viewport) so iOS Safari's retractable URL bar doesn't leave a dead strip.
  • body becomes position: fixed; inset: 0; overflow: hidden; overscroll-behavior: none; touch-action: manipulation â€” blocks the bounce-scroll.
  • Safe-area insets respected via env(safe-area-inset-*) so the iPhone notch / home indicator don't eat controls.
  • Global touchmove handler calls preventDefault() unless the target is inside a scrollable element (shop lists / inventory modals etc.) â€” stops rubber-banding without breaking menus.
  • gesturestart blocked to suppress iOS pinch-zoom.

Aspect-ratio fit

  • New _fitGameToViewport() runs on load, resize, and orientation change. Computes scale = min(availW / 960, availH / 560) and writes it to --game-scale.
  • .game-wrapper uses transform: scale(var(--game-scale, 1)) with transform-origin: center â€” every absolutely-positioned child (mobile controls, minimap, tower HUD, modals) scales with the wrapper so hit-testing stays pixel-accurate.
  • Border / radius / shadow removed on coarse-pointer or small screens â€” edge-to-edge immersive look on phones.

Rotate-to-landscape nag

  • On touch devices in portrait orientation < 768 px wide, a fullscreen overlay prompts the user to rotate with an animated ðŸ“± icon.
  • Dismiss button ("Play in portrait anyway") sets body.dataset.nagDismissed = '1' so it doesn't flicker back on orientation changes.
  • Overlay auto-hides the moment the device goes landscape.

v0.14.3 Skill rework Â· shadow clones Â· 9-reset

Warrior Power Strike now sweeps 3 vertical lanes, every rogue skill hits 2â€“6 lines, Mirror Shadow summons three black silhouette clones that attack for 4 s, and 9 is a global reset shortcut.

Warrior
Power Strike 3-lane
High / mid / low sweep
Rogue
Multi-line rework
3â€“6 hits across the kit
Master
Mirror Shadow Ã—3
Autonomous silhouette clones
QoL
Press 9 = Reset
Double-confirm, instant

Warrior â€” Power Strike (S) 3-lane cleave

  • Was a 2-hit combo. Now a 3-strike vertical sweep â€” high (-36 px), mid (0), low (+28). Each strike has Â±28 px extended vertical reach so airborne and grounded foes both get hit.
  • Damage curve 1.5Ã— / 1.8Ã— / 2.2Ã— ATK â€” the low finisher stuns 0.5 s.
  • New performMelee options tall and yShift let future skills extend their hitbox vertically or shift it up/down without rewriting the helper.
  • Slash crescent paints at each lane's height so the animation reads as three distinct swipes.

Rogue â€” every skill hits 2â€“6 lines

  • Backstab (S) â€” blink + 3-stab combo: quick / quick / heavy crit finisher. No target â†’ fallback triple-thrust across high / mid / low lanes.
  • Throw Dagger (A) â€” 3-dagger fan became 5 daggers stacked on 5 vertical lanes (caps at 6 with multishot bonuses). 30Â° downward base so they stream like a waterfall.
  • Smoke Dash (E) â€” dash corridor widened from Â±20 px to Â±60 px vertical, hitting 3 effective lines. Mid-lane hits get +0.5Ã— damage. Still drops the 3-tick smoke cloud.
  • Flurry (W) â€” already 6 strikes in the V-pattern; description updated to reflect.

âœ¦Mirror Shadow (Shadowlord master)

Complete rewrite. Instead of a single clone that mimics your moves, you now summon three black silhouette clones that act autonomously for 4 seconds.

  • Each clone spawns at a triangle point around the player (fan angles Â±2.2 rad), bobs with a sinusoidal orbit, and auto-targets the nearest enemy within 240 px.
  • Each swings every 400 ms (staggered phase offsets so you hear rapid-fire strikes), dealing ~140% ATK + 6 per hit with rollable crits.
  • Up to 3 enemies hit per swing per clone â€” ceiling at 9 hits per 400 ms cycle, theoretical ~70 hits over 4 seconds.
  • Visuals: pure-black #0a0010 chibi silhouette with a violet outline and purple under-glow that breathes with each swing. Violet bar eyes. Drops a purple-and-black crescent slash on each strike.
  • New drawShadowClones() renders them behind the player in the main pass. Fades over the last 400 ms of life.
  • Old single-clone mimic system retired â€” the action-queue code path at cast is now a no-op; clones don't copy skills, they just attack relentlessly.
  • Cast burst: 48-particle violet ring + 3 dark spawn puffs + crit sfx + 60 ms hit-stop.

Press 9 â†’ reset save

  • Global hotkey 9 fires resetSave() â€” same double-confirm browser prompt as the Amnesiac's final stage.
  • Doesn't fire while paused (can't accidentally reset during a modal).
  • Added to the help panel and Skills Reference modal.
  • Existing Amnesiac dialog flow still works â€” the key is just a faster escape hatch for dev/testing.

v0.14.4 Potion CD fix Â· mobile deck

Fixes a 50-second potion cooldown that should have been 3 seconds, and extends the mobile controls beyond the game canvas so the D-pad / skills never cover gameplay.

Potion cooldown â€” real 3 seconds

  • Bug: POTION_CD_MS = 3000 was being added to game.time, which counts frames (+1 per frame at 60 fps), not milliseconds. Effective cooldown was ~50 s.
  • Fix: renamed to POTION_CD_FRAMES = 180 (= 3 s Ã— 60 fps). Cooldown toast now divides by 60 (frames â†’ seconds) instead of 1000.
  • Affects both HP (PgUp) and MP (PgDn) â€” each tracks its own CD so they don't compete.

âœ¦Mobile control deck â€” outside the game

The D-pad and action buttons are now in a dedicated deck element rendered outside .game-wrapper, so they no longer overlap the canvas. The tiny HP/MP/menu cluster stays inside the game area as a compact top-right overlay.

  • Portrait orientation: body uses flex-direction: column â€” game canvas stretches across the top of the screen, control deck sits below it in its own bar.
  • Landscape orientation: body switches to flex-direction: row â€” canvas on the left (as large as height allows), vertical 260 px control strip on the right. D-pad above, actions below.
  • Canvas fills more of the screen: _fitGameToViewport now measures the deck's live width/height and subtracts it from the available viewport before scaling. The wrapper's transform-origin moved from center center to center top so the upward-growing wrapper snaps to the top of its flex cell.
  • Slight stretch: touch devices get an extra 4% scale bump on top of the aspect-preserving minimum, capped so it never overflows the available box.
  • Re-fit fires 4 times on orientation change (0 / 120 / 350 / 800 ms) to catch Android's delayed layout settle.
  • The control deck has its own touchmove preventDefault so swipes on the deck don't rubber-band the page.
  • hide-mobile-ctrl body class now hides the deck too, for hybrid laptops that prefer the keyboard.

v0.15.0 Crusader rework Â· perf pass Â· GBA controller

Divine Aegis becomes 5 hovering holy orbs with âˆ’50% damage taken for 8 s. Big performance pass removes 734 lines of dead code, caches DOM refs + gradients, diff-updates the skill bar, and frustum-culls offscreen projectiles. Mobile controls redesigned into a plastic GBA-style handheld.

Master
Divine Aegis rework
5 orbs + Â½ dmg taken Â· 8s
Perf
DOM ref caching
updateUI ~19Ã— fewer lookups
Perf
Skill bar diff
Built once, patched in place
Perf
Frustum cull
Offscreen projectiles skip paint
Perf
-734 lines dead code
Legacy renderers purged
Mobile
GBA handheld look
Cross D-pad Â· A-button Â· shoulder pills

âœ¦Crusader â€” Divine Aegis rework

Was: Heal 60% + short damage barrier. Now: heal + 5 hovering holy orbs that pendulum around the player, striking enemies they pass through. While active, the player takes half damage from all sources (melee contact AND enemy projectiles) for 8 seconds.

  • Orbs arranged at 5 fan angles, pendulum radius 30â€“110 px via sinusoidal phase, rotate around the player at ~60Â°/sec.
  • Each orb checks for monsters within 22 px, deals ~110% ATK + 6 with a 350 ms per-orb cooldown â€” prevents chain-stun but lets them reliably strike in combat.
  • Damage reduction hook added at both melee-contact and projectile-hit sites (if (player._aegis) dmg = Math.max(1, Math.floor(dmg * 0.5))).
  • New drawAegisOrbs() renders with additive blend â€” outer amber halo, warm yellow core, white-hot center, fades in last 600 ms.
  • Ambient sparkle trail per orb; consecration cast burst still fires on activation.

âœ¦Performance pass

  • updateUI DOM cache â€” new _uiGetRefs caches every getElementById on first call; _uiSetText / _uiSetStyle write only when the value actually changes. Cuts ~19 lookups/frame to 0, eliminates redundant style writes that triggered repaints.
  • renderSkillBar rebuilt once â€” was bar.innerHTML = '' + 9 createElement per frame. Now _sbBuild() runs once per class/job/master change, _sbSlots[] caches the DOM refs, renderSkillBar() patches icon/MP/CD/ready state in place with per-slot dirty flags. Major churn removal.
  • Projectile frustum cull â€” drawProjectiles early-outs on projectiles outside the viewport (+60 px buffer). Saves draws during heavy arrow/rain skills.
  • Sky gradient cached â€” createLinearGradient was rebuilt every frame in drawBackground. Now cached via _skyGradCache with a colour-key invalidation; only rebuilds when the map's sky changes.
  • Dead code purged â€” 734 lines of unreachable legacy renderers removed:
    • 440 lines from drawTownDecor (old medieval town art, after return;).
    • 294 lines from drawPlayer (elaborate procedural character kept "for reference" behind if (false) { ... }).
  • File shrunk from ~19,150 â†’ ~18,615 lines. Expected lag reduction on lower-end devices: ~30â€“40% on combat-heavy frames.

ðŸ•¹GBA-style mobile controller

The flat-rectangle button deck becomes a proper plastic handheld. Purple/indigo shell with inset shadows, glossy button highlights, a power-LED indicator, and a "LEVELX" brand plate.

  • D-pad â€” single chunky cross (4 buttons inside a circular recessed plate), not 4 separate pills. Glossy purple plastic, rounded corner-matched directional badges.
  • Face buttons â€” 3Ã—3 diamond: 
    [W] [Jump]  [C]
[A] [Basic] [S]
[X] [E]     [Q]
    Basic attack is the red "A button" at the center (54 px circle, red gradient). Jump is the orange "B button" at the top. 7 skill slots ring around them in purple circles.
  • Shoulder pills â€” Block (ðŸ›¡, blue) and Dodge (ðŸ’¨, green) are long pill-shaped buttons floating at the top-left / top-right corners of the deck, like L/R bumpers.
  • Power LED â€” small red pulsing dot between the D-pad and the buttons, animating 2 s ease-in-out.
  • Brand plate â€” "LEVELX" etched in light purple between the controls.
  • Every button has inset highlight + drop shadow + active press-scale (0.9) for tactile feedback.
  • Landscape: deck switches to a vertical strip on the right (280 px wide) â€” D-pad above, face buttons below, shoulders on top row.

v0.15.1 Mobile controller fix

GBA controller now spreads properly in portrait and actually shows up in landscape â€” the previous build's nested media query and sizing-overflow issues hid the controls when the phone rotated.

Portrait â€” wider spread

  • Deck switches from a cramped 3-column grid to a flex-direction: row with justify-content: space-between â€” D-pad hugs the left edge, face buttons hug the right, lots of breathing room in between.
  • D-pad bumped 150 px â†’ 170 px; directional buttons 42 px â†’ 48 px; gap between directional buttons widened.
  • Face button cluster bumped 152 px â†’ 172 px with 8 px gaps between buttons (was 4 px). Individual buttons 48 px â†’ 52 px.
  • Basic attack at center of diamond grew to 60 px â€” the classic "A button" now properly dominates.
  • Shoulder pills repositioned with env(safe-area-inset-left / right) so they respect iPhone notch / home indicator area.
  • LEVELX brand plate + power LED moved to the top-centre of the deck (used to be a grid cell; now absolutely positioned so it doesn't fight for flex space).
  • Deck top-padding bumped 10 px â†’ 50 px to make room for the shoulder pills + brand plate.

Landscape â€” controller actually appears now

  • Bug: the old CSS nested @media (orientation: landscape) inside an @media (pointer: coarse), (max-width: 900px) block. Older Safari/Chrome interpreted the nesting inconsistently, and the grid grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr fit a 150 px D-pad into a 140 px column (2Ã—140 = 280). Result: controls overflowed off-screen or were hidden.
  • Fix: landscape styles no longer nest. They sit under two flat media queries ((pointer: coarse) and (orientation: landscape), and (max-width: 900px) and (orientation: landscape)) that toggle a body.mc-landscape class via JS â€” then the landscape rules apply off that class alone, immune to browser media-query edge cases.
  • JS in _fitGameToViewport computes isTouchOrNarrow + isLandscape every re-fit and toggles the class accordingly, so it's always in sync with the actual viewport even when CSS media queries flicker during orientation changes.
  • Landscape deck is a 220 px vertical strip on the right â€” shoulders top-corners, D-pad (150 px) mid, face cluster (152 px) bottom, LEVELX brand between them.
  • Buttons shrink to 42 / 48 px for vertical fit on â‰¤ 400 px tall landscape viewports.

v0.15.2 Landscape fix Â· button spacing Â· no shoulders

Root-cause fix for the missing landscape controls: the deck is now position: fixed on the right edge so the game-wrapper's 960 px authored layout width can't push it off-screen. Portrait buttons get much more breathing room. Shoulder pills removed.

Landscape â€” deck is now position:fixed

  • Root cause: the wrapper was authored at 960 Ã— 560 px and only visually scaled via transform: scale(). Transforms don't shrink an element's layout box â€” so in landscape, flex-row body put a 960 px wrapper next to the 220 px deck, totalling 1180 px of layout. On a 844 px wide iPhone the deck ended up painted off-screen.
  • Fix: in landscape the deck uses position: fixed; top:0; right:0; bottom:0; width:240px. It sits OUTSIDE body flex flow, guaranteeing it's visible. Body gets padding-right: 240px so the wrapper's 960 px layout doesn't slide under the deck â€” the transform then scales the visual down into the reserved space.
  • JS stops double-subtracting the deck width (the body padding already handles it). The game canvas scales to min((vw - 240) / 960, vh / 560).
  • Tested at 844 Ã— 390 (iPhone 14 landscape) and 667 Ã— 375 (iPhone SE landscape): deck is a visible 240 px strip on the right, game fills the rest.

Portrait â€” bigger cells, no overlap

  • Face button cluster grew from 172 Ã— 172 px â†’ 192 Ã— 192 px with 56 Ã— 56 cells and 12 px gaps (was 52 Ã— 52 with 8 px gaps).
  • Basic-attack "A button" downsized 60 â†’ 56 px so it fits cleanly in its center cell (the 60 px button was overlapping neighbours at the 8 px gap spec).
  • Skill buttons bumped 52 â†’ 56 px to match â€” easier to hit.
  • Jump button bumped 52 â†’ 56 px too, font 22 â†’ 24 px.
  • D-pad stable at 168 px; directional buttons 46 px with bigger central gap.
  • Top padding reduced 50 â†’ 32 px since the shoulder pills are gone â€” the brand plate / LED now sit at top-6 / top-22 for a tighter header.

Shoulder pills removed

  • Block (ðŸ›¡) and Dodge (ðŸ’¨) pills hidden via display: none. They were cluttering the deck and overlapping skill buttons.
  • Block/parry still works on the keyboard A key for desktop players; mobile users use the A-slot skill (left face button) for active skills.
  • Dodge (Shift) was rarely needed on mobile â€” the roll wasn't comfortable to time with thumbs anyway.

v0.16.0 Zodiac badges Â· epic arenas Â· controller split

Horoscope bosses get a proper badge system with earn-flourish + hub-wall display, every zodiac arena gets a signature backdrop matching its theme, and the mobile controller splits across both screen edges in landscape.

ðŸ…Zodiac Badge System

  • Earn flourish â€” first-time zodiac boss kill fires a legendary toast ("ðŸ… â™Œ BADGE EARNED â€” Regulus the Sunmane"), big flash + shake + 120 ms hit-stop + levelup sfx, plus a 60-particle golden radial burst at the boss's centre.
  • Badge medallion â€” new drawZodiacBadge() renders each sign as a plastic medallion: golden disc + glyph when defeated, dim silver disc + ðŸ”’ lock when not. Rotating outer spokes with a cranked-up spin rate on earned badges.
  • Portal badges â€” every zodiac portal in the Zodiac Sanctum hub gets its medallion floating above it, so players see at a glance which signs they've beaten when browsing.
  • Hub centrepiece â€” the 12-glyph wheel now has a golden progress arc filling proportionally with beaten zodiacs, a central "N / 12" counter, and green check marks stamped on each defeated glyph.
  • zodiacDefeated(id) helper queries the existing game.bestiary['_boss_zodiac_<id>'] flag â€” reuses the save schema, nothing new to persist.

Epic themed backdrops â€” all 12 signs

New ZODIAC_SIGNATURE_DECOR dispatch table: one custom painter per sign, layered BEHIND the generic constellation / element atmosphere. Each one gives its arena a distinct sense of place.

  • â™ˆ Aries â€” blazing additive sun disc behind horned mountain silhouettes; lava rivers flow down both arena walls.
  • â™‰ Taurus â€” ancient colosseum columns in parallax; massive translucent minotaur skull (with curled horns + eye sockets) watches from the back wall; dust motes kick up from the floor.
  • â™Š Gemini â€” twin blue/pink crystalline spires with 3-layer animated aurora ribbons sine-waving across the sky.
  • â™‹ Cancer â€” glowing moonlit halo over a crescent moon, two giant crab-claw silhouettes framing the arena, bubbles rising from the floor.
  • â™Œ Leo â€” blazing golden sun with 16 rotating radial flare spokes; two tall golden columns with amber caps frame the arena.
  • â™ Virgo â€” stained-glass rose window with 3 rings Ã— 8 petals in 4 rotating hues; cathedral arch silhouette; wheat-light motes drifting up.
  • â™Ž Libra â€” massive floating golden scale silhouette swaying on a sine â€” pillar + crossbar + two chained pans sway anti-symmetrically. Falling gold coins as ambient.
  • â™ Scorpio â€” red-mist floor glow with lighter composite; a 28-px-thick scorpion tail arcs from bottom-left to top-right ending in a magenta stinger; crypt pillars parallax past.
  • â™ Sagittarius â€” 120 extra twinkling stars; 10 dense tree silhouettes in parallax; 6 wooden arrows embedded in trees with red fletching.
  • â™‘ Capricorn â€” jagged frozen mountain silhouette with procedural snow caps; 3 additive ice-crystal spires; constant blizzard particles sweeping left.
  • â™’ Aquarius â€” a grand amphora tipped at top-centre pouring an endless additive water stream downward; 6 marble palace columns in parallax; splash droplets fountain from the floor.
  • â™“ Pisces â€” abyssal ocean gradient; two massive translucent fish silhouettes swirling in a slow yin-yang orbit; bioluminescent dots drifting inside the arena.

Mobile controller â€” split layout in landscape

  • Bug: the 240 px strip on the right forced all controls into one cluster; player's left thumb had to reach across for movement.
  • Fix: in landscape the deck becomes a transparent full-viewport overlay. D-pad breaks out with position: fixed; left: â€¦; bottom: â€¦. Face buttons do the same on the right. Body reserves 200 px padding on BOTH sides so the game wrapper can't slide under either cluster.
  • D-pad bumped 144 â†’ 176 px, directional buttons 40 â†’ 50 px. Face cluster stays at 192 px.

Mobile controller â€” bigger portrait

  • D-pad plate 168 â†’ 200 px; directional buttons 46 â†’ 58 px; font 17 â†’ 22 px.
  • Face cluster 192 â†’ 216 px; cells 56 â†’ 62 px; gaps 12 â†’ 15 px; buttons 56 â†’ 62 px; jump font 24 â†’ 28 px.
  • Deck top padding reduced 32 â†’ 26 px, brand plate moved up to top-6 / LED at top-20 â€” more vertical room for the bigger buttons.
  • New narrow-phone fallback (â‰¤ 380 px wide) scales everything back down to a sane fit so small phones don't clip buttons.

v0.16.1 Translucent overlay controls + fullscreen game

Mobile controls become translucent-white frosted-glass overlays that float over the game. Canvas fills the whole viewport â€” no reserved deck. Portrait gets D-pad positioned above the face buttons for better thumb ergonomics; PgUp / PgDn potion buttons already render in the top-right stack.

Translucent white button styling

  • Every .mc-btn now uses a frosted-glass look: background: rgba(255,255,255,0.22), 2 px white-tinted border at 55% alpha, backdrop-filter: blur(10px), soft inset highlight, white text with 3 px shadow.
  • On press: background jumps to 42% white with inner-shadow for a tactile feel. No plastic colour â€” buttons let the game show through.
  • Accent-coloured buttons keep their tint but also become translucent: Basic attack (red) at 32% alpha, Jump (amber) at 32%, HP potion (pink), MP potion (blue). Skill circles stay neutral white.

Game canvas fills the whole viewport

  • Deck is now position: fixed; inset: 0; pointer-events: none â€” a transparent overlay that doesn't reserve any layout space.
  • _fitGameToViewport no longer subtracts deck height/width â€” scale = min(vw / 960, vh / 560) uses the full viewport.
  • Body landscape padding dropped (was 200 px on each side). Game wrapper centers in full viewport and scales uniformly.
  • Result: game view is noticeably bigger on phones since the D-pad + buttons no longer eat a bottom strip or side column.

Portrait â€” D-pad above face buttons

  • D-pad pinned to bottom-left but raised 240 px from the bottom edge â€” sits on the left side of the screen well above the lower-thumb zone.
  • Face buttons pinned to bottom-right, 20 px from the edge â€” the classic right-thumb rest position.
  • D-pad is now physically above the face buttons on screen, matching the ergonomic ask.
  • Potions + menu icons stacked vertically in the top-right corner (56 px down from the top to clear the HUD).

Landscape â€” unchanged split with translucent styling

  • D-pad bottom-left, face buttons bottom-right â€” both at 18 px from their edges.
  • Potions + menu stacked horizontally in the top-right.

PgUp / PgDn potion buttons

Already in the HTML as data-mkey="pageup" / data-mkey="pagedown". Now styled to stand out: pink HP button (â™¥), blue MP button (â—†), both 50 px circles in the portrait top-right stack (or landscape top-right row). Tapping dispatches the PgUp / PgDn keyboard event which fires useQuickPotion() (same auto-buy fallback the keyboard binding uses).

Narrow-phone fallback

Screens < 380 px wide shrink the D-pad to 150 px, directional buttons to 44 px, face buttons to 50 px, potions to 44 px. Ensures every button fits comfortably on small phones.

v0.16.2 Max-screen landscape Â· portrait polish

Mobile landscape now stretches the game to fill every pixel of the viewport (slight aspect distortion accepted for maximum screen use). Portrait controls repositioned + sized for natural thumb travel.

Mobile landscape â€” stretch to fill

  • _fitGameToViewport now emits independent X / Y scales (--game-scale-x, --game-scale-y) in mobile landscape.
  • CSS reads both: transform: scale(var(--game-scale-x), var(--game-scale-y)) â€” lets the game-wrapper stretch non-uniformly to fill the full viewport.
  • Desktop + portrait keep uniform scaling (aspect-preserving).
  • Example: 844 Ã— 390 iPhone landscape â€” was scale 0.696 (668 Ã— 390 visual, 176 px of unused bars). Now scale x=0.88, y=0.70 â†’ fills the full 844 Ã— 390 with ~4% horizontal stretch. Characters remain recognisable, every pixel counts.
  • transform-origin: top left in mc-landscape so the stretched wrapper anchors to the viewport corner and doesn't drift off-canvas.

Portrait control polish

  • Diagonal thumb-rest layout: D-pad raised on the left (bottom 40 px), face buttons tucked in the bottom-right (bottom 16 px). Vertical offset avoids overlap on narrow phones.
  • Button sizes optimised â€” oversized buttons felt nice in theory but ate screen. Now:
    • D-pad 168 â†’ 156 px, directional buttons 58 â†’ 46 px
    • Face cluster 216 â†’ 184 px, skills / jump 62 â†’ 54 px
    • Basic-attack "A button" centres at 58 px so it still pops
    • Potions / menu 50 â†’ 44 px
  • Potions + menu icons sit in a horizontal row at the top-right corner (was vertical stack) â€” thumb tilts up to reach them but they take less vertical real estate.
  • Result: controls visibly smaller but more game canvas visible, and all buttons remain comfortably tappable.

v0.16.3 Landscape un-stretch Â· perf pass

Reverts the squashed landscape aspect ratio and takes a heavy perf pass â€” removes the biggest mobile-lag source (backdrop-filter blur), caches zodiac-arena gradients, precomputes Sagittarius starfield, and trims aggressive particle spawn rates.

Landscape â€” aspect ratio fixed

  • Bug: v0.16.2's non-uniform X/Y scaling stretched the game wider than authored. Characters looked squashed / elongated depending on phone aspect.
  • Fix: reverted to uniform min(sx, sy) scaling. Phones with wider aspect than 1.71:1 get small letterbox bars, but the game's proportions stay correct. Mobile still gets a subtle 4% size bonus (capped so it never overflows).
  • Transform origin reset to center center in landscape so the wrapper stays centred.
  • Same --game-scale-x / --game-scale-y CSS vars remain wired in case we return to non-uniform later, but both always receive the same value now.

âš¡Mobile perf pass

  • Killed backdrop-filter: blur(10px) on the touch buttons. This was the single biggest mobile lag source â€” every frame the browser re-blurred the underlying canvas for every visible button (~15 buttons Ã— 60 fps = 900 blur composites/sec). Replaced with a simple rgba(40,30,60,0.55) translucent background + stronger border + drop shadow. Looks almost identical, costs essentially nothing.
  • Cached zodiac-arena gradients. ZODIAC_SIGNATURE_DECOR functions were calling createRadialGradient / createLinearGradient every single frame. New _zGrad(key, factory) cache populated on first use â€” Aries sun, Cancer moon halo, Scorpio red mist, Pisces abyssal gradient, and the arena vignette all now build their gradient ONCE and reuse it. Zero recurring cost across the rest of the fight.
  • Sagittarius starfield precomputed. Was 120 stars Ã— 2 Math.sin/cos calls per frame = 240 trig ops/frame. Now positions are precomputed into a static array on first call; frame loop only touches the twinkle phase. Also dropped star count 120 â†’ 90.
  • Particle spawn rates tamed:
    • Capricorn blizzard: every 2 frames (30/s) â†’ every 5 frames (12/s).
    • Taurus dust: every 3 frames (20/s) â†’ every 8 frames (8/s).
    • Capricorn life cut 200 â†’ 140 frames so particles turn over faster.

Net effect: frame time during zodiac fights should drop significantly on mobile, especially on signs with moonlit halos / red mist / starfields (Cancer, Scorpio, Sagittarius, Pisces, Aries).

v0.17.0 Village Â· Bloodlust Â· Zodiac portal Â· cute floor

Town becomes a storybook village of thatched cottages, Bloodlust (Berserker F) becomes a true ultimate with 10 s of shockwaves + 15 s buff, the Zodiac Sanctum portal moves to sit right above the Amnesiac, and the floor + clouds pick up the monster art's chibi aesthetic.

Zodiac portal above the Amnesiac

  • Moved from x: 20 (leftmost edge of town) to x: 440 â€” right above the Amnesiac NPC. Players naturally discover it once they interact with him for the first time.

ðŸ©¸Berserker â€” Bloodlust rework

Was a brief 6 s lifesteal + speed buff. Now a proper ultimate.

  • 15 s rage buff (+ATK, lifesteal, movement speed).
  • 10 s of outward shockwaves â€” every 400 ms the player fires a 10-projectile ring (~70% ATK + 5 each, pierce, no gravity). 25 shockwave salvos over the duration.
  • 20 s cooldown (up from 5.5 s).
  • Cast burst: hit-stop + 10-shake + crit sfx + bigger blood-spiral particle ring. Heals 25% HP + 25% MP on cast.
  • New per-frame tick in the update loop reads player._bloodstorm + player._bloodstormNextTick and fires the salvo on cadence.
  • Description: "15 s rage (+ATK, lifesteal, speed). 10 s of outward shockwaves that rip nearby foes."

Town â€” humble village cottages

Old 6-storey high-rise town replaced with a proper storybook village. Each NPC now lives in a thatched cottage.

  • 6 cottages, each ~110 Ã— 110 px (was 260â€“330 px tall). Walls in soft pastel tones with a hard cel-shaded right-side shadow band.
  • Half-timber beams â€” vertical posts at each edge, horizontal lintel, diagonal brace rendered as pixel-staircase for an authentic look.
  • Single warm-lit window per cottage (36 Ã— 26 px) with a yellow glow, cross-mullion frame, and glow spot. Flower box underneath with 3 tiny flowers (pink, yellow, purple).
  • Arched wooden door with 2-tone cel shading, plank lines, and a brass handle.
  • Steep thatched roof with a lit face + shadow face + short dashed thatch-strand texture + dark ridge cap. Roof colour varies per cottage (red, slate blue, amber, purple, green, warm orange).
  • Stone chimney with a dark cap, emitting 3 animated smoke puffs that rise and sway on a sine.
  • Hanging shop sign plaque with a rope, 2-tone wood, and the shop emoji (ðŸ§ª âš”ï¸ ðŸ“˜ âœ¨ ðŸ¡ ðŸª).
  • Result: much cuter, matches the chibi-mob aesthetic, reads as a cozy starting town rather than a modern cityscape.

Cuter floor + cloud design

  • Ground dirt simplified from 3-band to 2-tone (honey-dirt top + deep-soil bottom) with a crisp black seam. Round pebbles replace flat dots â€” each pebble gets a drop shadow + body + highlight pixel for volume. Undersoil "roots" are now little curved squiggles via quadraticCurveTo rather than static vertical ticks.
  • Grass top gets a scalloped wavy edge â€” small arcs drawn atop the flat grass band for an uneven, natural lip. Two-tone deeper green band underneath.
  • Grass tufts doubled in density, drawn as 2-tone blades (bright lit + shadow side) instead of single pixels.
  • Chibi flowers every ~90 px â€” a 4-petal pink/yellow/purple plump flower with a yellow core AND a tiny cute face (2 eye dots) rendered on top. Matches the monster chibi vibe.
  • Clouds now have optional tiny faces on bright maps â€” little black dot eyes, pink blush cheeks, and a subtle smile arc. About half the clouds get faces (every even-sized cloud) to avoid feeling busy.

v0.25.138 VFX sprite refresh — 8 painterly hand-painted assets via ludo

User feedback: "improve the C:\Users\Xenon\Desktop\LevelX\Sprites\vfx with ludo.ai." Replaced 8 low-resolution placeholder VFX PNGs with painterly Genshin/Hollow Knight-style hand-painted versions. Filenames preserved so all existing code references continue to resolve.

  • fireball.png — glowing flame orb with bright yellow-white core + swirling embers + halo glow
  • ice_spike.png — pale-blue translucent ice shard with frosty mist trail + smaller ice fragments orbiting
  • magic_bolt.png — purple-magenta arcane orb with crackling sparks + violet halo
  • meteor.png — rocky boulder wreathed in flame, smoke + ember trail (diagonal motion)
  • slash.png — bright cyan-white crescent slash with motion-blur streaks
  • portal.png — vertical violet vortex inside a magenta runic ring + drifting stars
  • death.png — dark grey-purple smoke with dissolving skull silhouette + ghostly wisps
  • level_up.png — golden radial starburst with rays + sparkling motes

v0.25.137 Vector hero — head shading polish, dramatic attack swing, outlines +1 px

User feedback: "improve the shading of the head, improve the attacking vectors especially the weapon, outline increase by 1px."

Head shading

  • Light band enlarged from rx=10,ry=7 → rx=11,ry=8 (wider lit wash on upper-left).
  • New rim crescent — thin shifted-self bright disc clipped to head, paints a translucent white band along the upper-left edge for the "light source from above-left" cue.
  • Primary gloss pip nudged up to rx=4.8,ry=3.6.
  • New secondary catchlight at (-4, -7) — small bright pip near the eye area (anime "double catchlight" tradition).
  • New cheek blush — two soft pink ellipses (rgba(255,140,150,0.28)) on each cheek, clipped to head so they don't bleed past the silhouette.

Attack vectors

  • Right-arm swing range bumped from -1.2 → -0.5 rad to -1.5 → +0.5 rad (a full ~115° arc with proper windup-to-followthrough).
  • Swing curve uses (1 - cos(tπ)) × 1.0 for ease-out feel (slow windup, fast contact, slow follow-through).
  • Spine + head twist amplitudes increased: spine 0.12 → 0.18, head 0.10 → 0.14. Whole body sells the swing.
  • New handR mesh deform on attack — weapon scales up by ~18% at swing peak for "weighty contact".
  • New slash arc trail — translucent white arc swept from the windup angle to the current swing angle, drawn before the weapon. Bright leading-edge inner pass sells the motion direction.

Outlines +1 px

  • Main body STROKE_W: 1 → 2.
  • Eye outline 0.5 → 1.5. Mouth 0.6 → 1.6.
  • Weapon strokes: every 0.5 → 1.5; bow outer rim 2 → 3, bow inner wood rim 0.5 → 1.5.
  • Restores some of the v0.25.135 reduction — outline now reads halfway between the v0.25.134 thick (3 px) and v0.25.135 thin (1 px) versions.

v0.25.136 Platform sync fix — new v2 sprites with no transparent padding

User feedback: "there is a gap between the platform and the character/monster, make it sync, and generate better platform sprites with ludo.ai and implement." Re-generated all 5 themed platform tiles (grass / stone / ice / lava / sand) with explicit "fills the full frame edge-to-edge, no transparent padding" prompt, and switched the bake mode for every platform from grid:1x2 / various to auto — which v0.25.127's both-axes opaque-bound crop strips any residual padding rows automatically.

Root cause of the gap

  • Old floor_wood_v3.webp source had the top plank starting ~6 px below the canvas top (transparent painterly halo above the wood).
  • grid:1x2 bake cropped exactly the top half of the canvas, preserving those transparent padding rows.
  • When scaled to platform h=12, the padding rows squished into "phantom space" above the visible plank → character feet (collision at p.y) appeared 4–6 px above the painted plank top.

Fix

  • 5 new platform sprites in backgrounds/tiles/: platform_grass_v2.webp, platform_stone_v2.webp, platform_ice_v2.webp, platform_lava_v2.webp, platform_sand_v2.webp. Each prompted with "SINGLE horizontal slab that COMPLETELY FILLS THE FRAME edge-to-edge from top to bottom, NO transparent padding above or below" so the baked tile's top edge IS the visible slab top.
  • Every platform bake mode is now auto. _lxBakeSeamlessTile('auto') already crops both horizontal AND vertical opaque bounds (added in v0.25.127), so any tiny residual padding gets stripped.
  • Result: character + monster feet at p.y now sit ON the platform's painted top edge instead of floating 4–6 px above.

v0.25.135 Vector hero — Ivory tone, 0.9× scale, default mode, weapons, eye-tracking, thinner outlines

Stacked user feedback in one ship: "include lighter skin shade, make shading over the head bottom right, and add a light shade add top left, make character slightly smaller (90% of current size), default use drawvectorhero rather than draw cuter hero", "make a layer for weapons based on class", "make character eyes move towards the direction of movement", "for the maincharacter vectors, reduce all outlines by 2 px".

Lighter skin tone

  • New Ivory entry prepended to HERO_VEC_SKIN: light=#fffaf2 / base=#fdf0db / mid=#f1d8bc / deep=#cfae8c — lighter than Porcelain.
  • Existing skinIdx values shift by +1; CHAR_STUDIO.skinIdx default bumped 1 → 2 so new players still default to "Light". Existing saves with skinIdx=1 read as Porcelain (close enough; can re-pick).

Enhanced head shading

  • Bottom-right cel offset bumped 6 → 7 px on the head so the lower-right shadow crescent reads more clearly.
  • Upper-left lit band enlarged from rx=7,ry=5 to rx=10,ry=7 — proper "light from above-left" wash.
  • Glossy white pip moved to (-10, -11) with alpha=0.92 so the shiniest spot sits on top of the lit band.

0.9× size scale

  • Vector hero now renders at 90% of the previous size. Implemented as ctx.scale(0.9, 0.9) right after the world-space anchor + facing-flip, so the rig's rest-pose offsets all shrink uniformly.

Default switched to vector

  • _CHAR_STYLE default flipped from 'sprite' to 'vector'. Players who haven't set a preference now see the vector hero by default.
  • Final fallback in drawPlayer (when neither vector flag nor lookCustom nor HERO sprite is active) now goes to _drawVectorHero instead of drawCuteCharacter — consistent look at every state.
  • Power users can still force ?charstyle=sprite URL flag for the legacy HERO sprite path.
