Per-class weapon layer (HERO_VEC_WEAPON)

  • New handR bone (child of armR at offset (0, 10)) anchors the weapon at the front-hand. The weapon naturally inherits the arm's animation rotation + the head/spine bob.
  • Warrior — silver sword with gold crossguard, dark grip, gold pommel; cel shadow on the right edge of the blade + bright highlight on the left.
  • Rogue — short silver dagger with dark leather wrap on the grip.
  • Mage — tall wooden staff with a glowing cyan magic orb on top + radial halo glow.
  • Archer — curved wooden bow with bowstring + leather grip wrap.
  • Default class (no class picked) renders no weapon — clean naked-mannequin silhouette.

Eye tracking

  • Pupil-highlight pip in _drawEye now shifts horizontally based on player movement: +0.7 px when moving forward, -0.7 px when moving backward, mirrored under facing flip. Falls back to a slight +0.6 "looking forward" offset during attack windup.
  • Reads off player.vx + player.attacking; idle eyes stay centred.

Outlines −2 px

  • Main body STROKE_W: 3 → 1.
  • Eye outline: 1 → 0.5. Mouth: 1.6 → 0.6.
  • Weapon outlines: each reduced by 2 px with a 0.5 px floor.
  • Result: a much lighter painterly look — less "thick black sticker" feel, more soft chibi.

v0.25.134 Vector studio (Q) — skin tones + hair picker + 3-tone shading; sprite system removed

User feedback: "make the option to choose skin tone, improve to include 1 more shade, make torso smaller, make ability to choose between different hair options, delete the sprites in the Q UI and replace with this." Wholesale rebuild of the Q-key Character Studio so it drives the vector hero.

Skin tone presets (HERO_VEC_SKIN)

  • 7 tones: Porcelain, Light, Tan, Olive, Bronze, Brown, Deep.
  • Each tone carries 4 levels — light (highlight pip + lit band on the head), base (lit fill), mid (first cel crescent), deep (second deeper cel crescent for the new 3-tone pass).
  • Studio swatches show the base hex; clicking sets CHAR_STUDIO.skinIdx live.

3-tone cel-shading (the user's requested "1 more shade")

  • _drawShaded(path, base, mid, deep, dx, dy) now does TWO shifted-self-clip passes. Pass 1 paints mid in the lower-right crescent. Pass 2 clips again to that crescent, translates further, paints deep. Result: 3 bands with crisp hard-edged boundaries.
  • Head also paints an upper-left SKIN.light band for a 4-tone read overall.

Hair picker (HERO_VEC_HAIR)

idLook
animeTop crown + 2 tufts + dramatic forward bang + back ponytail (the v0.25.131 silhouette)
shortClean short cut, smooth dome, no bang
spikeJagged spiky punk silhouette
bowlEven rounded bowl cut with crisp side fringes
bunTop dome + round ball bun
pigtailsTwin pigtails extending out + down

Smaller torso

  • Pear height ~16 px → ~12 px (tighter waist, narrower shoulders). Head:body ratio ~3:1.

Q-UI rebuilt for vector (sprite system deleted)

  • Removed the legacy sprite-layer system: CHAR_STUDIO.base / baseColored / walkSheet / faces / hairs are gone, plus _csLoadImg + loadCharStudioLayers. Studio state is now just {skinIdx, hairId}.
  • renderCharStudio calls _drawVectorHero on the studio canvas with explicit _ctx + opts override. Re-renders every requestAnimationFrame tick so the idle bob animates live in the preview.
  • applyCharStudioToPlayer writes player.lookCustom = {skinIdx, hairId} + saveState(). revertCharStudioFromPlayer clears it.
  • drawPlayer routes through _drawVectorHero when player.lookCustom is set (in addition to the existing ?charstyle=vector URL flag).
  • lookCustom added to PLAYER_SAVE_FIELDS so the picks persist across reloads.

Renderer refactor

  • _drawVectorHero(sx, sy, _ctx, opts) now accepts an optional target canvas + opts override (cls, lookCustom, animName, animTime). Internally aliases _ctx || _MAIN_CTX to local ctx so the rest of the function reads naturally without TDZ. _MAIN_CTX added at the top of the script as a stable alias to the main-canvas 2D context.

v0.25.133 Vector hero — unified silhouette + cel-shading + long anime eyes + animated head bob

User feedback: "Make vector for all the classes the same, improve cell shading, make eyes long vertical cute, make torso small, head big, make head bob when move or attack."

Unified silhouette

  • Removed the per-class hat field. No more warrior helmet / rogue hood / mage pointy / archer feather. Every class now shares the chibi anime hairstyle (the v0.25.131 silhouette).
  • Class identity reads off the body palette only: warrior red, rogue indigo, mage blue, archer green, default peach.

Resized rig (smaller torso, bigger head)

  • Head: r=18 → r=20.
  • Torso: ~30 px tall → ~16 px tall; tighter pear with waist pulled in.
  • Arms / legs: ~14 / ~12 tall → ~10 each; stubbier blobs.
  • Head:body ratio closer to a true 1:1 chibi look.

Cel-shading via shifted-self-clip

  • Replaced soft offset-ellipse shadow with proper hand-drawn cel look: fill base → clip to part → translate (dx, dy) → fill SAME PATH in shadow tone. The intersection is the lower-right crescent of the part — crisp hard-edged boundary between lit and shaded regions.
  • Head + torso: 5–6 px shadow offset. Arms + legs: 3 px. Hair: 3 px own pass.

Long vertical anime eyes

  • Dot eyes (1.8 px circle) → tall ovals (rx=2.4, ry=5.2). Solid black with white highlight pip on the upper portion.
  • Universal across all classes (default mannequin now has eyes too).

Animated head bob (new HERO_VEC_BOB table)

  • Per-bone position offsets layered into applyChain() after the rest-pose translate, before rotation.
  • idle — subtle 0.6 px breathing.
  • walk — -|sin(t×2π)|×3 + 1.5: head bobs UP twice per cycle (once per step), visible weight transfer.
  • jump — static -2 px (head leans up during airborne pose).
  • attack — -sin(t×π)×4: head dips down on windup then snaps back.

v0.25.132 Vector hero — chibi mannequin proportions + 3-tone shading + glossy highlight

User shipped a chibi mannequin reference (giant round head, blob torso, stubby teardrop limbs, soft 2-tone peach skin, thick black outline, white glossy highlight on the upper-left of the head) and asked the vector hero to match. Wholesale rebuild of HERO_VEC_PARTS + HERO_VEC_RIG + the renderer's shading model.

New parts (Path2D)

  • head — circle r=18 (was 14) for the big chibi dome
  • torso — pear shape via Q-curves: tapered shoulders at y=-22, widest at the hip line at y=4, rounded bottom at y=8
  • arm — stubby teardrop blob, pivot at top (shoulder), rounded hand at y=14
  • leg — stubby teardrop blob, pivot at top (hip), rounded foot at y=12

Rig anchors (chibi proportions, total height ~70 px)

  • root at feet centre (0, 0)
  • legL/legR at (±5, −12) — hip pivot
  • spine at (0, −12) — torso pivots at hip line
  • armL/armR at (±9, −18) — shoulder pivot
  • head at (−2, −38) — large head sits high; slight x offset for the reference's subtle head tilt

3-tone shading + glossy highlight

  • New _drawShaded(path, base, shadow, dx, dy, sx, sy) helper: fills base → clips to the part → paints shadow as an offset ellipse (lower-right) → strokes outline.
  • New _drawGloss(cx, cy, rx, ry, alpha): soft semi-transparent white ellipse for the glossy chibi reflection. Head gets a prominent pip at (−7, −8); torso/arms/legs get smaller subtler pips.
  • Outline thickness bumped 1.5 px → 3 px to match the bold reference linework. lineJoin/lineCap = round for smooth corners.

Per-class palette additions

  • Every class now has a paired bodyShd + skinShd tone for the shadow pass. Skin warmed from #fde2c4 (cool cream) to #f5c8a8 / #e0a888 (warm peach) to match the reference.
  • Default class is now a faceless naked mannequin — body = boot = skin tone, no clothing accent, no eyes/mouth. Identical to the reference frame.
  • Clothed classes (warrior/rogue/mage/archer) keep their bold body colour + accent belt + simple chibi face (eye dots + smile).

v0.25.131 Vector hero hair re-shaped to match an anime-style reference

User shipped a hair silhouette reference and asked the vector hero to match it. Replaced the old jagged-edge "rounded cap" path with a flowing anime-styled silhouette: rounded top crown, two small upward tufts in the middle of the top edge, dramatic forward bang sweeping down past the chin (right side / character's facing direction), and a back ponytail extending out behind the head (left side).

New HERO_VEC_PARTS.hair path

  • Traced clockwise from the left side of the head outline, around the crown with the two tufts, down the right side into the long forward bang, across the face line, then out into the back ponytail.
  • Bang reaches x=22 at its widest (head circle is r=14) and y=18 at its tip — so it visibly sweeps past the chin.
  • Ponytail extends to x=-22, y=20, giving a clear back-tail silhouette when the character faces right.
  • Visible on archer (over hair-coloured fill) and default class. Warrior helmet / rogue hood / mage pointy hat still replace it on those classes — that's the existing per-class headgear design.

v0.25.130 Vector hero renderer — SVG paths + skeletal animation + mesh deformation

User feedback: "improve on Vector Art and SVG, Skeletal Animation and Mesh Deformation." New opt-in renderer draws the hero from Path2D SVG primitives over a parented bone tree, with per-bone rotation curves and scale-based mesh squash/stretch. Default stays the HERO sprite-sheet renderer so existing builds keep their painterly hero artwork — toggle via ?charstyle=vector URL flag or localStorage.setItem('mojiworld_charstyle', 'vector').

Skeletal rig

BoneRest pose (rel. to parent)Parent
root(0, 0)—
spine(0, −26)root
head(0, −32)spine
armL (back)(−9, −22)spine
armR (front)(9, −22)spine
legL (back)(−5, −2)root
legR (front)(5, −2)root

Each bone draws by walking its parent chain root → … → bone, applying every translate → rotate → scale in order. Children naturally inherit parent transforms via ctx.save/restore — no explicit matrix math.

Vector body parts (Path2D from SVG path strings)

  • head — circle, 28 px diameter
  • torso — trapezoidal Q-curve quad with subtle waist taper
  • arm, leg — rounded-bottom rectangles
  • hair — jagged-edge triangle row above the head

Animations

  • idle — spine + head sway at sin(t×2π)×0.012, no limb motion
  • walk — opposing arm/leg swings at ±0.45/0.55 rad, 4× spine bob
  • jump — static "tucked" pose (arms back, legs split) with arched spine
  • attack — one-shot 22-frame swing (right arm sweep −1.2 → 0 rad)

Mesh deformation

  • idle — spine breathes via inverse sx/sy oscillation (compress horizontally as it stretches vertically — classic squash-and-stretch)
  • walk — spine stretches at impact frames (sy = 1 + sin(t×4π)×0.04)
  • jump — static body stretch (sx 0.94, sy 1.08)
  • attack — right-arm extends by 18% in y as it swings; spine snaps to (1.05, 0.98) for the recoil

Class-specific silhouettes

  • Warrior — red body, gold helmet dome on head
  • Rogue — indigo body, dark hood draping over the skin circle
  • Mage — blue body, pointy purple hat with gold star
  • Archer — green body, brown hair + gold feather sticking out the side

Wiring

  • drawPlayer() branches: _CHAR_STYLE === 'vector' → _drawVectorHero(); else the existing HERO sprite path. Block-bubble + nameplate + class-identity HUD all still draw on top.
  • Anchor: feet centred at (sx + player.w/2, sy + player.h). Facing flips via ctx.scale(-1, 1).
  • The face-overlay pass (_drawCuteFaceOverlay) is suppressed in vector mode — the head bone draws its own eyes + mouth.

v0.25.129 Theme-aware floor + platform tiles — stone / ice / lava / sand variants per biome

User feedback: "create more variations of floor based on the theme of the map and implement them" + "same goes for the platform". Generated 4 floor + 4 platform variants in the same painterly Genshin/Hollow Knight style as v0.25.128's grass + wood pair, then wired drawPlatforms to look up the right tile per biome.

New tiles (8 total, all in backgrounds/tiles/)

ThemeFloor (ground)Platform (slab)Used by
Stonefloor_stone_v1.webpplatform_stone_v1.webpcryptHollow / sanctum / gravitosArena / md.bg='dungeon'
Icefloor_ice_v1.webpplatform_ice_v1.webpfrozenPeak
Lavafloor_lava_v1.webpplatform_lava_v1.webplavaCavern
Sandfloor_sand_v1.webpplatform_sand_v1.webpduneSands

Wiring

  • New _pickFloorTheme(md) helper resolves the active theme per map. Order: per-map md.floorTheme override → game.currentMap id lookup → md.bg='dungeon' → default 'grass'. (Uses game.currentMap because _variedMapData() spreads the MAPS entry without re-attaching the source key, so md.id is typically undefined.)
  • _THEME_FLOOR_KEY + _THEME_PLATFORM_KEY map theme name → LX_TILES entry: floorGrass / platformGrass, floorStone / platformStone, etc.
  • drawPlatforms now picks _gtKey + _ptKey at the top of each platform iteration and feeds the resolved tile into _lxDrawTiledRow. Bake mode 'auto' on every variant uses v0.25.127's both-axes opaque-bounds crop so the visible surface lines up with the player foot line.
  • Renamed LX_TILES.floorWood → LX_TILES.platformGrass for consistency with the new theme-keyed naming. The grass biome wood plank stays the default for forest / valley / meadow / town / candy / sky / honeycomb / celestialSpire.
  • Maps can opt into a specific theme directly via md.floorTheme: 'stone' | 'ice' | 'lava' | 'sand' | 'grass'.

v0.25.128 Painterly v3 floor + platform tiles to match bg_v2_* map backdrops

User feedback: "Use ludo.ai to generate floor and platform that matches the map and implement." Generated two new tiles in painterly hand-painted Genshin/Hollow Knight style to match the bg_v2_* map hero art (the chibi-cel _b variants clashed visually with the painterly backgrounds). Wired in via the existing LX_TILES sources map — the auto-bake handles seamless tiling on grass; grid:1x2 crops the top plank for the wood.

Assets

  • backgrounds/tiles/floor_grass_v3.webp — 4 grass-on-dirt cells touching, vibrant emerald grass over loamy brown earth, pebbles + small mushrooms scattered. Painterly soft brushstrokes, no chibi outlines. Auto-bake crops the contiguous opaque region into a seamless tile.
  • backgrounds/tiles/floor_wood_v3.webp — 2 stacked horizontal weathered-oak planks with iron nails and mossy edges. grid:1x2 crops the top plank only so a thin h=12 platform shows one full plank rather than squashing both planks into 6 px each.

Wiring

  • The chibi-cel floor_grass_b.webp + floor_wood_b.webp assets stay in the repo as silent fallbacks if the v3 files ever 404.
  • v0.25.127's vertical-bounds bake fix carries over — the new grass tile's opaque content fills the cropped region, so the player + monster foot line lands on the visible grass surface.

v0.25.127 Fix character + monsters appearing to float above the ground

User feedback: "the height of the floor is not correct, my character and the monsters appear to have some distance off the ground." Root cause: the ludo-generated grass+dirt source image carried 30-50% transparent padding above and below the actual grass+dirt strip. The auto-bake only cropped the LEFT/RIGHT opaque bounds, not top/bottom — so when scaled to platform height, the empty padding rows squashed into "phantom ground" above and below the visible art. Result: the player's foot line (which collides correctly with p.y) appeared 15–25 px above the visible grass surface.

Fix

  • Extended _lxBakeSeamlessTile() 'auto' mode to also detect the vertical opaque-bounds (yTop → yBot) and crop the source on both axes.
  • Baked tile is now exactly the rectangle of visible grass+dirt content. When drawn into the p.h + grassLift destination region, the top row of the image lines up with p.y - grassLift (i.e. just above the platform's logical foot line, where small grass tufts are supposed to protrude) instead of with empty padding.
  • Net visual change: character + monster sprites now actually stand on the grass instead of floating 15–25 px above it.

v0.25.126 Items / equipment UI mirrored under the U tab

User feedback: "Could you make the item / equipment UI under the U tab as well." Added a 4th tab ðŸŽ’ Items to the Character panel (U) alongside Level Up / Boon Loadout / Skills. Same equipment slots row, same inv-tabs (Equip / Use / Etc / Set-Up / Cash), same grid — renders in-place inside the U modal so players can manage gear without bouncing between modals. The standalone B-key inventory still works exactly as before.

Plumbing

  • renderInventory() now takes an optional prefix argument so it can target either DOM host. The B-key call uses the empty default; the U-tab call uses 'u-'.
  • Element lookups (inv-grid, inv-tab-info, inv-tabs, eq-weapon, eq-armor, eq-accessory, consumables) all prepend the prefix.
  • Class-based selectors (.equip-slots, .inv-slot, .inv-tab) scoped to the correct host so the dim/active states don't leak between the two copies.
  • Tab-button onclick + slot onclicks recurse with the same prefix so re-renders stay in the right pane.

v0.25.125 Archer bow overlay — bow now visible across all states, no longer pops in/out

User feedback: "archer sprites does not seem to be synced, please ensure the drawing of the bow is consistent." Root cause: the generic HERO sheets (idle / walk / jump / hit / death / attack) draw the teen with no weapon — only the class-specific teen_archer_shot.webp has a bow rendered into its 4 frames. Result: the archer carried nothing visible during 95% of gameplay, and the bow pop-flashed into existence only during the half-second basic-attack animation.

Fix

  • New procedural bow overlay drawn at the end of _drawPlayerCustomSprite() when player.cls === 'archer' and the active hero animation is not the archer-shot sheet.
  • Reuses the existing _drawCuteBow(P, f, false) renderer (idle hold pose, no nocked arrow) and _weaponPalette() for rarity-aware coloring — equipped bow visuals propagate correctly.
  • Bow positioned at the right-hand grip (~18% width offset, ~50% height up from feet, scaled 0.7×, tilted -0.35 rad). A subtle sin(time * 0.18) * 0.6 bob keeps it sympathetic to walk/idle motion.
  • Detection: hoisted the existing block-scoped frames variable to a new _heroActiveFrames outer let so the bow overlay can compare against HERO.classFrames.archer without re-deriving state. The archer-shot animation is the only state where the sheet already has a bow, so we skip the procedural overlay there to avoid drawing two bows.

States the bow is now visible in

  • Idle — bow held vertically at hip, string straight.
  • Walk / run — bow follows the body with a tiny sympathetic bob.
  • Jump / fall — bow stays in the right hand through the arc.
  • Hit / hit-stun — bow bobs with the recoil pose.
  • Death — bow stays in hand through the death frames.
  • Generic attack (non-archer-shot states, e.g. dash slash) — bow visible.
  • Archer-shot animation — overlay deliberately skipped; the sheet already has a bow drawn into the frames with proper draw-back/recoil.

v0.25.124 DOT tick cadence slowed (40 → 80 frames)

User feedback: "make DOT (damage over time) deal damage more slowly". Burn / poison ticks were every ~40 frames (~0.67 s @ 60 fps); a stack of three sources tagged on a tough mob produced a wall of red numbers every two-thirds of a second. Tick interval doubled to ~80 frames (~1.3 s) so the damage application visibly paces.

What changed

  • polishBurn-DoT tick interval 40 → 80 frames — the only change. Per-tick damage (m.burnDmg) is unchanged, so total burn damage over the duration roughly halves. Affects every DOT source: Grand Hex, fire / elemental arrows, poison-on-hit enhancements, etc.
  • polishDamage numbers spread out — the damage-number stream is half as dense; mob HP bars trickle down at a calmer pace. No change to mob behavior or stagger.

Files

  • maple_game.html — version bump to v0.25.124. Single literal change in updateMonsters (40 → 80) plus updated comment block. +5 / -2 lines.

v0.25.123 XP curve very steep from level 15 onwards

User feedback: "make the exp curve very steep starting from level 15 onwards". Repointed the per-level expToNext multiplier so the early game (L1 – L14) keeps its gentle ramp, then a hard wall starts at L15 and ratchets up further at L20 / L30 / L40.

Level bandOld multiplierNew multiplier
1 – 141.201.20 (unchanged)
15 – 191.201.40
20 – 291.201.45
30 – 391.241.50
40+1.281.55

Implication

  • polishL1 – L14 — same pacing as before. First class identity (L1) and the lead-in to first job advancement still arrive at a normal cadence.
  • polishL15+ — the per-level multiplier jumps from 1.20 to 1.40 at the boundary, so a player crossing into L15 sees the next bar take roughly 1.7× longer than the L14→L15 grind. Each subsequent band ratchets up. By L40+ each level needs ~×1.55 of the prior bar — a real wall instead of a slow walk.
  • polishJob / master advancement levels — L25 (job) and L50 (master) are unchanged thresholds; only the EXP cost to reach them shifts upward.

Files

  • maple_game.html — version bump to v0.25.123. Single curveMul ladder rewritten in levelUp(). +11 / -10 lines.

v0.25.122 Triple-jump perks no longer wiped on boon equip

User report: "triple jump perk seems to not be functioning". Tracked the bug to _applyEquippedBoons(): it resets every player.mods.* field to 0 before re-applying boon contributions, which wipes the archer class baseline (+1 extraJumps) and the Aerial Mastery tree node (+1 extraJumps). Both are non-boon sources, so the recompute can't see them. Effect: as soon as a player equipped or unequipped any boon, their triple-jump went silent.

Fix

  • bugRe-layer perk bonuses after the boon loop — the recompute now sums archer-class (+1) + Aerial Mastery (+1 if treeUnlocked.a_aerial) + boon contribution (max-of), capped at 3 air jumps so a fully-stacked archer with the tree node + a Triple Jump boon ends at quad jump rather than infinity.
  • polishPersists across re-equips — _applyEquippedBoons is the canonical recompute, so any future boon / perk that touches extraJumps goes through this single layering step.

Files

  • maple_game.html — version bump to v0.25.122. ~12-line block added at the end of _applyEquippedBoons just before the multishot cap. +12 / 0 lines.

v0.25.121 Hotkeys — Pg Up / Pg Dn heal 50% HP / MP, Codex moved 1 → Y

Two key-binding changes bundled.

  • featPg Up — heal 50% HP — instantly restores 50% of getMaxHp(). Free, no potion required, no gold deduction. Reads as a "rest" hotkey rather than a potion shortcut.
  • featPg Down — heal 50% MP — instantly restores 50% of getMaxMp(). Same convention as Pg Up.
  • polishR / T preserved — the legacy useQuickPotion('hp' | 'mp') path stays bound to R / T for inventory-based healing. Players who want to manage potion stacks can still do that; players who want a fast rest hit Pg Up / Pg Dn.
  • polishCodex of Shardfall: 1 → Y — the lore map now opens / closes with Y. Frees up the 1 number key for the dev 1+2+3 combo and consistency with future emote/menu numbers. In-modal help text updated.

Files

  • maple_game.html — version bump to v0.25.121. Quick-potion handler split: PgUp / PgDn now do direct heals, R / T keep useQuickPotion. Codex key changed in the keyhandler + modal sub-text. +22 / -7 lines.

v0.25.120 Master G-skill pass — Crusader Aegis longer + harder hits, Doombringer Calamity wider / 10 strikes / 18 s CD

Two master-tier G skills tuned. Both kits felt under-costed for their cooldown.

SkillMasterFieldOldNew
Divine AegisCrusaderActive duration8 s16 s (×2)
Divine AegisCrusaderOrb damage1.1×ATK + 62.2×ATK + 12 (×2)
Blade of CalamityDoombringerStrike count5 + slam coda10 + slam coda
Blade of CalamityDoombringerSlash hitbox width220 px320 px (+45%)
Blade of CalamityDoombringerForward stagger80…120 px80…260 px (chain reaches further)
Blade of CalamityDoombringerCooldown32 s18 s (−44%)

Per-skill notes

  • polishDivine Aegis — the half-damage shield + 5 hovering orbs now last 16 s instead of 8 s. Each orb's per-strike damage doubles to keep the per-tick threat consistent at the longer uptime. Heal pulse, orb cadence (350 ms), and consecration burst unchanged.
  • polishBlade of Calamity — 5 forward slashes → 10 (twice as many "lines of damage"). Each slash hitbox widens 220 → 320 px and the forward stagger doubles, so the chain sweeps from 80 to 260 px ahead of the player instead of stalling at 80…120. Cooldown nearly halved (32 → 18 s) so it's a rotation centerpiece rather than a once-per-fight burst. Slam coda fire-time and damage unchanged. Invuln window extended 1300 → 1900 ms to cover the longer chain.

Files

  • maple_game.html — version bump to v0.25.120. SKILLS-dict edits (doombringer_apoc cd + desc). Three numeric edits in crusader_aegis + the orb-strike block. Five numeric edits in doombringer_apoc. +22 / -8 lines.

v0.25.119 Warrior & Warlord pass — Power Strike ×2, Rampage 8 s CD, Banner fires shockwave fan

Three warrior-line tunings bundled. Power Strike was the lowest-damage of the three early warrior actives (Slash / Power Strike / Ground Slam) and felt skippable. Rampage's 20 s cooldown made it more of a panic button than a rotation finisher. Warlord's Banner was a great buff but the cast itself only did one weak AOE pulse; now it also fires a 3-shockwave forward fan so the cast carries real burst on top of the buff.

SkillClass / MasterFieldOldNew
Power StrikeWarrior (S key, slot S)Lane damage mult1.5 / 1.8 / 2.23.0 / 3.6 / 4.4 (×2)
RampageBerserker / Warlord (V)Cooldown20 s8 s (−60%)
Warlord's BannerWarlord (G)Cast effectbuff + 1 AOE pulsebuff + AOE + 3-shockwave fan

Per-skill notes

  • polishPower Strike — all three lanes (high / mid / low) double their damage multiplier. Stuns and hitboxes unchanged. The skill now hits roughly as hard as Slash + Ground Slam combined, justifying the 1.4 s cooldown.
  • polishRampage — cooldown 20 s → 8 s. Active duration is 3 s, so a player on rotation has roughly 5 s of "off" time between casts instead of 17 s. Aligns with the skill's design as a "spinning AOE finisher", not a once-per-fight emergency.
  • featWarlord's Banner — cast keeps the existing buffs (warCry + bloodlust + Warlord-Banner range, heal, pet/pack heal, AOE pulse). On top of those, fires a forward fan of 3 piercing shockwave projectiles at −25° / 0° / +25°, ~270 px reach, damage getAtk() * 1.4 + 12 + stacks * 4. Reads as "the banner plants and a wave of force radiates forward."

Files

  • maple_game.html — version bump to v0.25.119. SKILLS-dict cd edit on rampage. Three lane-damage edits in powerStrike. ~22-line shockwave-fan block added to warlord_warcry. +30 / -5 lines.

v0.25.118 Beastmaster pack lasts 35 s · Skyhunter Gale Storm fires 10 arrows

Two archer master-tier ult buffs bundled together. Both kits felt costed for a brief burst window when their identity wants longer fight presence (Beastmaster's pack as a fight-long companion, Skyhunter's storm as a sustained barrage).

SkillMasterFieldOldNew
Call of the WildBeastmasterWolf life18 s35 s (+94%)
Gale StormSkyhunterArrow count510 (+100%)

Per-skill notes

  • polishCall of the Wild — the 3 summoned wolves now stick around 35 seconds (was 18). Cooldown is 34 s, so a Beastmaster can re-summon as the previous pack expires — effectively permanent wolf company once the rotation lands. Description in SKILLS dict updated.
  • polishGale Storm — arrow count doubled 5 → 10. Each arrow still homes round-robin across visible mobs and fires staggered every 100 ms, so on a single target it lands 10 hits sequentially; on a busy fight 10 homing arrows distribute across all visible mobs. Description updated.

Files

  • maple_game.html — version bump to v0.25.118. Two numeric edits in the skill-effect functions, two SKILLS-dict description tweaks. +12 / -6 lines.

v0.25.117 Archer Z basic locked to single-target (no pierce / explode / split)

User feedback: "make archer z attack only hit 1 target". Basic Z (arrowShot) was inheriting arrowPierce + explosiveArrows tree skills, Sniper job pierce, and Ranger split-chance, so a fully built archer's Z shot was hitting multiple targets and an AOE radius. Z is now strictly single-target regardless of build — pierce / explode / split modifiers still apply to Multi Shot, Charged Shot, and other arrow skills as before.

Implementation

  • polish_singleTarget: true flag — only the basic Z arrow projectile carries this flag. Multi Shot fan and the secondary arrow skills don't set it, so their existing AOE / pierce keeps working.
  • polishFour gates added — arrowPierce tree, explosiveArrows tree, Sniper pEnh.pierce, and Ranger pEnh.splitChance all now check !p._singleTarget before applying. Default 1-target behaviour reaches Z without touching the unrelated AOE-arrow skills.

Files

  • maple_game.html — version bump to v0.25.117. arrowShot() projectile gains the flag; four gate edits in updateProjectiles. +10 / -4 lines.

v0.25.116 Master-tier ult rebalance — Sky Lance bigger, Grand Hex / Soul Vortex hit harder, Cascade cools faster

Bundled four user-requested ult tunings into one balance pass. Three of the four master-tier ultimates were under-performing on impact-per-cast; the fourth (Prismatic Cascade) was costed too heavily for its damage profile. Numbers chosen so each kit feels like an ultimate rather than a long-cooldown filler.

SkillMasterFieldOldNew
Sky LanceDragoonSlam AOE radius180 px280 px (+55%)
Sky LanceDragoonStun column reach200 px320 px (+60%)
Grand HexHexmasterDOT per tick0.08×ATK0.20×ATK (+150%)
Soul VortexLichCooldown80 s30 s (−62.5%)
Soul VortexLichPer-tick ATK0.45×0.80× (+78%)
Prismatic CascadeElementalistCooldown18 s5 s (−72%)

Per-skill notes

  • polishSky Lance — Dragoon's signature leap-and-slam ult had a 180 px radius, smaller than its visual VFX implied. The +55% bump (now 280 px) makes it actually clear a screen of mobs. Stun-column reach grew alongside (200 → 320 px) so the downward spear pierce hits everything inside the AOE.
  • polishGrand Hex — Total DOT damage over the 8 s burn was ~96% of player ATK (12 ticks × 0.08). Now ~240% (12 ticks × 0.20). Reads as ult-tier instead of a flavour DoT.
  • polishSoul Vortex — The 80 s cooldown was prohibitive given the 30 s active window (rotation overlapped with itself only once per 80 s). 30 s cooldown means the vortex can be re-deployed as the previous one expires, giving Lich its identity as a "permanent zone" caster. Per-tick atk jumped 0.45 → 0.80 to match the higher uptime expectation without the new uptime feeling oppressive.
  • polishPrismatic Cascade — Cooldown cut from 18 s to 5 s. Already the lowest-damage of the master ults; the short cooldown turns it into a rotation-staple instead of a once-per-fight finisher.

Files

  • maple_game.html — version bump to v0.25.116. Two SKILLS dict numeric edits (lich_harvest cd, elementalist_cascade cd). Two skill-implementation edits (Sky Lance radius + column reach, Grand Hex burnDmg, Soul Vortex per-tick atk). +11 / -4 lines.

v0.25.115 Three new monster attack types — spread fan, mortar lob, self-enrage

User feedback: "make more different attack types for monster". Added three brand-new MONSTER_SKILL_FNS kinds and wired each onto a previously plain mob so the new patterns appear in regular play.

New skill kinds

  • featspreadFan — 5-projectile cone fired all-at-once across a 45° arc. Distinct from rapidFire (sequential shots); the simultaneous fan forces the player to weave or jump out of the lane.
  • featmortarLob — single arcing projectile, gravity-affected, lands at the player's current position after ~55 frames and detonates in a 60 px AOE. No homing — player dodges by moving toward the caster (overshoots) or perpendicular (underflies). Re-uses the existing selfExplode + explode projectile flags.
  • featenrageSelf — 4-second self-buff. m._selfRageTimer ticks down in updateMonsters; the contact-damage line reads it as a 1.5× multiplier. Telegraphed by an outward red particle ring at cast and trailing red sparks every ~10 frames while active.

Mob assignments

MobTierNew skillCooldown
orange (Sunbun)3spreadFan4800 ms
stump (Stumpy)3mortarLob5200 ms
scorpion (Pincer)3enrageSelf8000 ms

All three were previously plain melee/shooter mobs without specials, so the additions raise the threat ceiling on these tiers without disrupting any existing rotations.

Files

  • maple_game.html — version bump to v0.25.115. Three new entries in MONSTER_SKILL_FNS. Three new entries in MONSTER_SKILLS. Per-frame m._selfRageTimer tick + telegraph particles in the regular-AI block of updateMonsters. Contact-damage line reads m._selfRageTimer for the ×1.5 multiplier. +105 / -1 lines.

v0.25.114 Faster boot — reveal gate trimmed from 16 sprites + HERO_READY to 4 critical sprites

User feedback: "make the loading faster". The old loader gated the reveal on 16 sprites + a HERO_READY event (which itself awaited a Promise.all over 10 hero sprites) with an 8 s hard fallback and a 600 ms fade. On a cold cache this was 3-5 s before the world appeared. Trimmed the gate to the 4 assets the first frame actually needs, kicked off background prefetch for the other 12 in parallel, dropped the HERO_READY wait, and shrank the safety net.

What changed

  • featREQUIRED list trimmed 16 → 4 — only the player's idle + walk sheets and the two starting-town NPCs (Elspeth + Brok) gate the reveal. Anything else loads opportunistically.
  • featBackground prefetch — the other 12 sprites (jump / attack / hit / death / 4 class anims / 4 NPCs) get a fire-and-forget new Image(); .src = â€¦ at boot so they're warm in the browser cache by the time the runtime tries to draw them. They never block the reveal.
  • featHERO_READY wait dropped — maybeReveal used to hold the overlay until HERO_READY fired (after all 10 hero sprites finished decoding). Now it fades the moment the 4 critical sprites are in. Procedural drawCuteCharacter covers the gap if the hero sheets resolve a frame later — in practice they're usually already cached by the time the camera draws.
  • featHard fallback 8000 → 1200 ms — if a critical sprite 404s or stalls, the overlay still tears down in 1.2 s instead of 8 s, falling back to procedural art.
  • polishFade duration 600 → 220 ms — matches the snappier reveal cadence; the slow fade was bottlenecking even fast loads.

Expected speed-up

  • Cold cache, average broadband: previously ~3-5 s, now 0.5-1.5 s typical.
  • Warm cache: previously ~1.5 s (still gated by HERO_READY's Promise.all), now < 300 ms.
  • Slow / flaky network: previously 8 s hard fallback, now 1.2 s.

Files

  • maple_game.html — version bump to v0.25.114. _wireLoadingScreen IIFE rewritten: REQUIRED trimmed, BACKGROUND prefetch added, maybeReveal simplified, fallback + fade timings shortened. +38 / -16 lines.

v0.25.113 Plush / cute UI restyle — rounder corners, soft sheens, bouncy buttons

User feedback: "make it more plush, cute style". Targeted CSS pass on the most-visible modal surfaces — the default .modal shell, inventory tabs, shop rows, shop buttons, and powerup cards. Same dark-purple base palette so existing screens still feel like the same game; the shape language gets softer and the surfaces grow a glossy plushie sheen on top. Lore modal (.modal.lore-modal) keeps its parchment override untouched.

Surface-by-surface

  • polishModal shell — corner radius 10 → 18 px, accent border #8866cc → softer #b389e8, two-layer background (gloss top sheen + diffuse linear). Dropshadow expanded with a 4 px "soft ledge" + inner-top sheen line so the panel reads as a soft sticker resting on the canvas.
  • polishModal h2 + close button — header gets a warm cream tone (#ffe2a3) with a 10 px golden glow. Close button reshaped from a bare × into a 28 px rounded badge with subtle bg, scaling slightly on hover into a peach tint.
  • polishInventory / Character tabs — pill shape (radius 14 px all corners, was 6 6 0 0), active state gets a warm cream gradient + bottom-rim shadow that reads as a "pressed plush". Hover lifts 1 px.
  • polishShop rows — padding bumped 6 → 9 px, radius 4 → 12 px, inner top sheen, hover slides 2 px right. Buttons fully rounded (999 px pill), gradient fill, small drop ledge shadow + inner highlight, bouncy hover/active transforms.
  • polishPowerup cards — corner radius 8 → 16 px, glossy top sheen, drop ledge + inner highlight, bouncier hover (cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.36, 0.64, 1) spring) lifting + slightly scaling. Icon picks up a soft drop-shadow filter.

Notes

  • Pure CSS pass — no JS or markup changes. Existing screens (inventory, character sheet, shop, powerup picker, skill tree, etc.) all pick up the new styling automatically.
  • Lore modal (.modal.lore-modal) explicitly overrides background + border later in the stylesheet, so the plush rework only affects default-shell modals; lore stays parchment.

Files

  • maple_game.html — version bump to v0.25.113. Five CSS rule blocks rewritten in the <style> head: .modal shell + h2 + close-btn, .inv-tab, .shop-item + button, .powerup-card. +86 / -25 lines.

v0.25.112 Mobs flank instead of stacking on the player's pixel

User feedback: monsters cluster at the same pixel location on the player. Cause: every aggroing mob aims for player.x + player.w/2 exactly, so the entire crowd collapses to one stack. Fix: each monster picks a stable per-mob aggro offset (±35..110 px L/R) on first aggro, re-rolled every ~3 s so a long fight has mobs shuffling slots, cleared when the mob drops out of aggro range so re-engagement gets a fresh slot.

What changed

  • featPer-monster m._aggroOffset — randomised ±35..110 px L/R on first aggro. Target X = player.x + player.w/2 + offset, so a wave of three mobs naturally spreads to left, right, and middle slots instead of all converging on one pixel.
  • featRe-roll cadence — m._aggroSettleT ticks every frame; offset re-rolls at > 180 frames (~3 s at 60 fps), so mobs shuffle their flanks during a long fight rather than freezing in formation.
  • polishSettle dead-zone — within 8 px of the chosen slot the mob's vx dampens (× 0.85 / frame) instead of accumulating, so mobs sit at their slot cleanly. Outside the dead zone the original chase-accel kicks in. Within 4 px the facing-flip is skipped so mobs don't oscillate in place.
  • polishCleared on aggro drop — when distToPlayer >= 500 or yDiff >= 200, the offset is wiped (null) so the next aggro round picks a fresh slot.
  • polishAttack ranges unchanged — per-skill attack triggers (typically ≤ 320 px) still fire on a settled mob at ±110 px from the player, so flanking doesn't break melee mob design.

Files

  • maple_game.html — version bump to v0.25.112. Regular-AI chase block in updateMonsters (~6 lines) replaced with per-mob target / settle / dead-zone block (~25 lines). One-line offset-clear added to the out-of-aggro branch. +30 / -5 lines.

v0.25.111 Cleaner anime hair — head-hugging dome + swept bangs + ahoge

User feedback: "the shape is bad". The v0.25.71 procedural hair had two structural problems — the silhouette flared 5–8 px past the head edge on each side (read as horns / wings sticking out at ear level), and the five symmetric forelock strands packed across the forehead like comb teeth with no negative space. Fully redesigned both: tighter head-hugging dome with no side flares, and a single big left-sweep bang plus one right counter-strand instead of five.

Shape changes

  • polishSilhouette no longer flares — old ctrl points pushed bezier nodes to sx−5 on the left and sx+38 on the right, both well past the head's vertical edges (sx .. sx+30). New silhouette stays inside those edges with a gentle inward curve on each side; right side rises a touch higher than the left for an asymmetric swept-crown read.
  • polishFive strands → two bangs — replaced the symmetric 5-strand teardrop fringe with a layered bang structure: small right counter-strand in deep tone for asymmetry, large left-sweep bang in lit tone covering most of the forehead, an inner highlight ribbon hugging its lit edge, and a thin centre cowlick at the part line. Same eye-clearance from v0.25.108 (bang tip at sy+11, eye baseline at sy+14).
  • polishTwo flyaway wisps → one ahoge — the v0.25.71 wisp pair (one antenna sticking up, one fin sticking out the right side) read as noise. Replaced with a single curved cowlick spike on top — classic anime "ahoge" charm-piece.
  • polishCrown ribbon tightened — old gloss ribbon wove all the way across the silhouette; new one stays on the un-banged crown only so it doesn't compete with the bang highlight. Same three white sparkles + bright pip kept for catch-light feel.
  • polishInk outline rewritten — traces the new silhouette path. Picked up a third stroke (thin, rgba 0.55) along the lit edge of the main bang for the "drawn-with-a-brush" double-line feel.

Files

  • maple_game.html — version bump to v0.25.111. Hair block in drawCuteCharacter (~220 lines) rewritten as ~190 lines across the same seven sections (silhouette · mid-shadow · deep cel-shadow · bangs · crown ribbon · ahoge · outline). Class palette unchanged — still pulls cc.hair / hairDeep / hairHi / hairHi2.

v0.25.110 Skill Ranks folded into the Skills tab + projectile-tag bridge for the rank multiplier

The ðŸŽ¯ Skill Ranks tab introduced in v0.25.109 has been merged into the existing ðŸ“– Skills tab so each skill's keybind, MP / CD / unlock level, description, growth chart, and rank state read as one card. The U panel now has three tabs (Level Up Â· Boon Loadout Â· Skills) instead of four. Also fixed: ranks invested in magicBolt / fireball / iceSpike / arrowShot / throwDagger were silently doing nothing because their projectiles tag hits with short tags (bolt / fire / ice / arrow / dagger) that didn't match the SKILL_DEFS id the rank UI lets you invest in. New SKILL_HIT_TAG_TO_DEF_ID bridge resolves that.

UI merge

  • polishOne card per skill — each row in the Skills tab now carries a "RANK" sub-row with the 5-pip strip, a current "+N% dmg" indicator, and the + button. Lv-1→50 growth chart still renders below.
  • polishSkill Points pool header — the cyan badge ("SKILL POINTS Â· N to invest") that lived on the standalone Ranks tab now sits at the top of the unified Skills tab.
  • polishBlock / Parry row stays unchanged (no skill id, no rank). Locked rows render the dimmed "(Slot X — locked)" text with no rank block. Basic-melee slots (warrior slash, rogue stab) show "basic · no rank" to explain why their rank can't be invested.
  • polishTab routing — legacy game._uTab === 'ranks' auto-routes to 'skills' in swapPane() so any reload mid-session still lands on the merged view.

Rank multiplier bug fix

  • bugHit-tag ↔ SKILL_DEFS-id bridge — new SKILL_HIT_TAG_TO_DEF_ID table maps the runtime tags hitMonster actually receives back to the SKILL_DEFS ids the rank UI exposes. getSkillRankMul(tag) now tries direct id match first, falls back to the alias map, then returns 1 + rank * 0.08. Pre-fix, ranks invested in magicBolt / fireball / iceSpike / arrowShot / throwDagger applied +0% damage; post-fix they apply the documented +8% / rank.

Files

  • maple_game.html — version bump to v0.25.110. Tab header trimmed; renderSkillsReference rewritten to absorb rank pips / button / pool header; standalone renderSkillRanks retired (replaced with a one-paragraph stub comment); SKILL_HIT_TAG_TO_DEF_ID + getSkillRankMul alias bridge added near the helpers. +82 / -132 lines (net −50; the merged renderer is shorter than the previous tab + standalone panel).

v0.25.109 Skill Points — per-skill damage rank system + Ability Points relabel

Two currencies now flow from each level-up. The legacy currency (used for tree unlocks + Str/Vit/Agi spending) is renamed to Ability Points — the field player.skillPoints stays put for save compat but every user-facing label now says "Ability Points". A new currency, Skill Points (player.skillRankPoints, player.skillRanks), is granted +1 per level-up and spent in U → ðŸŽ¯ Skill Ranks to upgrade individual class skills with up to 5 ranks each, where each rank adds +8 % damage to that skill (cap +40 % at rank 5).

What landed

  • featNew currency — player.skillRankPoints + player.skillRanks (map of { skillId → rank }) added to PLAYER_SAVE_FIELDS. Granted +1 in levelUp() right next to the existing ability-point grant.
  • featDamage scaling — getSkillRankMul(skillId) returns 1 + rank * 0.08. Applied inside hitMonster after the global rebalance multipliers so the rank scales with the post-rebalance number. Reactive damage tags (melee Â· thorns Â· burn Â· minion Â· pet Â· pack Â· afterimage) are blacklisted via SKILL_RANK_BLACKLIST so basic-attack / passive damage doesn't double-dip.
  • featSkill Ranks tab — new tab in the U Character panel between Level Up and Boon Loadout. Lists every kit skill with a 5-pip rank track and a + button. Sorted by slot order (D / S / A / E / W / Q / C / X). Reactive skills render dimmed with "reactive Â· no rank".
  • polishLabel rename — "SKILL POINTS" pill in the Level Up tab is now "ABILITY POINTS". Mobile toast renamed from "+N Skill Point" to "+N Ability Point". Internal field name player.skillPoints kept for save-file compat.

Tuning

  • Cap 5 ranks per skill, +8 % per rank → max +40 % damage uplift on the most-invested skill.
  • Granted at 1 Skill Point per level. Reaching Lv 50 yields 49 points — enough to fully max ~9-10 skills, or spread thinly across the whole kit.
  • Sub-skills only available at job (Lv 25) / master (Lv 50) advancement still appear in the panel and accept ranks once unlocked, since skillsForClass already filters to the current advancement.

Files

  • maple_game.html — version bump to v0.25.109. Player init + save schema, SKILL_RANK_CAP / getSkillRank / getSkillRankMul helpers, level-up grant, hitMonster multiplier, U-panel tab markup + new renderSkillRanks(host) function, label rename in two places. +131 / -8 lines.

v0.25.108 Hair clears the eyes + mage 2nd / 3rd job cooldown rebalance

Two pointed tweaks. (1) The procedural fringe in drawCuteCharacter had three forelock strand tips landing at or below the eye baseline (sy + 14), so the mid-fringe read as "covering the eyes" â€” fixed by lifting all five strand tipY values to the sy + 9..11 range (entirely above the brow line) without touching the silhouette / mid-shadow / gloss-ribbon / wisp passes. (2) Mage advanced-job cooldowns were noticeably stiffer than warrior / rogue / archer 2nd-job kits despite mage relying on rotation instead of basic attacks â€” shaved ~30 % off every archmage / warlock / master mage skill so the rotation has a tempo closer to the other classes.

Hair fix

  • bugForelock strand tips — old tipY: 11 / 14 / 15 / 13 / 11; new: 9 / 10 / 11 / 10 / 9. Centre-longest strand still reads as the dominant strand, just no longer dips into the eye box.

Mage cooldowns

SkillTierOld CDNew CD
meteorarchmage (2nd)7000 ms5000 ms
elemental (Elemental Convergence)archmage ult21000 ms14000 ms
soulSiphonwarlock (2nd)7000 ms5000 ms
darkPulsewarlock ult19000 ms13000 ms

v0.25.107 Hero animation pass — expressive face, state colour rim, anticipation-driven swing

Layered four small visual upgrades onto the procedural drawCuteCharacter rig without touching the existing body / hair / cel-shading passes. Same offset principle as the body bob — new state reads (jump, hit, attack, low HP, buff, crit) drive small per-frame deltas over the existing draw stack instead of branching on a different sprite.

  • featEyebrows — hidden by default. Three expressive states: arched-up on jump (airborne with |vy| > 0.6), furrowed \\/ on hitStun > 80, focused down-inward while attacking. Drawn after the eyes on the upper-eye line so they read at chibi scale.
  • featHit-stun mouth — replaced the v0.25.66 surprised "o" with a 5-segment zigzag stroke. Reads as a real "ouch!" bite instead of a passive surprise.
  • featAttack mouth gape — the open-mouth ellipse during a swing now scales with swingBase (the bell curve), so the gape grows at mid-swing and snaps shut on follow-through. Tongue strip lengthens with it.
  • featLow-HP red rim — pulsing additive ellipse rim around the body when HP < 30 %, with a harder/faster pulse below 15 %. Painted as a top layer after everything else — survives any palette / equipment changes.
  • featBuff accent rim — subtle pulsing rim while a buff is active. Gold for warCry / rampage / holy, red for bloodlust, ice-blue for holyShield.
  • featCrit gold flash — hitMonster stamps player.lastCritT = game.time on every crit. drawCuteCharacter paints a 280 ms gold halo (additive) over the body silhouette and fades it out with a slight outward growth.
  • polishAnticipation-driven swing — the body lean (swingAng) and the blade angle (drawCuteSwingingBlade) both swapped from a single easeOutBack bell to a piecewise three-phase curve: 0–18 % anticipation (body + blade pull BACK), 18–55 % strike (cubic ease-out snap to the peak), 55–100 % follow-through (damped wobble). Sells the wind-up → impact → settle rhythm that the old single-curve swing was missing.

Files

  • maple_game.html — version bump to v0.25.107. hitMonster stamps player.lastCritT. drawCuteCharacter picks up the eyebrow block, mouth-zigzag/gape variants, and a state-colour top-layer block. drawCuteSwingingBlade + the swing-state block in drawCuteCharacter use a new piecewise lean/angle curve. +118 / -22 lines.

v0.25.106 Removed excess post-FX (aurora / bloom-halo / grain shimmer / god-rays / leaf frame)

User feedback: the world had too many overlay effects competing with the actual art. Default art-FX mode flipped from 'soft' to 'off', and the two god-ray render paths gated so they skip when disabled. Backdrops and characters now render clean. Power users can still opt back in via ?artfx=soft URL param or localStorage.setItem('levelx_art_fx', 'soft').

  • Aurora colour-grade — drawArtPostFX early-returns on !_ART_FX_ENABLED (already gated), so the gold/teal/violet gradient wash is gone.
  • Bloom halo — same gate skips the radial-gradient bloom drawn over the top of the screen.
  • Grain shimmer — same gate skips the noise-pattern overlay.
  • God-ray sweep — both render paths now check _ART_FX_ENABLED: the daytime cinematic shafts in drawBackground and the screen-composite rays in drawWorldArtOverlay.
  • Foreground leaf frame — gated in drawWorldArtOverlay; the painterly leaves at screen edges now skip with the rays.
  • Mid-ground fog — KEPT (it's responsible for legibility/depth, not "excess flair").

v0.25.105 Pillar sprites regenerated with transparent backgrounds

User feedback: "for the pillars remove the background colour to make it transparent." The original pillar_sanctum_a / pillar_crypt_a sprites baked a dark-purple solid into the canvas, which read as a flat panel behind every pillar in-game. Regenerated both via ludo.createImage with image_type: 'sprite' + emphatic "isolated cutout, transparent alpha channel" prompt — new files have a clean transparent backdrop and read as standalone props.

  • pillar_sanctum_a.webp — cream-and-gold marble shaft with a magenta crystal orb capital, on transparent. (Was 762 KB → now 118 KB — the missing background was most of the byte cost.)
  • pillar_crypt_a.webp — cracked grey-blue stone with green moss patches and ghostly cyan crack-glow, on transparent. (Was 818 KB → now 186 KB.)
  • drawSanctumCosmos + drawBossDecor render paths unchanged — they already used ctx.drawImage(_pillarImg, …) which respects alpha. Pillars now composite cleanly over whatever bg the boss arena renders.

v0.25.104 Renamed to Mojiworld + polished loading screen

Game brand renamed from LevelX to Mojiworld across every user-facing string (page title, version-tag tooltip, level-up tab badge, rotate-to-landscape nag, loading-screen headline, README, both changelog headers). The loading screen got a top-to-bottom polish pass to feel like a proper title moment.

Rename touchpoints

  • <title> tags in maple_game.html, CHANGELOG.html, MOBILE_CHANGELOG.html.
  • Loading-overlay <h1> headline + document.title level-up badge + version-tag tooltip + rotate-nag prose + README header + both changelog eyebrows.
  • Save data preserved: localStorage keys (levelx_save_v1, levelx_phaser, levelx_art_pack, levelx_mp_*) intentionally NOT renamed — keeping them frozen means existing player saves still load on the rebrand.
  • GitHub repo URLs in the changelog footer left alone — repo name is still LevelX on github.com so those links would 404 if rewritten.

Loading screen polish (CSS-only, no JS perf cost)

  • Drifting starfield — pseudo-element ::before with 8 layered radial-gradient stars, animated via lo-drift 60 s linear translate. Genuine motion without canvas/JS work.
  • Title halo — ::after 460 px gradient breath (4.2 s ease-in-out) sits behind the headline; pulsing scale + opacity gives the logo presence.
  • Logo typography — MOJIWORLD rendered with a 4-stop gradient (cream → amber → epic-purple → sky-blue) clipped to text via -webkit-background-clip. 48 px / 800 weight / +3 px letter-spacing.
  • Bobbing emoji (✨) above the title with rotation + drop-shadow glow at 3.8 s ease-in-out.
  • Progress bar — glassy 8 px track with inset shadow, 5-stop rainbow fill that shimmers continuously via lo-shimmer 2.4 s linear. Width transition uses cubic-bezier(.2,.8,.3,1) for a satisfying snap to each new percentage.
  • Status text rotates through 5 phrases as load progresses ("Conjuring sprites…" → "Painting biomes…" → "Awakening monsters…" → "Tuning skill VFX…" → "Aligning the celestial spire…") with a live NN% readout. On done: green "Ready ✓".
  • Tip strip — bottom-of-screen line teaches the new keybinds (W world map, J quests).
  • Fade-out upgraded from 0.45 s linear to 0.6 s + 1.02× scale-up so the splash gracefully retreats instead of just vanishing.
  • Mobile media query at <600 px tightens the headline + bar width.

v0.25.103 Celestial Spire angels — stat boost + 3 new tricky AI passives

User feedback: "make the 3 monsters very hard and tricky to battle." Stats sharply boosted (cherub +63% HP, seraph +66% HP, archon +100% HP and +37% ATK), plus three new passive behaviors hooked into the per-monster update loop that give each angel a distinct combat identity.

Stat boosts

MobHP (was → now)ATKDEFEvasionOther
Cherub920 → 150064 → 909 → 11130 → 170+33% rewards
Seraph1750 → 290096 → 13515 → 24120 → 130shootCd 2000 → 1700
Archon3400 → 6800135 → 18522 → 36105 → 115shootCd 2300 → 2000

3 new passive AI behaviors

MobBehaviorTriggerEffect
CherubBlink-teleportPlayer within 80 px, 5%/frameWarps 80–140 px to opposite side of player; 14-frame i-frames; particle pop at both endpoints
SeraphHeal auraEvery 90 framesHeals self + nearby angels (180 px radius) for 3% of their maxHp; floating green +N numbers + green pulse ring
ArchonEnrage at 50% HPOne-shot flagPermanent: shootCd × 0.55, speed × 1.3, halves any pending skill timer; toast prompt + continuous golden ember trail; flash + shake on transition

Combat identity

  • Cherub — annoying gnat. 170 evasion already eats hits; the blink turns "I almost cornered it" into "where did it go?". De-prioritise.
  • Seraph — support priority. Group fights become a brick wall if seraphs heal-tick before you DPS the cherubs/archon down. Take her out first.
  • Archon — boss-grade. The first half is a heavy melee/sword fight. The 50% threshold is the danger spike — skill-spam doubles, summons re-trigger faster, and you eat cherub adds on top of the buffed sword routine.

v0.25.102 😇 Celestial Spire — ascending angel tower with 3 angel-tier mobs

A narrow vertical-feeling tower map (960 px wide, 6 tiers of zig-zagging platforms from ground to peak) wedged between the existing endgame biomes. Three new angel-tier mobs guard the climb â€” chubby cherubs swarm the lower tiers, golden seraphs cast divine bolts mid-tower, and the gold-armored archon at the peak summons cherub reinforcements.

The map (Celestial Spire, Lv 30+, taxi-accessible)

  • Layout: 19 platforms across 6 ascending tiers (y=420 â†’ y=120). Each tier offsets horizontally, forcing zig-zag jumping. Single wide cap at the peak (280 px) where archons spawn.
  • Backdrop: reuses the meadow sky-kingdom hero art (bg: 'meadow') with a divine pastel sky.
  • Single back-portal to Everdawn Bazaar. World map shows it under Endgame with the ðŸ˜‡ icon.

3 new angel-tier mobs (sprites in Sprites/monsters/)

MobTierHPATKDEFSpeed/JumpSpecial
ðŸ‘¼ Cherub59206491.3 / 5Floating melee with 130 evasion
âœ¨ Seraph6175096150.9 / 0Floating, fires mdark bolts; homingShot on 4.4 s CD
âš” Archon7+3400135221.0 / 4Sword msplinter, summon on 11 s CD â€” spawns 2 full-strength cherubs (special-cased)
  • Cherub + Seraph added to FLOATING_TYPES so the renderer hovers them above the foot line.
  • summon() generalised to special-case m.type === 'archon': spawns full-strength cherubs (instead of mini-self) since cherubs are already the floor mob and don't need scaling down.

Roster milestones

  • Total monster roster: 32 mobs across tiers 1â€“7+ (was 29 after v0.25.99).
  • Total maps: 25 (was 24 after v0.25.98).
  • Total monster-special inventory: 10 distinct mechanics â€” Archon's summon-cherub variant rides on the existing summon kind.

v0.25.101 3 more monster skills + 2 VFX sprites (frost beam Â· gravity well)

Continuing the elemental mob expansion: frostkin, stoneling, and skywisp each gain a signature special with brand-new mechanics. Two ludo-generated VFX sprites paint the new hazards; procedural fallbacks render if the images don't load.

New monster skills

SkillMobCDEffect
â„ freezeBeamFrostkin (ice)5.8 sWide horizontal frost cone, 280 px reach, locked to monster's facing at cast. 24-frame charge â†’ 36-frame active. On hit: damage + 1.5 s movement-slow (50% speed penalty via player._slowTimer).
ðŸª¨ summonStoneling (rock)9.0 sSpawns 2 mini-stonelings beside the caster. Each minion is 60% size, 40% HP, 50% atk; specials disabled (_mskTimer = 9e9). Throwaway pressure rather than free DPS.
ðŸŒ¬ gravityWellSkywisp (wind)6.0 s3-second swirling vortex on the ground that pulls the player horizontally toward the cast point. Pull strength scales inversely with distance (close = strong). Light contact damage every 30 frames. Player can resist by jumping out of the radius.

VFX sprites (2 PNGs in Sprites/vfx/)

  • frost_beam.webp â€” pale-blue cone with jagged edges. Drawn along the hazard box, mirrored when h.dir < 0. Telegraph fades in during charge; strike phase pulses at full alpha.
  • gravity_well.webp â€” purple swirling spiral on transparent background. Continuously rotated by (game.time * 0.04) radians so the swirl visually spins.
  • New LX_VFX preloader at script bootstrap with _lxVfxReady() guard. Hazard render paths use drawImage when ready and fall back to procedural shapes otherwise.

Plumbing

  • Two new hazard types â€” mob_frostbeam and mob_gravity â€” with full update + render + damage logic.
  • player._slowTimer wired into getSpeed() (50% multiplier) and decremented in the player update tick alongside _poisonTimer / frozenTimer.
  • All three skills reuse the existing MONSTER_SKILLS dispatch (cooldown timer + range gate + aggroTarget) â€” same lifecycle as shockwave / rapidFire / lightningStrike.
  • Total monster-special inventory: 10 distinct mechanics across 13 mobs (was 7 mechanics before).

v0.25.100 Projectile sprites + 3 new monster attack mechanisms

Hand-painted PNG sprite versions of the 4 main monster projectile archetypes replace the procedural canvas blits when loaded. Three new boss-grade special attacks (shockwave / rapidFire / lightningStrike) wired to the elemental mob trio, each with its own hazard render + damage logic.

Projectile sprites (4 PNGs in Sprites/projectiles/)

ArchetypeUsed bySprite keyBehaviour
msporemushroom, orange, cookie, honeyBuzz, emberlinglx_mspore_spriteCluster â€” axis-aligned
mdarkhorny, mummy, wraith, coralImp, nimbusFox, voltipup, sandhusklx_mdark_spriteMagenta arrow â€” rotates with velocity
msplinterstump, skeleton, frostkin, stonelinglx_msplinter_spriteWood spike â€” rotates with velocity
mtoxiczombie, froglx_mtoxic_spriteGreen glob â€” axis-aligned
  • Loaded async in LXProjectileScene.preload(); baked to 64Ã—64 canvas via addCanvas on img.onload.
  • textureForProj prefers *_sprite when its texture exists, falls through to procedural canvas otherwise â€” game keeps rendering even if sprites 404 or fail to decode.
  • _texBaseSize + _rotatesWithVelocity updated to know about the new keys.

New monster attack mechanisms

SkillMobCDEffect
shockwaveSandhusk (rock/desert)5.0 sRadial ring expands from monster over 50 frames; sweeps damage outward to maxRadius=150. Single-hit so a stationary player only eats one tick.
rapidFireEmberling (fire)3.6 s4-shot spore volley over ~20 frames. Each shot tracks player at fire-time with a small fan spread, so movement breaks the volley.
lightningStrikeVoltipup (electric)4.2 sVertical lightning pillar at the player's column. 30-frame telegraph (pulsing yellow line on ground + ! marker), then a 60-frame strike with jagged bolt + 400 ms stun on hit.
  • Two new hazard types â€” mob_shockwave and mob_lightning â€” with full render (drawHazards) + update (game.hazards loop) + damage application logic.
  • Telegraph particles burst at cast on every skill so the player has a clear visual prompt.
  • All three reuse the existing MONSTER_SKILLS dispatch (cooldown timer + range gate + aggroTarget check); only the function bodies and skill kinds are new.
  • flash() + addShake() on every hit so the screen sells the impact.

v0.25.99 4 more elemental mobs â€” fire / ice / wind / desert biome signatures

The four climate-themed maps that previously spawned only generic mobs now have signature creatures. Each new mob plugs a specific gap in the existing biomes â€” same chibi-cel-shaded ludo.ai style as the v0.25.98 quartet.

Monsters (sprites in Sprites/monsters/)

ðŸŒ¬ SkywispSky Garden (Lv 5+)2240263Floating, very evasive (140), no melee jump

Wiring

  • Each new mob is now the lead spawn (count 5-6) of its native biome â€” sandhusk pushed scorpion + mummy down to support roles in Dune Sands, etc.
  • Registered in MONSTER_SPRITE_TYPES auto-loader and monsterTypes stats table.
  • Bestiary / quest hooks pick them up automatically (no per-mob wiring needed).
  • Total monster roster: 29 mobs across tiers 1â€“7 (was 21 before v0.25.98).

v0.25.98 Elemental quartet â€” 4 new themed maps + 4 new monsters (water / grass / electricity / rock)

Four new taxi-accessible maps slot into the early-mid level progression, each with a signature ludo.ai-generated chibi monster matching the theme. Tab opens the world map; the new biomes appear in the same hash-bucketed tier groups as everything else.

  • Layouts use 2400â€“2700 px wide rooms with 13â€“16 platforms scattered across 3 vertical tiers â€” comparable to the existing forest / swamp pacing.
