  • Bestiary / quest hooks pick them up automatically since they live in monsterTypes.

UI

  • _WM_BIOME_ICON extended with the 4 new biome IDs so each map gets its themed emoji on the world-map cards (ðŸŒ¼ / ðŸŒŠ / ðŸª¨ / âš¡).
  • The four new maps automatically slot into the world-map tier groups: Beginner (Wildflower), Intermediate (Tidal Lagoon, Granite Bluffs), Advanced (Thunder Plateau).

v0.25.97 Defensive guards â€” a thrown drawImage can't kill the render loop anymore

User reported "main character sprites does not appear" after the v0.25.94â€“96 art pass. Most likely cause: an unhandled drawImage throw inside drawPlatforms() (tainted canvas from CORS, or a corner-case argument) propagates up through loop() â†’ kills the requestAnimationFrame chain â†’ no further rendering, including the player. Confirmed via direct probe: drawPlayer() renders correctly when called manually, so the player path itself is fine; what's missing is the loop continuing to call it.

Fixes

  • _lxDrawTiledRow now wraps its drawImage loop in a try/catch and returns false on failure. First failure logs a single console.warn for diagnosis, subsequent failures stay silent.
  • drawPlatforms only takes the continue shortcut when the image actually painted. If _lxDrawTiledRow returned false the procedural fallback runs in the same iteration, so platforms always render even if the new tile assets misbehave.
  • drawSanctumCosmos and drawBossDecor wrap their pillar ctx.drawImage calls in try/catch. Procedural shaft + cap fallback is now reachable when the image path is the broken one.
  • Extra zero-dimension guard in _lxDrawTiledRow (sw <= 0 / w <= 0) for paranoia â€” these would have thrown INDEX_SIZE_ERR in some browsers.

v0.25.96 Floor tile gap fix â€” bake seamless single-tile crop at image load

The grass+dirt floor was showing gaps between repeats. Root cause: the source image has ~88 px of transparent padding on the left and ~94 px on the right; tiling the whole image meant every repeat started with that transparent edge, producing visible see-through bands across the platform. Same shape for the wood image, which is a 4Ã—2 plank grid that was getting squashed into a 16 px tile.

Fix

  • New _lxBakeSeamlessTile(img, mode) helper. Two modes:
    • 'auto' â€” sample alpha across 3 Y rows, find the leftmost contiguous opaque column run, crop to that range Ã— full image height. Used for floorGrass: 1152Ã—864 source â†’ 970Ã—864 baked tile (88 + 94 px of transparent padding stripped).
    • 'grid:CxR' â€” crop the top-left cell of an explicit C-column Ã— R-row layout. Used for floorWood: 1152Ã—864 source â†’ 288Ã—432 baked tile (one plank instead of the whole 4Ã—2 grid).
  • Pillar assets stay untouched ('none' mode) â€” they're drawn whole, not tiled.
  • _lxTileReady + _lxNatW/H updated so the helper accepts HTMLCanvasElement as well as HTMLImageElement. Baked canvases are dropped into LX_TILES in place of the original image after load completes.
  • Verified with browser probe: floor_grass_b bakes to 970Ã—864 (was 1152Ã—864 with bands of transparent padding), floor_wood_b bakes to 288Ã—432 (was 1152Ã—864 4Ã—2 grid).

v0.25.95 World map modal â€” press W to see every reachable map

New ðŸ—º World Map modal opens on W. Cards are grouped into 7 tiers (Hub / Beginner / Intermediate / Advanced / Endgame / Boss Arenas / Tower) and the player's current location is highlighted. Clicking an unlocked, non-current card fast-travels via loadMap(id). (Note: the in-skill-bar "slot 5 = w" naming refers to an internal slot id; the literal W keyboard key is unbound â€” KEY_TO_SLOT binds skills to z/x/s/c/d/f/v/g.)

UX

  • W opens / closes the modal â€” same toggle pattern as J (Quests), U (Attributes), K (Skills).
  • Cards show biome icon, name, level requirement, and one of â–¸ YOU ARE HERE (current map, gold border) or ðŸ”’ Need Lv X (level-gated, dimmed + cursor:not-allowed).
  • Hover lifts the card 1 px; click closes all modals + calls loadMap(id) + shows a Travelling toast.
  • Same lifecycle as the Quest Journal: pauses the game while open, requires class + alive.

Coverage

  • 17 of 20 reachable maps are bucketed automatically by their levelReq: Hub (town), Beginner (forest, mushroom, candyCanyon, sunsetBeach), Intermediate (slimeCave, skyGarden, ancient, frozenPeak, lavaCavern, bubblegumSwamp), Advanced (duneSands, cryptHollow, honeycombHollow, sanctum), Endgame (stardustAtrium, gravitosArena, pearlBathhouse), Boss Arenas (boss/Queen's Hollow), Tower (carriage of ascension).
  • Zodiac maps + zodiac hub + carriage-internal scenes are filtered out â€” those are gated through other UI (zodiac portal in town, taxi NPC).

v0.25.94 Hand-painted tiles â€” floors, pillars, and 5 new map backdrops

Replaces procedural canvas art with hand-painted bitmaps in three places: the grass+dirt ground band, the boss-arena pillars (sanctum + crypt), and every map biome backdrop. Procedural code stays in place as a fallback â€” if any image fails to load (404, fetch error, decode error) the legacy fillRect/arc path takes over, so the game always renders.

Floors + pillars (ludo.ai, 4 prompts Ã— 2 variants)

  • 8 PNGs saved to backgrounds/tiles/: floor_grass_{a,b}, floor_wood_{a,b}, pillar_sanctum_{a,b}, pillar_crypt_{a,b}. Currently wired: the *_b grass + wood (more uniform) and *_a pillars (more ornate).
  • New LX_TILES preloader at script bootstrap with an _lxTileReady() guard and an _lxDrawTiledRow() helper that preserves source aspect â€” chibi flowers and plank seams stay readable instead of getting squashed when the platform is short.
  • drawPlatforms(): type:'ground' tiles floor_grass_b across the width with a 9 px lift so grass tufts protrude above p.y like the legacy art. type:'platform' renders floor_wood_b behind the existing dark OUT halo so it still reads against busy backdrops.
  • drawSanctumCosmos() + drawBossDecor(): pillars use pillar_sanctum_a / pillar_crypt_a (display height 314 px, locked aspect). The bobbing magenta crystal-orb glow and flickering torch flame still draw on top of either render path so the animation keeps popping.

5 new map backdrops (ChatGPT-rendered, 1672Ã—941)

  • 5 hero PNGs dropped into backgrounds/New background/ (1.png â€“ 5.png). Copied into the main backgrounds/ folder as bg_v2_{forest,valley,meadow,misty,homestead,dungeon}.png so paths stay simple and the originals are preserved.
  • Aesthetic mapping: 2 â†’ forest (lush day), 1 â†’ valley + homestead + main (mountain ruins, also reused for town village vibe), 4 â†’ meadow (sky kingdom), 3 â†’ misty (mystic night with glowing mushrooms), 5 â†’ dungeon (crypt with chains + torches).
  • Both bg loaders updated in lockstep:
    • Phaser LXBackgroundScene.preload() â€” 6 textures including the new bg_dungeon key.
    • Vanilla BG_IMAGES â€” 6 keys + main aliasing the valley image.
  • _BG_CYCLE and Phaser's pickBgKey cycle both extended from 4 â†’ 5 entries (forest, valley, meadow, misty, dungeon). Non-town maps with no explicit md.bg override now reach all 5 backdrops via the existing map-id hash. Boss/eerie maps can opt into the dungeon look explicitly with md.bg = 'dungeon'.

Verification

  • node --check on the extracted <script> block â€” syntax OK after every edit.
  • All 4 ludo tiles fetch with HTTP 200 and img.decode() succeeds; recorded natural sizes: floors 1152Ã—864, pillars 768Ã—1344.
  • All 6 new bg textures show _loaded:true with naturalWidth:1672, naturalHeight:941; center-pixel paint test confirms biome-appropriate hues (forest green [136,191,132], valley sky-blue [121,183,225], misty deep blue [51,81,183], dungeon dark gray [27,44,55]).

v0.25.93 Ground anchoring fix â€” characters now actually touch the floor (8 floating types lifted)

Bug fix + new feature. Generated sprites consistently have 8-19% empty padding below the character â€” the old -targetH Ã— 0.95 hard-coded anchor put canvas-bottom near feet, leaving most monsters floating 5-15 px above the ground. Now the renderer detects each sprite's actual bbox-bottom at load and anchors THAT to the foot line, with a small ground bleed for grounded mobs and a deliberate lift for the 8 floating types.

Auto-detect bbox-bottom per sprite

  • New _detectSpriteBboxBottom(img) walks the sprite's pixels bottom-up to find the lowest opaque pixel Y.
  • Stored at load time in MONSTER_SPRITE_META / BOSS_SPRITE_META / ZODIAC_SPRITE_META caches.
  • Sample fills (out of 768-tall canvases): snail 81% Â· slime 85% Â· mushroom 92% Â· skeleton 90% Â· wraith 91% Â· nimbusFox 87% Â· pearlSprite 86%.

Floating type set

FLOATING_TYPES = {
  honeyBuzz, wraith, nimbusFox, pearlSprite,  // mobs that hover
  aetherion,                                   // cosmic dragon (boss)
};
FLOATING_ZODIAC = { gemini, virgo, pisces };  // star spirits / angel / koi

Anchor formula

// Position so the character's bbox-bottom lands on the foot line:
const dyOffset = -((bboxBottomY + 1) / srcH) * targetH;
// Then add a small offset:
//   ground monsters: + targetH Ã— 0.02 (2 % bury below feet, "pressed into ground")
//   floating types:  - targetH Ã— 0.18 (18 % lift, character hovers above feet)

Bonus fix

  • Boss facing convention: _drawBossSprite was mirroring on m.facing > 0 (backwards) â€” bosses faced the wrong way going right. Now matches monsters: mirror when facing < 0.
  • Aspect-aware boss scale: bosses no longer forced to square aspect; they preserve the source aspect ratio.

v0.25.92 23 new sprites â€” full mob roster + 4 bosses + all 12 zodiac + atmospheric haze removed

Big sprite content drop: every remaining monster, boss, and zodiac sign now has a ludo.ai chibi sprite, all style-anchored to the existing coralImp via generateWithStyle for consistent feel. Plus the translucent atmospheric haze overlay is removed per user request.

Translucent overlay removal

  • The 18%-opacity dark-violet haze (rgba(20,24,48,0.18) fillRect over the whole screen each frame) is gone. Backgrounds + characters now render at full vibrance.

Sprite content (~30 credits)

  • 7 missing monsters (Sprites/monsters/): honeyBuzz Â· mummy Â· zombie Â· wraith Â· nimbusFox Â· cosmicMochi Â· pearlSprite. Brings the wired monster roster to 21 / 22 types covering all tiers 1â€“7. Generated via createImage with explicit MapleStory-chibi style prompts.
  • 4 main bosses (Sprites/bosses/): king (King Slime crowned) Â· mushmom (mama mushroom + babies) Â· aetherion (cosmic dragon) Â· gravitos (gravity warlord). Generated via generateWithStyle referencing coralImp for cohesive style.
  • All 12 zodiac bosses (Sprites/zodiac/): aries Â· taurus Â· gemini Â· cancer Â· leo Â· virgo Â· libra Â· scorpio Â· sagittarius Â· capricorn Â· aquarius Â· pisces. Generated via generateWithStyle.

Runtime â€” boss/zodiac dispatch

  • New BOSS_SPRITES + ZODIAC_SPRITES caches alongside MONSTER_SPRITES. Loaded from Sprites/bosses/<type>.webp and Sprites/zodiac/<sign>.webp at module init.
  • drawMonster dispatch now checks the sprite caches first. If a sprite is loaded â†’ uses _drawBossSprite (renders at 2Ã— bbox-h to fit boss aura). If not â†’ falls back to existing procedural draws (drawKingSlime, drawMushmom, drawAetherion, drawGravitos, drawZodiacBoss).
  • Failure-safe â€” if any sprite fails to load (network, missing file), the procedural path takes over with no error.

Style consistency note

  • Per user feedback "ensure the general feel of the sprites are similar", the bosses + zodiac were generated via generateWithStyle (not createImage) using the existing coralImp sprite as the style reference. Output sprites inherit coralImp's line weight, palette saturation, and 4-tone cel shading.

v0.25.91 Head-Y locked at 49 across every frame in every state (range = 0 px)

Switched walk to head-anchor too. Head Y is now exactly 49 across all 16 walk frames + idle + 16 jump + 16 attack + 4 class skill anims = 57+ frames, 0 px head-Y range. Truly locked.

What changed

  • v0.25.90 had walk on feet anchor â€” head bobbed 49 â†’ 67 across the cycle (18 px). Natural for walking but the user wanted head fully locked.
  • Switched walk to head anchor in HERO_META. Now head is at canvas Y=49 in every walk frame.
  • Visual change: legs alternate below the locked head; body/torso may shift slightly but head stays put.

Verified

  • Probed 27+ frames across walk/idle/jump/attack/class skills.
  • min head Y = 49, max head Y = 49, range = 0 px.
  • Centroid X stays within 1 px (319-321) across all states.

v0.25.90 Per-anim anchor strategy â€” head-top for in-air / asymmetric, feet for ground

Identified and fixed a 500+ pixel head-drop on attack and jump-apex frames. Switched in-air and asymmetric-pose animations to head-top anchor (head locked at idle's head-Y across every frame). Walk/idle stay on feet anchor.

The remaining drift

  • v0.25.89's centroid-X anchor fixed horizontal wobble. But VERTICAL drift remained on poses where bbox-bottom isn't actually the feet â€” jump-apex (legs tucked, bbox-bottom = back/butt) and attack-extension (forward lean, bbox-bottom = lower-than-standing).
  • Attack frames 4-10 had bh~250 (vs idle's 771) due to the AI drawing a forward-leaning compressed pose. Feet-anchor placed those at canvas Y=565 â€” head DROPPED 516 px from idle's Y=49.
  • Jump-apex tuck had bh=159 â†’ head dropped to Y=660.

Fix

  • New per-anim anchor field in HERO_META: 'feet' (default â€” bbox-bottom â†’ idleFootY) or 'head' (bbox-top â†’ idleHeadTopY).
  • Anchor assignments: walk + idle = feet (correct â€” bbox-bottom is the planted foot). Jump / attack / hit / death / class skills (warrior/rogue/mage/archer) = head.
  • Runtime computes HERO.idleHeadTopY = idleAnchor.minY at load and passes it through to _heroExtractAndNormalize.

Verified â€” head Y locked at 49 across all in-air / asymmetric states

Statehead Ycxnote
idle49320reference
walk[0/8]58/67321/321feet anchor â€” natural step bob
jump[0]49321head anchor
jump[8] (apex tuck)49 â† was 660!320head anchor
attack[0/4/8/15]49/49/49/49321/321/320/321head anchor â€” was 304/573/565/306
warrior[0/2]49/50321/321head anchor

v0.25.89 Pixel-perfect cross-frame sync â€” centroid anchor + uniform per-sheet scale

Replaces bbox-center horizontal anchoring with centroid of opaque pixels (mass center, dominated by torso + head â€” barely shifts when a thin arm extends). Plus a uniform per-sheet scale instead of per-frame so tucked-leg / curled poses stay proportionally smaller (correct) instead of being stretched to match the tallest frame.

The bug

  • Bbox-center anchoring put bbox horizontal mid-point at canvas center. For asymmetric poses â€” like the punch frame where the right arm extends right â€” the bbox extends right, so the body had to shift LEFT to "center" the bbox. Result: the head visibly wobbled across frames in the same animation.
  • Measured drift in attack[2] (mid-punch): bbox-center X = 337, vs idle's 320 â†’ 17 px wobble.

The fix

  • New _findChibiAnchor returns centroid X (alpha-weighted mass center) plus the bbox + feet.
  • Animation normalizer maps each frame's centroid to canvas-center X. Centroid only shifts slightly under thin-limb extensions, so body axis stays stable.
  • Per-sheet uniform sheetFitScale = idleBh / maxBhInSheet. Tallest pose in a sheet matches idle's height; shorter poses (tucked legs at jump apex, curled body during punch wind-up) stay proportionally smaller. Natural body bob during walk preserved instead of vertically stretched away.
  • Idle uses the same centroid + feet anchors with scale=1, so it aligns to animation frames by the same body-axis reference.

Verification

Framecentroid Xfeet Ybbox hbbox w
idle320819771406
walk[0/4/8/12]321/321/321/321819/819/819/819762/753/753/756~407
jump[3] (launch)320819512279
jump[8] (apex tuck)320819159191
attack[2] (punch out)321819507134
attack[8] (recover)320819255265

Centroid X stays within 1 px tolerance across every state and every pose. Feet Y identical. The natural bbox h/w variations now correctly reflect pose differences (curled vs extended) rather than artifacts of normalization.

v0.25.88 Smoother jump (16 frames) â€” idle/walk/jump/attack all fully smooth

Jump regenerated with 16 frames at Eagle quality. Combined with v0.25.87, every primary motion state (idle, walk, jump, attack) now plays at 16-frame fluidity with pixel-perfect cross-state positioning.

Smoother jump

  • 9 â†’ 16 frames (4Ã—4 grid, Eagle, 2 s source, 14 fps in-game).
  • Phases now properly subdivide: crouch prep â†’ spring up â†’ arms-up at peak â†’ tucked legs at apex â†’ smooth descent â†’ land/recover.
  • Cost: ~5 credits (animateSprite Eagle 2s).

Positioning verified

Statebbox hfeet Ycenter X
idle771819320
walk[8]771819320
jump[3]771819320
jump[8]771819320
attack[8]771819320

Identical to the pixel across every state. No horizontal pop on any transition.

v0.25.87 Smoother walk + attack (16 frames each) + perfect cross-state positioning

Walk and attack animations regenerated at 16 frames each (vs 9) for almost 2Ã— the position updates per cycle. Plus a hidden 19 px horizontal drift between idle and walking states is now fixed â€” every animation state shares the same character size + position to the pixel.

Smoother animations (~10 credits)

  • Walk: 9 â†’ 16 frames (4Ã—4 grid, Eagle, 2 s source, 12 fps in-game). Smoother leg-step transitions and arm swing arcs.
  • Attack: 9 â†’ 16 frames (4Ã—4 grid, 18 fps). Snappier punch with smoother wind-up and recovery in-between frames.
  • Both regenerated via animateSprite with model:"eagle"; cost ~5 credits each.

Cross-state positioning fix

  • Bug: ludo's source idle had the character drawn ~19 px right of canvas center (centerX=339 vs the 640-wide canvas's center=320). Animation frames were bbox-normalized to canvas-center (320), but idle was used raw â€” so when the runtime switched between idle and walk, the character popped 19 px horizontally.
  • Fix: HERO.idle now goes through the SAME _normalizeFrameToReference pipeline as every animation frame. Output is a normalized canvas, not the raw image, with character centered at canvas center.
  • Verified: idle / walk[0] / walk[8] / attack[0] / attack[8] all measure bbox height 771, feet Y 819, center X 319-320 (1 px tolerance). Identical position to the pixel.
  • Studio "Apply" path also benefits â€” PLAYER_CUSTOM_SPRITE_CANVAS now points at the normalized idle.

Console stamp

[CharStudio] v0.25.87 loaded â€” 16-frame walk + 16-frame attack + idle centered
[Hero] loaded teen idle + walk(16) + jump(9) + attack(16) + hit(4) + death(4) + class anims (4/4)

v0.25.86 6 NPC sprites + background seam fix

Replaced the procedural pixel-NPC bodies in the homestead with ludo.ai-generated chibi NPCs, and fixed a long-standing seam visible in the parallax background.

Background seam fix

  • Problem: parallax background tiled by drawing two side-by-side copies. The bg images aren't seamlessly tileable, so the right edge of one copy met the left edge of the next visibly mismatched â€” a hard cut every W px of camera scroll.
  • Fix: mirror the second copy horizontally. Right edge of copy 1 now meets right-edge-of-copy-2 (its mirror), so pixels along the seam match exactly. Also rounded the parallax shift to integer pixels to kill sub-pixel blur.
  • Single 7-line change in drawBackgroundLayer; works automatically with every existing background image.

6 NPC sprites (homestead)

  • Elspeth (potion seller) â€” pink-haired herbalist with potion bottle + leaf clip
  • Brok (weapon-smith) â€” bearded blacksmith with apron + iron hammer
  • The Amnesiac â€” hooded mysterious traveler clutching head
  • Sage Mira â€” purple-robed elder mage with crystal staff
  • Old Arlen (info giver) â€” bearded village elder with walking stick
  • Caravan Yara (traveling merchant) â€” golden-cloaked adventurer with satchel
  • Pipeline: createImage n=1 + removeBackground per NPC. Cost ~6 credits.
  • Runtime: new NPC_SPRITES cache + NPC_SPRITE_FILES nameâ†’file map. drawNPCs branches: if NPC_SPRITES[npc.name] is loaded, draws sprite via _drawNpcSprite() with subtle bobble; else falls through to existing procedural pixel-NPC.
  • Loading screen now preloads all 6 NPC sprites alongside the 10 hero sheets.

v0.25.85 Class-specific skill animations Â· one-shot attack timing

Each of the four classes now has its own skill animation, and attacks play through as proper one-shots synced to when the skill was cast (instead of looping for as long as player.attacking happened to be true).

4 class skill animations (~20 credits)

  • Warrior â€” sword swing: arm cocks back over shoulder, swings in wide diagonal arc, body twists for power. teen_warrior_swing.webp Â· 14 fps.
  • Rogue â€” dagger stab: arm pulls to chest, snap-thrusts forward, body leans in. teen_rogue_stab.webp Â· 16 fps (snappiest).
  • Mage â€” spell cast: hands raise, magical energy crackles between palms, push outward to release. teen_mage_cast.webp Â· 12 fps.
  • Archer â€” bow shot: left arm extends with invisible bow, right arm draws to cheek, releases forward. teen_archer_shot.webp Â· 14 fps.
  • All 4 frames at 2Ã—2 grid, ~1 s clip; bbox-normalized to idle's reference height + feet Y like the rest of the hero set.

One-shot attack timing

  • Problem: previously the attack animation looped frames keyed on absolute game.time while player.attacking was true. If the attack started mid-frame-cycle, the user saw the animation joining in progress (bad timing); if attacking flipped off mid-anim, the rest of the animation was cut.
  • Fix: anchor-based playback. When attack begins, capture player._heroAtkAt = game.time. Frame index = floor((game.time - anchor) / framesPerStep), clamped to last frame. Animation plays from frame 0 â†’ last every time, regardless of when the attack triggered relative to game.time.
  • Bonus: animation now completes even if player.attacking goes false before the last frame â€” feels much more decisive. Same fix applied to hit + death with _heroHitAt and _heroDeathAt anchors.

State machine â€” final priority order

death (hp <= 0)        â†‘ one-shot, anchor _heroDeathAt
hit (hitStun > 0)      | one-shot, anchor _heroHitAt
attack (class-specific)| one-shot, anchor _heroAtkAt â€” duration = frames/fps
jump (|vy| > 0.5)      | loop (8 fps)
walk (|vx| > 0.5)      | loop (9 fps)
idle                   â†“ static

Class selection via player.cls

  • HERO.classFrames[player.cls] resolves to the right frames at runtime; falls back to the generic attack set if player.cls isn't mapped.
  • Loading screen now preloads all 10 hero sheets (idle + walk + jump + attack + hit + death + 4 class anims). Progress bar shows N/10.

v0.25.84 Hit + death anims Â· loading screen Â· 2Ã— skill cooldowns

Three changes shipped together: full hero state machine completion (hit + death anims), a polished loading screen so the player never sees a blank world before sprites are ready, and a balance pass that doubles every player skill's cooldown.

Hit + death animations

  • Hit: teen_hit_sheet.webp â€” 4 frames (2Ã—2), 12 fps, 1 s recoil cycle. Plays while player.hitStun > 0. One-shot (clamps to last frame, doesn't loop).
  • Death: teen_death_sheet.webp â€” 4 frames (2Ã—2), 4 fps, slow collapse. Plays while player.hp <= 0. One-shot.
  • Cost: ~10 credits (2 Ã— animateSprite Eagle, 4 frames each).

State machine â€” full priority order

death (hp <= 0)        â†‘ one-shot, clamps to last frame
hit (hitStun > 0)      | one-shot, clamps to last frame
attack (attacking)     | loop (14 fps)
jump (|vy| > 0.5)      | loop (8 fps)
walk (|vx| > 0.5)      | loop (9 fps)
idle                   â†“ static

Loading screen

  • New full-screen overlay (#loading-overlay) preloads all 6 hero sprite assets in parallel before revealing the game.
  • Progress bar fills as each asset loads; status reads "Loading spritesâ€¦ N/6 â†’ Ready".
  • Hides via 0.45 s fade-out only after both: (1) all required images loaded, (2) HERO_READY event fires (so the JS-side normalization is also complete).
  • Hard 8 s fallback: if anything stalls, overlay hides anyway and the procedural fallback takes over.

Balance: 2Ã— skill cooldowns

  • Every entry in the player SKILLS object had its cd doubled. 52 skills affected.
  • Examples: slash 360 â†’ 720, powerStrike 720 â†’ 1440, groundSlam 1000 â†’ 2000, warCry 7000 â†’ 14000, bloodlust 18000 â†’ 36000, lich_harvest 60000 â†’ 120000.
  • Monster skills (MONSTER_SKILLS) and class defense moves (block / parry / vanish / roll) are untouched â€” only player skills got the nerf.
  • One-shot Python script Sprites/character/double_cooldowns.py handles the regex replacement; saved in case the user wants to roll it back or re-tune.

v0.25.83 Hero state machine + jump + attack + consistent frame normalization

Three states wired in to the hero (walk, jump, attack) plus a normalization fix that makes every animation frame render at the exact same on-screen height + feet position.

Consistency fix

  • Problem: each frame was independently bbox-fitted to ~92% of canvas height. A frame with extended legs (taller bbox) and a frame with legs together (shorter bbox) both got scaled to 92% â€” so the character APPEARED bigger when legs were tucked, smaller when extended.
  • Fix: added _findChibiBbox + _normalizeFrameToReference. Now the IDLE sprite's character bbox is the canonical reference; every animated frame gets scaled so its bbox HEIGHT matches idle's exactly, then anchored at idle's feet Y position.
  • Result: character is precisely the same size + at the same feet Y across idle / walk / jump / attack. Position is consistent, no shrinking or shifting.

2 new animations (Eagle, 9 frames each)

  • Jump: teen_jump_sheet.webp â€” crouch â†’ spring up â†’ arms overhead at peak â†’ land. 8 fps. Cost: 5.2 credits.
  • Attack: teen_attack_sheet.webp â€” wind-up â†’ punch â†’ recovery. 14 fps (snappier). Cost: 5.2 credits.
  • Plus their .mp4 previews. Total: ~10 credits this version.

State machine in _drawPlayerCustomSprite

// Priority order (highest first):
if (player.attacking)              â†’ HERO.attackFrames
else if (|player.vy| > 0.5)        â†’ HERO.jumpFrames
else if (|player.vx| > 0.5)        â†’ HERO.walkFrames
else                                â†’ HERO.idle
  • Each state has its own fps tuned to feel natural â€” walk 9 fps (smooth), jump 8 fps (medium), attack 14 fps (snappy).
  • Hero path only â€” the studio mannequin composite still uses the simpler walk/idle split (it doesn't have jump/attack frames).
  • Detection signals: player.attacking (boolean set by skill firing), player.vy (vertical velocity > 0.5 = airborne), player.vx (horizontal velocity > 0.5 = walking).

Loader refactor

  • HERO_META is now per-action: { walk, jump, attack } â€” each entry has its own { sheet, frames, gridW, gridH, fps }.
  • Loader uses async/await + Promise.all on idle + 3 sheets, then runs each through _heroExtractAndNormalize using the idle's bbox as reference.
  • Console log now reports loaded counts: [Hero] loaded teen idle + walk(9) + jump(9) + attack(9)

v0.25.82 Hero proportions + walk frame normalization + facing flip

Three quick fixes for v0.25.81: aspect-aware scaling (was forcing square; squashed the 640Ã—864 hero), bbox-normalize walk frames to idle's exact dimensions, flip facing flag (3/4-view teen reads as left-facing in-game).

v0.25.81 Hero character â€” replace mannequin with complete teen sprite (Eagle-quality walk)

Quality pivot. The faceless mannequin + extracted face / hair overlay system was producing muddy, alignment-prone output. Replaced with a single complete pre-styled teen character sprite (white tee + blue jeans + brown hair) animated at higher quality (Eagle model, 9 frames).

Why the pivot

  • Layered approach (mannequin + face overlay + hair overlay) compounded scaling artifacts and the face/hair often misaligned with the bobbing animated head.
  • animateSprite Blitz model is rated 4/10 visual quality by ludo â€” produced visible blur and frame-to-frame inconsistency.
  • One full sprite is simpler to keep on-model. ludo's Eagle model (8/10 visual) holds the t-shirt, jeans, hair, and face features stable across all 9 walk frames.

What v0.25.81 ships

  • Sprites/character/hero/teen_idle.webp â€” single transparent canonical sprite. 640Ã—864. Teen boy, white t-shirt, blue jeans, brown sneakers, medium-brown shaggy hair, gentle smile, 3/4 right-facing stance.
  • Sprites/character/hero/teen_walk_sheet.webp â€” 1536Ã—1536 spritesheet, 3Ã—3 grid, 9 frames at ~9 fps for a smooth ~1 s walk loop. Generated via animateSprite with model:"eagle", duration:2, frame_size:256.
  • Sprites/character/hero/teen_walk.mp4 â€” preview video.
  • Cost: ~7 credits (1 createImage n=2 + 0.5 removeBackground + 5.2 animateSprite Eagle).

Runtime

  • New HERO + HERO_META globals. Auto-load both PNGs at module init, pre-extract 9 walk frames as offscreen canvases.
  • On hero load (if studio hasn't already applied a custom avatar): default window.PLAYER_CUSTOM_SPRITE_CANVAS + PLAYER_CUSTOM_WALK_FRAMES + PLAYER_SPRITE_FACES_RIGHT = true to the hero. Player sees the teen by default.
  • Per-sprite facing convention: _drawPlayerCustomSprite now mirrors when PLAYER_SPRITE_FACES_RIGHT doesn't match the in-game facing â€” works correctly for both the right-facing hero AND the left-facing studio mannequin composite.
  • Adaptive fps: hero uses 9 fps for the 9-frame loop; the studio's 4-frame mannequin walk uses 6 fps. Both feel right.
  • Revert button now restores the hero (instead of falling all the way back to procedural drawCuteCharacter).

What still works

  • Character Studio (Q) â€” fully functional. Apply still composites the layered mannequin and overrides the hero per-sprite. The mannequin's facing convention is honored separately.
  • Procedural drawCuteCharacter â€” fallback for the case where neither the hero nor the studio output is loaded.

v0.25.80 Player walk cycle + facing direction fix

In-game player now plays a 4-frame walk cycle when moving, falls back to the idle snapshot when stationary. Also fixes the long-standing "mannequin facing the wrong way" bug.

Facing direction fix

  • The ludo-generated mannequin sits in a 3/4 front-facing-LEFT view by default â€” when the player walked right, my scale(-1, 1) mirror was inverting the wrong direction.
  • Fix in _drawPlayerCustomSprite: mirror when player.facing > 0 instead of < 0. Single condition flip; no asset changes needed.

Walk cycle pipeline

  • Generation: animateSprite on the canonical peach mannequin, motion prompt for "side-scroller chibi walk cycle, legs alternate, body bobs slightly, arms swing in opposition". 4 frames, 2Ã—2 grid, ~1.33 s loop. Cost: 4 credits.
  • Asset: Sprites/character/layers/base/mannequin_walk_sheet.webp (22 KB) + mannequin_walk.mp4 (49 KB preview).

Runtime

  • Pre-render at Apply time: applyCharStudioToPlayer now generates per-frame composites â€” for each of the 4 walk frames, it crops the cell out of the spritesheet, then layers the current face overlay + hair overlay on top. Saves to window.PLAYER_CUSTOM_WALK_FRAMES (Array<canvas>).
  • Frame pick in _drawPlayerCustomSprite: if |player.vx| > 0.5 AND walk frames ready, cycle through them at 6 fps; otherwise blit the idle snapshot.
  • Caveat: skin-tone tinting only applies to the idle snapshot for now â€” the walk anim source was the colored mannequin, not the neutral grayscale one. Walk frames default to peach skin regardless of the tone the user picked. Fix is to re-animate the neutral mannequin (4 more credits) and apply per-pixel multiply per frame; deferred for now.
  • Revert button now also clears PLAYER_CUSTOM_WALK_FRAMES so the procedural fallback shows when reverted.

v0.25.79 Monster animation pipeline â€” first idle spritesheet (snail, ludo.ai animateSprite)

Adds an animation runtime alongside the static sprite path. Monsters with an entry in MONSTER_ANIM_DEFS now render from a frame-cycled spritesheet; everything else continues to use the static sprite or procedural fallback. First wired type: snail.

Pipeline

  • Generation: animateSprite with model:"blitz", duration:1.5, frames:4, frame_size:192, image_type:"sprite", loop:true. Returns a 2Ã—2 grid spritesheet + a preview MP4. Cost: 4 credits per animation (Blitz min, regardless of duration).
  • Long-running tool: animation generation can exceed Claude's default tool timeout. Use getSpriteResults?request_id=<id> to poll if the call returns a timeout â€” the actual job keeps running on ludo's side and the result is retrievable.
  • Source: passes the bg-removed canonical sprite URL as initial_image. The motion prompt drives the deformation (e.g. "subtle idle breathing, antennae sway, body squish slightly").

Runtime

  • MONSTER_ANIMS cache + MONSTER_ANIM_DEFS registry. Each entry: { sheet, frames, gridW, gridH, fps }. Lazy-loaded at module init from Sprites/monsters/anim/<sheet>.
  • Frame ticker: frameIdx = floor(game.time / framesPerStep) % frames with framesPerStep = 60 / fps. Each monster gets a small phase offset (m.x % frames) so a cluster doesn't bob in lockstep.
  • Cell extraction: ctx.drawImage(sheet, col*cellW, row*cellH, cellW, cellH, dx, dy, dw, dh) crops the right grid cell into the same anchor + mirror setup the static sprite uses.
  • Fallback chain: animation â†’ static sprite â†’ procedural drawCuteMob. Failure-safe; loading errors don't break rendering.

First animation shipped

  • snail â€” gentle idle breathing, antennae sway softly, body squishes slightly. 4 frames at 3 fps, ~1.33 s loop. Files: Sprites/monsters/anim/snail_idle_sheet.webp (40 KB) + snail_idle.mp4 (32 KB preview).

Adding more monsters

// Drop the new spritesheet into Sprites/monsters/anim/
// Then add an entry to MONSTER_ANIM_DEFS â€” that's it.
const MONSTER_ANIM_DEFS = {
  snail: { sheet: 'snail_idle_sheet.webp', frames: 4, gridW: 2, gridH: 2, fps: 3 },
  // slime: { sheet: 'slime_idle_sheet.webp', frames: 4, gridW: 2, gridH: 2, fps: 4 },
  // ...
};

v0.25.78 Monster sprites batch 2 â€” 8 more chibi mobs (14 total wired)

Eight more early/mid-tier monsters get ludo.ai chibi sprites, bringing the layered-art roster to 14. Same pipeline as v0.25.77; just new prompts and an extended MONSTER_SPRITE_TYPES.

8 new monster sprites

  • gummy (lvl 4, candy biome) â€” translucent strawberry-red gummy bear with sugar-crystal speckles + classic teddy silhouette.
  • cookie (lvl 4, candy biome) â€” golden chocolate-chip biscuit body with stubby chocolate-chip mitt arms + biscuit-cube feet, surprised o-mouth.
  • frog (lvl 5, swamp) â€” plump lime-green with bulging gold dome eyes, wide curved smile, slight squat-ready pose.
  • axolotl (lvl 5, water) â€” pale-pink salamander with feathery red-pink gill tufts and a wide paddle-tail.
  • horny (lvl 6, forest) â€” small reddish-violet imp with curled ram-horns, yellow slit-pupil eyes, fanged grin in a battle stance.
  • orange (lvl 7, forest) â€” sentient citrus-fruit with green leaf-stem on top, twiggy arms with leaf-mitt hands.
  • stump (lvl 8, forest) â€” short fat grumpy tree stump with concentric rings, side mushrooms, leaf-arms.
  • scorpion (lvl 12, desert) â€” chibi-proportioned warm-amber scorpion with forward pincers + glowing yellow stinger tip.

What stayed the same

  • Pipeline: createImage (chibi / side-scroll / square) + removeBackground (creative_edit:false, crop:false). Total cost: 8 credits (8 + 8).
  • Runtime: same MONSTER_SPRITES cache + lazy Image() loader + _drawMonsterSprite + dispatch branch from v0.25.77 â€” only the type list grew.

Coverage

TierWith spriteProcedural fallback
1-2snail, slime, mushroom, gummy, cookie, frog, axolotlâ€”
3horny, orange, stumpâ€”
4scorpion, nougatBearhoneyBuzz
5+skeleton, coralImpmummy, zombie, wraith, nimbusFox, cosmicMochi, pearlSprite

v0.25.77 Monster sprites â€” ludo.ai-generated chibi mobs (6 types wired)

Six starter monster types now render as ludo.ai-generated chibi sprites instead of the procedural drawCuteMob. Other types fall back transparently â€” no behaviour change for unmapped mobs.

Pipeline

  • Generation: createImage with image_type:"sprite", art_style:"Chibi", perspective:"Side-Scroll", aspect_ratio:"ar_1_1". Each prompt asks for thick black manga ink outlines + 4-tone cel shading + clean isolated white background to match the existing storybook chibi aesthetic.
  • Background removal: removeBackground with creative_edit:false + crop:false preserves the canvas size for transparent compositing.
  • Total cost: 4 credits (6 createImage + 1 mushroom re-roll + 7 removeBackground).

6 monster sprites shipped

  • snail (lvl 1) â€” honey-brown spiral shell + golden swirl, mint-green body, antennae with eye dots, 3/4 winking smile.
  • slime (lvl 2) â€” squishy mint-jade gelatinous blob with translucent shine spots.
  • mushroom (lvl 3) â€” Mario-red toadstool cap with cream spots, big anime eyes with angry slanted brows + mischievous grin (re-rolled from a too-soft first attempt).
  • nougatBear (lvl 16, candy biome) â€” plump cream-pink candy bear with red heart on chest + blush cheeks.
  • skeleton (lvl 18) â€” bone-white skull with red glowing eye pinpoint + toothy grin + rusted shortsword in battle stance.
  • coralImp (lvl 38, ocean biome) â€” coral-pink scaly body with iridescent teal-aqua dorsal fin + tail + pointy fangs grin.

Runtime wiring

  • MONSTER_SPRITES cache: lazy-loaded Image per type at module init. Add new entries to MONSTER_SPRITE_TYPES + drop the matching transparent .webp into Sprites/monsters/ and they wire up automatically.
  • _drawMonsterSprite(m, sx, sy): anchors the sprite at the monster's feet (footX = sx + m.w/2, footY = sy + m.h), scales to m.h Ã— 1.4 so the chibi head doesn't get clipped, mirrors horizontally when m.facing < 0.
  • Dispatch branch added to drawMonster just before the drawCuteMob fallback: else if (MONSTER_SPRITES[m.type]) _drawMonsterSprite(m, sx, sy).
  • Failure-safe: if a sprite fails to load (404, network), it stays out of the cache and the monster falls back to procedural drawing â€” no crash, no visual gap.

Asset tree

Sprites/monsters/
â”œâ”€â”€ snail.webp        # transparent canonical
â”œâ”€â”€ slime.webp
â”œâ”€â”€ mushroom.webp
â”œâ”€â”€ nougatBear.webp
â”œâ”€â”€ skeleton.webp
â”œâ”€â”€ coralImp.webp
â””â”€â”€ *_raw.webp        # white-bg ludo originals kept for re-extraction tuning

v0.25.76 Hair z-order fix â€” bangs occlude eyes (chibi convention) + outline on top

Swapped Character Studio's render order so the hair overlay sits on top of the face overlay. Bangs now correctly occlude the eyes (chibi/MapleStory convention) and the hair's baked-in black outline reads cleanly on top of everything.

What changed

  • Old z-order: base â†’ tint â†’ hair â†’ face â€” eyes peeked through bangs at full alpha, face's pixel data clipped the hair outline at intersections.
  • New z-order: base â†’ tint â†’ face â†’ hair â€” bangs visually occlude eyes (face still visible through gaps in the hair), hair outline always wins.
  • Single-line change in renderCharStudio(); same change propagates automatically to the in-game custom sprite (drawn from the studio canvas).

Studio panel layout polish (v0.25.75c bundled)

  • Apply / Revert buttons moved to the top of the controls column with a gold accent on Apply (was previously buried at the bottom and easy to miss on smaller viewports).
  • Panel is now scrollable on small viewports (max-height: 92vh; overflow-y: auto) so all controls stay reachable.

v0.25.75 Apply Character Studio avatar to live in-game player

The Character Studio output (skin tone Ã— face Ã— hair) can now be applied to the live in-game player sprite. Procedural drawCuteCharacter stays as the fallback when no custom avatar is applied.

Apply / Revert buttons

  • Two new buttons under the Character Studio panel: Use in game and Revert.
  • Use in game snapshots the current 384Ã—384 composited canvas into window.PLAYER_CUSTOM_SPRITE_CANVAS (one-time copy, no per-frame allocation) and shows a toast.
  • Revert clears the global â†’ drawPlayer falls back to procedural chibi rendering.

Render-time integration in drawPlayer

  • Added a branch at the existing drawCuteCharacter(sx, sy) call site (line ~22560): if PLAYER_CUSTOM_SPRITE_CANVAS is set, the custom sprite renders instead via _drawPlayerCustomSprite().
  • Custom sprite anchors at the player's feet (using the existing _chibiApply transform), scales to player.h Ã— 1.45 (taller than the bbox to fit the chibi's big head), and mirrors horizontally for left-facing.
  • The procedural face overlay (_drawCuteFaceOverlay) is skipped when a custom sprite is in use â€” the studio's face overlay is already baked in.
  • Block / parry shield bubble + name tag still draw on top regardless of which character path was used.

Caveats

  • Idle pose only. The studio sprite is one fixed 3/4-front pose. While the player walks / runs / attacks, the same sprite is drawn (no animation frames yet). Feels static during action â€” that's the next batch (per-pose generatePose calls).
  • No clothes layer yet. The mannequin's plain body shows when applied â€” top / bottom / weapon overlays come next.
  • Apply doesn't persist across reloads (no save). Re-apply after each refresh until the persistence step ships.

v0.25.75b coord-fix patch

  • Initial v0.25.75 had the sprite drawn at user-coord origin assuming _chibiApply translated the origin to player feet. It does not â€” _chibiApply applies a scale-anchored-at-feet but the coordinate system stays in screen space (which is why drawCuteCharacter takes sx, sy as parameters).
  • Result: the custom sprite was being drawn in the upper-left of the screen at roughly (-0.19 Ã— footX, -0.06 Ã— footY) â€” far off-screen. Player appeared invisible after Apply, masquerading as "sprite doesn't show".
  • Fix: _drawPlayerCustomSprite now derives footX, footY from the same player.x - game.camera.x + w/2 + player.y + h formulas, then ctx.translate(footX, footY) before the draw. Anchor + scale now work correctly.

v0.25.74 Hair slot + soft-alpha extraction (4 hair styles)

Adds the hair layer slot to the Character Studio with 4 starter styles, plus a soft-alpha extraction mode that fades feature edges naturally instead of capturing AA pixels at hard full alpha.

Soft-alpha extraction mode

  • Why: hair edges have wide anti-aliased gradients (yellowâ†’cream for blonde, blackâ†’cream for spike). Hard-alpha extraction captured those gradient pixels at full alpha with their gray-blend RGB, producing a visible halo when composited over a tinted skin tone.
  • What changed: extract_layer.py now defaults to soft_alpha=True. Per-pixel alpha ramps from 0 at the diff threshold to 255 at threshold + soft_alpha_ramp (default 80). Edges that are barely-different fade to transparent; clear feature pixels stay fully opaque.
  • Optional soft_alpha=False falls back to the previous binary mask if a layer needs hard edges (e.g. logo overlays).

4 starter hair overlays

  • Blonde Shaggy: chunky single-silhouette golden tousled bangs, MapleStory warrior-class look.
  • Black Spike: anime-shounen spiky black hair with subtle gray sheen highlights.
  • Brown Pony: chibi straight-cut bangs with a high red-ribbon ponytail. Iconic anime-girl silhouette.
  • Mage Hood: deep purple wizard cowl with silver trim â€” covers hair entirely, classic mage class read.
  • Plus a Bald option (no hair layer rendered) so the player can opt out.

Studio runtime â€” 5th picker + new z-order

  • Hair-style picker added below the Face picker (5 buttons matching the existing button styling).
  • Render z-order updated: base â†’ multiply skin tint â†’ hair â†’ face. Hair below face means eyes peek through bangs (chibi convention), face overlay always reads cleanly.
  • Lazy-load now fetches 9 layers in parallel on first studio open (1 base + 4 faces + 4 hairs). Status: Ready Â· 9 layers loaded.

v0.25.73 Character Studio â€” 4 face styles + face-aware extractor + halo fixes

Builds on v0.25.72. Three new face styles join Classic (Sleepy, Sharp, Big Grin), plus a face-specific extraction pipeline that fixes both the silhouette fringe halo and the missing-pupil-highlight bug.

Halo fixes â€” render pipeline + extractor

  • Silhouette halo (mannequin edge looked fuzzy after multiply blend): the old multiply+fillRect+destination-in pipeline over-tinted anti-aliased silhouette pixels with pure skin tone. Replaced with a per-pixel ImageData multiply that touches only existing pixels, preserves alpha exactly, and gives mathematically correct shading. Black outlines stay black; AA edges fade naturally to any skin tone.
  • Eye halo + missing pupils: the generic extract_layer.py pixel-diff captured the AA fringe of the black eye with its gray transition RGB at full alpha (visible halo over tinted skin) and missed the white pupils because they were too close in colour to the cream-white face background.
  • New extract_face.py handles faces specifically: dark feature pixels become pure black with alpha = 255 âˆ’ max(R,G,B) so AA fades to whatever's underneath; pupil whites are detected via erode + dilate inside the dark cluster (centres only â€” no cream face leakage) and forced fully opaque white. Tunable thresholds (dark_max_rgb=180, white_min_rgb=248, pupil_erosion_px=1, pupil_dilation_px=1).

3 new face overlays

  • Sleepy / Calm: smooth closed-eyelid arches with eyelashes + small calm mouth. Quiet thoughtful vibe.
  • Sharp / Serious: angular narrow black eyes with thin pupil glints + slight scowl. Warrior / commander look.
  • Big Grin: oversized round anime eyes with sparkle highlights + open pink-tongued grin + scattered sparkle particles for the excited / delighted vibe.
  • All four styles re-extracted with the fixed face extractor â€” pupils opaque, no edge halo at any of the 6 skin tones.

Face style picker in Character Studio

  • 4 buttons (Classic Â· Sleepy Â· Sharp Â· Big Grin) under the skin tone palette. Click to live-swap the face overlay.
  • Lazy-load: all 4 face PNGs fetched in parallel with the base when the studio first opens. Status line now reports Ready Â· 5 layers loaded.
  • Swatches and face buttons share the same active-state styling (gold border on selection).

v0.25.72 Layered avatar system â€” ludo.ai sprite pipeline + Character Studio (press Q)

First step toward a MapleStory-style customizable character. Ships a runtime layer compositor (toggle with Q) backed by ludo.ai-generated overlays. Each visual layer (face, hair, top, etc.) is a transparent PNG anchored to a canonical neutral mannequin base. Skin tones are tinted at runtime via canvas multiply blend so any RGB colour works.

Pipeline â€” edit + extract + neutralize

  • Canonical base: faceless chibi mannequin (3/4 front-turned right, 4-tone cel-shaded, anatomically simplified). Generated via ludo.ai then background-removed for clean alpha. Saved to Sprites/character/layers/base/mannequin_base.webp.
  • Neutralized base: neutralize_skin.py converts the peach body fill to grayscale luminance while preserving the black manga-ink outline and alpha channel. Output at mannequin_base_neutral.webp â€” the runtime tinting target.
  • Layer extraction: extract_layer.py pixel-diffs a "mannequin + feature" composite against the bare mannequin to produce a transparent feature-only layer. Threshold-based, supports y-region masking. Used to extract the first face overlay (eyes + gentle smile) from a ludo edit.
  • Preview compose: preview_compose.py stacks layers in z-order for visual verification.

Character Studio â€” runtime compositor in maple_game.html

  • Self-contained module added at the bottom of the file (CSS + DOM + script). Zero coupling with existing player rendering â€” the in-game character is unchanged.
  • Toggle: Q opens / closes the studio overlay. Esc closes. Ignores keypresses while a text input is focused.
  • Compositor stacks four canvas operations: (1) draw neutralized mannequin â†’ (2) multiply blend with the selected skin tone â†’ (3) destination-in re-mask to the original alpha so transparent pixels stay transparent â†’ (4) source-over draw the face overlay on top.
  • Skin tone palette: six swatches (Porcelain Â· Light Â· Tan Â· Olive Â· Brown Â· Deep). Click any to live-update the canvas. The black outline stays black under multiply (any Ã— 0 = 0); the 4-tone shading variation tints proportionally to give natural-looking lit and shadowed skin in any colour.
  • Lazy-load: layers are fetched only on first open; the status line shows Loadingâ€¦ â†’ Ready Â· 2 layers loaded.

Asset tree

Sprites/character/
â”œâ”€â”€ extract_layer.py        # pixel-diff feature extractor
â”œâ”€â”€ neutralize_skin.py      # colour â†’ grayscale luminance for runtime tinting
â”œâ”€â”€ preview_compose.py      # stacking verifier
â””â”€â”€ layers/
    â”œâ”€â”€ base/
    â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ mannequin_base.webp           # canonical (peach default)
    â”‚   â””â”€â”€ mannequin_base_neutral.webp   # grayscale tinting target
    â”œâ”€â”€ eyes/anime_classic.webp           # eyes + gentle smile face overlay
    â”œâ”€â”€ face/   hair/   top/   bottom/    # ready for next batches
    â””â”€â”€ glove/  shoe/   weapon/   hat/   cape/

v0.25.67 Curved-path stylish hair â€” spiky tufts + forelocks + side locks + glossy shine

Replaced the old flat pixel-rectangle hair with a proper layered curved-path system. Six layers compose into a stylish anime-chibi hair silhouette: back hair mass with form shadow, four spiky top tufts, three front fringe forelocks, two side-lock sweeps, the anime glossy shine band, and a crisp ink outline.

ðŸ’‡ Six layers in one block

  1. Back hair mass â€” quadratic-curve silhouette extending past the head circle for visible volume. Wider than the head with side-flow down to y=22. Right side gets a cc.hairDeep form-shadow wedge.
  2. Top spiky tufts â€” four angular peak triangles spaced across the top (x=4, 11, 19, 26). Centre tuft is tallest (-6 px), outer tufts shorter for natural variation. Each has a 2-tone inner highlight wedge using cc.hairHi.
  3. Front fringe forelocks â€” three teardrop strands hanging over the forehead (x=7, 15, 24). Centre forelock is longest (12-px tip). Each forelock casts a small cc.hairDeep shadow on the forehead.
  4. Side-lock sweeps â€” curved tendrils on each cheek. Left lock uses cc.hair with cc.hairHi stripe (lit side); right lock uses cc.hairDeep (form-shadow side).
  5. Glossy shine band â€” thin curved highlight in cc.hairHi2 hugging the top contour. Three crisp white peak pips at x=9, 15, 22 + a sparkle pip on the upper-left lit side.
  6. Ink outline â€” 1-px stroke in CUTE_PAL.outline following the silhouette's top + side curve for confident definition (matches the chick-sticker reference vocabulary).

ðŸŽ¨ Why curved paths instead of pixel rectangles

The previous hair was assembled from ctx.fillRect calls â€” straight edges only, blocky silhouette, no organic flow. Quadratic curves (quadraticCurveTo) give the silhouette real volume and let the four spiky tufts feel like they grew out of the same mass. Combined with the per-strand cel shadow on each forelock, the hair now reads as a layered structure with depth instead of a flat colour block.

ðŸŒˆ Class palette compatibility

All six layers use existing cc.hair / cc.hairHi / cc.hairDeep / cc.hairHi2 tones from _ensureCelTones(). So:

  • Warrior â€” dark brown w/ amber highlights
  • Rogue â€” dark violet w/ lavender highlights
  • Mage â€” dark indigo w/ blue highlights
  • Archer â€” chestnut w/ warm amber highlights

All four classes get the same hair shape; only the palette differs.

What stays

  • v0.25.66 anime-chibi eyes (class-coloured iris + shine pips) â€” unchanged. The new hair sits behind the eyes naturally.
  • Hair bob animation (hb per-frame y-offset) â€” kept; all curves use the same hb so the entire silhouette bobs together.
  • Hat / helmet / cap rendering â€” drawn AFTER hair so equipment still overlaps cleanly.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.67. One block in drawCuteCharacter rewritten (~25 px-rect calls â†’ ~50 path operations across 6 layers). +115 / âˆ’24 lines.
  • No save/network/protocol impact.

v0.25.66 Big anime-chibi eyes + class-coloured iris + expression variants

Adopting the chibi-anime visual vocabulary for the procedural character. The previous 2Ã—3 black eye dots felt minimal next to the rest of the artwork. Replaced with proper anime eyes (iris + shine pips) and added expression variants for the mouth tied to player state.

ðŸ‘€ Eyes â€” fully reworked

  • Outer dark ring â€” 2.4 Ã— 3.0 px ellipse in CUTE_PAL.eye giving each eye a defined silhouette.
  • Class-coloured iris fills inside the ring:
    • Warrior â€” amber #c47a32
    • Rogue â€” violet #7a44b8
    • Mage â€” blue #3a78cc
    • Archer â€” teal #3a9a78
    player.inRage overrides to red #ff4444.
  • Iris highlight band â€” lighter shade of the class colour on the upper half (mix iris â†’ white 0.40).
  • White shine pip on the upper-left â€” the classic anime sparkle, 2 px stack.
  • Secondary lower-right gloss pip at 85% white for extra glossiness.
  • Soft pink under-eye blush band (rgba 255,180,200, 0.45) â€” subtle "fresh face" colour distinct from the cheek blush on the temples.
  • Blink animation upgraded from a flat black line to a 1.5-px closed-arc stroke â€” the classic anime closed-eye expression.

ðŸ˜Š Mouth â€” expression variants

  • Default â€” small closed-arc smile (1.5 px stroke, 2.5 px radius). Replaces the old 3-pixel mouth strip.
  • Open smile â€” when a buff is active (warCry, rampage, bloodlust, holyShield) OR during attack: small filled ellipse (2.2 Ã— 1.4) with a 1-px pink tongue. Reads as energised.
  • Surprised "o" â€” when player.hitStun > 100: tiny 1.5-px circle. Reads as "ouch!".
  • Rage â€” same chosen mouth shape but tinted red #ff4466.

ðŸŽ¨ Why this matches the reference vocabulary

  • The reference image you shared shows the canonical anime-chibi style: big rounded eyes with colored iris + white pip, soft blush, open expressive mouth. The new render adopts those same building blocks WITHOUT copying the specific designs (different proportions, different palette, our own existing chibi silhouette).
  • The class-colour iris doubles as a build-identity signal â€” players reading the screen can tell their class from the eye colour even when the equipped gear obscures the body palette.
  • Expression variants tie the face to gameplay state, so the character "feels" what they're doing â€” surprised when hit, eager when buffed, relaxed when idle.

What stays

  • v0.25.49 hard-edged head cel-shading (single crescent shadow + white pip) â€” kept; the new eyes draw on top.
  • v0.25.41â€“48 cel-shading on body / hair / equipment â€” unchanged.
  • Cheek blush on the temples â€” unchanged. The new under-eye blush is a separate band.
  • Other facial draws (eye position, hair drop, hat overlap) â€” preserved through the rewrite.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.66. One block in drawCuteCharacter rewritten (eyes + mouth). +87 / âˆ’9 lines.
  • No save/network/protocol impact.

v0.25.65 Storybook feel â€” thatched cottage village + drifting petals + hopping bunnies

User: "yes dial up the storybook feel." Three more original procedural layers added on top of v0.25.64: a tiny thatched-cottage village clinging to the mountain foothills, drifting flower petals across the upper sky, and ambient hopping bunny critters at the grass mound depth.

ðŸ¡ Layer 1 â€” Thatched cottage village (parallax 0.10)

Sits between the near mountain range and the pine treeline. Six cottages per row, each:

  • Small rectangular body (28-40 px wide, 18-24 px tall) with a wall-shadow stripe on the right edge.
  • Steep thatched roof â€” 2-tone triangle (lit left half + shadow right half) with a 2-px ridge seam at the apex.
  • Stone chimney with a curl of smoke that rises and recycles via game.time (bright maps only).
  • Single warm-lit window with a cross mullion (gold on bright maps, blue on dark).
  • Tiny dark door at the base.
  • Bright maps: pastel cream-peach walls + warm brown roof. Dark maps: deep blue silhouette form.

ðŸŒ¸ Layer 2 â€” Drifting petals (parallax 0.18)

14 4-pixel petals drifting horizontally across the upper sky with a sine vertical wobble. Bright maps only. Three palettes rotate per petal: pink, yellow, lilac. Each petal has a 1-pixel highlight pip and a slow rotation hint to suggest tumbling. Adds the enchanted breeze storybook signal.

ðŸ° Layer 3 â€” Hopping bunny critters (parallax 0.34)

Four bunnies hopping along the grass mound depth, each on its own hop phase + horizontal drift. Per-bunny detail:

  • Round body (3-px radius) with a belly shadow.
  • Two long ears that tilt back during airborne phase, upright on landing.
  • Tiny eye dot that flips with hop direction (alternating bunnies face left / right).
  • Puffy tail on the trailing side.
  • Hop motion via sin(phase) * 8 y-offset. Phase length ~2 seconds. Half the cycle airborne, half on ground.
  • Landing dust â€” 2 small puff particles spawn on touchdown frame.
  • Bright maps: cream-white body + pink ears. Dark maps: silver-blue body + cool ears.

ðŸ“š Combined parallax stack (now 10 depth layers)

DepthLayerParallax
0Sky gradient + sun/moon0
1Distant cloud puffs (v0.25.64)0.04
2Furthest mountains0.015
3Mid mountains0.04
4Near mountains (snow caps)0.08
5ðŸ¡ Thatched cottages (NEW)0.10
6Rounded oaks (v0.25.64)0.14
7Pine treeline0.18
8ðŸŒ¸ Drifting petals (NEW)0.18
9Flower tufts0.15
10Grass mounds (v0.25.64)0.32
11ðŸ° Hopping bunnies (NEW)0.34
12Chibi clouds + birds0.15

What stays

  • v0.25.64 layers (cloud puffs, oaks, grass mounds) all kept.
  • Themed biomes (Candy Canyon, Bubblegum Swamp, Pearl Bathhouse, Stardust Atrium, Honeycomb Hollow) draw their decor LAST so lollipops / lily pads / brass pipes / cosmic eggs / hex pillars sit in front of the new storybook layers.
  • All three new layers gated on !isDark for petals + cottage smoke; cottages and bunnies have dark-map variants. Same gating logic as the existing v0.25.4 ambient layers.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.65. Three new procedural blocks inside drawWorld(): cottages (before pines), petals (before grass mounds), bunnies (after grass mounds). +135 / âˆ’1 lines. Net: 13 parallax layers per frame, ~400 canvas ops total â€” still well within budget.
  • No save/network/protocol impact.

v0.25.64 Storybook parallax mock â€” distant cloud puffs + rounded oaks + grass mounds

Mock-up: 3 new fully-procedural depth layers added to the existing parallax background, all original art (no copyrighted reference). Stacks with the existing faceted mountains + pines + flowers, giving 7 total parallax layers from far cloud puffs at the horizon down to grass mounds in front of the player.

ðŸŒ¥ï¸ Layer 1 â€” Distant cloud puffs (parallax 0.04)

Drawn just after the sky gradient, before the furthest mountain range. Five 5-circle pillowy clusters per row scrolling extremely slowly so they read as horizon haze. Bright maps only â€” dark maps keep the existing moonlight as headline. Tinted from game.mapData.sky[0] so each biome's sky pulls the cloud colour into its palette automatically.

ðŸŒ³ Layer 2 â€” Rounded storybook oaks (parallax 0.14)

14 oaks scrolling at 0.14 â€” slightly slower than the existing pine treeline (0.18) so oaks read as one depth-step further back. Each tree:

  • Chunky 6-px brown trunk with a 2-px shadow stripe.
  • 3-lobe pillowy canopy (left + right + top arcs) for the storybook silhouette feel.
  • 2-tone cel: lit body, a darker bottom shadow band, and a lit highlight catch on the upper-left.
  • Tiny 1-px specular sparkle pip on each canopy.
  • Canopy radius + trunk height vary per tree (4-step variation) so a pack of oaks doesn't look like clones.
  • Colour pulled from the map's sky palette via _mixHex(sky[2], #2a4a2a, 0.62) so each biome tints its own oaks.

ðŸŒ± Layer 3 â€” Rounded grass mounds (parallax 0.32)

Front-most ground decor â€” sits in the player's depth layer, just behind the player + chibi clouds. 9 mounds per row:

  • Soft semicircle arcs (40-64 px radius), 2-tone fresh green (lit + shaded).
  • 3 grass tufts on top of each mound (V-stroke pairs).
  • Every other mound carries a small 3-circle bush silhouette with cel outline.
  • Bottom shade band sits on the ground line for contact-shadow read.
  • Dark maps swap the green for a moonlit teal automatically.

ðŸŽ¨ Authoring notes

  • Original procedural art, no copyrighted reference. Captures the storybook side-scroller vibe with shapes + colours, not by tracing.
  • All three layers compose with the existing _mixHex helper so they pick up any map's sky palette automatically â€” a sunny map gets warm green oaks, a dusk map gets cool teal.
  • Cheap to render: layer 1 is 5 fillCircles per cluster Ã— 5 clusters; layer 2 is ~5 ops per oak Ã— 14 oaks; layer 3 is ~10 ops per mound Ã— 9 mounds. ~270 canvas ops per frame total.
  • Themed biomes (Candy Canyon, Bubblegum Swamp, Pearl Bathhouse, Stardust Atrium, Honeycomb Hollow) draw their decor LAST, so their lollipops / lily pads / brass pipes / cosmic eggs / hex pillars sit in front. The new oaks + grass mounds compose underneath, adding depth without fighting the themed art.

What stays

  • Existing faceted mountains (3 ranges), pine treeline, flower tufts, chibi clouds, birds â€” all unchanged.
  • Themed biome decor renderers untouched. The new layers paint behind them naturally.
  • Performance: extra draws are cheap fills, no shaders or expensive ops. Mobile perf gate from v0.25.14 still applies.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.64. Three new procedural blocks inside drawWorld(): distant cloud puffs (before mountains), rounded oaks (after pines), grass mounds (before chibi clouds). +85 / âˆ’1 lines.
  • No save/network/protocol impact.

v0.25.63 Distinct layouts per biome + low-gravity Stardust Atrium

User: "Could you make more variation to the monster maps, its all too similar." All five new biomes (Candy Canyon, Bubblegum Swamp, Pearl Bathhouse, Stardust Atrium, Honeycomb Hollow) used the same zigzag platform template â€” 10 main platforms + 4 small upper-tier puffs. Now each biome has its own distinct platform shape that matches the theme, plus Stardust Atrium gets low gravity as a unique movement gimmick.

ðŸ­ Candy Canyon â€” mountain peak

Platforms ascend on the left, plateau at the marshmallow summit (two wide 280-px platforms at y=190), then descend on the right. Plus two lone overhead candy stalactites at y=120 for high-jump rewards. Reads as a candy mountain rather than a flat zig-zag.

ðŸ¸ Bubblegum Swamp â€” scattered lily-pad islands

Smaller platforms (80-90 px wide) with bigger gaps between them, requiring proper jump timing. Three pad clusters (3 pads tightly packed each) give safe footholds, separated by isolated stepping stones over the bubblegum water. Stair-step cluster on the right side for the final approach to the exit portal.

ðŸ› Pearl Bathhouse â€” stepped bath tiers

Three distinct elevation bands forming a multi-level Roman bath:

  • Lower tier (y=400) â€” long bath rim ledges on each side.
  • Mid tier (y=340) â€” wider 340-px middle bath ring.
  • Upper tier (y=270) â€” top bath landing (380-px platforms).
  • Top spire (y=180) â€” pearl chamber accessible from the upper tier (140 px wide).

Plus 4 small connector ledges between tiers so the player has stair-step access without needing the main platforms.

ðŸŒŒ Stardust Atrium â€” floating islands + LOW GRAVITY

No connected staircase. Discrete celestial islands at varied altitudes, ranging from y=80 (top pinnacle) down to y=430. Tiny stepping stones in the centre at y=160-200 require precise jumps.

The new biome gets gravityMul: 0.55, re-using the existing gravity-multiplier system already used by Gravitos (which is gravityMul: 3, opposite end). Player jumps higher + falls slower in the atrium, creating a "drifting through space" feel where the wider gaps between platforms become reachable. Mobs + projectiles are also affected so the whole biome feels low-G.

ðŸ¯ Honeycomb Hollow â€” true hex pattern

Platforms arranged in a real honeycomb pattern: alternating offset rows with 110-px cells. Three full rows (bottom y=420, mid y=340, top y=260) + a 2-cell apex at y=180. The mid + top rows offset by half-cell-width to give that classic 6-sided packing. Reads as the inside of a giant beehive.

What stays

  • Biome decor renderers (lollipop trees, cattails, brass pipes, cosmic eggs, hex wall) all unchanged â€” they sit on top of the new layouts naturally.
  • Monster spawns + types per biome â€” same. Spawns adapt to wherever the new platforms are.
  • Old non-biome maps (forest, mushroom, ancient, sunsetBeach, duneSands, cryptHollow, skyGarden, frozenPeak, lavaCavern) â€” untouched. Only the five new biomes got reshaped.
  • Veteran scaling (v0.25.59) + monster skills (v0.25.58) + empowered tier (v0.25.53) â€” all compose normally with the new layouts.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.63. Five map blocks rewritten (each platforms array re-authored), gravityMul: 0.55 added to stardustAtrium. +95 / âˆ’62 lines.
  • No save/network/protocol impact. Existing saves keep their position; on entering the new layouts the player will spawn at the configured entry x and physics resolves them onto the nearest platform / ground.

v0.25.62 Bug fix â€” N key NPC dialogue (toggle, range, z-index, closeAllModals)

User: "when i press n to talk to npc the dialogue with npc does not pop up." Three real bugs in the N keybind path were stacking + a missing modal cleanup. Fixed all four. The most-likely cause of the user's report is the z-index issue â€” the NPC dialog had no z-index set, so any modal opened just before talking would visually cover it.

ðŸ› Bugs found

  1. Toggle-close mismatch. The N handler tried to close the dialog with display === 'flex' but openNPC sets display = 'block'. So pressing N a second time while the dialog was open did NOT close it â€” instead it fell through to the else branch and re-rendered the same dialog. (Not the user's symptom but related.)
  2. NPC detection range too tight. Was 60 px around the player. Town NPCs sit ~140 px apart, so the player effectively had to be standing on top of one to be detected. Widened to 80 px AND switched to closest-match instead of array-order first-match.
  3. Dialog z-index missing. #dialog had no z-index set. .modal-overlay sits at z:50. If any modal had been opened just before pressing N, the dialog would render BEHIND it â€” invisible to the user. Bumped to z:60.
  4. quest-modal not in closeAllModals. v0.25.61 added the new quest journal but didn't add it to the closeAllModals() id list. So pressing Esc or opening another dialog wouldn't close the journal cleanly.

ðŸ› ï¸ Fixes

  • Toggle: dlg.style.display === 'flex' â†’ dlg.style.display && dlg.style.display !== 'none' (covers 'block' and 'flex').
  • Range: find(npc => Math.abs(pcx - npc.x) < 60) â†’ loop over npcs and pick CLOSEST within 80 px.
  • Z-index: added z-index: 60 to #dialog.
  • closeAllModals() id list now includes 'quest-modal'.

What stays

  • NPC roles, dialogue trees, shop integration â€” all unchanged.
  • Press N behaviour: nearby chest â†’ open it, nearby NPC â†’ talk, otherwise â†’ open Codex (unchanged priority).
  • v0.25.61 quest journal still toggles via J. The closeAllModals fix means pressing Esc now also closes the journal cleanly.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.62. Four small edits: #dialog CSS gains z-index, N keybind handler refactored (toggle + range + closest-match), closeAllModals id list gains quest-modal. +30 / âˆ’5 lines.
  • No save/network/protocol impact.

v0.25.61 Quest system â€” kill / visit / boss quests with rewards (press J)

User: "Could you add more rpg gaming elements." Built a lightweight quest framework. 12 starter quests across kill / visit / boss objectives, tracked via journal + auto-progressed via existing kill + map-load hooks, with mesos + EXP rewards on completion.

ðŸ“œ The journal (press J)

  • Three sections: ðŸŽ¯ Active, ðŸ“‹ Available, âœ… Completed.
  • Quests auto-unlock into "Available" when you reach the level requirement (toast pops on unlock so you know).
  • Click Accept to start a quest â†’ moves to Active with progress tracking.
  • Click Abandon to drop an active quest (no penalty, can re-accept).
  • Each quest card shows: icon, name, description, level requirement, current progress (kill/boss), and reward summary.

ðŸŽ¯ 12 starter quests

QuestKindLvReward
ðŸŸ¢ A Slimy Welcomekill 5 slime1220ðŸ’° + 120 EXP
ðŸŒ Slow & Steadykill 8 snail1280ðŸ’° + 150 EXP
ðŸ­ Sweet Discoveriesvisit candyCanyon4500ðŸ’° + 320 EXP
ðŸ¬ Sweet Toothkill 6 gummy4480ðŸ’° + 280 EXP
ðŸ¸ Pondhoppervisit bubblegumSwamp5600ðŸ’° + 380 EXP
ðŸ¦Ž Pond Patrolkill 5 axolotl5720ðŸ’° + 450 EXP

âœ¨ Stargazervisit stardustAtrium302400ðŸ’° + 1500 EXP
