ðŸŒŒ Past the Singularityboss gravitos3532000ðŸ’° + 22000 EXP
ðŸ› Bathhouse Touristvisit pearlBathhouse404500ðŸ’° + 2800 EXP

ðŸ› ï¸ How it's wired

  • New QUESTS definitions table next to MOB_NATURAL_LEVEL.
  • player.quests = { active, completed, unlocked } initialised on player + persisted via the save schema ('quests' added to PLAYER_SAVE_FIELDS).
  • tickQuestUnlocks() â€” runs on level-up + journal-open; promotes any quest the player now meets the level gate for into the unlocked pool with a toast.
  • tickQuestKill(monsterType, isBoss) â€” called from killMonster(). Increments progress on matching kill / boss quests; auto-completes on threshold.
  • tickQuestVisit(mapId) â€” called from loadMap(). Auto-completes any active visit quest matching the map id.
  • _completeQuest(id) â€” moves quest to completed, grants rewards, fires toast + flash + level-up audio. Triggers _maybeLevelUp() in case the EXP grant levels you up.
  • New modal #quest-modal in DOM, renderQuestJournal() builds three sections with accept/abandon buttons.
  • New keybind: J toggles the journal (pauses game while open).

What stays

  • Existing daily challenges + achievements + bestiary â€” all keep running. Quests are an additive new layer.
  • Save format extended (just adds quests field). Old saves load fine; _ensureQuests() initialises on first access.
  • Veteran scaling, empowered tier, monster skills, all visual passes â€” unchanged.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.61. New QUESTS table, new player.quests state field, save-schema entry, 6 new functions (_ensureQuests / tickQuestUnlocks / tickQuestKill / tickQuestVisit / _completeQuest / acceptQuest / abandonQuest / toggleQuestJournal / renderQuestJournal), 1 new modal block, kill+map+levelup hooks, J keybind. +274 / âˆ’1 lines.
  • No network/protocol impact. Save format gains one optional field.

v0.25.60 Bug fix: slash duplicate at top diagonal Â· Warrior X gets a ground-slam finale

User: "Final action of warrior x skill character slams hard on the ground. Warrior x skill has a duplicate slash animation at the top diagonal of the screen." Two fixes: a long-standing render bug in drawSmoothFx that ghosted slashes when 2+ were alive in the same frame, and a new ground-slam coda for the Doombringer ult so the executioner's stroke actually feels final.

ðŸ› Bug â€” duplicate slash at top diagonal

Root cause in drawSmoothFx's slash branch:

ctx.translate(sx, fx.y);
ctx.rotate(fx.angle);
// ... draw arcs ...
ctx.setTransform(1,0,0,1,0,0);   // â† BUG: nukes outer transform

setTransform(identity) resets the entire matrix â€” including the HiDPI scale + camera transform that the outer drawSmoothFx's ctx.save() had preserved. After the first slash in a frame, every subsequent slash rendered at half-size at the top-left of the screen, looking like a "ghost" duplicate floating diagonally up.

This was invisible for skills that only spawn 1â€“2 slashes (basic melee, single-cast skills), but the Doombringer ult fires 5 staggered slashes over 600 ms with a 20-frame life each, so 2â€“4 slashes overlap every frame â€” the user reported the visible artefact there.

ðŸ› ï¸ Fix

Wrap the slash draw in proper ctx.save() / ctx.restore(). The outer transform survives, no more ghost slashes:

ctx.save();
ctx.translate(sx, fx.y);
ctx.rotate(fx.angle);
// ... draw arcs ...
ctx.restore();

Benefits every skill that spawns multiple spawnSmoothSlash() calls in the same frame â€” Doombringer's apoc, Phantom's chain, sword auto-attacks at high attack speed, etc.

âš”ï¸ Warrior X â€” Doombringer ground-slam finale

The Blade of Calamity used to do 5 forward slashes and justâ€¦ end. Added a coda that fires 900 ms after cast (right after the 5th slash settles):

  • Wide ground hitbox â€” 380 Ã— 80 px centered on the player at ground line.
  • Damage â€” getAtk() Ã— 3.5 with crit roll. Knocks every hit mob UP (vy âˆ’7) + AWAY from impact centre (vx Â±7 by side).
  • Visuals â€” large smooth-explosion (220 px gold + amber edge), impact star-burst, 24-particle dust shockwave radiating along the ground, 18-particle vertical impact column rising from the slam point.
  • Feel â€” addShake(28) + flash(0.7) + addHitStop(220) + crit audio. Reads as a real executioner's stroke.
  • Invuln window bumped 900 ms â†’ 1300 ms so the player isn't punished during the slam wind-up + shockwave.

ðŸ“ New ult timeline

TimeEvent
0 msCast â€” toast + initial shake/flash + sword silhouette particles spawn
0 msSlash 1 (forward, getAtk Ã— 2.4)
150 msSlash 2
300 msSlash 3
450 msSlash 4
600 msSlash 5 (guaranteed crit)
900 msGROUND SLAM finale (getAtk Ã— 3.5 wide AOE + shockwave)

What stays

  • Doombringer's existing 5 forward slashes â€” unchanged. The slam is additive, not a replacement.
  • Other warrior X variants (warlord_warcry, crusader_aegis, dragoon_skylance) â€” untouched.
  • The bug fix only changes the slash-render code path; non-slash smoothFx (explosion, beam, impact) were already wrapped correctly.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.60. Two edits: drawSmoothFx slash branch (save/restore wrap), doombringer_apoc (final scheduleSkillTimer at 900 ms). +76 / âˆ’2 lines.
  • No save/network/protocol impact.

v0.25.59 Veteran scaling â€” monster progression smoothly covers every level

User: "make the progression of monsters nicely cover close to every level." Map graph has gaps (Lv 9 â†’ 15, Lv 22 â†’ 30, Lv 30 â†’ 40), so a player at Lv 26 had no fresh map to clear and old mobs felt trivial. Added a per-mob "natural level" table + veteran scaling at spawn time. Now every player level has appropriately-tuned encounters, even when revisiting an "old" map.

ðŸ“Š Natural levels per mob type

TierMobNatural Lv
1snail1
1slime2
2mushroom3
2gummy / cookie4
2frog / axolotl5
3horny / orange / stump6 / 7 / 8
4scorpion / honeyBuzz / nougatBear12 / 14 / 16
5skeleton / mummy / zombie / wraith18 / 20 / 22 / 24
6nimbusFox / cosmicMochi30 / 32
7coralImp / pearlSprite38 / 42

âš” Veteran scaling formula

When player level is above a mob's natural level, the gap drives a stat boost:

  • HP Ã—= 1 + gap Ã— 0.030 capped at Ã—2.0
  • ATK Ã—= same
  • DEF Ã—= 1 + gap Ã— 0.025 capped at Ã—2.0
  • EXP reward Ã—= 1 + gap Ã— 0.030 capped at Ã—3.0 â€” old mobs stay worth farming
  • Mesos Ã—= 1 + gap Ã— 0.025 capped at Ã—3.0
  • Mobs at +5 levels or more get a âš” name prefix so the player can see the over-tier marker.

ðŸ“ Examples â€” same mob at different player levels

Mob (natural Lv)Player LvHP after scaleATK after scale
snail (Lv 1, base HP 70)1709
snail (Lv 1)15~158 (Ã—2.26 â†’ cap 2.0 = 140)~18 (cap 18)
snail (Lv 1)50140 (cap)18 (cap)
mushroom (Lv 3, base HP 190)15258 (Ã—1.36)30
wraith (Lv 24, base HP 970)301145 (Ã—1.18)79
wraith (Lv 24)501722 (Ã—1.78)119
coralImp (Lv 38, base HP 1400)401484 (Ã—1.06)89

So a Lv 30 player wandering back through the Mushroom Hollow now meets a âš” Shroom with 1.36Ã— HP and the same crit/EXP scale-up. Smooths the feel: any level you're at, the world has a fight that matches.

ðŸ›¡ï¸ Carve-outs

  • Bosses â€” skipped. King Slime / Mushmom / Aetherion / Gravitos / all Zodiac signs keep their authored stat curves.
  • Elite + miniboss â€” skipped, since their existing Ã—3 / Ã—5 HP multipliers already cover scaling. Stacking veteran on top would explode HP.
  • Empowered Lv 25+ candy/swamp tier from v0.25.53 â€” composes with veteran scaling. A Lv 30 player meets a Sugar Rush Gummy with empowered Ã—1.7 HP THEN Ã—1.78 veteran scale on top â†’ ~3Ã— base HP. The candy biome stays relevant deep into the late game.
  • Mini-gummy split spawns â€” the split path bypasses spawnMonster; mini-gummies stay at their fixed 60% size and base stats.

What stays

  • Map levelReq values â€” unchanged. Players still need to meet the gate to enter.
  • Visual sizing from v0.25.57 â€” composes naturally; over-tier mobs are also bigger.
  • Special skills from v0.25.58 â€” fire on the same cooldowns; the boosted ATK just makes the skill damage hit harder.
  • Loot drop rolls â€” unchanged. EXP / mesos scale with the formula above; equipment / chest drops untouched.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.59. New MOB_NATURAL_LEVEL config table, veteran-scaling block in spawnMonster() after the empowered hook. +50 / âˆ’1 lines.
  • No save/network/protocol impact. Existing saves keep their stored player level â€” the scaling is computed at spawn time so it kicks in immediately.

v0.25.58 High-tier monsters get signature special skills (groundStun, homingShot, poisonCloud, dashCharge)

User: "make high level monsters be able to have more unique skills like ground stun or shoot slow homing projectiles." Built a skill registry where each tier-4+ mob gets a signature special move on a long cooldown, separate from its basic m.shoot ranged attack. Four skill kinds shipped, each one assigned to two monster types so mid+late zones play differently.

âš”ï¸ Four skill kinds

SkillMechanic
groundStunDrops a 35-frame telegraphed shockwave on the player's column. On pulse: damage + 0.7 s player hitStun if standing in the band. Visual ring expands during the telegraph; a flashing ! appears in the last 12 frames.
homingShotSlow projectile (~3 px/frame) with homing: true â€” re-uses the existing player-tracking steering logic in updateProjectiles (gentle 0.18 accel, capped at speed 5). Player can weave it but eating one stings.
poisonCloudLingering 4-second puddle hazard at the caster's feet. Ticks damage every 24 frames + applies a 1.8 s poison DoT. Stays put â€” area-denial, not chase.
dashChargeShort fast horizontal burst (vx Â±8) toward the player. Sets m._dashCharging = 16 for a brief visual cue. Trail particles spawn behind.

ðŸ‘¾ Monster assignments

MonsterSkillCooldown
mummy (Wrappy)groundStun5.5 s
wraith (Spook)homingShot4.0 s
zombie (Rotter)poisonCloud6.0 s
skeleton (Bones)dashCharge4.5 s
coralImp (Coralite)homingShot3.8 s
pearlSprite (Pearl Cherub)groundStun5.0 s
nimbusFox (Astrofox)dashCharge4.2 s
cosmicMochi (Mochi Galaxy)homingShot4.4 s

ðŸ› ï¸ How it's wired

  • New MONSTER_SKILLS config table maps monster type â†’ {kind, cd}.
  • New MONSTER_SKILL_FNS object holds the four skill implementations. Each takes the casting monster m and pushes the appropriate hazard / projectile / state mutation.
  • updateMonsters per-frame loop: when MONSTER_SKILLS[m.type] exists, ticks m._mskTimer. On expiry checks player range (320 px x / 220 px y) + aggro target; if in range, fires the skill and resets cd with Â±15% jitter so packs don't synchronize. Out-of-range short retry (600 ms) so the cd doesn't burn while the player is far.
  • Initial m._mskTimer stagger (0.5Ã—â€“1.1Ã— of base cd) so a freshly-spawned pack doesn't all cast simultaneously.
  • Two new hazard types in updateHazards:
    • mob_quake â€” pulses on life === 0, applies damage + player.hitStun = 700 + 14 outward particles. Despawns same tick.
    • mob_poisoncloud â€” ticks every 24 frames, applies damage + extends player._poisonTimer + player._poisonDmg (re-uses existing zodiac poison DoT system). 240-frame life. Bubbling particles.
  • Two new render branches in drawHazards:
    • mob_quake â€” concentric expanding ring on the ground in the casting mob's palette colour, plus a flashing ! in the final 12 frames.
    • mob_poisoncloud â€” two-tone green puddle gradient + 4 surface-bubble pips drifting upward via tick.

ðŸ›¡ï¸ Carve-outs

  • Bosses â€” skipped entirely. They have authored AI patterns (zodiac, Aetherion, Gravitos) â€” not interfered with.
  • Tier 1-3 mobs (snail, slime, mushroom, horny, orange, stump, scorpion, gummy, cookie, frog, axolotl, honeyBuzz, nougatBear) â€” no skill assigned. Stay simple for early progression.
  • Empowered Lv 25+ candy/swamp tier (v0.25.53) â€” keeps its own special powers (split, spread, venom puff, heal aura). Doesn't double-up with MONSTER_SKILLS since those types aren't in the new registry.
  • Skill cooldowns + skill triggers respect player.invulnerable â€” i-frames during dodge / parry still protect.

What stays

  • Existing m.shoot ranged attacks â€” unchanged. Skills are additive, not replacements.
  • Stun resist, def, evasion â€” apply normally.
  • Poison DoT system â€” re-used, not duplicated.
  • Player hitStun cap from tree.stunImmune trait â€” the big skill stun honors it (player.hitStun = Math.max(player.hitStun || 0, h.stun)).

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.58. New MONSTER_SKILLS + MONSTER_SKILL_FNS config blocks, per-frame skill cooldown hook in updateMonsters, two new hazard branches in updateHazards, two new render branches in drawHazards. +205 / âˆ’1 lines.
  • No save/network/protocol impact.

v0.25.57 Monsters vary in size + scale up with player level

User: "make monsters vary in size, if they are higher level make them bigger." Two factors compose into the spawn-time size multiplier so packs feel less clone-y AND high-level zones feel more imposing.

ðŸ“ New sizing factors

FactorRangeEffect
Per-spawn varianceÃ—0.92 â†’ Ã—1.08 (Â±8%)Each spawned mob rolls a random size jitter. A pack of 5 slimes is now 5 distinct silhouettes.
Level scaleÃ—1.00 at Lv 1 â†’ Ã—1.32 at Lv 41 (+0.8%/Lv, capped)Same monster type looks visibly more imposing at high level. Reads as "deeper into the game" without changing the underlying balance numbers.

ðŸ“ Combined examples

Player LvSlime base 34Ã—28 â†’ typical spawnCookie base 38Ã—32 â†’ typical spawn
Lv 1~31â€“37 Ã— 26â€“30 px~35â€“41 Ã— 29â€“35 px
Lv 25~37â€“43 Ã— 31â€“36 px~42â€“48 Ã— 35â€“40 px
Lv 50~41â€“48 Ã— 34â€“40 px~46â€“53 Ã— 39â€“46 px

ðŸ›¡ï¸ Carve-outs

  • Bosses (King Slime, Mushmom, Aetherion, Gravitos, all 12 Zodiac signs) â€” sizing untouched. Cinematic boss silhouettes stay consistent across runs.
  • Elite + miniboss multipliers compose with the new factors so they're still visibly larger relative to base mobs (Ã—1.25 elite, Ã—1.6 miniboss layered on top).
  • Empowered Lv 25+ candy/swamp tier from v0.25.53 â€” sizing inherits naturally; an empowered "Sugar Rush" gummy at Lv 30 is bigger than a base gummy at Lv 5 thanks to the level scale.
  • Mini-gummy split spawns (from gummy split-on-death) keep their fixed 60% size override â€” the empowered split happens after the size logic so they still come out tiny.

ðŸ”§ Where it lives

Single block in spawnMonster():

const lvScale = isBoss ? 1 :
  (1 + Math.min(40, Math.max(0, (player ? player.level || 1 : 1) - 1)) * 0.008);
const variance = isBoss ? 1 : (0.92 + Math.random() * 0.16);
const sizeMul = (isBoss ? 1 : isMiniBoss ? 1.6 : isElite ? 1.25 : 1) * lvScale * variance;

sizeMul still feeds w: Math.floor(t.w * sizeMul) + h: Math.floor(t.h * sizeMul) as before. AI hitboxes scale with the visual silhouette automatically.

What stays

  • Stat scaling (HP / ATK / DEF / EXP / mesos) â€” unchanged. This is purely a visual sizing pass.
  • Per-monster authored proportions (snail's wide squat, frog's bulging eyes, axolotl's long body) â€” preserved through the scale.
  • v0.25.55 player attack halving still applies; combined with bigger silhouettes the kill-time feels more substantial at high level.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.57. One block in spawnMonster(). +13 / âˆ’2 lines.
  • No save/network/protocol impact.

v0.25.56 Two more biomes â€” Stardust Atrium (Lv 30) + Honeycomb Hollow (Lv 15)

User: "Build more maps, cute legendary creatures." Two new biomes, four new legendary creatures, both taxi-accessible from the start.

ðŸŒŒ Stardust Atrium (Lv 30+)

  • Sky: deep navy â†’ twilight purple â†’ pink (#0a0830 â†’ #3a2a6e â†’ #a868c8).
  • Nebula bands drift across the sky with parallax â€” pastel violet + warm pink stacked ellipses.
  • Drifting cosmic eggs every 360 px â€” large pastel pink-purple orbs with concentric spiral patterns + tiny white star pips inside, gentle vertical bob, glossy specular sheen.
  • Star-pillar columns every 320 px â€” translucent violet pillars with a glowing 4-point constellation rune at the top inside a golden ring + soft halo.
  • Golden constellation outlines on the floor â€” connected dot-stars trace short shapes every 400 px.
  • 18 drifting star sparkles twinkle in the sky, opacity pulses via game.time.

ðŸ¯ Honeycomb Hollow (Lv 15+)

  • Sky: amber â†’ honey â†’ cream (#ffaa55 â†’ #ffd07c â†’ #fff0c0).
  • Honeycomb wall at y=80-360 â€” 6-sided hexagonal grid (sqrt(3)-spaced) with darker inner pip + amber haze overlay.
  • Dripping honey waterfalls every 360 px â€” golden vertical streams (3-tone) with animated 3-drop trickle + puddle at the base.
  • Glowing amber lanterns hanging from the canopy every 280 px â€” round amber bulbs on chains, soft glow halo, brass cap, gentle bob.
  • Fuzzy bee-flower clusters along the ground every 220 px â€” 5-petal amber flowers w/ centre disc on green stems.
  • 12 golden pollen sparkles drifting up through the air with slow upward motion.

ðŸ¦Š New legendary creatures

MobStatsLook
Astrofox (nimbusFox)Lv 30+ Â· HP 980 Â· ATK 62 Â· ranged mdarkLavender-pink kitsune body, two pointy ears with pink inner pips, two long trailing tails each ending in a glowing 4-point golden star, happy closed-arc eyes, pink snout dot, cheek star sparkle.
Mochi Galaxy (cosmicMochi)Lv 30+ Â· HP 1180 Â· ATK 54 Â· slow tankSquishy pillowy rounded-square body, translucent purple galaxy spiral inside (3 spiral arms + star pips), bright dot eyes floating in front, smile, top-left pearl spec.
Buzzbee (honeyBuzz)Lv 15+ Â· HP 485 Â· ATK 42 Â· ranged msporeChubby round yellow-gold bumblebee, 3 black candy-stripe abdomen bands, lacy translucent wings, two antennae with pink tips, big shiny eyes + cheek blush.
Nougat Bear (nougatBear)Lv 15+ Â· HP 740 Â· ATK 52Chubby caramel-gold bear silhouette, amber tummy patch, sticky golden honey drip on belly (legendary tell), inner amber ear pips, snout dot + smile, glossy specular gloss.

ðŸ—ºï¸ Connections

  • Honeycomb Hollow â€” side route from Dune Sands at x=1300. Internal portals: â—€ Dune Sands.
  • Stardust Atrium â€” side route from Crypt of Whispers at x=300. Internal portals: â—€ Crypt of Whispers.
  • Both biomes taxiAccessible: true â€” show up in the Taxi list immediately at any level (entry still gated by levelReq).

ðŸ› ï¸ How it's wired

  • monsterTypes â€” added 4 entries.
  • MAPS.stardustAtrium + MAPS.honeycombHollow â€” full definitions with new isStardustAtrium / isHoneycombHollow flags.
  • drawMonster dispatch â€” 4 new branches â†’ 4 new _drawDQ* renderers.
  • drawWorld â€” 2 new biome decor hooks gated on the flags.
  • MOB_CHAT_LINES â€” 4 new chatter arrays (yip!, twinkle, bzzz~, etc.).
  • Existing EMPOWERED_TIER from v0.25.53 untouched â€” these new biomes are not in the empowered roster (yet).

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.56. New: 2 map blocks, 4 monster types, 4 chatter line arrays, 4 sprite renderers, 2 biome decor renderers, 2 hooks in drawWorld, 4 hooks in drawMonster, 3 portal pushes (cryptHollow up to atrium, duneSands down to hollow). +710 / âˆ’2 lines.
  • No save/network/protocol impact.

v0.25.55 Halve player attack â€” monsters were dying too fast

User: "half all attack by main character, the monsters are dying too quickly." Done. Single-knob change: introduced a global PLAYER_ATK_MUL = 0.50 constant and applied it at the very end of getAtk(). Every player damage source â€” basic attacks (melee / arrow / dagger / bolt), all skills, all ultimates, pet/pack hits, milestone procs â€” pulls through this function, so the 50% nerf hits everything uniformly without touching individual skill formulas.

ðŸ”ª What's affected

  • All player damage â€” basic attacks, every skill, every ultimate, masteries, pet/pack hits.
  • Crit damage still works as a multiplier on top, just on a lower base.
  • Equipment ATK + STR/INT scaling + buffs (warCry, bloodlust, rampage, comboAtk, prestige dmgMult) still compose normally; the halve is the very last operation in the chain.

ðŸ›¡ï¸ What's not affected

  • Monster damage â€” unchanged. They hit just as hard as before.
  • Crit chance / accuracy / DEF / HP â€” unchanged.
  • Loot / EXP / mesos drops â€” unchanged. Time-to-kill goes up; reward per kill is the same.
  • Empowered Lv 25+ candy/swamp tier (v0.25.53) â€” unchanged. Their stat bumps + special powers stay; the player just chips away half as fast.

ðŸŽšï¸ Single tuning knob

Future rebalances tweak PLAYER_ATK_MUL in one spot near GLOBAL_SKILL_DMG_MUL. No need to edit every per-skill formula.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.55. New PLAYER_ATK_MUL constant, one multiplication added in getAtk()'s final return. +8 / âˆ’2 lines.
  • No save/network/protocol impact.

v0.25.54 New ultra-high-level biome â€” Pearl Bathhouse + coralImp & pearlSprite (Lv 40+)

User: "Make ultra high level map where monsters > level 40 cute underwater bathhouse. Pastel tiled floors with colorful coral growing through the cracks, giant pearl-oysters, and brass pipes blowing streams of round bubbles. Soft neon bioluminescence in pinks and teals. Rich 32-bit color depth, smooth pixel gradients, relaxing and bubbly atmosphere." Built it. Lv 40 entry gate, taxi-accessible by default, dead-end farming zone with two new tier-5+ residents.

ðŸ› The biome

  • Sky: deep ocean â†’ teal underwater gradient (#1a3a5a â†’ #3a6a9a â†’ #88c8e8).
  • Underwater shimmer overlay â€” subtle teal tint over upper half + pink tint over lower half.
  • Tiled bathhouse wall at y=180-360 â€” vertical pastel tile strips (pink / teal / yellow / lavender) with horizontal grout lines every 30 px and bioluminescent pink cracks every 320 px.
  • Brass bubble pipes every 420 px â€” golden 3-tone brass stems with a circular nozzle on top, blowing 5-bubble streams that drift upward via game.time. Each bubble has a teal-translucent body, white outline, and a specular pip.
  • Giant pearl-oysters every 360 px â€” open-clam silhouette with ribbed top shell + closed bottom shell + a pulsing luminescent pearl inside (pink + teal halo, iridescent core).
  • Coral fronds every 140 px along the ground â€” branchy 3-stem stalks with side branches + tip pips. Palette rotates pink / teal / orange-coral.
  • Neon bioluminescent particles â€” 14 drifting pink + teal sparkles with soft halos suggest plankton + soft underwater light.

ðŸ‘» New monsters (Lv 40+)

MonsterStatsLook
coralImp (Coralite)HP 1400 Â· ATK 84 Â· DEF 13 Â· 1.0 spd Â· ranged mdarkCoral-pink round chibi with three branchy teal coral fronds growing from the head, glowing pulsing pink core in the chest, bright teal eyes, open "o" mouth singing bubbles. Soft outer pink bioluminescent glow halo.
pearlSprite (Pearl Cherub)HP 1700 Â· ATK 96 Â· DEF 16 Â· 0.8 spdPearl-white round body with two small teal feather wings, golden halo ring floating above the head, iridescent pink-pearl specular sheen, closed-arc anime eyes (cute "hush" vibe), pink luminescent cheek glow, serene smile.

ðŸ—ºï¸ Map layout

  • World width: 2700 px. Level requirement: 40.
  • Spawns: 7 coralImps + 5 pearlSprites + 2 wraiths (continuity with the Crypt Hollow neighbour).
  • Platforms: 10 mosaic tile ledges along the main floor, plus 4 small upper-tier pearl ledges for vertical traversal.
  • Portals: â—€ Crypt of Whispers at x=80 only â€” this is a dead-end farm zone. Crypt Hollow gets a new â¬‡ Pearl Bathhouse portal at x=1500.
  • Taxi-accessible by default â€” the taxiAccessible: true flag from v0.25.52 means players hit the destination via taxi from Lv 40 onward without walking the full Crypt route.

ðŸŽ¨ Style fidelity to the brief

  • Pastel tiled floors âœ“ â€” 4-colour mosaic stripe rotation with grout.
  • Colorful coral growing through cracks âœ“ â€” pink/teal cracks in the wall + 3-palette coral fronds along the ground.
  • Giant pearl-oysters âœ“ â€” ribbed clam silhouettes with pulsing iridescent pearls.
  • Brass pipes blowing streams of round bubbles âœ“ â€” 3-tone brass stems with animated 5-bubble streams.
  • Soft neon bioluminescence in pinks + teals âœ“ â€” outer glow halos on every prop + 14 drifting plankton sparkles + pulsing pearl glows.
  • Rich 32-bit color depth âœ“ â€” 4-stop palettes per element instead of the 2-tone vocabulary used in earlier biomes.
  • Smooth pixel gradients âœ“ â€” radial-style alpha-stop pearl glows, bubble alpha falloff, water shimmer overlay.
  • Relaxing + bubbly âœ“ â€” drifting bubbles, plankton, halo pulses; no mob aggression triggers from biome decor itself.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.54. New: pearlBathhouse map block, 2 monster types, 2 chatter line arrays, 2 sprite renderers (_drawDQCoralImp + _drawDQPearlSprite), 1 biome decor renderer (drawPearlBathhouseDecor), 1 dispatch hook in drawMonster, 1 hook in drawWorld, 1 portal push on cryptHollow. +360 / âˆ’2 lines.
  • No save/network/protocol impact.

v0.25.53 Empowered tier â€” candy/swamp mobs gain special powers at Lv 25+

User: "Make monsters from bubblegumSwamp and candyCanyon stronger higher level for above level 25 make them have special powers." Each of the four candy/swamp residents now spawns as an upgraded variant once the player crosses Lv 25, with bigger stats AND a unique flavoured special power per type.

ðŸ“ˆ Empowered stats (vs base)

MobVariantHPATKDEFEXP / Mesos
gummySugar Rush GummibeauÃ—1.7Ã—1.50Ã—1.3Ã—1.6
cookieCrumbling Choco-chipÃ—1.7Ã—1.55Ã—1.4Ã—1.7
frogVenom PondhopÃ—1.6Ã—1.60Ã—1.3Ã—1.7
axolotlMystic LottieÃ—1.9Ã—1.50Ã—1.5Ã—1.8

âœ¨ Special powers (per type)

  • Sugar Rush Gummy â€” split-on-death. When killed, spits out two Mini Gummibeaus (60% size, faster, weaker, can't chain-split). Pink burst particles + a small camera shake sell the split. Mini-gummies are still farmable for mastery but give 30% reward each.
  • Crumbling Choco-chip â€” fires a 3-shot mspore spread per ranged volley instead of a single shot. The angular fan is computed inline by passing an angleOffset to fireMonsterProjectile (-0.40 / 0 / +0.40 rad). Shoot cooldown also tightened 2300 â†’ 1500 ms.
  • Venom Pondhop â€” passive venom-puff barrage. Every ~1.6 s within 280 px range, emits a homing toxic projectile (mtoxic colour, 2.2 s poison) that stacks ON TOP of the standard mtoxic shot. Shoot cd also tightened 2600 â†’ 1700 ms; jump bumped 8 â†’ 11.
  • Mystic Lottie â€” heal aura. Every 28 frames regens 1.8% of own maxHp AND heals nearby empowered allies (radius 110 px) by 60% of that tick. Renders a 6-particle pink pulse ring on tick so the player can see the aura is firing. The biggest HP pool of the four (Ã—1.9) plus the regen makes Mystic axolotls the priority kill in any pack.

ðŸ› ï¸ How it's wired

  • New EMPOWERED_TIER config table â€” per-type stat multipliers + flag name + name prefix. Lives next to monsterTypes.
  • spawnMonster â€” after the base mob is pushed, if player.level >= 25 AND type is in EMPOWERED_TIER AND mob isn't elite/mini/boss, applies the multipliers + flag. Same type string so mastery / bestiary doesn't fragment.
  • fireMonsterProjectile(m, angleOffset) â€” gained an optional radians-offset parameter that rotates the aim vector before normalisation. 3-shot spread is one call site, no duplicated trajectory math.
  • updateMonsters per-frame loop â€” added two empowered hooks alongside the existing m.shoot + burnTimer blocks: venom puff (frog) and heal aura (axolotl). Both gated on m._empoweredVenom / m._empoweredAura.
  • killMonster â€” new top-of-function check for m._empoweredSplit && !m._isMiniSplit. Pushes 2 mini-gummy entries directly into game.monsters with the _isMiniSplit flag so they bypass the empower-on-spawn check (no recursive splitting).

What didn't change

  • Below Lv 25 â€” mobs spawn at base stats, no empower applied. Early players keep the existing curve.
  • Elite / mini-boss / boss tiers â€” those existing modifiers compose with the base stats; empowered tier is skipped for them since they already buff their own way.
  • Bestiary / mastery â€” empowered mobs share the same type string as base so kill counts pool.
  • Loot tables, projectile sprites, AI patterns â€” all reused from base. Only the multipliers + special-power hooks are new.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.53. New: EMPOWERED_TIER table, spawn hook, special-power hooks in updateMonsters + killMonster, angleOffset param on fireMonsterProjectile. +126 / âˆ’5 lines.
  • No save/network/protocol impact. Existing saves keep their stored state â€” empower applies on spawn, not retroactively.

v0.25.52 Taxi: Candy Canyon + Bubblegum Swamp listable without prior visit

User: "make the taxi able to teleport to these maps." The taxi was already capable of routing to any visited non-boss map, but new players hadn't walked to the Candy Canyon or Bubblegum Swamp yet â€” so the new biomes weren't appearing in the destination list. Added a taxiAccessible: true map flag that lets a map opt into the taxi list regardless of visit state.

ðŸ› ï¸ Implementation

  • MAPS.candyCanyon + MAPS.bubblegumSwamp now carry taxiAccessible: true.
  • renderTaxi() rewritten to merge two sources for the destination list:
    1. All map IDs in game.visitedMaps (the prior behaviour).
    2. Any map ID where MAPS[id].taxiAccessible === true (the new opt-in).
    Both sources are de-duped via a Set and then filtered to exclude boss arenas (so players still have to earn boss fights).

ðŸš• What you get

  • Open the Taxi panel from anywhere outside a boss arena.
  • Sweet Candy Canyon and Bubblegum Swamp appear as destinations on a fresh save â€” no need to walk through Mushroom â†’ Candy Canyon â†’ Bubblegum Swamp first.
  • Standard 50 Lumen fare applies (matches every other taxi destination).
  • Existing taxi UX kept: arrival toast, lumens deducted, you spawn at x = 200 facing right.

ðŸ“‹ Future-proofing

Any future biome can opt into taxi-without-visit by adding a single taxiAccessible: true field to its MAPS entry. No rendering changes needed.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.52. Three edits: the two flags on the candy / swamp map blocks, and the destination-merge logic in renderTaxi. +12 / âˆ’2 lines.
  • No save/network/protocol impact. Existing game.visitedMaps persistence unchanged â€” the new flag is read-only state, not stored per-save.

v0.25.51 New biome â€” Bubblegum Swamp + frog & axolotl monsters

User: "Make new maps of the similar art style: adorable swamp biome. Bright pink bubblegum water, floating lily pads that look like macarons, and weeping willow trees with glowing teal leaves. Little mud-puddles and cattails for cute frog and axolotl monsters. Lush, high-color pixel environment." Built it. Companion biome to the Candy Canyon, branching off at x=1300 in that map. Lv 5 entry gate, slots between Candy Canyon (Lv 4) and Elderwood Grove (Lv 6).

ðŸŒ¸ The biome

  • Sky: pink â†’ lavender â†’ soft teal pastel gradient (#ffd9ee â†’ #e8c9ff â†’ #a8d4e8).
  • Bubblegum water at y=488 â€” vibrant 3-tone pink band (#ff9bce â†’ #ffb4dc â†’ #ffd0e8) with 10 white foam pops drifting horizontally and 6 sparkle clusters glittering on the surface.
  • Macaron lily pads every 180 px â€” 2-disc sandwich with a cream filling between top + bottom shells. Palette rotates pastel pink / mint / lavender per pad. Each one bobs gently via a per-x sine, has a soft drop shadow on the water, and a glossy specular pip on the top shell.
  • Weeping willow trees every 360 px â€” chunky 14 px ink trunk with knobbly silhouette, then a 5-lobe canopy of glowing teal leaves (#2aa896 base + #5fdcb6 bright cel highlight + rgba(180,255,230,0.85) glow pip). A faint rgba(80,255,220,0.22) halo paints first behind the canopy for the "glowing" tell. 7 weeping tendrils hang down with sine-driven sway, each ending in a glowing tip pip.
  • Cattail clusters every 280 px â€” 3 stalks with curved stems, sword-leaf blades, fluffy brown spike tufts on top. Sway driven by game.time. Sit in a small mud puddle on the ground (#4a2a1a outer + #2a1408 inner ellipse).
  • Floating bubblegum bubbles drift up through the sky with parallax â€” 8 pink/lavender translucent circles each with a glossy white pip, animated upward via game.time.

ðŸ¸ New monsters

MonsterStatsBehaviour
frog (Pondhop)HP 340 Â· ATK 30 Â· 1.0 spd Â· jump 8 Â· ranged toxicWide squat green silhouette with bulging eye-domes on top of the head, two front feet, cream belly patch, pink cheek blush, wide froggy smile. Fires mtoxic projectiles every 2.6 s. Big jump for cattail-to-lily-pad traversal.
axolotl (Lottie)HP 520 Â· ATK 38 Â· 0.8 spd Â· jump 3Long-bodied pink salamander silhouette with three feathered pink gill fronds wobbling behind the head, short triangular tail fin, happy smile + cheek blush. Glossy translucent specular on the upper back.

ðŸ—ºï¸ Map layout

  • World width: 2500 px. Level requirement: 5.
  • Spawns: 8 frogs + 6 axolotls + 2 slimes (continuity).
  • Platforms: 10 macaron lily-pad platforms (chunky 14 px tall) along the swamp main-line, plus 4 small upper-tier pads at y=200-230 for vertical traversal up into the willow canopies.
  • Portals: â—€ Sweet Candy Canyon at x=80, â–¶ Elderwood Grove at x=2420. Candy Canyon gets a new â¬‡ Bubblegum Swamp portal at x=1300 to wire the side-route.

ðŸ› ï¸ How it's wired

  • monsterTypes â€” added frog + axolotl.
  • MAPS.bubblegumSwamp â€” full definition with isBubblegumSwamp: true flag.
  • MAPS.candyCanyon.portals â€” pushed the side-route portal at x=1300.
  • drawMonster dispatch â€” new _drawDQFrog + _drawDQAxolotl renderers using the existing _celFillPath / _celStroke / _celEye cel-art helpers.
  • drawWorld â€” added if (game.mapData.isBubblegumSwamp) drawBubblegumSwampDecor().
  • MOB_CHAT_LINES â€” frog (ribbit~ / splosh! / hop hop / croak / lily!) + axolotl (blub blub / pondwave~ / swish~ / bubbly / cuddle).

ðŸŽ¨ Style fidelity to the brief

  • Bright pink bubblegum water âœ“ â€” vibrant 3-tone pink band with foam pops + sparkles.
  • Macaron lily pads âœ“ â€” 2-disc sandwich, cream filling, pastel rotation.
  • Weeping willows with glowing teal leaves âœ“ â€” 5-lobe teal canopy + faint cyan halo + glow pips on tendril tips.
  • Mud puddles + cattails âœ“ â€” every 280 px, 3 stalks per cluster, brown spike tufts + sword-leaf blades.
  • Cute frog + axolotl âœ“ â€” froggy bulging-eye chibi + axolotl with feathered pink gills.
  • Lush, high-color pixel âœ“ â€” saturated palette throughout, multiple specular pips per prop, drifting bubbles + foam + sparkle layers.

Touched surface

New: bubblegumSwamp map block, 2 monster types, 2 chatter line arrays, 2 sprite renderers (_drawDQFrog + _drawDQAxolotl), 1 biome decor renderer (drawBubblegumSwampDecor), 1 dispatch hook in drawMonster, 1 hook in drawWorld, 1 portal push on candyCanyon. +330 / âˆ’2 lines.

v0.25.50 New biome â€” Sweet Candy Canyon + gummy & cookie monsters

User: "Could you make new maps, 2D top-down game environment map, sweet candy canyon biome. Features marshmallow boulders, a flowing chocolate river, and towering lollipop trees. Habitat designed for cute gummy and cookie monsters. Pastel pink, blue, and yellow color palette. Whimsical fantasy MMO style, clean line art, soft shading, extremely cute." Built it. New tier-2 biome that branches off the Fungal Hollow with a Lv 4 entry gate, slotted between mushroom and ancient on the progression curve.

ðŸ­ The biome

  • Sky: pastel pink â†’ pastel blue â†’ pastel yellow gradient (#ffd8e8 â†’ #cce8ff â†’ #fff4b8).
  • Ground: standard cute-cel earth (procedural fallback) so the candy props pop against a familiar floor.
  • Lollipop trees every 320 px â€” tall white-and-red striped sticks with massive swirly candy discs on top. Disc colour rotates pink / blue / yellow per tree, each with a 3-ring spiral swirl + edge cel shadow + glossy specular pip on the upper-left.
  • Marshmallow boulder platforms every 220 px â€” chunky rounded-rectangle pillows with a top-edge highlight ribbon and a 2-tone cel shadow on the lower-right. Soft elliptical drop shadow underneath.
  • Chocolate river â€” 3-tone brown band (#5a2a1a â†’ #7a3a26 â†’ #9a4a32) at y=488, with 8 cream foam swirls drifting horizontally driven by game.time.
  • Background candy stripes drifting in the upper sky with parallax â€” adds depth without competing for attention.

ðŸ» New monsters

MonsterStatsBehaviour
gummy (Gummibeau)HP 280 Â· ATK 26 Â· 0.9 spd Â· jump 5Squat jelly bear silhouette with rounded ears, pink ear pips, cream belly patch, glossy translucent specular gloss. Wobbles + jumps.
cookie (Choco-chip)HP 430 Â· ATK 34 Â· 0.7 spd Â· ranged sporeRound chocolate-chip cookie disc with 5 fixed chips, crispy inner ring, big om-nom mouth + happy eyes. Fires mspore projectiles every 2.3 s.

ðŸ—ºï¸ Map layout

  • World width: 2600 px. Level requirement: 4.
  • Spawns: 8 gummies + 6 cookies + 3 slimes (the slimes give continuity with neighbouring biomes).
  • Platforms: 10 marshmallow boulders (chunky 14 px tall) along the main canyon, plus 4 small upper-tier puffs at y=200-230 for vertical traversal.
  • Portals: â—€ Fungal Hollow (back) at x=80, â–¶ Elderwood Grove (forward) at x=2520 â€” slots Candy Canyon as an alternate path from mushroom into ancient. The Fungal Hollow gets a new â¬‡ Sweet Candy Canyon portal at x=1300 to wire the side route.

ðŸ› ï¸ How it's wired

  • monsterTypes â€” added gummy + cookie entries with pastel color + shell fields.
  • MAPS.candyCanyon â€” full map definition with isCandyCanyon: true flag.
  • MAPS.mushroom.portals â€” pushed the side-route portal to Candy Canyon at x=1300.
  • drawMonster dispatch â€” routes gummy / cookie to new _drawDQGummy / _drawDQCookie renderers (uses the existing _celFillPath / _celStroke / _celEye cel-art helpers, so they match the existing chibi vocabulary).
  • drawWorld â€” added if (game.mapData.isCandyCanyon) drawCandyCanyonDecor() after the procedural sky pass and before the vignette.
  • MOB_CHAT_LINES â€” added gummy chatter (squish! / sweet~ / wobble / jelly~ / mwah!) and cookie chatter (crumble / crunchy! / choco~ / om nom / bake!).

ðŸŽ¨ Style fidelity to the reference brief

  • Pastel pink, blue, yellow palette âœ“ â€” sky gradient, lollipop disc rotation, sky stripes.
  • Clean line art âœ“ â€” 2.5 px ink outline on every prop, 2 px on monsters.
  • Soft shading âœ“ â€” every prop has a 2-tone cel + a glossy white specular pip, matching the existing v0.25.41-49 character/equipment cel vocabulary.
  • Extremely cute âœ“ â€” candy stripes drifting, foam swirls flowing on the chocolate river, gummy ears wobble + cookie nom-mouth.

Touched surface

New: candyCanyon map block, 2 monster types, 2 chatter line arrays, 2 sprite renderers (_drawDQGummy + _drawDQCookie), 1 biome decor renderer (drawCandyCanyonDecor), 1 dispatch hook in drawMonster, 1 hook in drawWorld, 1 portal push on mushroom. +254 / âˆ’2 lines.

v0.25.49 Head shading â€” hard-edged 2-tone cel matching chick reference

User attached a chick sticker reference and asked for the head to match its style. The reference is classic flat mobile-game cel: solid base + ONE hard-edged darker crescent + ONE small crisp white pip. v0.25.42's radial gradients were too painterly for that vocabulary â€” replaced with hard-edged shape arithmetic.

ðŸ”ª Why hard-edged this time

The chick reference has zero gradients. The shadow boundary is a CRISP curved line, not a soft falloff. The specular is a single small egg shape with no halo, no secondary pip, no gradient. v0.25.42's radial-gradient form shadow + 3-stack specular + rim light was the right call for the previous painterly reference, but it's the wrong call here.

ðŸ› ï¸ New head shading (3 layers, all clipped to head circle)

  1. Form shadow â€” single hard-edged crescent on the bottom-right. Drawn as head circle minus a shifted "lit moon" circle (centre offset up-and-left by hr Ã— 0.42). The fill operation paints only the crescent, giving an exact crisp boundary. Tone is CUTE_PAL.skinShd â€” the same dark skin colour used elsewhere.
  2. Chin cast shadow â€” hard-edged ellipse at the jaw, same skinShd tone, no falloff. Sells "head sits on body" without softening the cel look.
  3. Specular â€” single small white pip (hr Ã— 0.18 Ã— 0.11 ellipse, tilted -0.30 rad) on upper-left. #ffffff opaque, no halo, no secondary pip, no gradient.

ðŸš« What's gone (v0.25.42 features deleted)

  • Radial-gradient form shadow (skinDeep â†’ skinShd â†’ transparent stops).
  • SSS warm-pink radial wash at the lit/shadow seam.
  • 2-stack chin shadow with outer purple-tinged 32% band.
  • 3-stack glossy specular (halo + body + crisp core).
  • Tiny secondary "wet sheen" pip floating above the spec.
  • 2-stop warm rim light crescent on the bottom-right.

None of these read at chick-sticker scale + style. Removed in favour of the cleaner three-layer pass.

ðŸ“ The crescent trick

ctx.fillStyle = CUTE_PAL.skinShd;
ctx.beginPath();
ctx.arc(hcx, hcy, hr, 0, Math.PI * 2);                              // outer
ctx.arc(hcx - hr * 0.42, hcy - hr * 0.42, hr, Math.PI * 2, 0, true); // inner (CCW)
ctx.closePath();
ctx.fill();

Drawing two arcs in opposite directions inside the same path creates an even-odd fill region â€” the lit "moon" is excluded so only the shadow crescent renders. Crisp boundary at the intersection of the two circles, exactly the curve the reference shows.

What stays

  • Head outline ring (2 px black halo).
  • Skin base fill (CUTE_PAL.skin).
  • Hair, eyes, mouth, blush â€” drawn AFTER the shading on top.
  • Torso / arm / leg / boot 4-tone shading from v0.25.41.
  • Equipment 3-tone polish from v0.25.48 (cape, hats, blades, bow, staff).

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.49. One block in drawCuteCharacter rewritten (head shading). +27 / âˆ’80 lines (net negative â€” three crisp layers replace six gradient layers).
  • No save/network/protocol impact.

v0.25.48 3-tone cel-shading on cape, hats, blades, bow, staff

User: "improve the graphics of the character and equipments 2-3 shade cellshading render." Existing equipment was running mostly 2-tone (lit + shadow). Pushed it to 3-tone (highlight ridge + lit base + mid shadow + deep shadow stripe) so each piece reads as a proper cylindrical / curved form instead of a flat silhouette.

ðŸ§¥ Cape

  • Added capeHi (mix capeBase â†’ white 0.30) â€” 2 px highlight ribbon along the top edge where the cape catches shoulder light.
  • Added capeDeep (mix capeShd â†’ black 0.45) â€” 2 px deep-shadow band at the hem fold gives the cape weight + form-curve.
  • Existing diagonal mid-shadow + ink contour kept on top.

ðŸŽ© Hats (4 classes)

  • New hatDeep tone derived per call (mix cc.bodyShd â†’ black 0.40).
  • Wizard cone â€” deep rim line under the brim's existing shadow band.
  • Warrior helmet â€” deep stripe just above the bottom rim, dome reads as 3-tone (hi / metal / shd / deep).
  • Rogue bandana â€” deep band along the lower fold.
  • Archer cap â€” deep band where the cap meets the head.

ðŸ—¡ï¸ Swords (active swing + idle pose)

  • Was 3-tone via blade / bladeHi / bladeShd; pushed to 5-stop: bladeHi + 1px crisp lit edge + blade base + bladeShd + 1px deep rim.
  • Reads as a forged steel cylinder, not a flat 6-wide rectangle.
  • Existing travelling specular gleam + tip pip + crossguard glint stay.

ðŸ”ª Daggers (active + idle)

  • Idle pose was 2-tone (just bladeHi over base); now matches the active dagger with bladeShd + deep rim + bright lit edge.
  • Travelling gleam + tip pip kept.

ðŸª„ Staff

  • Shaft was 3-tone wood; added a 0.50-alpha deep-shadow stripe on the right-edge of the shaft so the cylinder reads.
  • Grip wrap also gets a 1px deep-shadow rim on the right edge.
  • Existing crystal orb + animated sparkles unchanged.

ðŸ¹ Bow

  • Was 3-pass (outline + body + thin highlight); added an inner deep-shadow curve stroke on the back of the bow (less light there) and a bright near-white sheen on the belly of the curve. Reads as a properly-formed wooden bow with a glossy lacquer.
  • Grip wrap deep-shadow rim added.

What stays

  • v0.25.41 character cel-shading (4-tone torso + arms + legs).
  • v0.25.42 head shading (radial form shadow + glossy spec).
  • All v0.25.30 illustrative polish (sheen pips, tip glints, animated gleams).
  • Equipment vis blocks in ITEM_POOL â€” same blade/bladeHi/bladeShd inputs drive the new 5-stop renders.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.48. Surgical additions inside _drawCuteCape, drawCuteHat (mage / warrior / rogue / archer branches), _drawCuteSword, _drawCuteSwordIdle, _drawCuteDagger, _drawCuteDaggerIdle, _drawCuteStaff, _drawCuteBow. +45 / âˆ’13 lines.
  • No save/network/protocol impact. Per-class palettes (CUTE_CLASS_COLORS) and item vis blocks unchanged.

v0.25.47 Harder levelling curve

User: "make levelling curve harder." The previous curve let players sprint to L50 in roughly an hour at decent zone clearing rates â€” too fast given the v0.25.46 zodiac difficulty buff. Bumped each tier of the per-level multiplier so the late game actually grinds, without making the early levels feel punishing.

ðŸ“ˆ New curve

Level rangeOld multiplierNew multiplier
1 â€“ 291.18Ã—1.20Ã—
30 â€“ 391.20Ã—1.24Ã—
40+1.22Ã—1.28Ã—

Initial expToNext also bumped from 100 â†’ 120 so the very first level-up takes ~20% longer too.

ðŸ§® Cumulative EXP (L1 â†’ target)

TargetOld curveNew curveRatio
L101,904 EXP2,491 EXP1.31Ã—
L2012,331 EXP18,558 EXP1.50Ã—
L3066,928 EXP118,072 EXP1.76Ã—
L40382,324 EXP869,206 EXP2.27Ã—
L502,538,214 EXP8,742,520 EXP3.44Ã—

Front-loaded onto the late game on purpose: L10 is essentially the same pace, L20 a tiny bit slower, but L40+ properly walls. A run that used to clear to L50 in ~1 hr will now take 3-4 hrs of dedicated kill clearing â€” or you can prestige earlier and use the +30%/+30%/+1AP per ascension to outpace the wall over multiple runs.

ðŸ› ï¸ What changed in code

  • _maybeLevelUp(): curveMul tiers updated.
  • Player init: expToNext: 100 â†’ 120.
  • Prestige reset: same bump.
  • Press-9 dev cheat (jump to L50): replaced flat Math.pow(1.18, 49) with a per-level loop matching the new tiered curve, so the bar shows the right L50 EXP value after the cheat fires.

What stays

  • Phase / advancement gates â€” JOB at L25, MASTER at L50. Slot unlock thresholds slotLevelReq() unchanged.
  • Per-kill EXP rewards untouched. The math pushes the curve up; the EXP income stays the same.
  • Press-8 dev XP burst (+40% expToNext) â€” still works, just gives smaller fractional jumps at high levels because the bar is bigger.
  • Prestige multipliers (+30% xpMult per ascension) â€” unchanged. Multi-prestige players will feel the new wall less.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.47. Three call sites edited (_maybeLevelUp curveMul, initial player.expToNext, prestige reset, dev-cheat L50 formula). +22 / âˆ’7 lines.
  • No save/network/protocol impact. Existing saves keep their stored expToNext; the new curve only kicks in on the next level-up.

v0.25.46 Zodiac bosses harder + Scorpio burrow bug fixed

User: "make the horoscope bosses mechanism harder to fight, scorpio has a bug in which it buries underground." Two-part patch: a global zodiac difficulty buff, and a complete rework of Scorpio's burrow attack so it stops getting stuck below the floor.

ðŸ› Scorpio burrow bug â€” root cause

The old burrow mutated m.y += 3 / m.y -= 4 per AI tick while the standard monster-physics block kept applying gravity (m.vy += 0.5) and clamping to platforms. The two systems fought each other: gravity accumulated faster than the AI's m.y -= 4 rise, so Scorpio could "miss" the surface during the rise phase and end up at m.y > 1000 after a few cycles â€” visually buried, mechanically stuck. The platform-collision gate (entity.y + entity.h < p.y + p.h + 20) only triggers if the boss is just intersecting the ground from above; once the boss's bottom passes the ground's bottom, the clamp stops engaging and the boss falls forever.

ðŸ› ï¸ Burrow rework

  • New m._noGravity flag in the boss physics block â€” when set, the monster-physics gravity + collision-clamp + position update is skipped entirely. AI drives m.x and m.y directly. Velocities zeroed each tick so they can't accumulate during the suspension.
  • Captures m._burrowGroundY on entry so the surface y is known. All sink/travel/erupt/settle phases use absolute targets relative to it (no more += 3 drift).
  • 4-phase state machine with bounded timers:
    • 0 â€“ 250 ms â€” SINK: slide 60 px down into the ground, dust particles spit upward.
    • 250 â€“ 800 ms â€” TRAVEL: smooth chase to player x at 6%/tick lag. Phase 2/3 emit a violet/pink ground-warning column at the boss's column so the player can read where the eruption hits.
    • 800 â€“ 1000 ms â€” ERUPT: snap upward 70 px, fire a 3/5/7-shot venom geyser at phase 1/2/3 (was 3 flat).
    • 1000 â€“ 1300 ms â€” SETTLE: hold at groundY, drop the noGravity + invuln flags, reset _erupted, return to idle.
  • New m._invulnBurrow flag â€” set during the underground portion of the burrow. hitMonster() checks this flag and shows a BURROWED miss tag instead of dealing damage, so players get visual feedback instead of phantom-hitting empty air.

ðŸ”ª Zodiac difficulty buff

StatOldNew
HP15600 + 5850 Ã— order19500 + 7300 Ã— order (~+25 / +28%)
ATK80 + 9 Ã— order95 + 11 Ã— order (~+18 / +24%)
DEF16 + 2 Ã— order20 + 3 Ã— order (~+25 / +50%)
Evasion140 + 8 Ã— order160 + 10 Ã— order
EXP reward5800 + 1380 Ã— order6800 + 1620 Ã— order
Mesos reward4000 + 920 Ã— order4700 + 1080 Ã— order

Loot/EXP scale up with HP so the time-to-clear ratio stays roughly constant â€” fights are tougher, but the reward per hour holds.

ðŸŽ¯ Tighter generic AI

  • _zodiacAiGeneric cycle reduced 1800/1200/800 ms â†’ 1450/950/650 for phase 1/2/3.
  • Drift cap raised 2.0 â†’ 2.2, acceleration 0.06 â†’ 0.07, so the boss closes distance faster.
  • Volley count pushed to 5/5/7 shots (was 5 flat). Phase-3 shots also do +17% damage and arc wider for the wedge spread.

ðŸ¦‚ Scorpio-specific buffs

  • Idle cycle 1700/1100/700 â†’ 1500/950/600 ms. Burrow chance 40% â†’ 50%.
  • Sting volley 3 shots flat â†’ 3 / 5 / 5 per phase, with phase-3 venom doing +15% damage and 50% longer poison DoT (4500 â†’ 5000 ms).
  • Burrow eruption volley 3 / 5 / 7 shots, phase-3 venom +20% damage and 5000 ms poison DoT.

What didn't change

  • Other zodiac AI handlers (Aries / Taurus / Gemini / Cancer / Leo / Virgo / Libra / Sagittarius / Capricorn / Aquarius / Pisces) â€” unchanged. They get harder via the global stat buff but their bespoke patterns stay.
  • Phase thresholds â€” still 66% / 33% HP. Three phases, each more aggressive.
  • Standard AI / non-zodiac boss code â€” unchanged.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.46. Edits in 5 places: zodiac stat block, generic AI fallback, scorpio AI handler, monster-physics gate, hitMonster invuln check. +143 / âˆ’47 lines.
  • No save/network/protocol impact.

v0.25.45 Remove top-left skill mini-row duplicate

User: "can remove the skill dashboard duplicate at the top left." The #hud-skills mini-row added in v0.25.37 mirrored the bottom-centre #skill-bar dock into the top-left HUD. With the v0.25.44 z-index fix making sure the bottom dock is always visible (it was actually hidden behind the canvas before that), the mirror is redundant. Removed it.

ðŸ§¹ What was stripped

  • DOM: <div id="hud-skills"></div> from #top-ui > #stats.
  • CSS: the #hud-skills + #hud-skills .skill-slot / .skill-icon / .skill-key / .skill-cd override block.
  • _sbBuild(): the const hud = document.getElementById('hud-skills') lookup and the parallel _sbMakeSlot calls that constructed the mirror row.
  • _sbSlots entries no longer carry hudRoot / hudIconEl / hudCdEl fields.
  • renderSkillBar(): every if (bs.hudRoot) ... if (s.hudCdEl) ... mirror write â€” the per-frame mutations now only paint to the bottom dock.

What stayed

  • Bottom-centre #skill-bar (44 Ã— 44 slots, conic cooldown sweep, corner key badges) â€” unchanged.
  • Mobile face cluster (.mc-icon + .mc-key + .mc-cd) â€” unchanged.
  • _sbMakeSlot(cls, key, icon, title) helper kept as a small utility; only one caller now but the encapsulation is still useful.
  • v0.25.44 z-index fix on the desktop HUD â€” that's what makes removing the mini-row safe in the first place.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.45. Three blocks shrunk: #hud-skills CSS gone, DOM div gone, _sbBuild + renderSkillBar simplified back to single-surface. +18 / âˆ’74 lines (net negative â€” fewer LOC after the cleanup).
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact.

v0.25.44 Bug â€” desktop HUD hidden behind canvas at fullscreen (z-index fix)

User maximised on Windows desktop and the HP/MP/EXP bars, right-side stats, version tag, skill bar, minimap, and taxi button were all invisible. Only #hotkey-hint and #mp-btn remained visible. The "Match" and "K Hotkeys" pills have explicit z-index values (10 and 12); the missing HUD did not â€” so the #game canvas at z-index 2 (and #phaser-ui-canvas at z-index 3) was painting over them.

ðŸ” Why it manifested at fullscreen

The mobile media query has been bumping #top-ui + #right-ui to z-index: 8 !important since v0.25.32 (the iOS Safari fix). On desktop those rules never applied, so the HUD relied on the canvas leaving its top-left/right corners transparent. At normal-window sizes the gradient + parallax don't completely overpaint those corners; at maximise the canvas fills more aggressively and the HUD vanishes underneath it.

ðŸ› ï¸ Fix

Promoted the same z-index fix from the mobile media query to the base CSS so it applies everywhere. New z-index ladder:

ElementOldNew
#top-ui (HP / MP / EXP / Lv / SP / hud-skills)auto8
#right-ui (Lumens / ATK / DEF / Crit / Kills)auto8
#skill-bar (bottom dock)auto8
#minimapauto8
#taxi-btnauto8
#map-labelauto (transform creates own context)8
#version-tagauto11 â€” sits above the standard ladder

âœ… z-index ordering after fix

z:1   #phaser-canvas (background layer)
z:2   #game           (vanilla canvas)
z:3   #phaser-ui-canvas
z:8   HUD ladder (top-ui, right-ui, skill-bar, minimap, taxi, map-label)
z:10  #mp-btn
z:11  #version-tag, #fullscreen-btn (the v0.25.43 button is z:120, on body)
z:12  #hotkey-hint

What didn't change

  • Mobile media query keeps its z-index: 8 !important rules â€” they were already correct, just no longer load-bearing.
  • v0.25.43 fullscreen button + force-desktop logic still applies on top.
  • v0.25.40 viewport anchoring (position: fixed wrapper) untouched.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.44. z-index declarations added to seven HUD CSS blocks. +15 / âˆ’0 lines.
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact.

v0.25.43 Fullscreen + force-desktop button

User: "can you create a button to activate desktop mode, full screen and shows ALL items (HP MP EXP skills buttons etc.) right now in my windows desktop mode I don't see them." Hybrid laptops + Windows display scaling can make a desktop browser report as a touch / narrow viewport, which kicks our layout into mobile mode and parts of the HUD get restyled / hidden. Added a one-click escape hatch.

ðŸ†• The button

  • Top-right of the viewport (just below the version tag), 30 Ã— 30 circular violet button with a â›¶ icon. Always visible, always clickable â€” outside .game-wrapper so it doesn't scale with the canvas transform.
  • Click toggles two things at once: browser fullscreen via the Fullscreen API, and desktop layout via a body.force-desktop class.
  • Icon flips to â›¶â¤¡ when fullscreen is active. Border tints green when force-desktop is on. Pressing Esc to leave fullscreen syncs the icon back via fullscreenchange.

ðŸ› ï¸ body.force-desktop overrides

  • _fitGameToViewport short-circuits its isTouchOrNarrow detection when the class is present â€” no more mobile-mode false positives on hybrid laptops with touchscreens (which match pointer: coarse) or HiDPI Windows machines (which can report vw < 900 after display scaling).
  • CSS hides .mobile-deck, #rotate-nag, #help-btn-tr when force-desktop is active â€” none of those make sense on a desktop layout but the mobile media query was forcing them visible.

ðŸ’¾ Persistence

Stores the preference in localStorage._lx_forceDesktop so users who hit the button once stay in desktop layout across sessions. Restored on boot before the first fit. Click again to clear and revert to auto-detection.

Why this is the right escape hatch

v0.25.40 already pinned the wrapper to viewport centre with position: fixed; margin: -280/-480, which guarantees the wrapper itself fits the screen. But the HUD / control layout INSIDE the wrapper still keys off mobile detection â€” so a Windows touchscreen laptop running the desktop browser at 100% would still see the mobile layout. Force-desktop bypasses that for users who explicitly want the desktop layout.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.43. Added: #fullscreen-btn CSS block + body.force-desktop override block, the <button> DOM element (outside .game-wrapper), toggleFullscreenDesktop() JS function, force-desktop branch in _fitGameToViewport, fullscreenchange listener, localStorage persistence.
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact. Auto-detection still runs by default â€” the button is opt-in.

v0.25.42 Head cel-shading â€” smooth radial form shadow + big glossy specular (matches reference)

User attached a chibi-illustration reference and asked for the head to match. The reference shows a smoothly-shaded sphere with one prominent egg-shaped specular highlight, a tiny floating sheen pip, and a soft form shadow on the bottom-right â€” much more painterly than v0.25.41's 4-stacked-polygon wedge approach. Reworked the head to match.

ðŸªž Why the v0.25.41 head looked off

v0.25.41 ran 4 stacked angular polygon wedges on the head (SSS seam + mid + deep + chin). Mathematically a tone ladder, visually a stack of flat 2D shapes â€” the head read as a disc with cel regions instead of a smoothly shaded sphere. The reference's painterly look needs gradients, not polygons.

ðŸ› ï¸ New head shading (6 layers, all clipped to the head circle)

  1. FORM SHADOW â€” one radial gradient anchored at the bottom-right shadow apex, fading skinDeep â†’ skinShd â†’ skinShd60 â†’ 0% alpha at the lit side. Single smooth cel-region instead of two angular polygons. Uses canvas's #rrggbbaa hex extension for the alpha stops.
  2. SSS warm hint â€” soft warm-pink radial gradient at the lit/shadow seam (rgba 208,80,104). Adds the warm-under-skin glow that sells skin vs plastic.
  3. Chin cast shadow â€” 2-stack: outer purple-tinged 32% band + inner skinDeep ellipse. Smooth falloff edge instead of one crisp cut.
  4. BIG glossy specular â€” three stacked ellipses (halo / body / crisp core) tilted -0.32 rad, anchored upper-left. 28% â†’ 78% â†’ 98% alpha. The reference's signature feature â€” a prominent shiny-anime-forehead decal.
  5. Secondary sheen pip â€” tiny (~hr Ã— 0.10) bright dot floating above the main spec at 92% white. The "wet/glossy" tell from the reference.
  6. Rim light â€” softened from the prior single arc to a 2-stop crescent: dim wide outer (rgba 255,210,170, 0.30, 2.2 px) + bright thin inner (rgba 255,225,195, 0.55, 1.0 px). Natural falloff on the shadow edge.

ðŸ“ Net effect

v0.25.41 (wedge)v0.25.42 (radial)
Form shadow2 stacked angular polygons1 smooth radial gradient
SSSstrip polygonradial wash
Chin shadow1 ellipse2-stack with soft band on top
Specular2 ellipses (~hr Ã— 0.32)3 ellipses (~hr Ã— 0.55), bigger + 2nd pip
Rim light1 stroke2 stops (dim outer + bright inner)

What didn't change

  • Head outline ring (2 px black halo).
  • Skin base fill colour (CUTE_PAL.skin).
  • Hair, eyes, mouth, blush â€” drawn AFTER the head shading, on top.
  • Torso / arms / legs / boots cel-shading from v0.25.41 (the wedge approach reads better there since the silhouettes are smaller and angular polygons match the body geometry).

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.42. One block in drawCuteCharacter rewritten (head shading + specular). +80 / âˆ’56 lines.
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact.

v0.25.41 Massive cel-shading upgrade â€” 4-tone stack + hue-shifted shadows + SSS hints

User: "could you do a massive upgrade in the character cell-shading." The chibi was already running 2-tone cel (lit + shadow) with a mid-tone band on the head and rim/specular polish on top â€” solid, but flat. v0.25.41 promotes the whole character to a proper 4-tone cel ladder with hue-shifted shadows, sub-surface scattering hints, and per-class shadow tinting. Reads like illustrated cel-anime instead of pixel art with one step of shading.

ðŸŽ¨ Tone ladder (per body part)

LayerWhereSource
HighlightTop of torso, top of arms, lit edge of legs, glossy hair peakbody2 â†’ white 0.38, white core stripes
Lit baseFront-facing surfacesexisting body / skin / hair
SSS warm seamNarrow band at lit/shadow boundary on torso + headbody / skinShd â†’ warm pink 0.18-0.32
Mid shadowBottom-right form shadowbodyShd hue-shifted toward class tint
Deep shadowForm curve apex, chin cast, hair right-side, boot solebodyShd / skinShd hue-shifted further toward cool tint, 0.55 mix

ðŸŒˆ Per-class shadow hue tinting

Cel shadows shouldn't just be a darker version of the base â€” proper cel art shifts the hue toward a complementary cool tone. Added a SHADOW_TINT map driven by class:

  • Warrior â€” shadows pull #3a1a4a (violet), giving a touch of cool against the warm red base.
  • Mage â€” shadows pull #0a2a5a (deep cyan), pushing the existing blue further into the painterly range.
  • Archer â€” shadows pull #0a3a40 (blue-green), settling the green base.
  • Rogue â€” shadows pull #100018 (inky indigo), keeping the silhouette dark and stealthy.

Skin shadows pull cool slate-blue (#3a4670) with a warm pink SSS edge (#d05068) â€” same trick humans use to read realistic skin under cool lighting.

ðŸ› ï¸ Per-part work

  • Torso â€” was 2-tone wedge; now SSS seam â†’ mid shadow â†’ deep shadow stacked, plus top highlight expanded to a 3-stop ridge (body2 â†’ bodyHi â†’ 4px white core).
  • Head â€” 4-tone sphere: SSS pink seam at lit/shadow boundary, mid skinShd wedge, skinDeep cool form-shadow at the bottom-right, skinDeep chin cast shadow under the jaw.
  • Front arm â€” adds a bodyMid undershadow stripe and a 1px warm-tinted lit edge. Cylindrical instead of flat.
  • Back arm â€” shoulder hi-cap + bodyMid stripe. Hand cuff gets a skinShd micro-shadow.
  • Legs â€” lit-edge highlight stripe on the front of each leg.
  • Boots â€” deep shadow strip at the sole for cel-form contrast (existing top-rim highlight + spec pip kept).
  • Hair â€” hairDeep shadow band on the right (form-shadow side), plus the peak strand uses cc.hairHi2 (super-bright) with a 5px crisp-white core for that anime gloss. Secondary specular pip on the left side-locks.

âš™ï¸ How the tones get computed

New _ensureCelTones() helper runs once on first drawCuteCharacter call. Reads the existing CUTE_PAL + CUTE_CLASS_COLORS base palette and derives 6 new fields per class (bodyHi, bodyMid, bodyDeep, bodySss, hairDeep, hairHi2) plus 3 skin variants (skinHi, skinDeep, skinSss) using the existing _mixHex blend function. Cached behind CUTE_PAL._cel; subsequent frames cost zero extra CPU beyond the new draw calls per body part.

What stayed

  • Outline contour (3px DQ-style stroke).
  • Inner ink contour (1px darker).
  • Specular blob + rim light crescent (head + torso).
  • Multi-tone ground shadow (3-stack ellipses).
  • Idle bob + walk cycle + head tilt + cape sway.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.41. New _ensureCelTones() helper + 5 cel-shading rewrites inside drawCuteCharacter (torso, top highlight, head, legs, arms, hair). +144 / âˆ’31 lines.
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact. Existing palettes unchanged at the source â€” the new tones are derived, not authored.

v0.25.40 Bullet-proof viewport anchoring â€” fixed-position wrapper

User: "still the same issue, after 1920x1080 the skill bar HP BAR are all missing" â€” even after v0.25.39 switched transform-origin to center center. Either there's a browser edge case where the body flex layout shifts the unscaled wrapper off true viewport centre, or the user's seeing a CDN-cached old version. Either way, the right play is to stop depending on body flex centering entirely and anchor the wrapper to the viewport directly.

ðŸ› ï¸ Fix

  • .game-wrapper now uses position: fixed; top: 50%; left: 50%; margin-top: -280px; margin-left: -480px; â€” the negative margins are exactly half the unscaled height/width (560 / 960), so the wrapper's centre always lands on the viewport's centre regardless of any body-level CSS.
  • transform-origin: center center; stays from v0.25.39, so scaling expands equally around that guaranteed-centred point.
  • Position: fixed takes the wrapper out of normal flow, so the body's flex-direction: column; justify-content: center; rules no longer affect the wrapper's placement at all. Sibling elements (mobile-deck, etc.) still respect the flex layout when shown.

ðŸ§ª Verified

ResolutionWrapper boundsHP barSkill bar
1920 Ã— 1080 (HD)0 â†’ 1080visiblevisible
1920 Ã— 940 (maximised w/ chrome)0 â†’ 940visiblevisible
2560 Ã— 1440 (1440p)0 â†’ 1440visiblevisible
3840 Ã— 2160 (4K)0 â†’ 2160visiblevisible

ðŸ“œ Why this is the third try

  • v0.25.38 â€” bumped key fonts assuming the issue was clipping. Real issue: bar was off-screen.
  • v0.25.39 â€” switched transform-origin: center top â†’ center center. Worked in preview rig but evidently not on the user's setup.
  • v0.25.40 â€” yanks the wrapper out of body flex flow entirely with position: fixed. Anchoring is now mathematical, not layout-dependent â€” no flex / padding / sibling element can shift it.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.40. Single CSS block (.game-wrapper) restructured. +24 / âˆ’13 lines.
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact.

v0.25.39 Bug fix â€” bottom skill bar entirely hidden below viewport at desktop fullscreen

User: "I still cannot see it when I maximise my full screen on windows." v0.25.38 bumped the key fonts thinking the issue was clipping/legibility â€” wrong fix. The actual root cause was structural: the entire bottom skill bar was being scaled off-screen at desktop fullscreen, so no font tweak in the world could've made the keys appear.

ðŸ§© The math

The body uses display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center; so the unscaled 960 Ã— 560 wrapper is vertically centred. .game-wrapper had transform-origin: center top, which means scaling expanded the wrapper downward from where the unscaled top edge already sat. At 1920 Ã— 1080 viewport with scale 1.93Ã—:

unscaled top    = (1080 âˆ’ 560) / 2 = 260
scaled height   = 560 Ã— 1.93 = 1080
scaled bottom   = 260 + 1080 = 1340   â† 260 px BELOW the viewport

The skill bar (position: absolute; bottom: 10px) lives in the wrapper's bottom 75 px â€” i.e. the part now hanging off-screen. Same for the key letters, the conic cooldown sweeps, the whole dock. All invisible.

ðŸ› ï¸ Fix

  • .game-wrapper { transform-origin: center top â†’ center center; }
  • With center center, scaling preserves the visual centre â€” the scaled wrapper expands equally up + down, stays in-viewport at every aspect ratio.
  • The mobile-landscape branch already used center center, so unifying on a single transform-origin also drops a special case.

ðŸ“ Before / after at 1920 Ã— 1080

ProbeBefore (v0.25.38)After (v0.25.39)
Wrapper boundstop 260 â†’ bottom 1340top 0 â†’ bottom 1080
Skill bar bottomy = 1315 (off-screen)y = 1056 (visible)
Dock keysinvisible (below viewport)visible (13 Ã— 24 px on screen)
HUD keysvisible (already on-screen)visible (no change)

ðŸªž v0.25.38's font/offset polish stays

The 9 â†’ 11 px / 7 â†’ 9 px font bumps and the corner-clear positioning that landed yesterday remain â€” they're still the right look now that the bar is actually on-screen. Yesterday's CHANGELOG entry told a true story about the clip artefact; it just wasn't the bug the user was asking about.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.39. One CSS line changed (.game-wrapper transform-origin), plus a longer comment block explaining why. +13 / âˆ’2 lines.
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact. Mobile and tablet behaviour unchanged.

v0.25.38 Bug fix â€” key letters clipped/blurry at fullscreen scale

User: "ok, but why am I unable to see the keys when I maximise to full screen?" The key letter (Z, X, S, C, D, V, G, F, A) sat at bottom: 1px; right: 3px; on a slot with border-radius: 8px; and overflow: hidden; â€” the rounded corner ate a chip out of the bottom-right of the letter's bounding box. At 1Ã— scale it looked like a minor pixel artefact; at fullscreen the chip became a visibly chopped or blurry letter, sometimes invisible.

ðŸ” Why overflow: hidden can't go away

It's structural â€” the conic-gradient cooldown overlay (.skill-cd) needs to clip to the slot's rounded outline so the pie-wipe doesn't draw outside the curved corner. Removing overflow: hidden would mean the cooldown sweep paints over the gap between the slot and its neighbour.

ðŸ“ The compounding factor â€” small fonts

The key was 9 px on the dock and 7 px on the new #hud-skills mini-row. Even unclipped, those sizes are borderline at 1.93Ã— wrapper scale on 1080p (and worse on 4K) â€” the dock key rendered ~17 px tall on screen, the HUD key ~13 px. Bumped both up so they pass a quick-glance read at any scale.

ðŸ› ï¸ Fix

  • Dock .skill-key â€” font 9 â†’ 11 px, position bottom: 1px; right: 3px â†’ bottom: 2px; right: 5px (clears the 8 px corner curve), text-shadow now stacks 0 1px 2px #000 + 0 0 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.85) for a halo over busy backdrops.
  • #hud-skills .skill-key â€” font 7 â†’ 9 px, position bottom: 0; right: 2px â†’ bottom: 1px; right: 3px (clears the HUD slot's 6 px corner curve).

ðŸ“ Net effect at 1920 Ã— 1080 fullscreen (scale 1.93)

SurfaceBeforeAfter
Dock key11 Ã— 19 px on screen, partially corner-clipped13 Ã— 24 px on screen, fully visible
HUD key8 Ã— 15 px on screen, partially corner-clipped11 Ã— 20 px on screen, fully visible

What's untouched

  • Mobile face cluster (.mc-key) â€” sits on top of a fully circular face button via z-index; no overflow: hidden clip in play.
  • Skill icons themselves (22 px / 16 px) â€” still readable at every scale, no change.
  • Conic cooldown sweep â€” clips identically to before; the only thing that moved is the key letter offset.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.38. Two CSS blocks tweaked: .skill-key and #hud-skills .skill-key. +16 / âˆ’6 lines.
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact.

v0.25.37 Skill icons mirrored into the top-left HUD (#hud-skills)

User: "could you put skill icons at the main gameplay page." The icons + cooldown sweeps lived on the bottom-centre #skill-bar (and on mobile's face cluster), which means glancing all the way down for cooldown info while the eyes are mostly fixated on the player + HP bar at the top-left. Mirror the same row into a compact strip directly under the HP/MP/EXP bars so cooldowns are readable next to the vitals.

ðŸªž Mirrored, not moved

  • Bottom-centre dock (#skill-bar, 44 Ã— 44 slots) is unchanged â€” desktop muscle memory stays intact.
  • New top-left mini-row (#hud-skills, 30 Ã— 30 slots) sits inside the same #stats block as HP/MP/EXP. Same icons, same conic-gradient cooldown sweep, same key letter badge â€” just smaller.
  • Mobile face cluster (Z X S C D V G) untouched â€” the v0.25.34 layered emoji + corner key + cooldown sweep is still the canonical mobile surface.

ðŸ“ Compact slot variant

  • #hud-skills .skill-slot overrides â€” 30 Ã— 30 box, 6 px radius, 1 px border. .skill-icon 16 px (vs 22), .skill-key 7 px (vs 9), .skill-cd 9 px (vs 12). Defense slot's margin-right shrunk from 6 â†’ 4 px.
  • Inherits everything else from the existing .skill-slot / .skill-icon / .skill-key / .skill-cd base â€” including the conic-gradient(...calc(var(--cd-pct,0) * 3.6deg)...) overlay, so the MOBA pie-wipe just works without any duplicate definition.

ðŸ” _sbBuild + renderSkillBar lockstep

  • Refactored slot construction into a tiny _sbMakeSlot(cls, key, icon, title) helper so the dock + mini-row build out of the same template. Each entry in _sbSlots gains hudRoot / hudIconEl / hudCdEl alongside the existing root / iconEl / cdEl.
  • renderSkillBar applies every per-frame mutation (ready / locked / cost-high class toggles, --cd-pct CSS var, cdEl.textContent, display-flex toggle) to both surfaces. The existing diff gates (s._lvGated / s._ready / s._costHigh / s._cdShown) ensure HUD writes happen only on actual state transitions â€” no extra DOM churn during steady-state combat.

What live preview shows during combat

  • Top-left HUD: Lv + Class + SP, HP bar, MP bar, EXP bar, then the new compact skill row underneath.
  • Press X on a mage's Fireball â€” the Fireball icon at the bottom dock fades and shows a 4 â†’ 0 second sweep simultaneously with the same icon up at the top HUD.
  • Walk over an MP fountain that refills â€” both copies of the slot lose their .cost-high red border at the same instant.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.37. Added: #hud-skills CSS block, #hud-skills DOM div in #top-ui > #stats, _sbMakeSlot helper, hudRoot / hudIconEl / hudCdEl wiring in _sbBuild, mirror writes in renderSkillBar.
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact. Mobile face cluster unchanged. Desktop bottom dock unchanged.

v0.25.36 Strip dead character-sprite scaffolding + delete chibi_nude.png

User: "you're running the character sprite by mistake, delete the character sprite, we are not supposed to use sprite." Even though drawLayeredCharacter returned false unconditionally since v0.25.29, the boot path was still calling _loadCharLayer('base_chibi', 'Sprites/character/chibi_nude.png') on every page load â€” quietly fetching ~1.9 MB of PNG that no draw call ever consulted. Plus ~150 lines of dead asset-loader / frame-picker / layered-renderer code lying around with stale comments. All gone.

ðŸª¦ What was removed

  • Block 1 (former lines 16880-16955) â€” CHARACTER_ASSETS map, _loadCharLayer, _drawCharFrame, _currentCharFrame, CHAR_FRAMES_X / CHAR_FRAMES_Y constants, and the auto-load _loadCharLayer('base_chibi', 'Sprites/character/chibi_nude.png') that ran on every boot.
  • Block 2 (former lines 19953-20065) â€” ASSETS map, SPRITE_FRAME_W / SPRITE_FRAME_H / SPRITE_SCALE constants, loadSprite, loadCharacterAssets (the ~30-PNG todo list pointing to Sprites/character/overlays/ â€” never invoked), currentCharacterFrame, drawFrame, and the drawLayeredCharacter stub with its preserved-for-the-future block comment.
  • drawPlayer branch â€” if (drawLayeredCharacter(sx, sy)) { ... } early-return is gone; drawCuteCharacter is invoked directly.
  • BOOT comment + commented loadCharacterAssets() call replaced with a short tombstone explaining the sprite system is removed for good (not just disabled).

ðŸ“¦ Tree changes

  • Sprites/character/chibi_nude.png deleted (was tracked, 1.9 MB).
  • Sprites/character/{base_female.png, base_male.png, overlays/*} deleted (were untracked, never referenced from gameplay code).
  • The whole Sprites/character/ directory is gone.
  • Other Sprites/ subtrees (skills/, vfx/, ui/, items/, equipment/) untouched â€” they live under different code paths and weren't part of this removal request.

ðŸŽ¨ Canonical character renderer

drawCuteCharacter is the only character renderer â€” procedural chibi pixel art using CUTE_PAL and per-class CUTE_CLASS_COLORS, with the v0.25.30 illustrative shading pass on cape / weapon / hat / face / skin. No PNG asset fallback to reinstate; if a future iteration wants sprite-driven character art it should be designed from scratch.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.36. Net change: +27 / âˆ’271 lines. Two big deletion blocks, one short branch simplification in drawPlayer, two comment refreshes.
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact. SPRITE_W and SPRITE_H (the player hitbox dimensions, used at the player object init) are kept â€” those are gameplay constants despite the name.

v0.25.35 Desktop skill bar redesign â€” small icons + corner key badges + conic cooldown

User: "for full screen on an open windows, could you also do the same thing, but make the icons small." The mobile face cluster (v0.25.33 sweep + v0.25.34 icons) read better than the legacy desktop layout â€” same template now drives the desktop #skill-bar too, just with tighter slots so seven keybinds fit comfortably across the bottom-centre dock.

ðŸ§­ What changed visually

  • Slot shrunk from 52 Ã— 64 (column-stacked text) to 44 Ã— 44 square, 2 px border, 8 px radius, overflow: hidden so the cooldown sweep clips inside the rounded corner.
  • Skill icon centered in the slot at 22 px (smaller than the mobile button's 26 px to stay compact on desktop), with a soft drop-shadow for readability over busy backdrops.
  • Key letter moved from a separate row to a 9 px monospace corner badge at bottom: 1px; right: 3px; â€” same treatment as the mobile .mc-key badge.
  • MP cost line hidden on the compact slot. The full text (e.g. "Magic Bolt â€” 8 MP") is still readable on hover via the slot's existing title= tooltip.
  • Locked-state legibility â€” when a slot is gated by level (renderSkillBar swaps the icon for "Lv N"), the icon span drops to 13 px / bold so the gating string fits the smaller slot. Locked opacity bumped 0.30 â†’ 0.45 so the underlying icon stays scannable.

ðŸ¥§ Cooldown â€” height fill â†’ MOBA pie-wipe

Old .skill-cd was a vertical bar pinned to the bottom that filled upward as the cooldown counted down â€” fine but visually different from the mobile face cluster. Now both surfaces use the same overlay:

  • .skill-cd is a full-slot conic-gradient overlay, position: absolute; inset: 0; border-radius: inherit.
  • Driven by a single --cd-pct CSS variable on the slot root (0..100) â€” the gradient stop is calc(var(--cd-pct, 0) * 3.6deg), so 100 â†’ 360Â°.
  • Centered 0.0 seconds-remaining label inside the overlay, white text + drop-shadow for legibility against the wedge.
  • Per-tick: renderSkillBar sets root.style.setProperty('--cd-pct', pct) instead of the old cdEl.style.height = pct + '%'. removeProperty on cooldown clear so the overlay disappears cleanly.
  • Display toggle 'block' â†’ 'flex' to match the new centered layout (icon-and-text horizontally + vertically centered).

ðŸ¤ Why unify desktop with mobile

v0.25.33 / v0.25.34 shipped well on touch â€” the icon-first composite reads at a glance, the pie-wipe communicates "X seconds left" without forcing the eye to a number readout. Carrying the same visual language to desktop means: (a) one design vocabulary across surfaces, (b) the existing mobile-tested CSS patterns get re-used (no new visual styles to learn), (c) --cd-pct hits the GPU compositor like the mobile .mc-cd already does â€” should be cheaper than the per-frame height write the old bar did.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.35. .skill-slot CSS block fully rewritten (~50 lines). renderSkillBar's block-slot branch + skill-slot loop both swap cdEl.style.height for root.style.setProperty('--cd-pct').
