  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact. Mobile face cluster unchanged. Slot wiring (_sbBuild / _sbSlots[]) unchanged â€” the same DOM works for both old and new visuals because the icon, key, and cd children were already separate spans.

v0.25.34 Skill icons on mobile face buttons (with corner key badges)

User asked for skill icons + cooldown timers on the gameplay screen. The cooldown timers were already on the mobile face buttons (v0.25.33's pie-wipe sweep). Missing piece: the buttons were just Z / X / S / C / D / V / G letter labels â€” no indication of what skill each fired. Each face button is now a layered composite: big skill emoji + tiny corner key badge + cooldown sweep on top.

ðŸŽ¯ Three layers per face button

  • .mc-icon â€” 26 px emoji of the matched skill, drop-shadowed. Pulled live from skillBySlot(slot).def.icon.
  • .mc-key â€” 9 px key letter tucked in the bottom-right corner so the keyboard hint stays for desktop+touch parity.
  • .mc-cd â€” existing conic-gradient cooldown sweep on top (z-index 2). Already wired from v0.25.33; unchanged.

ðŸ” Live sync

The poll loop in _wireMobileCooldownSweep() already resolved each button's skill every 50 ms; now it also pushes the matched skill's def.icon into the .mc-icon span. Icons auto-track class pick / job advance / master pick â€” no extra event hookup needed.

ðŸšª Empty-slot fallback

V (job ult) and G (master signature) start empty until the player advances. Buttons detect this via missing icon and set data-empty="1", which falls back the .mc-key style to centered-larger letter â€” i.e. the original letter-only look â€” so the button is still readable as "press G" until G unlocks. Once a master is picked, the icon swaps in and the key badge shrinks to the corner.

F stays letter-only

F is intentionally skipped (still has data-dynamic="f-block"). Its dispatch is dynamic (interact / portal / Q-cast / block depending on context); a static icon would be wrong for at least one of those modes.

What live preview at 375Ã—812 mobile shows for a mage

  • Z â†’ âœ¨ Magic Bolt
  • X â†’ ðŸ”¥ Fireball
  • S â†’ â„ï¸ Ice Spike
  • C â†’ ðŸŒ€ Blink
  • D â†’ ðŸ’¥ Arcane Burst
  • V â†’ "V" (no job picked yet)
  • G â†’ "G" (no master picked yet)
  • F â†’ "F" (dynamic dispatch â€” intentional)

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.34. Two new CSS blocks (.mc-icon + .mc-key + the empty-slot fallback) inside the mobile media query. _wireMobileCooldownSweep() grows: button DOM restructure on first wire (replaces letter text with two child spans), per-tick .mc-icon update.
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact. Desktop unaffected.

v0.25.33 Bring back MOBA-style cooldown pie-wipe sweep

User changed their mind: "I would like MOBA-style cooldown pie-wipe sweep and skill face buttons." Reverts both v0.25.31 reverts in one shot â€” the conic-gradient cooldown sweep on the mobile face cluster (Z/X/S/C/D/V/G) is back, along with the iOS-compatible <span> DOM rewrite that makes it actually render on iOS Safari.

â†ªï¸ What's restored

  • MOBA-style cooldown pie-wipe sweep. Conic-gradient overlay on each circular face button draws a clockwise dark wedge whose angle equals the remaining cooldown fraction. Greys out the button and shows a seconds-remaining label while cooling, snaps back to full saturation the instant the cooldown hits zero.
  • Real <span class="mc-cd"> child element instead of the original ::after pseudo-element. iOS Safari has long-standing quirks rendering pseudo-elements on <button> elements with display: flex parents â€” the conic-gradient simply didn't paint. The real <span> renders reliably across iOS / Android / desktop.
  • Top-right "?" help button (#help-btn-tr). Sits at top: 38px, right: 12px, opens the existing Controls modal via openHelp(). Mobile-only; desktop CSS hides it. The pre-existing top-left #help-btn stays as well, and the #hotkey-hint chip from v0.25.28 still works as the K-panel tap target.

ðŸ›¡ï¸ z-index hardening preserved

Both v0.25.32's surgical z-index fix and the original 365d301 z-index hardening converged to #top-ui { z-index: 8 !important; } + #right-ui { z-index: 8 !important; }. Conflict resolved by keeping v0.25.32's version (without the redundant position: relative override that the original 365d301 added â€” z-index applies fine on the existing position: absolute base).

ðŸªœ The pendulum log (for the curious)

  • v0.25.31 â€” reverted both 43179b0 (cooldown sweep) and 365d301 (DOM rewrite + z-index). User said "old layout" wanted.
  • v0.25.32 â€” re-added z-index lines after HP/MP/EXP disappeared on iOS.
  • v0.25.33 â€” re-applied the rest. User wants the cooldown sweep back. Cooldown visuals + DOM rewrite + top-right help button all restored via reverts of the v0.25.31 revert commits.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.33. Two git revert commits (9387c89 + 4775bfc) that re-apply the original 43179b0 + 365d301 changes. One small CSS conflict resolved by keeping v0.25.32's z-index lines without the position: relative override.
  • MOBILE_CHANGELOG.html â€” entries for the two re-applied commits restored.
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact.

v0.25.32 Re-add iOS HUD z-index hardening â€” HP/MP/EXP bars back

User report after v0.25.31: "I don't see the Z X S C D V G skill bar... bring the HP MP EXP bar back as well." A live mobile preview confirmed the face cluster (Z/X/S/C/D/V/G) was actually still visible â€” but the HP/MP/EXP and right-ui (Lumens/ATK/DEF/Crit/Kills) bars had disappeared on iOS Safari. The defensive z-index hardening originally bundled into 365d301 got removed by the v0.25.31 revert. This release re-adds only that fix â€” no cooldown sweep, no DOM rewrite, no top-right help button.

ðŸ©¹ The fix

@media (pointer: coarse), (max-width: 900px) {
  #top-ui   { z-index: 8 !important; }
  #right-ui { z-index: 8 !important; ...rest unchanged }
}

Position kept as base position: absolute (not promoted to relative as the original 365d301 did) â€” z-index applies fine on absolute-positioned elements, and there's no need to alter the existing top:10 left:10 absolute positioning relative to the wrapper.

ðŸ§  Why this happens on iOS Safari

The Phaser UI canvas (#phaser-ui-canvas, z-index 3, added in v0.25.17 onward) sits on top of the same top-of-screen area as the HUD blocks. iOS Safari's GPU compositor can hide DOM siblings that share a stacking layer with a transparent canvas â€” even when the canvas is display:none by default. Pushing the HUD blocks to z-index: 8 puts them safely above and the bug goes away.

What's still gone (intentionally)

Per the v0.25.31 user request:

  • MOBA-style cooldown pie-wipe sweep on face buttons â€” still gone.
  • Cooldown DOM rewrite from ::after to real <span> â€” still gone.
  • Top-right help button â€” still gone.

The #hotkey-hint chip from v0.25.28 still works as a tap target for the K (Hotkeys / Skills / Level Up) panel.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.32. Two CSS lines added inside the existing @media (pointer: coarse), (max-width: 900px) block. No JS, no DOM, no other changes.

v0.25.31 Revert mobile cooldown sweep + DOM rewrite (restore old skill bar look)

User asked for the old skill-bar / cooldown look back. Reverts the two recent mobile-cooldown commits: 43179b0 (MOBA-style conic-gradient pie-wipe sweep on face buttons) and 365d301 (DOM rewrite from ::after to real <span> + HUD z-index hardening + top-right help button). Mobile face buttons now go back to plain solid-colour with no cooldown indicator.

â†©ï¸ What got reverted

  • Conic-gradient cooldown sweep on Z / X / S / C / D / V / G face buttons â€” gone. Buttons no longer dim or grey out while cooling; they stay solid colour the whole time.
  • Cooldown DOM rewrite â€” gone. The ::after pseudo-element approach is also gone (since the sweep itself is gone). No CD overlay element is rendered at all on mobile face buttons.
  • Top-right help button (#help-btn-tr) â€” gone. The pre-existing top-left #help-btn remains, and the #hotkey-hint chip from v0.25.28 also still works as a tap target for the K panel.
  • HUD z-index hardening on #top-ui / #right-ui for mobile â€” gone. These two blocks no longer get the z-index: 8 + position: relative defensive bump that was added because iOS Safari's GPU compositor was occasionally hiding DOM elements that share a stacking layer with the transparent #phaser-ui-canvas (z:3).

âš ï¸ Known trade â€” possible iOS HUD disappearance

The collaborator's z-index hardening was added defensively after a live mobile run reported "Player's stats bar is missing" on iOS Safari. With it reverted, the HP/MP/EXP and Lumens/ATK/DEF/Crit/Kills blocks may disappear behind the Phaser UI canvas on iOS again. If that recurs in your testing, ping me and I'll re-add just the z-index fix as a one-line patch without restoring the cooldown changes.

ðŸ“‹ What's still on the desktop skill bar (unchanged)

Desktop #skill-bar at the bottom-center has the same vertical-fill grey cooldown bar that grows from the bottom up, the same per-slot icon + key + MP cost layout, the same ready/locked/cost-high colour states. None of the reverted commits touched the desktop skill bar â€” desktop view is identical to v0.25.30.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.31. Two git revert commits stacked: 0f2a4e1 (version), 1e82db5 (revert 365d301), 79dfcfb (revert 43179b0).
  • MOBILE_CHANGELOG.html â€” entries for the two reverted commits removed (resolving merge conflicts during revert).
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact.

v0.25.30 Equipment + face/skin illustrative polish (cape / weapon / hat)

Continues the v0.25.29 illustrative-shading pass into the equipment and face/skin layers. Adds a procedural cape (which the procedural path was missing entirely), travelling specular gleam on blades, class-specific shine bands on hats, glossy double-catchlight on eyes, and a 3-tone skin shading band that bridges the lit/shadow step on the head.

ðŸ¦¸ Procedural cape (NEW)

Capes only existed in the disabled v0.25.26 layered-sprite system; the procedural path drew nothing. New _drawCuteCape renders a trapezoid silhouette anchored at the shoulders, draping to mid-thigh, with a sine sway that grows with walk speed. Class-coloured trim band along the hem (warrior gold / mage purple / rogue teal / archer silver) gives instant class identity. Drawn BEFORE the torso so the body silhouette overlaps it from the front.

âš”ï¸ Weapon polish â€” travelling blade gleam

  • Travelling specular gleam â€” vertical 1Ã—3-4 px near-white strip that scrolls along the blade as game.time advances. Modulo loop so it reads as a moving reflection. Different phase per blade variant (sword swing / sword idle / dagger) so multiple weapons on screen don't sync.
  • Tip pip â€” single bright pixel at the blade point.
  • Crossguard glint â€” warm-white pip on the gold guard.
  • Pommel highlight â€” small warm-white circle on the upper-left of the round pommel.

ðŸ‘‘ Hat polish â€” class-specific shine bands

  • Mage wizard hat â€” sheen band on the brim + 3 fading cone-tier shine strips + brim glint pip.
  • Warrior helmet â€” metal sheen across the dome + glint pip + warm gold glints on the side plates + plume tip catches light.
  • Rogue bandana â€” cloth sheen across the band + white pip on the forehead gem.
  • Archer cap â€” cap sheen band + glint pip + white glint on feather tip.
  • Three reusable rgba constants (SHEEN / SHEENZ / RIM) at the top of drawCuteHat keep the polish language consistent.

ðŸ‘ï¸ Face overlay polish

  • Cheek blush picks up a brighter inner pip (1 px rgba(255,200,210,0.85) peak inside the 2Ã—2 pink rect) â€” the hand-painted "rosy peak" instead of a flat colour patch.
  • Glossy anime double-catchlight on each eye: primary white pip top-left of pupil + secondary 60%-alpha catchlight bottom-right. The iconic "wet eye" gloss of high-end chibi art.
  • BIG fix â€” replaced the per-frame full-figure 'lighter'-blend wash glow with a small head-only radial. Old code brightened the WHOLE character every frame, which under the alpha pulse made the body / weapon / cape look hazy and low-contrast at the peak. New version reads as a class aura around the head without flattening contrast on the body.

ðŸŽ¨ Skin polish â€” 3-tone mid-band

Old: hard 2-tone step on the head (lit base â†’ cel shadow). New: 3 px mid-tone strip along the shadow boundary, mixed 45% between base skin and shadow tone. Gives proper 3-tone shading:

  • Highlight (specular blob + pip from v0.25.29)
  • Lit base
  • Mid band â† new
  • Cel shadow
  • Chin cast shadow
  • Rim light (warm crescent on shadow side from v0.25.29)

Six distinct lighting bands on the head now â€” the standard for hand-painted chibi illustration. _mixHex-driven so future skin tone changes flow through automatically.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.30. New _drawCuteCape function (~85 lines). _drawCuteSword / SwordIdle / Dagger each pick up gleam + glint passes. drawCuteHat per-class blocks each get shine bands + glints (3 reusable rgba consts at top). _drawCuteFaceOverlay rewritten â€” eye double-catchlight + targeted head-only aura replacing the wash glow. drawCuteCharacter head section gets the mid-tone band.
  • No save/network/gameplay impact. Pure visual polish.

v0.25.29 Sprite removed â€” procedural chibi gets illustrative shading polish

Reverts the v0.25.26 sprite path (proportions / lighting / facing didn't read well in-game) and pushes the procedural drawCuteCharacter further toward an "illustrative cel-shaded" look â€” soft drop-shadow, anime specular highlights on head + torso, warm rim light on the back-shadow side, inner ink contour on the body, hair shine band, boot specular pip.

ðŸšª Sprite path disabled

drawLayeredCharacter returns false unconditionally again (as in v0.25.2). The whole CHARACTER_ASSETS infrastructure (loader / frame helper / slot dispatch) is preserved as a comment block â€” a future asset pass with proper lighting + matching equipment layers can flip it back on by removing the early return. chibi_nude.png stays in the repo as reference for what NOT to ship without lighting work.

ðŸŒ‘ Ground shadow â€” multi-tone soft falloff

Old: single 13Ã—3.5 ellipse @ 35% alpha, hard ring with a visible edge.

New: three stacked ellipses for proper soft-drop-shadow falloff â€” outer 17Ã—5 halo @ 10% alpha, mid 14Ã—4 transition @ 22% alpha, inner 10Ã—2.4 contact dot @ 42% alpha. Plus a subtle idle-only pulse (sin(idleT Ã— 1.5) Ã— 5%) so the shadow breathes when standing still and smooths to zero as movement ramps up.

ðŸ‘¤ Head polish â€” specular + rim light

Three new passes inside the head circle clip:

  • Specular blob â€” soft white ellipse near the upper-left temple at 65% alpha. The classic anime "shiny forehead" highlight.
  • Specular pip â€” tighter brighter (95% alpha) core inside the blob for the wet/glossy peak.
  • Rim light â€” thin warm crescent (rgba(255,210,170,0.55)) traced along the lower-right shadow side, simulating back-light bouncing around the silhouette.

ðŸ§¥ Torso polish â€” same lighting language as head

Mirrors the head pass for visual consistency:

  • Specular highlight on the upper-left chest (45% alpha ellipse).
  • Rim light warm crescent (45% alpha) traced from shoulder down to waist on the back-shadow side.
  • Inner ink contour â€” 30% black stroke offset inward by 1 px (via 0.5 px aliasing translate). Outline now reads as ink on top of paint, not a hard cut-out edge.

ðŸ’‡ Hair shine band

Thin near-white horizontal strip across the top of the hair mass. Two stops: 12 px @ 42% for the base sheen, 5 px @ 85% for the brighter middle peak. Replaces the flat hairHi rim with the iconic glossy-anime-hair band.

ðŸ‘¢ Boot specular pip

Tiny 2Ã—1 px white highlight (70% alpha) on the front of each boot. Walks with the existing wc leg-cycle offset. Sells the leather sheen.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.29. drawLayeredCharacter early-returns false (preserves the body as comment block). drawCuteCharacter picks up ~90 lines of new shading passes â€” ground shadow, head specular + rim, torso specular + rim + inner ink, hair shine band, boot specular pip.
  • No save/network/gameplay impact. All purely visual; flag-off and Phaser-on paths both see the same polished art.

v0.25.28 Persistent K-Hotkeys hint chip + sprite-facing fix

Two small but visible polish items. A new persistent chip in the top-left corner reminds players that K opens the Hotkeys / Skills / Level Up panel (and is tappable on mobile, where the keyboard hint strip is hidden). The chibi sprite sheet's facing was inverted in v0.25.26 â€” fixed in v0.25.27 by flipping the mirror condition.

ðŸ“‹ The four "essentials" status

  • HP / MP / EXP bars â€” already in #top-ui (cel-shaded fills, names overlaid). No change.
  • Level + class â€” already in #top-ui ("Lv.N "). No change.
  • Skill bar with cooldowns â€” already at bottom-center (#skill-bar) with per-slot cooldown sweep overlays. No change.
  • "Press K for hotkeys guide" â€” NEW as #hotkey-hint chip.

ðŸ†• The K-Hotkeys chip

  • Persistent at top:80px / left:10px inside .game-wrapper, just below the existing HP/MP/EXP stack.
  • Visible on both desktop and mobile. The existing #controls hint strip (Enter/N/B/R/T/M) is hidden by display:none !important inside the mobile media query â€” mobile users had no entry point to the K panel without typing. The new chip survives the mobile rule.
  • Tap/click dispatches synthetic k keydown + keyup events, triggering the same panel handler as the physical key. Works as both a label AND a button.
  • Styled to match the rest of the dark/gold HUD chrome â€” dark plate, gold text, kbd-styled key indicator.

ðŸ§­ v0.25.27 sprite facing fix (folded into this release)

The chibi sprite sheet shipped in v0.25.26 natively faces LEFT, but my _drawCharFrame assumed RIGHT â€” visible result was the chibi moonwalking right while facing-left frames played, and vice versa. Flipped the mirror condition so the sprite now walks the same direction it's moving.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.28. New #hotkey-hint CSS block (~20 lines) + DOM element. New IIFE wires the click handler. _drawCharFrame facing condition flipped (1-line semantic change).
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact.

v0.25.26 Layered chibi sprite sheets â€” base body in, equipment slots wired

Reintroduces the layered-sprite-sheet pipeline disabled in v0.25.2, this time as a proper modular system designed for adding equipment incrementally without redrawing the base. The nude chibi base body is on screen now; hair / armor / cape / helmet / shield / weapon / boots slots are wired in but empty â€” drop a PNG matching the 4Ã—4 pose layout into Sprites/character/<slot>/<id>.png and it appears.

ðŸ–¼ï¸ The asset

Sprites/character/chibi_nude.png â€” 1216Ã—2312, 4Ã—4 grid of 304Ã—578 walk-cycle frames. Pre-loaded at boot via _loadCharLayer('base_chibi', ...). Other equipment slots load lazily on first lookup.

ðŸ§© Why layered sprite sheets and not skeletal

The big concern with flat sprite sheets is equipment variety â€” every armor / helmet / cape would multiply the per-frame asset count. Layered sheets sidestep this: each equipment piece is its own per-pose PNG drawn to match the base's frame layout, transparent everywhere else. Composited at runtime, base + gear reads as one unified character. This is how MapleStory, Stardew Valley, and most chibi-2D MMOs handle the equipment problem â€” you draw each gear piece once across the same 16 poses, not once per body Ã— armor Ã— helmet Ã— etc combination.

Skeletal animation (Spine / DragonBones) was the alternative â€” lower per-piece artist cost, but requires rigging the base, paid tooling, and weeks of pipeline work before anything ships. The layered-sheet path lets you ship the base today and add equipment one piece at a time without restructuring.

ðŸ› ï¸ How equipment will work

  1. Artist draws a gear piece across all 16 poses, matching the base sheet's pivot points (transparent where no gear).
  2. Save as Sprites/character/<slot>/<id>.png (slot âˆˆ hair / armor / cape / helmet / shield / weapon / boots).
  3. Set the relevant equipped item's spriteKey to '<id>'.
  4. Item appears on the character with zero code changes.

ðŸ“ Architecture

  • CHARACTER_ASSETS â€” module-level Map keyed by '<slot>_<id>'. Lazy-load on first access via _loadCharLayer; missing files silently no-op.
  • CHAR_FRAMES_X / CHAR_FRAMES_Y = 4 Ã— 4 â€” frame grid layout, configurable per future sheet.
  • _currentCharFrame() picks frame index from player state. v0.25.26 implements idle (frame 0) + 4-frame walk cycle. Future iterations extend this with attack / jump / hurt rows.
  • _drawCharFrame(img, sx, sy, frame, facing) blits one frame of one layer with horizontal-flip support. Anchored to the player's feet so the figure stands on the ground regardless of source frame height.
  • drawLayeredCharacter orchestrates the layer stack: base â†’ hair â†’ armor â†’ cape â†’ helmet â†’ shield â†’ weapon â†’ boots. Returns true on successful base draw so drawPlayer skips procedural fallback.

ðŸ”„ Fallback path preserved

drawPlayer's existing dispatch is unchanged: it tries drawLayeredCharacter first, falls back to procedural drawCuteCharacter if anything fails. If you delete the base PNG or the network blocks it, the game still renders via the procedural pixel art that's been there since v0.25.2.

âš–ï¸ Known scope trades

  • Procedural face overlay still draws on top. The sprite has no facial details; _drawCuteFaceOverlay brings the character to life. If the eye position is off vs the sprite head, future polish can add face frames as a separate layer.
  • Frame layout assumed 4Ã—4. If your future sheets use a different grid (e.g. 8 columns Ã— 2 rows for a more granular cycle), tweak CHAR_FRAMES_X / Y or move to per-asset metadata.
  • Walk cycle uses row 0 only. Other rows in the current sheet may be different states â€” adjust _currentCharFrame() when you know the layout.
  • Procedural cape / weapon swing animations skipped when the sprite path is active. They were calibrated for the procedural body; sprite-based gear handles those visuals once the gear PNGs land.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.26. New CHARACTER_ASSETS map, _loadCharLayer / _currentCharFrame / _drawCharFrame helpers (~75 lines). drawLayeredCharacter rewritten from return false to the full slot composition (~50 lines).
  • New asset â€” Sprites/character/chibi_nude.png.
  • Vanilla / Phaser flag-off path unchanged.

v0.25.25 Phaser 3 migration â€” Phase 5b (boss banner + flash overlay)

Phase 5b closes out the HUD migration. The two remaining canvas-rendered HUD pieces â€” the super-boss top-of-screen banner and the white flash overlay â€” move to a new LXOverlayScene on the UI canvas at the topmost depth. Combined with Phase 5a's mob HUD migration, every canvas-2D HUD draw is now batched through Phaser when the flag is on.

âš¡ LXOverlayScene

  • Flash overlay â€” single full-canvas Phaser.Rectangle (depth 100). Each frame setAlpha tracks game.flashOverlay. Replaces vanilla's per-frame fillRect(0,0,W,H).
  • Boss banner pre-built scene tree at create():
    • Plate backdrop (phase-coloured stroke, hyper-boss variants 3 px instead of 2 px)
    • Name label (16 px bold, phase-coloured, prefix "âš  HYPER BOSS â€” " when m.hyperBoss set)
    • HP bar background (1.5 W full width, dark plum)
    • Phase tick marks at 33% and 66% (translucent white)
    • HP fill (single solid colour per phase â€” see scope trade below)
    • Shimmer highlight along top edge of fill
    • White inner border
    • HP/phase readout text ("currentHP / maxHP Â· PHASE n")
  • Hidden by default. Banner only visible when game.monsters contains a live m.superBoss. _setBossBannerVisible(v) toggles all 8 components in one call.

ðŸšª Decay split for flash overlay

Vanilla's flash block both painted the rect and decayed the alpha (game.flashOverlay *= 0.75). If the whole block were gated by Phaser, decay would stop and the overlay would stay at full alpha forever. The fix splits paint from state mutation: the gate only short-circuits the canvas paint; the decay step keeps running unconditionally so both renderers stay in sync.

âš–ï¸ Scope trade â€” HP bar gradient

Vanilla's HP fill used ctx.createLinearGradient with a 2-stop horizontal gradient that varied per phase. Phaser Rectangle objects don't support per-frame gradients without per-phase pre-rendered gradient textures + a TileSprite. Phase 5b uses a single solid colour per phase as a first-pass approximation. If the bar reads as flat in play, follow-up polish can add the gradient back â€” but it's a 60 px-tall element only visible during boss fights, so the cosmetic loss is small.

ðŸŽ¯ What's left on vanilla

After Phase 5b, the remaining canvas-2D draws are:

  • Monster body silhouettes for non-blob types (humanoid mobs, bosses, zodiac bosses) â€” pending Phase 4d-3+
  • Hit-flash filter applied to monster bodies (vanilla wraps body in save/restore)
  • Burn FX (fire particles overlaid on monsters)
  • Mob chat bubbles (_drawMobChat)
  • Player character sprite + procedural weapons/cape â€” Phase 3b
  • A few projectile types (voidbeam) â€” Phase 4c-2 follow-up

Player stats panel, minimap, level-up modal, inventory, chat log, combo meter, low-HP vignette, level-up DOM toast â€” all DOM, never need Phaser.

ðŸ“… Migration roadmap

  • âœ… Phase 1-5a (v0.25.15 - v0.25.24).
  • âœ… Phase 5b. Boss banner + flash overlay (this release).
  • Phase 4d-3+ Remaining mob bodies (humanoids, bosses, zodiac).
  • Phase 3b. Character sprite.
  • Phase 6. Retire vanilla canvas.

How to try it

Append ?phaser=1 and trigger any boss fight (Aetherion, Gravitos, zodiac bosses). The banner now renders through Phaser. Take a hit to see the flash overlay. DevTools:

const s = window.__LX_PHASER_UI.scene.getScene('lx_overlay');
s._flash.alpha           // tracks game.flashOverlay
s._bbName.text           // boss name when banner visible
s._bbInfo.text           // "current / max Â· PHASE n"

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.25. New LXOverlayScene (~100 lines) added to UI scene: list. New _phaserHandlingOverlay() gate. Vanilla drawSuperBossBar early-return + main-loop flash block split (decay always, paint guarded).
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact. Flag-off path unchanged.

v0.25.24 Phaser 3 migration â€” Phase 5a (mob HP bars + names)

Phase 5a opens the HUD migration: the per-monster HP bar + name label â€” the highest-fire-rate canvas HUD elements in the game â€” move to a pooled Phaser scene on the UI canvas. Every visible mob fired 5+ canvas-2D ops per frame on vanilla (bar bg, bar fill, measureText, name bg, name text); pooled rectangles + Text objects in Phaser collapse all of those into batched WebGL draws.

ðŸ“Š LXMonsterUIScene

  • Four parallel pools â€” _bgBarPool, _fillBarPool, _bgNamePool, _namePool. Each pool batches independently; depth ordering keeps fills above backings.
  • Phaser GameObjects.Rectangle for the bars + name backing â€” no preload, no texture lookup. setSize() per frame mutates the in-place vertex buffer (cheap).
  • Phaser.Text pool for mob names. Text width readback (t.width) is cached internally by Phaser and only recalculates when the string changes â€” replaces vanilla's per-frame ctx.measureText.
  • HP bar fill colour matches vanilla's three-tier scheme: green > 50%, yellow 25-50%, red < 25%. setFillStyle per frame is a one-uniform update.
  • Boss differentiation â€” name labels render at 12 px bold orange for bosses, 10 px white for grunts. Both come from the same Text pool with per-instance setFontSize / setFontStyle / setColor.
  • Cull envelope matches vanilla (Â±40 px around viewport).

ðŸšª Gate

New _phaserHandlingMonsterUI() mirrors the existing pattern. drawMonster gets a single-line return between ctx.restore() and the HP bar block â€” short-circuits when the scene is up. Importantly, the gate fires after the body's save/restore + burn FX, so those continue to render on vanilla without disruption.

ðŸ’¡ Why this is the perf-impact pick for HUD migration

Most "HUD" elements in this game are DOM (player stats panel, minimap, level-up modal, inventory, chat log). The canvas-rendered HUD pieces are: per-mob HP bars + names (high count, every frame), boss banner (one per frame in boss fights), flash overlay (one per frame on hit). Per-mob HUD is the only one that fires > 1 per frame â€” and during boss arenas with 3-5 elites + a boss + their HP bars on screen, it's been measurable.

ðŸ¤ Combined with prior phases

With this release, monsters now flow through Phaser for:

  • Body (slime/snail/mushroom from Phase 4d-1/4d-2)
  • HP bars + name labels (every mob type â€” this release)

Future 4d-3+ types automatically get the HUD migration "for free" â€” the gate doesn't filter by mob type. Body ports are independent and additive.

ðŸ“… Migration roadmap

  • âœ… Phase 1-4d-2 (v0.25.15 - v0.25.23).
  • âœ… Phase 5a. Mob HP bars + name labels (this release).
  • Phase 5b. Super boss banner + flash overlay.
  • Phase 4d-3+ Remaining mob bodies (humanoids, bosses, zodiac).
  • Phase 3b. Character sprite.
  • Phase 6. Retire vanilla canvas.

How to try it

Append ?phaser=1 and engage any mob. HP bars and name labels are now Phaser-rendered. DevTools:

const s = window.__LX_PHASER_UI.scene.getScene('lx_monster_ui');
s._bgBarPool.length     // grows to peak visible-mob count
s._namePool.length      // same
s._fillUsed             // mobs currently showing damage

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.24. New LXMonsterUIScene (~110 lines) added to UI scene: list. New _phaserHandlingMonsterUI() gate. drawMonster gets a one-line early-return.
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact. Flag-off path unchanged.

v0.25.23 Phaser 3 migration â€” Phase 4d-2 (snail + mushroom)

Phase 4d-2: two more blob mob types land on Phaser. Snail and mushroom (plus its orange variant) follow the slime template from 4d-1 â€” pre-rendered body texture at boot, dispatch via textureForMob(), vanilla drawCuteMob short-circuits via the existing _phaserHandlingMonsterBody(m) gate. No new architectural work; the slot of bench from 4d-1 is doing real work now.

ðŸŒ Snail â€” 36Ã—28 px texture

  • Wide squat oval body (foot) â€” white base for runtime tinting.
  • Round purple shell on top with two concentric spiral lines â€” not tinted (shells stay purple regardless of mob colour, matching vanilla).
  • Two antenna stalks with eye dots, body smile.
  • Shell sheen highlight, white-base body edge stroke.

ðŸ„ Mushroom + orange mushroom â€” 30Ã—32 px texture (shared)

  • Big red dome cap with 4 white polka dots â€” not tinted (caps are iconically red across all mushroom variants).
  • White-base stem with eye dots, sparkle highlights, smile, blush, and tiny black feet â€” tinted at runtime via setTint(m.color) so regular and orange mushrooms differentiate via the stem colour.
  • Both m.type === 'mushroom' and m.type === 'orange' route to this single texture.

ðŸ› ï¸ Plumbing

  • New addMob(key, w, h, draw) helper in preload â€” convenience wrapper around createCanvas + addCanvas so each new mob's texture stays a self-contained block. Reduces boilerplate as more types land.
  • New _texBaseSize(key) method returns natural dimensions per texture; the update loop now derives scale from m.w / m.h Ã· baseSize rather than hardcoded 28Ã—22 from the slime port.
  • No new gates â€” vanilla drawCuteMob still short-circuits via the v0.25.22 _phaserHandlingMonsterBody(m) check, which calls into the scene's textureForMob dispatch. Adding new types is now a 2-line change (texture entry + dispatch case).

âš–ï¸ Visual trades (per type)

  • Snail. No body squash; single static silhouette + bob. Antenna stalks are static (vanilla redraws them per-frame angled toward facing â€” minor difference).
  • Mushroom. Orange mushroom's grumpy frown collapses to the same smile as regular mushroom. Mood now reads via stem colour alone. Polka dot positions don't shift across spawns (vanilla's are deterministic so this matches).
  • HP bars / name labels / burn FX / hit-flash filter still vanilla on top of the Phaser sprite, same as 4d-1.

ðŸ“… Migration roadmap

  • âœ… Phase 1-4c-2 (v0.25.15 - v0.25.21).
  • âœ… Phase 4d-1. Slime monster (v0.25.22).
  • âœ… Phase 4d-2. Snail + mushroom (this release).
  • Phase 4d-3. Horny / stump / humanoid mobs (zombie / skeleton / mummy / wraith / scorpion).
  • Phase 4d-4. Bosses (king slime / mushmom / aetherion / gravitos).
  • Phase 4d-5. Zodiac bosses (12 of them).
  • Phase 5. HUD.
  • Phase 3b. Character sprite.
  • Phase 6. Retire vanilla canvas.

How to try it

Append ?phaser=1 and head to a wilderness map. Slimes, snails, and mushrooms (regular + orange) now render through Phaser. DevTools:

const s = window.__LX_PHASER_UI.scene.getScene('lx_monsters');
s.textureForMob({type:'snail'})     // 'lx_mob_snail'
s.textureForMob({type:'mushroom'})  // 'lx_mob_mushroom'
s.textureForMob({type:'orange'})    // 'lx_mob_mushroom' (shared)
s.textureForMob({type:'horny'})     // null (still on vanilla)

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.23. Two new texture blocks in LXMonsterScene.preload (~70 lines combined). New addMob helper + _texBaseSize dispatch in the scene. textureForMob grows from 1 type to 3 types (slime/snail/mushroom+orange).
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact. Flag-off path unchanged.

v0.25.22 Phaser 3 migration â€” Phase 4d-1 (slime monsters)

First step into Phase 4d: the biggest remaining surgery. Monster bodies are procedurally rendered with squash/breathing animations, hit flashes, facing flips, and per-type silhouettes â€” far more state than projectiles or particles. This release ports the simplest mob (slime) end-to-end and lays the architectural groundwork for follow-up sessions to add more types incrementally without re-deriving the wiring.

ðŸŽ¯ Why such a narrow first slice?

A naive "port all monsters at once" approach would mean re-implementing 9+ distinct procedural draw functions (each 30-60 lines) plus boss variants and zodiac bosses. That's 2-3 sessions of work and the kind of monolithic change CLAUDE.md's scope guardrails explicitly warn against. Slime alone proves the full pipeline (scene â†’ texture â†’ pool â†’ vanilla skip) and gives every later type a copy-paste template.

ðŸ‘¾ LXMonsterScene

  • Pre-rendered slime body at preload â€” 28Ã—22 px white-base teardrop with dark outline, eyes, smile, top sheen. Tint applied at runtime so each slime matches its m.color.
  • Sprite pool on the UI canvas at depth 2 (under the depth-5 particle pool â€” hit sparks correctly sit OVER the body).
  • Per-frame sync â€” position centered on hitbox with bob (sin(animTimer Ã— 0.02) Ã— 1.5 matching vanilla), scale derived from m.w / m.h, facing via setFlipX.
  • Hit flash reproduced via setTintFill(0xffffff) in the bright phase â€” approximates vanilla's ctx.filter = 'brightness(2.5)'.
  • Type filter via textureForMob(m) â€” returns 'lx_mob_slime' only for m.type === 'slime'; bosses and zodiac bosses always skip; everything else returns null â†’ vanilla.

ðŸšª Vanilla skip

New gate _phaserHandlingMonsterBody(m) mirrors the projectile pattern. drawCuteMob's very first statement returns when true. drawMonster's outer wrapper still runs HP bar, name label, burn FX, and the hit-flash filter â€” only the silhouette is yielded.

âš–ï¸ Scope trades

  • Squash/breathing animation lost â€” vanilla slime pulses via animTimer-driven width/height oscillation around the ellipse. The Phaser sprite has a static silhouette + bob, no width oscillation. Subtle visual regression.
  • Pink drip effect skipped â€” vanilla draws two tiny pink pixels below the slime every other 60-frame cycle. Could come back as a particle emission in future polish.
  • Burn FX still vanilla on top â€” when a slime is on fire and the flag is on, the orange burn flames render on the vanilla canvas underneath the Phaser sprite. Visible as flames "behind" the slime instead of in front. Cosmetic regression; documented for follow-up.

ðŸ“… Migration roadmap

  • âœ… Phase 1-4c-2 (v0.25.15 - v0.25.21).
  • âœ… Phase 4d-1. Slime monster (this release).
  • Phase 4d-2. Snail + mushroom + horny + stump (other "blob" mobs).
  • Phase 4d-3. Humanoid mobs (zombie / skeleton / mummy / wraith / scorpion).
  • Phase 4d-4. Bosses (king slime / mushmom / aetherion / gravitos).
  • Phase 4d-5. Zodiac bosses (12 of them â€” share _zBodyPath).
  • Phase 5. HUD.
  • Phase 3b. Character sprite.
  • Phase 6. Retire vanilla canvas.

How to try it

Append ?phaser=1 and head to a wilderness map. Slimes (the pink/blue/yellow blob mobs) now render through Phaser; every other mob still runs through vanilla. DevTools:

const s = window.__LX_PHASER_UI.scene.getScene('lx_monsters');
s.textureForMob({type:'slime'})         // 'lx_mob_slime'
s.textureForMob({type:'snail'})         // null (vanilla)
s.textureForMob({type:'slime', isBoss:true})  // null (king slime â†’ vanilla)

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.22. New LXMonsterScene (~130 lines) added to UI scene: list. New _phaserHandlingMonsterBody(m) gate. drawCuteMob gets a one-line top-of-function early-return.
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact. Flag-off path unchanged.

v0.25.21 Phaser 3 migration â€” Phase 4c-2 (full projectile port)

Phase 4c-2: the v0.25.20 arrow-only port grows to cover every projectile type except voidbeam. Two pieces of work â€” a refactor that splits side-effect particle emissions out of the render loop, and an expanded LXProjectileScene that pre-renders eleven projectile textures at boot and dispatches per-projectile through a single batched WebGL pipeline.

ðŸ”§ Refactor â€” emissions out of render

Vanilla drawProjectiles historically pushed trail / dust / sparkle particles inline inside its per-skill render branches. New emitProjectileTrails() function pulls every emission out byte-for-byte (same spawn math, same game.time % N cadence) and runs from the main update tick â€” guaranteed every frame regardless of which renderer draws the projectile shape. Affects:

  • fire â€” trail every frame
  • mspore â€” orange dust every 4 frames
  • mdark â€” purple sparkles every 3 frames
  • mtoxic â€” green drips every 4 frames
  • shard â€” violet trail every 2 frames
  • voidbeam â€” 2 plasma particles every 2 frames

ðŸ–¼ï¸ Pre-rendered texture set (11)

Each projectile archetype renders ONCE to an offscreen canvas at preload and registers as a Phaser texture. Runtime per-frame cost collapses to setPosition / setRotation / setScale / setTint on a pooled sprite â€” same sprite-batched WebGL pipeline as Phase 4a particles.

TextureMaps to
lx_arrowarrow / charged / deadeye / rainArrow / elemArrow (player)
lx_firefireball
lx_iceice spike
lx_daggerdagger
lx_boltplayer default magic bolt
lx_msporeenemy spore pellet
lx_mdarkenemy dark bolt
lx_msplinterenemy wooden splinter
lx_mtoxicenemy toxic blob
lx_shardenemy crystal shard
lx_enemyenemy default ellipse bolt

ðŸ” Per-projectile dispatch

The scene's update loop drives a unified pipeline:

  • Texture lookup via textureForProj(p) â€” also used by the vanilla skip gate so the two can't disagree.
  • Rotation â€” arrow / ice / dagger / msplinter rotate to match velocity (atan2(vy, vx)); shard spins continuously (+ game.time Ã— 0.15); circles/blobs stay axis-aligned.
  • Scale â€” uniform p.w / texWidth for rotated rectangles; circles/blobs target a diameter of max(10, p.w Ã— 1.8) so the bitmap diameter matches vanilla's radius-based render.
  • Tint â€” only arrows tint by p.color (vanilla re-colors the shaft); other textures are pre-rendered with their vanilla colors and stay tint=white.
  • Pool reuse â€” pool sprites swap textures via setTexture(key) when reassigned to a different projectile type; pool grows to peak count, never shrinks.

ðŸšª Gate

New _phaserHandlingProjectile(p) asks the scene whether it owns this specific projectile. A single early-out at the top of vanilla's render loop short-circuits any projectile Phaser handles. voidbeam (complex linear gradient + direction-dependent geometry) returns null â†’ vanilla keeps drawing it for now.

ðŸ“… Migration roadmap

  • âœ… Phase 1. Scaffolding (v0.25.15).
  • âœ… Phase 2. Background (v0.25.16).
  • âœ… Phase 3a. Nameplate + two-canvas (v0.25.17).
  • âœ… Phase 4a. Particles (v0.25.18).
  • âœ… Phase 4b. Halos + damage numbers (v0.25.19).
  • âœ… Phase 4c. Player arrows (v0.25.20).
  • âœ… Phase 4c-2. Full projectile port (this release).
  • Phase 4d. Monsters â€” biggest surgery (atlas-gen per type).
  • Phase 5. HUD.
  • Phase 3b. Character sprite.
  • Phase 6. Retire vanilla canvas.

How to try it

Append ?phaser=1 and trigger every projectile type â€” basic attacks, fireballs, ice spikes, daggers, mob attacks. All flow through Phaser. DevTools:

const s = window.__LX_PHASER_UI.scene.getScene('lx_projectiles');
s._pool.length        // peak sprite count
s.textureForProj({owner:'enemy', skill:'mspore'})   // â†’ 'lx_mspore'
s.textureForProj({owner:'enemy', skill:'voidbeam'}) // â†’ null (vanilla)

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.21. New emitProjectileTrails() function (~50 lines) wired into the main tick. LXProjectileScene grows to ~250 lines (preload generates 11 textures, update loop dispatches per-type with rotation / scale / tint logic, helpers textureForProj + _texBaseSize + _rotatesWithVelocity). New _phaserHandlingProjectile(p) gate; vanilla drawProjectiles gets a one-line top-of-loop early-out.
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact. Flag-off path unchanged.

v0.25.20 Phaser 3 migration â€” Phase 4c (arrows)

Narrow Phase 4c: player-owned arrow projectiles render through a new LXProjectileScene on the UI canvas. Scoped deliberately tight â€” only arrows, not the variety-heavy magic / enemy projectile branches â€” because arrows are the only projectile type with no side-effect particle emissions baked into vanilla's render loop. Doing the rest properly needs a render/emit refactor that deserves its own session.

ðŸ¹ LXProjectileScene

  • Pre-rendered arrow texture generated at preload: a 32Ã—8 canvas with white shaft, gray arrowhead triangle, brown feather bars. Registered as lx_arrow. Tinted per-projectile at runtime so arrows pick up the vanilla p.color field.
  • Sprite pool same pattern as particles / damage numbers. setRotation(atan2(vy, vx)) points the arrow along its velocity; setScale(p.w / 32) matches vanilla's per-projectile width.
  • Skill filter â€” skill âˆˆ {arrow, charged, deadeye, rainArrow, elemArrow}. All share the arrow visual in vanilla; Phaser does the same.
  • Owner filter â€” only p.owner === 'player'. Enemy arrows (if any) stay on vanilla.
  • Â±60 px cull matches vanilla.

ðŸšª Gate

New helper _phaserHandlingArrows(). Vanilla drawProjectiles skips ONLY its arrow branch (continue) when true; every other projectile type continues to render normally through vanilla along with their side-effect emissions (fireball trail, spore dust, dark-bolt sparkles, toxic drips, etc.).

âš–ï¸ Scope trades

  • Deadeye / charged / elemArrow glow overlay intentionally dropped â€” minor visual regression on those three skill variants' secondary glow rectangle. Can be restored in a follow-up pass with a second ADD-blend sprite per glow-arrow.
  • Fireball / ice / dagger / default-bolt / enemy projectile types stay on vanilla. Each has particle emissions inside the vanilla render branch â€” moving those to updateProjectiles is a separate refactor (Phase 4c-2, future session).

ðŸ“… Migration roadmap

  • âœ… Phase 1. Scaffolding (v0.25.15).
  • âœ… Phase 2. Background (v0.25.16).
  • âœ… Phase 3a. Nameplate + two-canvas (v0.25.17).
  • âœ… Phase 4a. Particles (v0.25.18).
  • âœ… Phase 4b. Halos + damage numbers (v0.25.19).
  • âœ… Phase 4c (narrow). Player arrows (this release).
  • Phase 4c-2. Remaining projectile types (fire/ice/dagger/bolts/enemy) â€” requires render/emit refactor.
  • Phase 4d. Monsters (biggest surgery).
  • Phase 5. HUD.
  • Phase 3b. Character sprite.
  • Phase 6. Retire vanilla canvas.

How to try it

Append ?phaser=1 and play archer. Multishot / Arrow Rain / Ranger elemental barrage all now render through Phaser â€” visually indistinguishable aside from the dropped deadeye glow overlay. DevTools:

window.__LX_PHASER_UI.scene.getScene('lx_projectiles')._pool.length
window.__LX_PHASER_UI.scene.getScene('lx_projectiles')._used

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.20. New LXProjectileScene (~90 lines) added to UI scene: list. New _phaserHandlingArrows() gate. Vanilla drawProjectiles arrow branch gets a one-line continue early-out.
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact. Flag-off path still byte-identical.

v0.25.19 Phaser 3 migration â€” Phase 4b (halos + damage numbers)

Phase 4b: restores the glow-halo pass that 4a intentionally dropped, and ports the per-frame floating damage-number render to a Phaser scene. Both land on the UI canvas (z:3) â€” same batching story as 4a. Flag-off path still byte-identical.

âœ¨ Glow halos restored

  • Second sprite pool in LXParticleScene (_haloPool) with Phaser.BlendModes.ADD. For each bright particle (maxChannel > 200, matching vanilla _isBrightHex), a halo sprite is drawn behind the core at ~3Ã— scale, 0.35 Ã— life-alpha. Depth 4 (under the depth-5 core pool).
  • Still single-draw-call per batch. ADD-blend sprites batch separately from NORMAL-blend ones; both batches are single-draw regardless of count, so the halo pass is effectively free on GPU.
  • Cull envelope widened to Â±30 px (from Â±20) â€” halos are bigger than cores and drop off the visible edge later.

ðŸ’¥ LXDamageNumberScene

Pooled Phaser.Text objects on the UI canvas. Reproduces the bulk of vanilla drawDamageNumbers per-frame animation:

  • âœ“ Elastic pop-in scale (first 10 frames, back-overshoot â€” crits overshoot harder).
  • âœ“ Idle breathing bob during mid-life.
  • âœ“ Tail shrink + fade in the last min(14, life Ã— 0.32) frames.
  • âœ“ Wobble rotation settling over the first 12 frames, sign per-number.
  • âœ“ Black outline via setStroke â€” 6 px for crits, 4.5 px default.
  • âœ“ Shadow glow via setShadow â€” crit blur 16, big blur 8, normal drop-shadow (2, 3).
  • âœ“ Same Â±80 / Â±50 px culling envelope as vanilla.

âš–ï¸ Scope trades

  • Vertical-gradient fill on crits / big hits â†’ solid tint color. Phaser.Text has no native vertical gradient; reproducing it would need a per-number off-screen canvas re-render, defeating the batching. Crits still read punchier because of the heavier stroke and stronger shadow.
  • Two-pass thick outline + inner gold-foil stroke on crits â†’ single setStroke at heavier thickness. Close visually; the gold-foil detail is lost.

ðŸšª Gate

New helper _phaserHandlingDamageNumbers(). Vanilla drawDamageNumbers returns immediately when it's true â€” no double-draw.

ðŸ“… Migration roadmap

  • âœ… Phase 1. Scaffolding (v0.25.15).
  • âœ… Phase 2. Background (v0.25.16).
  • âœ… Phase 3a. Nameplate + two-canvas architecture (v0.25.17).
  • âœ… Phase 4a. Particles (v0.25.18).
  • âœ… Phase 4b. Halos + damage numbers (this release).
  • Phase 4c. Projectiles.
  • Phase 4d. Monsters (biggest surgery â€” requires atlas gen).
  • Phase 5. HUD.
  • Phase 3b. Character sprite.
  • Phase 6. Retire vanilla canvas.

How to try it

Append ?phaser=1 to the game URL (or localStorage.setItem('levelx_phaser','1')) and engage a boss â€” bright particles now have additive halos, damage numbers pop + bob + shrink just like vanilla. DevTools:

window.__LX_PHASER_UI.scene.getScene('lx_dmgnums')._pool.length
window.__LX_PHASER_UI.scene.getScene('lx_particles')._haloPool.length

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.19. LXParticleScene gains _haloPool + _isBrightHex + _getHalo(); update loop emits halos conditionally. New LXDamageNumberScene (~95 lines) added to UI scene: list. New _phaserHandlingDamageNumbers() gate; vanilla drawDamageNumbers gets a one-line early-return.
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact.

v0.25.18 Phaser 3 migration â€” Phase 4a (particles)

Phase 4a of the Phaser migration â€” the biggest perf target. The per-frame particle draw loop (up to 500 sprites on desktop, 200 on mobile) moves from vanilla Canvas 2D fillRect calls to a pooled Phaser sprite layer on the UI canvas. WebGL batches all same-blend-mode sprites into a single GPU draw call, so the marginal cost per particle drops sharply â€” this was the dominant per-frame vanilla cost during boss fights and combo bursts.

ðŸŽ† LXParticleScene

  • Lives on the UI canvas (#phaser-ui-canvas, z:3). Particles naturally render on top of vanilla entities (hit-sparks, damage numbers, etc.), so the UI-layer placement is correct visually.
  • Sprite pool of Phaser.Image objects backed by a generated 2Ã—2 white texture. Each pool entry is tinted + scaled per-particle. Same-blend-mode sprites are batched by WebGL into a single draw call regardless of particle count.
  • Text pool of Phaser.Text for p.text particles (CRIT! popups and similar).
  • No allocation per frame. Sprites are reused from frame to frame â€” the pool only grows when the live particle count exceeds the previous high-water mark.
  • Life-based alpha fade (last 20 ticks) and Â±20 px culling match vanilla exactly. Colors converted from '#rrggbb' â†’ 0xRRGGBB via the scene's _parseColor helper.

ðŸšª Gate

New helper _phaserHandlingParticles() mirrors the Phase 2 / 3a pattern. Returns true when the UI Game is up and the particle scene is ready. Vanilla drawParticles returns immediately in that case â€” no double-draw.

âš–ï¸ Scope trade-offs for 4a

  • Glow halos (vanilla pass 1) intentionally skipped â€” the additive-blend larger halos behind bright particles are a minor visual contribution. If they read as too flat in play, Phase 4b adds a second bright-particle sprite-per-entity at ADD blend mode for the halo.
  • Damage numbers, projectiles, monsters stay on vanilla for now. These land in Phase 4b/4c â€” each is smaller in count than particles but benefits from the same batching treatment.

ðŸ“… Migration roadmap

  • âœ… Phase 1. Scaffolding + proof-of-concept (v0.25.15).
  • âœ… Phase 2. Background image layer (v0.25.16).
  • âœ… Phase 3a. Two-canvas architecture + player nameplate (v0.25.17).
  • Phase 3b. Character sprite â€” deferred until after Phase 4.
  • âœ… Phase 4a. Particles (this release).
  • Phase 4b. Damage numbers + glow halos for bright particles.
  • Phase 4c. Projectiles (boss-fight batching win).
  • Phase 4d. Monsters (sprite atlas; biggest surgery).
  • Phase 5. HUD.
  • Phase 3b. Character sprite.
  • Phase 6. Retire vanilla canvas.

How to try it

Append ?phaser=1 to the game URL (or localStorage.setItem('levelx_phaser','1')). Particles now render via Phaser â€” visually indistinguishable from vanilla aside from the skipped halo pass. DevTools:

window.__LX_PHASER_UI.scene.getScene('lx_particles')._imgPool.length
window.__LX_PHASER_UI.scene.getScene('lx_particles')._imgUsed

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.18. New LXParticleScene (~100 lines) added to the UI Phaser scene: list. New _phaserHandlingParticles() gate. Vanilla drawParticles gets a one-line early-return.
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact. Flag-off path still byte-identical.

v0.25.17 Phaser 3 migration â€” Phase 3a (player nameplate)

Phase 3a of the Phaser migration. Establishes the two-Phaser-canvas architecture (BG under vanilla, UI over vanilla) and ports the first UI element â€” the player nameplate â€” to a Phaser scene. Small visible surface, but the architectural move is the important piece: future HUD migrations (level-up panel, inventory, chat) now have a clear home.

ðŸ—ï¸ Two-canvas architecture

Phaser renders to a single canvas per Game instance. To have content both under vanilla (BG) and over vanilla (UI), we need two Game instances while the migration is in flight. Once Phase 6 retires the vanilla layer, the two canvases merge back into one:

  • #phaser-canvas (z-index 1) â€” hosts __LX_PHASER_BG running the existing LXBackgroundScene.
  • #game (z-index 2) â€” vanilla entities, particles, effects (unchanged).
  • #phaser-ui-canvas (z-index 3) â€” new, hosts __LX_PHASER_UI running the new LXPlayerNameplateScene.

window.__LX_PHASER kept as an alias for __LX_PHASER_BG so any code still referencing the Phase 1 global name keeps working.

ðŸ·ï¸ LXPlayerNameplateScene

Reproduces the vanilla _drawPlayerNameTag look byte-for-byte:

  • Gold 0xffdc8c border at 0.55 alpha, 1 px stroke.
  • Dark 0x140e1e fill at 0.72 alpha.
  • Trebuchet MS bold 11 px, #ffeeaa text, center-aligned.
  • Falls back to title-cased class name when player.look.name is empty.
  • Hidden while paused, dead, or no name available.
  • Text measurement cached on the scene; recomputed only when the display string changes.

ðŸšª Gate

New helper _phaserHandlingNameplate() mirrors the Phase 2 _phaserHandlingBg() pattern. Returns true when the UI Game is up and the nameplate scene is ready. Vanilla _drawPlayerNameTag returns immediately in that case â€” no double-draw.

ðŸ“… Migration roadmap

  • âœ… Phase 1. Scaffolding + proof-of-concept (v0.25.15).
  • âœ… Phase 2. Background image layer (v0.25.16).
  • âœ… Phase 3a. Two-canvas architecture + player nameplate (this release).
  • Phase 3b. Character sprite (procedural atlas-gen at boot, Phaser Sprite animated by player state).
  • Phase 4. Monsters, projectiles, particles â€” biggest perf payoff.
  • Phase 5. HUD (bars, inventory, level-up panel) â†’ UI Phaser.
  • Phase 6. Retire the vanilla canvas; two Phaser Games merge.

How to try it

Append ?phaser=1 to the game URL (or localStorage.setItem('levelx_phaser','1')). The nameplate visible below your character is now Phaser-rendered. DevTools sanity checks:

window.__LX_PHASER_BG.scene.getScene('lx_bg')._currentKey
window.__LX_PHASER_UI.scene.getScene('lx_nameplate')._text.text

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.17. New #phaser-ui-canvas DOM host. Bootstrap now creates two Phaser.Game instances. New LXPlayerNameplateScene (~40 lines). New _phaserHandlingNameplate() gate + _phaserHandlingBg updated to reference __LX_PHASER_BG directly. Vanilla _drawPlayerNameTag gets a one-line early-return.
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact. Flag-off path still byte-identical.

v0.25.16 Phaser 3 migration â€” Phase 2 (background layer)

Phase 2 of the Phaser migration. The background image layer â€” every outdoor map's bitmap + haze â€” now renders through Phaser when the flag is on, via a WebGL-batched TileSprite. The flag-off path is byte-identical to v0.25.15; flag-on users drop the per-frame drawImage Ã— 2 + fillRect sequence in exchange for a single GPU draw call.

ðŸª› What landed

  • Canvas stacking. Z-index swap: vanilla #game (z:2) now sits over Phaser's #phaser-canvas (z:1). Phaser paints the bg image underneath so monsters, particles, player, and HUD still render on the vanilla canvas as before.
  • LXBackgroundScene replaces the Phase 1 test scene. Preloads all 5 bg PNGs into Phaser's texture cache at boot. A pickBgKey() method mirrors vanilla's _pickBGImage selection rules exactly â€” explicit mapData.bg override, town â†’ homestead, combat maps â†’ hash-cycle across the other four.
  • TileSprite for tiling. Replaces the vanilla two-image drawImage pattern. WebGL batches arbitrarily-wide parallax scroll into a single draw call. tilePositionX tracks game.camera.x Ã— 0.12 so parallax matches the vanilla factor exactly.
  • Haze overlay. Painted as a Phaser Rectangle at depth 10 with the same rgba(20,24,48,0.18) tint as vanilla's atmospheric haze fillRect.
  • Texture swap on map change. The previous TileSprite is destroyed before a new one is created, so GPU texture slots don't leak across dozens of map loads.
  • Vanilla gate. New helper _phaserHandlingBg() returns true iff (flag on) + (scene ready) + (scene has a texture for this map). When true, drawBackground's very first statement is ctx.clearRect â€” vanilla leaves the canvas transparent so the Phaser layer underneath shows through. Sky gradient, procedural mountains/pines/flowers/sparkles, haze, and vignette all skipped in that branch.

ðŸŽ¯ Expected wins on the BG layer

Vanilla cost per frame (flag off, bitmap map): 1 full-canvas gradient fill + 2 drawImage tile blits + 1 full-canvas haze fill = 4 full-screen-sized ops. Under HiDPI (2Ã—) that's ~8.6 MP of fill rate per frame just for the bg layer.

Phaser cost per frame (flag on, bitmap map): 1 batched WebGL draw call for the TileSprite + 1 batched draw for the haze Rectangle. GPU batching makes the marginal cost of the tile negligible once the texture is uploaded.

Special backdrops unchanged

Sanctum cosmos, zodiac boss arenas, subway carriage, and the zodiac hub all still render through the vanilla canvas â€” pickBgKey returns null for those and the vanilla fallback path is untouched. Boss-arena decor also still rides on vanilla (painted on top of the Phaser bg).

ðŸ“… Migration roadmap (reminder)

  • âœ… Phase 1. Scaffolding + proof-of-concept (v0.25.15).
  • âœ… Phase 2. Background image layer (this release).
  • Phase 3. Character + nameplate â†’ Phaser sprites.
  • Phase 4. Monsters, projectiles, particles (biggest perf payoff).
  • Phase 5. HUD.
  • Phase 6. Retire the vanilla canvas path.

How to try it

Append ?phaser=1 to the game URL, or run localStorage.setItem('levelx_phaser', '1') in DevTools and reload. On any outdoor map the bg image is now GPU-rendered; the test-scene yellow diamond from v0.25.15 is gone (replaced by the real bg). window.__LX_PHASER.scene.getScene('lx_bg') is the scene object for DevTools debugging.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.16. Three focused edits: #game gets position:relative; z-index:2, #phaser-canvas z-index drops to 1. Phaser bootstrap scene body rewritten into LXBackgroundScene (~55 lines). drawBackground gets a _phaserHandlingBg() early-out gate.
  • No save/network/gameplay/protocol impact. Flag-off path is byte-identical to v0.25.15.

v0.25.15 Phaser 3 migration â€” Phase 1 (scaffolding)

Phase 1 of a multi-session migration to Phaser 3. No visible gameplay change. Lands the plumbing (CDN load, parent DOM element, bootstrap IIFE, proof-of-concept test scene) so future sessions can incrementally port the render layer (background â†’ characters â†’ monsters â†’ particles â†’ HUD) without another scaffolding pass. Flag-gated â€” the vanilla canvas build is byte-identical for any user who doesn't explicitly opt in.

ðŸŽ¯ Why Phaser 3?

The vanilla Canvas 2D render layer issues one GPU call per fillRect/arc. On mobile GPUs that's the dominant frame-time cost â€” v0.25.9 and v0.25.14 were both perf passes patching this. Phaser 3's WebGL batcher collapses hundreds of sprites into a single draw call. Expected mobile FPS win: 2-4Ã— on the same content.

ðŸª› What landed

  • Flag. window.__PHASER_ENABLED is set at script-eval time from either ?phaser=1 (URL) or localStorage.levelx_phaser=1 (persistent). No visual or cost impact when off.
  • CDN load. Second inline <script> appends a Phaser 3.80.1 CDN tag to <head> only when the flag is on. Zero download cost otherwise.
  • Parent DOM. New #phaser-canvas div sits over the existing #game canvas with pointer-events:none, display:none by default. Becomes visible when the bootstrap flips it on.
  • Bootstrap. _bootstrapPhaser() runs at page load; short-circuits when the flag is off. Polls for the Phaser global every 50 ms, then spins up a Phaser.Game configured with type: Phaser.AUTO (WebGL preferred, Canvas fallback), transparent: true, 960Ã—560 logical size, arcade physics pre-wired.
  • Test scene. Renders a 32-px yellow diamond (generated in-memory â€” zero asset dependency) plus a "PHASER ON" label in the top-left. Seeing those confirms the entire pipeline works end-to-end.
  • Debug handle. window.__LX_PHASER exposes the Game instance for interactive DevTools inspection and for future migration-step wiring.

ðŸ“… Migration roadmap (for reference)

  • v0.25.15 (this release) â€” Phase 1. Scaffolding + proof-of-concept. Done.
  • Phase 2. Port drawBackground â†’ Phaser scene with the 5 BG images as textures + a Phaser particle emitter for the drift sparkles.
  • Phase 3. Port drawCuteCharacter and the player nameplate â†’ Phaser sprites. Retire _chibiApply / _chibiRestore.
  • Phase 4. Monsters, projectiles, particles. Biggest batching win lives here â€” boss arenas with 3+ elites currently pay the bulk of the fillrate tax.
  • Phase 5. HUD (HP/MP bars, level-up panel, inventory overlay).
  • Phase 6. Retire the vanilla canvas path; maple_game.html becomes a pure Phaser scene.

How to try it

Append ?phaser=1 to the game URL, or run localStorage.setItem('levelx_phaser', '1') in DevTools and reload. You should see a rotating yellow diamond + "PHASER ON" label in the top-left corner of the game canvas, overlaid on the usual vanilla render.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.15. Two new <script> blocks in <head> (flag eval + CDN loader). New #phaser-canvas parent div next to #game. New _bootstrapPhaser() IIFE + _PhaserTestScene class at the top of the main script (~70 lines). No changes to existing render paths.
  • No save/network/gameplay impact. Flag-off behaviour is identical to v0.25.14.

v0.25.14 Mobile perf pass + dreamier backgrounds

Two-in-one release: a targeted mobile frame-rate pass (DPR cap, halved particle budgets, skip ambient sparkles) and a full replacement of the five pixel-art backgrounds with a more tonally consistent pastel/dreamy set. Mobile fill-rate cost drops by roughly 4Ã—; desktop is unchanged aside from the new artwork.

ðŸ“± Mobile perf

  • Cap HiDPI at dpr = 1 on mobile. Single biggest win. Phones commonly report devicePixelRatio of 2-4; the v0.25.2 cap was Math.min(2, dpr), still 4Ã— the fill rate per frame on every Retina-class mobile. The coarse-pointer / narrow-viewport heuristic (same one _isMobileClient uses) now drops to 1. Desktop still caps at 2Ã—.
  • Halved particle / dmg-number / afterimage budgets on mobile. MAX_PARTICLES 500 â†’ 200, MAX_DMG_NUMBERS 40 â†’ 24, MAX_AFTERIMAGES 25 â†’ 12. 500-particle combo bursts were stuttering mobile GPUs during boss phase transitions. 200 still carries the visual read.
  • Skip the drift-sparkle ambient effect on mobile. The 14-18 sparkle-loop in drawBackground ran under globalCompositeOperation='lighter', which forces a full RMW per pixel â€” particularly expensive on mobile GPUs for pure decorative fidelity. Wrapped in !_IS_MOBILE_AT_LOAD.

ðŸ–¼ï¸ Background replacement

All five slots swapped for a new painterly pixel-art set that share a pastel/dreamy palette and big cumulus cloud compositions:

  • bg_forest.png â€” purple & green trees, pink cumulus, boulders, dark grass baseline.
  • bg_valley.png â€” stone arch bridge + flanking trees + distant misty mountain.
  • bg_meadow.png â€” teal tree on rocks + pink sunset clouds + flower-speckled grass.
  • bg_misty.png â€” huge rainbow autumn oak on stacked rocks + moody pastel palette.
  • bg_homestead.png â€” big oak + giant pink cumulus + red autumn tree (town default).

Selection logic unchanged â€” town still picks homestead, combat maps still cycle by map-ID hash across the other four, special backdrops (sanctum / zodiac / subway) still ignore the folder.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.14. HiDPI IIFE gets a mobile cap. _IS_MOBILE_AT_LOAD module-level flag feeds three budget constants and the sparkle-loop guard. No save/network/gameplay impact.
  • backgrounds/bg_forest.png, bg_valley.png, bg_meadow.png, bg_misty.png, bg_homestead.png â€” binary content replaced. Filenames and slot mapping unchanged.

v0.25.13 Five named background slots + map-hash cycle

Expanded the v0.25.12 background system from three slots to six (five named outdoor scenes plus the legacy main slot). Combat maps now deterministically cycle through the loaded outdoor backdrops by hashing their map ID, so each map gets its own visual identity without you having to hand-assign every one.

ðŸ–¼ï¸ New slots (drop into backgrounds/)

  • bg_forest.png â€” bright sunny forest framing distant mountains.
  • bg_valley.png â€” mountain valley with oak + snow peaks + dirt strata.
  • bg_meadow.png â€” meadow with blossom tree, rocks, big cumulus clouds. New.
  • bg_misty.png â€” foggy mountain forest with mossy ledges + distant eagle. New.
  • bg_homestead.png â€” farmhouse on a golden-grass hillside + sky clouds. New.

ðŸŽ¯ Smart selection

  1. Per-map override (highest priority) â€” any map in the MAPS object can set bg: 'forest' | 'valley' | 'meadow' | 'misty' | 'homestead' to pin a specific scene.
  2. Town default changed â€” isTown: true now defaults to homestead (farmhouse reads more like a village hub), falling back to valley.
  3. Combat-map cycle â€” non-town maps run their ID through a cheap string hash and pick one of the loaded outdoor slots. Same map always gets the same backdrop; different maps get different ones. Zero per-map config required.
  4. Unchanged â€” special backdrops (sanctum, zodiac, subway) still ignore this folder; procedural parallax still kicks in when no slot is loaded.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.13. BG_IMAGES map grows from 3 slots to 6. New _hashStr helper + _BG_CYCLE constant drive the combat-map variety selection. _pickBGImage's town branch switches to homestead-first, and the combat branch now picks by hash instead of a fixed slot.
  • backgrounds/README.md â€” full rewrite covering the five named slots, the selection rules, sizing guidance, and a worked example of pinning via mapData.bg.
  • No save/network/gameplay impact. All additive â€” behaviour with zero images present is unchanged from v0.25.11.

v0.25.12 Drop-in background images â€” forest & valley slots

Expanded the single-image BG_IMG loader from v0.24.1 into a three-slot system. Drop bg_forest.png and bg_valley.png into the new backgrounds/ folder and the game picks them up automatically on the next load â€” forest for outdoor combat maps, valley for the town hub. If a slot is empty, the procedural parallax from v0.25.11 takes over as the fallback, so the game never looks broken.

ðŸ–¼ï¸ Slots

  • backgrounds/bg_forest.png â€” all combat / field / outdoor maps.
  • backgrounds/bg_valley.png â€” town hub (Everdawn Village).
  • backgrounds/main_bg.png â€” legacy single-image slot (v0.24.1), ignored if either of the two above is loaded for the current map.
  • Special backdrops (sanctum cosmos, zodiac arenas, subway carriage) never use a bitmap â€” they keep their authored art.

ðŸ§© How selection works

  1. Map can set an explicit override via mapData.bg = 'forest' | 'valley' | 'main'. Takes priority if that slot is loaded.
  2. Default rule: isTown â†’ valley, everything else â†’ forest, fall back to main.
  3. If no slot is loaded, return null and fall through to the procedural faceted-mountain parallax.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.12. BG_IMG â†’ BG_IMAGES map with _loadBG(path) helper, plus _pickBGImage() resolver. drawBackground's image-path branch calls the resolver instead of checking the single image directly. BG_IMG kept as an alias pointing at BG_IMAGES.main for back-compat.
  • backgrounds/README.md (new) â€” explains the slot list, sizing guidance (960Ã—560 logical, 16:9-ish), draw order, and which maps use which slot.
  • No save-compat / network / gameplay impact. Fully additive â€” if you don't drop any images in, the game looks identical to v0.25.11.

v0.25.11 Art pass â€” faceted mountains, detailed pines, richer sky

Big visual upgrade on the background parallax. The smooth sine-wave mountain silhouettes got replaced with flat low-poly faceted peaks in the "origami mountain" style of modern indie chibi RPGs. Pines got three-tier detail with visible branch silhouettes and split lit/shadow faces. The sky gradient picked up two extra colour stops for painterly atmospheric depth. Matches the art reference shared in-thread.

ðŸ”ï¸ Faceted low-poly mountains (3 layers)

  • Farthest range. Furthest silhouette now renders as angular peaks instead of sine-wave hats. A darker shadow facet overlays the right half of every peak, so each mountain reads as a 3D wedge. Atmospheric perspective preserved (alpha 0.75, sky-blended colour).
  • Distant range. Same faceted rewrite, with the shadow tone mixing the lit colour against a cool indigo (#0a0820 at 35%) for a stronger volume read than the old flat fill.
  • Mid range. Sharpest, closest mountain layer â€” darkest shadow facet for max foreground pop. Snow/crag caps split into lit + shadow halves instead of floating isoceles triangles, so they match the range's cel-shading language.

ðŸŒ² Multi-tier pine silhouettes

Old treeline was two stacked triangles per tree â€” read as "green pyramid" from a distance. New pines have:

  • A thin brown trunk drawn first (drawn under the branch tufts).
  • Three branch tiers stacked up the stem: widest tier at the bottom, narrowest spire at the top â€” unambiguously conifer.
  • Each tier split into lit + shadow triangle halves using the same cel language as the mountains. 6 fills per tree Ã— 20 trees = 120 fills per frame, still well under budget.
  • Taller overall (52 + i%3 Ã— 18 vs. the old 44 + i%3 Ã— 14) so trees command more of the foreground silhouette.

ðŸŒ‡ Enriched sky gradient

Sky linear gradient went from 3 stops (top / 0.6 mid / horizon) to 5 stops with _mixHex-blended intermediates at 0.35 (upper-mid) and 0.88 (horizon glow). The 0.88 stop lifts the just-above-horizon band into a painterly warm glow that makes the mountain silhouettes sit against real atmosphere instead of a solid colour bar. Cache still refreshes only on palette change â€” same perf budget as before.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.11. Three mountain-range draw blocks rewritten in drawBackground (farthest, far, mid). Treeline draw block rewritten for multi-tier pines. Sky gradient cache expanded to 5 stops. No new helper functions â€” all changes live inside the existing drawBackground body. Special backdrops (sanctum cosmos, zodiac arenas, subway carriage) are untouched â€” they have their own art and were already at a higher visual fidelity.
  • No save/network/gameplay impact. Pure visuals.

v0.25.10 Zodiac cel-shading polish

A second pass on the shared _zBodyPath helper that renders all 12 zodiac bosses. v0.25.1 introduced the 2-tone cel-shade; this release sharpens the ink, deepens the shadow step, brightens the highlight, and adds a specular pip so bosses read as glossy cel-shaded characters rather than flat-painted cards.

ðŸŽ¨ Six tuning passes

  • Thicker outline, inkier black. Flat 4 px #120615 â†’ scaled max(3.5, r * 0.09) px #0a0310. Big bosses (Taurus, Leo, Aries) now get a proportionally thicker ink so they don't look wiry.
  • Inner ink contour. A second soft stroke INSIDE the silhouette in a fill Ã— 35% black tint, alpha 0.6. Simulates the "paint bleed" next to inked lines in real cel-shaded art â€” the outline reads as ink on paint instead of a cut-out edge.
  • Deeper second shade. The "deep shadow" cel step mixes the midtone with black at 0.55 instead of 0.45. The three-tier rail (lit / shade / deep) now has real volume instead of two slightly-different mids.
  • Brighter highlight. Upper-left highlight wedge mix 32% â†’ 42% white, alpha 0.9 â†’ 0.95. The lit side actually reads lit now.
  • Specular pip. A small near-white circle (radius scales with r) inside the highlight zone. Classic cel-shading trick for shiny surfaces â€” bosses now have that "living glass" glossiness.
  • Thicker cel-step rim lines. Boundary stroke 1.2 px flat â†’ max(1.6, r * 0.045). Small bosses keep a delicate 1.6 px rim, big bosses get 3-4 px so the shading transition reads as deliberate cel ink.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.10, rewrite of _zBodyPath's outline / shade / highlight passes. All 12 zodiac signs share this helper and inherit the improved look automatically. Per-sign draw functions (horns, pincers, etc.) untouched. No save-compat impact.

v0.25.9 Perf pass â€” cached gradients + measureText

Pure performance work, no visual change. Four per-frame hot spots identified from a render-path audit were caching-class wins â€” gradient rasterization and measureText calls that were being redone every frame despite producing identical results. Under HiDPI (v0.25.2's 2Ã— canvas scaling = 4Ã— fill rate), these adds up to measurable stutter, especially on boss arenas with multiple elites.

ðŸš€ What got faster

  • Vignette gradient cached once. The full-screen radial vignette at the end of drawBackground was rebuilding its gradient object every frame, even though W and H are constants for the life of the page. Now lazily built once into _vignetteGradCache and reused forever. Under HiDPI this gradient fills ~2.15 million pixels per frame â€” reallocating the gradient object that often was a real cost.
  • Carriage vignette cached too. Same fix for drawCarriageInterior's ambient vignette (cached in _carriageVignetteGradCache).
  • Elite + mini-boss auras â†’ off-screen canvas cache. Both aura types used to build a fresh createRadialGradient every frame for every on-screen elite/miniboss. New strategy: a Map-keyed off-screen canvas cache indexed by (width, height). First time any unique size appears the aura renders ONCE into an off-screen canvas; every subsequent frame just blits via drawImage. Pulse animation moves from varying the gradient radius to modulating globalAlpha (+/- 8-10%) â€” visually near-identical, hugely cheaper. On maps with 3+ elites on screen the framerate win is noticeable.
  • Nameplate measureText cached. The peer render loop called ctx.measureText(name) once per peer per frame â€” measureText forces font-metrics recalculation. Same bug on the local _drawPlayerNameTag. Now cached on the peer object (p._nameCache / p._nameTw) and a module-level _playerNameTagCache, invalidated only when the display string actually changes.

Not in this pass

The ambient flora loops (treeline + flowers in drawBackground) and the HiDPI toggle itself were flagged but not changed â€” they're either cheaper than they look (flora is just 52 small path/fills) or need a settings UI to expose properly. If lag persists on low-end devices, a HiDPI opt-out toggle is the next step.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.9. Four caches introduced (_vignetteGradCache, _carriageVignetteGradCache, _auraCache Map with _getEliteAuraCanvas / _getMiniBossAuraCanvas, and the peer/player nameplate width caches). No rendering output changed; every cache hands the draw call identical pixels to what the per-frame path produced before. No save-compat impact.

v0.25.8 Ballista Siege Volley â€” 8-shot barrage

The Archer â†’ Sniper â†’ Ballista master signature (G slot) now fires 8 piercing shots instead of 4. Vertical spread tightened so the whole burst fits cleanly inside the player's silhouette; cadence tightened so the barrage still resolves in under half a second. Feels like a wall-of-lead now rather than a slow sprinkler.

ðŸ›¡ï¸ Siege Volley (Ballista master)

  • Bullet count 4 â†’ 8 â€” double the payload.
  • Vertical spacing 10 â†’ 7 px â€” 8 Ã— 7 = 56 px tall, fits just outside the 44 px player envelope so the fan reads as one wide swath, not a scattered column.
  • Cadence 90 â†’ 70 ms â€” full skill resolves in ~490 ms, still a single burst.
  • Per-bullet stats unchanged â€” damage (ATK Ã— 3.5 + 25), range, pierce, defBreak all intact. Total skill damage â‰ˆ 2Ã— the old by design; this is a Lv 30 master-tier signature and should feel like one.
  • Tooltip updated to "8-shot piercing barrage; breaks defense." to match.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.8. Two edits: the ballista_volley skill implementation (loop count, y stride, scheduleSkillTimer cadence) and the MASTER_SKILLS.ballista_volley.desc tooltip string. No other skills touched.

v0.25.7 Balance pass â€” smoother climb, richer loot

Numbers-only rebalance across XP progression, drop rates, chest rewards, and the death penalty. The long L40â†’50 wall came down, every mob in the world can now theoretically drop a legendary, chests pay 25-50% more mesos, and dying costs 35% of your stash instead of 50%. No mechanics changed â€” this is purely constants being retuned.

ðŸ“ˆ Progression

  • XP curve softened at Lv 30+. Per-level multiplier 1.22 â†’ 1.20 at Lv 30-39 and 1.26 â†’ 1.22 at Lv 40-50. The old 1.26Ã— back-half acceleration compounded into a 2-hour wall between 40 and 50, especially painful across prestige runs. 1.22Ã— is still a clear ramp without feeling like a brick.
  • Global kill XP 1.25 â†’ 1.35. Applied at the kill site, so prestige and combo multipliers still stack cleanly on top. Combined with the curve change this takes about 8% off the L1â†’50 climb.

ðŸ’Ž Drops

  • Normal-mob drop rate 14% â†’ 18%. Bosses still 100%, elites still 50%. LCK attribute + Scavenger's Eye milestone still multiply on top.
  • Normals can now drop legendaries. Rarity pool went from {common:32, rare:10, epic:2, legendary:0} to {common:32, rare:10, epic:3, legendary:1}. At the new 18% drop rate that's ~0.4% legendary per kill â€” grind finally has a lottery-ticket feeling.
  • Elites juicier. {common:8, rare:14, epic:5, legendary:1} â†’ {common:6, rare:12, epic:6, legendary:2}. More epics, 2Ã— the legendary weight. Elites should feel like a prize fight â€” now they do.
  • Boss pool unchanged. Already drops legendaries ~25% of the time; doesn't need a buff.

ðŸ’° Chests & Death

  • Wood chests 40-120 â†’ 60-160 (+50% floor / +33% ceiling).
  • Silver chests 200-400 â†’ 260-500 (+30% floor / +25% ceiling).
  • Gold chests 600-1200 â†’ 800-1500 (+33% floor / +25% ceiling). Dawnshard legendaries at the shop cost 7000-8000; the old Gold range meant 7+ Gold chests per shop legendary, disproportionate to how rare Gold chests are.
  • Death meso penalty 50% â†’ 35%. Still stings enough to matter; no longer wipes over a third of your stash per death. XP penalty (0 / 3 / 5 / 8% by level bracket) is unchanged and remains the primary "don't die" deterrent.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.7. Eight surgical constant edits, each in its own commit for easy rollback: _maybeLevelUp curveMul, awardXp xpCurveMul, dropChance base, rollItemDrop rarityWeights (two tiers), openChest rewards table, death meso factor. No code paths moved â€” purely number tuning.
  • Save-compat: yes. Existing characters inherit the new rates on their next kill / chest / death.

v0.25.6 Codex of Shardfall â€” press 1 for the lore map

A new in-game lore reader. Press 1 to pause the action and open a tabbed parchment codex with the game's whole setting laid out in one place â€” the world's backstory, all twelve zodiac bosses, every class/job/master branch, and the legendary Dawnshard regalia. All data is pulled from the live CLASSES / JOBS / MASTERS / ZODIAC_SIGNS constants, so the codex auto-updates when new content lands â€” no stale copy.

ðŸ“– Four tabs
