  • The World. Prose intro to the Shardfall (the shattering of the sky twelve ages ago), Everdawn Village (the last lit town and its Bazaar economy), and the expedition's purpose (reclaiming the fallen shards one zodiac at a time).
  • The Twelve Zodiac. 3-column grid card-list of every zodiac boss â€” glyph, name, element, required level, and a one-line flavour blurb. Pulled from ZODIAC_SIGNS so it stays in sync automatically.
  • The Four Paths. Class â†’ Job â†’ Master tree for all four classes. Each class block shows its tagline and both job branches, with the two master-tier endings listed under each job (color-coded by job/master palette).
  • Relics & Legends. Dawnshard regalia cards (Blade / Aegis / Medallion), the Prestige mechanic, and lore on Lumens â€” the currency of Everdawn.

âŒ¨ï¸ Keybind

1 toggles the codex open / closed. Guarded against the existing 1+2+3 dev-console combo by requiring that 2 and 3 are NOT currently held when 1 fires, so holding all three still opens the dev console. Toggle pattern matches B / K / U.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.6. New DOM: #lore-modal with .lore-modal / .lore-tab / .lore-card / .lore-path CSS styles. New JS: openLoreMap, closeLoreMap, toggleLoreMap, _renderLoreTab, and the static LORE_WORLD_HTML block. Keydown listener gets the solo-1 toggle.
  • Fully additive â€” no save-format change, no network protocol change, no gameplay/combat impact. Game pauses while the codex is open.

v0.25.5 Press 0 lets you choose your job path

Small but meaningful dev-shortcut tweak. 0 used to silently auto-pick CLASSES[cls].jobs[0] and the first listed master after boosting to Lv 50 â€” great if you wanted "any build right now," awful if you actually wanted to test a specific path. It now opens the advancement picker so you choose.

What changed

  • 0 still boosts to Lv 50 â€” same HP / MP / ATK / DEF / skill-point catch-up as before, so the fully-kitted-out feel is preserved.
  • New: choice cards instead of silent auto-pick. After the boost, resetJobAndMaster clears any current job/master (cleanly un-applying their stats), then openAdvancement fires. The existing card.onclick chain flows you straight into master selection after you pick your job â€” so a single 0 press gets you both choices offered.
  • Toast updated to "Dev: Lv 50 â€” pick your job + master path" so the new behaviour is self-announcing.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.5, 0-key handler in the global keydown listener. Auto-pick branch (applyJob(jobs[0]), applyMaster(masters[0])) replaced with resetJobAndMaster() + openAdvancement(). No save-compat impact; existing characters who already advanced keep their picks unless they press 0 again.

v0.25.4 Cuter skies â€” cute sun, varied clouds, sparkles, flowers

A storybook polish pass on the background parallax. The celestial disc now has a personality (beaming sun by day, sleepy moon by night), every cloud gets one of three cute expressions, pastel sparkles and tiny hearts drift across bright skies, and 4-petal flower tufts sprinkle along the treeline. All additive â€” zero gameplay impact, pure scene-setting.

â˜€ï¸ What changed

  • Cute sun face. Bright-map celestial disc now has round eyes with sparkle highlights, pink blush circles, and a beaming smile. Lives inside a new _drawCelestialFace helper so the sun disc itself stays untouched.
  • Sleepy moon. Dark-map moon gets closed-eye arcs (^_^) and a tiny smile painted on the lit crescent, plus soft pink sleep-blush. Makes dungeons feel less lonely at night.
  • Cloud personalities. Previously only ~half of the clouds (even-sized ones) drew faces, and they all had the same smile. Each cloud now picks a stable face variant at buildBackdrop time â€” classic smile, closed-eye happy (^_^), or heart-eye grin with open-mouth excitement. Dark maps still leave clouds as silhouettes for atmosphere.
  • Treeline flowers. 32 tiny 4-petal blooms tucked between the pines at the treeline base. Bright maps pull from a pink / buttercup / lilac / daisy palette; dark maps use cooler moonlit blue / dusk lilac / mint. Parallax slightly slower than the trees so the flowers read as sitting behind the trunks.
  • Drifting sparkles + hearts. A slow sprinkle of twinkles and tiny hearts floating across the sky. Bright maps: pink / yellow / mint / lilac pastels with occasional heart accents. Dark maps: cooler snowflake-style blues and lavenders. Additive blend gives a soft glow, camera-parallax anchored so they drift with the world.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.4. New helpers: _drawCelestialFace, _drawCloudFace, _drawMiniHeart. buildBackdrop stashes a face variant per cloud. drawBackground draws flowers after the treeline and sparkles after the birds.
  • No save-compat impact, no gameplay change, no network protocol change. Pure visual polish. Skipped for sanctum / subway / zodiac arenas (they have custom backdrops already).

v0.25.3 Chat bubble lift Â· IGN always below character

Two small nameplate / chat polish passes. The speech bubble that pops above your hero after you send a chat line now sits clearly above the head with breathing room â€” previously the tail was kissing the hair. And the IGN label is now guaranteed to render below the character even if you never opened the character creator, with the class name used as a graceful fallback.

ðŸ’¬ What changed

  • Local chat bubble lifted. Anchor moved from player.y - 4 to player.y - 34. The bubble now hovers with ~30 px of clearance above the head instead of overlapping the hair.
  • Peer chat bubble matched. Remote players got the same lift â€” chat from sy - 24 to sy - 54, emote icon from sy - 32 to sy - 72 so the emote still floats above the speech bubble without stacking into it.
  • Peer name tag moved below the feet. Remote players used to show their name + Lv tag above their head; it's now anchored at sy + 58 (below body, matching the local hero's placement). Airspace above peers is now owned by HP bar â†’ chat â†’ emote, which is the classic MMO stack.
  • Local IGN fallback. If player.look.name is empty (fresh character, name input never touched), _drawPlayerNameTag now falls back to the title-cased class â€” "Warrior", "Mage", "Archer", etc. â€” so there's always a label below the character.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.3, drawClassIdentityHUD bubble anchor, peer render block (chat + emote + name tag coords), _drawPlayerNameTag fallback. Pure UI positioning; no gameplay, no save format, no network protocol impact.

v0.25.2 Procedural art restore Â· HiDPI canvas

Rollback of the experimental PNG sprite composite â€” the procedural Dragon-Quest-style hero (drawCuteCharacter) is back to being the canonical renderer. At the same time the canvas picked up a devicePixelRatio upgrade so the game renders crisp on Retina and other high-DPI displays without changing its on-screen size.

ðŸŽ¨ What changed

  • PNG sprite path disabled. drawLayeredCharacter() now returns false unconditionally, so drawPlayer always falls through to the procedural drawCuteCharacter. The original sprite composite is kept as a block comment for reference â€” not deleted, just dormant.
  • Boot-time sprite loader silenced. loadCharacterAssets() is commented out in the boot sequence, so the ASSETS map stays empty and no Sprites/character/ PNGs are fetched. Zero network work, zero decoding, zero flicker on first frame.
  • HiDPI canvas. On displays with devicePixelRatio > 1, the canvas's backing buffer is scaled up to W Ã— dpr by H Ã— dpr (capped at 2Ã— to keep fill-rate sane), while the CSS size stays locked at the logical 960 Ã— 560. A matching ctx.setTransform(dpr, 0, 0, dpr, 0, 0) means every existing drawing call still addresses the logical grid â€” no per-call changes, just sharper pixels.

Why revert the sprites

The PNG composite looked fine in isolation but didn't match the rest of the game's procedural, palette-driven visual identity. The DQ-style cute hero was always the art direction; the sprite experiment was a branch that didn't pan out. Keeping the composite code commented means a future art pass can re-enable it instantly without a diff archaeology session.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” version bump to v0.25.2, HiDPI IIFE added right after the ctx binding, drawLayeredCharacter stubbed with a return false + commented body, loadCharacterAssets() boot call commented.
  • No save-compat impact. No gameplay changes. Pure render / visual.

v0.25.1 Zodiac bosses â€” 2-shade cel-shade

Every zodiac boss now draws with proper two-tone cel-shading. The shared _zBodyPath helper that renders all 12 signs (Aries ram, Taurus bull, Gemini twins, Cancer crab, Leo sun, Virgo maiden, Libra scales, Scorpio, Sagittarius, Capricorn, Aquarius, Pisces) got four new clipped passes for richer silhouettes.

ðŸŽ¨ What changed in _zBodyPath

  1. Bold outline (unchanged) â€” 4 px round-joined #120615 stroke for silhouette pop.
  2. Base fill (unchanged) â€” fully lit tone.
  3. Midtone shadow (unchanged) â€” existing lower-right diagonal wedge.
  4. NEW â€” Deep shadow (the "second cel shade") â€” computed as _mixHex(shade, '#000000', 0.45), rendered as a lower-right triangular band that gives the body weight and grounds it.
  5. NEW â€” Highlight wedge â€” upper-left, _mixHex(fill, '#ffffff', 0.32) at 90% alpha. Gives a subtle sheen that makes the ram / bull / lion / etc read as 3D volume rather than flat.
  6. NEW â€” Cel step rims â€” thin 1.2 px deep-shade lines along the transition boundaries between the midtone and deep shadow. Crisps up the tonal step so the shade reads as intentional cel-shading rather than an accidental gradient smear.

Why 2-shade matters

Classic cel-shaded art (Dragon Quest, Zelda: Wind Waker, MapleStory itself) uses two discrete shade tones: a midtone and a deep shadow. One shade alone reads as slightly rounded; two tones create visible volume by showing the lit side, the turning edge, and the cast-shadow core. The zodiac bosses are big, slow, dramatic fights â€” they deserve the extra tonal depth.

Affected

  • All 12 zodiac bosses share _zBodyPath, so every sign picks up the new shading automatically.
  • Zodiac elite / mini-boss variants also benefit (same draw path).
  • Regular mob art is unaffected â€” this is a zodiac-only helper.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html â€” _zBodyPath(path, fill, shade, cx, cy, r) rewritten with five sequential clipped passes (outline, base, midtone wedge, deep shadow, highlight + step rims). Same call signature, so the 12 per-sign draw functions need no changes.
  • Save-compat: yes. Pure visual update.

v0.25.0 Backend: Accounts Â· Passwords Â· Sessions Â· Persistent saves

Server-side MMORPG foundations land. The server/ directory gets a full account system â€” HTTP register/login endpoints, scrypt password hashing, HMAC-signed stateless session tokens, per-account SQLite save persistence, WebSocket auth, and in-memory rate limiting. Dockerfile + Fly.io + Railway deploy configs are wired up. The game client still runs as a guest for now; the client-side login UI + save-sync ships next in v0.25.1.

ðŸ“š New: server/auth.js

  • SQLite schema â€” accounts (id, username, email, password_hash, password_salt, created_at, last_login_at, oauth_provider, oauth_subject) + saves (account_id, data, updated_at) with cascade delete. oauth_* columns wait for Google/Discord wiring without a schema change.
  • Password hashing via Node's stdlib crypto.scryptSync (N=16384, r=8, p=1, 64-byte output) with 16-byte random per-password salt. Verification uses crypto.timingSafeEqual to defeat timing attacks.
  • Session tokens are HMAC-SHA256 signed JSON â€” <base64url(payload)>.<base64url(sig)>. Stateless: no session table, no DB lookup to verify. Default 30-day TTL, configurable via SESSION_TTL_DAYS.
  • Input validation baked in â€” username 3-20 chars [a-zA-Z0-9_], password 8-200 chars, optional email with RFC-lite regex.
  • Per-account save blob â€” 1 MB cap, stored as JSON text in SQLite. getSave(id) / putSave(id, data) round-trip.
  • Uses Node 22+'s built-in node:sqlite â€” zero native compile, zero extra deps beyond ws.

ðŸŒ HTTP endpoints

MethodPathBodyResponse
GET/ Â· /healthâ€”{ ok, version, rooms, totalPlayers, accounts, saves }
POST/api/register{ username, password, email? }201 { token, user } or 400/409
POST/api/login{ username, password }200 { token, user } or 401
POST/api/logoutâ€”{ ok } (advisory)
GET/api/meBearer token{ user } or 401
GET/api/saveBearer token{ data, updatedAt } or 401
PUT/api/saveJSON save + Bearer{ ok, updatedAt }
DELETE/api/accountBearer token{ ok }
ALL/api/oauth/:providerâ€”501 (stub)

ðŸ” WebSocket auth (optional, guest mode still works)

  • New {t:'auth', token} message promotes a guest socket to an authenticated account. Server replies with {t:'auth_ok', user} or {t:'error', code:'bad_token'}.
  • New {t:'save', data} WS message persists a save blob server-side. Requires a prior successful auth; otherwise replies {t:'error', code:'unauthed'}.
  • welcome now includes an authed boolean so peers can show an account badge if/when we add that.
  • All existing guest behaviour (hello / state / chat / emote / map) unchanged â€” no breaking change for v0.24.x clients.

ðŸ›¡ï¸ Safety + rate limiting

  • In-memory per-IP rate limiter: 5 registers/min, 10 logins/min. 429 with a friendly message past the limit.
  • CORS is fully open (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *) â€” tighten to your game's origin when deploying publicly.
  • Uniform 401 Invalid credentials on login failure (no username probe surface).
  • SECRET env var becomes mandatory in production â€” server refuses to boot without it when NODE_ENV=production.
  • Graceful shutdown: SIGTERM/SIGINT closes the SQLite handle before exit.
  • Idle socket sweeper (60s) unchanged.

ðŸš¢ Deploy-ready

  • Dockerfile â€” Node 22-alpine, 58 MB final image, VOLUME /app/data for SQLite persistence.
  • fly.toml.example â€” one-shot Fly.io config with volume mount, auto-scale-to-zero, /health probe, secrets workflow.
  • railway.json.example â€” Railway equivalent with Dockerfile build + healthcheck.
  • .env.example â€” documents every env var with production guidance.

âœ… Smoke-tested end-to-end

Before shipping I ran the full register â†’ login â†’ /api/me â†’ PUT /api/save â†’ GET /api/save â†’ wrong password â†’ duplicate register â†’ OAuth stub â†’ validation â†’ unauth â†’ self-delete loop, plus a WebSocket auth/hello/save round-trip, plus rate-limit overflow. All 14 paths responded correctly:

REGISTER 201 OK id=1
LOGIN    200 OK
ME       200 hero123
PUT SAVE 200 OK
GET SAVE 200 {"level":42,"inventory":["sword","potion"],"mesos":5000}
BADLOG   401 Invalid credentials
DUPREG   409 Username or email already registered
OAUTH    501 OAuth not yet implemented
VALIDAT  400 Username too short (min 3)
NOAUTH   401 Invalid or expired token
DELETE   200 true
WS auth_ok  â†’ authed account
WS welcome  â†’ authed: true
WS save_ok  â†’ persisted
RATE 6/7   â†’ 429 Too many registration attempts

ðŸ”œ What's NOT in this ship (coming in v0.25.1)

  • Client-side login UI. Register/Login form in the Multiplayer modal, token storage in localStorage, auth-on-WS-connect wiring.
  • Server-side save UI. Manual save button + auto-sync hooks.
  • OAuth flow. Endpoint is a 501 stub; schema is ready.
  • Email verification / password reset. Needs an SMTP provider.
  • Authoritative combat. Still client-side; per-client mob instances. The big milestone.

Touched surface

  • New files: server/auth.js (390 LOC), server/fly.toml.example, server/railway.json.example, server/.env.example.
  • Rewritten: server/server.js (HTTP routing + WS auth + rate limiting), server/package.json (ws only, Node 22+), server/Dockerfile (Node 22-alpine, single stage), server/README.md (full API + deploy guide).
  • maple_game.html â€” version bump only. Client still runs as guest.
  • Save-compat: yes. No client save format change.

v0.24.3 Typable chat system Â· 60-char bubbles Â· Works offline

Chat is now a real in-world feature. Press Enter any time to type a message â€” solo or multiplayer â€” and it floats as a speech bubble above your character for 4 seconds. Bubbles hard-cap at 60 characters each, enforced at the input level and on send.

ðŸ’¬ Chat input

  • Press Enter while playing to open the chat input (bottom-left). Previously only worked while connected to a multiplayer server.
  • Press Enter again (or click outside) to send. Press Esc to cancel without sending.
  • Hard cap at 60 chars per bubble â€” enforced three ways:
    • <input maxlength="60"> stops you typing past 60.
    • _mpSendChat() .slice(0, 60) on send defensively.
    • Server trims to its own 200-char max, but clients never send more than 60 anyway.
  • Control chars (tabs, newlines) are stripped. Blank messages are ignored.

ðŸ’­ Local chat bubble

  • Always fires â€” whether or not you're connected. The bubble renders above your character using the same _drawBubble() helper as mobs / NPCs / peers, so the look is consistent.
  • 4 s lifetime, fades out over the last 500 ms.
  • Messages also land in the chat log (bottom-left), tagged as me so your lines are colour-highlighted.
  • New player._chat field initialized on the player literal. Decayed in drawClassIdentityHUD each frame.

ðŸŒ Multiplayer still works

  • When connected, the same Enter flow broadcasts to the room via the existing WebSocket chat message.
  • Server-echoed own messages are now suppressed to avoid a duplicate log entry â€” the local bubble + log already showed it when you sent.
  • Peer chat bubbles unchanged (still drawn above peer ghosts by _mpDrawPeers).

ðŸ“¢ Surface

  • Bottom controls strip now lists Enter Chat alongside the other toggles.
  • Multiplayer modal hint mentions the 60-char cap and that chat works offline.
  • Input placeholder updated: "Say somethingâ€¦ (max 60 chars Â· Enter send, Esc cancel)".

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html: #mp-chat-input maxlength 200 â†’ 60 + new placeholder; player literal gains _chat; Enter gate changed from net.connected to player.cls && player.hp > 0; _mpSendChat() rewrites to cap 60 + always set player._chat + always log + conditionally send over WS; _mpHandle('chat') suppresses own echo; drawClassIdentityHUD adds the local chat-bubble draw + decay next to the emote block; Multiplayer modal + bottom controls copy updated.
  • Save-compat: yes. No schema changes. _chat is runtime-only.

v0.24.2 Dev cheat Â· Gate levelup Â· Multishot cap Â· U-panel Skills tab Â· F no portal

Quality-of-life patch: press 8 for a dev XP burst, level-ups throttle to one at a time, the Double Shot boon caps at +1, unequipped boons shrink into compact chips, the Skills Reference merges into the U panel with per-slot growth charts, the I key retires, and F stops triggering portals (â†‘ still does).

âœ¨ Press 8 â€” dev XP cheat

  • Grants ~40% of next-level XP per press. A few presses crosses a level; combined with the new level-up gate below, this stays grindy even with the cheat.
  • Retired the ðŸ’¤ sleep emote to free the 8 key. Emotes are now 4 ðŸ˜Š / 5 ðŸ˜² / 6 ðŸ˜‚ / 7 ðŸ˜¢.

ðŸš¦ One level per trigger

  • Level-up loop changed from while to if â€” one level per kill / trigger max.
  • Any overflow XP beyond the next level is clamped to 95% of the new expToNext, so huge XP drops don't chain multiple levels but still leave you close enough to trigger again next kill.
  • Level-up logic extracted into _maybeLevelUp() so the press-8 cheat, kill rewards, and future XP sources share the same animation + advancement check path.

ðŸŽ¯ Double Shot capped at +1

  • Boon template max dropped from 2 to 1 â€” each rolled instance grants exactly +1 projectile.
  • Even if multiple Double Shot boons are equipped, _applyEquippedBoons() hard-clamps player.mods.multishot to 1. No more +3 projectile stacks.

â—ˆ Boon Loadout â€” compact unequipped chips

  • Equipped boons keep the full card with rich violet border + box shadow + full action row.
  • Unequipped boons collapse to a single-line chip: icon + name + roll + ðŸŽ² reroll + âœ• discard + Equip. Much denser â€” the whole 25-boon bag fits in the same scroll area.
  • Fewer pixels per boon, more room for the grid.

ðŸ“– Skills Reference â†’ U panel Skills tab

  • Added a third tab in the U panel: ðŸ“ˆ Level Up Â· â—ˆ Boon Loadout Â· ðŸ“– Skills.
  • Shows your current class's full binding table (Z/A/X/S/C/D/F/V/G).
  • New: per-slot growth chart. Each row gets a horizontal Lv 1 â†’ 50 track showing:
    • Gold â–¼ marker at the slot's unlock level
    • Green "you" marker at the player's current level
    • Green shaded band from unlock onward (available range)
    • Tick labels: Lv 1 Â· Lv 25 Â· Lv 50
  • Locked slots (no job / no master yet) grey out with an explanation of what's needed.
  • Old standalone Skills modal path removed; openSkillsReference() now just opens U at the Skills tab for back-compat with any external caller.

âŒ¨ I retired

  • The I key no longer has a binding â€” the Skills Reference moved into the U panel (same content, better layout, shared tab navigation).
  • Help panel + README updated.

ðŸšª F no longer enters portals

  • tryInteract() drops the portal fallback. F now only opens chests / picks up drops / casts the job-signature skill. Use â†‘ to enter portals.
  • Stops the "I pressed F next to an NPC and got warped somewhere" problem.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html: emote map removes 8 â†’ sleep; new 8 branch grants XP burst; _maybeLevelUp() extracted and gated to one level per call with overflow clamp; POWERUPS.multi max 2â†’1; _applyEquippedBoons multishot clamp; renderBoonPanel splits equipped / unequipped rendering; renderSkillsReference(host) renders inline + adds per-slot Lv 1â†’50 growth chart; U panel gains Skills tab + tri-pane swap; I-key branch removed; help panel + bottom-bar references updated; tryInteract drops portal fallback.
  • README.md: controls table simplified to merge K/U Character panel.
  • Save-compat: yes. Old characters with multishot > 1 from past saves get clamped to 1 on next _applyEquippedBoons.

v0.24.1 MMORPG balance Â· Mastery Â· Cel-shaded UI Â· Map depth

Retunes the game for MMO rhythm (tiered XP, death penalty, tighter potions, quadratic rerolls), adds a per-mob Mastery grind, a cel-shaded HP/MP/EXP bar refresh, a reorganised Level-Up panel, moves the player nameplate below the character, and drops a new furthest-mountain layer + god rays + cinematic vignette into the map backdrop for cleaner parallax depth.

Retunes the game for a long-haul MMORPG rhythm â€” steeper endgame XP curve, real death penalty at higher levels, tighter potion economy, quadratic boon rerolls, and a new per-mob mastery grind that rewards farming specific foes. On the UI side, HP/MP/EXP bars get a cel-shaded glow-up and the Level Up panel gets a full layout refresh.

ðŸ“ˆ Endgame XP curve (tiered)

  • Lv 1-29: Ã—1.18 per level (unchanged â€” same onboarding rhythm).
  • Lv 30-39: Ã—1.22 per level (noticeably longer).
  • Lv 40-49: Ã—1.26 per level (real endgame wall).
  • Net effect: Lv 40 â†’ 50 now takes roughly as much XP as Lv 1 â†’ 39 combined. Classic MMO "last ten levels are the grind" feel.

ðŸ’€ Death XP penalty (Lv 20+)

  • Lv 1-19: no XP loss. Early game stays forgiving.
  • Lv 20-29: 3% of current-bar XP lost on death.
  • Lv 30-39: 5%.
  • Lv 40+: 8%.
  • Capped at the current bar so you never drop below the level. Shown on the death overlay alongside the existing 50% Lumen loss.

ðŸ’° Economy tightening

  • Potion prices +40% â€” healing is now a Lumen decision, not a freebie.
    • Red Potion: 60 â†’ 85 Â· Orange Potion: 180 â†’ 250 Â· Pure Water: 500 â†’ 700
    • Blue Potion: 80 â†’ 115 Â· Mana Elixir: 220 â†’ 310 Â· Elixir (full): 900 â†’ 1250
  • Boon rerolls are now quadratic â€” 500 + rerollsÂ² Ã— 200. 1st costs 500, 2nd 700, 3rd 1300, 4th 2300, 5th 3700, 6th 5500. Chasing a god-roll is a meaningful sink.

â­ Mastery â€” per-monster farming grind

  • Every kill of a non-boss mob increments player.mastery[type]. Thresholds grant permanent damage bonuses vs that mob type:
    • 100 kills â†’ +1% damage Â· â˜…
    • 500 kills â†’ +2% damage Â· â˜…â˜…
    • 1000 kills â†’ +3% damage Â· â˜…â˜…â˜…
  • Milestones toast with the mob's name when you cross a threshold.
  • Mastery is applied in hitMonster â€” stacks cleanly on top of boons, buffs, crits, and class-identity multipliers.
  • Saved per character. Builds up over every run, creating long-term "I'm better at fighting Pincers than Rotters" investment.

ðŸ©¸ Cel-shaded HP / MP / EXP bars

  • Bars got a chunkier silhouette â€” crisp black outline, inner highlight band on the top 30%, shadow band on the bottom 35%.
  • Fill gradient now uses a stepped two-tone cel shade instead of a soft linear blend. Reads as a toon element, not a window UI.
  • Label text gets an 8-direction outline stroke for contrast on any background.

ðŸ“Š Level-up panel refresh

  • Big glowing SKILL POINTS pill at the top of the Level Up tab, counter in oversized gold.
  • Each lane card now shows an icon (â¤/ðŸ’§/âš”/ðŸ›¡/âœ¦/âš¡) in the lane's signature colour, clean progress track, and a per-state footer (+ Invest / MAXED / Not enough SP).
  • Hover states â€” affordable lanes lift + glow in their own colour. Maxed lanes turn green. Locked lanes grey out.
  • Same radar chart + stat summary + Boon Loadout tab underneath; just the lane grid got re-styled.

ðŸ“› Player nameplate below the character

  • The MMO-style floating name now sits below the player's feet instead of above the head. Follows the sprite through jumps / movement.
  • The Lv N suffix is gone from the nameplate â€” level already lives in the top-left HUD, no need to duplicate it in world space.
  • If no name is set, the nameplate doesn't draw at all (used to always show at least the level).

ðŸ” Map background â€” deeper parallax

  • New furthest-mountain layer behind everything: muted, barely parallaxes (0.015Ã— camera), paints atmospheric depth on every outdoor map.
  • God rays on bright maps â€” soft diagonal sunbeams streaming from the celestial disc area. Additive, subtle, gone on moonlit maps.
  • Cinematic vignette â€” radial darkening toward the corners on combat maps. Focuses the eye on the action and makes the four layers of parallax read cleanly. Disabled in town so the village stays cozy.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html: level-up loop tiered curveMul; triggerDeath XP penalty + overlay copy; POTION_ITEMS price +40%; boonRerollCost quadratic; player.mastery field + killMonster tracking + hitMonster bonus + PLAYER_SAVE_FIELDS adds mastery; .bar / .bar-fill cel-shade CSS; .lu-card CSS + renderLevelUp rewrite with icon + colour per lane; _drawPlayerNameTag now renders below the sprite without Lv suffix; drawBackground adds furthest mountain + god-ray + vignette passes.
  • Save-compat: yes. Missing mastery on old saves defaults to {}.

v0.24.0 Multiplayer â€” real WebSocket server

First step toward a real MMORPG. A Node.js WebSocket server lives in server/; the client gets a ðŸŒ Multi button, presence tick, ghost-peer rendering, emote relay, and in-world chat. Everyone on the same room name sees each other run around and can chat. Combat / mobs stay per-client for this milestone â€” that's authoritative-lite, not full MMO.

server/ â€” Node.js WebSocket relay

  • Single-process server.js using ws. HTTP wrapper so /health returns version + room/player counts for load balancers.
  • Room-based â€” any unique room id, default lobby. 16 players per room hard cap, 60 s idle kick, 40 ms per-player state-tick floor, 200-char chat cap, control chars stripped.
  • Protocol: hello / state / chat / map / emote (client â†’ server); welcome / joined / left / state / chat / emote / error (server â†’ client). All JSON. Full table in server/README.md.
  • No database, no auth, no persistence â€” rooms die when the last player leaves.
  • Deployment: Dockerfile included, Fly.io / Railway / VPS instructions in server/README.md. Fly's free tier covers a single small instance.

Client â€” net module

  • ðŸŒ Multi button top-right of the HUD opens the multiplayer modal. Fields: server URL, your name, room id. All three persist in localStorage.
  • Presence tick â€” _mpTick() runs after updatePlayer and sends a state frame every 70 ms (~14 Hz): x/y/vx/vy, facing, map, hp/mp, level, cls/job/master, anim.
  • Peer rendering â€” _mpDrawPeers() draws each peer in the current map as a translucent violet silhouette with name + level + HP bar above their head. Chat + emote bubbles float above them.
  • Emote relay â€” pressing 4-8 emotes to everyone in the room.
  • Chat â€” press Enter while connected to open a bottom-left input. Enter sends, Esc cancels. Messages show as speech bubbles above the sender for 4 s and in a rolling log of the last 30 lines.

What it IS

  • Presence + chat across the room â€” friends running around in the same map.
  • Per-map scoping â€” peers on different maps are hidden.
  • Full stat broadcast â€” HP, MP, level, class, advancement all mirror to peers.
  • Free to run locally or on any tiny VPS / Fly.io / Railway instance.

What it is NOT (yet)

  • Not authoritative combat. Every player instances their own mobs.
  • No shared loot, no trade, no party XP, no PvP.
  • No accounts / persistence. Reconnect = new id.
  • No anti-cheat â€” server trusts client state. Fine for co-op, not competitive. Server-side combat is the next phase.

Run it

cd server
npm install
npm start
# [levelx-server] listening on port 8080

Click ðŸŒ Multi in the game, enter ws://localhost:8080, pick a name + room, hit Connect. Share the URL + room with a friend.

Smoke test

  • Verified: client connects, receives welcome with id, second client joins, state frames round-trip, chat echoes to sender, joined/left notifications flow. Console clean of errors.

Touched surface

  • New files: server/server.js, server/package.json, server/Dockerfile, server/README.md, server/.gitignore.
  • maple_game.html: multiplayer modal + button + chat input/log markup + CSS; net module (connect/disconnect/tick/handle/send/chat/emote/draw); closeAllModals includes multiplayer-modal; chat input wired on DOMContentLoaded; _mpTick called in main loop; _mpDrawPeers called in render pass; Enter opens chat when connected; emote keys relay to peers.
  • Save-compat: yes. Multiplayer is opt-in; offline play is unchanged.

v0.23.1 Sniper X/F swap Â· Piano BGM Â· Skill-gate tune Â· Rogue nerf Â· `-` toggles advance

Combined follow-up patch covering five small adjustments: the Sniper Xâ†’F skill swap, piano-classical BGM, skill-unlock levels dropped by 1, rogue Momentum halved, and a new - key to toggle the job-selection page.

ðŸ¹ Sniper X / F swap

  • Sniper X (slot s): reverts to MultiShot (the archer default). The old Railshot on X was too punishing at Lv 5 â€” instant piercing line with escalating damage dwarfed every other class's early game.
  • Sniper F (slot q): now Railshot. Same effect, costs climb to signature tier. MP 16 â†’ 28, CD 1300 ms â†’ 4200 ms.
  • Strafe Volley retired (SKILL_FN kept as dead code; no skill references it).

ðŸŽ¹ Piano-classical BGM

  • Six tracks rewritten as soft chord arpeggios in minor / major keys. Tempos 420-520 ms/beat.
  • All leads + basses switched to sine waveforms for a piano-adjacent fundamental.
  • Each note now layers a quiet (~22%) octave overtone for a warm "bell" body that pushes sine closer to a real piano partial.
  • Envelope changed to piano mode: 20 ms attack, slow decay to 38% of peak across the note body, soft release past the end â€” legato / sustain-pedal feel.
  • Track volumes trimmed ~15% so the music sits behind SFX.

ðŸ“‰ Skill unlock levels â€” all âˆ’1

Each skill-slot gate moved down one level so unlocks land on clean multiples of 5.

  • X 6 â†’ 5, S 11 â†’ 10, C 16 â†’ 15, D 21 â†’ 20, F 26 â†’ 25 (aligned with 1st advancement), V 31 â†’ 30, G 50 â†’ 49.
  • Job advancement stays at Lv 25, master at Lv 50 â€” unchanged. The slot-level changes just bring the availability in at the "0-of-5" boundary instead of "1-of-5".

ðŸ—¡ Rogue Momentum halved

  • Momentum's damage-bonus cap dropped from +100% â†’ +50% (formula: 1 + m/200 instead of 1 + m/100).
  • Build rate / decay rate unchanged; only the output multiplier is halved.
  • Rogue base crit (25%) + crit damage + mobility still make them a glass-cannon class; this just prevents the 2Ã— Momentum Ã— full-crit combos from one-shotting zodiac bosses.
  • Class-select identity tooltip updated to reflect the new cap.

âŒ¨ï¸ - toggles the job / master selection page

  • Press - any time (with a class picked) to open the advancement modal. Press again to close.
  • If you don't have a job yet, this presents the 1st-advancement picker. If you already have a job + master, it lets you re-pick both so commitments aren't permanent.
  • Separate from 0, which remains the Lv-50 dev max-out cheat.

ðŸ—ï¸ N also opens treasure chests

  • N is now a context-aware interact key: chest â†’ NPC â†’ Codex.
    • If you're standing on a treasure chest, N opens it.
    • Otherwise, if an NPC is in range, N starts their dialog.
    • Otherwise, N opens the Codex.
  • The in-world chest prompt now reads [N] Open.
  • F still works for chests via the legacy tryInteract path, so muscle memory holds.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html: SLOT_LEVEL_REQ values âˆ’1 across the board; SKILLS.deadeye removed + SKILLS.snipe_railgun moved to slot q with MP/CD bumped; MUSIC_TRACKS rewritten with piano flag + sine waveforms; music._tone piano-mode envelope + octave overtone; _computeClassSkillMul rogue branch changed to 1 + m/200; CLASSES.rogue.identity.desc updated; keydown handler adds - branch for advancement toggle.
  • Save-compat: yes.

v0.23.0 Skill cadence Â· Epic class-select Â· Balance pass

Two small follow-ups. The Sniper's X goes back to the archer default (MultiShot) because Railshot was too strong for an early-cadence slot; Railshot is promoted to the F job-signature slot, displacing Strafe Volley. Separately, the map BGM gets a soothing piano-classical rework â€” all tracks use warm sine fundamentals with a quiet octave overtone, slower tempos, and longer legato envelopes.

ðŸ¹ Sniper X / F swap

  • Sniper X (slot s): reverts to MultiShot (the archer default). The old Railshot on X was too punishing â€” instant piercing line with escalating damage at Lv 6 dwarfed every other class's early game.
  • Sniper F (slot q): now Railshot â€” same effect, but the cost / cooldown climb to the job-signature tier. MP 16 â†’ 28, CD 1300 ms â†’ 4200 ms. Still powerful, but budgeted like Shadow Strike / War Cry / Smoke Bomb.
  • Strafe Volley is retired (the SKILL_FN stays in the file as dead code; no skill definition points to it any more).
  • Net effect: Sniper's early game plays closer to the base archer feel, then Lv 26 unlocks the screen-clearing Railshot as their defining signature.

ðŸŽ¹ Piano-classical BGM rework

  • All six tracks rewritten as gentle chord arpeggios in minor / major keys:
    • Town â€” C-major pastoral lullaby at 480 ms/beat
    • Forest â€” A-minor â†’ C-major overworld at 440 ms/beat
    • Mushroom â€” D-minor contemplative at 520 ms/beat
    • Boss â€” C-minor dramatic but calm at 420 ms/beat
    • Zodiac â€” A-minor â†’ C-major cosmic at 500 ms/beat
    • Tower â€” G-major ascending reverent at 460 ms/beat
  • Sine waveforms everywhere for lead + bass â€” the piano fundamental. Square / sawtooth / triangle retired from music (still used for SFX).
  • Quiet octave overtone layered on every piano note at ~22% amplitude â€” gives the sine a warm "bell" body closer to a real piano partial.
  • Longer envelope â€” 20 ms attack, slow decay to 38% of peak over the note body, soft release past the note end. Creates the legato, sustained feel of a pianist holding the sustain pedal.
  • Tracks opt in via piano: true on the track object. SFX unaffected â€” only music._tone() checks the flag.
  • Volumes trimmed ~10-20% across the board so the music sits behind sound effects without competing.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html: SKILLS.deadeye removed, SKILLS.snipe_railgun slot moved from s to q with MP/CD bumped; MUSIC_TRACKS fully rewritten with piano: true + sine waveforms + chord-arpeggio phrasing; music._tone gained a piano-mode envelope + octave overtone.
  • Save-compat: yes. Sniper characters that had Railshot bound to X will find it on F after load; MultiShot takes X automatically via skillBySlot's class fallback.

v0.23.0 Skill cadence Â· Epic class-select Â· Balance pass

Major progression + balance rework. Skills unlock every 5 levels instead of all at once. Job advancement moves to Lv 25, master to Lv 50. The class-select page is fully reskinned with animated cosmos rays, per-class glow, and dramatic taglines. Mage basic spells get proper recast cadence, rogue F goes purple-and-black, and every skill takes a global 20% haircut for balance.

ðŸ“œ Skill cadence â€” one slot every 5 levels

  • New constant SLOT_LEVEL_REQ:
    • A Block/Parry â€” Lv 1 (always)
    • Z basic â€” Lv 1
    • X skill 2 â€” Lv 6
    • S skill 3 â€” Lv 11
    • C skill 4 â€” Lv 16
    • D skill 5 â€” Lv 21
    • F job signature â€” Lv 26 (also requires job advancement)
    • V job ultimate â€” Lv 31 (also requires job)
    • G master signature â€” Lv 50 (also requires master)
  • Advancement levels: 1st job â†’ Lv 25 (was 10). Master â†’ Lv 50 (was 20).
  • isReady(id) now checks the slot's level requirement. The skill bar greys out gated slots and shows "Lv N" in the MP line until unlocked.
  • The Warrior charge-hold, archer hold-to-draw, and the poll-cast loop all pass through isReady so level-gated slots don't fire.
  • 0 dev key now snaps you to Lv 50, auto-advances to the first job + first master, and grants the missed HP/MP/ATK/DEF and 49 SP. Use for quick testing.

ðŸ“ˆ XP curve + rewards

  • XP growth softened from Ã—1.30 per level to Ã—1.18 per level. Reaching Lv 50 is now realistic under the 5-level cadence.
  • Monster XP rewards globally +25% to keep the climb from feeling longer than the old Lv 1 â†’ 30 experience.
  • Prestige offer bumped from Lv 50 â†’ Lv 60 since 50 is now the master gate.

âš” Epic class-select page

  • Backdrop: radial purple gradient with a slowly spinning conic-gradient ray field (cs-rays) and twinkling star-dust layer (cs-stars).
  • Title: "âš” THE SHARDFALL EXPEDITION âš”" subline in letter-spaced violet, plus a pulsing gold "Choose Your Hero" headline that breathes every 2.4 s.
  • Per-class colour variables (--cls-color, --cls-glow, --cls-shadow) drive a radial-gradient wash that fades in on hover + a hover scale-lift + class-coloured box-shadow.
  • Dramatic taglines under each class name:
    • Warrior â€” "Strike first. Strike hard. Bear the burden."
    • Rogue â€” "The shadow falls before the blade."
    • Mage â€” "Speak the word. Reshape the world."
    • Archer â€” "A breath. A heartbeat. An arrow."
  • Advancement levels surfaced in the header copy ("First Advancement at Lv 25, Mastery at Lv 50") so players know the new pacing up front.
  • Modal grows up to max-height: 94vh with inner scroll to accommodate the longer content on smaller viewports.

â—ˆ U-key panel: tabbed layout

The combined Level Up + Boon Loadout panel was too tall to fit on a typical viewport. Now split into two tabs inside the same modal:

  • ðŸ“ˆ Level Up â€” SP radar + lane grid + current-stats summary. Radar dropped 280 â†’ 220 px, lane grid is now 2-column, stats inline.
  • â—ˆ Boon Loadout â€” equipped slot row + inventory grid + reroll/discard controls. Same UI as before, now on its own tab.
  • Tab state (game._uTab) persists across opens.
  • Modal body has max-height: 88vh + scroll fallback so even monster-sized inventories fit.

ðŸ”® Mage base CDs

  • Z Magic Bolt â€” 210 ms â†’ 300 ms
  • X Fireball â€” 800 ms â†’ 1 000 ms
  • S Ice Spike â€” 650 ms â†’ 2 500 ms
  • Cleaner cadence distinction between the three basics â€” bolt spam, fireball punch, ice spike as a deliberate cast.

ðŸ’œ Rogue F recoloured (purple + black)

  • Shadow Strike (ninja F): pink/magenta particles swapped to #1a0230 deep black, #7722aa royal violet, and #b866ff amethyst.
  • Smoke Bomb (assassin F): cloud recoloured from grey to #0c0118 / #5a1a8a / #9a66cc. Plays as an obsidian smoke rather than dust.
  • Matches the rogue class fantasy â€” proper assassin palette.

âš¡ Nightreaper G â€” Blink Barrage (rework)

The old Ricochet Daggers was serviceable but not memorable. The new master signature is the signature zip-zap.

  • Scan all visible mobs, mark up to 14. Rogue dissolves, then blink-strikes each mark at 65 ms cadence â€” blindingly fast.
  • Each strike ignores DEF, always crits, deals ATK Ã— 2.2 Ã— critDmg, and leaves a 10-point violet afterimage trail showing the path between strikes.
  • Alternates flanks (left / right) of each target for readable rhythm.
  • 2.4 s invulnerability blanket across the entire barrage â€” no interrupt damage.
  • Finale: a twin-crossed slash (violet + deep purple) + 240 px shadow nova at the landing point, 650 ms stun on everything caught, +18% max HP heal on exit.
  • MP 25 â†’ 55, CD 6.5 s â†’ 15 s. Fits the master-ultimate cost bracket (Voidrift / Divine Aegis / Gale Storm).
  • Whiffs cleanly with partial MP refund if nothing's on screen.

ðŸ’¢ Global damage balance knob

Added a single tuning constant in hitMonster so the whole roster can be rebalanced at once.

  • GLOBAL_SKILL_DMG_MUL = 0.80 â€” blanket 20% nerf on all non-basic skill damage.
  • GLOBAL_ULT_DMG_MUL = 0.75 â€” additional 25% trim on master ults (they stack with the skill nerf, netting 60% of the old damage).
  • ULT_SKILL_TAGS set lists which skill tags are considered ultimates (earthbreaker, deathBlossom, phantom_nova, sage_meteor, arrowRain, etc).
  • Basic attacks (melee / arrow / dagger / bolt) keep full damage â€” their tempo is already balanced by the new cooldown cadence.
  • Thorns / burn DoT also pass through unmodified.
  • Monsters got a +25% HP bump in v0.20 + new slower class-identity gating; the 20%/25% nerfs are calibrated so skill-to-basic damage ratios stay roughly where they were, just with less one-shot-ultimate drama.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html: new SLOT_LEVEL_REQ / JOB_ADVANCE_LEVEL / MASTER_ADVANCE_LEVEL / GLOBAL_SKILL_DMG_MUL / GLOBAL_ULT_DMG_MUL / ULT_SKILL_TAGS constants; isReady() + slotLevelReq(); hitMonster() nerf block; level-up loop bumps to 25/50 + new 1.18Ã— curve; mob-kill XP gets +25% multiplier; 0 keydown branch rewritten as Lv-50 cheat; openLevelUpPanel() split into tabs; CLASSES gets tagline field on each class; renderClassCards() wires per-class CSS vars; SKILLS.magicBolt / fireball / iceSpike cooldowns; SKILLS.nightreaper_mark rename + stat tweak; SKILL_FNS.shadowStrike + smokeBomb recolours; SKILL_FNS.nightreaper_mark full rewrite; skill-bar _sbBuild + live renderer show level locks; class-select HTML + CSS.
  • Save-compat: yes. Existing characters see the new skill cadence immediately based on their current level.

v0.22.0 MapleStory nostalgia pack â€” Emotes Â· Chat Â· Tabs Â· Music

Four features pulled straight from the MapleStory feel-book: emote speech bubbles on number keys, idle chatter over mobs and NPCs, a proper five-tab inventory, and synthesized chiptune music per map. All Web-Audio / Canvas â€” still single-file, still no binary assets.

ðŸ˜Š Emotes (4-8)

  • 4 ðŸ˜Š smile  Â·  5 ðŸ˜² shocked  Â·  6 ðŸ˜‚ laugh  Â·  7 ðŸ˜¢ cry  Â·  8 ðŸ’¤ sleep
  • Pops a rounded speech bubble above the player's head for ~2 seconds with a tiny pointer tail.
  • Fades out over the last 400 ms.
  • Doesn't pause the game, doesn't cost MP, doesn't interrupt combat. Pure flavour.

ðŸ’¬ Chat bubbles â€” mobs + NPCs

  • Every monster rolls a chatter timer every 8-25 seconds with ~40% chance to speak. Bubble sits for 2.2 s. Per-type phrase pools (snails go "zzz..." / "slippery!", zombies "braiiins" / "ughh", wraiths "oooo~" / "fade...", etc).
  • NPCs idle-chatter every 12-30 seconds with their role-specific lines (Elspeth: "Fresh potions!", Brok: "Clang! Clang!", Amnesiac: "...who...", Sage Mira: "The boons await", etc).
  • NPC chat hides when the player stands next to them â€” the [N] Talk prompt takes priority.
  • Bubbles draw outside the squash/stretch transform so they stay upright when the mob wobbles.
  • Shared _drawBubble() helper powers emote + mob + NPC bubbles with a consistent pill + tail look.

ðŸŽ’ Inventory tabs (5-tab layout)

  • âš” Equip â€” weapons, armour, accessories. The default tab. Click items to equip/unequip.
  • ðŸ§ª Use â€” potions and throwables. HP/MP potions now display as stacked icons with Ã—N counts in the top-right of each cell. Click to consume.
  • ðŸ“¦ Etc â€” crafting materials and quest items. Empty for now; populates as future drops land.
  • ðŸª‘ Set-Up â€” chairs, mounts, pets. Reserved bucket for future features.
  • âœ¨ Cash â€” cosmetic-only items. Reserved for event rewards.
  • Active tab highlighted in gold. Equip slots dim to 55% opacity on non-Equip tabs (still visible â€” you know what's worn).
  • Item classification helper _itemTab(it) routes existing inventory items into the right bucket based on slot, type, and a few opt-in fields (cosmetic, chair, etc).
  • Active-tab state (game._invTab) persists while the modal is open so you don't get bounced back to Equip after clicking a potion.

ðŸŽµ Web-Audio music per map

Short looping monophonic-lead + monophonic-bass chiptunes synthesized in Web Audio. Zero sample files, zero download cost.

  • 6 tracks, one per map archetype: town, forest, mushroom, boss, zodiac, tower.
  • Each track defines lead + bass patterns as [note, beats] tuples with per-track tempo (180-300 ms per beat) and waveforms (triangle / square / sawtooth / sine).
  • Short envelope on each note â€” quick attack, gentle decay, soft release â€” for a gameboy-advance chip feel.
  • Scheduler uses audio.ctx.currentTime with a 600 ms look-ahead; re-arms every 150 ms. Seamless loop at the end of the pattern.
  • loadMap(id) automatically swaps to the new track. Maps starting with zod_* all share the Zodiac mystical-triangle theme.
  • M mute / unmute now also pauses the music cleanly. Unmuting resumes the last-played map's track.
  • Music fails gracefully if the audio context isn't yet initialised (browser autoplay policy) â€” first user gesture activates it.

Track vibes at a glance

  • Town â€” C-major pastoral triangle lead over sine bass. Warm, friendly, Henesys-adjacent.
  • Forest â€” D-minor adventurous square lead, driving tempo. Classic overworld.
  • Mushroom / Fungal â€” A-minor sawtooth lead, sparser bass. Slightly eerie, inviting-creepy.
  • Boss â€” C-minor square lead over sawtooth bass. Fast tempo, tense. Reserved for Jelly King / Momma Shroom / Aetherion / Gravitos arenas.
  • Zodiac â€” A-minor triangle lead over sine bass. Mystical, contemplative. Every zodiac arena.
  • Tower â€” C-major ascending square over triangle bass. Climbing rhythm, carriage-of-ascension feel.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html: player literal gains _emote; keydown handler gets the 4-8 emote block; _tickClassIdentity decays the emote timer; drawClassIdentityHUD renders the emote bubble; new _mobChatTick / _drawMobChat helpers + MOB_CHAT_LINES + NPC_CHAT_LINES pools + _drawBubble shared renderer; updateMonsters calls _mobChatTick; monster render loop calls _drawMobChat; drawNPCs gets NPC chatter + near-player suppression; inventory HTML gets a 5-button tab bar + #inv-tabs / #inv-tab-info elements + .inv-tab CSS; renderInventory() rewritten as a tab-filtered renderer; _itemTab(it) classifier; new MUSIC_TRACKS + MAP_MUSIC + _mapTrackId + music object with play / stop / _schedule / _tone; loadMap calls music.play; audio.toggleMute pauses/resumes music.
  • Save-compat: yes. No schema changes. game._invTab defaults to equip on load.

v0.21.2 Class Identity unlocked from Lv 1

Charge & Release (Warrior), Momentum (Rogue), Chain Cast (Mage), and Rooted Fire (Archer) are now available the moment you pick a class â€” no more waiting until Lv 10 to feel your class's signature mechanic.

What changed

  • _classIdentityActive() now returns true as soon as player.cls is set, instead of requiring both player.cls and player.job. The charge-hold, momentum gauge, chain-cast detection, and archer draw/steady all light up immediately.
  • Mage crit CDR refund (every crit shaves 150 ms off all other cooldowns) no longer gates on job advancement either â€” base Mages can start feeling the rotation pressure from their first kill.
  • Class-select cards â€” the identity block on each class card dropped the "From Lv 10:" prefix so the pitch reads as a day-one feature.

Why

The Lv 10 gate was originally a design hedge â€” we wanted the job-advancement to feel like a meaningful milestone. In practice it made the first 10 levels feel generic: all four classes basically chose which basic animation to watch. Opening the mechanics from Lv 1 means a fresh Warrior can hold a skill and feel the charge ring; a Rogue sees their momentum bar climb; a Mage gets rewarded for cycling through basic skills; an Archer learns to plant their feet before a shot. The learning curve matches the moment-to-moment gameplay.

Balance note

No damage numbers were adjusted. The charge at +100% is still +100%; momentum still caps at +100%; chain still empowers to 1.5Ã—; archer draw still caps at +140% with full Steady. Mobs haven't been re-tuned â€” the difficulty bump in v0.20 already assumed these mechanics were in play.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html: one-line change in _classIdentityActive(), one condition removed in hitMonster (mage CDR refund gate), four description strings in CLASSES.
  • Save-compat: yes. No schema changes.

v0.21.1 Modal toggle â€” press same key to close

Every pop-up panel is now dismissible three ways: the same key that opened it, the âœ• button, or Esc. Previously only B (inventory) and Esc closed â€” pressing K/U/I/N/? again did nothing.

What changed

  • K / U â€” toggle the Level Up panel.
  • I â€” toggle the Skills Reference card.
  • N â€” toggle Codex (or the NPC dialog when one is in range).
  • ? / / â€” toggle the Help panel.
  • B already toggled (via toggleModal); unchanged.
  • Esc and the âœ• close buttons continue to close via closeAllModals(); unchanged.

Bonus: variant swap on shared modal

The Level Up panel and Skills Reference share the same attributes-modal container. The new toggleSharedModal(id, variant, openFn) helper tracks which variant is currently rendered via modal.dataset.variant:

  • Press K with nothing open â†’ opens Level Up (variant lp).
  • Press I while Level Up is showing â†’ content swaps to Skills Reference (variant skills).
  • Press K again â†’ swaps back to Level Up.
  • Press the key that matches the CURRENT variant â†’ closes the modal.

No accidental close when you're swapping between the two panels.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html: new toggleModalOrOpen() + toggleSharedModal() helpers next to toggleModal(); keydown branches for K/U/I/N/? updated to call the toggles.
  • No changes to closeAllModals(), the âœ• buttons, or Esc handling â€” those paths already worked.
  • Save-compat: yes. No schema changes.

v0.21.0 Accuracy Â· Archer Z aim Â· MISS rolls

Three linked combat changes. The Archer's Z basic no longer auto-repeats on hold â€” each press fires one arrow, and damage scales with Steady Aim so patient shots hurt. A new Accuracy stat drives a real miss-roll against monster Evasion, and the gap between the two matters: if your accuracy is too far below a target's evasion, you'll watch "MISS" float over their head.

ðŸ¹ Archer Z â€” aimed single shot

  • Edge-triggered. Holding Z no longer auto-spams arrows. Each arrow is one press. The auto-cast poll skips arrowShot for archers; a dedicated keydown handler fires on the down-edge only.
  • Steady Aim now empowers the shot. Standing still builds Steady stacks (0 â†’ +40%). Each Z press consumes most of the stacks for a bigger arrow: +damage, faster velocity, less drop, brighter tracer. Fire-and-reposition.
  • High-Steady shots (â‰¥ 35% stacks) get a louder flash + extra muzzle sparks so you can see the "aimed shot" land.

â—Ž Accuracy & Evasion

A new combat axis. Basic attacks (sword / arrow / dagger / bolt) now roll against a monster's evasion â€” if your accuracy is too low, the attack misses outright and floats "MISS" in grey.

  • getAccuracy() â€” class-based floor plus AGI, equipment, and boon contributions:
    • Archer: 130 base (highest â€” class fantasy)
    • Rogue: 110 base
    • Warrior: 100 base
    • Mage: 90 base (weakest basic-hit accuracy â€” mages lean on AOE)
    • +1.5 per AGI point, +steadyÃ—0.9 (archer only), + equipment accuracy, + Eagle Eye boon
  • Hit-chance formula: chance = clamp(0.15, 1, 1 âˆ’ (evasion âˆ’ accuracy) / 150). If accuracy â‰¥ evasion you always hit; each 150 points of gap drops hit chance by 100% (floored at 15% so high-evasion foes aren't un-hittable).
  • Skill AOEs always connect â€” only melee / arrow / dagger / bolt tags roll accuracy. Earthbreaker, Meteor Shower, Aegis, Bloodwave, etc. bypass the check so big MP/CD casts never whiff to RNG.
  • Thorns and burn DoTs also bypass accuracy.
  • U panel now shows your Accuracy value next to the other derived stats.

Monster Evasion table

Every monsterType now declares an evasion value. Elite variants +20%, mini-bosses +40%. Tower floors add up to +100% evasion at high floors.

MobEvasionNotes
Slippy (snail)40Slow, easy target
Jelly (slime)50Bounces a bit
Shroom55Pops spores
Horncap75Fast-ish
Sunbun85Hoppy
Stumpy60Slow but tough
Rotter100Mid evasion
Pincer (scorpion)130Speed 1.7 â€” very elusive
Wrappy (mummy)90Shambles
Bones (skeleton)105Joints rattle out of the way
Spook (wraith)140Phases
Jelly King (boss)110Boss
Momma Shroom (boss)130Boss
Aetherion (superboss)180Warden of the Sanctum
Gravitos (hyperboss)210Even light falls
Zodiac bosses140 + orderÃ—8Aries 148 â†’ Pisces 244

ðŸ¦… New boon â€” Eagle Eye (Accuracy)

  • Rolls +15 to +45 Accuracy per boon. Rerollable like any other.
  • Maps to the new player.mods.accuracy stat. Summed across equipped boons.
  • Essential if you're fighting zodiac bosses or hyperbosses on a low-AGI warrior / mage.

Balance implications

  • A warrior with default 5 AGI has 107 accuracy. Hits snails always. Hits Pincer (ev 130) at ~85% rate. Hits Pisces (ev 244) at ~15% (miss floor). Needs Eagle Eye boons or high-AGI AP investment to reliably land basics on endgame zodiac bosses.
  • An archer with 30 AGI + full Steady + 1 Eagle Eye boon can push accuracy to ~220, reliably landing on Pisces.
  • Skills (powerStrike, groundSlam, all job / master ults) are untouched â€” they still always connect. Only the basic-attack chip damage respects evasion.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html: new getAccuracy() helper next to getEvasion(); new _rollAccuracyHit() + BASIC_HIT_SKILLS set; miss check at top of hitMonster; monsterTypes gain evasion field; zodiac registration loop adds evasion; spawnMonster scales instance evasion for elite / mini-boss / tower; player.mods gains accuracy; POWERUPS gains acc (Eagle Eye); renderLevelUp shows Accuracy in the stats line; poll loop skips archer slot-d auto-cast; keydown handler adds archer Z edge-trigger; arrowShot SKILL_FN gains Steady damage scaling + cash-out.
  • Save-compat: yes. Existing characters gain accuracy on next getAccuracy() call. Missing evasion field on monsters defaults to 40 (snail-tier) via fallback.

v0.20.1 Sniper F â†’ Strafe Volley

Sniper's job signature (F) was "Deadeye" â€” a single guaranteed-crit piercing shot. It's now Strafe Volley: a sideways hop + three piercing arrows fired in rapid succession on different vertical lanes.

Strafe Volley

  • 3 piercing arrows fired at 0 ms / 60 ms / 120 ms â€” reads as a rhythmic bang-bang-bang burst.
  • Lane stagger: Arrow 1 angles high, Arrow 2 flies dead-straight, Arrow 3 angles low. Covers grouped enemies at different heights.
  • Sideways hop on cast: the sniper strafes backwards away from the facing direction with a small vertical lift. Gives the "strafe-fire" silhouette. Skipped if the cast came from a hold-to-draw release (the draw flow already positions the archer).
  • 260 ms invulnerability during the volley so you don't eat a swing to the face mid-burst.
  • Damage: 1.55Ã— ATK + 12 per arrow. Three arrows = ~4.65Ã— ATK total â€” comparable to the old single Deadeye but spread across 3 hits for cleave potential.
  • MP 22 â†’ 26, CD 3.5 s â†’ 3.8 s (tiny uptick for the added utility).
  • Works with the archer's hold-to-draw â€” hold F, charge the draw, release to fire the empowered volley.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html: SKILLS.deadeye rename ("Deadeye" â†’ "Strafe Volley") + MP/CD tweak, SKILL_FNS.deadeye rewritten as a 3-arrow staggered volley with sideways strafe hop + muzzle flashes per arrow.
  • Keeps the skill id deadeye for save-compat â€” no schema change.

v0.20.0 Archer charge Â· Boon bag cap Â· Harder mobs

Four linked changes tightening the economy and class balance. The Archer now holds to draw (like the Warrior), the Warrior's full charge loses its free crit, the Boon bag caps at 25 so overflow has to be discarded, and every combat mob tier got a difficulty bump to catch up with stronger players.

ðŸ¹ Archer â€” hold-to-draw

  • Holding a skill key now charges a draw meter (same timing as the Warrior â€” ~0.75 s to full) while the archer's feet are forced still. Release fires the skill.
  • Damage scales multiplicatively with hold time: tap = 1Ã—, half-hold = 1.5Ã—, full draw = 2Ã—. Steady Aim stacks (max +40%) still add on top, so full draw at max Steady caps at +140% damage.
  • No more auto-root on skill cast. Tap-fired skills keep the archer mobile. The old 0.4 s post-cast freeze was removed â€” if you want bonus damage, commit to the hold.
  • Visual: green-tinted charge ring (vs the Warrior's orange), foot-bracket indicators at the ground, "ðŸ¹ LOOSE" prompt at 100%.

âš” Warrior â€” full-charge crit guarantee removed

  • Full-charge releases still double damage (up to +100%) but no longer auto-crit. Crits are earned through Crit %, Sharp Eye boons, Crit Damage stacking, and skill tree nodes.
  • This was a small but meaningful nerf â€” a full charge on a class with 5 stacked boons was hitting for absurd numbers with the free crit. Stays impactful, no longer silly.

â—ˆ Boon bag cap at 25

  • New cap: BOON_INVENTORY_CAP = 25. You can own at most 25 rolled boons. Equip cap of 5 is unchanged.
  • Bag-full behaviour: when the bag is full, orb pickups / Sage buys / level-up rewards toast "Boon bag full â€” discard one first (press U)" and the new boon is not consumed. You don't lose the opportunity â€” you just can't hoard indefinitely.
  • Loadout UI now shows N / 25 in bag with a color that shifts orange â†’ red as you approach the cap, plus a "Bag 100% full" footer when at cap.
  • Discarding already existed per-boon (red Discard button with confirm dialog). No UI changes needed â€” just make room.

ðŸ’¢ Difficulty rebalance

Class identity multipliers and stacked boon rolls pushed player power too high â€” fights outside bosses felt trivial at Lv 20+. Monster tiers rebalanced to restore tension:

TierHPATKExamples
1 (early)+15%+10%Slippy Â· Jelly Â· Shroom
2 (mid)+25%+15%Horncap Â· Sunbun Â· Stumpy
3 (late)+35%+20%Rotter Â· Wrappy Â· Bones Â· Pincer Â· Spook
Bosses+30%+15%Jelly King Â· Momma Shroom Â· Aetherion Â· Gravitos
Zodiac+30%+15%All 12 zodiac bosses
  • XP / Lumen drops scale up alongside HP so grind time stays roughly constant despite tankier foes.
  • Elite / mini-boss multipliers (Ã—3 / Ã—5 HP, Ã—1.5 / Ã—2 ATK) unchanged â€” they compound on top of the new base stats automatically.
  • Player-side damage math untouched â€” you keep every multiplier you had; fights just aren't over in one charged Power Strike anymore.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html: monsterTypes (all tiers + zodiac registration), _computeClassSkillMul (warrior no-crit, archer charge formula), tryStartWarriorCharge / tryReleaseWarriorCharge renamed to tryStartClassCharge / tryReleaseClassCharge with back-compat aliases, _tickClassIdentity (archer root-during-hold), drawClassIdentityHUD (archer charge ring + foot brackets), acquirePowerup (bag cap gate), renderBoonPanel (N/25 header), CLASSES identity copy.
  • New constant: BOON_INVENTORY_CAP = 25.
  • Save-compat: yes. Existing characters retain all boons in their bag (no saved characters had more than 25).

v0.19.1 Bloodlust rework Â· Phantom ultimate

Two skill surgeries. Bloodlust stops being a passive ring and becomes an active amplifier for basic attacks. Phantom's ultimate goes from a single teleport-slash to a cinematic chain-execution on up to 6 marked targets.

ðŸ©¸ Berserker â€” Bloodlust (nerfed + reworked)

The old 10 s auto-shockwave ring felt "cast and ignore" â€” you popped it and the game played itself for 10 s. Now the buff rewards active swinging.

  • No more auto-shockwave ring. The 400 ms projectile ring-of-10 that auto-fired for 10 s is retired.
  • Z basic attacks launch forward shockwaves while Bloodlust is active. Every basic swing spits out a getAtk() Ã— 0.9 + 4 shockwave that pierces in the facing direction. Player-driven damage, not passive.
  • Duration trimmed 15 s â†’ 12 s. On-cast heal / MP refund trimmed 25% â†’ 20%. Cooldown shortened 20 s â†’ 18 s to compensate.
  • Net effect: Bloodlust damage now depends on how fast you're swinging. Rewards aggression; no free DPS from standing still.

ðŸ‘» Phantom â€” Voidrift Execution (new ultimate)

The old Dimensional Cut was one teleport-strike for ATK Ã— 4 â€” punchy but short. The rework turns it into Phantom's signature multi-target execute.

  • Marks up to 6 nearest enemies in 900 px. Each mark gets a floating violet sigil.
  • Chain-teleports through every mark at 110 ms intervals â€” rogue flashes from target to target on alternating flanks, leaving a shadow trail.
  • Each strike ignores DEF, always crits, and hits for getAtk() Ã— 3.2 Ã— critDmg. Execute fodder: dead targets are skipped so the chain doesn't waste on corpses.
  • Final mark triggers a shadow nova at the rogue's landing point â€” 220 px radius, ATK Ã— 2 + 20 to every mob caught, 700 ms stun, violet explosion rings + radial spokes.
  • Survival reward: +15% max HP heal after the nova fires. 2.2 s invulnerability blanket during the full chain.
  • If no enemies are in range â†’ whiffs gracefully with a 30 MP refund instead of consuming full cost.
  • Cost rebalanced: MP 30 â†’ 55, CD 7.5 s â†’ 14 s. Bigger, slower, defines the class.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html: SKILLS.bloodlust def (duration / CD / desc), SKILLS.phantom_cut def (name "Voidrift Execution" / MP / CD / desc), SKILL_FNS.bloodlust simplified, SKILL_FNS.slash gains the Bloodlust shockwave rider, SKILL_FNS.phantom_cut rewritten as a 6-strike chain + nova, old _bloodstorm tick in updatePlayer reduced to a back-compat drain.
  • No gameplay data lost on old saves â€” player._bloodstorm still exists but emits nothing.
  • Save-compat: yes.

v0.19.0 Equippable Boons + Lumen Rerolls

Power-ups, Sage Mira's purchases, and every other boon source now drop rolled instances that the player has to equip. Up to 5 boons can be active at once; the rest wait in inventory. Each boon has a rolled stat value within a range â€” spend Lumens to reroll for better stats.

What changed

  • Boons now roll stat values. Every boon template (Iron Muscles, Lifesteal, Sharp Eye, etc.) has a [min, max] range. Acquisition rolls once; e.g. Iron Muscles rolls +8% ATK to +22% ATK. What you get is random, but you can reroll.
  • Equip cap of 5. You own as many boons as you want; only 5 can be active at a time. Orb pickups / level-up picks / Sage buys auto-equip when a slot is open and sit in inventory otherwise.
  • Press U for the new Loadout panel. Shows 5 equipped slots at the top, full inventory below. Each card has Equip / Unequip / Reroll / Discard buttons. Rerolls cost 500 Lumens base (+250 per previous reroll on the same boon) and shuffle the roll within the boon's range.
  • Old saves migrate automatically. Legacy player._powerups (array of boon ids) gets converted on load: each old powerup becomes a rolled instance with a median roll, auto-equipped up to the 5-slot cap. No player action needed.
  • Sage Mira's shop still sells a random boon for 1 500 Lumens â€” but now the boon drops into inventory instead of auto-applying. Spend more Lumens to reroll it if the stat is bad.
  • Level-up boon picks show the actual rolled value on each card so the choice isn't blind. Pick the one whose roll landed best in the range.

The 17 boon templates (roll ranges)

BoonStatRange
ðŸ’ª Iron MusclesATK %+8% to +22% ATK
ðŸ—¡ Keen EdgeATK+3 to +10 ATK
ðŸ›¡ Thick SkinDEF+3 to +10 DEF
â¤ VitalityMax HP+20 to +60 Max HP
ðŸ’™ Inner FocusMax MP+12 to +40 Max MP
ðŸ‘ Sharp EyeCrit+5% to +15% Crit
ðŸ’¢ Lethal StrikeCrit Damage+25% to +75% Crit Damage
ðŸ‘Ÿ Quick FeetMove Speed+0.3 to +1.2 Speed
ðŸ¦˜ Leap BoostJump+0.6 to +1.8 Jump
ðŸ©¸ LifestealLifesteal4% to 12% of damage
ðŸ”¥ Burning TouchBurn tiersÃ—1 to Ã—3 burn stacks
ðŸŽ¯ Double ShotMultishot+1 to +2 projectiles
â™» Mana SurgeMP regen+1 to +3 tiers
ðŸŒµ ThornsReflect15% to 40% reflected
ðŸ’Ž Treasure HunterLumen drop+20% to +60% drops
ðŸ“˜ Quick LearnerEXP+15% to +45% EXP
ðŸª‚ Triple JumpExtra jumpunlocks third mid-air jump (unique â€” no variance)

Under the hood

  • POWERUPS is now a data schema (stat, min, max, fmt, scale). The old apply() functions were removed â€” stats are derived via scale(roll) and summed in _applyEquippedBoons().
  • player.boons holds instances {id, roll, rerolls}; player.boonsEquipped is an array of inventory indices (max BOON_EQUIP_CAP = 5).
  • player.mods is now purely derived â€” _applyEquippedBoons() wipes it and re-sums every equipped boon's contribution, gated by stat key. Called on equip, unequip, reroll, discard, and on load.
  • Helpers: rollBoonInstance, getBoonDef, formatBoon, boonRerollCost, equipBoon, unequipBoon, rerollBoon, discardBoon, _migrateLegacyPowerups.
  • UI: new renderBoonPanel() slots into openLevelUpPanel() below the radar chart. Equipped-slot grid (5 cells) + inventory grid (auto-fill) with inline Equip / Unequip / Reroll (cost pill) / Discard buttons.
  • Save: boons and boonsEquipped now in PLAYER_SAVE_FIELDS. Legacy _powerups kept in save list so migration has something to read; it's deleted by the migrator.

Balance notes

  • Old cap was 8 total / 3 same-type, all always-active. New cap is 5 active with no same-type limit (you can equip three Iron Muscles if you have three of them â€” just takes three of your five slots).
  • Reroll curve: first reroll 500ðŸ’°, second 750ðŸ’°, third 1 000ðŸ’°, fourth 1 250ðŸ’°, etc. No upper bound â€” chasing god-rolls is an intended Lumen sink.
  • Legacy saves get median rolls ((min+max)/2) so post-migration power is close to pre-migration with no RNG penalty on existing players.

v0.18.0 Class Identity â€” four distinct playstyles

Every class now plays meaningfully differently after job advancement (Lv 10+). Existing skills stay the same â€” a new mechanical layer wraps around them so how you cast starts to matter as much as what you cast.

âš” Warrior â€” Charge & Release

  • Hold a skill key to charge. A ring fills around the warrior over ~0.75 s. Release fires the skill with up to +100% damage.
  • At 100% charge: guaranteed crit + hit-stop + screen shake + orange particle burst. Toast reads "âš” EMPOWERED".
  • Quick-tap (<120 ms) casts at minimum charge â€” normal damage.
  • Charge cancels if you get stunned or die. Auto-cast polling is suppressed while a key is held, so you don't accidentally spam the skill.
  • Applies to every warrior skill except basic attack â€” X S C D F V G all support charging.

ðŸ—¡ Rogue â€” Momentum

  • Moving builds a gauge (0-100). Running full-tilt fills it in ~1 s. Standing still drains it faster than it builds.
  • Skills spend most of the gauge (âˆ’60) for up to +100% damage. A full-gauge cast is a massive burst.
  • Visible as a thin purple bar above the rogue that brightens to pink near 100%.
  • Rewards hit-and-run / corridor-running play. Standing still to channel skills gives 0 bonus.
  • Quick-dash, rush, and dodge roll all count as movement for gauge purposes.

ðŸ”® Mage â€” Chain Cast

  • Cast two DIFFERENT skills within 1 s â†’ the second one deals 1.5Ã— damage. A floating "âŒ¬ CHAIN" indicator appears above the mage after the first cast so you know the window is live.
  • Every crit shaves 150 ms off every other skill's cooldown. Mage perks already give 30% CDR; this stacks on top â€” critting into a rotation feels like the cooldowns evaporate.
  • Gated to once per frame so a single AOE tick can't reset everything.
  • Rewards spell-rotation play instead of spamming one nuke.

ðŸ¹ Archer â€” Rooted Fire

  • Casting a skill freezes the archer in place for ~0.4 s (forces vx = 0, gravity still applies). In exchange you get +25% damage.
  • Standing still builds Steady Aim â€” stacks climb +0.25/frame up to +40% damage. A "â—Ž AIM +N%" label appears above the archer.
  • Moving horizontally at any speed breaks Steady instantly.
  • Skill damage formula while rooted: base Ã— (1.25 + steady/100). Max boost: +65%.
  • Rewards planting your feet and actually aiming â€” suits the class fantasy of a sniper who can't move-and-shoot.

How it works under the hood

  • New module CLASS IDENTITY near castSkill. Each class has a _tickClassIdentity pass per frame and a _computeClassSkillMul hook called before every skill effect runs.
  • Central damage multiplier on player._classSkillMul with a 2-second expiry window, so delayed projectiles / tethered volleys from the same cast still pick up the bonus.
  • Applied in hitMonster for non-melee / non-thorns / non-aegis hits.
  • All four states (_warCharge, _momentum, _steady, _rootedTimer, _mageLastCastT) are cleared on death, respawn, loadMap, and loadState. No leaking between runs.
  • Class-select cards gained a new purple â—ˆ Identity block under the perk describing the mechanic.
  • Save-compat: yes. No schema changes. An existing Lv 20 character gains their mechanic immediately on load.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html: CLASSES (identity copy), player literal (init fields), castSkill (pre-cast hook), hitMonster (multiplier + mage crit refund), updatePlayer (tick + charge polling suppression), keydown/keyup (warrior start/release), triggerDeath + respawnAtTown (state clear), render pass (drawClassIdentityHUD), class-select card markup.
  • Version bumped to v0.18.0.

v0.17.3 Talk moves to N

Small but annoying conflict: F used to be both "Talk to NPC" and "Job signature skill" (slot Q). Pressing F next to an NPC would open their dialog and cast a skill in the same frame. Talk is now on N; F is reserved for chest / pickup / Job signature.

What shipped

  • N is the primary Talk key. If an NPC is within 60 px of the player, N opens their dialog; otherwise it opens the Codex (achievements + bestiary). Context-aware â€” one key, two meanings, no conflict.
  • F no longer doubles as Talk. Still opens chests, picks up drops, and casts the Job Signature (Q-slot) skill â€” which was the original intent of the key. Pressing F near an NPC will continue to talk via the existing tryInteract chest/portal/NPC fallback, so muscle memory isn't broken, but the primary affordance is now N.
  • In-world prompt updated. Floating NPC label now reads [N] Talk instead of [F] Talk.
  • Help panel + Skills Reference updated. The ? Menus & Items column now lists N Talk to NPC / Codex and F Open Chest / Pickup. The I-key Skills Reference card describes the new context-aware N binding.
  • Mobile deck button repurposed. The ðŸ’¬ bubble button at the bottom-right of the mobile controller now dispatches N instead of F, so mobile players get the same context-aware Talk / Codex behaviour.
  • README Controls table now documents N = Talk / Codex and F = Open chest / Pickup, with a footnote explaining the context-aware behaviour and the legacy F talk fallback.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html: keydown handler for N (context branch), drawNPCs prompt label, help panel markup, Skills Reference row for N + F, bottom-bar controls pill, mobile deck button, version bump to v0.17.3.
  • README.md: Controls â†’ UI & utility section updated.
  • No game logic changes beyond the keybind remap. Save-compat: yes.

v0.17.2 Onboarding hotfix

Six small fixes pulled out of a headless-browser playthrough. All new-player friction â€” wrong docs, invisible zodiac portal, hostile entry spawns, dailies that require items a fresh character doesn't have.

What shipped

  • README keybind table rewritten to match KEY_TO_SLOT. Previously said D was basic attack â€” actually Z is basic attack and D casts War Cry (7 s CD). Every skill key was shifted. The table is now split into Movement / Combat / UI sections with an explanatory line about the left-hand cluster layout (Z X S C D F V G).
  • Zodiac Sanctum visual cue. The Zodiac portal shares an x-coord with The Amnesiac, so its purple swirl was completely hidden behind the cottage. Added a floating 8-point zodiac sigil (pulses, rotates, orbits 3 sparkles) above the Amnesiac's cottage roof, plus a "ZODIAC SANCTUM" banner that appears when the player is within 200 px. Portal hitbox unchanged â€” the sigil is pure visual advertisement.
  • HUD label LP: â†’ SP:. Matches the standard MMO convention for Skill Points. Tooltip added so players know what it spends on. Carried through to the Level-Up panel copy and the Skills Reference card.
  • Stuck input on map change. loadMap now clears game.keys and player.lastTapKey. Previously, if a key was physically down during a portal transition, its keyup often never reached the window after the swap â€” leaving game.keys['arrowup'] true, which caused ghost movement / repeated portal attempts / phantom skill casts on the new map. Paired with cancelPendingSkillTimers() from v0.17.1 â€” same principle, clear transient state on transition.
  • Forest entry safe zone. On combat maps, mobs that spawned within 240 px of the entry point are now pushed deeper into the map (max 320 px clearance). Fixes the Lv 1 "lose 40% HP before the first swing" issue in Emerald Thicket where the default entry x=160 overlapped a 5-mob cluster. Boss arenas and the tower are exempt â€” they deliberately spawn close.
  • Daily Challenge pool gated by level. Added minLv on each challenge. Lv 1-5 rotates through three beginner-friendly ones (Defeat 10 enemies, Visit 3 different maps, Talk to 3 different NPCs); Lv 6+ unlocks the old kill 50 / combo 30 / open 3 chests set; Lv 15+ unlocks enhance an item / reach Lv 25. Tracking for visit/talk uses per-day sets so revisits don't re-count. Fixes the Lv 1 "Enhance any item once" first-impression where new players have no weapon and no enhancement stones.

Touched surface

  • README.md: Controls section rewritten into Movement / Combat / UI tables.
  • maple_game.html: HUD SP label, drawNPCs (zodiac sigil), loadMap (input clear + forest entry buffer + visit tick), openChest + openNPC (daily hooks), DAILY_CHALLENGES + _pickDailyChallenge + tickDaily.
  • Version bumped to v0.17.2.
  • No combat-math changes. No skill-tree changes. Save-compat: yes.

v0.17.1 Code-health hotfix

Five surgical bug fixes pulled out of a 3-agent parallel audit. No gameplay changes â€” purely stops ghost effects from lingering past death, kills an infinite-buff exploit, and flattens the per-frame particle cost.

Bugs fixed

  • Ghost Bloodlust / Aegis / Clones after death. triggerDeath() and respawnAtTown() now null out player._bloodstorm, player._bloodstormNextTick, player._aegis and player._clones. Dying mid-ultimate no longer leaves shockwaves / hovering orbs / shadow clones firing on the corpse through the death screen.
  • Mute state desync. audio.muted is now the single source of truth. saveState() mirrors it into game.muted only at serialize-time; toggleMute() no longer writes to two places. Reload-while-muted no longer drifts.
  • Infinite-buff save/reload exploit. loadState() now zeros every player.buffs.* timer and every transient combat flag (invulnerable, rushTimer, dodgeTimer, _aegis, _clones, _pendingTimeouts) after the Object.assign. Previously: pop Bloodlust â†’ refresh browser â†’ 15 s buff persists forever.
  • Queued skill ticks surviving the player. New scheduleSkillTimer() + cancelPendingSkillTimers() wrapper. All 29 setTimeout call-sites inside SKILL_FNS now register their id into player._pendingTimeouts. Death, respawn, and map transitions clear the queue. A 7-strike volley mid-cast no longer keeps detonating on an empty arena after you die or portal out.
  • Per-frame particle O(nÂ²). The particle / damage-number update loops previously did splice(i, 1) per expired entry â€” O(nÂ²) in bursty frames. Replaced with a single-pass write-index compactor (O(n)). The overflow-cap still drops oldest-first via splice(0, excess), but only in frames that actually overflow.

Touched surface

  • maple_game.html: audio block, player literal, loadState / saveState, triggerDeath / respawnAtTown, loadMap, updateParticles, SKILL_FNS (29 setTimeout swaps).
  • Version bumped to v0.17.1.
  • No asset, controls, or balance changes. Save-compat: yes.

Documentation shareable

Gaming-psychology audit + phased roadmap

Added GAMING_AUDIT.html â€” a self-contained, dark-themed audit doc designed to be shared with collaborators. Covers a 12-lever psychology scorecard, cross-confirmed gap analysis from three parallel codebase audits, and a ROI-ranked roadmap (Phase 1 quick wins Â· Phase 2 structural Â· Phase 3 big bets) with code anchors into maple_game.html. The doc is an audit only â€” no gameplay changes yet.

Auto-pull SessionStart hook

Added .claude/hooks/session-start.sh + .claude/settings.json so every Claude Code session begins with a git fetch origin and a fast-forward / rebase of the current branch onto origin/main. Prevents drift against a human collaborator's pushes. Opt out per-session with CLAUDE_DONT_PULL=1. Always exits 0 so it never blocks a session; handles dirty tree, detached HEAD, fetch timeout, and rebase conflicts gracefully.

Mobile-branch hooks â€” auto-rebase before push + zone guard (branch mobile-ui-pass only)

Two new PreToolUse / PostToolUse hooks scoped to the mobile-ui-pass branch so the mobile UI pass can run in parallel with gameplay work on main without late-stage merge pain:

  • Pre-push auto-rebase (.claude/hooks/mobile-pre-push.py). When Claude is about to run git push on mobile-ui-pass, the hook fetches origin/main and rebases first. Blocks the push with a clear conflict list if the rebase collides; exits clean (hook skipped) if the working tree is dirty or the branch is already up-to-date.
  • Zone guard (.claude/hooks/mobile-zone-check.py). After every Edit / Write / MultiEdit on maple_game.html, the hook computes the changed hunks' line ranges and flags any that fall outside the mobile "safe zones" (7â€“10, 33, 65â€“322, 472â€“593, 1135â€“1155, 1464â€“1500, 3317â€“3744). Warning only â€” never blocks. Safe-zone list documented in CLAUDE.md.

Both hooks no-op on branches other than mobile-ui-pass, so they're safe to keep registered in .claude/settings.json.

How to try it

git fetch origin main
git switch main
open maple_game.html

Or drop the file into any static server / preview. No install step.

Verification checklist

  1. Save. Pick a class, reach level 2, kill a mob, refresh. Expect Welcome back! toast + same level / mesos / map (you'll land in town).
  2. Audio. Hit a mob â†’ tick. Land a crit â†’ bright two-tone. Level up â†’ 3-note rise. M toggles mute.
  3. Daily. Banner top-right shows progress. Complete the challenge â†’ reward toast.
  4. Codex. Press N â€” 12 achievements, live kill count, best combo. Kill the first mob â†’ First Blood unlocks.
  5. Respec + Reset. Open attributes (U). Respec refunds AP + wipes milestones. Reset (red button) wipes the whole save.
  6. Prestige. Dev console (hold 1+2+3) â†’ set level 50 â†’ kill a mob â†’ ascend prompt. After: L1 with permanent Ã—1.3 XP / Ã—1.3 DMG.
  7. Combos. Farm a weak mob past 50 / 100 / 150 hits â€” three escalating buffs.
  8. Juice. A 500-damage crit should be visibly huge; rare drops glint, legendary drops explode. Aetherion and Gravitos phase shifts hit-stop the frame.

Controls summary

KeyAction
NOpen codex / achievements
UCharacter sheet (attributes, respec, reset)
KSkill tree
BInventory
MMute / unmute
FInteract
R / TQuick HP / MP potion
1+2+3 (hold)Dev console
